text: [[VIDEOID:2a7ad2a91afb1e17ff4a1c143f7e10a2]] Received a damaged item. The mold has a hole in it making it useless.  I am hoping to get it replaced as it is worthless to me as-is.  I will update my review later.
3rd one that arrived damaged! I give up!

These are regular mica powders!  One appears to have had some interference added, but the others are devoid of the addition.  They have literally just mixed 3 colors together so instead of getting the cute colors they advertise in their photos you get an ugly brown from the purple/red-blue/green, a gold from the blue/green/gold & a bronze from the yellow/orange/bronze.  Only one of my envelopes even appeared to have the added micas. Two envelopes appeared to only have the hold mica and bronze mica without the additional two colors they were supposed to have as if they decided to just not even bother adding them.  I purchased these to make color shift watercolor paints & as such they are utterly useless. There is no way in the world that you can possibly get the results that they advertise in they’re pictures from the envelopes they mail you. It is physically & chemically impossible!  I am disgusted.  $8 for 15 grams of useless product & worse it’s the exact same mica powders “Riya” that I purchased in a set of 100 last week & had already tried to mix that way for a color shift which was why I was specifically looking for color shift mica!  These are metallic. They are in no way color shift!  Now I’ll have to see what the return/refund policy is with amazon as how do you return something you have made & put into ur own paint palettes before you found out they were garbage? Hmm?

[[VIDEOID:8644a01103c90ab8f83af816b83881be]] Likes: the colors are fabulous. I love that the stamp pads can be stacked! I like that I can run my stamps over the pad & not just press it down inside a stamp pad. Yes, it messes up the coloring, but I can use it on larger stamps.
Dislikes: the pads are very thin & dried out very fast while literally just sitting in my craft room & not even being used which is seriously annoying me right now. I’m disabled & I live on a fixed income so purchasing products that are consumable & have to be replaced drives me nuts to begin with, but then purchasing a consumable item that has a shelf life of used twice & now useless is beyond frustrating. If I had realized before I would have returned it for a replacement obviously bc even tho I now know it’s not got longevity on its side I would probably still purchase it & just be ready to go on a stamping marathon. Lol. As I have adhd & usually need little sleep it’s always a possibility lol.

Unfortunately I was in an accident on 2/4 so I only got to try these a couple of times (literally 2x!!! Woe is me!). & was bedridden & unable to do anything after that until today 3/24 when I opened these again & decided to try to carve a stamp of my Pekingese /Japanese Chin/ Shih Tzu mix puppy Sophie Wynter. Btw: the stamp carving kit is the speedball one sold here on Amazon for $15. I also bought the one by Pixiss for around $10 I think and honestly both are worth the $ imo. I live in chronic pain & I am always trying to find things that will help get my mind off the horrors of living with it day in & day out. It doesn’t make the pain go away, but it does give me something to remind myself that I survived another day with the help of my service pups.
The Stamps: great out of the pkg. they were moist & juicy, but had no staying power. If you have kids that will use them fast or if you intend to use them fast then I think you will like them. However, if you are looking for something to add to your craft stores for ongoing project & future projects & would want to go back & add to stuff later then you need to keep looking or just be prepared to buy more.

Lowered from a 3 star review to a 1 star bc the very obviously defective stamps were replaced with another set of very obviously defective stamps! It drives me nuts to waste my time, $, & energy to purchase something, wait on it to arrive, then go through the hassle of the returns to then wait for a replacement only to have the replacement be just as defective or often worse than the original. Apparently the entire tim of these stamps was bad & nobody ever bothered to fix it. Ok fine if you wanna sell DEFECTIVE stamps, but be HONEST about it so ppl don’t waste their time, $, & energy on DEFECTIVE items they would not have chosen to begin with. I am sure some ppl would still purchase the set. I might even at the right price point, but not like this. Not believing I’m buying something in good condition & not a DEFECTIVE item which both of these are.
OLD REVIEW:
Some of these are pretty nice, but the cardinal & starling are a hot mess. The pigeon is flawed too, but works. Idk if their designer messed up or their manufacturer did, but the actual bird designs are not true to life on several either which I find to be most annoying as a bird lover. It would make an ok gift for kids if you can find them on sale & know 1/4 are useless imo. I have no idea why they bothered to put the bird names on the stamps bc you cannot read them. Great in theory, but lousy execution. I would definitely buy them again if they fixed the flaws & I am actually hoping that Amazon will replace them as these are flawed & I am just stupid enough, foolish enough, naive enough, or hopeful enough that the replacement will arrive perfect. Lol! Yes ppl- I live in my own little make believe world. It’s downright magical & days like today I truly prefer my imagination world where anything & everything is possible & Covid doesn’t exist & ppl don’t hate each other for their politics & ppl believe in peace & abhor war to the real one happening outside my door right now. Bottom line: some birds good, some birds very bad, do yourself a favor & cut the names of the birds off the stamps if you choose to buy them & keep them. It’s just a little minor surgery between friends & nobody gets hurt. Besides: doesn’t everyone feel like wielding a sharp instrument every now & then? Especially when you know nobody is going to get hurt? Lol. Safety warning: buy the safety gloves amazon sells so you don’t knick ur own fingers or just start by being smart & using a tweezers or cuticle stick to hold down the stamps while you remove the bird names. Me, myself, & I am too old to start learning how to live without appendages so I always make sure something else is between me, myself, & I & anything Me, myself, & I am cutting. Yes- I am totes all up in my feelings today. All full of myself hence the me, myself, & I. Lol. Happy crafting shoppers! Now get off ur devices & make something! This is a throw down: I hereby challenge you to create a bird journal arts & crafts page today. That’s it! Clock is ticking! Get a move on! Now! Now! Now! Why yes! I was in the Navy! What gave me, myself & I away? Lol. Peace & Love ppl. Live ur beautiful lives!

🤬I am so tired of receiving items that are so obviously damaged & never should have been packaged or sent in the first place.  Apparently this was not sold by Amazon, but sold by an Amazon third party. Idc anymore. I’m still sick & tired of it. I feel like Amazon needs to do more due diligence to make sure that obviously damaged items & crap & returned items are not being sold on here. Bc you can clearly see both fingerprints on these pics & an obvious burn mark.  Sure, the laser burning the stencil could have made the burn marks. It also could have been left by someone doing pyrography.  Check out the pic of the packaging.  The black sheet that came behind the stencil. I get those from Joann’s or Michael’s 99% of the time they have not a mark on them.  Lately tho anything from Amazon or any of their parties & it looks like the shipping truck has been involved in an accident or it’s come from a war zone.  I swear these third party companies are buying stuff that is seconds or returns or literally insurance write-offs from trucking accidents & selling it as new.  I had sworn off buying anything from third party companies for a long time & foolishly decided to give it a try again. And this is what I got. Lesson learned.  I know there are some good ones out there & I have been fortunate enough to purchase from them & maybe this company was just having a bad day, but I am tired of being burned. It makes it worse that this original order was never delivered to my address as the amazon delivery drivers keep delivering my stuff even after 3 years of living in the same place & literally having 6” numerals of my address right next to my front door to the wrong address. So they write this up as a “replacement”. And they write it up as tho I “returned” the first set which I obviously did not as I never received it!!  38 months now of God knows what ppl receiving my service dogs cookies, food, leashes, harnesses, etc & receiving my art supplies etc.  And it usually starts with a csr saying : well you need to wait a couple hours bc sometimes it says it’s delivered & it’s not yet. Lol. Hello! There’s a picture of a door that is not my door!  Or the other version:  can you go look for it? Lol No! I am a service connected disabled veteran & I pay for amazon prime exactly  for the delivery exactly so that I don’t have to “go look for it” with 18” of snow outside my door & living in an “open carry firearms” state!  Ppl do not like you snooping around their homes with ur phine trying to match amazon pictures of front doors to theirs. Trust me bc I stupidly tried it a few times. And luckily lived to type about it. Barely.  So no Amazon. I will no longer be doing your delivery drivers’ jobs for them.  And if it sounds like I am ticked off it’s bc I am. They have some internal issue where even when I notified them that my service dogs were missing 2 bags of treats & there was not even a picture of the delivery to even remotely guess where they might have been mistakenly delivered they were then unable to replace them!  They had to wait 24 hours to reorder them. This my service dogs did not get the missing dog treats for 4 days!!!  Bc they had issues on their end. So much for next day delivery. Lol. That’s it. That’s my rant. Yes. I feel better. No. I’m not keeping the nasty stencil. It’s going back. My best guess is the blade needed to be changed when they were cutting the stencils & it must have been loose bc you can see extra cut lines all over the top of the stencil from multiple passes. Something was off at the factory.

& I can fit a lot of yarn in it. I like that I am able to open it up entirely unlike a lot of other bags on the market that have weird zipper placements & openings that limit your ability to completely open them. I’ve bought it in several colors & I will probably buy more because I basically use them as project bags.
Works great!

Works great! Looks Dino-mite! Sorry, I just had to. Lol

Overall I was pleased with these, but being new to resin I had no idea that the obviously srrrctuve own molds wouldn’t “even out” once the resin was in the molds. I was expecting the resin to push the sides of the mold out so they lined up correctly. It did not. Lesson learned. Lol. A couple are hard to get the resin down into the very tip of the pens & I have several pens that are missing 1” of resin by the pen tip. I’ll have to apply numerous layers by paint brush by hand I guess. I tried to remold it & that didn’t work. Lol.  Idk if they moved these before the molds were dry or if it was again lousy packaging they created the defects in the mold bc the resin molds I’ve received this past month have literally the worst packaging I’ve ever received.  Resin molds folded over and banded together is not a good way to package them.  4 of the molds seem ok, but 2 are hard to get the resin in & 2 are defective.  Idk if the mold maker just didn’t cut away some of the mold they should have to make putting the resin in easier or not, but I might try removing the resin there as one mold has it removed & the identical mold does not.  They need a better quality control person imo.  Amazon is sending a replacement so I’ll update it when they arrive. The actual pen refills are excellent. I did not expect them to be such high quality!  I’m pleasantly surprised. I was expecting some cheap ballpoint junk.  I just wish someone made a refillable resin pen mold.

Packaging needs some cardboard to keep the molds flat!  I noticed flaws in my mold, but as I’m new to resin I didn’t realize the flaws would be in my finished product. Idk if the molds were touched before they were dry or dryer too fast, but mine have crazing & uneven surfaces. The molds are very shallow & the defects are pretty obvious when you look at them, but hard to photograph. I’ve only used one mold Bc I didn’t wanna waste more resin on the other 2 after seeing how the defects showed up in my finished product. Amazon is sending a replacement. Fingers crossed. I’ll update after I receive them & try them.  I did think about trying to cover the defects with an opaque resin, but the molds are so shallow I’d have to try it out of the mold. No thx on that.  I gave them a 4 on easy to use bc being so shallow makes them not so easy to use. You can’t do any layering of resins. I had planned to use shark stickers for my grandson, but it’s so shallow it hard to lay down a base coat then apply stickers and a top coat.  I couldn’t do it.  The packaging had the molds not lying flat so I couldn’t get my resin to lie flat as it wouldn’t level even tho my resin self-levels. The bottom would be overflowing & the top would have zero resin.  They seriously shipped it with the larger molds folded over on themselves & not one of the molds lies flat.  I thought about trying to weight them down before trying them, but was afraid that would just cause it to be more misshapen.  I have a similar issue with pen molds. They came distorted & I foolishly believed adding resin would reestablish the correct shape. Wrong! Lol. Misshapen/defective molds means misshapen/defective products.

Bc I live on a fixed income I counted my beads & separated them by letters. Who needs 28 z’s? Or 24 x’s?  The letter distribution was awful. In one set I received only 5 of several letters.  The letter paint is literally see through on some of them with the bead color showing & others have extra black paint on them like the painter touched them with paint on their fingers.  Amazon is sending a replacement so I will update the review after I receive them.  I wish the seller would have distributed the letters better. Giving nearly 30 z and x, but only 4 or 5 of other letters is just ridiculous. It feels like they were just trying to get rid of letters they had an abundance of from other kits. So far I’m not impressed. I suggest you go over the letters on the beads with paint or pigma pens & seal them w a clear coat as other reviews say the paint washes off.  My plan was to use them in resin.  If the replacement comes just as bad I’ll probably return them bc having to take the time to repaint them & seal them negates the purpose of purchasing them in the first place.

The add clearly shows 3 molds with a price tag of $23 & so I expect  3 molds at that price. For some idiotic reason the single mold I received was folded over in 2 places & won’t lay flat which means the resin won’t lay level if I use it & will produce odd lopsided pendants if I use it.  The mold came with a substance already on it so I guess it’s already been used because there weren’t any other stray substances in the bag floating around like loose glitter or something, but there is definitely something on the mold giving it a used appearance.
I bought this for daughter Sam bc she is doing 7 of the Diamond fits kits with her family & she loves it.

My grandson Sebastian aged 4 is having a blast doing this kit. He kept doing his mom’s zebras so I had to buy him & his siblings & father their own kits. The family is currently making 7 of these & they are absolutely loving it.

Theee is a little bit of flaming like crayons & I am a little worried about possible and ax bloom in the future, but so far so good. The colors are lovely.  I just wish they came in a zippered storage case instead of in the tin.

Bought this for my nearly 3 year old granddaughter who Kroc doing her mom’s kit. She loves it. I let her pick out her own design & this was it bc it looks like her puppy.  I have no idea what the age is listed o. The craft box, but my grandkids ages 1-11 are loving these kits they do with their mom & dad. The irony is I bought these AFTER Xmas. Lol.

I love these kits where I have all my supplies on one place. The only issue I’ve had is that the plastic storage case which is the main reason I purchased this kit in the first place keeps coming apart the little pieces that hold the case together keep popping off. Luckily so far I’ve found them.  So I dinged a star bc the case could be better IMO.

I’ve bought about a dozen of these now. My daughter has 5 kids & I bought these for her entire family to keep them busy during cv. She was stunned that her 1-1/2 - 13 year old kids all sit & work on these. I had bought her two, but the kids kept doing hers so I had to buy the kids & her hubby all their own sets.  It’s been about a month & she says they all still love working on their kits. Best $ I ever spent for her to get some peace & quiet.

Idk who packaged these, but I didn’t more time trying to get the blasted caps off than I actually did using the ink. I still have not managed to get 5 bottles opened at all!  They feel glued shut & won’t budge. I’m a 54 year old woman with man hands that generally have no issues opening bottles like this. I bought a generic brand that worked just as well, but opened far easier.  The other issue I had was one bottle of white arrived 85% gone. Literally just a drop of ink in it!  It was obvious that the bottle had previously been used & it had ink residue all over the inside of the lid & the screw on parts.  Colors; for some reason the turquoise is a green color not blue like every other color  turquoise in the world. It’s like a teal which is not turquoise at all!  I suggest dabbing a bit of the color on a label bc they aren’t quite what you expect sometimes. I learned the hard way. Amazon does not take returns on this item either.

There is nothing worse than believing yiutube have everything you need to steer & finish a project before a planned event only to find the materials you ordered arriving defective. It appears that the silicone they used to make the molds was not done drying & blobs of silicone are on several of the molds.  This was supposed to be my first first into resin jewelry.  Not off to a great start.  How did their quality control people not catch such an obvious defect?

I purchased this single skein just to see if I liked the yarn. I tried it knitting & crocheting & it is quite lovely. Kicking myself for not buying more when it was cheaper. It’s out of my budget now, but it’s still lovely. I have a book called One Skein Wonders or something g like that.  I use it to try out new & different yarns before I make any large purchases I could regret later.  You will not regret this yarn. It’s wonderful.

I am a scissors freak. Love me some sharp scissors. I usually only buy the pricey Gingher’s that I still have not unpacked since moving. I needed a small pair quickly and these are great. I have big hands for a girl and didn’t expect these to fit well, but surprisingly they do. Yay!

These were a crumbly mess. All dried out. I liked the box they came in and the few tools they provided. This is a gift u would give ur frenemy. It would drive them batty. I tried to work the clay, but it just would not go together. In desperation I even added water when elbow grease didn’t work
Just didn’t do it. The one small pack that wasn’t completely dried out also stained my hands. So again, give it to ur frenemy or their kids. Lol. Replacement order was exactly the same. Maybe it was a bad batch. Maybe they dried out in transit or being exposed to freezing temps in Colorado during delivery. Idk, but they could not be used.

My favorite crochet hook.  I love the design and the lightness of the wood. I was afraid it would not work for me bc I have large hands and most crochet hooks make my hands cramp. I’m glad I tried it bc it fits perfectly and I don’t feel hand fatigue w/in minutes of starting my projects.  Amazon refused to post my other review, but I put it out on Twitter. Ha!!  Now I need one in every size they make. Sadly, I have been unable to locate a 9mm by Divit. I’m hoping they make them soon. I also hope they will start using a laser to engrave the size of the hook in them. I’ve used mine so much that the paint that shows the size is wearing off.  It’s that good!  I literally use it even when patterns call for different sized hooks since so far this is the only one I own. I intend to purchase more.

I wanted to love these bc my daughter has the exact same kind. I purchased them on her advice.  Sadly, I did not check them over until today when I needed them. Quite a few of them have the metal shank and hook off he 20-30 degrees. They are left or right facing as opposed to straight up in the plastic as they should be.  This might not be an issue for most ppl, but I’ve had surgery on my crocheting hand and it simply does not work for me. Whole tye handkes are very comfortable they also pick up dog hair, yarn fibers, and any dust floating around your environment. As I have forced air heating and live in the prairie part of Colorado this finish does not work for me. That said, my daughter Samantha is a lefty and has no issues at all with her set. I suppose I just got a bad set, but the plastic catching everything that flies won’t work for me even if I did get a replacement set that did not have the hooks all askew in the plastic ergonomic holders. They are pretty too, but not enough for me to overlook the defective parts.
Works well and is a nice durable product. I love the pink color bc it makes them easy to find in my jewelry toolbox.

Clover left side in green. Awesome! Years ago Inox made knitting needles that were Teflon
Coated like this feels although it’s plastic. It’s just really slick which I love. Crazy smooth! Now I need a full set of them. I have large hands and major issues from past hand
Surgery and this feels great.
Useful and sturdy enough.
These are perfect!

I normally buy Lion brand directly from their website.  I’ve lesrned to rewind my yarn to see what issues might crop up while knitting. This one has garbage like this all throughout it. Idk if it’s seconds Amazon bought or I just got a lousy ball.  I purchased two, but given what this looks like I’m not wasting my time rewinding the other. I’ll return these. I have 2 other colors I purchased to check next. Hoping this is just a bad lot, bc I really like the colors. Might try again later. Idk. Pretty bummed right now.

Overall a nice set. The issue I have is that several of the needles have large glue or sealant blind on them that will catch on the yarn. If I had sandpaper at home I might fix them myself, but as I don’t and I’m disabled & home bound I’m hoping they can be replaced. Im also hoping the next set arrives without quality control issues.

Disgusting! Every single tool
Is covered in black grease which I’m now wearing as I didn’t have any gloves handy. Why any company would send product in this condition is beyond me. I know tools need to be oiled regularly, but they should not be sent with black grease on the handles as well. A clear oil on the metal only would be a great idea. God only knows what solvent my hands have been exposed to now.

These are excellent watercolor pencils in my opinion. They are also my first set of watercolors, but I am thoroughly enjoying them.  A couple of the leads broke in transit, but I sharpened them & they are working beautifully.  I am very impressed with the color range too as it is nicely balanced.  I am new to watercolors, but I am finding it a lot of fun!

At first when I started this project, I thought I was crazy doing this. The "diamonds" are the size of a pin head! But once I got into the rhythm of it, it went pretty well. I finished in about 2 hours. I read in some reviews that some people had trouble getting the light to work. I did too until I realized that after I plugged in the cable I could tap the icon on the base to change the color or have it strobe through all the colors. Its working great and was a fast, fun craft to do. Thinking about getting more to make for my grandkids for next Christmas. Would make a cute night light for their room.

I got this for my two grandsons (ages 4 & 7) to use when at my house last weekend. They were a big hit. Lots of colors and easy for them to use. Clean up was a breeze (I covered the table with newspapers and a waterproof undercloth) and there was no mess to be seen once it was all removed. Even their little hands, fingers and faces cleaned up easily afterwards as well. Colors were bright and using brushes they were able to make several works of art! Highly recommend this set either to have in your home for when visitors come or to take as a travel set. Also would make a cute stocking stuffer gift this year during the holidays.

This is a huge set of quality watercolor pencils. I have recently been getting back into art after almost 40 years and I thought these would make a good start. Loads of colors, crystal clarity in colors, professional results and they are great pencils that you can maneuver easily with. These would make a great gift for an artist or art student in your life!

Really can't add much more than it looks realistic and is a lot of fun for a child to play using their imaginary train sets. Love this set, another quality product from Thomas & Friends. Kudos!

First, I want to state clearly that I got this from the Vine program. In my past, when I was in my teens and and twenties (after my sons were born), I did a lot of sewing. From making costumes fom plays I was in myself, to making Halloween costumes for my sons, my old Singer got a lot of practice. Now 20 years later, I haven't used a sewing machine and thought this would be a good refresher for me (preparation for my future grandchildren, lol). Anyway, the positives: The Singer 6199 is much more compact than anything I owned or used in the past. It is sleek, attractive and a piece of home equipment I am proud to desktop display. The stitches are tight and secure and changing the stitch patterns is easy. The downside, was the bobbin loading. I am not sure if it was me (since I am so out of practice) or not. In reading other reviews I see there are others (probably with more recent experience) having the same issues. It took me quite a while the first time to load and level, after painstakingly having to follow instructions. You will need a lot of patience at least the first few times. Anyway. I still recommend this sewing machine as it produces a quality finish and has a lot of features I haven't even tried yet (but will be over the next few months).

This set is wonderful. The hooks are super smooth and comfortable. The handles are not too soft or sticky. The case is well designed and has space for 2 extra hooks. It also includes great scissors, tapestry needles and a small ruler. I like to use these hooks for tunisian crochet, when I am not holding more than 10 stitches on the hook.
These are pointy and hold yarn well, no lost stitches with acrylic or wool.

This is a great interchangeable set. The needles are all smooth and the cables have no memory, so they uncurl perfectly and are easy to work with. I use two cables connected together to knit magic loop (connector is included in set). This set also contains needle place markers, ruler with gauge measurements, tightening pins and screw in end stoppers for small and large tips. I tightened the tips to the cables well, using the pins, and have not had any problem with the tips becoming unscrewed. Because of some of the problems experienced by other reviewers, I checked to be sure that the set contained everything.... all was there. I also checked to be sure the needles were bur free and that everything screwed in properly. I took off a star because one of the #3 tips does not screw into the longest cable. It must be a defect with the end of the needle because all of the other needles screwed into the cable. This isn't a huge problem for me because I connect the two smaller cables together for magic loop anyway.

I purchased 2 different pairs of sewing shears - THESE are my favorites of the two.  These are sharper, the handles are rubber covered - necessary for comfort with cutting a lot.  Comes in a nice box with a little razor cutter (I probably wont use - but ok).  The other set did come with nice plastic holder, but the handles are not coated - and were more uncomfortable to use, not to mention - not as sharp brand new.  Having said all of that,  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

These work just as you expect.  I am actually using them to organize my earring collection to give away - as I cannot wear earrings any more.  These cards (fairly thick - not easily folded) have angle cuts at the top and a couple of poked holes in the cards.  The set includes some cellophane like plastic bags and little rubber tips to help keep your earrings in place.  I am not a seller but I can see that these would be awesome if that was what you were looking for.

Yikes.  This is suppose to be "extra" sticky hold... NOT.  AND it's shiny!  It looks and acts just like old fashioned shiny cellophane tape!  I don't find it to "stick" any better than regular and I prefer the matte of regular scotch tape.  I have 6 rolls of this that I believe I will just toss out.  Live and learn.

I was hoping that the yarn would be the beautiful wine color shown in the photo... but... the color name is "cherry" red. So, I was just hoping.  They need to make the primary photo showing the orange/red it actually is. I am using it to crochet over plastic hangers (helps to keep clothes on the hangers) and so it's not like I'm knitting a sweater or something.  Still, the color matters.  I received the "jumbo" skein.  While the color is called "cherry red", it really it is an orangey red.  Eh.  If they had replaced the photo with the cherry red and not the wine colored skein, I would have rated 5 star even though it's not exactly what I wanted because they would have honestly represented the color.  I rolled the dice - HOPING I would get the color portrayed at the main photo.  Its acrylic yarn good for craft and some knitting or crocheting projects.

I purchased this set (comes together) for the wand really.  I needed an accurate measuring device for rings I own and have purchased.  For instance, I ordered a ring just recently that had a wider band.  I usually wear a 6 1/2-7.  I ordered the 8 because of the width of the band.  The ring bag SAID size 8 - but it was a size 6 - verified by the ring wand sizer.  Love the wand - sad about the ring I found too small - but it was nice to know it wasn't just me!  The wand is fairly light weight - aluminum? and the ring size loops are a base metal of some sort.  I am happy with my purchase and like that I have a backup measurement tool.
Very nice, bright white sheer ribbon.  I've used it and so far the roll is solid - without taped cuts - a bummer for me on another purchase.  It is sheer ribbon without wire - so no pokes.

Hmm.. this ribbon is sheer... but it's more gray than silver.  I really wanted silver... it's a bit darker even being sheer.  NO wire - which is what I wanted.  I don't like wired ribbon.  It's always pokey.

I ordered these along with some other longer picks... these are way better.  The colors... size of the berries and the sparkly decoration stayed on.  They are shorter.. around 7" or so eyeballing them.  I would buy these again, the others no.

These are long... around 12"... I was disappointed that the plastic (clear snow/frost) are in the bottom of the bag with little left on the pick.  Eh.  The berries are small and really fake looking - mostly because they are too small and a very orangy red.  The pine cones are small - but real.  I just think the sizing and colors are a bit off for me - along with the decorations that didn't stay stuck on.
White shear ribbon... very pretty.  NO wire.  I have moved away from wire because it pokes out ... I like this ribbon.  Bright white.  Perfect for any time of year.

A lot of brown paper on a nice roll... just fyi... take out your measuring tape to look at 30"...  It's plenty wide.  I accidentally ordered the 48" too.  Yikes.  I will use it somehow.. but honestly... it's WIDE!  Paper is nice thick quality.

I purchased these for my Mom to use to display some of her smaller art pieces... these work perfectly.  And guess what?? They come FULLY ASSEMBLED.  Sold!  They are not for super heavy items but the smaller pieces she has include glass and they hold beautifully.  Very happy with my purchase indeed.

I purchased these to put some smaller framed pieces on for display.  I am all about "some assembly" BUT... You MUST included directions.  Further, these are very awkward once put together and NOT sturdy.  They are solidly black with brass(ish) bolts.  NO chain is included, even tho it is shown in the photos.  The rabbit ear bolts just look cheap.  Now, on the "pro" side, they are adjustable in that you can set the base bar pretty much anywhere you can get the tripod to stand and tighten the bolt for some security.  But in all honesty, it doesn't hold much - my pieces are framed and have glass so they are on the heavier side.  For me, these are just junk.

I purchased this to go with a faux orchid bouquet I purchased.  It made all the difference in the world - helping the faux orchid bouquet look so much better.  You get several different colors and types of dried moss - so there's something for every bouquet or other decor item.  Love it.

This is beautiful ribbon and a good size spool. The red I chose is very deep and rich. Love it. It is sheer, no wire. I also purchased the green (very dark and beautiful - pine green) and the "old" gold - slightly bronzy... beautiful.

UPDATE: 12/ 7/2020.... I really loved the ribbon... I purchased 2 old gold rolls, 1 green and 1 red roll. I am wrapping Christmas gifts and hit a cut in the green roll... right in the middle of a package I was wrapping. GRRRRR. So NOT cool. This roll should not have been sold, period. I can't imagine how that would ever be "ok". I have used some of each of the other colors, but not as much I have used the green roll. So I am concerned the others will have cuts as well. I added some pics. Just truly disappointing. So I dropped from a 5 star to a 2, tempted to make it 1. If I find more cuts, it will drop to 1.
This is a metallic gold, wired ribbon.  Spooled.  It is a very yellow gold.  Not a whole lot on the spool, but it is nice to keep when you need a quick wrap.  I think gold is a year round color.

I purchased 2 - 25 yards each, total of 150 feet including both rolls.  I chose ivory and this is ivory - but a bit too much yellowish.  It will work.. but beware - VERY yellow ivory.  The ribbon is sheer.. with wired edges.  Will work for my purposes.
I purchased this as a crafty project for my 81 year old mom.  She enjoys felt and succulents, so I thought this would be a win/win.  She loved it.
Purchased with some other craft projects for my adult daughter stuck at home - she reports the color is great fun.. but the slippers are not super comfy. ..  Eh.

Ok.. I'm not an artist.  I purchased these to use on the under side of my fingernails.  They work to a fairly good degree for this purpose. It's not like having a french manicure, but it looks better than nothing to me.

The stickers are rolled up and crinkled on a terrible plastic. The drills (diamonds) are all different sizes and there are no directions or a legend.  So good luck figuring out what goes where.  Eh.  Plus, the stickers are really impossible to reuse.  So work on sticking all the drills and then just throw it all away.  Honestly, pass on this stuff.  I purchased so my daughter could have some crafty time with her sons 6 and 9 as everyone is home for schooling and distancing.  Thought it would be a good "small" project for her to try diamond painting.  But if you did this as your first try at diamond painting you would hate it.

I purchased these in white (diamond), red and green for a pirate treasure chest.  I was SO disappointed to receive them and see the silver painted back.  Total crap.  I threw them all away.  Photos in ad are very misleading. You have to search for one that shows a silver back.  I would never have purchased them if I had seen this.  Expensive garbage decor for my garbage can.  I wanted a true glass "gem" ... not a piece of total crap looking like dime store gem.
I purchased these as part of a pirate treasure chest I was filling.... way too few... way too much money.  However, they do NOT have silver paint on one side... so they have a hint of realism.

I purchased these in white (diamond), red and green for a pirate treasure chest.  I was SO disappointed to receive them and see the silver painted back.  Total crap.  I threw them all away.  Photos in ad are very misleading. You have to search for one that shows a silver back.  I would never have purchased them if I had seen this.  Expensive garbage decor for my garbage can. Metal pirate coins.  Because I was going to use these in a game, I had to count to make sure we had the right number and we had exactly 500 coins.  There are 4 colors - I call them pewter, bronze, gold and silver.  The gold and silver are quite shiny - as you would hope and the bronze and pewter are more dull - but look good.  the coins are roughly shaped as authentic doubloons are.  They are roughly a little less than 1" in diameter - odd shaped. FUN FACT:  In regards to the odd shapes - with real coins they would some times shave off pieces of the coin to pay small debts.  They are pressed/embossed with characters.  Because I am using them in a specific game, I separated all the colors and counted them.  There were exactly 125 of each color.  After all that handling, my hands were clean.  No color/metal rubbed off that I could see.  I'm pretty excited to use them.  We only needed 400 for the game, but the other 100 are going to be buried (our yard is mostly sand) around for my grandsons to practice with their metal detectors with for fun.  These are a bit of an investment- but I think they are probably worth it, even though they are not a bargain.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
These smell MUCH better than the plastic ones I tried first.  Eh.  These are great.  No odor.  Slick - so it's tricky but not impossible.

The bamboo of the knitting needles are smooth and comfortable.  But... the vinyl of the ring is really smelly.  Eh.  Comes with a little set of so so items... marker clasps.. a tape measure.. some plastic wide eyed needles.  I probably will look for something a bit less ... smelly plastic.
Cotton yarn - washable.  The colors are a soft yellow, cream and a taupey gray...  The yarn is soft - The color isn't exactly what I was expecting, but it's ok.  A fairly large skein.

This is a great set if you use a crochet hook every so often.  I just needed them for a quick project - worked great and I loved that I had multiple sizes to choose from - as it turned out - I needed just that.  Came with a tape measure, darning/large eye needle set/tube and a zippered storage case.  Perfect for what I needed.

When they say "1 lb" they aren't kidding.  It's a HUGE skein.  The gray I purchased is a lighter, heather gray look.  I use it to crochet onto hangers - so it works perfectly for me.  Good basic yarn and a lot of it.
Purchased to thread elastic for masks - works really well.  I think these are meant for darning or other purpose - but they worked well to thread elastic through mask edges.

I purchased these to help build masks for donation.  The elastic is perfect in width and strength and came with nose wire strips - which I wasn't expecting.  We are experimenting with making a slot and sewing down the ends of each side of the nose strip to keep it in place and safe for washing.... It might work.  They seem to have a "self stick" tape on one side (nose strips) but I don't think they'll last through washing... so .. we haven't tried that yet.  I would purchase this again - but wish the shipping was USA based and a LOT faster!

I needed some fairly disposable paint brushes - that were small - these fit the bill.  They are NOT a high quality - but the price point will tell you that.  The work for my purposes though - thus the 5 star rating.

I purchased a couple of cheapy sewing kits and I unloaded them into this tub.  Works great for a sewing kit.  It's not a super high quality but I don't use it that often.  The lid is a little wonky as is the latch... but like I said - works.
This is a NOT a quality tool set, but it will definitely get an emergency repair done.  The wooden box is ok.. but its a bit awkward.  I bought a plastic tub with a handle to replace it.
This is NOT a quality tool set... but it has needles and thread for an emergency fix.  The kit zips closed.  And could travel - but because of the scissors you'll need to check it.

I just needed a few needles and some thread.  This works.  But the items are cheap - but so is the price - so I didn't expect quality items or thread.  You usually get what you pay for.  So if you're looking a quality set - this isn't it.  It's an emergency use kit in my opinion.

I accidentally ordered a HUGE diamond painting project... even bigger than this light board... but I needed the biggest board I could get and this is apparently it.  I REALLY like the plug in.  All the other light boards I own use a USB mini or micro point.  Eh.  They fall out... the won't stay in.  This one has a great plug in.  there is a cord that falls away from the board about 3 1/2 -4".  THEN it plugs into the power cord tip (round) and then into the power strip.  It STAYS plugged in!  That is SUCH a high point.  You touch on, touch off - hold for dimming or brightening.  You can set to the perfect brightness. There is a small blue light that stays on while the board is plugged in.  The edges are black - about an inch all around.  I have to do this huge project in sections and this board is going to help me do a very big job.  I also notice that it doesn't get as warm as the others I have used.  This light board is now my favorite board.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review is helpful.

I just started the biggest diamond painting project I have ever done - approx 24"x 36" ... it's HUGE.  And I was getting a bit overwhelmed.  But then I found these release papers.  So I removed the main release paper and then applied these 4x6" pieces.  I work on just the area under one of these pieces so it is much less overwhelming.  AND they are totally reusable!  I am loving these.  IF I EVER do a another HUGE project, I won't do it without these.  They stay in place until you move them - they are reusable - and control the area you're working on perfectly.  Very happy.
If you diamond paint, you'll need most of what is in this set - especially if you do multiple kits at one time  - like I do.
If you diamond paint, you need most of the things in this set and needed a few spares.
Works... but I prefer the ones with handles... just a personal preference.

I've tried a couple different types of these, I like these probably the best... because there are two different lengths.  So you can use as you go along (the shorter one) until you get a bigger part done then you can use the longer one.  Having handles is useful too.  I have found they definitely straighten drill lines that can, especially on larger projects, start to go astray.  Even if you do the best you can to keep them straight, the drills are never perfectly the same size, nor are the prints of the design always perfect.  These tools can really help if you're particularly sensitive to wavy lines.

My husband is a HUGE fighter plane fan and my 8 year old grandson was intrigued so my husband purchased a series of these heavy metal planes with stands.  8 year is thrilled and they have spent hours discussing each of them in great detail.  One thing I will say is the plastic stands are kind of crap.  Husband made some that work.

My husband is a HUGE fighter plane fan and my 8 year old grandson was intrigued so my husband purchased a series of these heavy metal planes with stands.  8 year is thrilled and they have spent hours discussing each of them in great detail.  One thing I will say is the plastic stands are kind of crap.  Husband made some that work.

I purchased 3 of these spools... one white, one green, one red.  All are very nice.  I with the spool hole was a bit bigger because I have ribbon dispenser I like to use, but it's ok, I'll just get a smaller dowel to use on the dispenser.  The ribbon is sheer and very pretty.  I will be using it for a long time.  I actually purchased it to wrap around some Christmas candy bags - I do several hundred at a time, but even doing that, I will have these ribbon spools for a while there is so much ribbon.

UPDATE BELOW...I have been buying and making diamond painting craft projects for about 1 1/2 years. It's relaxing and satisfying as craft projects go, in my personal opinion. This particular kit is nice for a couple of reasons.
1. The drills (the plastic diamond pieces) are exceptionally gem like. All the colors are sparkly and bright. I've found some kits are dull and therefore look like less quality to me. I prefer the more gem like kits as a personal choice.
2. There VERY few drills that are stuck together. With some kits I've found that most or all the drills are stuck together, which leads to frustration and takes additional time to prep to do the project. Further, sometimes the drills have been very poorly made and random in sizing. In this kit I've found the drills to be fairly uniform with only a few that were unusable due to flaws and sizing.
In order to be successful in diamond painting I do recommend a good light board. Also, remember that the smaller the print, the less detail and more 'pixalation' there is. This kit is about the smallest I choose because I find the detail to be clear. You can purchase kits with round drills or square drills. I like either - I think I prefer square drills for larger projects - but it is tricky to line them up properly - so I believe round drills are better for beginners. Also be aware that some projects are not 'full drill' ... that means that only part of the picture is diamond painted. They are faster to complete, but, in my opinion a bit less dimensional.
All in all I believe this kit to be one of my better purchases. I have just begun the project, so if there are issues (such as not enough drills - which happens sometimes and is very frustrating *a further note: save all left over drills from other projects - on occasion you need a couple extras for subsequent projects) I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. I hope this review was helpful to you!
UPDATE 11/14/2019: an update already!! I am short of drills! Grrrrr! Contacted seller right away. Was promised another set to rob of drills needed. They say it will be sent 3-5 days. Honestly about 2 out 20 project are short of drills so this doesnt happen very often. Even tho - its frustrating. I should probably drop to 3 stars but I still really appreciated the quality of the drills and project overall. It would be nice if you could just buy more of the drill color you are short - but I have not found an outlet for that. I always save spare drills from other projects however none would work in this case. I'm literally short about 25 drills. Another thing you'll notice if you do enough projects is that while the 'universal' color code may say #3370 - every manufacturer will have a slightly different shade of that #. I've found this when 'filing' my spare drills. I will update further if it seems useful.

The usual 1" key ring - split ring style.. work as they should.  Always seem to need one for something so it's good to have some spares on hand.  Arrived quick (I have prime) and in great condition.  They are silver color metal.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review was helpful to you.

Husband wears industrial bibs to work in and every once in a while we need to replace these... the problem is these are REALLY light duty and actually very small - barely 1 1/2".  But the real issue is that they are so lightweight.  I purchased in three different metals too.... silver, gunmetal and bronze.  All are lightweight.

Husband wears industrial bibs to work in and every once in a while we need to replace these... the problem is these are REALLY light duty and actually very small - barely 1 1/2".  But the real issue is that they are so lightweight.  I purchased in three different metals too.... silver, gunmetal and bronze.  All are lightweight.

I have to say that these stencils would be most effectively used on a flat surface, using a dobbing or patting brush on a wall or other.  I actually purchased these to stencil my blah, common suitcases.  I use a variety of pens - acrylic paint pens, permanent markers...  and these particular stencils were a bit difficult to use in this way.  It was not impossible and ended up making it work.  The stars are retro like designs in my opinion and I like them.  The stripe stencil is not one I will probably ever use... but I can see how you could use these together in some room or surface as a pattern of come sort.  The stencils  are pretty large... as I said meant to be used on a wall or other large flat surface - large area coverage.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

At first I thought this board was going to be perfect for my small diamond painting projects, however, after about 2 uses - marginally, it is really frustrating.  It won't tap off, I have to unplug it.  It's supposed to brighten and dim, which it sort of did for about 2 uses. Now it will not adjust.  It's just garbage.  I would recommend a different brand.  I own 3 light boards and this one is by far the worst.  I am sad to report just a negative review, but I must honestly report my experience - which in this case is clearly very bad.  I am tossing it with a note to self, skip the JHF art (really unfortunate blending of branding)  brand.

I have tried several different things in exchange for the "red wax" for diamond painting... and I keep coming back to it.  Wish they would make in about 1 1/2 x 1 1/2 squares instead of these tiny squares.  But if you are doing a tiny project the small squares are fine.  Most of my projects a huge.

I had purchased a plastic roller... BIG mistake for many reasons - the most being it smelled so bad you can't keep it in the house let alone in your diamond painting supplies bag.....  The wooden ones, like this one are the only way to go.  They are stable - odor free - and effective.  You can hear it when you're rolling over the top of your diamond painting.  It's seating the drills effectively.  The wider one works well on larger projects, the smaller end for the smaller projects.  I would recommend this one if you diamond paint - it is reasonably priced - it was not cracked - the handle is easy to grip and it works as it should.  Having said all that - I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I purchased a wooden roller about a year ago, but it had a crack and broke.. so I thought I'd try one of these.  O M G.  DO NOT purchase this one. I am not one that is terribly sensitive to odors but this roller SMELLS HORRIBLE.  It knocked me over taking it out of the paper envelope it had been mailed in! It is a HORRIBLE musty, bitter, slightly petroleum odor - that just doesn't go away.  It comes in a lovely zippered plastic bag... why?  Because the smell is so bag when it's out of the bag you can't be in the same room with it.  To be honest, I walked it outside - placed in the bright, full sun for hours... the smell wasn't even slightly diminished - so it then got dumped right into the OUTSIDE trash bin.  I am going back to the wooden roller and hope it's not cracked when it arrives.

I usually have several projects going at one time.  These boxes are a life saver to me as I can load them - I number the boxes with the color legend and makes it so easy!  They are easy to fill and use with your trays. They stay closed - so far anyway and I've used them over and over. Having said that.. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. At this point, I would purchase these again if I needed more.

There are 16 rolls of ribbon.. approximately all the same amount (I figured around 24 or so yards each color). They are rolled onto thin, styrofoam like spools with pencil sized holes through them so it you have a pencil sized rod to thread them onto, could unroll the ribbon like a pro. The ribbon itself is pretty thin... it's not super quality.. BUT the price is fair in my opinion. I use the ribbon to wrap around candy bars I bring onto the plane for the crew when I travel. So... it doesn't have to be super sturdy. The colors are bright and cheery. The first one I un-taped at the edge, unraveled.. so carefully remove the tape stoppers or you won't have any ribbon! :) All in all I am happy with the purchase - having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this info was helpful to you.
UPDATE 9/2020 - my second purchase....NOTE: The ribbon is still rolled onto a Styrofoam core that has a hole in the center - about the circumference of a pencil ... It is really important - for ease of use- that you keep it on the Styrofoam core. If you accidentally have the ribbon fall off the core - it's a mess that you will end up rewinding. I'm thinking about getting a then dowel to thread the rolls onto. My husband is handy. Perhaps he could make me a wood frame to set the dowel on to roll it off of. Seems like a lot - but I like less frustration!

It is NOT a full drill... only frog.  Plus I ran out of 3 colors.  Very cartoony - which you can see, in hindsight, from the photo on the sale page - Disappointing to run out of the drill before the finish - fortunately I had some from other project that I could blend in.  I also noticed that the drills where very different sizes from color to color.  This makes it very difficult to finish the project neatly.  I would not purchase this one again.  :(

I purchased a really pretty pink luggage set and the first time I used them, they came back a real mess.  Haha!  That's what I get for purchasing a light color.  The luggage was a real investment - so I decided that I would get some stencils and try to help the corners (the worst of the dirt and grime). I used a permanent ink pen - black worked great because the accents of the luggage (handle, zipper, wheels) are all black..  Was a bit of a trick to roll the stencil over the corner, so I ended up free handing a bit.  And to be honest, I used a combination of 3 different paisley stencils - but 90% of it using this particular pattern.  I love the look.  The stencil was easy to use. Was a really good size for my use - smaller would have been really hard, using a pen.  Paint might have been easier - but the bag had a very tiny texture to it... I think the pen was the best option.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this info was helpful to you.

This a full drill (meaning the whole painting is covered by drills) and the drills are ROUND.  It was a very fun craft to do... but disappointing as I was short a few colors... I had extra drills from other projects to fill in.  I have not decided if I will frame this one... but it is cute.  Just wish they would have included enough of each color... AND that the drills would have been properly separated.  So many were stuck together.  Eh.  Unless you have a lot of spare colors and just "love" cats... I would pass on this one... Just my opinion.  I hope my review has been helpful to you in some way.

Ok... so I am using this to store my completed "diamond painting" projects... it gives me a safe place to store them as I decide which ones to frame and in what order.  I've found it is a process to get them framed... so it's nice to have a really great place to see them... keep them... and have them flat.  It's large enough to keep my largest projects... and I can keep numerous small ones in one sleeve.  I use tension clips to keep them in place. The black background pages are nice.  Now...  I read a few other reviews before I purchased this...  A few of the clear pages have some small scratches on a few corners.  That is ok for my purposes... but... just in case it might matter to you... I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  And I hope this info was helpful to you in some way!

I got these to apply to a cardboard visor I had colored.  *eh*  When you try to remove the rhinestones... the "self" stick part peels off the rhinestone - staying on the plastic board. So the rhinestones that don't have a self stick property are garbage or need a separate glue.  There is one plastic strip has a line of colors with graduating sizes.  The other strip has a bunch of rhinestones, the same size, but a random mix of colors.  Overall, I am not overly impressed.  I am eager to see if the stones will actually stay on the piece I have placed them... I am thinking the stones will separate from the self stick and leave a nice sticky spot, but no stone.  I gave them 2 stars because the colors were bright and the rhinestones were fairly responsive - not overly sparkly... but some life. But sadly, about 1/3 of them were useless for me as the self sticky part separated.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I definitely will on these... time will tell.

recently dived into diamond painting .... and so ... I use this to store all my projects... accessories ... spare drills, sticks, light boards... etc.  Works wonderfully.  It comes with 2 zippered pouches that are removable. And there are several zippered pouches on the outside.  I needed this size because one of my light boards is very long.  it has back rollers... front feet.  The handle is retractable.  and there are two substantial over handles if you want to lift and not roll.  There are also size (end) handles.  It is a bright, light green... "chartreuse" comes to mind....It is just a very handy craft trolley... even though it was meant to carry a sewing machine.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  And I hope that my review was helpful to you in some way.

purchased for my my mom to customize her hot rod purple walker... She loves orchids.  This has orchids and palm fronds.  The orchids are predominantly pinky with a little purple hue (?)... just my personal opinion on the color.  Forget to take a picture of it before I turned it over to Mom. I think it was well represented in the ad photo. Eh.  Fancy duct tape.  It's just fun.  Not rocket science.

Well.  These are awful.  They are HORRIBLE to pick off the paper... the paper comes too. OR they come off and stick to your hand and then they won't stick on the box because they have picked up your skin oils/cells. ... OR the corner folds as you are trying to pick them off.  *MEH*  They are clear background so you cannot see them very well on my clear box storage - as all the storage boxes are.  The numbers are very small, in order to fit on most storage tray boxes.  I am just going to use a small white tab and a sharpie pen.  These were a waste of time and money.

If you do "diamond painting" you need a light board.  Do you need one this size?  Well.. probably not.  But I love seeing the biggest part of my project possible - so I went for it.  There are smaller,  more reasonably priced light boards.  But I was new and didn't realize that a smaller one would work - still I do not regret my decision. HELPFUL HINT:  I do recommend that you purchase a power bank and braided cord.  The usb power cord that comes with the light board is pretty cheap.  Plus, using the power bank makes your work area easier than trying to hook up to your computer or even a power plug with USB port.  The board uses so little power than your decent battery bank could run for days and days. I am very pleased with my purchase so far.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  I hope my review was helpful to you in some way!

Got these to clean my vapo... and they are VERY scratchy.  They do fit in the tube area I want to clean - they are a tad more bristly than I had planned.  I probably will go back to regular pipe cleaners when I finish these.  If you're using them for decorating.. they are a light cream color with a gold bristle - just note: The gold bristle may snag things depending what you decorating. They are about 6" long.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

purchased for decor on my prelit faux christmas tree.  They are perfect.  The "sticks" are easy to wrap onto the branches, so I leave them on permanently or until I don't have the xmas christmas for whatever reason.  I will update my review should new info seem useful.

Used as a little cheek sparkle at christmas for a neighborhood christmas parade.  It was perfect.  A little is subtle... the more you put on the more you sparkle.  Be careful when you open because if you blow on it --- it will fly. Washed off fairly easily - note* I didn't apply a heavy coat

The perfect accent on my "natural" decorated christmas tree.  I will say that the stems are a bit stiff, so rather than twisting them around branches, I just "set and poked" them into the tree, which is working well.  They stay in place. I really love the look.  The small amount of light colored leaves and  twigs blends in with the tree nicely.  Perfect accents.

Bought this because as a beginner I thought I could figure this out.... NEGATIVE.  Followed every instruction - which are complicated - and never could get the bobbin to load. I do have a small amount of experience... frustration finally won out after 6 and 1/2 hours over 2 days and I finally just packed it up, put in a grocery bag and will ask around to see if anyone wants it.  GRRRRR.  Also.... the "bag" they show that it comes with  is a PROJECT - a piece of paper that shows you how you COULD make the bag IF you get the machine to work.  I even tried a YouTube video.  Over it.  Good luck to anyone who tries to do this.

Nice, sharp scissors.  I am hoping that because I bought this set of four, eventually I have a pair WHERE and WHEN I need them.  They seem to "walk" away from the utility drawer, if you know what I mean.  They seem very sharp.  Right handed.  I say this because I actually use both hands on occasion.  They are not really useful as lefty scissors.

Used as "santa" trim on a costume I was making.  Washed and dried before using.... did beautifully.  It is very, very soft and looks rich - not cheapy like some of the other straight poly blend faux furs... I think cause it's so soft and NOT "shiny" like some of those fauxs can be.  It IS fuzzy... so where it's cut, there is a lot of excess fuzz... but it eventually calms down. I bought WAY more than I needed... so I will have to figure out what to do with the rest of it.  Gladly.

It's a very gray "white" - NOT bright white as I expected, but further than that it has the strongest, oddest odor of moth balls?? I can't say too much good about this thread. To give a better idea.. the thread was wrapped in a small, very thin plastic bag, knotted at the top and then in a bubble envelope. The envelope it arrived in was just permeated ( in fact, I smelled it before I even opened the bubble envelope) in what I can only describe as a very strong moth ball odor all coming from the thread!
I just tossed it. Could have been a fluke... and to be fair, I ordered and received one spool.
I missed the part that said these were cloth... but it is.  If you're ok with a cloth retractable tape, you'll probably love this.  I personally was hoping for a metal one.

Husband uses to look at industrial plans in dark areas. It is large, but has a built in light. It works well and he is happy with the purchase.  It is exactly as shown in photos and the measurements are accurate.

Husband uses in reading industrial plans. He likes that it folds into a cover and he can carry in his pocket when using on the job and then in a carrying case he uses for storage.  It is exactly as pictured.

For the price, this works as I expected.  It's not terribly sharp or fine quality... so you have to really work it.  The cute little screw driver they included did not fit (it was too small) to loosen the punch wheel but did work on with the punch plate adjustment... Odd.  But it will partly punch through fairly thin, flat, non-textured leather.  I haven't tried it on anything else.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
I needed a little trim on a navy and white dress and this was perfect.  The lace was nicely produced.  I think it will last a long time.

Listen, these are what they are... they don't last for ever, but are useful for a time.  The nature of what they are makes them sort of disposable.  These at least last longer than the aluminum coin type which may last one or two times if you're lucky. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Purchased this to put on a charity gift raffle basket with a chocolate theme.  It is a gorgeous ribbon and perfect for my use. The measurements given are accurate. I purchased these with some snowflake stickers to use as xmas tags and they were lovely. I love to use these tags on gifts no only at xmas and there are enough to last for some time. I use these sharpies with stencils, or free hand to decorate my plain black suitcases making unique works of art.  Ok.. maybe works of art in MY mind.  But these are what I use. I LOVE LOVE LOVE these.  I use them on my plain black suit cases and either free hand or use stencils.  The are just as they say.. "an oil based" marker. Stays on well.  They do dry out eventually but I've used a couple that I have for a couple of years.

I needed a small, but effective needle and thread kit once in a while... listen, you get what you pay for... and this is cheap.  But it works in a pinch and that's why I bought it. Not quality tools... but like a said, in a pinch it all works.

I purchased these to go on some plain, brown tags for Christmas - they came off the sheet nicely ( one sheet) and are various sizes. I thought they looked perfect. They stayed stuck on to the tags and looked very high end.  I would purchase again.

If you need this to be a magnet in ANY way, you'll be OUT of luck. It had NO magnetic property at all. It went directly into the trash. If i could rate zero stars i would. I rarely write one star reviews but this was really frustrating and disappointing.

I use these stencils to decorate my plain black suitcases... using a paint pen. This one is cool. Very 60s/70s to me. The stencil is about 5x7 or so. With several stencils on one page. The stencil itself is a stiff (but not too stiff) plastic. It bends easily, but doesn't break. Would be useful in several different stencil applications. I never hesitate to update my reviews if new info would be useful.
UPDATE: 12/18/2016.... Ok.. I have now used these. They worked perfectly. Also, you should know, that each portion of this stencil set are separate... they are slightly sticky on the back side. So if you're using them on a wall or other flat surface, this is useful to keep the stencil in place. It wasn't really useful for my project (stenciling a plain black suitcase) but it didn't hinder it either. I would purchase this style again.

It is hard to buy yarn "online"... but I am using on hangers so I was ok with the risk.  This yarn is very soft... but not terribly fuzzy - which for my purposes is perfect.  Wrapping on hangers means I am not "washing" it... so I can't really rate that.  It is a golden rod color, well represented on my screen - hard to review as every computer and/or device will show a little differently.  I always update my reviews if any new info will be useful.  The yarn arrived quickly (I have prime) along with other items in excellent condition.

Purchased this yarn to crochet on clothes hangers.... it is a VERY small skein.. 4 oz is a the SMALL skein. The colors are well represented in pics which is nice. Just be sure to read your pattern so you purchase enough to complete. I estimate I will be able to get 6-8 large hangers with this skein...We'll see.

I am always trying to find my scissors... You get three pairs. They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition. They were inside of a separate, secure cardboard box. They are brand new and very sharp. I buy the Sharpie oil based markers to decorate my plain, black suitcases. I use fine, medium and wide points. These happen to be medium point which is fairly wide... but good for my purpose. I bought this stencil to personalize my plain, black suitcase.  I used a silver and white oil based Sharpies to detail.  The stencil is big.  The page is about 12"x 12"... I bought this to use when I have a splinter or need to look at something very close.  It's rather small, so it's easy to put away when not in use. The pinch piece is fairly sturdy and easily pinched the little table I used it with.  I am going to say that it pinch a table up to about an inch (?) thick comfortably.  There is a battery operated light.  Also handy when trying to see something like a splinter! It arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition. I purchased this for our parent center at school.  The paren volunteers wanted to create some crafty projects for Valentine’s Day and they raves about this kit.

I liked the fact that this used plexiglass instead of real glass.  With a large frame like this, real glass would have made the frame quite heavy.  It is light enough to hang easily.  The frame is nice and large and will accommodate most pictures.  It was actually a little larger than what I needed, but the picture still looks good, with just a little extra border.

Very fast and easy assembly.  It does need some painting to look realistic, since it is all molded in a single color (white) or actually two if you count the clear window glass material.  I used red paint for window frames and 4 of the gas pumps, white for two of the other pumps, silver for rain downspouts and vent on the roof and aged concrete for the pump islands.  Instruction sheet is easy to follow with parts numbered.  I have not quite finished building yet, as one thing is holding me up.  In the pictures on the box, it shows several thin black lines on the trim and I don't know how to get those put on without it oozing over into the white.  Even a very thin brush might not work; maybe a toothpick?  The kit comes with decals and I AWAYS have trouble with decals and usually end up destroying some of them.  What I do as a precaution is to make a couple color photocopies of the decals and then I can always cut out the pieces and glue them on in lieu of the decals. If you really want to do your decals well, you also need to buy a bottle of Micro Sol, which softens the decal and makes them adhere better. (I have it, use it and it works well.)  The kit comes with 3 different sets of 6 gas pumps, so you can use the pumps that fit the era you are modeling.  Unfortunately there are only two pump islands, so you can't swap pumps if you want to change the era, even though you have the pumps for the 3 eras.  (Too bad the kit didn't include three sets of pump islands.)  The only other thing that defines the era is the standing sign; two signs included; one to accommodate Standard oil and the other to accommodate either "Al's Victory Service" or "Jerry's Gas and Garage" on the sign.  The only thing that I really did not like about this kit is that the gas pumps seem to lack detail.  They don't look like they have any dispenser hoses on them; that can make a big difference in realism.  The decals are a bit "congested" on the decal sheet, so be careful.  Also be careful with the plastic sprues that the parts are attached to.  There are a few very tiny parts that can come loose; I almost lost the air pump when it fell off.  This is a decent gas station kit and probably a good kit for a beginner, but I do think there are better looking gas station kits out there.  Generally the Walthers kits are nice, but need a lot of painting to look realistic.  This kit needs less painting than some other kits.  Note: there are quite a few small pieces, so you need patience.  Also this kit and all the garage and gas station kits I have bought and assembled do not come with a base or concrete driveways, so you have to create your own; I used heavy card stock and gray spray paint.  The concrete joints can be made with a lead pencil and a ruler and look quite realistic.

Easy to assemble and easy to use.  Quiet and works well.  You don't need a computer to use it; you can set it up and use it right from the display panel.  There are a limited number of patterns available, so it would be nice if there were more included; however, there are enough for a beginner and you can always get more or presumably scan your own with the machine.  Haven't used the scanning portion yet.  Brother makes good equipment, so I anticipate that this will work for a long time without any trouble.  A great cutting machine for someone who likes crafts and has the need to do a lot of cutting.  Very pleased with it.

This is one of the most interesting and fun models from Woodland Scenics.  Very detailed and nicely painted with a somewhat weathered look.  I usually buy kits, which I enjoy assembling and painting, but the cost of the assembled version was only slightly more expensive and saved me a lot of time on assembly.  I don't think this is worth the over $80 that some people charge for this, but certainly worth $50-$60.  Includes a standing figure too, which you don't often see with built-up models.  I can't imagine anyone not liking this model, even if you don't have a model railroad layout on which to use it.

Easy to assemble and it's one of the cuter buildings that is available for model railroads. Looks very authentic and the interior detail gives it a realistic touch. This is one of my favorite buildings of all the different ones I have.
I don't think you will be disappointed with this at all. It's a great little kit with just enough assembly to make it fun, but not so much as to be overwhelmed.
Note that this is one of the few kits that doesn't require any painting to look really good. The only thing I added to mine was some parking stripes in the 3-car
parking spot in front.

The Windmill was a bit of a challenge to assemble, but the main problem is that not many of the parts were numbered.  Many of the parts were just loose, rather than hooked to a plastic strip with a number next to them, as some were.  Also the picture on the box was actually reversed from what the model turned out to be.  I think they printed it backwards. (This one might be the correct way.) The result doesn't look quite as good as the picture.  It would require some painting.  In spite of the shortcomings, I loved the result. Even without anything painted, there are enough colors to make this look very nice.  This is one of the nicest scale model buildings you are going to come across.  The added extras of the lady hanging up the wash and two cows included really makes it come to life.  They put a lot of detail into this model and it really shows.  Of the dozen or so scale model buildings that I have, I like this the best.  If you think you might want it, get it now, as I was told that some of the Life-Like Trains buildings are disappearing and may never return to market. They are the quite economically priced, compared to other scale model buildings, which can get very expensive.  They also have a very realistic look.  You won't be disappointed.  Just be sure you have patience to assemble the kit.  A younger child might find it too difficult.  There are a lot of tiny parts to glue together.  Be careful not to lose any!  I lost one small one for a while, but finally located it.  Requires modeling cement (not included) and some pieces of thread to make the clothes lines (also not included).  A really fun model.

I got this set and was excited to use them. The pens worked at first. Only at first. Each pen has run dry with minimal use. By minimal, I mean a few written lines at most. I've used the replacement cartridges for several immediately, but they are made with the same crappy manufacturing.

It seems like a steal with so many colors, but it's not. They are taking your money and the pens are literally empty except for a thin layer of dried ink coating the wall of the pen shaft. You can look inside each one and see they are coated inside to look full but are empty.

The package was nicely divided and orderly, and the array of colors/textures (flavors) is very delightful, says my cat. However, as I was affixing clasps to the feather spine shafts to make toys for my cat, they keep breaking with very little pressure applied by myself.

While I realize these are craft feathers, the stems should not be so fragile and hollow that they have very little support to last for whatever project they are being used for (you can crush them between two fingers very easily). That's the ONLY reason why I subtracted stars.

I'm a beginner and just bought this to add buttons and make basic repairs to clothes. I found it very easy to use and it has everything you need. Seam ripper, nice large collection of needles (my previous ones were all broken which is why I bought this), and a good selection of thread colors.

My main nit pick is that there's no navy. Navy seems to be the color I've needed the most. The kit comes with a medium-light blue that would stand out against navy. Black works ok instead.

Also I would like more needle threaders (only contains two). Those seem to be what breaks first and I end up having to order more.

Overall great value for money and would buy again. If you're looking for some very specific colors, you might want a different set with more colors.
