Would have rated a 1 Star, but the color is nice and vibrant even after melting, but the product didn’t work as others have.
I’m not new to using a sealing was stamp to add to my letters. This is not the right material that is used. It’s a glue stick with color added to it. The color is vibrant, and looks nice, but it’s not that easy to work with. Candle wax melts rather quickly making it easier to work with. This is a glue stick with color. It is NOT candle wax, or at least has a very small amount of actual wax in the product. You typically use a glue gun to be able to melt a glue stick, and you get a lot of long stringy fly aways. That’s exactly what this product does. I used a candle melting pot thing I use for my scented candle sprinkles. It takes a very very long time to melt. Candles do not take that long.
Then as I used the scoop to get some in the spoon to pour onto the back of the envelope, I would get these long strings forming just like a glue stick. It takes a lot time to get the product to cool enough to be able to use the stamp to make the image. If you don’t wait long enough, the glue is still too hot and the image won’t stay.
I tried experimenting with a real candle and it was much easier to work with. If you only have 1-3 letters to stamp then this may work for you. I wanted to stamp around 50 Christmas cards and quit after a few of them. Takes roughly 3-4 times longer than actual wax.

I was excited to use these from the reviews but mostly because I have only used watercolour markers on rare occasions and thought this would be a great idea to work with them again. I really like the watercolour effect and was super looking forward to it this time. I was a little let down because although they do kind of look like watercolours they don't actually painttt like watercolours I know I know it says to use water on them or they include actually they include a small brush so you can use them as watercolours but they don't quite work that way imo, it's almost as if you were using normal good quality markers and then spraying water on them they are a little more loose than usual markers but they don't really move well the colour stays on the same place and it's hard to get the watercolour effect. Other than that I did notice some colours were kind of dry, some others weren't, some were extremely wet and looked like watercolours from the start (just using them) others needed some water to move a little or give that effect, whilst others were super dry and I had to really struggle to get the colour out, it even left me wondering if they had been used before because it was so difficult to use them? I felt like they were already drying up??? I do not feel like these will last for a long time but we'll see. The overall effect is not bad and they do look pretty as well...markers but not watercolours like I said. Or at least barely if you don't colour a lot or don't really care then you probably won't even notice, but in my case I did notice. I actually bought some normal markers as well just to compare and although not the same they are very close as well. Just in general though I won't be giving this 3 stars because they are pretty eitherway and not too expensive which is what I want, but I will definitely keep looking for a better quality watercolour effect of these. Or might as well just buy actual watercolours myself. I would probably not gift these to someone who is truly interested in this but they could be alright as a birthday gift or just random since they are cute eitherway.

I did not order this that I remember I only ordered a red moon that I do have in my orders as well so I am confused why this is showing here, I also never got this item.

I expected them to be better. First impression is great right...but, that's as far as it goes. I planned on using this for an adult coloring book. At first, I was super impressed and figured these were the correct choice of markers to combo with my coloring pencils.

However, after the first use, they were so much drier, and by the third use, they're struggling hard. I'll be lucky to be able to finish off 1 picture before they completely dry up. I thought that I could make these last longer by pairing the coloring with color pencils.

I guess you get what you paid for. I would not recommend these or plan to purchase them again.

Whether you are starting a crafting lifestyle or want the occasional hassle free craft session, this is a great thing to start out with. Not any one part was like AMAZING BEYOND BELIEF but a lot of it together is convenient and of good material, so overall wonderful product.

I gave ultimately 3 stars because I didn't realize the colors were stamped onto the material. What's worse is that it's a very vivid stamp and the colors don't match the colors you will actually stitch. So it's hard to cover up the stamp after stitching. I ended up flipping the material over and stitching on the back side

This is my first ever drill pic. So first off, its full drill as in the whole "painting" is of drill. There are others where only main object is drill and background is printed on. The drill is round and therefore does not cover every single part of the canvas. The problem with this is that some of the grid squares do not match the color of the bead. One of them has a blue ish gray background but the bead looks more brown gray so you can clearly see the background is different than the bead.

My other problem was that there were large areas of similar numbers right next to each other. "S" right next to "2" or "x" around "K" and the colors would be similar. So that was annoying

All in all its a wonderful piece of decoration. There are PLENTY of beads and it is a nice size. I will be framing it

This is a beautiful holiday memory book. It definitely has the whole Kate Spade vibe. As far as actual content, it's a bit weak. There are not a lot of space for writing, though you could easily write on the pages that don't have writing prompts or dedicated writing space. The quality is good. This would make a nice gift.

These stickers are very cute. There are lots of different cat types/breeds represented in this assortment. The stickers are on clear backgrounds, so depending on what you are sticking these too, it may show through to what's underneath. I have used a few  of these so far, and I have had difficulties peeling the backing off. The quality of the stickers is good.

This is a nice kit for those into vision boards. I ordered this for my 21 year old daughter who likes making vision boards. She is the president of a small club at her college and plans to have a vision board activity for the members. What I like about the kit is that the pictures/cards are already cut so you don't have to cut anything out. The cards are 2 sided with pictures on one side and words or phrases on the other. There are even a few blank ones so you can create your own cards. The kit has a guide book to help you sort through and plan your vision board. This kit also comes with the board. I was a little confused with how the board works, but then I figured it out. The cards are inserted into the slots from the front of each window. The top of each slot has notches in it to allow you to slide the cards into it. Then the cards do not fully fit into the windows. If you look at the product pictures, you can see what I'm talking about. That makes it easy to remove the cards if you need to make changes later on to your vision. Things can change and so can your goals! I did like that there was a little bit of diversity in the cards, but it could be better. Overall, I think it is a good kit and is a great starting point.

I saw this kit and wanted to try making my own mosaics. You get supplies to make 2 coasters. I found the directions to be pretty poor. Some very crucial instructions were left out, such as how much water to use. I ended up using too much of the adhesive and making a mess! However, the end products turned out okay. I'm not sure that they will work well as coasters since they're not even across the surface, but they do look nice.

I have been having fun painting rocks recently. These markers work well for that. The lighter colors don't show up well unless you have painted the rocks before hand with a light base color. That's pretty much the same with other paint markers that I have tried. The fine tip is easy to use for details. I am very pleased with these.
These metallic markers work well. They do need to be primed at first and every once in a while, but the paint/ink is as expected. I am pleased with it.

This paint by numbers set is great for beginners. Of course, more experienced artists will enjoy it, too. It's a good quality set and it includes everything you need to get started. This would make a great gift!

My daughter is president of the Her Campus club at her university. She has started planning for the fall. She wants to have a vision board activity for the young women. This book is filled with a nice assortment of pictures that will be perfect for the vision boards. They still have to be cut out, just like magazines. I was specifically looking at the diversity shown in the book. It's not bad, but it could be better. Representation is important! The majority of the people in it are white. There are some Asian, Black, and others, but not as many as there are white. It looks like the family section was lacking same sex families which I think is important to include. While it may not be perfect, I do think that it was a good attempt and a good start.

I am new to the Diamond Paint craze. It didn't take very long before I discovered how much I needed something like this organizer. It's a must have item for diamond art! There are extra tools as well as the diamond storage tubes. It's a nice little case! I do have one suggestion for improvement. There aren't any marks identifying the top or bottom. So I was showing this to my daughter and it was upside down, but I didn't know it. I unzipped it and about half of the tubes fell out. What a pain! I have since used a permanent black marker to write on the top side so that won't happen again.

This is my first "real" diamond painting. I have tried really small keychain ones, but this is the first full size one. It comes with the basic supplies, but I have ordered another supply kit for more options. It's a bit slow, but it is repetitive and easy to do. I did watch a video online first because I was a bit clueless. After watching the video, it all made sense to me. I did order myself one of those organizer kits which is a must have! It makes it easier to store all those little pieces. I am having fun watching this bird picture come to life.

Micron pens have been a favorite of mine for years! I especially like their fine tip pens. Prior to getting this set, I didn't realize that they had brush tip pens, as well. There are 2 colors (as far as I can tell) -light gray and darker gray. Then in each of those colors, there are different size tips. The colors are great. I have been enjoying writing with gray in my planner instead of the usual black. In my planner, I only used the fine tip pens and I didn't see any bleeding through to the other side of the paper. I use the Happy Planner in case you're wondering for comparison. I am very pleased with my new set of pens!

I am not a seamstress. I did take sewing for several years in 4-H when I was a child but have not sewn since. I'm in my fifties now, so it's been a long time. During the pandemic my daughter expressed interest in learning how to sew. I have my Grandmother's old sewing machine, but I never use it. My husband used it a few times for various sewing repairs. I ordered my daughter one of those cute cheap little sewing machines and she gave it a try. She didn't like it. The instructions were difficult. I saw this machine and thought maybe this would be better. It's a Singer brand which is what my mom's sewing machine had been back when I did sew. Plus, there are lots of videos available online for this machine. Having not sewn in over 35 years, I was able to almost "feel at home" with this machine. I nearly remembered all the steps in threading it. My daughter felt more comfortable playing around with this machine than the other ones. It's a good little sewing machine and great for beginners.

I am unfamiliar with the Primrosia brand, but I really love their packaging and branding. This set of markers is Pastels. The colors remind me of gardens. The pens are double ended with a brush tip on one end and a fine tip on the other. I am trying to learn how to do fancy lettering, so the brush tip is great for that. The fine tip will be great in my planner. The inks are adequately juicy and write smoothly. I am really liking them! I just followed the brand on social media so I can keep up with their products!

I was a little unsure after reading other reviews, but these berries & pine cone stem picks are not bad. I wanted them to use as filler in my tiered tray decor. This is my first time making Christmas trays, so I wanted to have an assortment of supplies to choose from. I'm probably going to do 2 different trays, so I need a lot of stuff. These have a frosted look to them. I was worried about the crystals falling off all over the place, but there was just minimal displacement of those. Most of them stuck to the picks. The picks are easy to bend to shape as needed. They're definitely Christmasy looking and will be just fine for my needs.

This is a good assortment of craft tools. There are so many pieces that it will definitely come in handy when we're working on our craft projects. I appreciate that there are so many replacement blades! Individually, the items seem to be a decent quality. Time will tell. The cutting mat is very small. I was in need of a much larger one, so I will need to still get that. Overall, it's a value.

These thin balsa wood sheets are good for craft projects. They seem to be a good quality and comparable to what I have purchased at the local chain craft store. My husband recently started using a laser wood cutting machine to make various crafts. So far, we're pleased with these pieces.

My husband has been interested in laser cutting. He has a different model of this brand, but this one has more wattage/power so it moves faster and cuts through thicker items. The other one was a little difficult to set up. This one required the same amount of set up, but because he had experience with the other one, this one was easier. Because this one has more wattage, it can cut with fewer passes. The other model allows you to work with a larger area than this one. However, if you do have a larger item, you can work around that with this one and work in sections. He did have an initial problem with using this one because he forgot to remove the protective covering off of the laser. It burned a hole through the wood, but it was definitely limited. Once he removed the cover (which was a sticker type covering), it worked as expected. There's an open source software, but there's not a lot of documentation for it. He ended up watching several YouTube videos which he found helpful. It comes with a little flash card  (SD) that contains configuration files and documentation. Unless your computer has a TF reader, you will need an adapter. There was a USB adapter included, but he couldn't get it to work. So far, he's primarily used it for burning into wood, but not cutting. The open source software has a database with the different types of materials you might want to use with this. It will tell you the right speed and power to use, but you have to select your laser cutter and this brand is not listed in it. He did a lot of experimenting with various settings. He plans to document his own results so he can refer to it when working. I think this will keep him entertained for a while!

I've started decorating a tiered tray and change it seasonally. My husband has a laser cuter and has started making words and phrases for my tray. Of course, they look better when mounted onto wood pieces. These round wood circles are perfect! They have the shiplap look to them. The size is perfect for the tiered trays. I am very excited to the possibilities of what I can do with these!

My daughter likes making jewelry in her free time. She has an online shop where she sells them. She needed some professional looking display cards. These can work with earrings or necklaces or both. They are a good quality. There are plastic zipper lock baggies to go with them. She is pleased.

This is a nice set and appropriate for back to school time. There are scissors, traditional tape and decorative tape. The decorative tape is basically Washi-tape. One roll has perforated stamp size stickers and the others are continuous printed tape. All of them are easy to tear.

I have dabbled in many crafts over the years, but until now, I have never made candles. This is a complete set that is perfect for beginners. The only thing you need is a small pan to create a double boiler. There are enough supplies to make 6 small tin candles. You can make bees wax and/or soy wax candles. My daughter and her friend enjoyed making the candles and now want to make more!

I love using wax melts to add some scents to my home. These melts are very weakly scented. I can barely detect any smell from them when they're melting. They do smell when you hold the packages up to your nose but they are more concentrated since the package has all the cubes together. The scent must be evaporating without leaving any smell behind. I also felt that the colors were too bright and artificial looking. Most wax melting cubes that I get are more subdued in coloring.

This set of solid color washi tapes have a great assortment of colors. There are 2 widths of tape. The rolls don't seem to have a whole lot of tape on them, so they will be limited to smaller projects. The quality seems to be comparable to other washi tapes. They're easy to remove and reapply.
We go through a lot of washi tape at our house. It's a fun and easy way to decorate things. You can remove it without damaging most surfaces. These come in a good assortment of colors and prints.

I enjoy using washi tape with my planner. It's an easy way to add some color and design to it. These are great rolls of tape. The quality of tape is on par with other tapes out there. It's easy to tear. They're somewhat transparent, meaning you can see things underneath it. Each roll comes individually wrapped and they were kind of a pain to open.

My daughter is working at a camp this summer. After the first week, her stash of embroidery thread for friendship bracelets was almost gone! This set allowed her to replenish some of the basic colors for week #2. The quality is good and comparable to what she picked up at the craft store before camp started. There are a few skeins of each of the colors which she likes. When she buys them in person, she tends to get a variety of all different colors, but this allows her to have plenty of the basic ones which are most needed.

The instructions for this button maker were not helpful at all! I searched for similar looking machines and found a competitor's video. We watched that a few times and were finally able to get this working. The finished buttons are great! But it took over a dozen attempts to get it done right. I have an older style button machine that makes small buttons, so I am pleased to have another size. I consider this to be the average size of buttons. I appreciate that this set comes with all the supplies to create a lot of buttons! It also comes with a circle cutter that is very useful.

This compact sewing machine is good for beginners. It comes already threaded and ready to go. The instructions are just okay. My prior knowledge of sewing (from my youth) was more helpful. My 20 year old daughter was able to do a little sewing with this machine.  It works and seems to be simpler and more basic than the machine my mother had when I was growing up. We like the fact that it can run on batteries if desired. My daughter plans to study it some more and watch some videos online before her next attempt.

I have admired similar felt creations and wondering how they were done. This kit doesn't really have any instructions with it, but there are lots of videos online showing how to do it. The tools needed are included. This isn't a super fast craft, but it really does work and the finished products are so cute!!

The assortment of semi-precious stones that I received are gorgeous! They're all flatter type stones. They have smooth surfaces and rounded edges. The product description pictures does show the type that I received, though my assortment may be slightly different. All of mine are unique and do not look like others in the set. They would make stunning jewelry! I ordered these for my 23 year old son who collects rocks, crystals, and stones. He has been very impressed with these.

My son likes to draw and recently started painting. He's been enjoying these new brushes.  The bristles are soft and seem to hold the paint well. He hasn't noticed any loose hairs. There's a good variety of brush tips depending on your needs. Overall these are great brushes!

My reading/close up vision is awful! I can't read a thing without my glasses. I don't have any problem with distance vision. Crafting has been very challenging for me because of my poor eyesight. My sister was telling me about a new crafting project that she's gotten into - miniatures. I told her that I don't think I could do it because I can't see well. She told me about this type of lamp, so I was excited to give it a try. You have the option of using a clamp or base for the lamp. It's easy to use and it makes it much easier to do the little things I don't like to do such as sewing or other detailed crafts. I definitely recommend!

I love markers! It's something I've always been a fan of. These markers are skinny with a fine tip. The ink flows nicely. The ink dries pretty quickly and doesn't smear much if at all. Most of the colors retain their sparkle after drying, but one of the blue colors doesn't seem to have much glitter or sparkle after it has dried. The colors are absolutely beautiful!

This resin kit is great for beginners because it comes with more than just the bottles of resin. The items are comparable to other supplies that I have used when crafting with resin. Some items, such as jewelry are very conducive to the UV Resin. It speeds up the process greatly. My daughter has successfully made some earrings with this kit and has been pleased.

This is a beautiful assortment of dried flowers. They are preserved nicely and sealed for protection. There appears to be little to no flower crumbs or dust. The will work nicely with our resin craft making.

My college age kids started crafting with Resin this fall. I saw this Tic Tac Toe mold and that it would be a fun one to do. The mold is comparable to other silicone molds. The resin comes out easily after it has dried. The finished product looks awesome!! We've only made one set so far. Since you have to let the resin dry, often for a couple of days, production is a bit slow.

My 23 year old son decided he wants to play around with water colors. He loves to sketch and draw, but painting is something new. His drawing pads just weren't going to do the job. These pages are noticeably thicker than drawing pads. There's something there for the water to absorb into a bit as it dries without tearing or ripping the paper. The paper has perforations along the top near the spiral so you can easily tear out the pages. The quality of this art paper is very good.
My kids and I have gotten into epoxy resin crafting. We go through a lot of craft sticks when stirring the resin. This should last us a while. The quality of the craft sticks is as expected.

My kids (college aged) and I have had fun learning to work with epoxy resin crafts. We have some powdered pigment but these liquid ones are much easier to work with in our opinions. It's easier to get the color mixed in evenly and it's not as messy. There are 20 colors in this set and they are nicely pigmented. You can mix colors to create even more colors!

These faux fur squares were as expected. The quality is good. It doesn't seem to shed excessively. The size is great for smaller projects. My daughter wanted this so she could make some toys for the cats. It's just what she wanted!

My son likes to draw and sketch. He goes through a lot of drawing pads. He likes this kind best (spiral on top). The pages are a decent thickness. There are 2 drawing pads in this set. Overall they're pretty good.

A few weeks ago, my children and I started crafting with resin. One of our friends saw our initial pieces and requested an initial. Well, we needed a new mold then! This is a great mold that has all of the letters A-Z and digits 0-9. The molds aren't too shallow so the finished letters are a good thickness. The set also comes with some fun accessories such as the tassels and key rings. There's also some glitter and a tool for putting the holes in to the finished letters. I ended up ordering some different key rings, because I wanted clips so we could clip our initials to our bags if we wanted. Overall, this is a fun set to have.

My kids and I recently started crafting with resin. We started with jewelry, but immediately were eager to move up in size. Coasters felt like the next step. They're larger, of course, which makes it easier to arrange the embellishments such as gold flakes and dried flowers. These molds are a good quality and comparable to other silicone molds that we have used. I was especially happy that this set included a silicone mat to work on!

My daughter and I thought it would be fun to customize some hair clips. This set includes a bunch of gold colored metal hair clips in 3 different shapes/sizes as well as 3 different silicone molds. First of all, I really like that the molds have 3 of each shape. When we were working with earrings a few weeks ago, each shape had only one mold so it was difficult to create matching/identical looking earrings. Here, you can make 3 identical ones at the same time. The molds don't line up completely with the clips, especially the bar clip, however, we can go buy more clips if needed. We really wanted the molds! Overall, we have had fun with these!

My family has had fun making crafts with resin recently. This set has lots of possibilities. One thing that I especially like is that it comes with a few supplies such as sanding tools, pipettes, and measuring cups in addition to the fun silicone molds. The quality of the items is good. There are no instructions, however, there are plenty of online resources to help you out.

A few weeks ago, my college kids and I started playing around with resin. We all enjoyed it and the kids requested that I get some more molds. These coaster molds are a good size and much easier to work with than the jewelry that we started with. The set also includes 3 large bottles of gold foil pieces. It has yellow gold, rose gold and silver. The bottles are full of foil pieces! We have foil sheets that we used last time but these are much easier to work with. We have had a lot of fun creating some beautiful coasters!

I am new to the crafting of resin. I decided to choose the 1:1 ratio of products to make it easier for me with measuring. This set works as it is supposed to. It did take at least a couple of days before the resin was completely hardened. I found it difficult to clean off the measuring cups, but that's probably a resin quality, not specific to this brand. Overall, I was pleased with it. The finished resin projects turned out great!

These are pretty flowers that work well for crafty. We've been using them in resin. There is a slight learning curve when it comes to working with them. They are a bit fragile, but we're getting the hang of it. The colors are beautiful and these do add some beauty to our crafting.

My college aged kids and I started crafting with resin. This is a great assortment of colors that we can use with it. A little goes a long way, so it will last a long time. The colors come in little jars that are easy to identify the color inside. We have been pleased with them so far.

All of a sudden, I've started seeing resin crafting stuff everywhere! I was very curious about it. This seemed like a good set for getting started. My college aged kids and I sat down this week to make some items. We had never done this before. I had watched a few videos. This set does not include resin or the fun stuff to put inside the projects. While we did have fun, I felt that we were still unprepared. The quality of the molds seems fine. An instruction booklet is in the set but it's general and not specific to the kit. We're going to watch more videos and see if that helps us next time. The finished projects were nice but they definitely look like beginner crafters made them. I really wish that the earring molds had 2 identical mold sections for each so you can actually make a pair of matching earrings. My daughter made some earrings, but she only was able to make 1. She has to wait until she gets all the supplies out again before making another one. Since you have to let the resin set for about 24 hours, it would make more sense to make pairs of things at the same time so you have the consistency. It may be a while before she gets a chance to make more, so until then she is stuck with a single earring.

There are tons of foil sheets in this set. They are very fragile, but very pretty. We used these in our first attempt a making resin jewelry. There are 24 jars and each jar has several sheets of foil, separated in between paper. I do wish the jars were labeled. I found that I had to open up each one in order to see the color inside. I plan to watch some videos before we try this again.

These dried flowers are a bit delicate, but they're very beautiful. We used some in our first attempts at making resin jewelry. We need to work on our technique, but overall, we were pleased with these and how they looked in our finished work.

Last year I decided that I wanted to learn how to do fancy lettering. I bought a few books, printed out practice sheets, and watched online videos. I also purchased several different brush pens. Some good, some not so good. These ones are pretty good. I felt like they are easy to control. They have good ink flow. They're not very juicy, but they are inked well. The set has a nice variety of colors. They're pretty vibrant. I have enjoyed playing around with them.

We have accumulated many cloth masks at my house. My daughter, who is 19, has a smaller face than the rest of us and has had difficulties with some of the masks not fitting well. These small silicone pieces are quite helpful and allows her to wear many of the larger ones that we have. It's easy to put the pieces on the elastic, but there was no tool provided. I had to search for something that would do the job. They work on the masks with the elastic cord but not the ones with the wider elastic. And since there are so many in the package, it's no big deal if one gets lost. The bag is not resealable, so we had to find something else to store them in.

Both of my college kids have been wanting to paint this summer. These canvas boards are perfect! They're pretty thin so they don't take up a lot of storage space. My daughter likes to sketch her drawing first with pencil so these are easy to grasp and hold while doing that. There are 14 canvases, so there's enough for them to share!

My daughter has had trouble with several of our masks not fitting her smaller face. I ordered these to help with that issue. No instructions were included with these, however, there are pictures describing how to use them on the product description here. They really are easy to use. They work well and don't budge when wearing the mask. So far they have stayed in place and none have come off in the wash.

My 19 year old daughter was very excited to see this starter kit. She says that she has seen cross stitching/embroidery on social media lately and was enthusiastic to give it a try. I did a little back when I was a kid, but I did not keep up with the craft. This comes with most everything you need to get started. There are plenty of tutorials out there, but the directions are not too bad. It's really a great introduction kit!

My daughter has been excited to try this craft technique after seeing social media videos of it. I gave one to her (she's 19) and the other to my 11 year old niece. My daughter hadn't gotten around to hers yet, but my niece took to it immediately. I went to visit my niece and her family a couple of days after giving it to her. My sister said she had been in her room for the good part of the day working on it. I was impressed with how much progress she had made. The kit comes with all the supplies to complete the craft. It wasn't as tedious for the 11 year old as I anticipated. She actually enjoyed the process. She's more than half way through and has motivated my daughter to work on hers. It's definitely a fun activity to keep big kids busy with non-screen time!

I am primarily wearing cloth masks when I go out. These wire strips are game changers! The adhesive strip is really strong and keeps it in place. When wearing the mask, you can bend it to the shape of your nose. I have left them on when in the washing machine. Some have stayed in place and some have not. Since this comes with so many, I just put a new one on if needed. They do seem to stay in place better when used with masks that have the filter pocket and the strip is placed inside that between the layers of fabric. I would definitely recommend these!

My eighteen year old daughter was excited to give this tye-dye kit a try. The instructions were well written and easy to follow, though she has lots of previous tye-dye experience. After mixing the dye, she was able to decorate a twin XL set (top sheet, bottom sheet, and 2 pillow cases) as well as a cropped t-shirt. She probably could have done more items if she hadn't applied the dye as heavily, but she wanted the colors to be vibrant. She let the items set overnight before rinsing, then she washed them. She is very pleased with the results. This is a nice kit and at the current price point ($7.99) it would make a great gift. It does come with an iron on unicorn, but she did not use it.

I recently spent a week babysitting for my nieces and nephew. I thought this would be a fun activity for them to do after school one day. Their ages are 11, 9, and 6. The older two were familiar with flip books and immediately sat down to create their own. I had to demonstrate it to the younger one. There are 6 bound flip books in this set. It also comes with drawing pencils, an eraser, and a pencil sharpener. The pencil sharpener doesn't do a very good job sharpening pencils and it was complicated to use. The rest of the items are pretty nice. The kids had fun making their own flip books!

My 22 year old son loves to draw. He goes through a lot of sketch books over time. These are a nice, big size. The spiral binding is on the side. He prefers ones that are on the top as it stays out of the way, but he said that this side binding doesn't bother him too much because the size of the pad. The paper is a decent thickness. My son draws with thin fine tip black markers and it doesn't seem to bleed through the paper much, but he still only draws on one side of it. Every once in a while you can see some ink that shows through.

I am new to the world of heat vinyl and not that experienced with using my cutting machine. It took some trial and error on my part to get this working as it should. After a few mistakes, I finally got it right and was pleased with the results. I used my iron to heat it and followed some of the tips that I had seen online. The finished product looked great and has held up to washing so far. It seems to be a nice quality. Since it's a roll, I still have quite a bit left, even after my mistakes!

I am somewhat obsessed with markers. I have all kinds of markers for whatever needs I may have. These are good, multipurpose ones. They come in a nice selection of colors. The ink flows well. I tried a little calligraphy and wasn't pleased with the results. I prefer a pen with more control than the brush side offers for my fancy lettering. Though it does work well for the more contemporary or whimsical lettering. I really like the fine tip side. It works well in my planner. Just be careful to make sure you're using thick paper or it may show through to the other side.

I've had a few different cutting machines in the past. Some used cartridges and some worked with the computer. However, I have never been really totally happy with any of them for various reasons. My husband was the one who ended up using them the most. I would get easily frustrated and give up. I tried to set this one up on my own from the beginning. I found it a little confusing with all the different instruction sheets. I started throwing away the Spanish ones so they weren't in the mix. I didn't get very far on my own before I asked my husband to help me. Once we got it connected to the WIFI and downloaded the software to my laptop, I was able to figure out how to use it on my own. I even printed out a sheet of my daughter's name in vinyl, all in different sizes and fonts. To me, this was pretty incredible that I could do that. I never got that far with my other cutting machines that used the computer. There are lots of possibilities with this machine. I like the touch screen feature. I can't wait to try some of the other features.

This is a nice set of sewing tools. It's labeled as a beginners set, but it doesn't come with everything you would need to start sewing. The quality of the items range from good to excellent. The seam ripper is my favorite. It's a much nicer one than I've used previously. The tape measure is nice, too. It's a soft, flexible one, but it is kept in a dispenser which makes it easier to store. Overall, I am very pleased.

I work in a special education classroom and we use glue sticks regularly with our class work. I compared this extra strength glue stick to our regular ones. It's a larger tube than the others, but I really didn't notice any difference in the glue capabilities. It's a good glue stick. It goes on purple and dries clear. It will probably remain my go-to glue stick brand.

I was so excited to make my own cover for my planner, but this didn't turn out the way I wanted it to. First of all, the finished cover isn't very thick or sturdy. It's much thinner than the original ones. I ruined the first one because I didn't trim my cardstock enough, so the edges wouldn't stick together. I did a better job with the second one, but I didn't like how the cover doesn't go all the way to the spiral edge, so there's a gap which lets the first page of the planner show through.

I have 3 different Cri-Cuts and a Wishblade. I'm limited to what I can do on the Cri-Cuts because of the cartridges. I received the Wishblade for Christmas many years ago and found it to not be very user friendly. The Designncut has not been very user friendly to me, but my husband has been able to do all kinds of cutting with it. The software that you download to use with it has some neat projects, but I found them to be limiting. I really want to make some monograms so I can make some cute shirts. There were no available monograms to use. My husband was able to find images online of various objects and logos. He was able to add them to the software and cut them out onto vinyl with the machine. He made a decal for my daughter's laptop. He even made a Star Wars Rebel Alliance logo out of vinyl and that was more intricate. I still haven't been able to make my monograms, but I have been looking at different websites that have monogram generators. Once I find the right one, my husband says it will be easy to cut it. We have not had any issues with Wifi. My husband is pretty tech savvy and he thinks this machine is easy to use. So if you're good with technology, you should be okay. If you're clueless, like me, you may have some difficulties.

Let me begin by saying that I am fairly new to the whole iron on vinyl thing. I've inherited four different cutting machines and haven't spent a lot of time cutting. Prior to using this, I have cut some vinyl monograms to put on my stainless tumblers and some designs on cardstock, but that's it so far. The vinyl seems to be comparable to other vinyls that I have purchased as far as appearance. It is a little thicker than some of the others. I read some of the other reviews before cutting this, so I knew adjust the blade and test cut before I started my project. The cutting side of the vinyl is labeled, plus it looks different than the finished side. It took me a couple of attempts to get a good on my image, but that's probably attributed to my lack of inexperience with the whole process and equipment. Once my image was cut, it was a little difficult to remove the excess pieces, but not impossible. There are instructions on the inside of the box and they were easy to follow. The finished product looks wonderful! I am very pleased with how it turned out. I've washed the shirt at least once so far and it's held up well. My daughter has started coming up with more ideas for more shirts!

This set of heat transfer vinyl sheets comes in a great selection of vivid colors. It works well with my Cricut Explore. Any difficulties that I had whiled working with it were due to human error, not the product. The directions included on the package were fairly easy to follow. I was able to make a monogram t-shirt for my daughter. I've laundered the shirt once so far and there's no evidence of it not adhering to the fabric. I'm now planning to make a few more for Christmas presents.

This is my first time using transfer tape. I didn't know that such a product existed. I have cut out vinyl monograms with my Cricut before and had difficulties getting the letters straight onto stainless steel tumblers. Then recently I saw a co-worker putting a monogram on a cup and she pulled out a huge roll of clear transfer tape and I was fascinated at how easy it was for her to use and get her vinyl straight. So of course, I had to buy some myself. It's a big roll. There's no backing paper or grid lines. My friend had grid lines on hers, and I think I will get some of that next time. The tape is sticky enough, but it doesn't stick to itself. It's also removable and can be repositioned. It didn't come with any instructions, so I had to search for some youtube videos on how to use it. It is taking some time to get used to it, but I'm liking it.

My daughter enjoys painting in her free time. She uses acrylic paint that comes in little tubes and likes to blend her own colors. I ordered this for her because she was using a paper plate for her paints and this looked like it would be easier to use. It also keeps the paints separate and not touching accidently. It cleans up pretty easily, too.

I have always been stingy with my vinyl because it's expensive. I ordered a variety set of vinyl sheets last year and have been trying to conserve as much as possible. That set had fewer sheets, each sheet was smaller, and the price was about the same as this set. I am thrilled with all the colors that come in this package. The quality is good. The paper backing as grid lines for cutting. An sheet of paper was included with guidelines and tips. I found this to be very helpful since I consider myself a beginner with the die cutting machines. Even though I purchased this at a discounted price, I would not hesitate to repurchase it at full price because it is a great assortment and quality vinyl.

*I purchased this at a discounted price in exchange for my honest & unbiased review. My thoughts & opinions are my own.

This is a great set of colored pencils. The color assortment is nice and the pencil quality is very good. The pencils sharpen pretty easily. The colors are rich and vibrant. I color pretty hard and none of them have broken yet. Sometimes with lower quality pencils, they leads break way to easily with any pressure applied. They blend pretty well with water, but the paint brush that comes with it is a very poor quality brush. The hairs don't form a point when wet and they shed quite a lot. The paint brush is similar to what you would get in a children's paint set. The storage tin is nice. I like the unique shape. It won't roll off the table.

*I purchased this set at a discounted price for the purpose of trying out & reviewing. My thoughts & opinions are my own.

When I first opened this box, I immediately was reminded of my grandmother. She used a similar magnifier to read the newspaper. Now, I am in my late 40's and these are actually coming in handy with reading. The magnifier does a great job of magnifying. It does require 3 (AAA) batteries which are not included. The magnifier can magnify without the batteries, but if you want to use the light feature, batteries are needed. I've been keeping this in my craft room to help me with some small details.

*I received this magnifier for free for the purpose of trying out & reviewing. My thoughts & opinions are my own.

This is a good, basic sketch book. My teenage son is taking a drawing class at school this semester and needed a new drawing pad. I first bought him a really nice one with a hard cover, but that one was a bit too small. Then I ordered him a larger one with heavy weight pages. He said that was nice, but "too nice" for his class. Plus it only had 30 pages. So third time's a charm! This one has 100 sheets and is adequate for his school drawings. He told me that he needed "quantity over quality" regarding his pad. He also preferred spiral bound.

Wow! This is super nice sketch paper. It's heavier weight than most sketch pads that I have found. I ordered this for my teenage son who is taking a drawing class this year. The last pad I bought was too small for his needs. This is a much better size for his school work. His only comment was that it's too nice for his school assignments! He actually told me that he needs quantity not quality! So it looks like I'll be ordering another sketch pad, but that's not any strike against this sketch pad.

I am a teacher's aide for a Special Needs class and wanted something to carry supplies with me when accompanying my students to their fun classes such as Art. They aren't expected to take glue sticks, colored pencils and such with them to those classes, but there are times that they are needed. So I put all of those items in my new supply caddy & bring it with us. The caddy is a nice size to carry what we need. The handle is sturdy & comfortable. It's made out of a strong, canvas material. The Velcro dividers are a nice addition. I am not currently using the dividers, but it definitely gives you more options if needed.

This past year I discovered the fun of using adhesive chalkboards and labels around the house. We have a preference for liquid chalks over traditional chalk. Liquid chalks look brighter and more vibrant and they are easier to clean. And of course there's no chalk dust! We use both colored liquid chalks and white ones depending on where we're using them. Recently I was given the opportunity to purchase the Crafty Crock White Liquid markers at a discount so I could try them out & share my thoughts on them. They work just as well as other liquid chalk markers that I have tried. It takes a few moments of priming the marker to get the ink to flow. Because most of my adhesive chalkboards on walls or other upright containers, I do have to reprime it after every few words I write. One thing that makes this brand better than the others is that it comes with a reversible tip! It's really easy to switch the tips, so I recommend that you change the tips before you prime the ink! The chalk ink cleans easily with water and leaves no ghosting behind. I've included a picture of the chalkboard adhesive that I have on my kitchen refrigerator. For the last several months I have been listing the dinners for the week. I originally started by putting the day of the week next to each meal, but with our busy household, I prefer the flexibility. It works well for us! I keep my Crafty Croc liquid chalk markers in a magnetic holder on the side of the refrigerator so I can get them easily. After we've eaten a particular meal, I cross it off the list.

These high quality brushes come beautifully packaged. They come in a canvas storage case that rolls up nicely & fits in a canister. The brushes themselves are awesome! The handles are sturdy & heavy weight. The bristles are soft & secure. There are no loose brush hairs or shedding. The assortment of brush types is wonderful. There is a brush for every need. This would make an excellent gift!

What an amazing Loom Kit! I ordered this as a gift for my daughter and she is going to love it! After reading some of the other reviews, I decided to go ahead & open it because of issues other customers had, but everything was in perfect order. The bands were all sorted nicely & the dividers are secure & in place. I was especially impressed with the number of items. There were multiple hooks, clips, and even some charms. The variety of colors is nice. There are even some tye-dyed colored bands.  My daughter has a small box of loom bands & supplies. The bands often get mixed up & her box didn't hold all of the supplies. This organizer pretty much holds everything! The box is much larger than I was expecting by at least a couple of inches. I can't wait for my daughter to open this!

The apron & hat set is a good, basic apron set. The fabric is mid-weight - not too heavy & stiff, but also not too flimsy either. It adjusts easily for a good fit. Each pocket on the apron is about the size of my hand. I would prefer if the pockets were a little lower on the apron & if they were just a little wider. I'm not particularly short, (I'm 5'6") but I had to adjust the apron quite a bit so that the ties hit my waistline. I'm sure if I didn't have to adjust it so much, the location of the pockets would be lower. The color is nice & vibrant. In addition to the matching adjustable hat, there was a coordinating pot holder, too.
Great for having fun with my grandchild

I love this!! I put my planner and many books of stickers in it and still of plenty of room for tape and accessories. I use the bottom piece as a divider so the bottom isn’t as sturdy but it’s perfect for what I use it for and I could use the bottom piece if I chose to. Great value for the price!

It's not what I expected..I usually buy Bernate softee baby yarn, but for some reason Bernate does not offer baby yarn in solid sky blue. So, I decided to try Red Heart baby yarn, expecting it to be somewhat similar to Bernate softee baby yarn.

It is not as soft and smooth as Bernate, and my nose got all stuffy and my eyes were itchy.... fuzz fuzz fuzz.... guaranteed knots in every single roll! If you likes to pull it from the center, then you are screwed. There's also some grey yarns or some kind of dirty looking fuzz mixed in the thread. Untying knots and pulling dirty fuzz off the yarn dramatically slowed me down from crocheting.

MOST IMPORTANT:
I washed it by itself, using handwash cycle with cold water, and then tumble dry low. Do you have an old old old sweater? You know the fuzz balls that accumulated over the year? Well, you get that with one wash with this yarn!

With the exception of the scissors, which are shoddy in my opinion, I really like this scrapbooking set.  Mainly I like the fact that it is somewhat "old school."  I do not mean that it is old fashioned, but rather that it helps a kid create an actual physical book of memories, rather than a digital photo stream that he or she needs an electronic device to look at.  This is the sort of project that could keep a child occupied for hours, in the way I recall using my free time when I was a child.  I really liked the stickers and the suggestions for creating a scrapbook that come with this set.  It is definitely an introductory set, aimed at about a ten to twelve year old.  Depending on your child, this set would be a welcome gift for a kid going to camp or on a special vacation to document his or her memories.  Best of all, it is not overpriced.  But definitely get the kid some good scissors!

I basically liked this yarn. The shipping and packaging was great. But as you can see, even though the lot numbers matched, one skein was brighter than the other. So my socks are not a perfect match. Also, the skeins were wound in opposite directions. When working with striping yarn, it's important to start at the same place in the yarn. The first skein I worked from the outside. The second skein I had to work from the inside. It was not easy to find the inside end of yarn and resulted in a lot of unknotting " yarn barf". Also, the yarn had frequent fray spots.

I would like to add that I got 4 socks nicely out of 3 skeins.

We still had fun using the ones we had, I just read they come out less bright. The bottles of dye are easy to make and fill. Again would have been nice to know before hand. Another reason I took off a star was that the tie dye design instructions weren’t great and I had to YouTube how to wrap them up. It's not sticky so you can slide the paper or whatever into the right spot and then press it in place for a minute or two and it sticks fine. I purchased the 144 slot version, so I can't say anything regarding the smaller ones. I was looking for an organizer for all of my alcohol markers, and this one was perfect.

I only just got this organizer, so I don't know how well it'll keep in the long run. I'll post an update later.

Pros:
- fits most large markers
- holds a lot of markers
- can be opened up or folded into a box
- easy to reach all markers
- has a strap and handle for easy carrying
- appears solidly built, with velcro and clips to hold it together

Cons
- a bit pricey
- hard to pull out larger markers (especially my Caliart markers since they had a small protrusion on the side that gets caught in the straps)
- not overly rigid and falls down if not full
- a little bulky

If you are starting out with markers for the first time or are a little strapped for cash, this is a great alternative to fancy markers like copics.

They are vibrant, have a huge selection of colors, and blend relatively well if you have good paper for then.

The main downside is the lack of brush tip. A lot of nicer markers have a chisel and a brush, or pen tip and brush, because brush tops make blending incredibly easy. Without the brush tip, it's really hard to mix colors. But if you are mostly coloring in solid colors, dont care about blending, or have a couple of good brush tip markers already to blend with, you'll find this set more than sufficient.

Just remember, the "blend" pen (transparent one) is not used for blending! Look up tutorials for alcohol markers. It's a good way to push back color.

If they made these with a brush tip on one side, I'd buy these again in a heartbeat and recommend them to everyone.

Initially I thought the name brand would mean quality and hopefully excuse the fact they seem to have a monopoly on the product. The plastic needles were not smooth and continually snagged fibers when need to be able to run through yarns smoothly without snagging. After some time sanding and smoothing out all edges, they are now useful. I'll be glad when competition encourages a better end product or at least better pricing for such poorly finished products.
The Royal Silk fill is incredibly soft to touch and squishy-plush. Even tightly filled to capacity, cuddly stuffies are still squishy and pleasing to snuggle.

There are plenty of much better quality options out there at nearly the same pricing. This is a waste of money.
#1. Not truly non- tarnish unless you don't work it with metal tools and don't handle it much at all. At least the color doesn't fracture like paint or chip off, but it will easily scrape off. The inner copper quickly shows through the silver coating and can oxidize.
#2. The copper showing through so easily also makes it a poor choice for any jewelry or decorative fine wirework (unless you get the copper-colored variety, but no doubt it will still oxidize).
#3. Density is similar to others and easy to work, but this product is cheaply made. Not recommended.

Gesso goes all the way to the edge and edges are straight, no rounding or beveling. Arrived wet through the plastic with water stains on the gesso side and overall warped with the sharp corners bent. Quality is fine for what it is, but I won't be going with this brand again when better quality for about the same price is available.
The weight and feel are very similar to Schoeller & Stahl’s Limone’ line. The colors are incredibly vibrant, just gorgeous. I'm so pleased! Will definitely buy more!

I love this assortment of pins, and it's just what I needed. I'll say that the case is super handy to carry in my purse, too.

It was fine for my intended use, especially since I didn't need the full quantity, but some of these fasteners are smaller than the 3/4 inch described. I do appreciate the nifty storage box they came in.

I’ve been using Fabri-Tac for over 20 years.I think the name should be changed to All-Tac because it can be used on so many other things in addition to fabric.

I’ve used it to glue/adhere ceramic cracks, repair certain jewelry, holding light wood… As for fabric, when I don’t feel like sewing (which is most of the time), I’ve used it to raise hems on dresses/pants/jeans, embellish clothing, pillows, lamps… with cording, jewels, pom-poms… I can fix fabric/carpet tears… if you’re creative you could actually build a garment without a sewing machine.

It dries clear and fast, but not too fast. And it’s machine washable. I would suggest starting with small amounts to get a feel for how much you need. It’s flexible for a few minutes or so, then hardens well.

I’ve tried every kind of glue out there and some are specialized for a reason, but many times Fabri-Tac works as well. This is the first time I’ve purchase it on Amazon and I’m very happy. It may seem a bit pricey, but the 12oz. bottle lasts quite a long time and worth every cent. (I would not recommend paying more than $15 per 12 oz. bottle. There are some sketchy sellers out there as we all know.)

Worth a try—highly recommend. And this seller’s customer service is awesome.
