So, I simply added an extra magnet on each end and it's much stronger and works great! I'm buying more!  My only complaint is the price.

This was supposed to be a new bottle of glue, but when I opened the box and nothing slid out, I should have known something was fishy.  I had to tear the box open only to find a squished tube (like a halfway used toothpaste tube) and the glue had seeped out and dried to the inside of the box.  Trying to make the best of the situation, I wanted to use try it on my project, but I had to use pliers in order to open the glue top!  So, no, I wouldn't buy this again.  It didn't even work well.
Not good, I am using them for ejuice and they all leak.
My four-year-old absolutely love this set. She had so much fun making bracelets and necklaces for everyone.
This makes a great gift set for those who like a little touch of old fashion. It brings a little fun back to letter writing. Great for tweens through adults.

These markers are really good quality. They’re really similar to the brush too on the popular (and expensive) brand. The fine tip side is more of a fine tip pen. The only downside of this set is that the colors make no sense. They are lacking colors, have several colors that are pretty much the exact same, and have some colors that are basically clear. They’re numbered but not organized in a way that makes any sense. You’d expect all the reds to be in the same number range, but they are all over the place. Other than that, they’re great markers.
The paper quality isn’t phenomenal, but it is fine. It is a decent enough sketch book.
This is like friendship bracelet making for boys. You can make paracord bracelets without the holder, but the holder does make it easier— especially for more complex patterns. Great set.

I was so scared I was going to break this when setting it up the first time. Honestly, I’m pretty nervous about breaking it every time I set it up. The parts just don’t move well. It feels cheap, but looks decent from a little distance. It does work, but can be a little awkward to get set up.
These are not as good of quality as I get at the local craft store. I use them to make bookmarks. These always just look messy. They’re much too delicate.
My eight-year-old had a blast making candles. He made a little of each kind. It was easy for him to use. It took him about an hour to use the contents of the box.

The colors are not at shown. I feel like I just kind of got a random set of washi tape. But the tape itself is good. Easy to tear. Goes down smoothly. Doesn’t curl too much so you can easily put down larger strips without too much of a headache.
This is a good slime kit. It makes basic slime. Kids like it and are able to follow directions on their own. It does come out of fabric with water.

If you were getting this for a child, please be aware that it has a very sharp pencil sharpener and an X-Acto knife included. It also has a little sandpaper sharpener. It has quite a variety of different charcoals and graphite that you can use so you can get a feel for working with them. It also comes with a nice little sketch book. And the case is quite nice as well.

This is quite a large art set. It comes with one part of blank paper, and one pad that has coloring pages. It has oil pastels, watercolors, colored pencils, and crayons. It is in a nice wooden box for storage. The art supplies are at the highest quality, but they are pretty decent.

I only got one out of a two pack. The cover is thin and doesn't really offer much in terms of protection for your work. The paper is decent. I like the big size. The paper could hold watercolor if you aren't too wet with the application. It is better for sketches with pencils, markers, or charcoals.

This is a good beginner watercolor set. Is it the best? Not really. The palette itself leaves a little to be desired as the pans don't stay in place quite as they should. But the paints work well. They blend pretty well and don't leave a chalky finish like many cheap watercolors tend to do.
These colored pencils are nice. They sharpen easily and color nice and smooth. They blend easily and there is a wide variety of colors. I'm happy with the quality.

My kids loved this set. The 5 and 7 year olds were a little young to do them without adult help. However, the 9, 11, and 13 year olds were all able to complete their creations using the instruction book. The paper is colored to make the specific origami look perfect.

These aren't the best watercolor paints. They look a bit chalky when dry. They don't blend extremely well. But for kids, they're a big step up from typical kid paint. They're good for younger kids learning to watercolor paint. They're similar to Artist's Loft quality-- pretty much identical to those actually.

Really, to make a proper flip book, you need a light box or a light pad. However, if you already have that, these flip book refills are great. You can customize the length of your book. These seem to be the standard size for flip books. These work well. The paper seems to do well with pencil, ink, and markers.

This is a nice brush. They aren’t packaged well at all, so it might take you a couple tries to get a usable brush. But the brush itself is nice. It is a fine point that is easy to do line work with. It absorbs water and paint nicely. I would definitely buy again!

I liked this brush a lot. I usually use Silver brushes for watercolor, but opted to try these out. It held a good deal of water. I was also able to get very crisp lines along with wider washes. The brush is uniform, without little random hairs sticking out anywhere. It is a good brush. I’d definitely buy a Princeton Neptune brush again. (Picture was made with one size 10 round brush.)

I feel like this dip pen is maybe more for aesthetics than actual use. The nib doesn't hold a lot of ink like some other glass pens I have. The tip is very fine and a tad bit scratchy. It is also much too light. The sand is in a hollow place in the grip, which looks cool, but makes the pen a weird weight in the hand. I like more weight in the grip. It just feels more natural to me. It isn't awful, but it certainly isn't the best.
My boys are pot holder making fools. These don’t last very long. They can go through an entire pack and a single day. I wish there were bigger packs.
I love wooden rings for baby teething rings. These are great size for little hands to hold. You can jazz them up by adding toys or ribbons or leave them plain.

My teenage son is super into comics and things. He was so excited about this set. It comes with a smallish light box. It does not come with a plug for the light box. It is pretty easy to figure out how to make the flipbook. It’s a lot of fun. My son absolutely loves it.

This is thinner than normal brown Kraft paper. It feels more like parchment paper than kraft paper. Since I use it for crafting with kids, the feel of the paper does make a difference. If you’re packing with it, you should know it is thinner and more prone to tearing. It would make better filler paper than outer packing material. It does make kind of cool maps and scrolls, if you’re hoping to craft those types of things with it.
This set is just okay. One of the rings was broken. The dowels are really short, which makes it difficult to make much of anything.

These markers work well. We’ve had issues with other brands of markers coming with dried out markers, so we test them all on arrival now. Not a single dud in the pack! And it came with a card for testing the markers out!

Most beginners sets aren't the best quality. They assume you don't know the difference. This set has a really nice, thick quilting ruler. The scissors are fine. The other accessories are fine. The ruler is really the star of the show, for me. I consider it essential for most home sewing projects.

This ink is perfect for use with a dip pen or a brush. It does tend to settle, so I like to transfer a bit to my watercolor palette so I can keep it mixed. I think it’s be a little too thick and finicky to put into a pen, so I haven’t tried it.

I absolutely love India ink for painting. These are excellent. I had purchased a few individually before getting this big pack. This pack has all the basic colors you’ll need. Beyond this, maybe some shimmery metallics would be nice to add. You can use a dip pen and use them as ink. Or you can grab a brush and use them similarly to watercolor. You can dilute or mix them as needed. I really love them.

This watercolor book has great paper. I don't have any issues with paint bleeding through or ruffling the pages. I really love the size. My previous book was a square, so I wasn't too sure about having it landscape, but I've really enjoyed the size and shape. Not so big it feels daunting to fill. Not so small I have to break out the tiny brushes.

This brush set is pretty good. I admit I have very high standards when it comes to my brushes. These aren't going to be the same quality as higher end brushes. This is more of a mixed-media kind of pack. They are pretty good and I do like them.

These are not even close to being like paint. They are more like colored glue sticks. They are incredibly hard to use with a brush. They work best on slick finger painting paper, but even then, the colors aren't as vivid as finger paints or tempera paints. They are a bit diluted and clear looking. I'm not a fan of how these work at all. I have other paint type products in a tube, and they are difficult to work with but at least have color payoff. These don't have many positive sides to them.

My 12-year-old daughter loves art. This set was perfect for her. It gave her lots of new activities to try her hand at. She mostly enjoyed coloring the embossed paper. She is getting the hang of origami, as well. The set has a very limited color palette, but she didn't seem to mind the lack of color options.

I found the palette to be much darker than I anticipated. The pictures made it look a little lighter. That wasn't a particular problem. Most of the colors were nice and creamy. They didn't take much work to use. They are beautiful. And then you have the metallics. The Guava Meringue took a lot of mixing to get to finally work. I had to scrape the top of the pan to get to an underlayer and it finally started working okay. It still isn't the easiest to work with. The White Mocha is pointless. I couldn't get it to work any more than water. It wouldn't ever give me a pearl finish. The Earl Grey is particularly awful. It didn't matter how much scraping and mixing, I couldn't get any color payoff out of it. It was dry and cracked and visibly different from the other colors in the palette. It would give the slightest hint of grey. I tried layering it and never could get a nice even color.
I contacted the company about the issues I was having and got no response. I thought maybe I have an old palette or it is somehow defective-- but no one at Prima would return my e-mails. If the other colors weren't great, I'd give this one or no stars based on the three metallics alone. I'm not sure I'll buy more. I had planned to collect all of them, but I'm not entirely sure it is worth it if they are hit or miss like this.

I usually pick up cheap wooden hoops at the craft store. These are better quality than those. A lot thicker and the wood is a lot smoother. This set has 12 complete hoops. I was a little confused because it said "12 pieces" but it really should say "12 units" because it is 12 complete hoop sets. (Outer hoop, inner hoop, and screws.) I'm really surprised at the quality. I like to make doodle embroidery and leave it in the hoop for hanging, so I love that I can get sets of hoops like this. I will definitely be purchasing more in the future.

My twelve year old was super excited to dive into this set. She had never worked with clay before, but was eager to try. She has been able to make some really amazing creations with this set. She absolutely loves the set. It is perfect for beginners. It gives them a good enough variety to learn to tackle quite a few projects.

This is a really nice train set. Lionel Trains have always been good quality and this one does not disappoint. It comes with a small track, which you can expand if you choose or use with your other Lionel tracks and trains. It is very simple to use. You don't have to be a model train expert to figure it all out. You will need three AAA batteries.

This glue is more opaque than it appears in the pictures. We added liquid starch to make slime. It turned into a really pretty bubblegum pink opaque slime with glitter. My kids couldn’t decide whether to call it “unicorn” slime or “bubblegum” slime. It was really pretty. And it made really smooth bouncy slime.

Excellent slime! We mixed this glue with liquid starch and it made the perfect clear blue slime. It was bouncy and a nice texture. The blue color didn’t dilute much, so our slime was pretty much the exact color of the glue in the bottle. We also tried out green and it worked really well, too.

I love the Stabilo 88 pens, so basically had to grab the 68s, as well. These are more like markers than pens. The 88s are definitely fine tip pens. These are like a marker tip. I was really hoping for something a little more like the 88. Like a bold pen, not a marker. I like these as markers. Great color selection. The tips do fray, especially if you're writing on rougher paper. They aren't very crisp. The colors are really vivid and correspond to the 88 colors.

We had so much fun with this set! My 2 year old, 4 year old, 6 year old, 8 year old, and 11 year old had a blast. My 2 year old wasn’t quite able to do this on his own, so my 11 year old helped him. The other kids were able to complete this on their own. You get more than enough clay for the 4 boards. And my kids loved putting the stands on them and setting up an art gallery in the living room. It took them about 30 minutes to complete each board. They are not reusable. The tools are reusable.
These are super tiny! They are super thin slices, and they are very small. I’m so worried my son will break them while examining them. They are bright colored and vivid. Just very small.

My 11 year old daughter loves elephants. This craft kit was just calling her name! It comes with supplies to make two adorable boho elephants. You stack the cardboard pieces together and then just wrap them with the yarn. It is pretty simple, but does require a certain amount of hand-eye coordination. It is perfect for a preteen. My 11 year old had zero problems following the directions on her own and completing the kit on her own.

This glue gun and I have come to an understanding, but our relationship started out pretty rough. First, it comes with two glue sticks. Just two. Not a problem, I told myself. Glue sticks can be purchased. But then I forgot to purchase said glue sticks before I sat down to do my first project with this tiny gun. No on or off switch. Plugged in is on. Unplugged is off. The tip really does turn red when it is hot. However, that does not mean the glue is ready. Nope. I slid the glue stick in before I plugged it in. Plugged it in. Waited a very long time for the tip of the glue gun to turn red. I was beginning to think that might be a myth, but it wasn't. It did turn red. Get ready to glue! Squeeze handle. Nothing. Squeeze handle. Nothing. Squeeze handle. Nothing. Squeeze handle. Nothing. Squeeze handle, meet resistance, decide to squeeze harder, handle gets stuck. Freak out. Hit it a couple times, handle pops back out. Try to squeeze handle a little more gently, this time, I realize it isn't budging. So, I sat the glue gun down and deciding to wait. That was the right move. The glue stick wasn't melted yet. Wait another 2 minutes. Pick up the glue gun and try again. This time it works! Only, the gun itself is awkward. With the stand down, it is hard to work with. So, you put the stand back up. Then you glue, go to set the gun down so you can use both hands, only now it won't stand up because the stand is up. So you have to decide if you're going to try to press the pieces together one handed or risk setting the table on fire. I chose poorly and tried to one hand it. Made a mess and burned my fingers. The next time, just risked the table. It was fine. Continued the project in this manner. Until I got to the point where I realized just how fast glue sticks go and I probably should have had more than 2 ready. Oh well. Decide to call it a day and close up shop. So, to turn the gun off, you unplug it. After several minutes, the red tip does, in fact, turn back black.
Pros Summed Up:
- It is a glue gun.
- The tip turns red when it is hot.
- Frustration is an aide in the creative process. It helps you focus on the important aspects of the project at hand.
Cons Summed Up:
- The tip is stubby and not very fine tip.
- The stand is in the wrong place and too close to the stubby tip.
- Easy to break if you get impatient and try to force the glue stick through.
- It is so light, the cord gets bumped and the whole thing falls over.

This is a great starter kit. You get so much more than it appears, too. You get two DualBrush pens. One light grey. One black. (Each dualbrush pen has one side that is a brush and the other side is a felt tip marker.) Then you get a smaller dual sided pen with a 0.8mm side and a 0.4mm side. The calligraphy pen is like a smaller version of the brush pen. It only has one side. It also comes with an unsharpened 4H drawing pencil (very hard, not dark lines, perfect for sketching under something) and a white plastic eraser. So, even though it is a 6 piece set, you really get 9 individually usable things because of the double sided pens. It also comes with a basic instruction pamphlet for hand lettering, which is good, but not comprehensive or anything. But a great beginner set or even beyond beginner. And a great trial into Tombow Brush Pens without committing to a bunch of them.

These are really excellent brushes. Well, the large oval and the medium size round brush are really great. The script brush is just lacking. It is just not the size I like to use and the bristles get unruly. But these hold an awful lot of water, which makes them so great for color mixing and painting. The oval and round brush keep their shape really well. The thickness of the handles is ideal. If this set just had a smaller round brush instead of the script brush, it would be beyond perfect.

This is my favorite ink. I use it for hand lettering with a paint brush. This particular color is thicker than other colors. I'm not sure why. You can water it down to get it thinner. The color is great.

The hooks are lovely. Really, they look pretty. However, they are a bit rough. They grab and pull yarn like crazy on the rough edging under the hook. They definitely need some fine hand sanding to get them smooth enough to be of any use for crocheting. You would kind of expect that for the price.
I love doing a lot of crafts for my family. This glue gun to me is just the best!🥰🤩

I am so frustrated and disappointed in this machine! I normally make my hats on the peg looms, and it's a piece of cake. After watching videos of the larger Sentro, I decided to order both sizes. This mini one came first, and the big one is supposed to be delivered tomorrow. I have been trying for SEVERAL hours to get this machine to cast on without dropping stitches. At first I thought maybe I was doing it wrong, so watched several tutorials. Then I thought maybe my yarn was too thin, because I noticed the needle wasn't catching the yarn and bringing it down. So I tried a different yarn, and it's the same thing. If the big one still arrives tomorrow, despite cancelling the order today, I'll give it a try,  after I send this one back. There is not one single video of anyone using the mini, and maybe this is why. The needles just don't seem to be long enough. Also, it kept getting stuck, then chugging to a start, then repeat. I'm so disappointed,  because I make hats for charity, and I really hoped this machine would help me make hats faster, so I can donate more quickly. I'm hoping it's just the mini that is defective, and the large one will work properly. I guess it's true you get what you pay for. I'll probably end up buying the more expensive competition's knitting machine, if the big one tomorrow is as bad as the mini version!😢💔

I love this little craft iron! I use it to iron on name labels on my clothes and bed pads (I'm in long term care). It heats up quickly, and cools down quickly, as well. It is extremely light weight, and would also be good to pack while traveling, for a quick touch up to wrinkled clothes. I also love that you can detach the cord for storage, so it doesn't get kinked and damaged in storage. A very reasonable price for an awesome product! I highly recommend it!

I love this needle holder! It has room for plenty of needles in any length, and it's much sturdier than the plastic tube my needles originally came in. It's also the perfect size to fit in my neck pouch, for easy and immediate access. A great price for a fabulous product! I highly recommend this!

I love these hoops! Since they are rectangular, it's easier to see the canvas when cross-stitching words. Also, they have a unique feature I've never seen with hoops. The tightening screw actually has a Phillip's head,  so you can get the frame really tight. Every other hoop I've had, you can only finger tighten it! I highly recommend these hoops!!
Very pleased.
Gorgeous ribbon and great price!  I enjoy working with this ribbon.
These little tins work great for organizing little bits for crafting.  Take the plastic insert out and use for other purposes.
Very cheaply made.  I will stick with brand name needles in the future!

Recommendation was spot on, these are nice art pens! Very juicy.
Kit seems nice if you are doing nail ornaments.
I guess it will work but is very small.
Almost a little to rusty to handle but will try to use them in my crafting anyway.

Plates and shim that came with the machine are already bent and warped but it works pretty good for dies and most embossing folders, lacks the power for some though. I go through a lot of glue on my crafts and I really like this as a middle of the road paper glue.

I would buy these again as the quality and color are just what I needed for my book covers.

Okay my fault for not reading!!! These are individual stacking trays so nothing to hold them together. I gorilla glued them together and they hold all sizes (except for the really large Archival pads) and easier than digging through a drawer!!!  Love them now!

Love this paper for making journals and ephemera. The colors are vivid and the paper is quality!

Very nice for detail paper crafting!  Really sharp so use with car!  Love mine!
I really like using these little sticks for my paste glue when paper crafting.  They are the best utensils I have found for spreading the thick kind of glue paste.
These are a real nice design for paper crafting with a wave cut instead of the sharp pinking cut. Use them a lot!

Very sturdy tool but not that useful.  I make a lot of jelly roll rugs so thought this would be a time saver...more trouble than it’s worth!  Watched the videos so it’s not that I was using it wrong it just didn’t save me any time.  Much prefer just using quilting clips.
Smells like oranges, pretty strong but the gold color is vibrant.  Just what I wanted for my paper crafting!

This stuff frays something fierce!  Maybe i should have read more reviews. Don’t know if pinking the raw edges would help.  It is nice cotton and the colors are very good and for my purposes will be Adequate!

Like these a lot once I finally received them.  Great for cutting sticky craft pieces and don’t gum up!  Really should be a 5 star except for the time it took to get them.

I had higher hopes for this art project.  Basically it is a patience testing paint by numbers premise. The diamonds are actually mostly opaque plastic circles that you adhere one at a time to a preset image that ends up a pixelated picture that doesn't quite live up to the original picture.  The process is easy enough of using the applicator to put the properly colored "diamond" onto the proper circle.  Then repeat ad nauseum.  We have done a number of these that were smaller and while most of those were completed, this one seems to have not captured anyones interest and will probably remain partially completed.  The idea is OK, but there are more inspirational art projects out there.

I used this to attach magnets to the back of some miniatures to keep them in place. This glue did the job great, but I need so much that it would end up costing me $100 to get them all done. I wish I could afford to get that many boxes of this glue, but I am going to have to use a different solution.

However, ounce for ounce rather than dollar for dollar, this product works super well. I love the flexible finish it has and it holds like crazy. If they ever have a value sized block of this, I'm in!

I really wish this was a lot thicker and more presentable for a portfolio.  It is a good organizer for art, but because it is a very thin plastic, it does not make a great presentation portfolio.  It does fill up a bit when you fill the plastic sleeves, but not something you can use for interviewing.  I am using it for storing prints which it does a good job of organizing.  9x12 makes it easy to fit prints in, but not an exact measurement I work in often.

I have a number of these bags in different finishes and they are fantastic.  Comparable products are Michaels are no where near comparable in price but the same for quality.  So many of the places I looked for these a couple years ago were $10 for 10.  This is $10 for hundreds.  Highly recommend these if you are looking for great craft gears, check out their other finishes and large mixed bags.  Great quality, great price, highly recommend these!

My oldest son is a big fan of miniature games so he loves detail brushes.  This is a great variety collection of brushes for all detail uses.  They are good quality bristles and handles.  The expanded handles are great for my fingers which fit the handle like a modified pencil giving great control.  If you are looking for a good set for all the ought numbered sizes, this is a great starter variety.

I love the idea that there are six reasonably sized projects in this bag.  The neighborhood kids made an art club over the summer and we used this as one of the activities.  It is a little tedious for younger kids and I found the end results to be less than amazing.  I wish the pieces were more dynamic and crystal like.  They are solid plastic and the end result was a dull and pixelated paint by numbers effect.  Several kids finished theirs, but it didn't seem like anyone was overwhelmed by the result.

I have been strangely attracted to little crafting bags of gears.  I always thought they would be great to make little art projects out of, but the collections were always too small and too expensive.  This bag though is a great value.  I got one as a reviewer item, and would have passed on the skulls, but the clocks, keys, cogs and gears are all great. I have since found that the company makes a wide variety of bags with different finishes and selections.  We are definitely grabbing another bag of small gears.  My oldest has been using them in the gaming miniatures and our youngest is using the keys in jewelry making.  They are great and at a great price!

This is exactly what we were hoping for!  They clip easily into the zipper mechanism.  We had one the broke last winter with sharp edges.  I cut the remainder off and attached one of these and - good as new.  Such a simple product that makes such a big difference.

This is a great set, if it fits your needs.  Unfortunately it is just one size.  We chose to get a reviewer set to augment a bigger and better kit from this company.  The parts are a little nondescript but should work fine if we end up needing #5.  Price is OK, probably better if you are in the profession.

We have had a spate of broken zippers recently and we were very excited to get this. It is very complete yet compact with lots of replacement pulls and closures.  Love that they even put in a section of thread too.  Sadly, we don't have any broken zippers now, although we did replace one pull from this bag.  I do expect to need some this winter when the coats come out and get abused and feel ready for it.  I really like this kit and it fits so easily into our sowing box.

This is by no means delux paper, it is not colored differently on each side, no foil, no frills, just good, thin, basic paper.  It creases easily and takes tears well when you need to, sometimes when you don't need it... It is great for simple designs and beginners looking to practice.  It is a generous pile at an ok price and did everything we were hoping to do with it, which was as an art activity for covid quarantine.  We spent most of last night folding and the kids started in again today, so well worth it for dollar to hour entertainment.

This is a nice little aluminum easel.  It is not the most substantial easel, but it is pretty darn stable for just being lightweight aluminum bars.  Especially for a sign stand, this is far more stable than the pole easels I usually use, but it is so light weight it still would not do great in the wind, especially with a large sign or canvas.  I had one of the kids paint a canvas on it on a tabletop and it held the canvas well and even stood up to a little heavy handed application of paint. I can see this being a decent travel easel because of its size and weight and I think I would prefer it over my other lighter weight sign easels.  So its lightweight and inexpensive in design and price and does a good job for something this light, portable and inexpensive.

If you are a crafter, you need to get one of these little guys.  I have a desktop cutting board, but I'm loving the portability of this one.  Just slightly bigger than a letter sized page and you have enough real estate for all small projects in a form that stores wonderfully in a book shelf or small drawer.  It is thick and tough and I have not doubts that it will stand up to years of cutting.  Perfect for small jobs and for protecting desk and table tops.

These are unique pens.  My oldest son has been taking watercolor at school for three years and I putter with a very tight style.  I found that these did not really work with out styles but kind of forced us into some things we probably wouldn't have done without them.  They apply the pigment thicker than I would and although you can blend them well, I found you needed to work the applied paint far differently than I usually do.  We had a lot of fun playing with these and seeing what we could do.  In the end, he claimed them and took them off to use for class projects.  I look forward to see what he can do with them.

In general, we love every Klutz craft kit we have gotten over the years. This one strangely did not capture the imagination of the kids, despite being the best and most successful crystal growing kit we have had. However, like most efforts like this, our crystals did not live up to those in the pictures.

It is nice that this creates jewelry, albeit pretty fragile jewelry. The end products were nice. But it was more the journey than the destination that was important here. We did have some fun creating solutions and shaping and finishing the jewelry, but the end product probably will not be treasured. So four stars for the activity, less for the product.

With the passing of our mother and grandmother, we were looking for a way to make a slightly different holiday this year with new traditions. We are hoping that a change to the normal tree would be in order and adding a tree to the decorations seems like a nice way to bring a fun new setup chore for grandpa and the grandkids. I think this will do the job very well except for one thing, the track dimensions are a little small.

We know and love Lionel so I guess the sticker price did not shock me. It does seem a little steep, but the engine and cars are solidly made. I am a little less pleased with the quality of the decorations like the gifts and trees, but all in all this is a very good set. Other Lionels we have are pretty vintage and have a much older style of track that does not go together as well and to be honest, this new track looks better. I love that there are Bluetooth controls, welcome to the twenty-first century Lionel. The kids love the controls and loved to play with it.

I do wish the set was a little more generous with track, and a little more generous with packing space. It is pretty tightly packed and it turned out that it took more work to get it back into the box than getting it out. I think we will have to move it to a new storage container after the holidays are over. Otherwise, we couldn't be happier, a little old tradition, a little new tradition, a little bit of working together to set it up.

If there is a professional grade scissors, this is it. They just exude quality from the comfort of the right handed grip to the smoothness of the cutting action. I would say that these are to best scissors I have ever owned, but I actually think they are the best scissors I have ever used...and I have worked in graphic arts for a decent amount of time.

Their smoothness allows for a lot of fine work. They are a little big, perhaps a little over built for small stuff. They almost feel like kitchen shears for size and heft, but they are so finely engineered that they handle great on detail work. I have never been happier with a pair of scissor. The kids better not used these are wire cutters.

If success of a toy is rated by the fun it provides, this proved to be a great activity. Both my sons made a couple creations with the beads and proudly displayed them. They were familiar with the product while I was not, and I'm happy with the together time we spent creating animals. There is plenty of creative flexibility and opportunity for creative outlet.

I am now aware that these pieces are considerably bigger than the normal beads, almost the difference between legos and Duplo blocks. They seem a little harder to find refills for the beads but they work well for smaller hands. They are incredibly easy to use and arrange, and I'm impressed at how well the creations stay together once fused with an iron. All in all, I am very happy with this product.
Nice color.  It saturated the stamp very well.  I like the pine green color.  It's perfect for Xmas cards.  I bought the stamp and postcards from Amazon, too.

In middle of a project, left some glaze on the glass, when i returned it was on solid. I spritzed a little water, used my scraper and it wiped right off!  Best purchase ever!

Although it’s meant as a knitting bag, I use it to carry my crafts in it when i go to a paper crafting gathering!  It works perfectly for multiple uses! Don’t limit yourself to passing this by, before you think of how it may be useful for your need. It’s sturdy and easy to take everywhere!

These colored pencils came packaged with great care there was no way for them to shift in the Box which made them come all in one piece there were no broken tips the case is made out of canvas which is hand-stitched each pencil has its own elastic which holds it in place the pencils are separated by color and shade which makes easier picking for the right shade if pencils are easy to use they leave behind no stroke marks you do not have to bear down hard on them to produce color they are a great value the accessories alone cost what the whole set costs so it's like getting the pencils for free they would make a great gift for to keep for yourself the one thing I would say that could improve the set is to have a color chart that has the numbers corresponding with the name of the colors I definitely would recommend this product

Washi tape doesn't come with tons of tape, thats pretty standard. These are standard rolls. I found they had plenty to offer!  Fun and beautiful. Went well with some naturalistic paper stickers I had bought!

I can't believe how this product has changed my card making!  I read about the pen on Splitcoasters. I was skeptical but I owned at least a dozen white gel pens and Uniball white pens... and NONE ever worked for me.  They are now in the garbage.  This pen has worked from the minute I opened it.  The flow is even and opaque.  You can make an even row of white dots, each dot will be perfectly uniform.  If you have a little booboo in your white embossing, it can be EASILY corrected with this pen.  I am finding I am trusting it to add last minute touches/emphasis to all my Copic coloring.  Truly a great accessory to crafting.

No more problems counting lines!  This gizmo is so easy.  It fits on your thumb, you reach the end of a line, a quick turn and you are onto your next line.  The turn mechanism is perfect, not too tight that it can't be easily turned, and not too loose that you have to worry about an extra line being added or subtracted.  Truly an invaluable little tool in my knitting bag.

This zipper was so easy to install that I was amazed!  It’s very well made and isn’t going to break. If you have the broken zipper pull, look for a single digit size number on it, mine was a number 8.  That will make it easier to order the right size replacement zipper.  This great replacement pull saved my very expensive motorcycle jacket!

So easy to use these staple type magnets. Measure twice, cut once, insert magnet, fold over prongs, do the other side and you’re done. Nine it works pretty well and keeps my bag closed. Looks like it was installed as part of the pocketbook design. I’m using this for other bags soon.

The nylon bristle brushes with the lighter handles are more like Chinese calligraphy brushes, rough coarse nylon bristles..  The dark handle white bristle brushes are what you're buying this set for if you're painting, with finer  softer bristles.  The canvas holder is useful as many reviewers have noted, so just remove the lighter handle brushes if you're not needing calligraphy brushes and use the central holders for your other brushes. It rolls up into a 3 inch wide cylinder about a foot long. Top flap Velcro seals on either sIde.

I wanted a set of plain black buttons so I would have spares when I inevitably lose a button or two, and this set is exactly what I needed. It has a good variety of buttons in good quantities. It is not a huge set but there's enough to keep in a travel sewing kit.

This set has several sized of basic black buttons, sorted in a plastic separator container. All are 4-hole buttons and the sizes are fairly standard. If there isn't one that's an exact match, it's not hard finding one that is "close enough" to work without being obvious.

These seem to be well made - hard plastic with enough distance between the sew holes that they should not break in the middle under strain (I have seen it happen).

I can't complain with this set. It is a basic set of black buttons in assorted sizes that are well made. The price is very reasonable, and the sorted separator box makes this an easy set to recommend.

The very best utility knife I have ever used is a Zoid, and I try to keep some extra blades for it around. Since these are also Zoid brand I was hopeful they'd be better than other brands I have seen and used. So far they're not really superior but they're certainly no worse than the best blades I have used from other manufacturers.

These are trapezoid-shaped blades and fit cutters that use that type of blade. I have a utility knife by Sunex that takes this kind of blade and these fit it just fine.

I like that these blades some in a small, plastic case. This keeps them together and keeps them from causing damage to other things.

In use, these seem to be just as durable and sharp as other blades I have used. There's not too much that can be said otherwise. After cutting cardboard boxes for a while (a task that is notorious for dulling blades) the first blade is still sharp which is all I can really ask. With 10 in this pack, I have enough blades to last me for many months (and probably well into another year).

These are fine blades that work well. I use them with my Zoid utility knife, but any knife that takes a trapezoid blade can take these. They seem to be made well and they do hold their edge for a while, even under some tough use.

One of the best utility knives I have ever used is a Zoid, so replacement blades for it are something I needed. These do the job for me and work very well.

This is a 5-pack of utility blades, for use in a knife that takes trapezoid blades. If you have a snap-off blade knife, these will not work for you, but there are other blades available that will fit your knife.

Out of the package, my blades were coated in a very light film of lubricating oil. The only concern here is that you have to be careful where you set these down while you're putting them in your knife, as they can stain fabric and clothing with oil if you're not careful.

These are nice and sharp and they make quick work of cardboard, paper, vinyl and other materials. My knife is often used to open packages and cut open cardboard boxes and these blades work great for that.

I have no complaints with these blade and they work very well. They fit my Zoid utility knife perfectly and there's enough of them in the package to keep me in blades for a good while.

I selected this utility knife after having a good impression of another Zoid knife, which has proven to be one of the best utility knives I have ever used. I like this one too.

There are a couple of different types of utility knives. One type is a fixed blade and generally is the least safe. Another is a retractable blade and that is my usual preference. The third type I have seen is the swing-out blade, similar to a pocket knife, and that is the type of knife this one is.

In terms of build quality and design, this is a solid and well-made utility knife. It has some good weight to it and it feels solid. The contours are good for grip - this is important, especially when you are working with wet hands (not recommended but sometimes it is unavoidable). The blade swings out (not spring-assisted like many pocket knives) and locks into place solidly. It comes with one blade and it's very sharp.

One really nice feature is the wire cutter which is just below the exposed part of the blade. It makes hooking and cutting wire, twine and string easy without touching the main part of the blade, though some caution is still required since the main part of the blade is still exposed.

I like this utility knife and have added to the tool bag I keep in my basement where it will see some use and will save me from having to go upstairs to get my other utility knife. It's well made and feels very solid.

I frequently have need of a utility knife like this one. I selected this one because it looked like a good one and the cost was not prohibitive. This has quickly proven to be the best utility knife I have used so far. It is comfortable to hold and it has some good features.

I like the feel of it in my hand. It has a pistol-grip shape of sorts, rather than being a straight handle and shaft, so that it can be held and gripped a little more confortably. Though the body is mostly metal it does have a couple of larger rubberized areas that make it more comfortable to grip and even provide something to grip with the knife is well (though I do not recommend using it with wet hands, regardless). It has some heft to it too.

The handle has a compartment for blade storage and right out of the package the blade is stored there. Installation took no time at all and I was ready to go with this knife in less than 30 seconds. The blade clicks into place and feels secure.

The blade can be completely retracted or it can be extended to three settings so that it's only extended as far as is needed. This is a nice feature. However, the neatest feature is that this knife also has a safety cutter built right in. There's a notch under the blade housing that leaves a short section of blade exposed, but it is deep enough in the housing so it is impossible to touch the blade with a finger. This is great for cutting string, tape and other things, without needing to open the knife up.

The handle has a built in clip so it can be snapped to a chain or tool belt.

I really like this utility knife. It seems to be made thoughtfully and it has some really cool features. This is the utility knife I carry in my tool bag now.

I have wanted/needed a glue gun for a while. I like Dremel as a brand so I was excited to try this glue gun. So far it has worked out for me, though I have not used it for a major job yet.

This is a smaller glue gun than those with which I have worked in the past, and there are pluses and minuses to that. On one hand the smaller size makes the unit lighter and easier to hold which is especially helpful if you have small hands. The smaller nozzle also means using less glue and being able to get into slightly more cramped spaces. The downside is that because it uses less glue and has a smaller nozzle, some jobs require more effort and time.

Unlike some glue guns, this one runs on battery power and is rechargeable. It has a microUSB port where it can be plugged in for charging (whether this was the best choice for input is a different discussion). Because it does not need to be plugged in during use, it can be used when away from power sources or when there is a power outage.

This is an easy-to-understand and east-to-use unit. Power it one, let it warm up, squeeze the trigger and apply. That's really all there is to it. It's well-suited for small repairs, hobby work, and even decoration/artwork.

I am happy with this glue gun and will be working with it more. So far it has performed well for me.

I selected these pens with the idea of using them for rock art. I was looking specifically for multi-surface pens with bold colors, with the idea that using said pens would work better for me than paint and brushes. So far, I am pleased with these pens and likely will buy another set when they’re done.

This is a set of 12 pens, each a different color (meaning, no two blacks or blues). The colors are bright and bold without being neon or too vibrant, well suited for working on a number of projects. These write well on every surface I have tried - rocks, glass, paper, cardboard, and even vinyl and metal. These are ideal for crafts.

These are not completely waterproof and require some additional work in order to maintain coverage when wet. The packaging says baking; that’s not always practical. A light coating of varnish or sealant does the trick for me.

So far these are working well for me and I am happy with them. When I’ve used them up, I likely will buy another set.

I selected these pens for use in rock art. I was attracted by the bright metallic colors and the quantity of pens in the pack. These work and I like them but these are not good as a stand-alone marker for writing on rocks or anything that comes near water.

I'll get right to the point - the ink is not waterproof after it dries and as a result the colors run when they get wet. This isn't a deal-breaker by any means but it is important to keep in mind. If the idea is to use them for rock art that will be outside, or if writing on a coffee mug or similar surface, apply a finish afterward to make the ink remain when it gets wet or you'll end up with a mess.

Otherwise, I like these. The colors are bright enough for my purposes and I like the coverage. They write smoothly and they dry fairly quickly. I think these would be most useful in a classroom when working on construction paper, though I can see other uses as well. Several reviewers have mentioned trying these on fabric; I have not tried this so I cannot say if this works or not.

These are not perfect pens but they do the job and I like them. Just remember that if your work is going to get wet after you're finished, apply a finishing layer to it to keep it from running.

I am not an especially skillful creative artist though I do technical stuff that turns out reasonably well. There have been occasions when having a cut-out would come in handy, particularly when making replacement cards for certain games. I looked to this nifty cutting machine to help me, and so far it has worked out well though I have a lot to learn about it.

There is quite a learning curve and I have had a few battles with this device as I have tried to work through how to use it properly. It does not help that there was no manual in the box; I had to go out to the Brother website and download it in order to have the manual. While that's all right, it is inconvenient and it means that it is not so easy to grab the manual and read it when I am not near a computer. Once I had the manual in hand (so to speak), I have been able to learn this machine's basic functions and I have made some progress with it.

This is a large unit, about the size of an economy inkjet printer though smaller than a laser printer. It is not something you can simply put on the corner of a desk and it will be out of the way; plan to give this unit some real estate.

This unit has a built-in scanner so you can scan in your own work, but it also has a good library of pre-existing images that you can use as well. I am primarily interested in the scanning aspect of this unit so I have concentrated on using the scanner, and so far it was worked out reasonably well. It scans my image as expected and it cuts them properly, even when I have had areas that would require a delicate hand when cutting.

What really surprised me is that this unit is remarkably quiet, even when cutting card stock. I expected more noise than it makes, so the quiet operation was a very pleasant surprise.

I have not worked too closely with the software yet though I have installed it and figured out the basics. So far it has been stable, reasonably easy to understand and I haven't run into any limitations that get in my way. That said, more advanced users probably will find some point of contention with the software - par for the course with most packages included with hardware.

So, with this machine I can make fancy cards and embellishments for gifts which is my main interest. Others who are more creatively inclined are more likely to get much more use out of it, for scrapbooking, making labels, designing vinyl logos and much more. This is a neat little machine that is underutilized in my hands; in the hands of someone with more ideas and more crafting ability, this device will open up a world of opportunities.

I have always enjoyed coloring and my preference has been with colored pencils over markers or paints (and as a child I preferred colored pencils to crayons). I have a set of colored pencils I have used for some time, a set of Prang pencils which are relatively inexpensive and give me comparable results to more expensive pencil sets. I was very curious to see how well this set of iVenus pencils would compare. So far they have compared very favorably.

I have the set of 36 pencils and there is a very good variety of shades in the box. There are larger sets of pencils available and while convenient you likely will still have to blend colors to get the shades you want on occasion, regardless of the size of the set. For my amateur efforts, a smaller set like this is satisfactory. It is also good for someone learning to draw and color without having to make a huge investment in pencils.

These come in a very nice tin that is not only attractive but it also protects the pencils from damage. The tin has a plastic trough insert that holds them all in place when the tin is closed, and the tin itself is beautifully decorated, suitable as a gift.

When drawing with these pencils, I have found the coverage to be smooth and somewhat creamy. I have been able to get fine point coverage as well as wider shading without too much effort. They blend nicely and coverage is nice and uniform – no skips and no chunks.

Also with this set of pencils is a water-based blending brush. I decided to give this a try as I had not worked with such before now. The colors are somewhat water soluble and the brush made blending the colors exceptionally easy while still leaving line definitions. I have not tried this with my Prang set and I do not expect it to work with them. Before working with this set, I did not understand what watercolor pencils were – now I know and these are more versatile than typical colored pencils.

This is not a typical set of cheap colored pencils. As colored pencils these are better than what you will find outside of arts & craft stores (or online), but the ability to blend the colors with a small amount of water makes coloring and drawing with pencils a completely different experience. If you like working with colored pencils and you like watercolor as well, this set of pencils combines both. Even if you do not care about the watercolor aspect, these are fine pencils worth their asking price.

Disclosure: I was provided a sample of this product in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received this product at no/minimal cost I have rated and reviewed this product without bias, based solely on my experience with it.

A good pair of scissors is a very valuable asset for anyone who is involved in crafts. However, even a good pair of scissors might be ill-suited for the job depending on what you are doing. If you work on textile crafts, such as knitting, crocheting or embroidery, a huge pair of scissors designed to cut fabric might get in the way more than it helps. This is a small pair of scissors meant for small jobs like trimming threads and it is as sharp and functional as it is attractive.

In terms of looks, these are very attractive, with stainless steel blades and oversized brass-colored handles. They look a little awkward to hold at first, but even someone like me with large hands will find these to be very comfortable and easy to hold and handle. There is an image on each handle, one of a peacock and the other of a dragon (I think) and they add to the attractiveness.

These are reversible scissors and as such are usable by either hand. That is, left-handed or right-handed people will be perfectly comfortable with these scissors.

These are very sharp and I have been able to use them to trim loose strings and frayed edges on fabrics. They cut cleanly and they do not require you to work when cutting. Now, that said, the length of the blades dictates that these are ill-suited for large jobs like cutting fabrics beyond something like cornering. A larger pair of scissors, or a seam ripper, is a better tool for that kind of job.

These are very well made. The hinge is tight enough to keep the blades from wobbling back and forth and the blades are attached well to the handles such they do not feel loose nor do they wiggle.

These are very nice and very attractive scissors. They are packaged suitably for gifting, too, which makes them a gift great choice if you have a crafter in your family. They are made well and should last for many years.

Disclaimer: I was provided a sample of this product in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received this product at no cost I have rated and reviewed this product without bias, based solely on my experience with it.

I am the first to admit that I am very much an amateur when it comes to painting and artwork. I am trying to learn, though, and as I practice my abilities are slowly improving. Most of the work I have done has been on a digital tablet (an Intuos5 Medium, to be very specific) but I have worked some with colored pencils and more recently with paint. These panels are the first cotton canvases I have used and so far I am very happy with them.

These boards are cut to 8"x10" and this is an ideal size for me. With a larger canvas I find myself a bit overwhelmed with trying to figure out the coverage on the canvas. I suppose that will change as I gain more experience and confidence but for now that’s a big deal for me. These are just the right size - slightly smaller than a sheet of paper, I don’t have to “think big”.

As I run my hand across these panels, they are smooth and without deformity. They do have texture - they’re cotton weave, after all - but there is no uneven/oversized threads, no lumps, no loose ends and no sharpness. The weave is very tight and uniform. That means paint coverage is smooth and does not get impacted by the surface of the canvas in unfavorable ways.

Finally, I want to emphasize the pricing on these. As I write this, these are selling for $22 for a pack of 12 which is less than $2 per canvas. Considering that these are made very well and are even and smooth, this is a good deal. There are less expensive canvas packs out there, but these I know to be excellent from working with them.

Unfortunately, these don’t make me a better artist, but at least I get to practice more.

If you are a beginning artist like me, or if you are an experienced artist looking for a high-quality pack of smaller canvases, these are a great product for your needs. They are made very well and are priced very reasonably when considering the quality.

Disclaimer: I was provided a sample of this product in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received this product at no cost I have rated and reviewed this product without bias, based solely on my experience with it.

I am very much a beginner when it comes to art and drawing. I know the basics of pencil sketching and I do look for tools that will help me along in my learning. The Mead Academie set of 12 sketching pencils is a nice set that is very well-suited for beginning artist and those learning to draw.

I have a nice set of Prang colored pencils which I use for color drawing. The Mead set is my first all-black set of pencils. With 12 pencils varying in level of blackness and hardness it is easy to find the right shade of gray for shadows and outlines. I have not used these very long at all - I just received them less than a week ago - but so far I have been pleased with the way they mark the paper, I like the way the graphite wears when drawing (it does not crumble as I draw and it does not snap too easily when properly sharpened) and I like the fact that these come in a protective tin.

So far I can find only one improvement I’d make and it’s a personal preference, not a fault. I prefer writing and drawing tools with some weight to them as they feel more substantial in my hand. These pencils are very lightweight, a little lighter than I am used to. Again, this is me being picky, not a problem with the pencils. It’s easy enough to add weight to them.

This is a very nice set of black sketching and drawing pencils with a good selection of hardness and shade that is suitable for most drawing. It’s an affordable set too (about $15 as I write) which puts it in reach of almost everyone’s budget. This is a nice set that’s worth a look if you want an affordable set of pencils for artwork.
Nice scrapbook, great variety of embellishments and well worth the price. Colors would work well for several different occasions and could be used for male or female project pages.

Nice quality selection of really small flowers.The flowers are pretty small and may not be the best choice for people with limited hand and finger mobility. They can be difficult to work with, but may do well in flower layering as the center flower under a brad
Very nice quality pink stones. Good clear pink color, nice size and seem to be well made. These would go well on a table at a shower, or in crafting applications.

Bought these flowers and was very pleased with the style, color and quantity. The various options give you versatility in products placement. They build well with each other and other flower crafting brands. Can be glued down, taped with double sides tape or zots. In addition they work well with brads. We will definitely be buying more.

Nice variety of precut frames. No need to punch them out or worry that your hand cut frames sizes will be incorrectly sized or uneven. Colors are neutral enough to be easily added to a variety of paper crafting.
Great selection of family related stickers. Nice color, variety of fonts and sizes. Nice thickness.

Greal selection of sentiments accented in cool tones. It's a good variety of appropriate worded and designed nicely to work on card, scarp pages or any other craft project when you need to express concern, sympathy, and encouragement. They are nice size and you could easily apply this to a set of two or four scrap pages. Would also work separately for cards. Well made
Well made, with lots of attention to detail. The clarity and color, and thickness on each piece is nicely done. The medals, plane, stars and other pieces are perfect for a Marines memorial page.

Great colbalt blue color accents. Especially like the detailed pattern on the grey tone cap. The thickness and coordinating ribbon is a nice touch on the diploma. The gold star bust looking item with the ribbon is a unique piece. Each item is nicely layered and accented. You will not be disappointed. We have several other brands graduation sticker sets and this k & company set is the nicest quality and best put together. Wish there were other accent color combinations.

These floral gem pieces are just what we were looking for. Previously we has to purchase them with silk flowers. This set is a great option. The color is clear & provides a great sparkle. Nice quality and a good quality for the price.
These are really Nicky made Tags which would be perfect for ancestry scrapbook or heritage pages. Colors are nice. Well made and nice thickness.
Bought these for the crafters in the family. Really nice clear colors, well made & great value. Great for scrapbooking, cards & other family craft projects.

The quality is nice and it's a true brilliant white color. We purchased it as a gift to our son and new daughter in law. The silver square for the couples photo on front is a nice touch. You will not be disappointed.
One of the three rings does not close properly. No way to modify for approximate closure. Item will need to be returned.
Nice colors, reasonable price. Quality of paper weight great, our family loves dcwv products. This is the second set of this item we purchased. Our daughter loves it for circuit works
Versatile stickers and vintage papers for my art and craft projects.  These are easy to handle and fun. I liked the ease of handling and the variety provided me with options for creative work.

So unique! These jelly stickers are beautiful pastel colors. Various sizes. 5yr old granddaughter loved using these to make her class Valentine cards.
Will buy again for future crafting projects.
Every little girl will love these.

These small enamel charms are perfect to make jewelry for little girls. Good detail.  I am adding a jump ring to each & putting them in plastic for the Easter Egg hunt. Can’t wait to see the happy faces.

Unfortunately, the case is too small for these pencils. The tips are right up against the zipper so they break every time you remove one. If the case were 1” wider & 1” longer it would be great.
These pencils require a lot of pressure to apply color. They do not have “creamy” lead. I usually color for at least an hour. My finger tips ache from applying pressure.
The colors are lively.

This is a great coloring book for those of us who love small, detailed patterns. Relaxing & challenging. I use gel pens, primarily and enjoy the level of attention and control required to ‘color inside the lines’. This collection of 72 images is just what I have searching for. love it!

If you enjoy large print adult coloring books, you will not enjoy this.

These gel pens came without the usual plastic snap case. The arrived clipped to a strip of plastic.
The pens themself are great, but 36 loose pens is a pain in the butt. Especially since all my other pens arrived in a snap case. Sounds petty, but it annoyed the hell out of me.
Great pens.
Terrible bundle.

Disappointed when this arrived. I love to color fine line, detail mandalas with gel pens. These mandalas are proportioned for children to color with crayons or markers - the lined areas are too large with a lot of white space. Here is a photo showing the large white areas compared to the tip of a standard gel pen. The majority of the 50 designs are like this.
I am including a photo of the most detailed mandala in the book. There are just a handful of these within the 50.
On the plus side, the covers are thick & sturdy. The spiral bound top is great. The paper is a perfect weight & absorbency for gel pens or water color pens.
imo, This does not provide the intricate, fine line detail that avid adult colorers are looking for. Not worth $22.

This adult coloring is, hands down, my favorite. The spiral top & size are perfect for maneuvering to access every section of the detailed designs, without smudging, no matter which brand of gel pen I use. (I have a couple of sets that do smudge on other books). The thickness of the lines is just right to guide without being bold.
The intricately detailed designs, which require small motion pen stokes, is exactly what I like. It takes me up to 4 hrs to complete one design. The designs are original & challenge me to plan a pleasing color palette. I love the challenge.
The paper weight & quality is wonderful for gel pens. The front & back cardboard covers are thick & sturdy.
I hesitated to pay $15 for the small size, but so happy I did. I have already ordered the other books from this artist, as well as another copy of this one.

I had previously bought the traditional large round paint brushes for pre-schoolers, which my granddaughter seemed to like. Then we made a birdhouse & used my flat wide acrylic brushes to paint it. She loved my brushes. I was delighted to see how easy they were for her to use. Since she can be a bit aggressive 🤣 when painting, I bought 2 sets of these for her. She has so much fun using them with her non-toxic water based paints. She has discovered the variety of effects she can create by using the various edges/angles of flat brushes. I highly recommend this little set for little hands of your creative young ones. Oh, yes, super easy to clean. We have had no problem with strands falling out. Great value

The colors are exactly as shown. However, this is very thin paper. I would never hold it’s shape. Fir example, I could not use this to make a paper ring chain. I expect construction paper to be thicker & somewhat stiff. If you need construction paper for cutting & “constructing”, I suggest trying a different brand.
If you want colored paper that is comparable to the thin pages of a kids coloring book, this would do the trick

I love everything about this set of brushes. Variety of shapes. Both soft & stiff brushes included. Great storage case that can be setup to stand I hold your brushes conveniently while you paint. I am so happy with this purchase.

I have purchased gel pens from a variety of brands, over the years. This set has the best selection of colors, so far. 100 distinct colors - no duplicates. Also, this is the first set that includes 3 actual reds. Usually, you dont get a ‘real red’, let alone 3 variations  of real red (dark, med & light). love it.

Hands down, these are the best gel pens I have used. I do a lot of adult coloring & am delighted at how long lasting this ink is. It rolls out smoothly & is easy to coax to the line.
Vibrant colors.
The glitter pens are shimmery & smooth.
The metallic silvers, gold & bronze are true metallic - amazing.

This is the brand I will be using from now on. I have been using these for months & none of them have run out of ink, yet. Do not hesitate to try these.
Really good price. Might be easier / best suited for grammar school kids. Pre-schooler is having a hard time getting the precise angle needed to get them to cut.

These are the perfect size for little toddler hands. Painting with small sponge brush is so easy and fun for toddler. Not nearly as messy or frustrating ad brushes. The shape lets toddlers explore how the various edges apply and move paint on paper. Lots of fun!!!
Easy peasy cleanup. Re-useable.

What a great case for my Prisma colored pencils, which I want to protect. I love the design features:

1. Zipper extends into the case allowing the case to lie perfectly flat when completely unzipped

2. The elastics are snug which means as they stretch out from use, they will still be tight enough to grip each pencil.

3. There is one wider elastic band that holds my pencil sharpener.

4. The case is fabric with stiff inserts, so it maintains it’s boxy shape and never touches the soft tips when closed.

5. The zipper is well stitched and opens/closes smoothly

6. There is a handy 3” long loop tap

7. Love the bright color.

8. Great price for this high quality case.

These watercolor pencils are so versatile. Wet or dry, the colors are beautiful. They are soft and easy to apply and blend. I really love the feel of these pencils.
The thing I dont like is the case. The pencils are stored in layered, thin plastic trays which are diffucult to remove without spilling the pencils. The thin trays are not strong and I dont expect them to last.
This large set Does Not include a blender, which is a big CON in my opinion. I just assumed the blender would be in the set - nope!
If this set came in a roll-up tie pouch or fold out hard container and included a blender penciI, would give it 5 stars.

Just what I wanted. Good corrugated texture on a thin card stock. Deep color saturation. Cuts nicely with a good pair of sewing shears - no torn or frayed edges.
Minus 1 star because the package photo indicates there is an orange sheet. Mine does not contain orange or black.

Used this to protect a house number plaque from CA sun. The plaque was already intalled on the house so I could not lay it flat to spray.  This product is a bit watery when sprayed on and tends to drip if you dont apply a very light coat. I ended up applying very light coats, 5 times. Built up a nice protective surface.  Very clear and shiny. Strong odor - best applied outdoors. There is a lot of product in this small can. It’s been a few weeks now of hot desert sun and so far, great protection. No cracking, peeling or discoloration.

Gorgeous colors with a lovely sheen. This polyester thread is super strong and has just a toych of stretch to it. I am using this 20 spool set for hand embroidery and cross stitch. I triple the strands to get a perfect hand stitching weight. I love that this thread does not fray or tangle. The price for 20 spools is remarkable as compared to what it would cost me to purchase the same amount of tradirional floss.
The seller included 10 mesh spool covers as a free gift, which was a nice surprise. Includes a color and conversion chart and each spool has a large sticker on top with nice large color number - very easy to read. This is packaged in a sturdy box which comes in handy if you dont own a spool rack.

I will definitely be buying more New Brohead embroidery thread from this seller.

Wow! Wish I knew about this years ago. Very easy to assemble. Nice smooth hoops. Love the groove on the hoops - really keeps my fabric tight. There is plenty of room between the 3 pegs to allow my hands to move freely while sitting in a comfortable position. Best of all, my left hand/wrist is pain free at the end of multi-hours needle point session. No cramping. My work goes a lot faster with this hoop system.

The colors of the floss are gorgeous. The pattern is nicely stamped onto the cloth. The cloth is firm and easy to work with within your hoop. I found this kit to be pretty easy to comple within a week. I really love the color combos of the final product. I still cant believe what an incredible deal this was.
FYI: Be aware there are 2 pattern/color keys. To work this kit, you first find the color key on the paper pattern, then match it to the key on the cloth, by the corresponding number. (Dont try to match the color on the stamped pattern to the floss. There are a number of color kit options to purchase, so the paper pattern key reflects the color combo you chose and will be your primary guide)
There are no english instructions, but as long as you remember the paper pattern is your primary key, you will be fine. Hope you enjoy your kit as much as I did.

Just what I needed. Fits my middle finger perfectly and does not interfer with my needle work at all.  I really like the soft silicone. Very comfortable and sturdy enough to push any size needle through any fabric. I forget it’s there. I will never use a conventional thimbal again. Very happy with this purchase.
Vivid colors. Well made. Nice long length. Easy for young kids to manipulate.
Gorgeous color. Great price. Knits nicely (baby blanket).  I purchased 4 skeins and they are all the identical color. I will definitely be trying some of the other colors for future projects.
Vibrant color. The wire in the ribbon is strong and very flexible.  It's easy to mold it into the shape you want and it retains it's shape.

Unfortunately, there are 2 issues with this kit.
1. The ink stamping is not 'within the lines', meaning there are partial x's stamped outside the border of the pattern.
2. The ink did not wash out, at all. Put it thru the wash with detergent 4 times after completing the cross stitching.
Since there were stamped blue ink marks outside the completed cross stitched pattern on the bib, and the ink does not wash out, I was unable to give this as a baby shower gift as I intended. Tiny cross stitching took a lot of time to complete. Very disappointed.

Excellent quality. Vivid colors.  Great saturation. Used this set to decorate onsies at a baby shower with 16 people. All of the colors were used and none of the markers dried out.  Very easy to apply the color to the material.  None of the colors bled or smeared when rubbed.  The zipper carry case is very sturdy and handy.  Very, very happy with this purchase.

I prefer the plastic hoops to the wood hoops - no rough edges or worries about snagging delicate material or silk threads.  Also, once the hoop is in place and tightened, I can give the fabric a little tug here and there to get that smooth taunt fit without fear of any snagging.  The screw mechanisms are easy to tighten/loosen and work very smoothly.  The plastic if very pliable making it easy to attach the hoop to fabrics of varying thicknesses. Nice variety of sizes.  The storage bag is a nice bonus

I am so happy with this fabric. The print is adorable, the detail and colors of the duck pattern is wonderful and the fabric is super soft. This is going to be the perfect backing for a baby quilt I am making. love, love, love it

Oh my god, this is so adorable. The pattern is fully stamped on the material with no gaps at all. I love that the colored threads are bundles on a sorting card that is clearly marked making it easy to identify the correlating colors to the pattern. The sorting card seems sturdy and will probably keep all the thread/colors organized until I am done, but just in case, I took a photo with my phone :-)
The threads are richly colored and the colors match the product photo shown on Amazon. Directions appear to be easy to follow. I like the sturdy plastic bag the kit comes in - will use it to store and keep clean as work on this project. I would definitely order other kits from this company.
This is my first cross stitch project and I am looking forward to getting started. I bought hoops to help me along and extra needles. It's a pretty large piece with nicely shaded coloring so I think it will probably take me 3+ months to complete (grandchild #1 due in July). Cant wait to see how this turns out.

I have small hands and I am a fan of Xuron pliers. They are light weight, comfortable to grip and easy to use to manipulate small findings and delicate wires. The precision of the grooves for the 3 crimp sized is excellent. I never struggle to grease or pinch a crimp with this tool. Admittedly, this Crimping Plier is almost 3x more expensive than generic pliers, but the quality, precision and hand-comfort of this tool make it well worth the cost ,to me.
I have many different brand of pliers and cutters that I have collected over the years. Xurons are my favorites. Even when I work at my bench for 3 or more hours at a time, I never have sore/tired hands when I am using Xuron tools. If you can afford the price and have small hands, I highly recommend this tool.

I have been using old large mouse pads as my beading mats for quite some time. (ha!) The older type mouse pads are a thick and spongy, so the beads dont bounce, but depending on the bead they do sometimes roll around when trying to snag them with a needle. I saw these at a very good price on Amazon and decided to give them a try. They are wonderful! Beads do not roll around. No more chasing beads with a needle and I love the 3 different colors, light , medium and dark, which makes it so easy to see the beads for any project. I do lay this pad on top of my old mouse pad, which raises up my working surface nicely and provides a nice wrist cushion. An added bonus for me - they roll up and travel beautifully.
I am really happy I stumbled upon these beading pads. Cant believe how inexpensive that are. Definitely recommend you give them a try.

I was looking for a travel-size bead board that I could pop into my overhead roller-bag. This also fits nicely into a larger shoulder bag or standard size tote. I have a large Beadsmith bead board that I love and this smaller one is perfect for travel. Nicely flocked, sits level on your table with a construction that is firm enough to hold it's shape and remain level when you pick it up. The clear, snap-on cover fits snugly around the edges and across the top, keeping all the beads securely in place. I would not attempt to use the cover with beads larger than 8mm - I think if you tried to force the cover on you would damage it (or, it would not attach securely and you may end up with a beads-everywhere disaster :-(
This is perfect!  I was getting so frustrated trying to find a silicone mold for round mm sizes for my jewelry making.  So happy to find this 'pastry' mold on Amazon.  Thanks!

Nice set of tools. Delicate and well made - great for my jewelry crafting needs.  Comes in a soft clear plastic roll-case, which is nice for storing.  I think this is a really nice set of tools at a very competitive price. I recommend

PROS:

2. The tray with cover is nice an thin - no 'wasted/unused' space within the tray, so you can easily stack a number of these in a bookcase shelf
3. Easy grip containers - you can easlity remove 1 container without disturbing others.
4. Clear, clear plastic - so nice to be able to see the 'real' color and shape of your beads without opening
5. Nice fliptop - easy to open without spilling and snaps close, securely.
6. The design is wonderful - you can purchase different size containers to customize for your needs.
Each pak you purchase includes the exact number of containers to fill 1 column.

I do not have any CONS. Definitely recommend.
