I am so happy I stumbled upon the Darice 45-Piece Elizabeth Ward Bead Storage Solution, which contains boxes in EACH of the available sizes.

CUSTOMIZABLE SYSTEM:
I love the fact that I can purchase packages of any of the different size boxes, to suit my current project.
Each pak you buy contains the exact number needed to fill 1 row of the storage tray. Genius!

SECURE LOCKING LID:
Love the overall design, especially the lid-lock-handles. Once you snap them shut, they stay locked and the lid keeps all the tiny boxes securely in place.

INDIVIDUAL BOXES:
The individual boxes are made of high quality clear plastic - you can clearly see the color and cut of the beads without opening the box.
Each box has an easy-grip design that allows you to remove 1 box from any location in the tray without disturbing any of the others.
They have secure, attached flip-tops that are easy to open. I use the end of my bead-scoop to pop open the 'snap' lid.
Each box is flat on all sides which makes for easy access - they stand up or lay on their side, without tipping or warbling. No worries about accidentally spilling beads. I can reach in and remove 1 bead at a time or use my shovel handled bead-scoop. depending on the size of the beads.

THE LABELS:
I personally dont find the labels to be useful - they do not have enough space for writing the info I want to remember (Supplier, Manufacturer, Product, Size, Material, Price). I jot the price on the Supplier's label from the bead pkg and tuck it into the bottom of the box.

STACKABLE:
The tray is perfectly flat and the grooved cover ensures level stacking.
The bottom of each tray fits into the groove of the cover of the tray below it.

VALUE:
I have purchased many different beading storage items over the years and this is my new favorite.
Added bonus - the Amazon discounted price is fantastic!

this is PERFECT!! This is the only ring adjuster pack I could find that had more than 4 generic sizes, which all made the ring too tight on me. It's a half size too big when my hand is cold and just right when my hand is warm and swollen. It wouldn't make sense to get it altered permanently when it is only too big when I'm cold. I selected this set of paint brushes for when I'm working on my miniature craft projects and am happy with them. For the price it's a really nice set and the brushes have a lot of good features. I like the comfortable grip style, it allows me to paint without my hands cramping up. The set has a good variety of brushes too. The brushes clean up well, and there has been minimal fiber shedding. The roll up felt case is also really handy to store the brushes.

I've been using Brother sewing machines for 25 years now, and am a huge fan. They are a fantastic brand, well made, lots of features and a reasonable price. This is my very first serger and I'm really happy with it so far. It works great on a variety of fabrics from lightweight to heavy denim. It comes with all kinds of accessories, and an easy to understand manual & helpful DVD.

Usually we're not too keen on Lionel trains in this family. My husband is a bit picky, strongly prefers Bachmann, and has never quite cared for the three rail track characteristic of Lionel trains. However, we love Disney themed decor, and wanted a sturdy train to go around our holiday tree. My husband likes the style of the track in this set, he thinks it's a good improvement over the style he remembers. There's plenty of track to go around our 7.5' tree. It was easy to set up, works great, and looks really neat. I'm looking forward to setting it up this holiday season with the rest of my decor.

I've used many hot glue guns over the years, and I am pleased with the improvements on this one by Westcott. The price is right, and it has some really nice features. I especially like how the tip of the gun turns red when it's hot, that's a really nice safety feature. Dripping is a minimal issue compared to other glue guns I've used. It's great for small projects. Definitely buy extra glue sticks so you won't be caught needing one in the middle of a project. You'll want to make sure they're the high temp mini sticks, there are so many available it can be easy to mix them up.

I've been using Fiskar scissors for my sewing needs for over 20 years now. My all time favorites have been their "Softouch" brand, which work great for me to do all my fabric cutting without my hands giving out.

But sometimes you need other types of scissors, depending on the fabric you are working on. The Softouch Fiskars don't work as well on thicker fabrics for me, although they're supposed to. That's where the Amplify Razor Edge Fabric Shears come in. I've had no issue cutting thick denim, coated tablecloth material, or canvas. These also work nicely on thinner fabrics too.

I'm very pleased with these scissors and I'm going to pick up a pair of the Amplify Mixed Media scissors for my husband. Because he's going to be in for it if he uses mine for his projects!

For quite some time I thought perhaps I was missing out by not being able to sew on one of the top brand machines. I grew up learning to sew on my mother's & grandmother's Kenmores, and when I was an adult, bought a Brother because it was what I could afford. So I was looking forward to trying out this machine.

The SINGER 5532 32-Stitch Heavy Duty Sewing Machine is a really nice machine. It's sturdy and has plenty of stitch options for the usual sewing projects. I'm also a big fan of the drop in bobbin feature. It's a good all-around machine for basic sewing.

But having said all that, my favorite sewing machines are still made by Brother. They seem to be nearly as nice, for a price that is much easier on the wallet. My favorite machine, the Brother HC1850, is actually cheaper and has many, many more options than the SINGER 5532.

Oh my, I think I'm in love. I'm a long time fan of Brother sewing machines. My original machine that my husband bought me over 15 years ago, the XL3022, is still going strong. But it's time to have some fun and see what it's like to have all the bells & whistles. Last year I bought the CS6000i, and I am very pleased with it. It's a nice machine. I also had the opportunity to try the Project Runway CE7070PRW. Both are fantastic machines that have great features.

But wow, I'm really impressed with the HC1850. It has the best of both machines and much more. It has the start & stop buttons that I like similar to the CS60001, and loads of decorative stitches like the CE7070PRW. Similar to both machines, it has the great table attachment (perfect for large projects) and many different presser feet you might need. The LED light is fantastic, it really lights up the work space. Like my other new machines, I LOVE the drop in bobbin. It is so easy. Very quiet too. Maintenance of the machine should not be too difficult if you like to do it yourself. I've been able to remove the plates as needed, clean & oil, then put the plates back in. It's no more difficult than my old XL3022.

About the only thing I wish the machine came with is an enclosed case. In fact, it would be nice if they all did. Unfortunately they're sold separately. But it's not a bad price and the Brother 5300 Universal Carrying Case does a nice job at protecting the sewing machine. So I'd recommend it with your purchase. The Brother HC1850 is a great investment for those who want to sew, it's become my favorite machine and I'd encourage others to sew on it as well.

I grew up learning to sew on my grandmother's & mom's Kenmore. In the early years of our marriage, my husband bought me a Brother XL-3022. It's still going strong but does show it's age. Loud & clunky, but very serviceable. Last spring I bought the Brother CS6000i as a new treat to myself, so when the Brother Project Runway CE7070PRW was offered to me I was very interested in seeing the difference. Both machines are very similar, including in price.

Both the CE7070PRW & CS6000i are very lightweight. It's easy to move them from table to box and back again. They're amazingly quiet too. Lots of decorative stitches that are so fun to use, the CE7070PRW has 10 more than the CS6000i. I love the drop in bobbin that they both feature. That is a HUGE improvement over the old XL-3022. The automatic threader is fantastic. The wide table attachment is also a great accessory. I look forward to using it for my future large projects.

Now down the the "nuts and bolts." The price is great on the CE7070PRW, the machine is relatively easy to use. So I think it would be a great machine for a beginner. It's good to start with, and has all the bells & whistles for when your skills improve and you want to try something of higher level. It's sturdy so it should stand up to years of use. The same thing for the CS6000i. The only thing I wish it had was a case like my CS6000i but those should be relatively inexpensive. The other small item I prefer on my CS6000i is the "needle position" button. It's just a handy thing that I've gotten used to on that machine, so it really doesn't make much of a difference that the CE7070PRW doesn't have it. Sort of more a personal thing.

So I would equally recommend either the Brother CS6000i OR the Project Runway CE7070PRW. Both are fantastic machines in similar price ranges. As to which machine I'd recommend on an individual basis depends on what you want in features.

There are so many things about this product that boggle my mind.

First of all is the price. You can go to any coin dealer in America and get these exact same coins for half the price.

Secondly is the laughable COA.

I’ve been a coin collector for 10+ years and there is no such thing as “Collectible Condition”. Coins are graded on the Sheldon Scale from 1-70 and every single one of those grades is collectible.

My local dealer gets sets like these coming into his shop all the time and he always feels bad for the people bringing them in to sell because he knows they won’t get even 1/4 of what they paid.

Most of what you’re paying is for the fancy plastic case. Are you buying a case or the coins?

All of these coins combined could be had for $10 or so.

Everything is good about this except the fact that if you want to look at slabbed coin you’ll be constantly wiggling it around and rotating it and it’s a bit irritating.

Because of that I usually just use my handheld loupe.

I also wish it had a little more zoom. It says 30x but it only feels like 5x.

Other than that it works well.

This seems REALLY expensive for what you get. Considering the dates are random & the condition is only fine we must assume any collectible coins with numismatic value will be excluded.

Taking that in mind:

The Walking Liberty Half Dollar is worth about $10-12.

The Barber Quarter is worth about $5-6.

The Mercury Dime is worth about $3.

Lastly the Buffalo Nickel & Indian Head Penny are worth about $2-3 COMBINED.

Assuming we are generous with the value of these coins they are worth ~$24 but the seller is charging $40.

I highly recommend anyone considering buying these goes and checks out some local coin shops. Not only will you find cheaper prices for the same stuff but you’ll also get a lot more variety and the opportunity to pick the coins you want. You can also check out all kinds of different coins from all across history. I collect American gold coins.

I must applaud the seller because it is a brilliant way to profit off of people who don’t know much about coins. However to the seller I recommend charging closer to the ACTUAL value of these coins. NO there is nothing wrong with trying to make a profit but this seems a bit greedy.

I really like the 90lb Sax Sulphite drawing paper for every day art with my kids. It’s a great all around paper that feels nice to the touch and works well for a variety of mediums - crayons, markers and paint. I compared the Sax paper to this one.

This paper is thicker at 110lbs and my first impression was that it didn’t really feel nice. It doesn’t have any texture and almost felt like a really thick, cheap paper. However, it turns out that’s perfect for alcohol markers. Whereas the Sax paper is more absorbent, takes in more of the ink and requires more coloring to fill in a given area, this Bianyo paper is smoother and works better with the markers. The alcohol markers appear brighter and smoother on the Bianyo paper and darker with more streaks on the Sax paper. The alcohol markers don’t bleed through this heavy paper too. There was a little bit of bleed through on the 90lb paper.

We tried crayons and Crayola markers on this as well, and the markers got a bit smeared and the crayons didn’t work great either. This paper really is best for alcohol markers only.

My 7 year old played with this all day making pretend trading cards and game pieces. She thinks it works well, but said it works better on thicker paper than thin paper because it doesn’t fully punch it off on copy paper. When using printer paper, she had to pull off the scraps after punching. It worked better with cardstock. Overall a fun tool.
These short rolls of washi tape are ok, but the patterns are nothing amazing. If anything, they are a bit boring. Overall it’s ok but not very exciting - and I’m normally thrilled about art supplies.

This tape is decent and a good value but hard for my young kids to use by themselves. If you are looking for tape for kids under 8, we love the Bam! dispenser box. If you are an adult looking for yourself, this is a good option!

We’ve tried many different brands of cardstock for art projects, and this one is a good option. It does have a chemical smell when you first open it, but otherwise it is thick and nice that the glitter is double sided. The assortment of colors is great too.

These paints are nice, nothing stellar but fine for most kids crafting. There is a good variety of colors for a decent price. They apply similarly to Crayola or watercolors we’ve gotten from hobby lobby at similar price points.
Got this set even though I don’t have the original set it’s meant to refill, and this works fine by itself! Great quality!

I love this diamond kit from arteza that includes two large paintings to complete. I don’t have time to do it for many hours at a time, so I’ll probably still be working on them in 2023! :) I got a diamond organizer that has 70 little vials to store the diamonds and mark which diamonds are which. This allows me to just do part of one color without worrying about transferring to different bags. I’d highly recommend something like that for a project this big!

I have started this cute succulent diamond painting and worked on it for a few hours, but am nowhere near being able to take a meaningful picture of it! :) This is a relaxing hobby but does require a lot of eye focus, so avoid if your eyes get strained easily. I also purchased a diamond organizer case that holds all of the diamonds in different vials and allows me to switch back and forth without going crazy. I’d definitely recommend some sort of organizer for a project this large with so many diamonds!

I love having this for organizing all the diamonds in my large set. There are so many compartments to hold diamonds, but the case isn’t too bulky overall and can store everything in one place. The funnel works well to easily get the diamonds in the little jars and the extra pens and gel are an added bonus. I’m not sure how I’d approach a large diamond painting without this, I’d probably just go crazy. :)

The initial set up (removing the seals) and clean up (rinsing each sponge head) for these is a bit annoying, but other than that they are absolutely fantastic. My 5 year old and I had so much fun with no mess. They are easy to use, easy for her to squeeze, and yet they don’t apply too much paint/they don’t get messy. The sparkly colors are super fun. The stencils are very easy to use and to clean I just rinsed them in the sink then dried them with a paper towel. The box also states you can refill empty pens with any tempura paint when they are gone, which is AWESOME!! I love to see products that can be reused! Also they washed off our hands with no problems. Overall a win!

My daughter does a lot of crafting and has tried a lot of card stock over the past few years. This is the thickest one we have used. It’s sturdy, well made, and a vivid red color. It will be especially great for Fall, Christmas and Valentines crafts.

When my daughter was 3, I gave her a box of the thin crayola pipsqueaks and she managed to easily destroy all the tips the first day using them. Two years later and I still hadn’t bought her any fine tipped markers thinking that they weren’t for younger kids. Wrong!! I handed this box over to my now 5.5 year old and my 4 year old, and they have used them for about 5 hours now with no damage. I’m super impressed with the tips, and they are really loving the ability to color and write with finer detail. They are perfect for my 5 year old who is practicing writing letters. The caps are easy to get on and off and we haven’t had any caps only partially on (like we do with crayola). Also the markers wash off their skin as expected. Overall I’m impressed!

These are awesome and SO HUGE!! Perfect for kids crafting and art projects, my kids love using and spilling them. Ha. I immediately tossed the two smallest containers of eyes since my kids don’t ever use the tiny ones and they end up everywhere.

This is a cute set with a fun pattern, but the quality is not the normal Fiskars quality. The scissors in particular are not as good as the normal solid colored Fiskars. The ruler and pencil sharpener are fine, but lightweight plastic. Overall I would be happy paying around $6 for the set, but at the current price of $10, it seems expensive for what you get. It’s a bummer since these are super cute, but you are going to get higher quality tools buying them independently and not in this set.

This hook and loop can easily hold several pounds. I wouldn’t trust it for anything too valuable ( like an expensive mirror or the iPhone in the product page images), but this works great for picture frames. The hook and loop allow easy repositioning without putting lots of holes in the wall.

The first one I bought I intended to use for a small set of pipsqueak markers, and I ended up having to get the jumbo size instead after seeing it in person. If you want to put anything more than 8-10 standard pens or pencils, I’d recommend the jumbo size!

The textured exterior of this art portfolio looks classy and attractive, and I love that the archival sleeves are moisture-resistant and just thick enough to offer several layers of reassuring protection! The generously sized dimensions of this portfolio binder have made it a perfect accessory for me to have on hand in the studio because I paint many original pieces that are on 9x12” or 10x13” sheets of art paper. I really love that this album will lie flat when it’s open because that’s a feature that often seems to be overlooked with some of the other portable portfolios that are cheaply manufactured. This product is easy to transport, carry, and store away for later use. I love it!

I love this little pair of sewing scissors! The rainbow iridescence of the coating is so magical, and they arrive sharp, well oiled, and ready for action. I’ve honestly found them to be pretty comfortable to hold, and they’re great for small embroidery and cross-stitch projects. They’re affordably priced too!

This black pair of shears is easy to hold and so glamorously attractive! I love the decorative details that appear on the handle portion. They look both vintage and modern, all at the same time. They arrive super sharp and ready for action, and the ergonomic design of this pair of stainless steel scissors makes them quite functional and reliable. They’re perfect for all sorts of mending, embroidery, and seamstress-driven projects. Really pleased with these!

These miniature scissors are functionally adorable! I love the Victorian scrollwork design that appears on the antiqued-silver look of the handle portions — it reminds me of a set of heirloom silverware! I think the craftsmanship is of good quality, especially at the current price point, and the blades seem to be sharp and functionally reliable. I’m not entirely convinced that the finger grips are the most effective ergonomic design for a small pair of embroidery scissors, but they are easy enough to manipulate and angle into little crevices and fabric joints, especially when loose threads need a quick snip and trim.

I’ve very much enjoyed using this set of paintbrushes! The blue shade makes each handle visually appealing, and each one is quite comfortable to hold.  I am a mixed media artist and have used these brushes to varying degrees to apply gouache, acrylic, and watercolors to my smaller abstract assemblages. I think they offer a great value for the price, and I’d happily recommend them to any of my other paint creatives and artist friends. No concerns with this set!

Love this set of brushes! The grips are comfortably designed, and there is adequate variation in sizes. They help make the fine detail work in my mixed media paintings a breeze. Each brush arrives individually packaged within a clear plastic sleeve, and the velvety black containment pouch is an appreciated bonus too. The brushes are easy to clean, and they seem to exhibit an ideal amount of resiliency. Happy to have them!

I love using these sheets for some of my smaller watercolor mixed media pieces! They easily hold a variety of paint types, including spray paint, and they tear out easily for immediate use. The paper thickness heartily withstands applied moisture, and I like the subtle texturing present across each page. Would gladly seek this product out again for future use!

I have enjoyed using various-sized portions of this art paper roll to package and protect my original paintings! When I need to safely ship out an acrylic on canvas piece or one of my mixed media collage conglomerations, I can always depend on this product to serve as the most crucial, initial protective layer. It is easy to cut (or tear if the scissors aren’t handy) and easy to fold, layer, and shape around a variety of cornered art pieces. It is semi-transparent and super lightweight. It helps me pack, store, and ship irreplaceable originals! ⭐️ Highly recommend ⭐️

As a mixed media artist, I’m always on the lookout for quality art supplies that offer a wide range of colors and versatility! I love this rainbow-hued set of soft pastels. They come packaged with a thin piece of black foam that keeps them securely seated in their packaging slots beneath the lid, and they don’t break easily. Each stick provides generous length and longevity of use. They do produce a semi-substantial amount of dust, but the vibrancy of the resulting hues is undeniable! I enjoy using them in the creation of multicolored abstract landscapes on sanded art paper. Recommend these without reservation!

I love creating abstract mixed media art pieces! These sanded sheets are durable and will easily sustain the messy amalgamation of media sources I subject them to. I generally use some mix of watercolors, coffee, soft pastels, stitched thread, glitter, spray paint, charcoal, PanPastels, graphite, acrylic paint, oil pastels, creamy chalk markers, and colored pencils to achieve my desired outcomes - and these sheets never let me down!

I will say (like most paper art sheets) they tend to start to curl up at the edges once a medium has been applied, so to avoid this, I simply make a painter's tape border around the edges of the sheet (sticky side up), and then tape a slightly larger rectangular border down on top of that one - affixing it to a hard backing sheet or piece of cardboard. That way, the paper stays exposed all the way to the edges, while remaining perfectly flat throughout all the creative endeavors to come! I always have some packages of these on hand.

I grew up tie-dying t-shirts and always loved it! My kids were very excited to try this tie-dying kit.

Pros:
1. You can limit the mess by prepping ahead of time which makes this a great indoor activity. I can imagine doing this again when the weather gets cold and we are stuck inside.
2. It is easy for kids to participate. Simply put on the gloves and help them place the tips of the dye bottles inside of holes of the ball and squeeze.
3. Comes with enough dye to fill the bottles twice. They say that the dye lasts approximately three days so you could potentially dye a few shirts with one set of dye (given you are using different colors).
4. The kit comes with step-by-step instructions along with a variety of design options to choose from.

Cons:
1. I would much rather tie dye shirts outside without having to do all of the prep (assemble the contraption), but that's just a personal preference.
2. There are enough rubber bands for only two designs. So if you are planning to do more than two designs, better have some extra rubber bands handy.
3. The kit only comes with one set of gloves. I was doing this with my two daughters so we needed more. Luckily we had some extra gloves on hand. GLOVES ARE NOT A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE GLOVES. (I would also recommend using an apron like the instructions say or better yet just wear "painting" clothes).

Overall, a great activity when you are stuck inside with the kids and don't know what to do.
BE AWARE: Read the instructions ahead of time!! Your white cotton shirts need to be pre-washed and damp when starting this project.

I had no idea these existed until I bought a mask with them already included. What a world of difference. I had a relative that made our masks when the quarantine first hit, but they never fit right. As soon as these arrived I tested them on of the homemade ones. I made longer bands than necessary. What a huge difference. Otherwise, it's definitely my favorite Chicago hat as the colors are perfect for me and goes with my coat.

This was used for my second candle making attempt after having first tried the 464 from the same vendor.  I like the 464 better, which I have since learned is 98% soy 2% additives.  I added scent cubes and they didn't blend well at all.  I had to re-pour all of the pint sized ones and fix the tops on every one of them.  FYI I added 3 of those small scent cubes to each 16 oz candle and while the scent was nice, they wouldn't burn right.  Soy based waxes will need larger wicks than what is recommended.  I used pint mason jars with extra large wicks and large wicks for the half pint jars and fortunately that fixed the tunneling problems from the first attempt.  10 pounds made 6 pint sized candles so you can see what a bargain it is to just make your own.  I also took another reviewers suggestion and shopped the auction site for essential oils.  There are some real bargains there.

I bought each set that the Art Sherpa has for sale in her collection.  These are the first ever "expensive" brushes I've bought.  The brush shown was only used three times.  I am very particular on my care of brushes. This brush was never left to soak and I always clean my brushes after each use and left to dry per the instructions of the Art Sherpa.  I can't believe that this brush is peeling after only having used it three times!  The whole thing will flake off eventually.  I haven't even used 9/10's of the other brushes but now am not looking forward to it at all.  I have contacted the Silver Brush company but they won't do anything.  So disappointed in these.  None of my "cheap" brushes have ever done this.

Love, love, love these brushes.  I have now bought 4 sets.  I have many brushes, from cheap to pretty expensive, and while these are inexpensive, they are quality.  They hold their shape, feel great in your hand, the tops stay on, I've not had a single hair come out in any of the sets, they hold the paint nicely.  The variety in the sizes are perfect for just about any project.  They are a softer bristle but are strong. They wash up so nice, I don't even have to reshape them after cleaning.

I have ordered cheap brushes, expensive brushes and everything in between.  These fall into the category of inexpensive, but, they are anything but cheap. They are awesome. They have a great feel to them.  The heads stay on, the hairs don't shed, they retain their shape, they hold paint well.  They do everything a brush should do!  I use these for acrylic painting myself.  They are a softer brush, if you want stiff bristles then you would need to supplement that into your collection.  I have ordered these in white and silver colors.  I don't see that there is any difference other than the color of the ends of them. So far I have bought about 4 sets of these in the past few weeks for myself and for my mother.  As I said in the beginning, I have a huge collection of brushes and you just can't go wrong on these.  I will be ordering more as I see them on Lightning Deals!  I would not hesitate giving these as gifts to anyone.  They are that good.
The hairs on one of the brushes were so out of shape that nothing I did would make it go back into shape.

Love this easel. I have a good amount of easels and this one here is one I rate at the top!  It is very light weight, but strong enough. But, one has to realize that since it is light weight there are some limitations. Common sense should tell a person that there is give and take on it being light weight.  I would not think of using it outside with a stronger wind.  Can be used as a floor or table easel with ease.  Carrying case is a nice bonus.

I had two brushes with hairs that weren't uniformed.  I cannot get a clear, precise line with these two.  The number 6 and the 5/0 brush.  I most likely will have to try and cut them off at some point.  Right now I'm just avoiding them.  Other than that, there doesn't seem to be any loss of hairs.

I did NOT get these for a reduced price for an honest review.  Although, I wish I would have, send me all you want at a discount cause I think I'm going to be buying these forever! I put my Prismacolors to the side, picked out a design I wasn't that crazy about in case I didn't like these pencils.  All I can say is Wow!  I'm no professional, I have no idea if these are oil, wax, or pudding base.  I just know that I love them.  The colors are bright, they blend well, they're soft so I don't have to push hard and to me they are almost right up there with the Prismacolors.  Almost.  I've bought and tried many colored pencils and this is the closest I have gotten to the Prismas. And for the price difference, unbelievable!  Thanks Artist's Choice for making such a great, affordable, tons of different shades, of colored pencils!

I originally purchased and used on my hardwood floors And followed the instructions to the “t” but it was hard to spread and had to move very fast just to put it down! It was beautiful in places but did not spread well and lots of gaps! So I ordered more to finish in the needed spots .. the second batch of epoxy after again following directions again was thin and spread so nicely? I’m so disappointed! If the first 2 gallons had of spread as well as the 2nd two gallons I could recommend this brand but because they were inconsistent after following directions the same then I can not!

I am sorry my pic isn't clearer, but my phone advised to move further away for a clearer shot and zoom in. You have to zoom in because these are pretty small little cavities in the molds, but I knew that and was looking forward to using these.  I did not notice until putting a little spray oil on the mold to ensure easy release (when I was looking closely) that I noticed just how incredibly thin this mold was.  It was seriously so thin - the plastic was not brown in the thin spots, it was transparent (which looks white in the photo because the background is I think a white paper towel)... So is it even useable? or am i going to put something in only to see it dripping out making a mess somewhere.  who knows, but i think it is safe to assume it won't last more than a couple uses.  I will be buying only american made molds moving forward, unless someone can recommend a good overseas supplier who produces good solid, not thin, (leaving you wondering where else they may have skimped, did they use a cheaper chemical which maybe is banned by the FDA - really who would know? - and I am now making chocolate in it for my daughter?) do I want to be worrying about these things, no.  I'll pay the extra money.  Would love to find a reputable overseas supplier as I hate to discriminate.

Hilarious!  I saw a meme where someone punked their friend by putting googly eyes on everything in the fridge.  Of course, I had to get my husband!  My sister and I got out the glue gun and everything in my refrigerator has eyes!  For the price, it was a great joke!

I crochet, and I have to block some items, which means I put them on a board, and pin it down, when the article is wet, to help it keep it's shape in the future.  It's so important that the pins have a sharp tip, that won't snag, and that they don't rust, or the pins would ruin my hard work.  I have used these before, and this was my second box.  These are the ones I will always buy and trust!

The only thing I disliked about this glue gun, is that there is no auto shutoff.  I love the dual temp.  I suggest getting the longer glue sticks, as I had to put a short one behind the short one I used, to get it to work.  That's not a negative about the glue gun, just a suggestion.

I love these hooks!  They are comfortable to hold, even with arthritis, and have a perfectly smooth hook.  They come in a cute carrying case, with free goodies.  Great but!  I recommend this be product.
I have arthritis, so this ergonomic handle is wonderful.  I prefer metal hooks over plastic, and this hook does not disappoint.  It is perfectly smooth, no snags.
I love that the stitch markers work easily, come in multiple colors, and come in a its own case.

The metal hook part is too short to hold many loops besides a double crochet stitch; this hook is too short to hold many loops at one time.  This is truly sad, because the handle, although too long to fit in a traditional case for hooks, is the best I have seen for use by arthritic hands, it is a fat handle, making it much more comfortable to crochet with.
I love the variety of sizes and lengths of the needles.  My favorite part is that I now have a nice tube to hold all of my crafting, big-eyed needles!

This 6 Pack 5d Diamond Painting Kits for Adults Kids Beginners arrived well packaged. There were no loose or missing diamonds and the canvases were rolled very well with no creases. Everything was present and accounted for for all 6 diamond paintings. It truly is a set of 6 individual kits, all with their own canvas and diamond packs. The price is great for everything that you get. I will update this review after I get started on them!

These PMCDS2G Candle Warning Labels 1.5" Candle Jar Container Stickers 500 Pieces Per Roll Waterproof Candle Safety Labels Sticker Decal for Candle Jars,Tins and Votives (Navy Silver) stickers are very nice to have on hand if you make your own candles. I do make my own and often gift them to others for various occasions and these stickers make it easy to include a simple but professional looking reminder about candle safety. I received a roll of 500 stickers and, unlike other reviewers, my stickers had no typos. The stickers also have great adhesion - they stuck perfectly and as expected to both glass and metal candle containers. The only downside to my purchase is that the holographic nature of the stickers (I chose the Navy Silver ones) makes you have to tilt the sticker to be able to read the entire label due to the shininess. Other than that, these are perfect and will work great for my gift giving needs!

I ordered this case to corral all of my power cords when I travel. I love the dual compartments! One side for my cords, the other side for my husband's. The case is a good size, material feels like it will hold up to years of use and the zippers work flawlessly. I couldn't ask for more.

These Double Line Outline Marker Pens are very pretty!! The package contains 12 colors and the outline really does work and shows up really well, even on plain paper. The ink does want to run out of the tube when you first start a pen for the first time... I ended up with a metallic puddle from simply tapping the end of the pen on the paper. SO be careful when you first start using each pen. They also will bleed through the paper a little bit so be mindful of what is underneath your paper when using.

The colors are just amazing! I am the least artistic person on the planet but these pens make me look like the next Van Gogh! Other than the running issue with the first use, this is a fantastic set of pens. I like the case they come in too.

Bottom line... these metallic outline pens are a great deal for the price! Just remember to be careful when starting a pen for the first time and watch out for bleed-through and be mindful of what's under the paper. Otherwise, enjoy witnessing your inner artist come out!

This skyyue B4 Diamond Painting Led Light Pad Kit isn't missing anything! It includes everything you need to start diamond painting! I've been DPing for a few years now but I didn't have a light pad and knew it would help with my neck-crick. I got this kit and, obviously lol, have a light pad now! Plus all the extras! Everything is high quality. I highly recommend this kit - whether you're just starting out with your diamond painting hobby or if you're a seasoned veteran - this kit will help you out tremendously! It would make a great gift too!
Easy to use and holds up!

Wow! Talk about magnet power! I use these feet to photograph socks that I knit and I am truly pleased with the strength of the magnets that hold the feet in a tip-toe position. I can even poise the feet on a metal bookcase without the metal rings. They stand steady.
I really love these since they show my knitted heels off by their positioning. I am very, very pleased with them.

It's nice to look at. I use it for photographing the yoga socks I knit since the socks are toeless. The only problem I see is in the balancing. The foot tips over very easily, even if I put something like a book on the circular base to weigh it down. I guess the it is made shallow in order to fit toed socks into the base.

This is perfect for displaying and photographing my knitted sock and slipper creations and even for jewelry since all toes are separate. The skin tone is very good and the features are sharper than on another brand I had bought. Perfect for knitters, crocheters and jewelry creators.

This yarn is enchanting! No matter how much you knit or crochet, the shimmer is so beautiful that you are already planning on what you will make next with it. The moment people notice the scarves, fingerless gloves and whatever else I have made using it, they 'oooh & aaah'.
If I could only choose one yarn to have, this would be the one!

This is a great item. I used it for my husbands recliner where the imitation leather has worn off after just two years of use. I tried it per the directions. but for what I used it for it was easier to: 1. Peel the clear coat off of the vinyl (still easy to handle) 2. I put the vinyl where I wanted it on the chair.
3. I put a pillow case over that. 4. I used the iron on low steam to iron it on chair.., I did move it back and forth slowly and lightly and it actually did stick to the chair perfectly. The chair looks so much better I put it on the seat and on the arms too, still have to finish it when Hubby is gone so I can concentrate. Thank you for the products...what a difference and I'm sure it will last. If need be, you can also add a dab of liquid cloth stitch on the corners to help it and it doesn't show either. Thank you , thank you.

I thought these were great. I used two and a half boxes for my scrapbook. I don't really understand why people are complaining about them breaking or tearing--I didn't have a single one break.
I found it was easier to peel them off the line myself rather than letting the box do it for me. I'd unroll a length, peel them off to stick on the edge of my table, and then use them on photos. It was way easier than trying to let the box peel them one at a time for me.

I agree with some other reviewers--despite the fact that they're size-adjustable, they're still too small. I can extend one to fit my ring finger, but not my middle finger without the sharp edges poking me when extending it further. My hands aren't that big--I'd say my hands are medium sized. Don't bother with this ring set if you have hands that are anything but small with small fingers. And don't bother if you're looking to sell the rings because you're restricted to only buyers with small hands.

I love this paper. The convenience of buying cut paper has always outweighed the work it would be to cut my own  for my paper quilling (never mind finding specific colors), and it is for folding these stars as well. I tried using my 1/4" quilling paper strips to fold them but that width is just too small, so I started searching for 1/2" quilling paper, which I never found (largest I could find was 10mm). I stumbled across this paper specifically for folding these stars on Amazon and I'm so glad I did! I had no idea anyone cut paper specifically for this hobby. As a bonus I can probably use this paper for a quilling project if I need wider paper. What fun!

I love these tweezers. I'm on my second pair over the course of a few years. My first pair I wore the rubber pads out. They fell off, so I glued them back on with E6000 glue and they lasted at least another year before the pads were just so worn they wouldn't stay on anymore. I use them for paper quilling and they are better than any other tweezers I've used. I'll still keep my old pair as a back up, of course!
I was a dork. I totally cut myself using a brand new pair of these when I was using it to open a cardboard box. Don't be like me! They are SHARP!
I love this organizer. I use it to hold my paper quilling tools. It makes it easy to view what tools I have and easy to find what I need.

"Precision" is not a term they should use with these tweezers. The tops of the tongs aren't even the same length because one of them is blunted and they don't close all the way.

In my photo the gray pair of tweezers on the right was what I was trying to buy a second pair of. You can see they have sharp tips that close all the way.
I'll have to look for more ektools ones like my gray pair. This black pair is nearly useless for what I need them for.
Just what I needed, though it's bigger than I expected.

These are a lot of fun. I honestly don't know what to use them for--originally I just used a few colors to bind together two peg boards (so that they open like a book). It worked great for that, but I don't know what else to use it for. They're tucked away in my craft room for future endeavors.

I didn't even get to use it before it broke. The tongs were so tight I couldn't even get a regular piece of 1/8" quilling paper in it and when I suck my fingernail in to widen it slightly it promptly snapped. Great waste of money.

These knitting needles are a great option for new knitters.  They are smooth, durable and plenty long enough to hold a lot of stitches. Once you open these, it can be hard to sort them out, so be careful.  Overall, a fantastic deal, perfect for learning, plenty of sizes.

This cotton yarn is a decent option for making dish cloths and pot holders, but I would not recommend it for anything wearable. I like the colors, but they are a little lighter than the photo looks.  Overall, a good yarn for utility applications and to practice new stitches.

I bought these feathers to decorate a voodoo priest hat for Halloween.  They are relatively small and I'd recommend saving them for projects that will be viewed up close.  Overall, these feathers are a good value if you aren't too picky about size and colors.

I had never used either the umbrella thing or the winder before, but they are pretty easy to work with.  Before buying this set, I was dyeing my yarn and after it dried I would sit on the couch and use my knees to hold the yarn while hand-rolling into a ball.  Putting the yarn hanks around the umbrella thing is amazing, and the winder makes perfect yarn cakes.  My only complaint is the design of the winder's clamp that you use to secure it to a table.  I end up just holding the winder in my hands because it would take at least a good 4 foot table edge to clamp both of them down at the same time.  I think this could be improved if the winder had an adjustable joint that would let me also clamp it onto a vertical surface.  Overall, if you are winding yarn by hand, this will be a major improvement to your craft time!

This is a huge amount of mica powder.  I can’t imagine using all of this in my lifetime, unless maybe I spill half the bottle or something.  I use these powders in glass fusing, and it works well for that purpose.  Just be careful when removing the lid and the safety seal underneath!  Little golden dust went flying up into the air.  It was magical but also messy.  Open it very slow, and when you go to flip the cap back down to close it, do it gently.  I highly recommend this bottle of mica powder, it’s a good deal in my opinion.

This cutter works well,  but I do not recommend it if you are cutting very small glass tiles as I do at times.  When you press down, the head springs back to release the oil, but on small pieces this can be annoying.  I do not use the oil, and would not recommend purchasing this tool if you are just needing small cuts for fused glass or mosaics.  Go for the cheaper red one that you see at the hardware store.

These silver ring blanks are great, but please use caution if you are gluing glass or gems to them.  You really should scratch up or roughen the surface of the blank so that it accepts the glue better.  I’ve had one or two fused glass pieces pop off the blank.  I have used E6000 glue to affix them, but I do not believe this was the problem.  It just needed a more textured surface to grab onto.  Great value, just be sure of your glue job!

This is my first microwave kiln, so my review includes some first impressions.  I was happy to find that the kiln arrived pretty quickly.  I am an American currently stationed overseas in Germany, so the fact that it made it all the way here in one piece was great.  The kiln itself is quite large, (I selected the large, so this is no surprise).  It can easily fit a piece as large as 3 inches by 3 inches.  I do not recommend it, as the heating would not be very even, but the potential is there.  I used the kiln as soon as I got it, and did not pre-heat or pre-fire it in any way, as the instructions did not recommend it.  The kiln has worked very well, but please realize there is an art to properly firing glass in the microwave.  My tips for this kiln are:  purchase thin-fire kiln paper (protects your kiln from glass and extends life of the kiln), also use the provided thicker kiln fiber paper which is just another layer of protection.  Do your best to research compatible glasses so the glass you put into the kiln doesn't crack as it is cooling.  Please also realize that these kilns can last a very long time when handled properly.  Always use heat resistant gloves when opening or handling the kiln, and do your best not to drop the lid or cause any kind of sudden stress to the kiln.  The kiln will develop small cracks, this is normal.  The inside and the outside of the kiln cool at different rates, so the materials will always crack slightly.  If you notice any LARGE cracks, these might start to interfere with the heating, causing uneven areas of heat.  This kiln has not show any signs of cracking to this degree.  The "sample" pack of glass that is included with the kiln is a good selection to help you experiment and see what the kiln is capable of.  ALWAYS under-heat.  I would start with maybe 3 or 4 minutes with this kiln, then lift the lid about two inches to take a look, then quickly and carefully replace the lid to add additional heating time.  You can always add more time.  If you heat the glass too much, it WILL melt into the base, and you will have to find some creative way to repair the base.  (I just pulled apart fiber paper and stuffed it into a small crevice in the base, good as new.)  I hope this helps, and do yourself the favor of learning how to use this awesome kiln before it arrives.  Feel free to experiment! Add baking soda between sheets of glass to make bubbles!  Read about different metals you can include in the glass! :)

This mold arrived in great condition, and it appears to be exactly what I needed for my resin and polymer clay projects.  My only concern is that nowhere on the packaging does it specify what temperatures it is safe for.  I looked all over the product description as well, but there is no mention of using it for anything other than the Mod Podge melts.  I saw one answered question about using it in a 275 degree oven with no issues, but it would be nice if the manufacturer would plainly state what it can handle.  I know it is made of silicone, but I don't know enough about the types of silicone to assume this would not burn up.  Otherwise, great option for small projects.

I think the price of this crackle is good, but I am having some difficulty getting the results I would want.  I think it might be my technique, so I will give the product a 4 star review.  The crackle is obviously a liquid, milky in color and consistency, which goes on easily and dries clear.  I do not like that it dries glossy, but this can be fixed with a little micro-sanding or other aging products.  The crackle seems to be a bit on the small scale.  I tried applying it to some polymer clay jewelry pieces I made, but the crackle was so small that it almost looked like broken glass, not peeling paint.  As I  mentioned, this might be operator error, so I will continue to experiment with different amounts of the crackle liquid (thicker or thinner spread).

These stamps are a bit smaller than I would have liked, and they are also a little on the shallow side.  I wanted to press them into polymer clay, but I am worried the detail might not translate very well.  I might still try, or I could use these for actual ink stamping, but I recommend choosing a different stamp if you are needing something that will leave a deep impression with more large-scale details.  I realize I might not have paid close enough attention to the product description, so I give these 5 stars because they are beautiful stamps, even if they are a bit small.

Wonderful quality.  These shipped to me in a "padded" envelope, so I was concerned they would be smashed to dust by the time they got from China to my mail box.  I was surprised to find all of the sheets in great condition.  I think they look just like gold, so they are perfect to use for crafting or jewelry design experiments.  Try the fake stuff before you invest in the real deal.  Great product.

The other reviewers are right when they say "a little goes a long way"!  I love this stuff, it is really amazing how many surfaces you can use it on.  I have used it on polymer clay as well as wood and resin.  If you rub it in and want a higher polish, I recommend sealing it with some kind of wax.  I tried to polish the items after applying the Rub 'n Buff, but the polishing attachments tend to just make the metallic finish fade a bit.  Use Renaissance wax or some other basic wax to keep it looking fresh.  Great product if you are an artist or crafter who doesn't want to be limited to what they can use their supplies on.. This product seems to work on everythin!

This ring-sizer is exactly what I needed.  It seems to be made of a durable plastic, but probably not strong enough to actually form a ring around it, unless you're working with clay or some other soft substance.  I don't think you could "hammer" or "tap" a metal ring around this without breaking it.  But that's okay, I work mainly with wood and polymer clay, which it is great for.  I brought the little rings on the chain to my coworker, and was able to size her for a ring without having to guess.  Good quality product.

These came wrapped pretty well, but they still got a bit mixed up in the container.  The plastic "pie wheel" they come in is pretty cheap, so I immediately emptied them out and sorted them into small baggies.  I wouldn't trust the container, it will fail.  Good quality screw eyes, though.

I purchased this mala for prayer during work.  I work as a medic in the Army and needed something that was durable but not too flashy.  I don't carry this for fashion purposes, so the more plain and inconspicuous the color, the better.  It arrived along with another item I purchased from Amazon, but it was in it's own separate little bag.  The color is great, the size was okay (a bit longer and bigger than I anticipated), but the main problem was the texture.  I'm not sure if this is common with 'lotus' type malas, but it was very oily and sticky feeling.  Almost as though it was brushed with some kind of sealant that never really dries.  I expect this to eventually fade with use, but it was worth mentioning as some people might be put off by the feeling.  I haven't found it to be too distracting during prayer, as the point is to focus on the praying rather than what's going on around you.  Overall I am satisfied.

I am a novice, so take that at face value but this is what I noticed:

The tool to make the holes only works on thin leather. I had leather that was 1.6 mm thick and it really struggled to cut through. I decided to just hammer a nail through to make the holes instead.

The biggest rivet will go through two layers of the 1.6 leather but not a third.

The rivets do indent with even a slight push of my nail.

They hammer together easily and do look nice when completed. So far they are holding the leather together nicely.

I bought to use with cricut maker. It is too stretchy. I also tried to engrave but this fabric is not designed for that. I had to hand cut and use vinyl to achieve some results.

It does not work for my intended purpose but it is still a good product.
It worked but not as well as I would have liked. I will still use it until it is gone but next time I’ll order the more expensive cricut brand. The cricut brand does apply better to fabric.

I had to look up how to remove the original because those directions were not included. To remove original, squeeze the bottom area. Tabs will release it nice and easy.

They do the job. They are very thin but they are doing the job of protecting my work surface so I’m not complaining. I was very excited to do this diamond painting for my mom’s sewing room. It is a great size and the design is nice. After it was all done, I did go back and remove a couple rhinestones and put a different color there because the one for the pattern looked really out of place. I did that for 5 of the rhinestones.

The first time the package came, it arrived without any of the rhinestones. Had to send it back and order a new one. The new one arrived and everything was there this time. The tools you get are standard. The tweezers are much nicer than previous paintings I have done. The rest of the set left a lot to be desired.

Although it came with a sufficient number of rhinestones, they were not uniform in anyway and several had to be pulled out of the group altogether and tossed because they weren’t usable at all. The rhinestones varied in height and area. This made for it to be a little difficult for lining things up nicely.

The glue for the painting was not seated well at all. It was lifted off the canvas in several places and because of the creases caused and the lifting, some of the rhinestones were not sticking well. I sealed the painting so that this wouldn’t be an ongoing issue. I do this with all of them now but this one required it, unlike the others.

Overall the painting is okay, but I surely will not buy from this brand again. This is my third painting and the first two I did(took forever coming from China but I would get through them again) had none of the issues I have had with this one. Really disappointed because this one wasn’t just for fun and was for my mom and now I don’t feel like I am giving her the quality of work she deserves and I was looking for.

I would add pictures that I took but amazon doesn’t seem to be giving me an option for that for some reason.

Machine launders with no shrinkage. Non-dye lotted yarn.

Worth a try for the low price and four different size crochet hooks in a convenient folding case.

Houses incredible number of 4x6 photos in a self contained box that is easily transportable. Smaller sizes fit well too. I was struggling on my quilting with 'stock' brand from eBay that were dirt cheap. Finally got tired of struggling and ordered these from amazon. I am a happy quilter now!  Super sharp, cuts through multi-layers of fabric like a flash, seem to last much longer than the 'bargain brand' ones I purchased on eBay. Even have their own little case to prevent accidental injury. Never again for the 'bargain brand' stick with OLFA for quality products.

For those who knit or crochet (I'm a crocheter) these come in very handy when assembling the pieces of your garment or afghan. I loved the fact that they have different sizes in the same package and are much lower priced than at the familiar craft stores. Sturdy, not flimsy like the plastic ones. I store mine in an empty Tic-Tac box to keep them dry to avoid moisture which might tarnish or cause rust. Great quality and an excellent price on Amazon.  Happy stitcher!

I wasn't very happy with these. They required a great deal of streching to get over a standard skein of yarn and then snag the yarn as you stretch it over it. I've used other products that were far better and didn't create as many problems. Some of the smaller ones were too small to use. I felt it was a waste of my money and won't purchase again.

I am really enjoying my sewing cabinet!  It was fairly simple to put together, all of the pieces are well marked and I would estimate it took about 2 hours for me to get it fully assembled by myself. The color is truly a 'soft' white and the top is smooth and easily cleaned with a damp cloth. There is tons of storage and the shelves on the right of the cabinet are adjustable to each individual user. The rollers are a bit hard to push on carpeted surfaces.  Since my sewing room is dedicated solely for my sewing and quilting, I generally just leave the cabinet and the machine set up and ready to go.  For the price I paid, this is a very solid piece of furniture at an exceptional value.  One drawback is the 'cardboard' backing, I would have the 'privacy panel' to be the same material as the rest of the cabinet, especially since I didn't back it to a wall, but floated the cabinet to form an 'L' configuration with my cutting table/ironing surface.

Pin basting my quilts have always been a hassle, sore fingers and poked digits - so I avoided doing it until, with nothing left to put on the bed or a birthday/Christmas present approaching, I had to force myself into it.  This tool, when used in conjunction with the safety pin covers, makes basting a breeze!!!  My fingers weren't bloody, risking getting blood on my new quilt and the pins hold securely and fasten easily with this tool.  It does take some time to get used to managing, but well worth the minimal time investment to keep my fingers from pokes and prods of the safetey pins.  Now I almost look forward to pin basting the quilt/wallhanging because I know it will go smoothly and I won't have a boat load of pin poked fingers.  Highly recommend.

I like this because, when used properly, it will mark a stitching or folding line in the fabric without leaving a pen mark or pencil line.  The line isn't permanent and I can iron out the line when I'm finished with the stitching or pinning. It won't cut the fabric and seems to work well for my quilting process.

This is a good product for the home quilter and hand sewer to prevent the thread from twisting and curling, also preventing tangles as a result.  It seems to be a silicone product as opposed to the old fashioned bees wax customarily used for this type of hand stitching, but works well and does the job required.

This product is working for applique and sticks very well once ironed. The instructions on the package are seriously lacking. I hit it with an iron after applying to the fabric, because it would not stick! THEN, ironed again to make it adhere to the project. This worked but the package does not tell you everything. They direct you to the website. Yea... ok.
However I found that on every single "light" version has the sticky part on the inside back paper piece and not on the back of the grid sheet. So, you put your information in the grid, and that part is trash.
I was so disappointed to see that happening. I had to re write my information on each piece and cut out the shape twice because its on the grid side, then had to cut it out exact because of the sticky mishap, place on my fabric.. then cut it out again on the fabric. AND its difficult to see because the back side is fairly clear.. so I had to get under the bright light to see it. Which in Addition to that, makes your design come out backwards, so you have to consider that as well!
What a mishap. I have not opened my "regular" steam a seam package yet.

I bought a case of the Dollar Tree utility boxes to organize my storage room. These write on them without coming off (I had tried chalk paint pens and the wax crayon 'chalk' pens previously). If it didn't take abusing the tip with 100 presses & giving myself a shoulder injury incessantly shaking them JUST TO PRIME THE TIP I'd say 5 ⭐️. The annoyance of priming after recapping 15 minutes earlier deducts a full star, for me.

I have barely used once but, barring a bad apple, I'm gonna predict this to suit my newbie-on-a-budget needs. I've seen far too many crafters swearing by it after much use. Granted, none of them are engraving the canoe they whittled out of a Sequoyah, but still...

Using a ThermoPen, fresh batteries, the Universal tip recorded ≈500°F at the 5 minute mark. DO NOT LET CHILDREN OR IDIOTS AROUND THIS TOOL!!! I'm totally serious. I checked the temp 3 times. I used my old school stick thermometers, that top out at 220°, and they both went quickly beyond that...
... by the way, by idiots I don't necessarily mean people of low intellect so much as dinkus' like me who would try touching the tip of my finger to it (once-upon-a-time) to see if it "feels hot" 👱🏻‍♀️🤦🏼‍♀️
Double-sided, as stated. I'd say a medium layer of polyester fiberfill. Will buy more, at a later date, to make sofa/loveseat/chair covers.

My Granny made my clothes when I was in elementary school in the mid-70s (YES I AM 28!! 😂) and I don't know what brand she used then but I do recall the stretch would eventually, seemingly, permanently stretch out. I think there have been epic improvements in those types of materials - like spandex or whatever - that goes into this type product.
In the very early 90s, she made me some skirt sets to wear to my first job, in retail (I miss the 90s, when people actually dressed up to go to work and looked professional, even for their minimum wage jobs, because effort mattered) and she occasionally used Dritz non roll elastic to make me adjustable waists for my vests, on the sides, to give them that professional, fitted look (she'd say "Honey, if you've got it - FLAUNT IT! - because you won't always have it").

I inherited her sewing machines, cabinets, and materials 'stash' when she passed 13 years ago. The other day I needed some non roll elastic and remembered there was some way back in the sewing cabinet. THAT STUFF WAS LIKE NEW! No dry rot, no yellowing (it was white). I am going to make some button extenders for my son's shorts and do some alterations on his perfectly good store bought shorts that the elastic went to junk on, so I needed black. Of course I bought Dritz and am thrilled to say the quality doesn't seem to have changed in 30 years, even though the product has gone from Made In USA to Made in China.

I promise if the longevity/durability from the manufacturing change isn't the same, I will absolutely update this review. (Follow me & check out my other reviews... I follow through when I say I will update!).

3.5 Stars but I rounded up because I'm a flippin' ray of sunshine and positivity 😜

I wish I could have seen this fabric in a store 😞
As another reviewer mentioned - I so wish I had weighed her comment about how much more muted this fabric is in real life compared to (not only Amazon's but also customer) photos. There's also a degree of sheerness that I didn't anticipate or put much thought into (another review, maybe, I forget if it was the same one or not) until I received it. In the full daylight of my bedroom, I could faintly see my hand between the fabric and the ecru bedspread I had laid the fabric across. I am recovering a much deeper, darker fabric that is an almost tapestry weight. I anticipate having to remove that fabric rather than just lazily upholstering over it. (((Sigh)))

While the only Sample was for the Raspberry, I suggest requesting a sample of that and comparing it to the photo on your phone to give yourself a better idea of how much more muted the colors are than they are on the device your using to peruse.

*I tried to upload a photo that would clearly show the amount of differences in the photo fir the French Blue and the actual fabric, but after 20 minutes of the 'circle of death' because the internet at my house is the worst in the first-world, I just deleted the photo and posted the review.

I mistakenly ordered the regular, old fashioned iron on denim patches. The third-party retailer snail mails their products from Oregon... I live in Georgia so it took about 12 days before I received and realized my mistake. Rather than return the patches - I'm sure I'll use them someday, and it was my mistake, so I kept them - I ordered the correct ones. The BACKING PATCHES don't exist anywhere else, that I've found... and I searched pretty hard, not just on Amazon... so - if I were the Sellers, I'd list the BACKING PATCHES for about $6 more and Mail them USPS Priority in the tiny $5.xx box. I honestly think they'd sell a lot more if people could get the product quicker. Because BACKING PATCHES should be what most people use... I mean, who wants a big ugly patch on the outside of their jeans?!? People want patches on the inside, that they iron on to cover their bigger holes/tears/rips that can't be invisibly sewn closed. Most schools have prohibited ripped clothes (even the paid-for 'destroyed' ones) and if parents of boys bought new jeans every time our sons ripped a knee or somehow their crotch areas (how do little boys grow up and still have their 'nads?!?!?) we would all be bankrupt by the time they graduate! THESE BACKING PATCHES ARE WORTH EVERY PENNY! They are way to use and they stay adhered through washings and high heat dryings. So thankful to have found them.
...and as thick as actual leather is - what I recall these being marketed for - I figured surely they'd work on thin twillish fabric. Nope. Total waste for me 😞

Oh, brother, wherefore art thou? Or, rather, where have you been all of my life?!?  Super simple (LOVE the ease of not only winding a bobbin, but loading a bobbin...what a nightmare on my two mechanical Singers!); super easy to learn (I haven't sewed in 16 years, and even then I could barely hem without having bobbin issues. I literally returned two machine because of bobbin issues back then and 'retired' from sewing); and pretty darn inspiring.  I've hemmed 6 curtain panels, which I also sewed together to make one super long panel for a sliding glass door.  I've hemmed 3 pairs of jeans. All I want to do now is sew.  I don't want to knit, I don't want to watch TV, I don't want to sleep... I want to sew.  Hey, you! Your pants look a little long, how 'bout I hem 'em for ya'?

Moving from a house where everything was oil rubbed bronze into a house where I decided that antique brass was now my 'metal' of choice, and I didn't really want to get rid of some of my more expensive items that I could truly just get away with painting.  (For example, I have fantastic paper towel holder that honest-to-goodness you can pull and tear a sheet off with only one hand.  I don't remember where I got, I've looked everywhere to find another and cannot in any metal, and don't like any of the antique brass out there.  So, I'm painting my existing one.)  To get the true antique brass look, I first tried the RustOleum Antique brass paint.  No chance that stuff could pass by a blind man as real antique brass.  It's not good.  I dabbled and experimented and found that if I first paint the item with the RustOleum Aged Brass, then glazed with a single (medium) coat of the Elegant Finish in Glorious Gold, then did multiple super-thin coats of the Elegant Finish in Renaissance Brown, I have a very passable antique brass looking item.  Besides my paper towel holder I've also transformed a piece of metal wall art (that was just brown/black, possibly called a VERY aged rust), a couple of hall light fixtures, and I still have so so so very much paint left.  Well worth the money.  Trust me, as flooded as the market is with oil rubbed bronze, as much of the 90s bright brass still remains, and as much of the brushed nickel & stainless steel is nice but boring, the antique brass is coming back.  This way, I'm inexpensively ahead of the game.

Price was more than fair for an item that is increasing in demand (typical...I find something I like and I can never keep it to myself, the whole world jumps on the bandwagon) but has been out of production - from what I understand - for a few years.
