Measuring tapes come in many lengths and types. If you’re like me, you probably have several different measuring tapes, likely metal tape, contained in a small, hand- held container that automatically retracts when you’re finished with it. But if you need measuring tape that is loose and easily bendable, you need something different, like this soft tape measure from Unitedprime.

Useful for measuring fabrics, this soft tape is 60 inches in length on one side of the tape, 150 centimeters on the other side. The inches wide has lines that indicate as small as one- eight inch. The centimeter side has marks that indicate down to one millimeter.

This tape can be used to measure most anything 60 inches/150 cm length or shorter, no matter how it is shaped. The reason, of course, is that it’s not stiff tape, like metal tape. It is soft and can be stretched around the way you need it to stretch.

Tape like this doesn’t usually get a rough workout, so it’s the type of tape that will last. For storage, you can keep the resealable bag it comes in or just roll it up and place it in your sewing or craft box. It’s a good measuring tape for fabrics and other small- scale measuring needs.

Artists need paint and ink. They are one of the basic supplies found within any artist’s reach. An assortment of paints is good to have and if alcohol paint/ink is your desire, then a good option is this 24- bottle set from DecorRom.

What you get with this set are 24 bottles of ink. Half of them are the usual colors, the other half are metallic colors. The set come with a push- pin to open each bottle, which is helpful. You need to shake well before use, or else the ink won’t work the way it should.

I find this a good set overall, but some things worth noting are that the colors are not as bright and eye- catching as I first thought, at least not the 12 basic colors. The metallic colors are better. Also, the 12 basic colors are just that. There are no variations of most of the colors. If you want a different shade of green or a different shade of yellow, well, you’re out of luck.

The bottles of ink in this set are small, containing only 10 ml each. That isn’t much and if you find one you really like and use it too generously, the bottle won’t last long. Still, when all factors are taken into account, this set of paint/ink is a good one for the budding artist and a good supply to have on hand.

Whether you are an artist or not, it always helps to have a good set of black markers for regular use. And I don’t mean black markers with a wide tip, but rather markers with smaller tips for everyday writing and accenting. This is what you get with this set of multiliner pens from Martcolor.

The best quality of this set of pens is definitely the varied- sized tips. You get nine sizes in all, which means no matter how thick you want a line to be, you can find a marker to fit the need. The largest size is referred to as a brush because the tip is wider and different from the others. It’s a good choice when you want a wider line or when you have a larger space that you need to shade.

With the ink flow, it is quite good, smooth, and non- skipping. It dries quickly, too. If you write, then rub your finger immediately on the ink, it will smear a little but if you give it a few seconds, it won’t. in other words, unless you are deliberately trying to make this ink smear, it shouldn’t. The ink is waterproof, too.

The barrels of these pens are plastic and the ink in the larger- tipped pens may not last very long, depending on usage. But overall, this is a good set of pens, offered at a fair price, and a practical option for those who need to make marks with different thicknesses.

[[VIDEOID:72c5266eda10a86c4665e3db63f7c1b8]] Markers are something every teacher needs. It’s good to have an assortment of options and if a fine tip marker is what you need, then a good set is this package of 30 markers from Ohuhu.

With thirty markers in the package, you get a good variety of pastel and primary colors. Several of them compliment each other well, like bright red and a softer red. The fine tip writes and draws nicely, as my video shows. These are permanent markers, so you need to keep that in mind. Depending on the surface they touch, you may not be able to remove the ink.

This is a good set of markers, but one small issue is the plastic package they are stored in. The flap that closes doesn’t lock so that’s a bad thing. The marker lids attach to the front of the container, but since the flap doesn’t close tightly, markers can still fall out – as I found out when I opened the box and a bunch of the markers were out of the packaging.

Colored markers are useful for teachers and this set from Ohuhu is a good set, mainly for its marking ability and color selection. A better storage package would improve my rating, but this is still a good product overall.

People who enjoy crafts usually take up sewing at some point. Even if you don’t sew as a craft and only as an occasional hobby or for repair purposes, you still need a set of supplies for the task. If you want to take care of your sewing needs in a single purchase, consider this sewing kit, made by Socanby.

With this kit, you get 233 pieces, all kept organized in a simple to use case. There are 43 spools of thread, scissors, thimbles, measuring tape, plastic bobbins, etc. You even get lesser used but still useful options like a seam ripper, tweezers, stich markers, spare buttons, and more.

There are lots of supplies in this package and the reason the count is so high is because many of the items are small and take up little space. The buttons, for example, include 60 of assorted sizes and colors. The quality of everything is generally good, but it’s really intended more for a beginner. If you’re a serious seamstress, you may find some of the items included too simple and not durable enough. The plastic bobbins are one good example- they work, but more experienced people will want metal. The thread isn’t the most heavy duty either.

Still, for a young crafter, this sewing kit from Socanby has the things you need to get started on your craft or hobby. The organization is nice and it makes a good gift for the budding crafter who wants to have the supplies they need to get going.

An essential supply for those who like to sew is a set of straight pins. You need pins that are long enough to stick through multiple pieces of material and it also helps when the pins are distinguished in some other way, so that you can use them for different needs and tell them apart. Both bases are covered with this pack of 500 straight pens, made by Sewistix.

Each of the pins in this pack is 1.5 inches, or about 38 millimeters in length. The pins are easy to grasp thanks to the rounded, colored head. You get eight different colors, and this is perfect for those crafts/tasks when you want to use different colors for different needs.

These pins are generally good, but I have noticed that they are not as sharp as other straight pins. If you have tough, thick material, you may not be able to get these pins to pierce through without a good effort. Lucky for me, my kid uses them for mostly thin types of cloth, so they work for that purpose. But the lack of a sharp point, while it might make reaching into the box a little safer, is not going to help if you have tough material.

Straight pins are a necessary need for those who sew. Having a box of 500 pins on hand is nice, and I like the different colored heads on these pins. But the lack of sharpness convinces me to rate this product only average overall. As long as your needs involve thinner material, you should be fine. Otherwise, look for a different straight pin.

Do you have a crafter in your household? I do, and she loves to have an assortment of supplies on hand so that she can grab what she needs, when she needs it. One item that every crafter needs is thread so to make sure we had lots of thread for a good, long time, I decided to get this all- purpose sewing thread, 100 pack from Renashed.

What you get with this package is 100 colors of thread. Not only is that enough to satisfy most any need, there is also 250 yards of thread on each spool! That adds up to a ton of thread, and I don’t think I will need to get any more for a while.

Of course, this would mean nothing if the thread wasn’t quality, and I am here to say that this is, indeed, very good thread. It is made from polyester and it is very strong. My young crafter likes to sew some items by hand, but she also has a sewing machine. This thread can be used either way.

Thread is a crafting essential for those who like to make/mend clothing and create other items using cloth and like materials. With this set of 100 spools of thread, you will have this essential need covered for an extended period of time. It’s good quality and I love the variety- there is a shade to match just about any need.

Scissors are a staple item for any office, whether it’s school, home, or work. Having the right scissors for the task at hand does make a difference and a good option for those who want a larger, durable, multi- use pair of scissors is this one, from ATO- DJCX.

These scissors are larger than average, with long blades that make cutting items easier. They can be used for fabric, cardboard, clothing, etc and can handle multiple sheets of paper at a time. Thanks to the lengthy blades, you can make continuous cuts with less effort, since each squeeze of the handles cuts a good deal of material.

There is a good deal of comfort with these scissors and the different colors are cool. There is even a protective cover so that you don’t accidentally cut yourself when you reach for them. The tips are not exceptionally sharp, but the protection is good nonetheless. And these scissors are sharp. They cut through paper like its air and can cut other materials with relative ease.

It’s great to have a nice, sharp pair of scissors that are made to last. These scissors from ATO-DJCX are stainless steel in construction and will keep on cutting for a good, long time. They are an excellent pair of scissors, especially for the price.

Whether you need to write words, shapes, etc for either communication or art purposes, you need a set of pens or markers designed for the job. Ordinary pens with blue or black ink are fine for limited purposes, but if you want to expand on things a bit, you need greater variety and a good choice is this set of 24 journal planner pens from Esonstyle.

Stored in a plastic container, these pens have a wide array of colors so you can almost always find just the right shade. Unlike other markers, these are fine- tipped, with just 0.4 mm touching the paper or other surface. This is thinner than other markers and it makes these ideal for writing words or doing artwork that requires thin lines. Once finished, you just need to remember to pop the cap back into place and put the markers back into the plastic storage sleave.

These pens are great for artwork but I like them best for ordinary communication on my planner. I like having different colors because I can use different shades for different tasks. The ink flows smoothly and it doesn’t soak through to the other side of my paper, and that is important when you’re using the pens to mark in a planner. I admit, though, whenever I use these, I have to fight the urge to start drawing shapes and designs. I use them for writing words, but the cool colors and easy flowing ink puts me in an artistic frame of mind.

It’s always a good idea to have assorted pens, markers, highlighters, etc. This set of 24 pens from Esonstyle is an excellent addition to my writing options. There are lots of colors and the thin point makes them ideal for many uses.

A personalized touch makes everything better. Young people are often inclined nowadays to use electronic means for birthday greetings, party invites, get well cards, and the like. A more creative and fun way is to use Just My Style Wood Stamp Set by Horizon Group USA.

Opening this package reveals 15 wooden stamps, cards, markers, a 2-Tone stamp pad, etc. The stamp pad has two colors- purple and orange- while the markers include blue, pink, and yellow. These are somewhat limiting and kids may not like the color options. My advice is to add your own ink pads and markers for additional colors. And in a short time, you will need to supply your own cards, since the set comes with only six.

The main feature with this set is the stamps. There are 15, all made of wood, and they include such objects as cupcakes, donuts, unicorns, etc. The designs are cute and while the stamps are small, they should fit fine into the small hands for which this toy is intended. And since these are stamps with designs already on them, they are great for young people who want a little help in getting started down the path toward being more creative.

Electronic cards and the like are fine when in a hurry, but it’s nice to create cards of your own and Just My Style Wood Stamp Set by Horizon Group USA is great for encouraging young people to do exactly that. They can make cards for people, create their own stationary, and otherwise brighten someone’s day in their own creative way. Yes, you will likely need to add your own additional supplies, but this is still a fun kit with all the basic things you need to get started.

Clothespins used to be used primarily to hold clothing, but since almost no one hangs clothing out to dry anymore, they are now used mostly for other things. They can be used to hold crafts, photos, etc and if you want clothespins that are small and hidden from view, a good choice is LWR Crafts Wooden Mini Clothespins.

When I first saw this package of clothespins, my initial reaction was ‘wow, these are really small!’ Yes, the description clearly says they are one inch long and they are, but what misled me is that the description, mini clothespins, made me think they would be closer to the size of other mini clothespins I have used. They are smaller than that. However, they still have their use and the reason I wanted them is for hanging pieces of paper with artwork in my wife’s classroom. They are good enough for that use, even though slightly larger would be better.

Because these clothespins are so small, they are not particularly strong so don’t use them thinking you can pin and hold heavier items. You need to stick with things like paper, photos, and things like that. Because they are so small, you can barely see them and that is generally a good quality. They seem quite delicate, too. I could see these easily snapping apart, even though it hasn’t happened yet.

Clothespins are useful for many things besides hanging clothing. If you want tiny clothespins that are good for delicate tasks, then LWR Crafts Wooden Mini Clothespins are a good choice. Just be sure to limit their use to things that are very small and very light, and they should work well.

One of my kids loves art and anything associated with it. She is in junior high school and she likes to spend spare time doing artwork. This desire to create art is made easy with an art kit and a good choice is the TBC 33 piece Marbling Art Paint Kit.

Everything you need to create works of art- except the water- is included in this kit. To prep, you use the included tray, add water and the carrageenan, wait for it to set, then add drops of ink. You can then swirl the floating ink around to create different designs. When you like what you see, just place a piece of paper on top of the liquid and slowly lift off. Once dry, display your work of art any way you like.

This kit is simple enough to use. It comes with eight different colors of ink and while more would be nice, it’s enough to satisfy most young people. It can also be rather messy, so be sure you have a large table that can safely tolerate the occasional splash or drip.

Creating works of art is fun for many and the 33 piece Marbling Art Kit from TBC is a good way to keep creative minds occupied. It seems a little pricey to me, but it does contain all of the ingredients for creative marble art and my kid likes it very much.

One of my young teenagers loves art. She likes crafts of any kind, but seems to have a special affinity and talent for painting. This love of creative expression using running colors was enough to convince me to get her the Faber-Castell Do Art Paint Pour Studio No Mix Acrylic Paint Pouring Set for Kids.

This kit comes complete with five paint colors, a painting station, cups for the paint, and even an apron and gloves. The paint is ready to use and there is enough in the package to create six different paint pouring designs.

My kid likes this paint activity because it is different from what she is used to. Most paint sets involve brushes, flat grids with designs already drawn on them for painting, etc. But with the Faber Castell Do Art Paint Pour Studio, you create works of art by pouring. The different colors do, indeed, make interesting and unique patterns. No two will ever be the same, which means you could purchase multiples of this kit and get 6 completely unique works of art every time. This set also includes stickers that can be placed on the finished design, for even more individual, creative flair

I like that the set comes with an apron and gloves, to prevent messes. And, as my kid and I learned, if you wipe up any spills quickly, everything will be fine, since the paint takes time to set and become permanent. Just make sure to keep paper towels or a damp cloth handy so that you can clean up the mess as soon as it’s made.

My girl likes to create works of art and the Faber-Castell Do Art Paint Pour Studio No Mix Acrylic Paint Pouring Set for Kids is a good way to encourage her on her path toward artistic expression. It’s a fun activity for kids and great for the young art enthusiast in your home.

Artistic paint brushes might seem like something that doesn’t require much in the way of quality. But a good set of paint brushes does make a difference and this set by Chameleon easily sets itself apart from others. The first thing you will notice with each brush is the handle. They are not made from cheap, flimsy plastic. The handles are made from wood and are made to last. The brushes themselves are made from synthetic hair and they produce better lines/markings than other paint brushes. They are easier to rinse clean and they store nicely in the zippered bag, with a separate slot for each.

The above reasons are enough to recommend this paint brush set, but there’s more. The brushes also have different angles/tips so that the artist can select exactly the right one for the task at hand. My kids usually use watercolors when they paint and with these brushes, the paint flows smoothly and the varied tips help them to get just the right effect.

Oh, there’s one other thing I love about this set off paint brushes. I mentioned earlier how they come in a zippered package with separate storage for each brush. But did I also mention there are two of each brush, separated in the holder with six on each side? I have two girls so this is PERFECT! I don’t have to listen to my girls fight over brushes because each child has her own set of brushes. It’s a simple enough idea, but not something most other art supply companies consider.

A good set of paint brushes can make a big difference for an artist and Chameleon Art Supplies 12 Piece Paint Brush Set is an excellent set of brushes. Everything from the construction of the brushes to the performance to the storage is top- notch. You owe it to the junior Monet in your home to purchase a quality set of brushes and this set from Chameleon is certainly one to strongly consider.

Clothes get wrinkled, and the Singer Steamworks Pro Garment Steamer is ready to remove these unwanted creases and smooth out the surface of clothing, drapes, and more. This unit utilizes steam to remove wrinkles quickly and painlessly, giving shirts, pants, and other items a just- ironed look.

Removed from the box, this steamer has a few parts that need to be assembled together before the unit is ready. This doesn't take long and the instructions step you through the process very quickly. All that is left to do at this point is fill the small, plastic tank with water, turn on the unit, and wait for the steam to flow. In less than one minute, steam will begin to release itself from the end of the hose, making it ready for use.

I have used this Singer steamer a few times now and I am impressed by 1. How fast it heats up and 2. How easily it removes wrinkles. I turned on the unit and before I finished placing my first shirt on the hanger, the steam was already flowing. Then, I grabbed the hose and ran the head over the shirts. I wasn't sure it would work properly because I wasn't pressing the head against the shirt (there is nothing solid in back of it, so you cannot press). However, I quickly learned that this is not necessary. Just the lightest touch with the steam smoothes out wrinkles in seconds. One or two passes with the steam and wrinkles are gone. And once the job is done, garments are cool and dry almost instantly.

Looking at other qualities of this product, it includes a telescopic pole that extends to a good height for most uses. The pole is locked into place with a few plastic clips, which can then be unlocked to lower the pole, for storage. The unit has a wheeled base, so it is easy to move around and easy to store.

On the negative side of the ledger, I can find a few minor problems with this unit that could annoy or concern some users. One is the hose- it isn't as flexible as I would like and it isn't very long. It could present a problem if used on something like drapes because the reach is limited. The included glove/mitt is useless too, because it is so small. It is designed for the smallest of hands and I have found that it isn't really necessary anyway. There isn't enough heat to worry about, but I can understand why the manufacturer didn't want to take any chances and included it in the package.

Overall, I found the Singer Steamworks Pro Garment Steamer a useful device for removing wrinkles and I plan to use it on a regular basis. Some might argue that throwing clothing back into the dryer works just as well, but I disagree. The dryer method is more convenient, yes. But dryers take more time to remove wrinkles and, depending on the material, they do not remove all of the wrinkles. This steamer removes wrinkles quickly and effortlessly, and thus earns my recommendation.

I'm in the process of re-upholstering my sofa - I got the thing stripped down to the frame, and have rebuilt the foundation layers on the seat and back. Now I'm paused in the project while I decide what color fabric to choose for the long term commitment that the next phase will bring. Since it's nice to have a place to sit while I decide... I pulled out an old canvas futon cover and slipped the old seat cushions and some pillows in it and put them on the sofa as temporary cushioning. The futon cover was off white. One day my daughter had stretched a dark purple sleeping bag over the sofa planning to curl up in the sleeping bag... the color looked great! So.. I ordered a two 8 ounce bottles of purple Rit Liquid Fabric Dye and mixed them into 4 cups of very hot water, with salt, following the directions from the web site for dyeing fabrics in the washing machine. It came out looking kind of berry purple - not pink - but not the deep purple like the sleeping bag. So I ordered 3 more 8 oz purple bottles, and this one navy blue bottle and followed the directions again - adding all four in the washing machine and tossing the berry colored futon cover in for another round. It came out perfect! A deep purple / violet color. Now I just have to choose a type of fabric in about this color to finish my upholstery project.

My husband got these for my birthday present and I was beyond happy. I immediately opened the king size and started trying it out. After a little trial and error, a few YouTube videos (Alt Knots does a fantastic job explaining how to use it) and practice, I was able to easily make some double sided knit beanies and a scarf. It is a really fun tool, feels like a loom for weaving but circular. A great addition to my craft skills.

The only thing keeping this from 5 stars is that the smaller loom, the 22 needle, one of the feet is missing its pad on the bottom. I searched the boxes but it isn’t here, and it was wrapped well in plastic so it must have happened during production. It makes using it difficult in that I have to use something to help balance it otherwise it’s wobbly and I can’t get a good rhythm going. And sometimes the extra pad I find doesn’t quite work, slips out and I’m back to zero. Which is unfortunate, because I was looking forward to using both all the time.

I do like the king size, I just wish Quality Control would have caught the small looms missing foot pad. Otherwise it would have been perfect.

I'm seriously in love with this little machine! After using a Kenmore for over 20 years and fighting nearly every time with threading issues, and jammed needles, this is a breeze. I love all the amazing additions that normally are only found on machines costing thousands of dollars. I use the extension table almost exclusively because it has so much room to maneuver my items. And the auto threader.... seriously love this. I have had this for about a month now and used it nearly every day and it has yet to cause me frustration. The Kenmore? What Kenmore? The only recommendation I would have overall is that this is a machine for advanced users, a novice or one who has been just learning may become perplexed by the number of functions, stitches and things that this puppy can do. But those of us who have been sewing for a while, this is wonderful.

Nice; comfortable hooks with easy gliding yarn on the hook. Although not as great as the Soft Touch by Clover, these are my 2nd best, ones I'll take with me in public or travel so I'll be less upset if I lose it. I'd still be unhappy but not as badly as if I lost my Soft Touch.

Bought these for myself to help ease wrist and finger tension while working. I must say these Soft Touch hooks are the best I've worked with. The hook and shaft glide through yarn loops like a knife through hot butter! Quickly and effortlessly. The handle has a thumb pad that is cushioned to help was thumb stess and I find that using overhand or underhand holding technique still allows the thumb to comfortably rest on its pad. With these bonuses, i found myself crocheting much quicker and more accurately. I ended up buying lots of them to have as many as possible in my tool collection. Remember, these are your tools for your art, take care of you and your art.

I purchased this safety pin set because i was tired of always search for a safety pin and digging for the appropriate size. I like this set because of the multiple sizes (pictured) and colors (also pictured). The small black safety pins are especially nice. I wish that some of the other sizes came in black (you'd be amazed how nice it is to have safety pins that blend with a darker color).

Overall, these pins are the most flimsy I've seen, but they also aren't the most sturdy, they are pretty middle of the road. I've bent a few trying to stick it through thicker fabric, but if I take my time and brace the pins, they are fine.

One small nit, some of the pins came pinned together. I have no clue why this is and it's annoying. It's a small complaint though. Overall, I think this is a good light- medium duty set to keep around the house and keep your pins organized.

So - I am definitely more of a markers fan than a colored pencil fan. That is just a matter of personal preference. I decided to upgrade my Crayola's for better colored pencils to see if I could be turned in to a fan or suddenly capable of doing amazing shading in my adult coloring books. Sadly, this has not made me a better shader, but it has made me like using colored pencils more. This set has a very nice range of colors including two metallic like pencils in silver and gold. I would recommend you get a good sharpener. I attached a little color sample I use when selecting my colors. I hope it helps!

Maybe printable anything for iron on just doesn't work well.  The ink totally smudges, no matter how long you let it dry.  You need to spray something on them to keep it color fast - I don't know what, but probably something.  To me, wasn't worth it.

Here's the thing - This is probably great for a plain tee-shirt or something else.  It's a clear backing - so whatever shirt color is under there, is what you'll see in the white areas of your print-out and the instructions tell you that pressure and heat are your friend.  They are, but these sheets aren't.  Sometimes they'd stick sometimes not.  The ink got smeary - they are okay.  Don't use them on anything overly valuable or anything you really need to look precise.

My desktop is scarred from years of cutting and crafting. I wish I'd had one of these sooner. I do a lot of 3D printing and this mat makes post processing clean up of support materials and trimming the odd string and zits of plastic that inevitably crop up. Handily marked in imperial on one side and metric on the other. I actually like the smaller 12"x9" size. My desk is crowded but the size of this fit the free area next to one of my printers. Very easy to cut on and with a light hand I don't see any damage from the cutting I've done. The material is thick enough to add a little padding under your hands (also keeps stuff from sliding around) but not so soft that it throws off exact cuts. Very useful for me.

My primary use for glue sticks is 3D printing. These are just as good as the name brand product I've used at a better (currently) price. A thin coat on a hot bed, watch the purple color fade to clear. When it's clear start printing. I can go usually a week before the mirror I print on becomes grody enough to need cleaning. This, even after a week of printing, a dozen prints, several reapplications of glue stick, cleans quickly with warm water and soap in the sink. I've also used these sticks for gluing notes down that I need to refer to and a couple craft projects. It holds well and dries quick.

The glue holds well but is even more fun for writing or designing glow in the dark shapes. Make extra stiff slime and you can sculpt glow in the dark models. The product glows for quite a while. I charged the entire bottle with a high powered headlamp for ten or fifteen minutes and the bottle glowed for easily eight or nine hours. Smaller amounts used for crafting glow well but not nearly as long.

I use this on my Creality CR-10s 3D printer using an Ikea LOTS mirror. The glue goes on purple and dries white so you know when you can start printing. The glue has given me great results, good adhesion and good removal after the print has cooled. It works good on paper too but I really use this specifically for my 3D printing.

These are excellent hobby blades. I've used Excel blades and handles for quite a while now and these are no exception to the quality I've come to expect. The blades are often subject to stresses they're not designed for, such as getting prints free from my 3D printer, and fail long past the point when lesser blades would. They stay sharp through a copious amount of work cutting and scraping plastic, wood and paper. Very much recommend these blades.

A good glue, a little thinner and takes a little longer to dry than Elmer's good old white glue. Feels just as strong when it's dry. A fun way to add a pop to art projects for kids and adults too. Not really into making slime so we haven't used it for that.

Three stars. I bought this to use to clean up my 3D prints. The blade often gets stuck in the media (PLA) which is to be expected but what isn't is the blade staying in the print, even if it's only a couple millimeters in, and pulling free of the handle no matter how much I tighten down the grip. I like the soft handle it makes using the knife easier and more comfortable. I just hate having to handle blades more than I need to. The blades are sharp and hold an edge reasonably well though I probably will buy brand name blades when these run out. I'll try the handle for a little while longer and see how it works out.

Needed larger safety pins to keep a denim utility bag on a walker. I tried numerous pins from the package and every one of them was able to, with little effort, pierce between two and four layers of heavy denim cloth. They do what was required, can't ask more than that. Please with the purchase.

These are an interesting set of markers. They lay color down fast and heavily, far more so than any other markers we've used. They're double ended. One side is a chisel tip which is stiff and about a quarter inch in width, you can, by turning the pen, adjust the width of the pen. The other end appears to be a simple very-fine point marker but once you start using it you realize it's more like a short flexible paint brush. You can, with care lay down incredibly fine lines or with a little pressure or twist widen and extend the line you're putting down. They're remarkably versatile and fairly comfortable in the hand though the caps are a bit hard to get on and off (especially for my 85 year old mother who loves coloring) other than that we'll have to see about longevity. I'll update the review as or if needed. Right now we're very pleased with this set and the variety of colors included.
I received this item in exchange for my review.

I debated hard between three and four stars. The eyes are all cute. Every size wiggles. They seem reasonably well made. They stick well. You can put eyes on just about everything you own.On the down side I found I had to use a razor sharp knife to get the backing off even the largest of eyes. I just could not get a fingernail under the edge of any I tried. The next problem I ran into is the container is JUST large enough for the number of eyes you receive. After prying the case open, again having to resort to using my knife, static caused eyes of all sizes to stick to the lid and jump from compartment to compartment. I found it impossible to ensure the case was properly closed and not wanting to play a game of 'pick up hundreds of eyes from the carpet' I've put the case into a zip-top bag where even if they get out they can't make a mess.
I seriously suggest adult supervision, when allowing children to use these or play with items they've been placed on as they're small and can be a choking hazard. In the end they're what they described. They're very cute and we're having fun putting eyes everywhere so I went with four stars.
I received this item in exchange for my review.

I know this sounds stupid, but I like these because they put perfect holes in the filters of my cigarettes. I'm in my 60s and my doc wants me to quit smoking. That ain't gonna happen. Yet, I don't want to keep breathing in all the junk in the cigs that makes my daily walking more difficult. These put in perfect holes that do not rip the paper of the filter apart when they pierce other side. Most pins I've tried don't do that. These pins are nice and sharp, and putting the holes in the filters lets in more air and cuts down on the junk when I inhale.
It probably sounds crazy to you, but take it from me. It works. My lungs work better.
I would love to see the machine that these are fashioned on.

I ordered this last minute the day before Halloween because my son wanted to be a pumpkin and, being in the middle of a pandemic, we couldn't run to Party City or find orange face paint anywhere. So I ordered this and paid for speedy shipping. Luckily, I got it the day before Halloween, but it didn't work for us. First, I didn't know this was dry cake makeup. I thought it was cream. You need to add water. It's basically the pain that street buskers use to paint your kids' face. But no matter how much or how little water we used, we couldn't get anything but a thin consistency, and very little in the way of the vibrant full coverage you see on the package. I don't know if it was me, or if we got an old product, but it just didn't work. Plus, instructions are pretty much non-existent. The way I found tips and tricks was by going to YouTube. Still, the people demonstrating the product obviously had way more experience using it than I did, so there was no learning curb. It was very disappointing, all things considered. I gave it to a teacher I know who works in theater to see if she could make head or tails of it, but I won't be buying this product again.

Maybe this set fits some Thomas track but the female and male ends are straight in the middle of the track. The track we have, The Thomas The Train Zoo Set, the female and male ends are angled or curved just off center so they do not match up with this track. I would send it back but decided to keep it because it has four times the amount of Thomas track and of course the grooves match up with the trains.

The amount of track can be overwhelming for a toddler--so many smaller tracks to sort through. I would have preffered more long track and fewer short track of curved and stfraight.

I tried to build a fork using the track separators, but when I tried to reconnect with it the ends didn't match. I looked for one with two male ends and couldn't find them, but I guess I didn't look hard enough because it shows some in the picture and as I said there were scores of small track. No adapters for the Thomas track I have though.

I took the stars off because this set does not fit Thomas track as represented.

Buy it if you don't mind it won't fit the Thomas track unless you have straight male and female ends.

There are several issues with this. First of all, it is small - the entire structure, with the magnifying lens attached is about 5.5" tall. The lens is very small, and distorts everything unless you hold it very close to the lens. I ended up buying a much taller and larger one instead, and I removed the lens from this one and may use the clips as third hands if needed. It was essentially useless as a magnifier, as you have to hunch over to use it, close to your work surface, and there is not enough room beneath the lens to manipulate whatever you are painting or working on because it is so close to the work surface/tabletop. The new one I replaced it with is about 12" tall, and the magnifier is more than twice the diameter, and is lighted. Cost more than double, but worth it. I cannot recommend this.

The poster itself is actually great and is printed on a very thick, high quality stock. I would have been pleased with it, were I not expecting it to be laminated. If you want a piece of Mad Men memorabilia in your place, and are thinking of ordering it, I definitely recommend it. Note: it is quite a bit darker than the Mad Men print, but I love it anyway.

Cutting it down to size does not seem to have affected anything though, and enabled it to stick without creasing. My arm leans there all the time though--which is why the seat cracked there in the first place. Again, time will tell if it holds up. Hopes are high. -1 star for the price. $20 for something so simple seems like an awful lot--definitely feels overpriced.

I received this as a free review sample. Since Tinfoot's excellent April 15, 2015 video review covers this tool in detail I will discuss things you need to know and some personal experiences.

First, watch the video review that I mentioned for the physical aspects of this tool. Like others I checked Youtube for actual use of this and similar cutters, many of which are variations on the same theme and process. If you have cut wine or beer bottles in the past using home made rigs you probably do not need this. If you want to get into the act, but do not want to design and build your own tool, this one is nicely made and works perfectly. You still need to have the skills and patience to go through the cutting process, and as others have mentioned that entails a learning curve.

Also note that neither this tool nor similar ones mentioned on Youtube are going to allow you to crank out tumblers or other objects made from cut down bottles very quickly. It's still a manual process with a few steps. Or at least I will never achieve a high production rate. However, this is adjustable and well constructed, and can probably be adapted to more uses than just wine and beer bottles. I donated mine to a friend who loves anything to do with crafts and she loves it. That is testimony enough for me.

I received a pack of these as a free review sample. I have finally used them up for personal use so can provide real life observations.

They are perfect for cold or dry snacks, although are a little flimsy for heavy food. I have used them for single-serving snack bowls for salty snacks (pretzels, chips, nuts and the like), ice cream, and even mixed, fresh olives in olive oil. It's perfect for those, and even the olive oil did not seep through.

For hot foods the wax coating can change the flavor, not to mention that the thin construction is not good for handling hot dishes. But these are designed for parties, so I am not dinging it a star for that. Also, while these are obviously for children's parties, they are also perfect for 'It's a Boy!' parties and showers. This brand also has pink ones for it's a girl events, but the bottom line is to think beyond the obvious because these are not for kids only.

I received this as a review sample and have used it to examine nibs on vintage fountain pens. The magnification is not ideal for that since even the 5X inset is not powerful enough to see defects in the tipping and tines. On the other hand it is powerful enough to read the inscriptions on most nibs. Likewise engravings on the bodies, caps and rings The internal light provides enough illumination to allow you to more clearly examine details, but for me I would have preferred a stronger, more focused light source.

One thing is this is extremely stable when you are doing semi-detailed work or detailed work on larger objects. The base held rock solid to a kitchen counter that somethings serves as my work bench. I do want to clarify that the jaws opens to 1.5" as one review stated and not 1 5/8 that another claimed. What makes this exceptionally stable is the top jaw floats on a swivel and perfectly aligns to the perpendicular regardless of the arc of the jaws as it opens. Plus the stabilizing legs hold the entire unit in place without any movement whatsoever.

The clips to hold smaller objects are a nice addition. They are sufficiently strong and stiff to hold objects up to 3.7 ounces (I tested it with various weights and at 3.8 ounces there was a slight sag.)

Overall, this is handy for a number of uses. Mine will probably be confined to assessing vintage fountain pens and doing minor repairs or restorations. I will say that this is the most stable clamp-on magnifier I have ever used, and love the design of that mechanism as well as the strength of the clamp and magnifier goosenecks.

I'll address considerations before giving this to children first. While this can open up an avenue for expression, there are some things that require adult supervision before handing this set over. I received mine as a review sample and spent some time testing the markers on a few surfaces.

It's important to know that these markers need to be primed before you use them the first time. Moreover, there is a priming process that is less involved for each time you use them after the initial prime. Finally, some surfaces, including some chalkboards are not good candidates for these markers. These are reasons why an adult should be involved before giving them to a child - to explain how to properly use them. Also regardless of age you need to read the ebook that you will receive otherwise you'll be frustrated and tempted to 'chalk' them up as defective.

What I love after they have been primed per the ebook that you will receive is the liquid chalk smoothly flows from the tip. It's much smoother than a Magic Marker (probably due to some lubricating properties of the chalk formula used), and the colors are absolutely vivid. I performed an initial test of mine using the white marker on a light brown envelope and the opacity I was able to achieve was directly related to the amount of pressure I used and how much I layered trhe strokes. So you can get nuances and textures from these markers after you get the hang of their flow characteristics.

Above I mentioned that some chalkboards are not good surfaces. This is explained in the ebook. Specifically, some chalkboards use chalk paint for the writing surface and are porous. These are not intended for any type mof porous surface, including some papers with high rag content. On the other hand, they work beautifully on metal, glass and plastics.

If you do accidentally use them on the wrong surface and stain it, the booklet advises using an ammonia based cleaner like Windex to remove it. I did not test that.

Bottom line: these are smooth flowing, vividly colored markers that allow you some control over line and coloring characteristics. They are not a general purpose marking or art tool, but for many uses they are the right tool for the right job.

And that doesn't consider how much fun the game can be. I enjoy all types of games, but my family tends to be very wary of many of the games I come up with because they can be complex and difficult to learn. I talked my parents into trying it and at the end of the first game, my father said. "Shall we play again." After that game it was my mother who was saying, "I could play this again." I recently purchased a knee-length sweater with a hood and pockets in black cashmere.  It is a "cardigan" but had no buttons or buttonholes, so it just hangs open.  I started searching for "frogs" thinking I would end up purchasing the ones made from fabric cord - then I saw these.  Wow!  I love that they look "vintage" and that they came in a "matte" finish.  I'm not a "blingy"  person - don't like gold or silver buttons or buttons that don't match the garment.  They were easy to sew on and look really great.  I took the sweater on a trip and used it for everything - from a blanket on the plane, to a coat and even a bath robe.  These closures made it perfect.

I use them mostly for cutting paper products, like printer paper, despite my gnarly arthritic hands.  Once I have established a good entry cut, I can just hold down the scissors and push them through the paper (or plastic) with ease.

What happened to the wonderful embroidery/applique super sharp scissors of years ago that could be used on thin threads and fabrics?  They were also so smooth that I suspect a surgeon would have loved them.  This Allary Ultra Fine Embroidery Scissors are an ultra disappointment.  Don't waste your money.

About fourteen years or more ago, I bought very similar scissors at a local 99 cent shop and regretted not buying at least two more because the 99c scissors are a permanent kitchen tool for me. I've bought several other worthless, but more expensive scissors at Staples, Walmart, or through Amazon, including scissors made with titanium and Fiskars brand name scissors. None of those fancy scissors work as well as the 99 cent scissors or this recent acquisition. By comparison, the expensive and name brand ones are junk.

Anyway, I finally came upon these no-name ones at Amazon and they resemble the scissors that I bought at the 99c shop. When they arrived, I noticed that, except for the lip between the two finger loops (which is slightly longer on these) the scissors are practically identical and they cut well, without chewing anything. I recently cut thread as well as the hem and waist on sweatpants that were too large for me and I didn't have a problem with these scissors, despite the arthritic hands. For the price and effective use, I recommend these.

I bought these six tape measures through Amazon from Hobbytoolsupply.com for only $4.43, shipping and handling free; I don't think you can beat that price anywhere else. While the fabric isn't as thick as the two name brand measures that I had to replace due to the fact that my decades old ones somehow developed legs, I believe these six will serve their purpose and will probably withstand the test of time. They are thin enough, however, that you can see shadows of the numbers from the flip side of the tape. For me, that's not a problem. Each tape measure comes in its own plastic case, similar to a pill box. One side is standard and the flip side is both standard and metric. If you want to flip from one type of measure to the other, you need to restart at the opposite end of the tape because the numbers run in opposite directions on each side. So, if you need to do both types of measurements together, use the metric side, which includes standard along the top. The standard side has BOLD, easy to read numbers. The flip side numbers are also large enough and easy to read, but not as BOLD as the standard side. Each tape measure's width is 6/8th of an inch, or 2 centimeters (cm).

The product didn't get five stars because the ends do not measure a full inch; they're about 1/8th" short on each side.

A tiny kit that can save you a lot of heartache with your costume jewellery.
I got this after going through my collection of necklaces and bracelets, and discovering that buying costume jewellery is a hit or miss affair - although you do get some wins, many times you end up with a useless pile of random beads as things fall apart.

This relatively small kit contains enough hooks and loops to repair a drawer full of damaged baubles, and the tools to do the job. While the pliers are kinda obvious as to how you use them, and the tweezers allow you to remove the correct link from it's little cubby hole, the ring looking thing is a bit more challenging. A few videos later, I still don't have the hang of it, but at least I know what it is intended to do.

Recommended if you have a large scale collection of inexpensive costume jewellery that may need some emergency maintenance from time to time.

We go through a lot of cardstock at the office, as we do a great deal of greeting cards, invitations, promotional vouchers, point of sale advertisements and so on, as in-house projects. Quite often we find it difficult to source the quantities we need, and then we have to go scavenger hunting to different stores.

Thanks to Amazon this is no longer an issue, as with a couple of clicks I can have cardstock coming out of the wazoo (figuratively of course) without having to hunt, fetch or lift a finger.

This brand is sturdy and resilient, does not bleed, and best of all, doesn't stick in the inkjet printer when you chuck a whole batch in the tray. It's also a bit thicker than the other cardstock we've bought in the past, perfect for document covers and craft work.

It should be noted however that this particular cardstock isn't perfectly white in color, and actually leans a little towards a light cream. It's a yellow-white shade as opposed to a blue-white shade. This isn't an issue for me, but if you're looking for a whiter white to make your blue colors pop, then look for a whiter shade of pale.

As other people have noted, the packaging of the cardstock is inferior, and many of the reams arrived with the packaging damaged or shredded into pieces. The cardstock itself however was fine.

Amanda Richards, September 12, 2011

If you've one credit card to cut
One document to shred
This gizmo sure can handle that
But stack `em and you're dead

When dealing with a pile of cheques
It'll leave the job undone
It'll start the cut and just give up
Before you've shredded one

If you've got lots of stuff to shred
Well then, I wish you luck
You'll spend more time untangling
The paper when it's stuck

It doesn't cut right to the tip
It stops about half way
This is one gadget you don't need
To get you through the day

Amanda Richards, May 22, 2010

[[VIDEOID:c5ed77fd90bb58030a216929bcbae310]] Well, stick it down, baby, now (Stick it down, baby)
Twist and glue (Twist UHU)
C'mon, c'mon, c'mon, c'mon, baby, now (Come on baby)
Come on and stick it down too (Stick it down too)

You see me twist that little bottle (Twist, little bottle)
You see me twist so fine (Twist so fine)
Come on and twist a little closer, now (Twist a little closer)
And let me know that you're mine (Let me know you're mine)

Seriously now - this is a handy glue bottle. The applicator can be used in three different ways depending on the project you're gluing, and it changes from the pointy one to the flat surface with a twist of your hand.

Almost too cool for school!

Amanda Richards, January 2, 2009

Milo’s bored with everything

And couldn’t see the reason

For learning math or spelling bees

No matter what the season

At home one day he found a box

Not round, but not quite square

ONE GENU-INE TURNPIKE TOLLBOOTH

The label did declare

Intrigued, he jumped into his car

Although this was a toy

Through the tollbooth he then passed,

One jaded little boy

He found himself quite somewhere else

It happened very fast

“WELCOME TO EXPECTATIONS”

said a signpost that he passed

But in this land there was a feud

Between two stubborn brothers

One thought words were number one

While numbers were the other’s

Milo, Humbug, faithful Tock

Must help to set things straight

Get Rhyme and Reason to return

so the feuding will abate

The brilliance of this story lies

In the author’s verbal skill

The places and the characters

Provide a learning thrill

The characters are wonderful

The plotline never dull

You’ll read this story several times

Until your brain is full

So if you are a child at heart

From two to ninety two

I strongly recommend this one

To you, and you, and you

Amanda Richards, April 22, 2006

The thick coats obliterate fine details or patterns and it slightly flows on plain bisque.... but result is amazing and is a great accent. Not food safe for inside but outside is totally appropriate. I grew up on this. It was the standard of strawberry that was second to none. Along with blueberry and Nag Champa, I was Abbey&Sullivan's original groupie and biggest cheerleader! Then, the blueberry disappeared. When it surfaced a decade later it smelled so different, so raunchy, that I was weeping when I wrote them a letter asking them wtf? They had the 'nads to tell me they hadn't changed anything! The olfactory system does NOT lie. I was insulted. And pissed. I wore that stuff for centuries. How dare they? That was back in the 70's!!!!
Fast fwd to today. They won't make Nag Champa anymore. And this stuff smells as tampered with as the blueberry from days of yore.
I won't bother asking why.

The flavours from Nature's Oil are reliably true. With one glaring exception (see my Nag Champa review- if you dare) they're consistent, pleasant, and a great value! This is on the delicate side, I'll throw a few hits of sugar candy in with it from time to time. You won't be disappointed. I have quite a collection from this manufacturer, and I know I'll keep trying more!

Go easy- this stuff isn't watered down with extenders or mixed with any weird undertones, it's exactly as it should be. I like this manufacturer! I must have over a dozen flavours from them. And aside from one glaring exception (see my Nag Champa review- if you dare) they're all true and consistent. A good value as well. I'm relying on my diffusers to keep my world enjoyable- bad enough I live with a smoker, it's presently Winter and the windows can't be open as much as they need to be! Nature's Oil products have been my saviour.
You'll like this! Try mixing it with a good strawberry. Your smellbuds will thank you!

I gotta get more. I really plowed through this bottle! It's that nice.
I really like this manufacturer! With one glaring exception (see my Nag Champa review- if you dare) their scents are yummy, true and consistent. They're a great value as well since you don't need a lot- they don't water down their flavours with extenders like some places do.
You get what's on the label. How refreshing! You won't be disappointed.

I won't put this in a cotton candy catagory- it's on its own as far as being in the 'candy' school of fragrances... just what it says, sugar candy. It's a nifty boost when mixed with a fruity flavour, too!
I like this manufacturer! With one glaring exception (see my Nag Champa review, if you dare) they're consistently yummy and true. Good value, you don't need a lot, I've gotten over a dozen different scents from them over the years... and I'll keep on trying more!

I like this manufacturer! I decided to give this a whirl. Nowadays I have diffusers to make my environment more enjoyable and they're cranking 24/7. Another hit for Nature's Oil :)

Mercy!!!
This is the most vile rendition of Nag Champa I've EVER wrapped my nostrils around. I can't understand it... ya give the top of the bottle a whiff and, nothing. Pop off that dropper thingy and it's a little more, uh-well, fragrant- but not really Nag. Try adding an extra dollop and, heavens to murgatroid!! This stuff will open sinuses in ppl on another floor of your house!!! And as much as I need that (winter time stuffs my nose up) NOT at the expense of having the paint peel off my wall. This stuff is more like a Dragon's Blood on steroids. In a bad way, I like Dragon's Blood!
Here's the REAL tragedy~~ I've never purchased a fragrance from this manufacturer that hasn't been top notch before! And I have aLOT of their fragrances... so, imagine my shock, surprise and heartbreak at this. :(
I LOVE Nag Champa. I've really never met a Nag I've never gotten along with. Until NOW. I can forgive weak... or, say-"off"... but THIS is just atrocious. Run, do not walk, away from this.
Either someone was asleep at the vat and I got a bum batch, or I want an exchange for something else. Or my money back. OR BOTH.

Comfortable to wear. Made of sturdy plastic. The thing i did not like about this item is the light.  It started to overheat once the batteries were inserted.  It proceeded to get even hotter and it never powered on.  I tried new batteries, no difference.i bought these because they come with the light. Very disappointed it does not work.
I use this for all my handmade cards
I make homemade cards and I use this glue ALL the time. It’s great. I have tried double-sided tape but like the glue so much better. Have used this glue for several years.
I have used this product for years in making jewelry. It is the best product I have found for adhesive. I only use it to also make glass magnets. Wonderful product.

First painting I have ever done. I thought they worked well, based on what I know. Which is nothing. 🤣 The colors are rather nice and I found them easy to work with. Truth is, I got into Bob Ross on Prime Video and wanted to paint. I didn't realize he was an oil painter and the difference! So, off to grab some Bob Ross oil paint!

I bought this coin for my 12 year old son so he could start his own coin collection. When he saw it he was really excited. He commented on the detail and art on the coin. It really is done very well and a lot of detail went into this. Very happy with the purchase.
My kids really liked them fond memories of their dad
As expected
Love all the colors

The colors are so similar it is difficult to tell one green from most of the others, etc.  The colors do not range from light version to a much darker one (the blues are too similar....no sky blue and a navy blue.).

The gel pens are 1/3 to 3/4 full.  Some colors will run out much faster then others.  The box they come in is very flimsy so you must pay close attention to what you are doing.  My box came open during shipping and the pens that fell out were hard to put back in the correct position in order to keep them coordinated with the refill packs.  Not for very young children (under 10?).
These are great sponges, sometimes they come too small, but these are definitely great for a starter kit for a new potter.
We got these the same time we ordered or service puppy's vest.  Very good durable product.  I recommend this.

I bought this ink and inkers to give my paper an aged or parchment look.  The results come out nicely for coloring books and free hand illustrations.  I think I will eventually get other colors.  Tim Holtz has a lot of videos on his products that are really excellent for getting ideas how to use them.  It is fun to see how much he enjoys working with and teaching others how to use the products.

I really like this product.  It can be used for other things beside watercolor painting.  Colors can be easily mixed and can be used in watercolor brushes that have the well which is usually used for water. A little goes a long way.  I saw a woman on Youtube use this to refresh her stamp pads. I imagine there are so many more uses I haven't discovered yet.  I am currently using colored pencils and working on a way best to incorporate the two.

These pencils are a great value.  The pigments are nice and the laydown is beautiful.  There are *no color names* but there are numbers.  I put these in their color family before swatching and putting them in a colored pencil case.  When I swatched these pencils, I named them myself as I have a lot of experience with color from my experience as and oil paint artist and teacher. Because these pencils are square, a double hole sharpener only works for these.  However, in order to get these pencils super sharp, a lot of core will be lost and the pencils will become shorter faster because  they are square. However, this brand does offer a round barrel pencil.  There is no open stock but the sets are cheap enough to replace easily.  I have several brands of pencils but I typically use Holbein, Prismacolor, Arteza and Starjoy.  I highly recommend these though for performance and price.

These have no color name on the pencil in English, only Chinese.  There are numbers.  I had fun swatching these in my swatch book and I named mine according to my color education including the number on the pencil.  You will need a pencil sharpener with the 9 - 12 mm hole to sharpen these.  I use an electric sharpener for all my pencils. Be aware that some pencils, like Prismacolor, may have frequent breakage due to their very soft core, but, the buttery lay down is well worth it.  Brutfuner does not seem to have a problem with breakage.  I sharpened all of them and had no problems whatsoever.  Brutfuner is a harder core but does lay down well.  These pencils are primarily oil based but as I understand it, the oil is combined with a wax.  Although, more layers may be required with some colors to get the desired shade, they lay down nicely and hold a point for detail unlike Prismacolor.  These are a great buy for the money so I recommend them.

Seriously, you might want to get these with the 15% off coupon while you can.  This is another trusted buy from a Youtube channel "Color My World".  The host recommended these and I have bought colored pencils from her other recommendations.  These pencils lay down like butter, a close second to my Prismacolor and Holbein.  Even if you had a couple problem pencils, it is still an excellent buy.  I have a couple with a tip broken but the rest have been fine and sharpen well with my electric pencil sharpener.  I truly believe you will be happy with these.  They are a treat to use in a coloring book or original art.  I have used them for both..

I have only used this gouache a couple of times but I love it. It is a nice opaque white.  It mixes well with Ph. Martin watercolor and used alone, goes on like butter.  I paints smoothly and evenly.  This gouache is reasonably priced, a great value. I highly recommend this for all artwork needs.

These little watercolor journals are great and very good thick quality of paper.  I believe these are cold pressed paper.  One side is more rough and the other smoother, so you have a choice.  I have used this paper for graphite pencil,  colored pencil, watercolor and pen and ink.  These are a great size for pocket or purse.

I love this pencil case.  I needed something small so that I can take it traveling instead of taking my larger pencil cases.  It is just the right size to accomodate all the art tools I use for a project that I am currently working on.  Not only this, but I love the colorful pattern of the case.  Also, being able to remove two of the pencil holder panels is very convenient.  This case holds 72 pencils so that I could put all of the 48 Faber-Castell watercolor pencils I wish to buy and have lots of room left over (24 slots) for odds and ends such as pencil size erasers, fine point ink pens, and pencil blenders.  Not only this, but there is lots of room in the "bottom"  underneath the pencil sleeves to put more pencils, erasers, sharpeners, phone, phone cords, or whatever othe small items you feel you might need in your travels in order to make those items more accessible.  In the "top" there is a zippered netting pocket as well as some small fabric slots to put stuff in.  This design works well for me and I highly recommend this case especially for convenience, organization, capacity and style.

This is a really nice pencil case.  I already filled up all the slots with some pencils left over.  I should have gotten one with a bigger capacity but this one is so nice.  It holds all my watercolor pencils and my color mechanical pencils.  I like having the shoulder strap and a handle on the case.  These features make it especially nice for travel. The nylon(?) material is really nice and feels durable.  The zipper seems durable and it is easy to operate.  I highly recommend this product.  It is so nice to get organized.

This is essential especially if you are going to a gem or even a gun show.  When shopping for old or antique weapons this loop enable you to more easily see the manufacturer's name and numbers on each piece.  For gem shows, the loop is important in determining the cut, color and quality of a gemstone.

I got this tool for Christmas and it is fantastic.  I like being able to adjust the temperature control for different patterns.  The SE WP 28 tip set fits fine and make this a complete tool for woodburning.

These tips are a great variety and fit my Wood Hollow  woodburners.  This kit gives me complete versatility of lines and angles.  I think there is a tip for everything I want to do in woodburning. Great Christmas gift.
Great tool.  Comes through for fine detail and I use it in conjunction with carving tools.  Highly recommend this for burning detail.

Lots of purple, using two different knot methods the colors weren’t noticeably Rasta. Would not buy again.

I bought this pattern a year ago and just got around to doing it in February. Finally finished today and it's really quite pretty. The Siamese cats look good and the coloration is correct for the breed.

90% of the threads came apart easily without snagging, when one did get knotted, it was only a small loss. The ink dissolved and vanished entirely after soaking in cold water for 20 minutes. I usually let the finished image soak, dry fully and soak a second time, but this was good to go after just one soak.

We're partners on building three short term rental cabins near a National Park. In order to comply with local 911 regulations, each cabin needs it's own street number. These stencils made it easy to paint the numbers in two visible locations on every cabin. I just taped them up, attached the numbers, taped an overflow sheet around it and spray painted. We can reuse the stencils as needed in the future.

I only use printed cross stitch patterns because counting the old fashioned way gives me a headache. This moonlit water pattern is really nice once it's finished, don't let big swathes of dark colors overwhelm you. The threads are smooth and I haven't any problems with them.

I just spent a week on a cruise ship and found myself cross stitching rather than reading or partying by the pool. I finished a project and started this one on the ship. The final image should be gorgeous, but this is a large pattern and will take months for me to finish. There are a lot of different colors due to the detail in the image, so pay attention and be patient.

These are much smaller than my usual cross stitch projects - maybe 1/3 the size and significantly fewer colors than my last four projects. (36 colors vs 15 in the fox for example) That makes these go very quickly and I can get one done in a couple of weeks rather than literal months of work.

The fox is adorable and super easy to do and they included a little extra thread in every color. My set came with a needle and clear instructions on the inside papers. The printed pattern washes off completely and the finished stitching is quite attractive. I would totally suggest this set for a beginner to cross stitching.

I cross stitch daily and have two enormous patterns I'm working on for a friend. Unfortunately, one of them came short on floss in two colors. I am immensely relieved to find those two exact shades in this collection. I can finish the project without delay and it will look the way it is supposed to. These threads are easy to handle and don't fray nearly as much as the floss that came with both patterns, I'm not sure what the difference is, but this is definitely a superior product. I would totally buy this set again in the future

I've been working on this horse for many weeks now and I'm really happy with the final image. It's lovely and has a timeless feel. The threads were easy to handle and very few actually snagged when pulling the three strands apart.

This is a big cross stitch pattern, but they included a little extra thread in each color and the pattern rinsed away cleanly with no sign of it left behind. I'll buy from this brand again. (The photo was midway through the pattern)

I thoroughly enjoy cross stitching and I've realized that the stamped pattern method is far less aggravating than trying the old fashioned count stitching on a blank fabric. This set is a nice collection of fun patterns - especially the Groot. Unfortunately, not every pattern has enough thread to actually complete. Two of them were obviously short before I even started them and Groot is probably going to run out of thread at the 3/4 finished mark. I'll buy some more at some point simply because I like Groot and want to complete it, but I really hate having to try to match and purchase additional thread when it should have been a complete set.

I love cross stitch work. I find it relaxing and completion of a project is worth the hours and hours of work. This one is pretty and would probably be gorgeous finished.

Unfortunately, I've realized that a blank canvas with no printed pattern is just too darn difficult. I end up with a headache in a half hour trying to mess with this count and stitch system. Perhaps someone with younger eyes or more patience won't have the same issue. Lesson learned.

I took up cross stitch after a 20 year break and I've really been enjoying it. This is the 2nd pattern from this company that I've worked on.

The pattern is very easy to see and the thread color codes are distinct and easy to follow. This is an 11 count, so you split the 6 strand threads into 3 strands. I'm finding that the threads lays flat and stays put. However, watch your waste .... My last pattern ended up short on two key colors. Hopefully, this one won't.

We have a friend who is a nut for all things Yoda from every possible point of view. So I've put aside my other cross stitch projects just to make this one in time from Christmas. I think she will love it.

For me, the enormous variations of green thread is difficult to tell apart. Fortunately, the thread is all sorted and coded appropriately in the kit. The fabric is holding up despite my amateur handling. I'm not having any problems, but this hasn't been my favorite pattern. It is definitely a work of love.

Holy cow this is much larger than I realized. Honestly, this will be a months long project for me. That's ok, just be aware that for a beginner or someone like me who's getting back into cross stitching after many years, this pattern may be overwhelming.

The printed pattern is clear and easy to see. Differentiation between the thread colors is pretty clear and they did include the color code on the fabric. The included paper code is huge and broken out into sections. They do include needles, but these are thin with a narrow eye - I prefer my own slightly large needles with a big eye. It's just easier for me. I suspect the final design will be gorgeous, so I'm willing to put in the months of stitching.

I hate the thin, cheap needles in most cross stitch kits. I bought this to keep a supply of large eye needles on hand. There are several sizes so you can use what works best for your fingers and project. The threaders work perfectly and are easy to use. Everything fits 8n my little sewing kit bag.

I thought that I was getting a set of patterns for both embroidery and cross stitching. Nope, not exactly as described. These three pieces are embroidery only.

I have never done embroidery before, so there's a learning curve for me. The instructions on the different stitches are included with each set as are the needles, threads and hoop things. I'm leaning towards preferring cross stitching after working on this for a few days.

I haven't done cross stitching in literally 20 years. However, this particular design drew my attention immediately and I decided to try again. A new hand/eye coordination project is always good for the brain.

There's enough thread if you follow the directions and split each piece into three strands. The threads are soft, smooth and haven't been prone to bunching or knots. This pattern is big and there are over 20 colors with several spots that require stopping and changing colors after just four or five stitches. Because of that, this might not be the best choice for a first time cross stitching effort.

My complaint is the seriously garbage quality needles included. The eyes are weak and poorly constructed. I literally had one snap off at the eye in mid stitch 15 minutes into it. The second needle is still intact, but I don't trust it. I actually found some sturdy, slightly larger, far more appropriate needles at a local store - 10 for a buck. Now that I have real needles, I can work on this pattern more quickly and without drama. It's a lovely image and I want to finish it.

I'm restoring and remodeling a ranch cabin from the studs up. That means all new drywall, doors, cabinets and the trim for everything. Some painting and staining projects require a delicate touch and a small brush. I really love these small foam brushes for doing a smooth, light finish on wood trim.

On a smooth, sanded surface these don't snag or fall apart with paint and stain. I was able to stain 8 pieces of door molding with a single brush. I also used a couple of these for the polyurethane finish on the same molding pieces. The foam will deteriorate quickly with poly, but that's a known issue. These brushes give a smooth stroke and allow the paint to get into all the little crevices and crannies.

We're remodeling a cabin and redid the entire porch with new lumber. I hate that weathered grey look that wood gets over time, so I immediately stained it. We did samples of several colors and the Sedona Red was the prettiest.

One coat was all we needed. Tomorrow, I'll do the polyurethane over it and we'll be finished. This is a dark red stain so if you want a light or more subtle color, this probably isn't what you need. I love the deep red and I'm really happy with it. Not sure why it costs under ten bucks locally and not here as well.

This is cuter in person than the photos online. It's a shiny, metallic silver pouch made of one big zipper that opens all the way so you can lay it flat if you wanted. It actually holds a fair amount of random stuff. I'm using it for make up stuff in my travel kit because the long, rectangle shape is good for various brushes. I can see someone else using it for pens or hair scrunchies abd the like in a big purse.

I don't color unless my 6 year old niece is here. She's at that age where a new coloring book is worth celebrating. She always wants me to color with her so I figured having some pencils for this was a good idea.

This is a nice collection of colors in various shades. They can be sharpened in a regular pencil sharpener without falling apart. The color shades out as light or dark as you want depending on how much pressure you put on the pencil. I really like the storage case - all of the pencils stay in place and I can put them away neatly when my niece isn't here.

The last time I did t-shirt iron on transfers was in high school. A lot has changed for the better in lo these many years. This system from Avery is actually very easy to use at every step from the Avery website software to the printing and application on your shirts. It's not perfect, but for the simplicity and as an inexpensive choice for a home project, this is pretty darn good.

I used the Avery website and did two designs. One with my copyrighted slogan and my own photographs imported from my computer's files and a second one using the Avery clipart drawing of a horse. Both setups worked the same and the program was straight forward in placement, sizing, font choices, and colors. It allows you to see a preview of the final look before it reverses the image to print and you can save your designs to use again in the future. Pro Tip - You do need to print a sample on plain paper first so you know what side is up before you put the transfer sheet in your printer.

The bigger the image, the longer you need to apply the iron. Pro Tip - Use a dry iron only and as hot as it will go. The large images took about 90 seconds to 2 minutes to fully set. In one photo you can see under the horse where the transfer material is peeling up - that was because I didn't have the heat high enough on my iron. Then you let the shirt cool for several minutes before peeling away the transfer sheet to reveal your images. I did mine on a regular ironing board, but the instructions do suggest a hard surface with a pillow case underneath the shirt.

One caveat - this is indeed only meant for white shirts. I put a small horse on a grey shirt pocket and the transfer material is really visible after washing and drying. Like ugly, eye catching....don't want to wear the shirt in public ever kind of visibly ugly. Additionally, after a single trip through the washer on the delicate cycle and the dryer on delicate low heat, all of the images on five shirts look faded and a couple are showing a few cracks. See the photos. We haven't even worn these yet. Still.... for cute shirts to wear at an upcoming animal shelter fund raiser, this project was easier than expected and I will be doing 5 more tomorrow.

I thought this would be a cute little hook for dog leashes and a harness by the back door. It looks good on the wall and the two screws that came with it fit properly and ensure that its flush with the wall. However, the hooks themselves are actually too short/shallow to hold the leashes or the thicker large dog harness. Too shallow for dish towels also - they slide right off. So now it's reduced to holding two baseball caps. Not exactly what I wanted by the door, but they are handy for rainy days.
