This is well made and the stitching is clear, tight and easy to read. Got this for friend to put on his gear bag as he re-deploys to Afghanistan. Earned me a hug and a kiss. Good enough.

These colored pencils are definitely a great value.  They color smooth and the colors are easily build upon to achieve darker colors.  I almost returned these, but am very glad I opened them and gave them a try.  I really like them.  I also was impressed with the packaging.  I thought they would be all bumped around and have broken "leads" in them due to shipping.  However, when I rattled the package around before opening it up...they were securely packaged inside the nice tin.  Great product.

The way the lids are attached to each compartment is not very sturdy.  I bought a total of 4 of these and so far I have had a lid either fall off or completely break off on 2 out of the 4 boxes.  Not really the quality I thought I was purchasing.  If the lids didn't fall off, this would be great for my purposes.  The compartments are small, so just keep that in mind for what your application will be.

I would not give these away as a gift as they look like they have been used. There is regular scotch tape used to seal the package. And even that was already open when I received these from Amazon.

However, while I have not tried every single color yet, the ones I did try out seem to work fine.

These seem like a very easy way to get started with watercolors...and looks a lot less messy.
After spending  too much time threading & re-threading previously owned Brother sergers, finally purchased a keeper. Now I can actually finish projects in real time.

The first machine arrived broken, but the replacement  works beautifully. My previous Brother was an exercise in frustration,  as the thread continually broke after only a few seams. This machine makes serging a relaxing, enjoyable activity. Very happy with purchase.

Well balanced, comfortable grip & a razor sharp, smooth cut on both light & heavier weight materials makes these scissors FAR superior to every "big name" brand I've  used during 50+ years a seamstress. Ended up purchasing additional  pairs for portable sewing & crochet totes! Highly reccomend!
Lots of basic, versatile garments that permit seamstress to use imagination
Exceptional  quality, heavy gauge clear vinyl. Would buy again.

Provides ability to have a measuring tape in each room so you can grab a measurement quickly., which is advantageous  if you're  redecorating. Dislike  having metric on one side, but convenience outweighs this one flaw.

Have used this pattern more times than I can count, but lost it when we moved. Could only find it on Amazon. Pattern is suggests fleece, but by adjusting the size, you can use any stretchy knit with pile--including faux fur--and end up with some very cool, unique slippers
Nice, lightweight, ergonomic. Good price for set.
Love this brand of calligraphy markers & this particular  assortment is especially  versatile. Amazon was offering a better price than my local craft store. Highly recommend!
Incredible  machine!  Practically thinks for itself. Informative DVD very helpful. Great value!
Scissors arrived with one pair bent, plastic t broken on another pair & had to be oiled before even trying to cut with them. Yep. These oughta last about 5 minutes.
Great deal! Received enough leather to make skirt; cut my own lacing from scraps; will purchase more

THIS is gorgeous, feminine, delicate fabric. With one yard & some sewing experience, one can make a beautiful little spring jacket to dress up a tank top & maxiskirt or even the right jeans. Cautionary note: one will feel extremely  girly wearing an item made from this fabric!

Best & least expensive way to replenish your most used/favorite dyes. I have found that the colors are so intense that they can be mixed with twice the suggested amount of water. Also have achieved interesting effects by using a squirt bottle to apply colors, & the color seems to set better if I give the item a light mist of spray starch & allow to dry overnight before first washing.
NOT duct tape!  So thin you can see through it.  Could be used as "postal tape" to seal boxes, but I will avoid 3M in the future.

Plush, soft, thick, no flaws, washes well with no pilling. Excellent buy on high quality fabric with convenience of having it brought right to your own door. Would not hesitate to buy from this particular manufacturer again.  ☺
It's great for diamond art.  The stand helps as it is easier than laying flat on table.  So far so good.
many broken in pieces
I love these patches. I already brought more of these. Nice big patch, nice color's. Packed perfect & delivery was on time!
Very nice patch! Look's good on my son's Jean's!
Beautiful color's, sticks good & fast. If u need patches, buy these.
I could not believe the quality of these scissors! I don't know how they get the price point this low with such quality! Just buy them. You can thank me later.
Great adapters
Good stuff you really have to know what your doing to get it to work.
I've only used the gold so far but it's very shiny and exactly what I wanted. It does bleed through fabric if your not careful though
Do your Research Before Buying these wood resin Rods. Local hardwear store has ones made in U.S  without Formeldahyde Resin from Russia.

These safety pins are not like Any I have had before. These are SO delicate they bend & crumple when just gently trying to attach them to a shirt fabric. This is a WASTE of $. Must be Chinese metal becuz they bend easily & wont hold even the thinnest fabric in place! Ridiculous.In all my years I have never seen such a WORTHLESS Safety pin! Dont WASTE your $ on these.
Sewing machine bobbins.  Just what I needed.  Thank you.
Love Coats & Clark thread.  Colors are great.  Very strong.
I do a lot of sewing and these scissors cut my fabrics with ease.  I highly recommend.
I used it to get closer to my sewing machine needle and closer to the buckle lop I was sewing onto my guitar strap.
I love these pencils! They have made my colorings even more gorgeous. I uploaded a few colorings. In the one with the pink haired girl you can see the colors reflecting the light.
I like this bag. It holds all my colored pencils, and I am able to close a few of my little notebooks inside as well, along with a few pens. Definitely recommend!

Arrived a day earlier than promised, in December, Christmas crunch delivery time!
Was simply but well packaged in stiff, flat cardboard, do not received bent. A bit bigger than we wanted, but Durable material that we can adapt to our needs.
These stencils are obviously made to be able to be cleaned and kept to use over & over!
clumsy to use. Magnets not strong enough. Should have returned it while I had the chance. Really need a separate stand to put this on.
This is really extra long. Easy to read. Both inches and centimeters are marked very boldly. A length to measure any garment.
This tape measure is attractive, and a pleasure to use. Works very smoothly. Easy to read the markings for both inches and cm.
Works very nicely and stands up on a table. Of course you have to remember to click it after every row.
Very nice item for us knitters. Smooth and light. A great addition to my knitting tools. For marking or holding seams.

These would be great for small projects. I like the size. The boards are thin and could use a coat of gesso before painting on them.
Overall, not a bad value.
This basin is going to be very helpful next time  I set up to paint. I've always wanted one of these, but end up just using a very tippable plastic cup for brush water.

I love Aurifil thread and it is the main brand I use. My Brother machine loves it.

I have to buy all my threads online, sight unseen, since no stores anywhere within an hour of us carry Aurifil.

I had some cute but slightly weird-colored fabric I was trying to find thread for. I was using colors like coral to try to find it. They should call it salmon or shrimp color! But no matter what, it was a great match for my coral fabric, and always happy with Aurifil.

If you like to craft, you know how much fun it is to gave a nice assortment of things that can really make something "pop".

This is an assortment of wooden buttons painted to look like snowmen carrot noses. You get a few different sizes as well.

These are so cute! You can sew them on a project or glue them on. How cute would it be to make Christmas stockings with snowmen and an adorable 3D nose?

I am very happy with this assortment and would definitely buy them again.

I love metallic paint pens. They are great for making accents on your other craft projects. I am not a big fan of this brand of pens. It took pressing fairly hard to get writing to even show. I left it sit for about 10 minutes. I used my finger to see if it smeared or dried, and it easily REMOVED the writing entirely! I hope that was only because of the paper finish, but I'm thinking it just isn't thick enough to really be good.

I would say these are not the best quality of gel, and would not repurchase this brand.

[[VIDEOID:b545d3ddbb66036f847fa4ddaeea8095]] This ribbon has the appearance of burlap in the weave and color, but the texture is soft to the touch. It is not actual scratchy burlap.

This is NOT wired ribbon. The patterns are screened on and well done - not a bunch of partial designs. The reindeer have a beautiful copper glitter appearance, but it did not shed glitter everywhere.

This would be perfect ribbon for big decorative bows or to wrap a wreath. It could probably even be used on a smaller holiday tree.

I was very pleased with the quality and would buy this one again.

Ok, where have these been all my crafting life?
I love buttons. As in, obsessed with buttons. I always have been.
These are adorable buttons in carrot shapes. They are orange with black details on them. They are very cute and I was happy with the quality. I would definitely buy these again.

Somehow, I thought this was for several little disposable trays. But nope - you only get one tray.

It is small, but looks sturdy enough for at least a few rounds of painting. I hope it cleans up easily - I have not had any projects to do since receiving this item.

Okay, I LOVE these little hats!!
I confess that I have no idea exactly what I am going to do with them, but they are undeniably adorable!

I was very happy with the quality and overall construction. I would definitely buy these little guys again.

I do a lot of crafting, and use lots of ribbon. I wasn't quite sure what to expect on these.

First off, I was very happy with the red color. I kind of expected a faded or tweaked red, but it is a good, bright red.

Now, this is in no way what I would call tulle ribbon. It is nice, but not really tulle. It is more of a polyester/ nylon mesh. It has much more body than I expected. It will certainly still be useable, but if I had purchased based on "tulle ribbon", this would be going back.

Overall, a nice enough craft ribbon, but heavier than expected, with a very different texture.

I was pleasantly surprised by these simple bell decorations. You get a generous amount (although they are packed like sardines!). The gold toned bells have tiny little "clappers" in them - not enough to make a real bell sound, but it does make a sound. The pine sprig is also cute.

I plan to use some of these outdoors, so will see how they hold up to the elements. These could also be cute on name tag decorations, ornament toppers or even clipped on to an artificial tree.

I would buy these again. They are very basic, but everything works.

This is a pretty crochet hook. I ordered size G, which is what I use the most to make ear warmers.

I had to stop crocheting for a while, because the aluminum hooks, while comfortable enough, were rubbing calluses on my fingers.

This hook comes encased with this soft covering. There are flattened spots for better grip. I am hoping that it will be a little easier on my hand.

Now, I have not actually crocheted with this hook yet. Should it turn out to have any hindrances or problems, I will update my review.

This is a basic crafting or floral wire. You get three "sleeves" of it. It should be perfect for attaching all kinds of decorations to the bamboo sticks I ordered to make simple outside decorations for the holidays.

I am leery of any measuring tapes, especially those made in foreign countries. Their measurements are often slightly shorter than standard American measurements.

I sew for crafting and quilting purposes. Being even 1/8" off can make a big difference in your project outcome. My gold standard is my cutting mat. I use that for all my cutting, so any tape measure used must line up with my mat. These tape measures did. I was pleasantly surprised, as I've had to toss out more than a few tape measures that were off, some by as much as 3/8"!

So, so far so good.

This is a smaller hole
hole punch. The handles have a foamy covering, making them comfortable to use.

The punch is just the right size for my purposes - I wanted to be able to punch a small hole through which I could thread some decorative twine and attach either a business card or price tag for crafted items.

Very happy with the punch.

This is used to make temporary markings on fabric. I really liked the color assortment. It allows you to be ready for nearly any color fabric. They also come in a plastic container that keeps them all together. Several of mine were broken, but there are still plenty for my fabric marking needs.

You get six (6) gel pens which write in white. It took a bit to get the gel down.
I wrote on the package itself, which has a coated paper finish. The writing was not what I had hoped. It took pressing fairly hard to get writing to even show. Then, as you can see in the word in all capital letters, it leaves the little "balls" wherever you lift the pen. I left it sit for about 10 minutes. I used my finger to see if it smeared or dried, and it easily REMOVED the writing entirely! I hope that was only because of the paper finish, but I'm thinking it just isn't thick enough to really be good.

I wanted these to put white "dots" in eyeballs when painting. I hope the pens do better on wood or fabric, but I didn't have any handy to test it.

I would say these are not the best quality of gel, and would not repurchase or recommend this brand.

This is a nice assortment of black-based cotton fabric. There are several cute patterns included. Note that these are VERY thin, with a somewhat loose weave.
If I was doing a special project or wanted to gift something, I would not include this fabric. I have my doubts about it holding up well to washings and its longevity. (One of my photos is holding up a dark piece towards the daylight. You can see how thin it really is.)

But, it would be just fine for a fun project, a quick couch throw, doll bedding or pet bedding.

[[VIDEOID:7cd4783d4597a7823f2e8babbb5c5b40]] There is probably no such thing as too many needles for crafting. This is a package with a lot of plastic yarn or elastic needles.

These are great for finishing off the ends of crochet or knit projects. With a good quantity like this, you could tuck one into every package of yarn you are using.

The one precaution I would use is watching the edges. As with many mass-produced plastic items, these have some rough edges, especially at the eye end. The sides also have quite a few "funky" edges on them and some of the eyes are not completely open, but the eye ends are the worst, with rough spots where they were probably popped loose from molds.
The reason that matters is that these rough edges WILL snag your yarns. If you try to run a thin elastic with them, it will likely catch on your fabrics as well, especially if it is a knit or polyester.

So, it is nice to have a large quantity, but I'd take a nail file or emery board to the end before using one. These are not high quality needles.

I am not very bendy anymore. But I still like to craft and sew. Sometimes, pins fall off the table. I wanted something that was extendable and could easily pick them up off the floor so they did not get stepped on.

This item works very well and is pretty sturdy. It is likely made more for dropped screws and nails. It is a bit heavy for my hands, but it is definitely worth the price.

These are adorable! They are indeed very small, but nice and smooth.

My daughter and I plan to paint these with acrylic paints and seal them, then leave them in various subtle places for someone to find. We hope they will bring a smile to someone's face.

I would definitely buy these again.

I use feathers for crafting or cat toy purposes. So my expectations could be different, should you be looking for millinary or boa usage.

These came packaged beautifully. They are bagged in small groups by feather type, then vac-sealed together. In fact, they are packaged so well, I am not going to open them until I am ready to use them.

I saw no problems at all, and all the feathers arrived intact, without broken shafts. Excellent.

This is a package of eight seam rippers. If you sew, or if you don't but want to learn, you will need seam rippers!

You get four small, short-handled ones and four long-handled ones. The handles are plastic. You get one short and one long of each included color: red, blue, green and yellow.

Keep these with your sewing supplies, one by your nightstand, a couple in the kitchen too. They are not going to last forever, but they are sure handy to have on hand for tiny tasks. Of course, they are not as comfy in your hand as the ones with the padded handles or handmade rippers, but they still get the job done.

These are not top-of-the-line brushes. If you are a professional artist, you probably don't want these.

But - for the rest of us who dabble in painting and/or are crafters, these are great. There is a nice assortment of sizes and tips. I would say these are best for use with acrylics or fabric paints, even though the package states you can use them with oil paints as well (I wouldn't).

Be sure to get them damp in plain water and blot before dipping into your paints, and these should serve you well for crafting needs. Be sure to clean them thoroughly after each use, pinch the bristles tigether and stand them on the non-bristle end to dry. You can get inexpensive brush conditioner here on Amazon which can extend the life of your brushes as well.

Overall, a good crafting brush.

The fabric is nice. The quality is just fine, and is your basic cotton fabric...nothing super luxurious or silky.

What makes this one so fun is the expression on the faces of the kitties. While the blue background is a bit odd, it worked out perfectly for us. My old cotton curtains had sun rot, and I needed something new.

This fit the bill. I had to cut them a little differently, as the pattern was printed in the opposite direction as my cows had been. But it worked out just fine anyway.
Our cats are trying to get used to them. The cows were done in four panels, so there were three "openings" in the curtains. I made only two panels for the cats (because of the print direction). The poor cats keep trying to stick their heads through where the old openings were. 😄 I might have to make these into four panels to help them out!

The cats are adorable and the overall pattern very cheerful. Very happy with my purchase.

This fabric has a very nice hand to it.
I bought it because it was the perfect color and pattern for a quilt I am working on.

But I have to say you do not get much for your money on this one. Sadly, I have zero fabric shops in my area, so I have to shop solely online. I loved the color and pattern, but you get a shocking small amount of fabric for your money. It is too expensive for me to get more to do a second project. I guess we are paying for the name more than the actual fabric. But again, nice quality of fabric.

[[VIDEOID:b7e5c50ac327c0897f15e6df6b276e60]] I've had other metal punches in the past, so was not sure what to think about this mostly-plastic punch.

Well, I am happy with it overall.

It gives a nice, clean punch without enormous effort or hand strength. You can use both the outline and the punches themselves. The punched out hearts collect under the punch in a shallow tray with a sliding cover. No more chasing your punchies all over, or picking them up off the floor. I tested ours with cardstock weight paper, and it did a great job.

My ONLY criticism of this punch is with the actual heart shape. I wpuld have liked to have seen a much more defined peak at the top of the heart. Instead, it is a generically rounded plunge. Not a deal-breaker, but definitely a disappointment.

Overall, lightweight but has nice features and gives a good punch.

The fabric is adorable! The quality is nice as well. BUT, as is the luck of ordering fabrics online, just note that the background/theater seats look red here in the listing photos (and yes, monitors differ, but my settings have always been nearly perfect).
However, the piece I received was more burgundy, slightly leaning towards a purple hue. Most likely different dye batches, so if you have certain projects in mind, be sure to order enough at the same time to assure your fabric will be consistent.

This is a pack of 45mm rotary cutter blade replacements. While I had never heard of this brand, they will fit my rotary cutter just fine.

I like the little plastic case that snaps shut. It makes it easy to store with my other sewing tools.

Please be very careful when getting out a new blade. They are oiled to keep away corrosion. That makes them stick to each other very tightly. You want to be sure to only get one blade, or it will not cut fabric correctly.

I was not sure what to expect with this strip of reflective tape. It is hard to tell how stiff something might be from just a photo. I wasn't sure my sewing machine could go through a heavier trim.

I am happy to report that this is a wonderful weight trim. It is soft and flexible, despite the reflective strip. As you can see from my photos (taken in broad daylight with the camera flash on), the reflective quality is nice as well. I love the bright green color too.

Overall, very happy with the quality and flexibility of this safety trim.

What a great shade of blue!

Handmade projects already cost a lot of money. Materials are expensive, and so much time is put into them.
If you are going to do all that work, do NOT go for a cheap, discount-store thread! They fray and snap, and do not have the longevity of good threads.

I use a Brother machine. I have not found any thread that works as well as Aurifil threads do with my machine. I use only 100% cotton for my projects for ease of use, maintenance and durability. I can honestly say, Aurifil has never let me down. They might cost a little bit more, but they are completely worth it. (And sewing is cheaper than therapy! Ha ha!)

As a crafter, I welcome all kinds of fabrics, and faux furs are always fun.

This comes in a long strip. It has a long pile, which makes it nice and soft. It is easy to sew on to something, and just as easy to glue, although I would highly suggest using a FABRIC GLUE, not glue gun type glue, for longer lasting results.

Just pay attention to the width you need for your project(s); this is cute, but only 2 inches wide, so it is a little limited in what you can do.

Very cute and easy to use.
For some reason, the pink flannel is not as soft as the other four colors I purchased. It is still nice flannel though.

I have this particular blender in many different colors and love them ALL.
They wash up well, have a nice thickness for nearly any project, and are perfect for when you need something that is not a plain solid, but you don't want a defined print either.

I really liked this fabric. We have zero fabric places where I live, so I have to shop online and hope for the best.

I was happy with the fabric quality and print for my project (a rag quilt). But I think it is overpriced for what you get. It would be great if the price was lowered some. But as is, still a nice accent pattern.

This fabric has a bit of a stiff hand, but washing it takes care of most of that.
This is my favorite red blender. It goes with holidays, sports logos, etc. I have used it for crafts and for quilting, and it works well fot both purposes.

I love this fabric. In fact, I love it so much, I came back and ordered more before the first piece arrived.

What I could not see on my tiny phone screen is that the background, which looks beige, is actually a light tan with white polka dots. Totally not a deal-killer at all, but noting it, as it can affect your thread color and coordinating fabrics.

So glad I bought this. I'll be building up more as I can afford it, as I like to make cat-themed projects.
This is a nice little foot to have on hand. It fit my Brother sewing machine just fine. I've found I don't use it much, but zero regrets on having it.

This is such a cute kit!

I love gnomes and love counted cross stitch. So, there is nothing to not love.

This kit makes a small bookmark. You could easily make this into a larger bookmark by adding your own fabric or ribbon to it.

The only thing I didn't like was a huge reminder that I am getting older. Ha!
I found the small weave of this aida cloth to be a challenge for my old eyes! It is smaller scale than I normally worked with, so it was a little harder than expected.

Overall, a cute set and I would recommend it for gnome fans.

I really like these markers quite a bit.

If you are a crafter, you'll understand what I mean when I say some markers are more frustrating than they are worth, because they either have incomplete coverage, drip or clog. These markers have done NONE of those things.

I get a nice, smooth line every time. I've only opened up two of them to try out, but I do not anticipate a quality change, especially from the same mfg batch.

I make holiday ornaments, and had bought a gold oil paint pen for metallic accents. I like these markers so much more, and they are easier to control.

This is a nice set, the markers are good, and I would definitely buy these again.

These are small squares of 4" by 4" of craft felt.

Now, you might think these are too small to be of much use. But if you are a crafter, you know you'll find uses for them, and having the color assortment on hand is much better than having lesser full sized sheets.

From kids' crafts to adult projects, this assortment offers a color for almost anything. I have not tried it yet, but I think these are thin enough to work with many types of shape punches.

From adding a star, a circle or a random strip of color, this is actually a decent set to have on hand. These are not thick wool felt sheets, but definitely a nice color assortment of basic crafting felt.

I've always enjoyed working with great detail, but the older I get, the less steady my hands get.

These brushes have handles made with a thumb depression built in. It might seem like nothing, but if you need all the stability help you can get, these are great brushes to have.

They are super comfortable to hold. There is a great assortment of fine brush tips for detail work. The numbers are on the handles. I mostly paint with acrylics, so these should work out well.

I've claimed this set as my own, and no one else is allowed to use "the white handled set".

I truly love these brushes. I've always enjoyed working with great detail, but the older I get, the less steady I get.

These brushes have handles made with a thumb depression built in. It might seem like nothing, but if you need all the stability help you can get, these are great brushes to have.

Are they the best bristles on the market? Nope. But they certainly do an adequate job. I am not a professional artist, but do love to work in acrylics. They are easy to clean as well. But I love them so much because of the extra thumb grip. I would definitely buy these again.

These are nice, big photo pages, and great for scrapbooking purposes.

Sadly, ours arrived bent up, crammed into a community mailbox. The creases have not come out. Some cardboard in with the pages would have helped a lot.

The photo slots are a good size for most developed photos, as well as those printed from home.

This is a GREAT set of black liner pens! I've bought these many times before, but I've never had such a size selection.

You get a set of 12 pens. This is archival quality ink. The smallest fine point is 0.05. This set included tips I have never had before, including two chisel tips and a brush tip.

We tried out the brush tip and loved it. Both the brush and chisel tips would work well for calligraphy lettering. The rest are great for precise text or outlining.

All in all, very happy with this set. As of this review, the price was a very reasonable $11. This set has a place in every artist's tool set.

This fabric is ADORABLE!
The colors are perfect for Halloween projects.

I will say that I was surprised by how "loose" the weave was on this fabric. I expected the normal smoothness of an all cotton fabric. I had to double-check my order to be sure I ordered cotton! I thought for a minute I might have ordered cotton duck fabric.

But, the cuteness and bright colors makes up for what was a disappointing fabric texture.

This is a really great assortment of charms. If you are in to steampunk, you'll like these.

The bag was generously full of bronze-toned charms in the shape of various sizes of gears and clocks. I loved the clocks the most, and am planning on attaching them to some upcoming projects.

Crafters, this is a nice assortment to have on hand. These are nearly all flatbacks, so they are not the kind you can hang off of bracelets or earrings. But they are fun for scrapbooking and wooden or metal projects.

If you love to do crafts and/or live steampunk, you will like this set.

It comes with a large assortment of clock-themed charms. While the majority are flatbacks, there are a few with openings to attach jump rings or put onto to earring findings.

There is a nice size assortment as well, and several loose clock "hands".
Overall, a fun crafting assortment.

[[VIDEOID:b3ccbabdaf23cc4e2592fe5981f31c4a]] This is NOT a name-brand, top-quality thread. But, it has a nice assortment of colors, and is great for embroidery practice. (I would not use these for a client embroidery project, but they are great for testing a new pattern out.)

They are useful, and I love having extra colors from which to choose.

A lot of manufacturers apparently want to call every pair of small scissors "embroidery" scissors. But that is not the case - they have to function well in small spaces.

This pair is heavy. Being heavy does not mean they are sturdy - it means they were made with heavy materials.

The finger/thumb openings are both large and the same size, which is great...no fumbling for the thumb side.
The carving designs are nice as well, but just a mold.

The blades seem to cut okay, but are certainly not delicate in any way. If I had very detailed threads to cut, this pair would not be my go-to.

These are heavy in my hands.
You have to "pull" them open and shut, as they don't move super smoothly. That means they will be heavy to pick up and snip threads, as well as very tiring on my hands.

Overall, just another heavy, clunky, small pair of scissors that aren't worth buying. I cannot recommend these.

I look forward to using this set of charcoal pencils. I've sketched for a very long time, but never had any brown pencils, which are included in this set.

You get 3 white, 1 brown, 1 "suntan" (just lighter brown), and 3 black pencils in various hardness levels.

Great set and looking forward to using them.

I find aluminum hooks to be more comfortable than wooden hooks.

This is a slightly larger size than I normally use, but I am hoping it will still be just fine.

The quality is nice, and I do not anticipate any issues.

This is a nice set of metallic markers. You get two dozen markers. They are very nice for craft purposes. I tried them on paper, cardboard and a coated paper, and they worked well. I think these would also work well on wood or stone surfaces.

These are also actually metallic looking. So many markers say that, but just have silver streak or similar in the ink. These colors are all metallic, with the exception of the white. It barely marked on anything and was not metallic.

They do give a LOT of very similar shades of blues and greens. But it is all good. I took them out of their flat package and put them into a repurposed plastic bin with a snap-on lid. It makes it easier to choose what I want, and will keep them extra air-tight.

[[VIDEOID:e6b96421516497d559b44fec21cabe6d]] These are special scissors that use a hand squeeze motion rather than traditional opening and closing of thumb and finger to cut.

The scissors are larger than I had expected. They move fairly easily. The loops are for making the scissors pop back open, NOT for putting on your wrist.

I was able to cut with both my right hand and left hand. I don't think these would do a good job on detail, but for those with hand motion limitations, this is a very good alternative for basic cutting tasks. Those with arthritis would likely benefit the most from these. Whether they wanted to cut out a coupon, or a photo for a scrapbook, these loop scissors would do the job.

Interesting product that definitely fills a human need.

This is a box of white acrylic markers. I've tried out a lot of different markers. I have to say, these do not make the "good" list.

For whatever reason, it was hard to get a simple solid white line from these. Every time I tried them, I got little gaps in the paint flow, or just rough edges, or even "lines" in the middle of the actual lines.

I'd say these will be okay to do little things, like eye highlights or polka dots, but if you want to do text, you'll be better off with a different marker.

These are adorable!
We've never used Washi tape before, so wanted to give it a try.

The assortment we received had several holiday-themed prints, as well as some basic designs. The tape is kind of thin (like thin masking tape), so some of the designs would lose visibility on darker papers. The adhesion is pretty good.

I'd guess there are better quality tapes out there, but these are cute.

Jaquard is hands-down my favorite brand of paint. They always work well, have plenty of pigment, and blend easily.

I use nearly the entire Jaquard line. I paint t-shirts, embellish quilts, and occasionally paint on wood. This brand is just top notch.

This magnifying glass does a very nice job of enlarging print on labels, the main reason I wanted to try it.

I love the colors. It seems to be very well made. Note that it is pretty heavy. I mention that, because if you have arthritis or other hand problems, this one won't take long to be awkward.

These are fairly strong little strips!
Everything is precut, so if you need a very long piece of hook-n-loop tape, do not get this item

These are great for craft purposes, or small sewing projects. I could also see using these to hold up a cover cloth on a display table.

You get a generous amount, ready to use on whatever project you have in mind that needs a medium-strength hold.

Oh my goodness! When I ordered this one, I thought it was a paint-by-number, as in, with acrylic paints. Nope! This is one of those "diamond" paintings, where you glue tiny little plastic chips into squares, like a pixel breakdown.

These chips are TINY.
We shall see how it works out. I got it for my daughter, who adores giraffes. But I don't think she'll have the patience for the detail - this one might fall back on me. Lol

Well, I thought these were basic svrapbook page protectors, especially since they were 12"x12".

However, these are photo page protectors, not standard scrapbook protectors. They hold three 4x6 photos vertically, and have a rectangular pouch at the top, which could hold movie stubs, additional photos, or something else flat and long.

They seem to be a decent quality, just not what I had expected.

This is a really nice assortment.

I love to do all kinds of crafts. So I am always looking for new or unusual things I cannot purchase locally.

These have a nice "heft" to them. Being faux leather, you won't need special tools to cut or shape these.
There is a great color assortment from the brown color family.

Overall, very happy with these.

There is no such thing as too many sewing feet, especially if they have a designated function.

I have a low shank machine and these will work great. Specialty feet can come in very handy, especially with various weight or unique fabrics.

At first glance, these look like giant needle-threaders. But they are not. Their alleged function is to pull faux hair through rubber bands. I guess I could see that.

The handles have an opening in the middle to hold thread or cord of some kind. The other end is wire, and has crossed, open ends.

Overall, not sure how functional these will actually be.

Ok, I LOVE THESE!!
I do art quilts, so they do not get washed. These are the perfect texture for so many things I make. I use fabric for my artwork, so these are just right. They are a little thicker than what I normally buy, so not sure how well they will go through my sewing machine, but I'll find out on my next project.

The color selection was nice, and I'm very glad I got these.

The skeins were much smaller than I thought they would be, but will be just fine for my hat projects.

They come with a few crochet hooks, which is nice, but the hooks are tiny. In my opinion, the hooks are too small to be used with this yarn. It seems very mis-matched. Still, a nice gesture.

I sew and craft, so extra pins are always welcome.
These come in a small plastic box. Now I could not get mine to fully open...there's something wonky with the box on mine. So, I carefully slid a pin out of the side that could open.

The gold heads are beautiful. The pin length is 1.5", so they are not super long. These would be useless for quilting, as an example. But the gold is a very nice color, the heads coated well, so they will be great to use as a decorative feature on my handmade ornaments.

If you've ever bought pins and found the head colors to look like they were briefly dipped in color and very uneven or splotchy, then you will appreciate the color consistency of the heads of these pins.

I would recommend them, but not for quilting...the length makes them pretty useless for that. But for other crafting, or perhaps pinning a pattern to fabric, these will work well.

These will be great sewing feet to add to my sewing options.

I have a low shank Brother machine and these will work great.
Specialty feet can come in very handy on some projects.

This is a really nice assortment of embroidery threads. You get a generous amount of various shades of your chosen color.

Now the downside is that I am a bit spoiled; I love doing counted cross stitch. But I have a definite preference for one brand that is colorfast and never fails me. These are NOT that brand, and I have no idea if they are colorfast or not. They look great, but there could be problems I haven't come across yet.

So, I plan to use these to accent art quilts, which do not need to be washed. The color assortment will make it easy to coordinate with natural backgrounds.

You might wonder why you would want such small circles of a hook-n-loop item.

They actually come in very handy. Of course, they are great for crafting, but there are many other uses. I have used them on the backs of lightweight photos to hold them on a certain place. I also used them to hold a lightweight shower curtain against a wall.

They work nicely, and I would buy them again if I run out.

These brushes have worked very well with acrylic paints. I don't currently have any other paint types to test them out on, but I would say these are intended for acrylics anyway.

The handles are long enough to be comfortable. The bristles did not shed or leave hair strokes in the paint a lot. All the brushes were easy to clean in just soap and water...just rub them in your palm until the paint is gone, rinse well and put in a cup bristles-up to dry. These are great basic brushes for craft uses.

These embroidery kits are larger than I expected them to be. That can be a good thing.

You get everything you need to complete the designs, including the wooden hoop. The thread comes wound on a card, so try your best not to waste any of it.

I do counted cross stitch, but have not done regular embroidery (which is what this kit is) for a very long time. I am looking forward to working on these, and giving myself a new project for cold January.

This is a really nice assortment of sizes for canvases. We lost most of our canvases during our last move, so this was a great way to get a few more.

I have not painted on them yet. Everything looks stretched and prepped well, and I look forward to trying them out. Should anything be wrong with them, I will come back and update my review.

I didn't expect high quality on this item, and expectations were correct.

These are fat quarters in various shades of blue. I chose to try them, because I do art quilts and it is always nice to have plenty of shade/tone selections from which to choose.

Would I use these in an actual quilt? Probably not. This is not the best weave out there, but was better than I thought it would be. Truly, our grandparents used all kinds of fabric bits to make warm quilts, so it would likely work just fine. I just wouldn't mix it with other fabric types.

This kit was not quite what I had expected.
It comes with a plastic divided tray with assorted hot fix faux rhinestones.
The hot fix tool itself comes in a black zippered bag. There are some assorted tips for different sizes of rhinestones. There are also a couple things not explained...a metal arch of some kind that is supposed to be a "stand", and a stiff-wire narrow brush.

The hot fix tool itself is pretty heavy and awkward. I haven't tried using it yet, as I do not currently have anything to put rhinestones on, but it feels awkward in my hand.

The listing also says "bedazzler". Not sure how they got away with using a trademarked term - this is nothing like the Bedazzler.

Overall, I think there are much better hot fix tools out there. Keep searching.

This was kind of disappointing.
For some reason (maybe decades of buying ribbon), I thought these would be on paper spools, like how most wired ribbons are sold.

You will get a plastic bag. Inside the plastic bag is the narrower strip of ribbon and another bag, with the wired ribbon in it.

The narrower ribbon is NOT wired, although the description suggests all the ribbon is wired. One side is stithed with red thread and the other side with black thread.

The wider ribbon in the bag (inside the other bag) is indeed wired. You don't get a whole lot, but you could do a bow or two, or maybe a couple packages.

Overall, as a ribbon lover (I have three bins of ribbon that I use for my ornanent crafting), I was not impressed and was in fact, disappointed in the cheapie packaging and quality of the ribbon. I would likely not purchase this one again.

I really, really liked these paintbrushes. I do a lot of little detail work, and these brushes helped with that.

The handles have a triangular grip that makes them easier to hold and control. Sometimes, especially if you have arthritis or other hand issues, small brushes can get hard to hold on to. Not the case with these.

I used them with acrylic paints and had no trouble with bristles shedding and no problems cleaning them. They worked very well for me.

As a crafter, I can appreciate all kinds of small items that can be used in various ways.

These little buttons are really nice looking. I got the silver toned shank backs.

Honestly, I would NOT use these as functional buttons on articles of clothing. They are a little too rough on the edges, and the shank backs are not consistent. Rather than having a one-piece shank back, the buttons have a flat back, and separate tiny shank backs are glued on. This is not a huge problem, except that they are not glued consistently and evenly. Some are right in the middle, but just as many are off towards the edges. This would make it harder to get the buttons lined up exactly on a blouse or cardigan. For crafting on other projects, it would not be a big issue.

Overall, very nice looking, but cheaply made. But like all craft items, they have their workable place too.

I do a lot of sewing and embroidery. So, you can't really have too many pairs of small scissors to help clip loose threads.

This purchase gets you two pairs of small scissors; a small ornate regular pair amd a pair of more traditional stork design scissors.

The stork scissors were actually my least favorite. They are metal, with a rainbow patina finish. While lighter in weight than the other pair, they move stiffly and are not particularly sharp. When trimming tiny threads, you need scissors that open and close comfortably and are sharp to get the job done. These aren't too great.

The regular pair is fairly heavy, with an ornate design in a copper color on the handles. These do not like to open and close without some hand force. The scissors were nice and sharp, much more so than the stork pair. But the heaviness and having to force them open and shut makes them a lesser choice as well.

Truly, I cannot recommend this scissor set. Too heavy, too hard to open and shut, and generally bothersome. If you have arthritis or other hand issues, these are not tye scissors for you. They'll go in the sewing box as a desperation pair, but I really cannot see using these stiff, heavy scissors on a regular basis.

Wool pressing pads seem to quite popular these days. While I had never used one before, I didn't want to miss out on a good thing for sewing either.

This is a nice size to sit on my ironing table. Since the majority of my fabric pressing is smaller pieces for quilting or crafting, I don't often get out my big ironing board any more. I use a wooden tv tray covered in a heat-resistant pad made for ironing.

The silicone iron rest that comes with this is also nice to have. It is a little too small for my iron, but useful nontheless.

The wool helps to make pressing go a little faster by helping to press both sides at the same time. It isn't a "must have" for me, but it is nice to have the option.

I had never really thought about using silicone mats as a craft mat. I can be messy when painting, so I usually put some parchment sheets under where I am working. But those can be very slick and cause your work to shift.

This is a great mat for crafting. I will definitely be using this when I paint. I can also see this mat being very helpful when working on earrings. It would be a nice, stable surface and would protect the wires and beads as well. Very nice, if not thin, mat. As you can see in my photos, this mat is not super thick, but it really does not have to be.

My inner Conehead is again completely satisfied. This is a box of HOT MELT mini glue sticks. Be sure you want the hot melt sticks and for your mini glue guns.

These are great! They come packaged in bags of 15 sticks each, so you don't have an open box with well over 100 loose glue sticks. Just open a bag as you need it. I had not used this brand before, but they work just fine.

This is not the worst lighted headband I have tried.

This fits like a pair of glasses, so it might not be comfy if you wear glasses already. There is an included black elastic cord to help keep these on your head while you use the glasses.

The glasses are powered by a permanent battery you need to charge via USB. They include a jokingly short cord to charge them, but it is standard USB/mini-B size, so your phone charger might work as well. (No charger outlet/plug was included. You'll need to use your phone charger or computer.)

The LED light has two options, soft and bright. Both put out a decent amount of light. You can snap on any of five plastic shields that have various magnification strengths. (Wish they were stronger!) They come in a nice storage case to keep them from getting scratched up.

Overall, I can see these being very useful for things like counted cross stitch. They MIGHT be useful for some aspects of jewelry making, but I'd need a higher magnification for any regular use.

Overall, these beat my former lighted headband, whose batteries made them super heavy to wear. The glasses structure is comfy and familiar.

UPDATE:
I used these to help light up some very small and dark areas in our vacuum cleaner while repairing it. Seeing the places where a new belt needed to go was very difficult. I decided to try these glasses.

The magnifier clips were just annoying. I took it back off and just used my regular 4.0 reader glasses and this headband. On bright, it put light exactly where I needed it, and really helped make the job go smoother.

This is a pretty decent set of gold and silver toned paint pens.

Each pen comes sealed in plastic to help eliminate evaporation. Like so many paint pens, you need to gently shake a few times to mix up the paint. The first time you use one, you will need to push down on the plastic nib for a few seconds until the paint comes down.

The paint seemed to flow fairly smoothly. I wrote on the cardboard to test it out, but plan to use these to make highlights on fabric and wooden ornaments. They are a bit stinky, and took a little longer to dry than expected.

This is a really cute paint-by-number set. My daughter is obsessed with Groot, so this was a natural choice!

The canvas has a plastic/vinyl feel to it. Of course, it came folded up. They should roll it instead. There are seemingly perma-wrinkles from the folds that have not come out. I would normally try to iron it, bur I think my iron would just melt this one and ruin my iron.

All the paints came sealed and intact, no leaks. The brushes are the worst part...they are those cheapest-of-the-cheap black plastic bristles that shed on the canvas. It was getting very annoying, so we brought out our regular paint brushes to use instead.

There are a LOT of very tiny places to paint on this one. I had her get out toothpicks for some of them.

Is it perfect? Nope! But it provided several days' worth of entertainment, and she has a picture she eventually wants to frame and hang in her room.
Cute!

These are pre-primed canvases about the size of a piece of regular notebook paper.
The texture feels right, and everything looks good. Nothing came warped either.

I will update my review when I have had the opportunity to use them with some various types of paints and see how they actually handle paint, and how the boards stay after everything dries.

READ CAREFULLY, much more carefully than I did.

I thought I was buying a regular sized roll of duct tape with less yardage. I was ok with that, because I just needed some white tape.

Holey moley. This is TINY. What a joke.

UPDATE: I've used it on my tv antenna trying to keep the cords directly under the windowsill. It lasts 3-4 days, then pops off. It is not abnormally humid here. I restick it, pushing harder, same thing. Then I tried two strips at a time on the cord. Same results...a couple days and then it pops off.

This must be more for scrapbooking and crafting than for use where you need real duct tape.

Now this is one of those purchases where you just have to smile when you open it!

Googley eyes are nothing new, and most of us crafters have used them a few times here and there. But there is something innately satisfying about a mass quantity of googley eyes, various sizes, various colors and different styles that just makes you happy!
Note these are NOT self-adhesive, so you will need to glue them on. Some glues eat away at plastic, so be sure to use an appropriate glue type.

These come in a plastic compartmentalized case with various eyes in each one. Ours arrived with one part of the case popped open and a few eyes trying to sneak a peek into life on the other side of their partitions, but we calmly corraled them back into their families.

I can't see how you could go wrong with this purchase. We are looking forward to some amusing crafting.

I sew a little, but most quilt. However, I wanted to get some wider elastic for a nursing home project.

I've used other brands of elastic before, so can make a few comments on this one.

This is NOT a thick or stiffer elastic. If you tried to use it in adult waistbands, it would likely just flip or roll. That being said, it is a softer elastic with just one weave - no open areas specifically for stitching.

I think this will work just fine for crafting projects and the 10 yard roll will be helpful. But do not believe it is sturdy enough to use for pants or skirts for adults.

I am not a great crocheter. I cannot follow a pattern at all, it seems. But I do crochet scarves and ear warmers. I've always used aluminum crochet hooks, and sometimes they rub blisters on my fingers.

That being said, I really like this set. I mentioned my mediocre skill level, because there might be something about this that someone who is expert level might not like.

The set comes with 9 different sizes of crochet hooks. (I had previously only used two!) Each has a wonderful grip on them that fits my hand perfectly.

The kit also comes with a tube with several sizes of darning/yarn needles to finish off your project. There is also a small bag of plastic stitch markers. (I'll never use them, but it was a nice optional item.) They are all packaged in a nice fabric tube with a sturdy zipper. There is room for me to add my own crochet hooks as well, keeping everything organized.

The only negative thing I have to say about this kit - and this is simply personal preferences - is that the case fabric is rather ugly. That's it! If you are thinking of trying a new hobby and want to give crochet a shot, this kit is a great way to start.

[[VIDEOID:6a6016971311d230549b370d0f5bc0f1]] I had seen a different project on another web site and the person had used this fabric. The item was so pretty, I had to try it out.

I ended up using it on a different project than I had anticipated, but wanted to share my experience with this brand/style of minky.

What. A. Mess.
This stuff is soft to the touch, but what a horrid mess it ! Much worse than other minkies I have used in the past. It is a shedding nightmare. I am still in the process of creating the project, so I truly hope once sewn, it will calm down. I was cutting for several hours last night, and the floor was so covered (as was I) that I had to vacuum twice. If you have pets, lock them out of the room while you are cutting. This looks like you gave your pet an extensive brushing, but it is polyester and you do NOT want your pet to ingest it!!

Overall, the fabric is soft, the color is pretty and I'm crossing my fingers for project outcome. But in my opinion, this one is so thin, it is WAY overpriced for what you get. Regular minky is more substantial and just as soft.

[[VIDEOID:dfede50508a31addbc41c200ecb2bde6]] I will give a more extensive review when I am not on my phone. 🙂

For the price, this machine cannot be beat! It offers features not available in most lower-priced machines. It offers nearly 60 decorative stitches. Best feature? Well, more than one, but the start/stop AND speed control right on the front of the machine is awesome! Being able to control the stitch speed without stepping on a foot pedal is FANTASTIC.

This machine also offers a needle threading function, which is great for folks like me who don't see as well as they used to.

If I had to find a flaw, it would be in bobbin winding. Perhaps it is just my machine, but the bobbins often don't wind very well. I've tried Brother brand and others, but they are all the same. They seem to not go all the way to the top without a little help from my finger. I've even had one overfill and break. Kinda weird. But I'll live with it for the rest of the features.

Mine didn't come with the table extension, so I guess different years offer a few different options.

I can not recommend this one enough. If you are getting a first machine for someone, get this one. With all these features, it will help assure there is joy in the sewing process instead of frustration.

Many people have had trouble with the tension. It is usually the bobbin causing problems, and improper threading usually the cause of the problem. It can cause bird-nesting of thread on the back of your fabric. I made a short phone-cam video about threading the bobbin which I hope might help someone. That little silver lever cannot be overlooked!

Overall, I love the CS6000i.

No one in our area carries Aurifil thread, so I have to order it online.
The hardest part is guessing on which color you need, and hoping the seller photos are pretty close to what your monitor displays.
(Yes, they sell a color chart, but I sure don't have the money to get one!)

Every once in a while, I get a spool that is "upside down", meaning the thread comes off the opposite side from what the normal is - however, so far, it hasn't caused much trouble - the biggest problem with the backwards spools (not sure if they are seconds, or what the deal is) is that the bobbin sometimes doesn't wind quite right.

This is relatively expensive thread, but it sews like a dream in my Brother sewing machine.
I don't get thread breakage, and not nearly as much lint as other brands.
I highly recommend trying Aurifil threads for your next project

I was having trouble with my machine thread breaking on my Brother machine.
After trying every adjustment possible, new needle, etc etc, someone online suggested
changing thread brands, and Aurifil was their suggestion.

So, I tried one spool. This stuff is really overpriced, in my opinion, because even though the quality is great, it is still just thread. However, I am hooked. I had to have 100% cotton for my projects, and this was my third brand of thread. It sews like a dream!
I had no troubles with tension, no troubles with needles, no thread breakage, etc. It does a great job.

So, I highly recommend Aurifil cotton thread (50 wt). Just know that you need to be wealthy to have a large collection!
I can no longer work outside the home, so I pick up spools as I can afford to (and I have to buy them online, as no stores in our area carry this brand), and hope to eventually have nearly all my cotton thread be Aurifil.

Well, it probably isn't going to last as long as I had hoped.
When I bought it, I thought I could stick it down then sew through it.
Nope!

Too much glue/sticky - it only gummed up and broke my needle.
So know that this is a press-on only item.

So far, it has stayed put on the bag I made that is used daily, but it does pick up all kinds
of fuzz and paper. I might need to comb it out! ;-)

Well, I can't complain for the price!
Know that this is not a thick, luxurious fleece - it is a very basic cheapie one.
As long as you are not expecting top quality, this will be fine.

Very pleased with this fabric for its crafting purpose.
Note that it is FAUX suede, made of polyester, not leather.
This will be used on an item that will not be ironed bu will be washed a lot, so it is a good substitute for the more expensive real suede.
The color is good and it feels good to the touch.

I am very happy with the quality of this fabric.
I bought it to practice certain projects on, and didn't really expect it to be so nice!

I love it - the backing makes it easy to cut and it doesn't ravel.
I would definitely buy this again, or try different colors.

I had heard of this many times before, but at the cost for what it was, I always passed.
It popped up as an "add-on" one day, so I tossed it into the cart.

Overall, it is nice to have, but I have found a flat, white one by a different company to be much more effective and turning curves.
While I don't regret the purchase, it would not be the first one I'd recommend to a friend either.

This is a great needle.
I love the Gold Eye needles for hand embroidery and cross stitch.
The quality is very good, and I've never had a problem with any of the Gold Eye needles.

I would recommend these to any crafter who uses threads to embellish or create.

I am an Aurifil convert!

After trying several brands of 100% cotton thread needed for some projects, I was getting frustrated with fraying threads,
breakage and lint galore in the machine. I spent way too much time ripping out seams where the thread would break inbetween
and leave me with an uneven line. (And no, it isn't a tension issue - tried every possible combo - it was the THREAD.)

I invested in my first spool of Aurifil (and for my budget, it is indeed an investment), not truly expecting much of anything different, but it came highly recommended by so many ladies in an online quilting group. Now I can see why!

Aurifil works very well with my Brother sewing machine. I've not had any tangles, breakage or abnormal lint buildup when using Aurifil.
My only actual complaint about it (besides it being very expensive) is the problem with getting colors correct. For those of us who cannot get this thread locally and must order online, it can be VERY difficult to get the color you want. Aurifil offers a thread color chart, but they are apparently lined with gold for the price they ask for it. You would think they would make the charts very affordable so customers can buy MORE thread from them.
They are forgetting the challenges of shopping online, with monitor/display differences. I tried using the color chart parts that are visible and still ended up with two nearly identical threads. I can't afford to buy every color they have, so it was a disappointment.

So, the quality is wonderful and I recommend it. Buy in person if you can.
I only wish Aurifil could see things from my side of the screen.

I bought these because I wanted to do some thread embroidery on my sewing machine and needed a hoop to hold the stabilizer.
These are really nice hoops IF you are going to use them for HAND embroidery.
They are TOO THICK to slide under my sewing machine needle!
So, they are hanging over with my other craft supplies. Maybe I'll find another use for them some day.

This isn't the best fabric out there, because the thread count isn't as high as some - it has a rougher hand (texture/feel in your hands) than
many other fabrics, but it is still very cute. Glad I bought it.

I am very happy with this fabric.
It isn't the highest thread count I've ever worked with, but the pattern, which looks a little like fur, is exactly what I needed for a project.
In fact, I like it well enough to hope to get it in every color offered.

Two thumbs up on this for quilting or crafting projects.

[[VIDEOID:448dc76e4c48d16b8b6b0c8c3da121e6]] I love this ribbon!
While I don't need wired ribbon, I just strip it of the wire and I have a beautiful
sheer ribbon with which to work.

These are a very nice quality - they don't shred like so many others.
Wish the glitter stayed on better (it sticks very well to ME!), but that's the nature of glitter.

I would definitely recommend Morex ribbons - I have never had a problem with them.

UPDATE:
I love the brand, but I got the blue ribbon out to work with today. Beautiful color, pretty pattern, but wowza, does this shed glitter! Yikes!! The floor is a mess, I'm covered, my clothes are covered, and itchy. Little glitter bits everywhere. Ugh.
The short video is my foot! Ha! It now sparkles when I move it in any direction.

It would be hard to go wrong with Aurifil thread.
I am making items that can be used in the microwave, and needed a good quality thread that was 100% cotton.
(I had tried other brands, but they were all linty or broke easily.)

It is a bit difficult to make sure you get the right color online just because of monitor variances.
(It is a shame Aurifil has such a high price on their thread color Charts! It would be to their benefit to make it more financially
affordable so their customers can order more thread!)

Very happy with this brand High quality, bottom of the spool pops off to
hold the thread ending, and it works great on my Brother machine.

I work with ribbon a lot for various crafts (not wreath bows). It is always a pleasure to find a ribbon that is a good quality and reasonable price.

You get a great quantity on this roll. It is packed to the center, not just a few feet on a large-sized cardboard tube.

The color is vibrant and perfect for holiday crafting. I would definitely buy this one again.

Hard pass on these bobbins!!
You would think generic bobbins would pretty much be the same, but they are not!

I have a Brother sewing machine. I need SA156 bobbins. I've bought generic bobbins before, because I see no reason for the high prices on the Brother brand bobbins. I've had good success with all others, EXCEPT this one!!

These are horribly made. They are shallower than all my other bobbins, so apparently, the mtg wanted to save money by shaving a hair or two width off of the edges.

These didn't wind worth a hoot, and I had endless tension problems with them. I was using Aurifil thread, and never had any problems with my other bobbins.

I've included photos... I got one wound by babysitting it and helping it move properly with my finger. I have a couple pictures of the bobbins while just trying to machine wind them.

These are not just inexpensive, they are cheaply made. Run, not walk from these. There are plenty of other generics, no need to mess with this junk.

This is a great package of chalk!
I have tried to order chalk two other times, and without fail, it has arrived in pieces.

This is very well-packaged and everything arrived unbroken. It is SO nice to have the color assortment! I'm ready for any fabric color. I've already used two of the colors and it works well. The blue is a bit harder to brush away, but it will brush out.

Excellent way to buy tailor's chalk, and I'm very happy with my purchase and this brand.

I always thought these things were a little pointless. When turning most projects, I just used chopsticks to get the corners out. It worked well, until I started making bowl cozies.

I suddenly found myself with tiny corners that didn't want to turn right. Pens poked through the fabric. Chopsticks were too big. Nothing seemed quite right. So, I ordered this. (We have zero craft or fabric stores where I live, so shop online for most things.)

Long story shorter, this little turner worked great. I could do the cozy corners without poking through the fabric. The cozies HAD corners without being round!

Such a relatively small item, but it has become a go-to tool. I love it!

This was smaller than expected. I also bought it as a gift, but checked it out since I also paint.

The assorted ridged areas in the bottom are nice to clean an assortment of brush tips. My favorite feature is the ridge around the edge of the lid; it has several sizes of holes so you can put your paintbrush handles in there to allow them to dry upright.

I think I'll be buying one for myself soon.

I needed some 100% cotton thread for some projects. We have no area fabric stores and the Walmart only offers Coats and Clark, which I do not care for. So, I came to Amazon for a choice.

I ordered Connecting Threads in the black/white/grays set. I've only used the black and the white so far. The black was fine, but the WHITE spool, the one I needed the most, has been a mess!

So far, the white thread has broken multiple times. One time, it looked like a place where two lots of thread were connected, but it broke where connected. It snapped off at the needle several times as well. So, I put a brand new needle on and rethreaded the machine.

Then it started making these little "coils" above the needle eye and of course, breaking. It also created some stitching problems that were unique to this white spool only.

I apparently got a bad/lemon spool with my white one. Darned shame, because the black stitched just fine.
Taking off one star for the bad spool and one star for the frustration and time lost fiddling with this breaking, faulty thread.

This is one of my favorite fabrics.
I love the design and the colors.
The quality is good and has worked well in every project I have used it in.

My next project for this one is a Doctor Who quilt.
If I could afford to, I'd buy this one by the bolt!

Love it!

I am very pleased with this thread!
It even feels nice to the touch.

I've been sewing craft projects that needed to stand up to heavy use. I used this thread with a triple stitch and it was perfect.

I have since purchased more colors of this brand. Excellent thread that my Brother machine likes too. It doesn't leave a load of lint like some threads do.

This is really wonderful thread. Very little lint, very strong, and it sews like butter.
I did ding one star, however, because the photo for this color set (for neutrals) has been tweaked by someone's photo editing program.
My monitor is spot on with things that are in my hand on everything else.
THIS listing, however, differed from what I actually received.

The numbers on the spools match up, but the photo is MUCH brighter and vibrant than the colors received.
So, the orange spool I thought I was getting is really more of a deep burnt orange or copper, not near the color shown here.
Even the white is not really white, it looks more ecru, while the off white thread in the photo actually looks beige.

Other than all the thread being much duller than what is shown, I still like the actual thread itself and will definitely keep it.

Very nice flannel.
Not the thickest one out there, but certainly decent quality. The turtles are adorable and the scale of the design lends itself to all kinds of projects.

I would (and probably will) buy it again.

I wasn't sure what to expect... buying fabric you are not familiar with online is always a gamble.

The color is great, it washed up nicely and has become one of my favorite flannels. I just wish it came in more colors.

The Deco Foil lines are great for shiny accents on almost any sort of craft you wish to do.
For some reason, the black color seems to never be in stock at our stores, so I tried shopping online for it.
For a while, it was pretty high in cost, but finally came down to the neighborhood of the other colors, and
i snatched it up!

If you love to scrapbook or paint, these foils add lovely accents. Note that you will need some glue to use them...
these are NOT iron-on foils. I prefer the foil glue, but nearly any glue could do the job.

First off, these do actually magnify what you are looking at - however...
They take batteries.
Once you put the batteries in, they become too heavy to stay put!

The light works well, the lenses work well, and the concept is great.
BUT - they just slide down my face when i actually tip my head to look at what I am working on.
(I bought these for extra illumination while doing craftwork.)

So, keep looking. These are not the lenses you are looking for! ;-)
Too awkward and heavy.

This stuff is GREAT!
My husband had asked me to mend a hole in his jeans pocket where he'd worn it through.
My sewing machine can't handle denim, and I'm not sure I had the skills to make a patch that he'd be willing to wear out of the house!

So, I ordered some of this to try it out.
It cannot be easier - just watch the video the product mfg provides. You just shake it well, spread it on both pieces of fabric if it is thick (like jeans), or just one side for thinner fabric. Put them together, and magic happens - it makes a bond that is just as strong as anything you could possibly do.

I ended up cutting a patch out of other old jeans that was slightly smaller than his pocket, and gluing it INSIDE the pocket.
Yes, you can see the frayed hole, but the pocket is back to better-than-original condition for holding his stuff (ranges from billfold to tools to phone).
He liked it so much he asked that I line the pockets of his other work jeans too, before they wore through, because the pocket was so much stronger.

I love this stuff, and will always have it around. Not sure how long a bottle will last after it is opened, but well worth the cost.
Note for allergy sufferers: I did have to be careful. After the first pocket, I was reading the ingredients better to see if I could glue a cardboard leg back on a cat toy. It has latex in it! I use gloves now when working with it, as I am allergic to latex. (Yes, I was itchy as all get out last time I used it, but never associated it with the glue.) And hint - on the cat bed, I decided to try using a piece of FABRIC between the leg and the bed (one of those cardboard scratcher round beds) - worked like a charm! The leg is back on, strong as ever, and the fabric bonded to the cardboard with this product. It just wouldn't bond cardboard-to-cardboard. I've also used it on ribbons to fabric, and that works great as well.

Well, I wanted to use these on fabric ornaments instead of the regular round head pins I'd been using. I thought it would add some pizazz to the appearance.

First off, know that there are a lot of knock-offs to the true Lomey pins. They are made with shoddy materials and you don't get the same sparkle.
That being said, this listing claims they are real Lomey pins.

We received them quickly, but as I was using the package last night working on my projects, I noticed a couple of things.
First off, many of them have very rough edges that I had to try to trim off. But the biggest thing I didn't like was that these had a LOT of blunt tipped pins in the box! By blunt tip, I mean they are rounded off rather than have a point. It makes a HUGE difference when trying to push them through layers of fabric! I broke off three heads trying to get them pushed in before I realized I had not suddenly grown weak, but rather that the pins themselves were flawed.

I do not know if the quality of the pins has changed, or if this seller offers seconds from the manufacturer. There is definitely a LARGE amount of blunts in the box, which means they are a general pain to use.

Very disappointed in this box. (I've purchased them before from a different seller on a different venue and did not experience this problem.)
Just be warned. If you are on a tight time frame and want them from here anyway, order extras because you'll be throwing a lot of pins away. :-(

This was a great value for the foil paper.
I ordered the wrong foil for what I really needed, but will be giving these to my daughter to use for scrapbooking.
Very colorful, large enough for almost any project you could need, and super for scrapbooking.

Another great fabric to have in your stash!
I had actually bought this one by accident, from shopping on a tiny screen instead of waiting until I was on my computer. I thought it was something else, but I still really like it. Great addition, and will look great on any project it ends up in.

Okay, this product is a joke.
Well, the product is fine in and of itself, but the PRICE for what you receive is an absolute joke.

The sellers should be ashamed of themselves.
I admit that I did not see the exact dimensions. I had read about these sheets over a year ago, and have wanted one for a very long time, as I like to free-motion quilt. I had previously noted that they came in regular size and queen size. Silly me - I thought that meant a reasonably sized sheet (regular) versus a really large sheet (queen) that covered a larger area, perhaps your machine base and part of your table.

Wow - shocker.
When I finally had the money to be able to order one, I nabbed it from the link on my wish list.
When I got the package, and I thought there was just no way this could be correct.
It was thin as a sheet of paper - I had thought it would be folded, and you'd unfold it and lay it across your sewing machine.

People - save your money!!
This thing is a THIN little sheet, literally the size of a piece of typing paper.
That's it.
No unfolding. No real help.
There's a hole punched in it to allegedly go over where your particular machine's needle hits.
The tiny thin sheet has a tacky side that will supposedly hang on to your machine surface, and the top is
silicone to allow the fabric to glide more easily.

This might indeed be the case. The item probably is okay, even though many people have said you still need to tape it down to keep it from hanging, or it will tear. Here's the thing - I get it that it is a helpful item. But the PRICE versus what you RECEIVE is a real joke!
Sadly, I won't be returning it because (per Amazon) I have to use UPS to return it, and it would cost me an additional $6.95 to return this tiny little piece of slick paper. I can't just use regular postal mail.

SAVE YOUR MONEY.
There are other brands out there now that will give you a larger sheet for less money.
I truly wish I had done my usual research, instead of buying off my own wish list and leaning on rumors.
I'm somewhat new to quilting, and have often just bookmarked an item or brand that was recommended by another
more experienced sewer. Last time I will do that!
Shame on ME. I would think the manufacturers and sellers of this item would hang their heads in shame.

Seriously, buy a different brand. I have not felt this ripped off in a very, very long time.

I have never used templates that much, but have been very happy with this one.
I finally got to use it last week, and it made my project SO much easier!

Templates that I have used in the past, and even ones ordered from Amazon, have been made of those thin sheets
of plastic. This one is VERY sturdy. While it is plastic, it is clear and thick (the orange color shown in just the packaging,
NOT the template itself). The template even has molded holes so it can fit into a 3-ring binder if desired.

Very happy with the quality, and imagine it will last a very long time (as long as I don't step on it!).
Worth the price.

I'm fairly new to quilting, and only had one ruler. It is two feet long, so it made cutting the small pieces
of fabric a little annoying! I saw several sets of rulers, but I am not that prolific yet - I just needed a short ruler
that would allow me to see where I was cutting and let me cut a straight edge.

This has worked really well, and I love the bright yellow/green color on the back.
It is my first Omnigrip ruler, but I am sure it will not be my last!

I love this ribbon. It is perfect for several types of craft projects. The gold is definitely metallic (vs pale or completely yellowed gold),
and goes well with several other fabrics. Nice feel, hefty enough to hold the shape.

These were a big hit!

I also tried them out, having been invited to color in the brand new Frozen coloring book!
I loved them too. Where were these when I was a kid? ;-)

Good fun, but a little overpriced.

Okay for some projects, but if you look closely, the word
"Firefighter" is mis-spelled in the vertical column.
Botched fabric on this one. Not seller's fault, but folks need to know this is flawed.

Well, this was not my favorite length of pin. I misunderstood the designations on pin numbers.
(I have since looked it up and have a much better understanding.)

These pins are longer than the other pins. It is a fraction of an inch longer, and you wouldn't think that would matter.
That entirely depends upon your project. For my project, it made the pin too long to work.
But, I've since learned, and will not ding stars on an item because I needed to understand the numbers better.
