I use an embossing tool [[ASIN:B005OTUKGG 3 Pc Embossing Stylus Tool Set- Scrapbooking, Stampin]] to shape the flower petals a bit, and then layer the snowflake on top of the flower, and attach with a small brad. I use this flower punch: [[ASIN:B000MATGVW Ek Success Paper Shapers Small Punch, Flower Power]]

The holly also works nicely punched from pearlescent paper [[ASIN:B0062TNFK0 Wausau Paper Astrobrights Glisten Pearlescent Cardstock 6 Color Assortment - 8.5 x 11 in]] and layered onto the border of a sentiment or focal.

The punches are well made, and punch easily. Nice set for the price.

I've been using this folder a lot for my holiday cards. I usually do my backgrounds with a patterned paper plus a wide strip of embossed solid card stock. Some embossing patterns, particularly snowflakes, are spaced too widely to be that useful for a 2"-3" wide piece of card. THIS folder, however, contains enough smaller snowflake images that it works beautifully for a narrower band.

I usually emboss this onto plain white stock then hit it with a little pearlescent transparent ink; or emboss directly onto pearlescent stock; or emboss onto dark blue stock and brush lightly with silver metallic ink. You get the ideal. Works great with white-core blue stock, too -- emboss, then sand lightly to reveal the white snowflakes on the darker blue background.

I use a Sizzix Big Shot die-cutter/embosser, and these Cuttlebug folders work just fine.

I purchased the Retro Flower Medium Punch. The description here gave no indication of the size of the punched flower, so I wasn't sure what to expect. Happy to report that it's just about perfect.

The flower is about 1" across. I also purchased the smaller version of this punch, so I can pair them side by side or layer them.

No problem with punching, either. It punches smoothly and easily through premium card stock. I then use an embossing stylus to add a bit of shape to the petals, and top it off with my favorite simple snowflake punch and a tiny brad.

Two very nice folders. One is a sort of knotted "net," with a bit of detail; the other is a sort of stylized scallop shell. I'm planning to fancy up some spring cards with a few tiny pearls in strategic spots (the "knots" on the net pattern; the tiny "loops" on the scallop design.

But these would work perfectly fine sans the "bling.". And they work just fine in my Sizzix Big Shot machine.

Very happy with this purchase!

You can never go wrong with Offray ribbon, and this gorgeous chocolate satin ribbon is no exception. It's the perfect size for my card making, and the chocolate brown color is one I use a lot. The double face satin finish makes bow making a snap -- you don't have to worry if the ribbon twists in the process since both sides look identical.

Twenty yards may seem like a lot, but the price is great, and I do use a lot of it, so it's super economical for me. You'd pay a LOT more per yard at a crafts store (I've seen similar ribbon sold on a 3 yd spool for 5 dollars!)

Absolutely gorgeous! I've been using these in my hand-crafted holiday cards this year with a variet of inks. These are beautifully detailed, very well made, and work with my Fiskars and Stampin' Up acrylic blocks.

The style harkens back to the early Victorian Christmas cards, and it works really well with traditional papers and designs.

I love this embossing folder! It works for just about anything: masculine cards, feminine cards, Christmas, birthday -- any occasion, really. I've had mine for only about a week, and I've already used it on two Christmas cards, a get well card, and one "thinking of you" cards.

I have a Sizzix Big Shot, and it works perfectly. Nice crisp impression, very classy looking!

I enjoy making greeting cards, and I'm always looking for new things to use in my crafting. Saw this and knew immediately what I wanted to do with it.

Metallic card stock can be VERY pricey, and I don't use a whole lot of it, which makes purchasing a 25- or 50-sheet package I bit uneconomical.

Enter this metallic duct tape.

I put a piece of the tape on a piece of (much cheaper) plain cardstock, and run it through my die cutter to create a trim, border, flourish, frame, letter/number, whatever. It cuts just fine, and it looks great! It's slightly textured from the woven fiber base, but that just adds to the appeal for me.

The tape has a tendency to stretch a bit as I'm unrolling it from the roll, but I found that if I just take my time and use even pressure, it comes cleanly off the roll.

Very happy with this! I also purchased the silver/chrome version as well. Wish they made it in copper!

They're elegant without being too fussy, which makes them a great choice for both masculine and feminine cards. I've already used several of them on cards for friends, and have plans for several more.

Very nice, easy to clean, and a fantastic selection of greetings for different occasions.

I really like these Spellbinders M-Bossabilities reversible embossing folders. I own several of them, and they're beautiful and easy to use.

I them with my Sizzix Big Shot die cutter/embosser with no problems. The impression is crisp and clear.

This "Venetian" folder is gorgeous -- two different patterns, both useful for any application where a touch of old-world elegance is needed. It's one of my most-used embossing folders.

I make greeting cards, and need a mat to protect my desk. I do a lot of cutting on this mat, and it shows hardly any wear after a year of use. Love having the grid lines printed on the mat, too -- they help me easily line up various elements on my cards.

The size is just right, too. Very happy with this.

I've been making cards for several years, but just got into stamping. I purchased these blocks because they looked like they would Be easy to use, and I thought the grid lines would help me line things up.

They do, indeed. It's easy to center a stamp on the block, and then use the grid to line it up on the card.

The variety of sizes is good, too. I did end up purchasing one more block: a large one that can stamp an A2 size card front.

Very nice set, easy to handle and clean. Good purchase.

Love this set! I've been making cards for several, but just got into stamping recently. I do greeting card assortments to donate to various charities for fund raising (silent auctions, etc), and I need a variety of sentiments. I've used outline stickers, but they only come in a few colors; I've also used my computer and its word processing program to create printed sentiments, but that doesn't always give the effect I want.

So I've been turning to stamps, and chose this set for the variety it offers: birthday, get well, sympathy, "just because," etc. I use it often because I can use different colors of ink, the sizes are perfect for cards (not too big, not too small), and they're easy to use with the [[ASIN:B0016859GU Fiskars 01-000068 Stamp Block Set]].

Good investment, for sure!

I've been using this with a set of "chocolate"-themed cardstock and papers to create some elegant greeting cards for friends and family. The cardstock has some gold foil accents, so this ribbon goes perfectly with it.

The best thing about us is that the gold edging doesn't make it stiff at all. It's still pliable enough to tie a small bow with feeling like I'm tying my fingers in knots.

Very nice stuff, indeed.

Oh my, where to begin? Well, first, these work perfectly to make holes in cardstock and paper, which is what I use them for.

You know those times when you need to make a hole for a brad in the middle of a 5x7 card? Most hole punches don't reach far enough; other hole punches require a hammer. These have a built-in "hammer": there's a spring connecting the top and bottom sections; you position the punch, pull up the top section, let it go, and you get a resounding "THWACK!" and there it is: a perfectly placed hole right where you want it.

Need to set an eyelet? No problem! Make a hole as above, insert the eyelet with the "business end" up, flip the Fiskars gizmo over, place the setter in the eyelet, pull up the top section, and let it go. Another satisfying "THWACK!" and your eyelet is set. It really is that easy.

This set has the sizes you're likely to use the most. I use brads in all sizes, from mini to oversized, so I use the small and medium most often.

Very nice set, very good price, too.

Elegant designs! You get 4 folders in this set: a large one with an all- over "damask" pattern A2 size (4.75" x 5.5"); a smaller envelope that embosses a frame w/ fleur de lys; and two small envelopes (a round medallion w/ fleur de lys and a small square one).

These are really nice, and super easy to use. Sizzix embossing folders are consistently high quality. If you need an elegant touch for a card or scrapbook page, this set might fit your needs well.

Nice pairing of embossing folders, both suited beautifully for Christmas. I've been using the holly folder with plain gold metallic cardstock, pairing it with some holly print cardstock with glitter highlights for some Christmas cards. Very nice!

The music folder will be used after Christmas to create a set of greeting cards for various events (birthday, thank you, sympathy, anniversary, etc.), all with a "music" theme.

I use these in a Sizzix Big Shot die cutting machine, and they're super easy to use. Cardstock goes in the folder, folder is sandwiched between two acrylic plates, the sandwich goes on the platform with one tab open, and the whole thing is cranked through the machine. What could be easier?

I own several embossing folders, and this set is one of my favorites.

This one has everything for paper crafters (I make cards):

Metal edge -- great to use with a craft knife (Exacto, etc)

Transparent -- so you can see what you're cutting

Centering -- includes a centering guide. I use this more than I thought I would. Want to cut a square or rectangle with your flower or text perfectly centered? Just place the "O" over the center of your item, and measure/mark according to the measurements on either side of the "O". Works perfectly every time.

Printed grid lines -- help you keep things "square" as you measure.

It's wider than most metal rulers, but it still fits in my craft bag with no problems. Very happy with this.

I purchased this to replace an old worn out Exacto, mostly because it uses the same blades as my old one, and I still had some spares left over. The choice was between another Exacto or this "fancier" one from Martha Stewart. I decided to give this one a shot.

Very impressed with the design. It fits my hand well, and the grip is secure and comfortable. I use the knife in my card making to cut cardstock, outline stickers, and clean up die cuts. The cap fits tightly, and is easy to remove, but not so easy that it might come off accidentally. And having the mechanism that loosens the blade on the other end of the knife, away from the blade end, means that you won't accidentally loosen the blade while working. The blade is held very securely -- no way it's going to wobble!

I'm very happy with my purchase.

I just started using a die cutting machine ([[ASIN:B001AGZZIU Sizzix 655268 Big Shot Cutting-and-Embossing Roller-Style Machine]]), and this was one of the first non-Sizzix dies I purchased. I make a lot of cards, and I wanted something to "fancy them up" a bit.

The dies work fine with my Big Shot, although I had to play a bit to get the "sandwich" set up properly. The instructions from Spellbinders explaining how to use the dies with various machines sort of work, but check around online, and you can find other suggested combinations that seem to work better, both for cutting and for embossing.

Also, these work better with good-quality cardstock. The cut piece should pop out fairly easily, leaving behind the "innies" in the die to remove with a pin or other pointed tool.

What fun! I recently purchased a Sizzix Big Shot [[ASIN:B001AGZZIU Sizzix 655268 Big Shot Cutting-and-Embossing Roller-Style Machine]], and I've been having a ball playing with it.

This set of dies has become one of my favorites. You get three separate dies: one each for the two larger flourishes, and one that contains the two smaller curlicues. I've been using them lately on a set of Christmas cards, using metallic cardstock to create a bit of "flash."

I can see these dies getting a lot of use in future projects.

I love the richness of the colors in these papers. I've been using them to make some Christmas cards, and the quality is impressive. They work great in a die cutting machine, too.

Definitely worth the money. You get 16 sheets, two each of 8 double-sided pages, plus a page of 45 die cuts. I've always thought of Prima as the manufacturer of excellent-quality paper flower embellishments; they also make very fine papers.

This is a well-made sturdy edge punch that adds a lovely lacy edge to my hand-crafted cards.

When thet say "large," they mean it. The punched area is about 7/8" wide from the edge of the card or paper.

It's easy to line up the punch to create a longer decorative edge by repeating the punches. You can also punch along the edge of some paper or card stock, and the trim the punched edge about 1/8" from the punched area to make a 1"-wide "ribbon."

Easy to use, cuts cleanly, well worth the investment. Will likely purchase more of these EK Success Tools edgers.

Well, maybe not *quite* that dramatic, but this little glue pen has solved a lot of problems in my card making. I now have a way to glue embellishments that have "bits" that are too small for glue dots or the usual liquid glues that are available. It's as easy as "drawing" around the edge of the piece, and dropping it into place. I prefer to use the "permanent option; the "removable" option reduces the "stickiness." I just use my tweezers to get the flower or butterfly (or whayever) placed correctly, and it works beautifully.

There seems to be a fair amount of glue in the pen, too. I've been using it for a week on a project (cards for a silent auction fund raiser), and I've barely used maybe 1/5 of it (24 cards finished so far, most with at least one element that needed the glue pen).

Very pleased with this purchase!

These perform as well as (or better than!) much more expensive scissors. The handle is super comfortable, and they are very sharp. I've used them for awhile now mostly for paper crafts (card making). It's easy to make accurate cuts in even the thinnest paper, as well as thicker card stock and die-cut cardboard.

I like the color -- it's easy to spot in my supply box. These are so well made that I have no doubt they will last for years. The price is definitely low for the quality of these.
This actually does work and will tell you who out here living life fraudulently or who real. Ha ha ha be safe!
Paint doesn't come through nozzle when you squeeze too hard it comes out everywhere else.  Wasn't able to use any of these

I am pretty sure these are not really sterling silver they turned pretty quickly and looked like the silver color came off within a few days of wearing.  I wouldn't be able to sell these because they tarnish so easy.
These are nice, firm, well-made panels in a professional grade. I use them for oil and acrylic paintings that I sell to collectors. No problems. No warping.
These are really unique and beautiful designs. Lots of curvy butterflies. Nice!

Like this modeling paste which I use under acrylics and oils. I am a professional artist.
Dries hard as concrete! I can get some marvelous textures with this paste.
It shrinks as it dries, just like all texture mediums.
Also way less expensive than the ridiculous pricey name brands.
Really nice quality oil paint. It is creamy and the color is rich and natural looking. It is a little wetter than other brands, but works well with cold wax.

Bought the mini set of metallics to try out in cold wax and oil painting artwork. They are creamy and have lots of metallic sheen. Goes on smoothly and works well for mark making on a cold wax/oil paint project.
You just have to remember to pull away a bit of the 'skin' that forms to get to the creamy paint. It pops off as you use the stick also, so no problem and it keeps the paint inside nice and moist.
I have used these with water mixable oil paints, no apparent odor.
Less expensive than the pigment sticks. I am going to buy a couple of the sets of bright colors - hard to find oil colors in tubes in those color ranges.

Lovely brush quality. I wanted to try this natural hair and synthetic blend. Works great. Loads lots of paint and keeps going and going.
The light color lets you see how loaded the brush is.

Nice product and great packing.
Basic mattes, backer boards and acetate envelopes. Clean and well packed in plastic and every corner cushioned with foam, then packed in a box and sealed. That box inside Amazon's box. So it arrived in perfect condition.
Very pleased.
Will use this for quick framing for art shows and for my own home. I frame a piece with this matte and board and set it on a tabletop easel. Then change it out for another piece later. Fun!
These work great for acrylic craft paints - writing and smaller line details in painting and crafts.

Very nice! Not sure it would be watertight if kept at an angle, but it is nice and roomy.
Seal-able and compact, makes it easy to keep my watercolors in the refrigerator, as I live in the tropics and they tend to mold here..
Made a light weight summer cover for my king size bed. Size was right and love the light weight.

Bought this to replace my 30 year old Singer. Works well. Has many features the old machine didn't have, and probably more than I will ever use. It is good if you want to try out embroidery and other features without getting a thousand dollar machine. Of course it is all plastic, not cast iron like the old machines, but works pretty well for my hobby, home decor, and apparel sewing. I have not had problems with the bobbin or threading. This machine does not include the walking foot that is shown. I contacted the company but they would not send it to me.
These were great for a painting party I had. I put a 11x14 canvas board on them, and they seemed to hold up pretty well. Nice for the price!

This particular item was obviously used. It looks like someone took it out of the package and refolded it, which, if you've used tissue patterns before, is a pain and nearly impossible to do. But then it looks like someone tore the pattern down the middle, maybe to mark it as dead stock to be thrown away? Anyway, I spent a few good hours taping it all together last night. An irritating inconvenience but I was able to salvage the pieces I needed. I would not have paid full price for this item if I had known it was going to be torn up.
Very soft and easy to work with.
My mom has a collection and needed this size to complete it. She loves this brand and this was a really good price.
Ordered 2 from another seller, they TWICE sent me the incorrect cable, BTW completely different from what was shown. So grateful that I ordered and received the correct one!

Got these for a quilt retreat because 'seams' like I always misplace mine. Ordered these as they looked cozy to hold. They were easy to grip, but a little short. They were very dull and actually destroyed some of my fabric. Not happy at all over that! Actually had to leave to purchase more fabric to replace what these destroyed. So, beware!
Fit my Janome!

Got this as one of the cats nibbled on my new bag. Had some trial and error as I used the paper that came with it to match. Unfortunately, not sure it said this, but mine darkened when it dried. Had to remix and came out much better. Still cheaper than getting a new purse!
Been looking for a replacement for a machine been restoring. Purchased about 4 that were supposed to fit the machine but didn't. This worked perfectly! Thank you!

I got this after my mom told me how awful sewing the flying geese blocks were. I saw a video on this product and thought I would try for my quilt guild BOTM. I'm happy with the product. You line it up against your seams to check accuracy and to trim. Now, if I could just consistently sew a straight line!

I wanted to try the magic loop for knitting socks. I've just seen reviews all over the place for different brands so wanted to try these. For the most part, I truly like them. The only issue I can say I have with them is that at the joint of the cord and the needle, it catches a bit. Outside of that, pretty happy with my purchase!
Okay, I was a little gun shy about getting these because of a previous purchase. These came and very happy with them. Good size, a little weight to them. I got them to use as zipper pulls on a coat.
Just got and like the organization, just waiting to see how sturdy.
As described. Worked well for me.
Love this brand and happy with the color. Exactly what I wanted.
Fits & works perfectly.
Took me a bit to recall how the belts went back on. Ran like a champ!

I purchased this with a gift card my dad gave me for Christmas. Been waiting for the weather to improve to use it. Okay, I need a LOT of practice but what a great value! The items came in a sturdy box and everything in it for the project. I had received a link for a video on how to do it as well. I followed along and didn't do too bad, well, except for the face, but I will get there. WARNING! This was very addictive and I didn't realize I was so focused on it and gripping so hard that I gave myself a blister. I will try to use in moderation in the future until I get a decent callous or gloves!
So much for such a small price! Liked it "sew" much, I ordered one for my mom as well. Thanks for the product!
I like the machine, just would've been nice if this came with it, purchase online or something....
Good price!
Very good quality and arrived on time. I plan to use them to make earrings. Very easy to use. I would definitely buy them again.
Very good quality and arrived well packed. They were packed in plastic bags that were sealed real well. They will work very well when I make some earrings.
They arrived on time and look great. They look very pretty. I plan to use them to make earrings. Very good quality.

Great for jigsaw puzzles! But does not come with a spreader, so I saved a scraper from an old bottle of another brand and used it to spread the glue over the puzzle. This is by far the best I have found for jigsaw puzzles, and the price is the best.
The best scissors I ever used. Sharp. Comfortable to hold. Build quality is excellent. Price is very high, but if you want the best scissors for use in the kitchen, these fit the bill.

The dimensions of this foam pad are supposed to be 60 inches by 80 inches. The size if off at multiple places regarding the width and the length, with as much as 2 inches more than what it's supposed to be.

Therefore, it's no good for use on a queen bed as there's too much overhang.
These are excellent blades. I use the box cutter regularly, and I really like it. The cutter's retractable mechanism glides very smoothly.
My favorite kitchen scissors. Multiple uses, from cutting a solar curtain to size to cutting open food packages. Feels good in the hand and it is sharp. I recommend this scissors.

The power of this thing is disappointingly weak. The housing feels like cheap plastic. The on-off slider switch is terrible. A tiny touch of it causes the power to go on. The slider switch and its problems gives the impression that this is a toy.

This tool does the job of helping to remove sewn-in labels in shirts. I wish there were no shirt-makers that used tags that can irritate. But as long as they keep doing that, this tool is there to help with the removal.

I recommend this good quality tool.
I use this for oiling my air conditioner spindle shaft to the fan !!! My dad still sews @ 91 & he uses in his sewing machines  !!!
My neighbour ordered this and she can't stop raving about the high quality.she said it was in a well made case and that she thought the magnification was higher than the description.
Works Perfectly.

I really like this finish for my diamond painting. It dries fast and leaves the canvas very bright .I would buy this again.
Using this for my grandchildren they will love them
I was having trouble with my beads getting all over the floor so these mats make it way better
Easy to read very sticky.Going to be so pretty I am almost done with it
The color of the bookmark looks better than the pictures here.  It is bright and beautiful.

I generally buy an organic DK weight cotton yarn for all my projects, but was looking for something with a little more bulk that was at least natural if not organic. I found this and liked the color variety so I decided to give it a shot. I am used to paying a lot for my organic yarn, but cannot justify the cost of THIS particular cotton yarn for what was received. The skein was small, loosely intertwined, and most importantly NOT WORSTED WEIGHT. It was a light worsted at best, but border-lining on a DK. It was soft enough for a cotton yarn, but without the width and weight expected you will not be able to properly complete any patterns or projects calling for worsted weight or Aran yarn.
This is a great Case.  Is is so nice to have all of my art supplies in one case.  Including my easel. Easy to clean ,very sturdy,and lightweight.
Perfect for tucking in your purse..so handy has everything you need for quick repairs
Exactly what I ordered! Last order from a different company smelled moldy. This has zero odor and very easy to work with. Will order again!

I usually give glowing reviews. Smelled like mold right out of the package. Soaked in vinegar for 2 days, soaked in Dawn dish detergent 2 days. Thoroughly rinsed. Still mold. Currently in my garbage can and it made it smell like mold. Seriously gross and unusable

Board and ruler is nice. Package came damaged with note on it from post office that they had to repackage it and the cutting tool was missing. Didn't realize right away because I ordered a long time ago and just now opened it

Been difficult to find this item in local craft stores.  Glad it was available at good price on Amazon.  Having it shipped and quickly was wonderful. Product made well and has been used for a project already.

I bought these because I bought an expensive cashmere sweater that arrived with a small hole in the shoulder. I was really sad because I LOVED that sweater but also because it couldn’t be returned. I had the idea of ironing on a patch to repair it. I picked these patches at random on a whim. Since my sweater has kind of a pop art theme I had a hard time picking which of these adorable patches to use. So many of them would have looked great. I picked a cute little butterfly, ironed it on, setting the iron a bit too hot admittedly, but it got the job done. I can’t speak to the durability but if I see it’s coming off I have about 50 more choices to fix it again. Thanks for turning my bad day good!

Wanted a flexible tape measure for my DIY sewing. I've owned it for a few days now, and it works great for me!
Cute beads. Some of the wood openings had to be made large to accommodate my daughters braids but overall I am happy with the purchase.
Portable and effective!

[[VIDEOID:f1ba31a5850125cda0c63ca694307d4e]] I do love the threaders, so I’m giving them a 2 star for that!
I’m just really disappointed in these sewing needles. They are extremely dull and challenging to get through a piece of THINb but double layered fabric! They might be previously used, because they came in a clear tube not in an original packaging. I might call Amazon over this. I’m that upset!
.... and very difficult to use without the eye wire!  I see the price dropped to $1.82 after i paid $3.99, i might just return them since i have to pay over double the price and no eye wire.
This is a great project for my grandchildren.  Very happy with this item.

Easy to work up a nice blanket for my newest grandchild.  :)  This was my first attempt at a C2C blanket, as I wanted something my daughter-in-love can use to bring baby home from the hospital.  I will be making a more complex one for her 6 month birthday.  I was concerned how small the skeins were, but the yarn is thin, so it worked very well.  I only found instructions for the stitch so didn't know how large to make the blanket.  I had ordered eight skeins, so simply worked the pattern with three skeins up, then three skeins down, saving some for the edging.  It turned out larger than 36x36, but I think that's okay. :)

Loved it!  Got it to cast my grandon's hand and mine.  He's three, so it was a bit difficult to keep him still, but it worked out decent.  I plan on doing it again when he is older.  I think it turned out pretty good, though, for a first time!  I'd advise having baby oil, a couple of disposable bowls, some measuring cups and spoons, and plenty of newspaper to help with casting.  After it sets, but before it dries, I'd suggest having some fine grit sandpaper and orange sticks on hand, along with the safety pin and small piece of sandpaper that comes in the kit, to help clean up any rough spots and parts that didn't come clean in the demolding process.  I like how you can make your own base by pouring on more of the casting mixture at the top.
This tool worked well with sizing my finger after I had a couple missed sizings without it. I will no longer order a ring before I have used this tool.  Great item.
These coins came well packed in their own clean plastic cases. The detail finish is clean and sharp. Great price on 13 coins. Rack is not included.
Great combination for edging , love it
Does the job, nothing more.
I can organize my sewing feet. I label on the top lid what presser feet are storages. Very handy.
Needed storage for my Westalee rulers and templates. This fits the need. I have another Yazzii bag and between the both of them I can store all my large rulers and templates.

Great for quilting when using jelly rolls
No waste of cutting regular square batting
This is my go to for jelly roll quilts
Was really small case not what I expected

I just love this sewing machine, after not sewing for many years, I decided to go back. My old Brothers was out of commission and I wanted to see what improvements had been made. WOW!!! This machine threads the needle, winds bobbins and has 150 different stitches. I don't have to use the pressure foot and can control the speed with a flick of a switch. It backstitches and is very easy to use.  I haven't had a problem with it. I have owned this machine for 3 years now and never had a problem. The only thing I would change is the lighting (its a bit to low for my eye sight) but that's it.
I'm using this pigment, added to matte medium to prime my panels in black.  It's working well.

These are the best paints for a beginner. Just open and pour or swipe. Add silicone and just go for it, and when it dries it looks so beautiful. Now I will only buy these in larger quantity. No more worrying over mixing a pour medium again.

These are VERY lightweight and DO NOT hold the cord tightly - so are pretty much useless, since this is what they are used for.  Look elsewhere!

I purchased this because it is advertised as "COTTON" yarn.  It is actually a blend of 85% cotton and 15% polyester!  This will NOT work for my project and had this been honestly disclosed in the description, I would not have purchased it in the first place.  Very unhappy - it is EASY to provide an honest description, so this seems deceptive to me.  Just want everyone else considering buying this to be aware that it is not as it seems!  The picture shows the label on the products I received with the content part...
This is lovely fabric with vivid colors that really pop!  I am very happy with this purchase and can't wait to finish my project.

This is the worst quality muslin I've ever seen!  It is as rough as burlap and certainly not worth the money.  I honestly don't know what you would use this for - maybe a lining for a dog bed or something for outdoors, but that's about it.  Do not waste your money like I did - it's garbage!

Very pleased with these earring cards - a bit on the light side, but they work for my purposes!  I needed cards with lever back cut-outs and these fit the bill.  They were shipped and received quickly and packaged carefully.  Give them a try - I think you'll be pleased, as well!  Overall, an excellent buy!

I like these bottles and have purchased them multiple times.  The reason I deducted a star is that the lids are nearly useless after using a few times.  I've found that using floral pins with large tops are perfect as stoppers once the lids fail, which they will.  Otherwise these bottles serve my needs well for various craft glues and liquids.

These zippers were a great purchase for me!  I got tired of buying BeautyRest Black (down alternative) pillows at a big box store to only have them be nearly unusable a couple months later.  So, I decided to deconstruct the pillow, remove the stuffing, sew in a zipper, sew it up and restuff it with the original stuffing and stuffing from another pillow.  This way, I can add or remove stuffing as needed and don't have to keep buying pillows!  Can still be washed and dried.  These are quality zippers and exactly what I needed!
It takes a bit to receive these but they are very good quality!  This is my second order and still the same great mini brads!

The mini brads are plentiful and that is the extent of the positive things I can say!  They are poorly machined and I had to fiddle with each one with jewelry pliers to make them work - several are simply unusable.  Too bad, they looked okay in the picture!

If you need something that is actually 8.5 x 11 inches - look elsewhere.  Every singe sheet was less than 11-inches, so didn't work for what I needed!  The "Cardstock" is pretty flimsy, but that is not my main issue - the sizing is not as advertised.  I do like that it is white on one side, but without the correct sizing - it doesn't matter.  The seller is unaffected by bad reviews, so don't expect any customer service!

These are very pretty square beads...until you start to use them and realize that the holes that should be drilled vertically are not.  The majority are drilled at a 10-20 degree vertical angle, so the beads do not sit properly in the design!  I'm very disappointed!  Out of one of the bags of 50 (I bought 2) only four of the beads were drilled relatively straight.  These are clearly 2nd's or 3rd's in terms of quality and should be stated as such!  GARBAGE!
These are excellent quality connectors and work perfectly with my bulk ball chain.  These are also an exceptional value - I paid more for less quantity and quality.  These are perfect for my needs!

I purchased a 10-pack in January and started using them in April.  All of them are either old or have been damaged by some environmental factor, such as heat.  I have used this glue for a very long time and the consistency should be a thick liquid, easy to spread.  These 10 I purchased are nearly impossible to squeeze out of the bottles and is the consistency of slightly liquid foam, LUMPY AND VERY HARD TO USE.  I definitely got ripped off on my bulk purchase and have never had an issue before, otherwise I would have checked them when they arrived.  I paid a lot more than the posted price today, as well!

These are a terrible deal!  Not at all like they are pictured - more than 90% are designs that must be centered but are NOT and so unusable for my purposes.  Sloppy and basically garbage!  Just flush your money in the toilet instead - it will be less frustrating! Only eight pieces are usable!  I HATE FALSE ADVERTISING!

This is wonderful embroidery floss!  I don't use it for counted cross-stitch, so I don't care about the lack of numbers.  The colors are beautiful and absolutely perfect for my use and I highly recommend it!
I ordered three of these and should have recd 3,000 3mm beads.  I counted and recd fewer than 1,500 - recd 1,458.  Not as advertised and I don't like being cheated.

This is a bargain for the price. It comes in a beautiful box. All of the items included are high quality. It's is simple and fun to use. It brings a personal touch to your cards and letters. I love it!

Everyone goes up and down in weight through the year, so all you have to do is place this button on your pants by the original button, then move it over to where it will give you a perfect fit.  Then take the backing and push it into the button stick and you’re done.  Sort of like putting on a pierced earring. If you need to move it, just pull of the backing, move the button then put the backing back on. I actually keep them on several pairs of jeans.  They don’t fall off.  I only remove them when I wash the jeans.

Great aroma, long lasting and revive your spirits, great for a oil burner or dilute with a carrier oil but always try a a patch test, I love it.

To be clear this is Faux Leather.. I'd give this 5 stars if the product mentioned the correct info. I would give it 3 stars because of the misleading info, but I'm actually happy with the quality of the product. Since this is not real leather. It's not leather.. it's faux, though I was double checking to see if they mentioned that in the product info because I thought I was buying "real" leather. This stuff burns and melts like plastic. It does smell odd right out of the package, it's somewhat stretchy (a dead give away it's not leather). I bought the off white color which is actually quite nice and you can un-braid it which is also nice if you want to use single small strands for other purposes.

I made the mistake of not quite recognizing HOW SMALL these really were. I should have gone with something more like a 12mm size - these are EXTREMELY SMALL, but depending on what you're using them for they're a nice quality and the amount is worth the money. They will fit over a 550lb paracord rope if you're interested but that's literally a snug fit (just so you understand the size/how small they are). I bought them because I thought they would make a nice connector ring for paracord projects but as mentioned, it was my mistake not realizing how small they are.

There are some slight varied differences in how well the swivel is holding the lobster clasp in place. I actually found that one came detached while in my pocket (no clue how that even happened) and then pressing with my fingers on the swivel section with slight force caused it to break like cheap metal... so I started looking at the rest of them for any issues. 2 out of the 40 were broken, which one might say are good odds but it does make me question the rest of them.
I noticed the size of the portion within the swivel ring varied and that's probably why the one came undone and detached from the swivel. It makes me wonder if it will happen to others and if you have something important attached, you obviously want something reliable.They also came in a zip lock back, not a box labeled "Prudance" as shown in the product images.
Received the cutter quickly use them for cutting thread during leather crafting works like a charm especially on waxed thread would buy again

This small press does everything stated in the description-came right on time well packed ready to go-very sturdy easy to assemble does the job well would recommend to anyone who does leather work good luck!

This little package was quite a  help for stitching it is well built an excellent addition to punching holes in a straight angle once you get the hang of the puncher it helps things move quicker has  a two prong and a four prong that t takes  seconds to switch out would buy again good luck

I’m always losing (misplacing) scissors. I decided to order these and put my name on them (I swear family take them and that’s how they disappear lol). Now I have them by my bed, in my backpack, in a hold all etc). They are great quality for a very fair price. Highly recommend.

I wear an ID tag around my ankle. It stretches and some of the Velcro on the bracelet hooks stick to the things like the blanket on the bed, a sweatshirt, socks, wool dress pants, etc. I finally figured out that cutting a bit of Velcro over the open area and putting it down on it, would eliminate it catching. It works like a charm. I had a terrible problem with it snagging on the electric blanket on my bed while I slept but this totally eliminated that. I don't use this in the comventional sense but it still am very very happy with the product.

I gave this to my brother for the holidays. He is an international seed corn equipment dealer. He also likes candles.

This looked like an ear of corn. There was nothing fake about it. I said to myself when I took it out of the box "oh wow". It may be hard to find a holder for it. This was just the candle but I figured he would have something. If you don't have a lot of candle holders, it may be hard to find something.

VERY bright and easy to use. I decorate kippot and am going to try to put a design on a knit cap. I think these will be the ones that will be better than any I have used before. I did a test kippah with these and the colors were vibrant and bright, easily spreadable and great to work with. Very happy!
I like these but there is not a lot of crayon is the plastic holder. They work wonderfully but they don't last long. Still....great fun while they last.

The chain part of these are pretty cheap but all in all, it's great for a kids project or something along those lines. I probably would not order again. The "leather" is also pretty rigid. I have seen better necklaces for DIY projects but if you are doing something with children, these would be ok.

I guess you get what you pay for. The cord was very nice but the chain was very fragile and we have had 2 that broke at the chain.
It will work for some things but just watch the chain part.

This is a sturdy good quality patch. A lot of patches anymore are flimsy but this one is not. I had it sewn to the sleeve of a parka and it looks really really nice.
I would recommend this in a heartbeat
Good quality patch. I had it sewn to a winter coat sleeve and it looks nice. I love the gold border
Quality of the headpins was excellent. Only the twisted wire which required straightening before use was an issue.
The handle is easy to hold, the brush is thin bristles that are easy to use.
Maintains its stretch over time.
The quality and color of the beads are excellent!
Very easy to hold and use! They cut effortlessly!!!
Vibrant, and easy to use. Ive been using it in soap and the colors are amazing

Was excited to get this today! Then I saw the package and realized it wasnt as much as I thought, BUT it was as advertised. So off we went to repair our chairs.Sadly it went down hill after that. The tape wouldnt pull apart in places, no matter how hard we tried. The paper itself would peel, several times but once you got to the last level so to speak the tape would not let go. See picture, better explanation I hope. Then there were places that the tape itself were missing, looked melted, also, please see pictures to explain better. All and all probably 1/2 the tape was useless and thats after spending loads of time trying to get the paper off. To be honest, some of it peeled off just like you would expect, but then bam, stuck on the paper. Just not worth the money for the frustration and amount that was usable

I got this for my brother who needed the case wrench to fix his Omega, I figured since he used to work with watches he could use the whole kit. He did fix his watch and saved a 400.00 dollar repair bill. His thoughts on this kit are :
Case wrench is great, he had zero issues
The screw drivers are nothing special.Just your typical inexpensive screw drivers
The Spring remover is better than the professional versions he has used
The case back remover is good, BUT only works on standard cases not chronographs. and yes I had to ask what a Chronograph was...its any watch with stop watch features, extra stems etc
all and all he was pleased. He did a crown and stem replacement on his Omega, and swapped out the bracelet on his tag and saved a big bill at the jewelers.
Me, Not being a watch person noticed the case, it is well constructed and has a place for everything. It looks professional.
I'm very happy with this purchase, saved my brother some money and it is something he can use.

I got this with a discount of 50%, even if I payed full price it would be worth the savings at the jewelers.

This is a great beginner set
Comes with a nice selection of paint colors.
The paints themselves give good coverage, and they blend well with each other and water.
I did 2 quick paintings to give the paint and the brushes a try and I was happy with everything. the brushes did NOT leave any bristles behind, I hate that, and these are well made for inexpensive brushes.
The caps on the paint go on and off with no issues,easy to keep the paint from drying out. Nothing worse than wonky caps.
If your new to or want to buy a great gift for someone discovering Acrylic painting this is a great set.You can learn a lot of techniques with the brushes and paints without spending a fortune.
My Only complaint was I found the brushes a bit short,BUT I have big hands, I think most people would be fine with them. the have a nice width, no finger cramping from trying to hold a skinny brush.
I'm newish to acrylics, and this set will give me hours of fun and learning.
OH, Important part..Cleanup
Easily washes up with water and /or soap.
While I was painting the background, My cat decided to skid through one of the wet canvases and run away, So, I had paint everywhere. It came off the table,carpet,and me with no problems. Just clean up while its still wet and its a breeze to cleanup

*I received these free to give them a try and give you my thoughts on the paint set.

This bears no resemblance to any jasmine (night blooming or otherwise) I’ve experienced. Other reviewers have said the same, so I moderated my expectations.

I’m usually pleased with Nature’s products, but this is just an odd scent to me. I’ll have to blend it, and if I hit a good one, I’ll update & upgrade this review. Something spicy may work, or even a blend with their Grape, which also doesn’t smell like grapes or grape candy (😅) but blends really well as a lifting note.

Scent is subjective, but my nose finds this a non-floral.
Buy if you like to experiment, but many will want to avoid.

This is absolutely not what most noses will define as carnation scent.
And yet after giving it a chance in wax melts and some glycerin soap, I’m really glad I bought it and will purchase more for my Y candle scent cup lamp.
It’s not heavy, it’s a fairly “green” smelling flower scent and it lingers long enough to be pleasant.
I would liken it more to a light white flowers blend. It did mix well with a bit of clove and cedar for soap; I did a bar with it pure and it was nice if you like flowers 💐 It also made an interesting blend with gardenia and baby rose.

Scent-love is so subjective!

Long time customer of Natures Oils thru Bulk Apothecary. Nice to be able to do small quick orders via Amazon!
My usual absolute-love-it scent sold out already so I tried this one.

It’s ok; people who like mild/bland orange blends will probably love it. I will try adding some different fir oils to it. As is, it will be used for small soaps for friends who prefer non-lingering quiet scents.

I didn’t care for it diffused.

This company is excellent, and I highly recommend them.

In love with natural ~~ be they wood or bamboo ~~ needles. I quit knitting many years ago because I found metal needles esthetically lacking, and plastic, don’t even ask.  Takumi makes the best bamboo needles I’ve found: sharp, even, smooth and pleasant to. Work with.  Much less fatigue with large projects.  Some knitters may not care for the very smooth surfaces of well crafted wood but except for extremely fine threads/yarns, i’m An all-natural needler now.  For folks having issues with extreme slick surfaces of needles, Knit Picks (available thru Amazon or on their direct website) make a ‘four-square needle’ which will help with thin delicate silk yarns, or the like. I’d love to see Clover come out with a similar product in bamboo in the smaller gage needles.

After reading about Addi clicks for so long, I decided to give them a try when this intro kit popped up. The needles themselves are beautiful, sharp points and smoothly surfaced. I had a bad time with the “push in and turn” that connects the cable to the needle but persevered. When I did a test run with silk and fine angora yarns, I was really disappointed to find the join hangs up the threads. Also, there is no “life line” hole built into these needles.
All in all, I much prefer my less expensive but ultimately better made Knit Picks interchangeables.

Brittany sp 4 mm needles, Birch.

Instantly fell in love with these. They are straight and true with just the perfect wee bit of flex if needed. Beautifully worked, not overly smooth. I’m currently knitting a baby alpaca/Merino/nylon Wonderfluff yarn and the glide on the needles is perfect. They are warm, strong, and long enough that you can establish quite a decent length of pattern before transferring to circular interchangeables (I use Knit Pick Sunstruck , available on Amazon or Knit Picks direct for Interchangeable needles. )
After 3 years of converting to wooden needles, I still marvel how much more comfortable (& esthetically pleasing!) they are vs old cold aluminium!!!!!!
I recommend these wonderful wooden needles without reservation.

Beautiful yarn, moderately challenging depending on what you’re doing with it. I purchased it for a narrow, lighter-weight scarf (compared to bulky-weight wools) in buttercream. As others have mentioned this yarn can be a wee finicky ~~ it stretches between needles unless you are knitting practically at the points, and I’m VERY thankful I had dark birch needles on hand as counting stitches can be a challenge. With all that being said, I will persevere with this because the results are worth it.
DO pay attention to those who’ve advised uncomplicated stitches; the “nap” will hide details and the stretch will frustrate. I swatched several options and decided on a basic seed stitch. Had to adjust my tension as well, for me tighter is better although that’s not my norm. I would suggest buying extra yardage to allow for some experimenting, and know that it does not frog well, the fluffy nature of it is not forgiving =^_^=
Like to use this doubled. Beautiful colour. Better buy on their website but I needed this fast! Love amazon. 💞

Beautiful needles. Too slick for some yarns. Wiping with wax paper helped.
I may convert them to shawl pins.

Love this! Luxurious merino; SOFT, plush, knits up beautifully with nice stitch definition. Very few flying fibers, and on US 15 birch needles I’ve had no splitting issues. The consistency is amazing with no thick/thin spots. My skin is sensitive to chemicals and poorly treated wools of any kind; this yarn totally passes my finicky skin test! My initial purchase was Alpine, a gorgeous silvery dark grey-green, and I’m buying more colours as fast as possible for Xmas projects.
Highly recommend this fabulous super-bulky yarn!

COLOUR NOTE: yarn in pictures IS Alpine. Not intense indigo as shown under verification!

Great little gauger. Inside has 4” x 1.25” area, perfect for tension swatches. No rough edges to ding expensive wooden tools! Cutter in trunk is a nice, protected sharp for fibers. US and metric measurements for needles and hooks. Fits in my yarn bag pocket perfectly.
Bonus for beaders: great visual reference for bead mm dimensions.
It’s plastic, but seems pretty durable.

Lovely kit. Although I already knit, wanted to try this brand’s yarn and needles and this was a great way to do so. The yarn is easy to work, seems well twisted and has not split ~~ Several cotton yarns I’ve tried split easily. The colours are great and the skeins are generous in size.
These are my first quality wooden needles and I LOVE them! Smooth, sharp, and so aesthetically pleasing.
For true beginners ~~ be patient with cotton yarn and small needles. Do a couple small practice pieces to get the hang of tension because cotton yarn can stretch. But it’s worth taking some extra time ~~ IT’s COTTON!
I did receive the correct pamphlet ( some reviewers did not). Decent instructions for those who need them.
Definitely recommend this kit, and if the brand is consistent I’ll be purchasing more.

This is my 6th mold from Longzang. I was worried when I saw it as the raised bamboo pattern is nowhere nearly as deep & defined as the picture, or my previous molds. The mold itself is Longzang's usual heavy duty yet flexible silicone. I gave it a shot, & while the raised pattern is indeed much less defined than others, it is obvious enough to "paint" with mica powder. The soap itself was very easy to unmold as the silicone sides are super flexible. Washes easily & passes the silicone "pinch" test.
One star removed for lack of pattern definition, but I do recommend this company. The photo shows the bar's size on a 9" dinner plate.

I'm so pleased with both this and the lotus-dragonfly mold I ordered from Longzang. The details on the finished soaps are precisely as pictured. The molds pass the pinch test (no white), have no odor, wash up easily, and release without any hassle. I poured clear hemp oil and Shea butter bases without issue. Both molds arrived well before the estimated delivery dates. After my first use I ordered two more designs.
Unless you have a dedicated soaping area I do recommend placing these molds on a firm, mobile surface prior to pouring. (I used a re-purposed salad bar container with a lid.) They are soft-sided and extremely flexible; until your soap sets up a wee bit moving the mold is not a good idea.
Absolutely recommend this seller and these molds.

Beautiful little angels, gift recipients were quite impressed. One friend slipped the gold thread hanger I added over her hair clip & wore it thru the holidays. I substituted gold-coloured wire for the silver supplied in kit. Delivery took longer than anticipated, had to push hard to finish by Xmas, so 4 stars.

I'm very short and bought a very long parka, and used these to tuck the hem up when I'd rather have it fall to my knee rather than my ankle, which is most of the time. The loops are easy enough to sew through and the magnets can hold a bit of weight. So far so good!
Arrived in about the same half-folded, half-rolled configuration you'd get from the fabric store, and fused my wool project just fine.
This stuff is great for machine work.
This stuff was perfect for binding seams on a wool gown. I could not for the life of me fold them to fell them down, but this was a good plan B.
This is one of the colors in Gutermann's assortment of 26. Surprisingly hard to figure out what to get if you've lost the stickers on the end!

I'm still on my honeymoon with this machine after over a year. The foot guide is amazing, it teaches you what foot is right for the job based on what you're doing, and how to use it. I feel like I can sew anything. I do seem to get more requests to hem awful synthetic bridesmaid dresses now, though.

This is a great item. The feathers are solidly attached to the band, and are quite dense. They have been flattened, which is good for sewing projects, and are somewhat flexible. The color is true black.
This stuff is fantastic!

I bought these on Prime day thinking I was getting the deal of a lifetime. The price today is exactly the same. While I feel slightly cheated, I would buy them again in a heartbeat. I bought them for my 7 year old daughter who loves art. She treasures them! I’ve been using them to make song lyric posters for Sunday School. The colors are awesome and pop against the white background. I started making the posters with Sharpie markers, but they don’t hold a candle to the quality of these markers. The colors are great and they glide easily on the surface of both the glossy and matte sides of poster board.

Initially I had no idea what the clear marker was for, but it’s now my favorite marker in the set. When you use markers to color something in, you end up with lines throughout. When you use the clear/blender marker to “color” over other colors, those lines disappear like magic! That may be common knowledge to most people, but I was surprised and impressed with it.

For the price, I don’t think you’ll find a better set of markers. I’ll be a customer for life!

I am loving these markers. I make a "chalkboard" for my daughters birthday each year with black poster board and chalk markers. Every year I end up buying a new set of markers and half of them dry up before I finish one project. These markers are even more vibrant than the ones I've had in the past. Every color works great (every other brand I've tried has at least one color that is dried up when I buy it).

To use these you press down on the marker tip and the liquid metallic ink runs down into the felt tip of the marker. They aren't runny, and you can make your letters or markings thick or thin depending on how you hold the marker. I have already used these for multiple projects and they are all still in great working order. I wish I had found these markers 3 years ago when I first started using them, it would have saved me a lot of money. I would recommend these to anyone who is crafty. My 3 year old daughter even loves to draw with these. She calls them her "shiny" markers.

This stand overall would have been close to perfect, especially for the low price if not for one flaw. Maybe mine was missing something but I don't think so, everything appeared to be there. I couldn't get it to sit securely without sliding all over the table. It has a small piece of rubber wrapped around the wire frame that rests on the table but it didn't keep it from going anywhere. I'll need to get creative and possibly use something like a couple of rolled up balls of poster putty stuck to the table and place the wire frame into the putty to keep it from shifting while trying to use a Light Pad on the Stand. Still not a bad price for the size, I just wish the person I'm giving this to for a gift wouldn't have to chase it across the table while using it.

This mold is very easy to use, I made the ashtray shown in the photo. It was very easy to release it from the mold, there was no residue left in the mold, and the mold is made from silicone, making it very easy to clean.

I had an order to put images on a lot of face masks, I used the white htv it worked perfectly. It was easy to weed and apply. I like that it came with so many colors, they were a great value. I would order these again.
Always convenient to order on line.
works fine
I love these boxes! They are easy to open and close. Good for storing small items.

The designs are nice, vibrant and very cute, colorful and elegant! I used them to decorate the walls in my guest room, they made the room very cozy and colorful! I highly recommend this product for all kinds of art projects :)

These are pretty crummy and not good enough for jewelry making for sale imho. Casting is shotty and the color/style distribution was terrible if you're expecting a variety.

Biggest problem is they're making these bezels from the outside filigree, NOT the size of the internal circle which is where you're going to be mounting something, so they're all too small for what I bought them for, making butterfly necklaces. The wings stick out of the mounting part of the bezel, out over the filigree part, which obviously won't work.

I've find a few other cute things to do with them but overall I'm pretty miffed they measured it that way, that I only got like 2 silver bezels, and the casting on them is quite poor.

Three stars because price was pretty reasonable and some of the bezels are pretty cute. I should have just ponied up and for more expensive bezels. You get what you pay for so it's partially my fault. Oh well, live and learn.

This is an excellent value for the money, so it's hard to go wrong even if you don't get the color you want or something. I bought these to take to a visit with my family, but I had a few extra so I've opened 3 already. Hoping for a black pearl, I chose the smallest ones based on another review -- they were white, white, and pink sadly, so all the small ones are NOT black, except maybe in certain batches or by coincidence. But the surprise is part of the fun I guess, if you MUST have a certain color, you should probably just buy it. Assuming these are random, and some colors are more common than others, who knows what you might get... you might get 10 white ones, so you have to be ok with that or else order specific colors. The pearls were of different sizes, which was good -- I tried popping one into one of the cage pendants I bought, and the pearl was too large (which is the fault of the pendant, not the pearl), but the other two fit. They're slightly oblong, but mostly round... I'm sure they're seeded, but they're not shaped like a grain of rice, more like an egg or rounder. As far as I could tell the pearls were in the meat of the oyster and didn't seem like they were placed in there. If you think about it, pearls have to be made in an oyster... it's a lot more work to grow them in and then harvest them from one oyster, and open that oyseter up, take the pearl out, open up another oytser and stick the pearl inside. That seems stupid. I can't comment on if some of these are dyed because I haven't found a black or purple one (realistically they probably are), but I didn't notice any color abnormalities in the pink one I found. It seemed natural to me, and even if it wasn't it'd probably fool the average person.

These oysters are NOT like what you get in a restaurant (heavy and thick-shelled), they're about 1 - 2 inches in diameter and the shells are paper thin. Of course you're only deaing with the shell for a second and then you throw it out, but it does seem a little "cheap". I found the easiest way to open them was to snap them in half in my hands... I tried to pry one open and the shell shattered anyways. They do come in a little juice which is a little gross, but it's minimal and not terrible. It's not so much it spills out or anything. I will say that several of them came "cracked", which is not to say open, but just that the shell had been broken but was held together. You still get the surprise of opening it, but I'll be sure to give my family the unbroken ones.

I think for the price it's important to have realistic expectations, and recognize what is and isn't acceptable. I think the for price these are a GREAT bargain. I hope there's a black pearl in here, but even if there's not it's still a good price for a fun little activity and 10 real pearls.

For $4.00, I thought these to be a reasonable price. Lightweight, sharp, comfortable feeling scissors. Great for cutting felt. I dont often have a need for cutting material, but when I do have a project coming up I tend to buy a fresh set of scissors. Makes it all go easier when the scissors are freshly sharp. After that they are usually put into rotation as a regular pair of scissors. I use scissors for some need or another nearly every single day, so I keep several on hand and often dispersed through house.

I notice and appreciate that the blades are sharp straight through to the tips. Not all scissors have that amount of care put into them. Often it is just at the midpoint and toward the handle. With fabric scissors it can be assumed that the tips are needed in cutting ore precise lines. This has that.

I have several Dunwell portfolio folders, they hold up and can be relied on for performance. This just happens to be slightly larger for specialized needs (artwork, scrap booking, etc). The cover is thin, but durable. Open folder, not zip. The pages are just like any other presentation/protective plastic sleeves-- just a little larger than typical page size, at 11x14 in stead of 11x8

As with the others I have, there is no ability add more pages. The product description states this, so it shouldn't be a surprise. So know that you have 24 sleeves to work with. You can conceivably place 48 items all together in this portfolio.

"Size: 11x14 Album measures 26-1/4” (open) x15-1/4”. Each clear sleeve is 11-1/8” x 14-1/8", 24 bound (non-refillable) top-loading plastic sheet protectors display 48 viewable pages of 11 x 14 artwork."

Take note that these are smaller scissors at just 7". Around the size of hair cutting scissors but bulkier, and not as large as kitchen scissors. Just to give you an idea :)

Two is one and one is none. I like when scissors come in a pack of two. These work. They cut. My need for scissors is reaching hoarding level. I like having several pairs available to me at any time, placed all over. I use them for light duty stuff-- poking open a box, cutting tags, cutting the occasional piece of construction paper. You get the idea.

No, you wont be spatchcocking a turkey with these, but trimming an errant thread or cutting through plastic packaging? yes.

5 stars as they are as described and work just fine.

2pk of fresh, sharp scissors for less than $7- that's right up my alley. I use scissors daily- always for something. I seem to buy them regularly enough. I don't spend much as I haven't really noticed any major difference on more expensive varieties. I replace because sometimes they get dull, sometimes they fall apart, sometimes I just like having lots of scissors all over the house to be able to reach for :) I rely on them most for opening boxes, getting into products with safety seals, and for cutting hair away from the carpet roller brush on my vacuum. Some styles of scissors are even great for use in the kitchen. Occasionally for crafts. Ever make a DIY snuffle mat for a dog? having extra pairs of sharp scissors on hand will save some effort. These will be relegated to office supplies (basically all non food scissor needs).

Comfortable right handed grip. Nice and sharp (but they are brand new, so I expect them to be!)
8.27" x 1.1" x 3.15"

No issues so far. Will be back to update if I experience anything else worth sharing.

I use mine for skin care. I bought because I out grew the slightly smaller Everything Mary Deluxe Craft Storage Box https://www.amazon.com/Everything-Mary-Black-Deluxe-Storage/dp/B079C56QZZ/ref=pd_sbs_261_6/139-8379977-1856649?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=B079C56QZZ&pd_rd_r=0a753fcd-33ee-4581-9943-3fc4bff2f285&pd_rd_w=cXvXX&pd_rd_wg=YR3id&pf_rd_p=1c11b7ff-9ffb-4ba6-8036-be1b0afa79bb&pf_rd_r=NTJ2GHTEPNBDEEQC9K3H&psc=1&refRID=NTJ2GHTEPNBDEEQC9K3H

I have to say, I prefer my other one. If that came in a larger capacity size, but maintained the same style, I would buy it. This one is pretty nice too, just will take some getting used to as I just "moved into" it. There are features that I really like:

--large inside compartment has options! You can see in the pictures that there are 5 smaller compartments, basically one in each corner, with center being free. If you push back one of the corners, it forms to the main corner, opening up that space. You can do that with 1, 2, 3, or all 4 corners to create one big space, or just what you need. I took pics to show some of the options.

--outer pockets are for the most part deep enough and wide enough for various items. I like that the pockets don't take away from other space. Two of the pockets have a cover, velcro closure. There is one wide zippered pocket. Lots of spots to choose from.

--everything feels secure and sturdy.

--Likely easy to clean up given that it is a nylon material.

--attractive colors and design. I chose the pink floral. It has a very light pink with slightly darker pink with black and grays. Interior is white. Handles are black.

I think if I can manage to pare down my skin care goodies, I may return to my other storage container and use this for something else. I wont rate based on my preference of one over the other. This is a solid storage container that looks nice, and I am happy to have it. I had looked at other potential options out there, but I came back to the Everything Mary, as I knew I would like the look and quality. This storage tote is indeed a little bigger than my other storage box, so I have some wiggle room to add in more of my products, or take out a few items and use it to carry additional stuff.

See, like with the other one (that I reviewed as well), I sometimes use certain skin care during the day, some at night, and sometimes I apply it at my desk, sometimes in a bathroom, sometimes in bed, etc.. I dont want to have to remember where something is, so I figured I would just keep it all in one portable organizational tote box. Not only that, but if I were toting it up to bed, I wanted to be able to toss a couple other goodies in there (book, phone, magazine, etc). So some organizers were just one open space, some were totally soft sided (I need structure), some were too deep, some were not suited to several small bottles for products. I found Everything Mary, and have been pleased.

One last thing.. In terms of sizing for your project- she has many different caddies, totes, etc available. Check out the sizes offered and then try to figure out which will work best for your needs.

I did buy these on sale, as an impulse purchase after thinking about how I wanted to get back into my art. Some of the reviews have me concerned about the durability. I have used canvas boards before without issue, so I am curious how these will do. They look like boards I have used in the past. I usually use oil paints, but I think I will refrain from that and instead go with lighter paints or other non saturating mediums. These boards say for "light paint applications". The product description does not say that. I will put these through the paces and see how they respond. I will be back to update.

As for having something to paint/draw/craft on without worry over longer term durability, I think they are great. What a great pack to pick up for a group project (with kids, with friends and wine), for doodling on something other than paper, etc.

Awesome little patch. I love books and cats, so this was a little something I bought for myself to add to a bag that I carry every day. I decided not to iron on, but instead added a piece of velcro to it so I could just stick it on my bag that way. My bag happens to be a tactical bag with molle strips- so making use of the soft fuzzy side of what equate to velcro on those strips by adding the teeth side of velcro to the patch made it easy. It is adorable. It has held up well. Nice colors, looks exactly like it does in the product description.

I love love love this little organizer tote. It is all that I have been looking for as a carry-all for my skin care. I have two bathrooms, one up stairs and one dinky one down stairs. Sometimes I do my routine in one bathroom, sometimes in the other. The frustrating thing is that sometimes I am in one location and what I need is in another. I could get two of everything, but sometimes that is just not feasible. In addition to my skin care, I like to carry a few other do-dads back and forth with me. I had been using a bag to toss in my stuff- some skin care, some books of the moment, my phone, hair bands etc- but it just gets all jumbled together in a bag.

I began searching for a compartment style containment system. I knew I wanted a handle, rather than an open box. I knew I didn't want a bag nor anything with too deep of pockets etc. I considered a utensil holder, like the kind used outdoors- has a few sections and a handle. Each search got me closer and closer to what I wanted. Eventually I landed on this product. It didn't seem too big, it didn't seem too small. Looked like I could put what I need in there with room to spare. A structured container with several pockets could keep my stuff visible but in place, as well as extra room for larger items. The big padded handle would provide additional stability and comfort in carrying a loaded container.
Sure enough, it does all that. I am impressed with the amount of spots to store stuff. I love that I can slide a book in as well as my phone, and a variety of daily skin care needs as well as other goodies. I carry it upstairs to bed (and bring it to the bathroom), I bring it downstairs with me and use things out of it as I see fit.

As far as the measurements go, I don't know exactly how it is figured out to be 12"x6"x10". Not counting pockets, I measured from hard seam to hard seam. 10"X8"X5" (shortest height), or 7" at tallest height -- again, just measuring to the thicker, hard seam. Not including handle or pockets.
However, use of generous pockets definitely gives this some great carrying capacity.

I can't think of anything I don't like!

I bought these to use for homemade lotions and salves. I haven't gotten around to using them yet, but I did take a look at the containers. They look good, no dings or scratches. Each tin is wrapped in a thin film of plastic. The smaller size is wrapped in plastic then stored in the larger size, which is wrapped in plastic. Then all are nestled into a sturdy cardboard box; perfectly sized.

Update: I have been using them for anything and everything. I love grabbing one to store spare change in, office supplies, earrings, hair ties. I have made a foot lotion and a salve- both are near solid, all contents have stayed within the containers. The screw on lid is quite secure. Again, all of my containers are in perfect condition. I have been most pleased with these.

This kit is great if you are just starting out. It has everything you need BUT the container is way to small. Even if you are doing a small DP you are not going to have room for all of your drills. If you are doing items like keychains, it would be fine. Overall I like the kit but they need to offer larger containers that hold more drills. Shipping was SUPER FAST!

This design is really pretty and the symbols are easy to read. There wasn't a lot of trash in the drills, nor were they "dirty" (avid DP'ers will know what I mean) Great relaxing project and shipping was top notch!
This was a very nice diamond painting. The symbols were clear, very little trash in the drills, and the details were fine for the size. Shipping was fast.

The canvas is a nice thickness, which I appreciate because sometimes I am a little rough on my canvases because I have to move them. The drills come in bags (THANK YOU!) so you don't need to really "kit" it if you don't want to. I haven't seen any trash, oily or dirty drills and they are all pretty shiny. The picture is really cute and turns out nicely. I would like to buy another in a larger size for more details but overall I am really happy with this one and can't wait to gift it!

I am still working on my painting but wanted to give a first impression. I will update my review as I get further along. They drills are in very nice shape. No trash, they are not dirty, and they shine nicely. The symbols are very easy to read (a huge plus for me). In fact I haven't even needed a light box, just my little overhead lamp. The only issue I am seeing so far is that the glue has run over the outside of the painting. So it picks up fuzz and pet hair. This will be an easy fix when I frame but I wanted to mention it, so the people that don't frame theirs know that there is some excess glue. Overall I am really impressed and can't wait to finish it!

First of all let me just say this is one the EASIEST TO READ diamond paintings I have ever owned and I do a lot of them. It is just stunning. There was no "junk" drills and everything was laid out very nicely. Even the tools were top notch. I will be purchasing more DP's from this seller because they are awesome and I love them! Shipping was fast too!

This kit is more for smaller diamond paintings because you aren't going to be able to fit a lot of drills in them. But they are made really well and I love how they snap shut. Also, the supplies that came with it are made really well and those pens are just too cute. They are may absolute favorite and my hands don't hurt using them. Also, they came with a few that were angled and I fell in love with that but unfortunately they hurt my hands because they are the regular pens you get with all kits. The trays were really nice too. I am very please! Shipping was fast.

I was really disappointed with this kit. All of the items are cheaply made and the tools are too. Most of the beads were plastic, which is not stated in the listing. The little clippers they give you broke the first time I used them. The wire and string are okay, as well as the charms but all in all I don't think many jewelry makers would find anything useful in the kit. It would be good for kids to just play around with but not at the price point. Shipping was fast though. Hence the two stars.

It shipped fast! It is lightweight and has great lighting levels. I use it for diamond painting and I like that I can make it super bright for dark colors and lo-brightness for bright colors. It is also pretty easy on my eyes. The price was perfect and it is definitely a good buy!

I wanted this for my smaller diamond paintings and it was a great value! The little containers hold a lot of diamonds. I also like the pocket because I can put my pen, wax, and tweezers there. I just wish it zipped up. Also, I wish there were more labels, but all in all I would definitely get more!
