This is a heavy duty glue gun and worth the price. I use glue guns a lot and most get to hot to handle after a short period of time. I can use this one for a long time and it doesn't get hot. Definitely worth it!

I bought these for adult coloring. I am pretty novice and just do it to relax so I don't need professional pencils. I really expected these to be better. The sharpening is a dull sharp, which is terrible for tiny spots. I had to sharpen them quite a bit while coloring. Some of the writing was rubbed off and that is just unprofessional. The tin was cheaply made with broken english. Also, the colors were okay but it needed more tones and at least one or two skin tones. Overall I was very unhappy with them.

Based on the reviews I wasn't sure what to expect but I like them. I love to do the fancy lettering and these did the job. I am not an artist, just someone that likes to pretend they are creative :) I thought they were a good deal and would purchase them again!

These were a little smaller than I was expecting but all in all I like them. I got them to store my "diamonds" for my diamond paintings. I will say if you are getting them for that, keep in mind if you have a lot of one color you will need to use 2 containers to fit them all (I actually had to use 3 for one color) so if you do the large paintings, you will need to buy more than one. Also, the lids are iffy. I had one that almost broke while I was opening it. I really love the fact I can take it with me when I go to visit my parents :) Everything stays in place!

I was actually pleasantly surprised with these. They have a lot of sparkle and are easy to affix. A lot of the ones I have purchased in the past have had the glue flow out on the sides but these didn't. That earned a lot of points. I use these for my fairy junk journals and love them. Would definitely purchase again!

I love this. It was so much fun to make the earrings. It took a little trial and error to get them looking nice, but once I figured it out, it was pretty easy. The colors are gorgeous and I am so in love with the glitter ones. It was a really nice craft project for me and my daughter. We are going to make sets for Christmas gifts!

I am an avid diamond painter and usually do huge ones. But I wanted some that were just small and simple so I could take a break from my "masterpieces". These are really nice. The canvas is sturdy, instructions are kinda clear, and the "diamonds" are labled well. Also, the symbols on the grid are easy to read, a huge plus for me.

I love how these are brightly colored. It makes seeing everything a lot easier. I do recommend a lightpad so you can see the symbols clearly. I always turn my canvas over and remove it from the plastic in sections. This gets rid of creases. As for the plastic, I remove that and use a piece of parchment paper. All in all a great product and will update with pics once I get them started!

I find these blades to be a lot easier to use than the Silhouette 4 Auto Blade. I was always getting different cuts with that one. Even when just minutes before it cut perfect. These are easy to set and my vinyl and card stock are cut perfect each time. Also, I like how you can "sharpen" these with a tiny a bit of aluminum foil. It will give you at least 3 or 4 more uses before having to switch blades. Plus the price can't be beat! In my opinion it is over all the best blade for the silhouette machines!

I bought this as a fun little project when I wanted to take a break from my huge Diamond Paintings:
Pros:
All the drills were there and there were several extras
It was super easy to do. The symbols were easy to read and it only took me about 1 1/2 hours from start to finish
It looks really pretty at night.

Cons:
You need to be very careful when taking it a part to complete. I actually pulled a wire out when taking it apart to add the drills. Thankfully my brother knows electric and was able to re-attach the wire but if it hadn't been for him I wouldn't have known what to do. So remove it gently.
The clear diamond drills were several different shades and a lot of them were super dull. So I had to pick through to find the shiny clear ones. It wasn't hard but I probably would have finished faster if it wasn't for that. Quality control was really lax because I found shards of plastic and some debris in the bags.

All in all, I really enjoyed it and will buy again. The cons were not enough to not consider making these for gifts. Plus you can't beat the price!
Gets super sticky when you get farther into the roll. You will really have to burnish good to get it removed from the vinyl. I am on the search for a better product.

~~~Now on to some secrets~~~
With all my projects I use liquid gesso first no matter what. Trust me get some cheap gesso it will save you some time and a few headaches. It's just an acrylic primer I got a 16 oz bottle on Amazon for around 10$. I've had it for months and only used maybe a quarter so far. Some are clear some have a white base. I suggest the white base saves you some more time.
I also see some people leave reviews saying the paint was coming up. I get the problem sometimes it's because I get impatient. Sometimes I don't let the first coat dry completely or it's already drying and I go over it again. I really don't know how to just leave it alone and walk away lol
If you want matte paint you can mix in some plaster of Paris. Also DecoArt makes something called chalk finish medium to mix on with your paint.

❤I really hope this helps someone❤

I would recommend this product!

[[VIDEOID:614fe8ff790d431d74bb0e0cb945c615]] Our 9 year daughter enjoys making creative figurines with this Polymer Clay Starter Kit; providing a lot of fun imagination. This kit comes with a nice plastic case to store the 62 polymer clay colors, tools and accessories.  We found that the polymer clay to have a soft texture and easy to mold with the sculpting tools. We used a toaster oven to bake the polymer clay and our daughter applies clear nail polish to give her creations a glossy look. This starter kit includes an user manual, baking instructions and example ideas. To provide some visual, our daughter created a video and uploaded her latest creations.

Our 9 year daughter enjoys making creative figurines with this Polymer Clay Starter Kit; providing a lot of fun imagination. From our perspective, this air dry clay provides value with regards to product price, quantity and quality.  The 50 colors of the modeling clay provides a lot of fun imagination.  In practicing social distancing and keeping a safe space from the COVID pandemic, our daughter utilizes Messenger Kids to interact with her friends to sharing their creativity in making their air dry clay animals. We added a few examples of her air dry clay animals.  In addition, we had a positive experience with the CiaraQ support team (Kelly) as she was very responsive to our inquiries.  Thank you for the quality product and customer service.

As described by other reviews, this product is best for creating solid crafts. It's pretty firm so I had to help the 9 year olds soften it. As a comparison, they're use to softening their own sculptey clay. Because we have to make solid objects, this product doesn't really stretch too far. I did try to make THICK leaves but it began cracking within minutes of completion. Wetting this to smooth out cracks or imprints you don't want makes this messy. Accidentally, getting a dried piece wet before painting it makes it messy. Don't leave the base unsealed. It's just not a forgiving product. Oh, it also took 2.5 days for objects the size of a slightly large golf ball and objects smaller than a pack of cheese to fully dry. So, map out your activities well.

I was looking for a non-oven bake option to use with my clients. This is not it. But, for home use, this might be ok.

I'll update after the holidays. I purchased this device because I know an elder with sciatica. She was a great seamstress before but developed difficulty standing too long. So, I'm hopeful, these electronic shears will help her return to her passion and become a happier person.

I did test the tool and one of the blades. I was happy that it came charged. But, I'll have to update you on how long the charge lasts. It cut through several layers of cotton no problem quickly and didn't seem heavy to me. It is a loud lil bugger but hopefully not too annoying for the elder.

Once the elder has used it, I'll update yawl with pics. I did not try to cut metal or cardboard yet.

The canvas is good and accepted acrylic well. The easel is handy but not super functional. It is fine as a drying station and to display paintings but I found that as we painted, the canvas continually fell off the easel. So, we painted on the table and used the easel to prop the paintings as they dried.

Good luck

They're good enough. I'd like the pack to have a couple of wider brushes and maybe a fan versus so many similarly sized small head brushes. It might be nice to have a couple of angled brushes. It would be nice to have a sponge brush or 2 as well. However, these are soft and handy for small paintings. The brushes sizes become a bit tedious for larger paintings though.

Overall, they get the job done. But, I'll need to get some other brushes to truly suit my needs.

I'm definitely going to try and get a refund. This was very watery and took 8 hours just to get kind of tacky. It was dry by the next day though. I can't tell if the nozzle or the thinness of the paint is the problem but it was very difficult to create smooth even lines and uniform dots. Also, with thicker paint, I've been able to somewhat push paint in the right direction a bit when the paint skips while creating a design. Nope, not this paint. It was very inconsistent and perhaps too many air bubbles. Also, the bottles aren't very full because I did a lot of squeezing on a thin plastic bottle just to get a little bit out. It was a frustrating mess for me. Good luck...

I would like for there to be a better variety in brush width. I think it's a little underwhelming for even a beginner painting on anything larger than 3x3 inch piece of paper. I hate wasting money but I definitely gave these away to my clients. I don't care to get these back once we're done.

I will say that the bristles are fine - not hard and scratchy. The paint palettes are fine.

I've zero intentions on getting these again.

I think this is a great product for beginners to learn about how to use water colors. It might be great for advanced artists if looking for practice paper. I'm a super novice; so, you'll have to defer to a pro's opinion on that. For Art Therapy, this suits me just fine. I will order more when I'm low on paper.

The deliver came as a ream of paper in a plastic sheet. There were no bent pages or awful smell. The paper is a thick weight. I'm showing SAMPLES using paint on both the flat side and the waffled side of the paper.

Even with lighter applications of water, the paper did curl and buckle. I will press the paper to see if that helps with flattening them afterwards but I'm not "pressed" about that right now. Anywho, there was bleeding on heavier water applications.

Disclaimer: I loosened acrylic paint with water and pearlizer. This may or may not have impacted the outcome. Also note: make sure your paper cutter is sharp before cutting this or it will fray or rip.

Good luck!

The rolls come as shown: small spools. The tape is u fortunately thin. I bought it to help secure wire on a tissue paper flower attached to a pen. For a quick social project or simple arts and crafts, this tape is fine. However, if you want something that'll last the test of time or to use in making something you'll sell, you'll do better to use something thicker and tackier.

Good luck

This is not richly pigmented construction paper. This is bright, extremely thin, regular paper. Lightweight is an extreme overstatement of what this vendor is providing. Notebook paper is thicker than this. I wanted an easily foldable construction paper and got darn near toilet paper thin garbage. I could have gotten regular colored paper from the dollar store for a better quality paper.

I love these acrylics. My 5 year old niece and I have doodled quite a bit to test the paint. The colors are very easy to squeeze into paint dishes. The colors are very pigmented. I had them a year before I tried them and there's no clumping. The colors are easy to mix. Sadly, I (& not the child) got paint on a rug and table. Those blue strokes are relentless and don't want to come out; so, make sure you protect your floors and tables. Clean brushes immidiately or throw them away.

I would definitely recommend this to others.

NOTE: This isn't listed as a child friendly product. The smell was not toxic and I am with my niece throughout each art session. She was pretty responsible. What little paint got on our hands easily washed off.

All I need now is a sealable container I can put excess paint in when I over poor in the paint dish.

I bought these to write capital and lowercase letters on one side of the chip and write numbers up to 100 on the other side for the kiddos to practice matching and organizing. I also bought this out of curiosity, as an alternative to Aves Apoxie. I thought it would be a similar product but aside from the fact that you combine equal amounts of part A and part B, that's where the similarities to Apoxie end.

The texture is actually kinda satisfying.... instead of being stiff and firm and more difficult to condition like Apoxie, both parts have a texture that is more similar to a marshmallow. When you mix them, the texture remains like a marshmallow. Another review mentioned crayola air dry clay- and before it dries, the texture is pretty similar!

Like Apoxie, this product does feel a bit sticky when you first mix it, but if you let it sit for about 30 minutes, the stickiness gets much less problematic and it seems easier to work and make details.

Another thing I didn't realize at first, is that it's recommended to wear gloves while working with this stuff. Saw that on the product website after I initially made a little test sculpture. I didn't wear gloves at all, and a lot of it stuck to my fingers and then the ball of clay kept sticking to where the product was on my fingers. I had to use rubbing alcohol to get it off and came back to sculpt it with clean hands and it was much better. Having a damp paper towel or a cup of water handy nearby is also a good idea- some of my sculpting tools had a tendency to stick to this stuff and I found that wetting my tools before using them helped quite a lot. And using damp fingers helped it not stick to my hands as much.

While the texture of the product reminded me of crayola air dry clay before it cured, when i woke up this morning i was pleased to discover that my test piece had cured to a surprisingly hard and solid feeling finish. This product is much lighter in weight than Apoxie which initially made me worry that the cured product wouldn't be very durable - but this does not appear to be a problem! I am actually excited now that I see how it cures- I can think of many potential project ideas where the lighter weight will be a big advantage! I like to make mixed media acrylic paintings on canvas; in the past I have used Apoxie to add a 3D sculpture effect to a painting. My favorite one is a painting/sculpture of a snake that wraps around the edges of the canvas and on the flat areas on the canvas, I made it look like that part of the snake is under water, like he's chilling out in a lil puddle. It looks awesome but the heavier weight of the Apoxie made it a bit awkward to get it hung up on the wall without it being crooked! I honestly can't wait to try another canvas piece with this product and hopefully avoid the weight issue!

I'm starting to think there's a learning curve with this product especially for people who are used to working with Apoxie or polymer clay. The texture and working style is quite different- I haven't had a chance to work with it beyond making a small simple sculpture. This review is just my initial impression as someone who knew nothing about this product before i bought it.

I would highly suggest that anyone else considering purchasing this, or if you already have some and you're finding out that no, this isn't just an off-brand Apoxie, it's an entirely different medium....do yourself a favor and visit the manufacturer's website before you start a project!!- You'll find info for this product and all the other items they carry, plus Blogs with tips and stuff. It sounds like the company mostly caters to epoxy/resin artists, and people who make tumblers and pens and whatnot. Not my area of experience but I was excited to find so much information on their website. I've been curious to try working with UV resin for a while, and based on the initial impression of the company (very positive!) I will definitely be buying some UV resin directly from them.

Shipping was fast, packaged well, AND they even threw in a free gift of some cute sparkly yellow glass stones...I use small tumbled rocks frequently to accent my sculptures, never realized these tiny glass stones existed 🤣 and I was delighted that they threw them in for free- not many companies do that anymore! So overall I am highly impressed with the company itself, and I think the only "problem" with the product is that I just don't know how to work with it yet. That's the ONLY reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5....maybe there should be a disclaimer that "hey, polymer clay/apoxie people, this isn't generic Apoxie, adjust your expectations" 🤣....if I eventually figure out how to make this stuff work with me, I'll come back and give an update! 🤣

[[VIDEOID:d981e69550c49d5df1e2b36a47c07b5a]] Hotep, Hotep brothers and sisters and thank you for looking at my review of the Dovov Tufting Gun, Cut Pile Rug Tufting Gun, Electric Carpet Weaving and Flocking Machine, 100-240V, Handheld Knitting Rug Gun Machine. 7-19mm Pile High. Tufting is a type of textile manufacturing in which a thread is inserted on a primary base. It is an ancient technique for making warm garments, especially mittens. After the knitting is done, short U-shaped loops of extra yarn are introduced through the fabric from the outside so that their ends point inwards (e.g., towards the hand inside the mitten).

Usually, the tuft yarns form a regular array of "dots" on the outside, sometimes in a contrasting color (e.g., white on red). On the inside, the tuft yarns may be tied for security, although they need not be. The ends of the tuft yarns are then frayed, so that they will subsequently felt, creating a dense, insulating layer within the knitted garment.

Tufting was first developed by carpet manufacturers in Dalton, Georgia. A tufted piece is completed in three steps: tufting, gluing, then backing and finishing. When tufting, the work is completed from the backside of the finished piece. A loop-pile machine sends yarn through the primary backing and leaves the loops uncut. A cut-pile machine produces plush or shaggy carpet by cutting the yarn as it comes through to the front of the piece. Tufted rugs can be made with colored yarn to create a design, or plain yarn can be tufted and then dyed in a separate process.

A tufting gun is a tool commonly used to automate the tufting process, more specifically in the realm of rug making. The yarn is fed through a hollow needle, that penetrates the stretched cloth backing for a modifiable length.

They can usually create two types of rugs, a cut or loop pile. A cut pile rug's yarn is snipped every other loop into the backing, creating a “U” shape from the side profile, while a loop pile rug isn't snipped and creates a continuous “M” or “W”. Tufting guns are useful tools for both mass production and home use due to its flexibility in scale and color variation.

Materials

Tufting cloth stretched on frame
Tufting requires the use of specialized primary backing fabric, which is often composed of woven polypropylene. Primary backing fabric is produced with a range of densities and weaving styles, allowing for use with different gauges of needles. Primary backing fabric must be stretched tightly to the frame so that it is stable enough to withstand the pressure of the tufting gun and taught enough for the yarn to be held in place. Tufting frames are generally constructed of wood, with carpet tacks or grippers around the edge to hold the primary backing fabric in place. Eye hooks are an important addition to a tufting frame, they are used as yarn feeders and work to keep the tension consistent. The frame must be sturdy and can be either freestanding or clamped to a tabletop. It is important to keep pressure and speed consistent when tufting so that the amount of yarn per square inch of the fabric is consistent.[5] Any mistakes in the design can be corrected throughout the tufting process by simply pulling out yarn strands from the primary backing fabric and re-tufting the area. Designs can drawn directly on to the primary backing fabric, this can be done freehand or with the aid of a projector.

After tufting is completed, the tufted piece requires a coat of latex glue on the back to keep the tufts anchored in their place. Latex glue is beneficial for tufted pieces as it provides flexibility and dimensional stability. The piece should remain stretched on the frame until the glue has finished drying to avoid loss of shape and the possibility of mildew. A secondary backing layer is then applied, providing further dimensional stability and protection for the finished piece as well as improving its appearance. A wide variety of materials can be used for the secondary backing fabric depending on the intended use of the piece. Felt, canvas, drill, and other harder wearing materials can be used for floor rugs, however backing fabric for wall hangings need only be aesthetic, as it is only required to cover up the glue layer and does not need to be hard wearing. • . Long boards can already clear the bottom of the engraving machines. Watch my video to see what I mean. But keep in mind, I'm a newbie to this world. This is just my newbie brain thinking out loud. Also, in the video, I forgot to suggest using the risers from the R3 without the roller, and without these towers, this way you can gain even more clearance. You'll understand what I mean after watching the video.

Overall, my un-scientific rating is 4-Stars. Not giving 5 because my gut tells me these towers are pointless, but I'm not going to give 3 or lower either because I'm a newbie, I could be wrong. These towers may have a purpose that my newbie brain don't understand yet. So 4-Stars.

[[VIDEOID:44fd1487b92345c424db5f9f5877461f]] THE GOOD:
--------------
* Superb build quality.
* Beautiful teal/aqua rail extrusions compared to the usual silver or black ones.
* There is a power switch on the main board, something the Atomstack A5 did not have.
* Laser module already shrouded by an acrylic green enclosure, thus, protecting your eyes.
* Though the beam is covered, they still included a pair of goggles.
* Easy 100% method to set the focal length. The laser focus has to be 20mm from the surface of the material being engraved. A 20 mm aluminum spacer/column is included to make the job a breeze. Watch my video.
* Ergonomically hex wrench included, and it's the only one you need! All bolts used in assembling this machine are M5 bolts. Why is this even noteworthy? Because when I put together the Atomstack A5, I believe I went through 4 or 5 different hex wrenches. At least 4, no exaggeration.
*The Atomstack A5 Pro and the Sculpfun S6 Pro seem to be direct competitors. In response to rival machines having onboard power switches, but since they can't redesign their main board, the Atomstack A5 Pro now includes a power cord with a switch. There are YT videos comparing the two directly that you should check out. Ones I've seen favor the Sculpfun S6 Pro.

THE BAD:
-----------
* A minor gripe. When it comes to tightening down the belts via the T-nuts, the instructions are convoluted. They want you to place the T-nut onto the rail from the top down, and parallel to the rail. Then they want you to tighten the bolt so tight that any further clockwise threading will then shift the entire nut perpendicularly to the rail. I tried it their way to no avail. The Atomstack's instructions were smarter when it comes to the t-nuts. They had you slide in the T-nuts from the side of the rails before you assemble the unit. So that's what I did, I had to unscrew the rails and slid the T-nuts in then re-threaded the bolts again. No big deal. Watch my video to see what I mean. BTW, if doing it my way, be sure to go ahead and tighten down the belt of one end first. This way, when you tighten the opposite end, you can tug without fear of the belt moving on the other end. It's a PITA if it moves because it's hard to get the belt back under the T-nut. So once you slide the T-Nut in and on top of the belt, tighten it down.
* Nobody talks about fumes.... It is bad for you. Do your cutting with your garage doors open, or take the machine outside.

Despite the little gripes about the installation process. This machine is 5-Stars!

THE BAD:

* Nothing at all. This design is a game changer. My most recent Go-To glue gun is the Ryobi one, but comparing this to that is night and day. The Ryobi is huge compared to this gun. This is more proper for arts and crafts, but I use it in the workshop too. 5-Stars!

We are rookies at heat pressing. Here are some noteworthy facts we've learned:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
* The transfer process for light vs dark is completely different. For light, the art you create is mirrored when you print it, and when you lay it on your shirt, you lay it face up. The dark version requires you to lay it face down, then tissue paper on top. The tissue paper is provided.
* It's a little confusing trying to find the design template at Avery. Here's how you do it. You go to their website, and you scroll all the way down to the bottom. Under the TEMPLATES section, don't click on "Find a Template." Instead, you need to click on "Avery Design & Print." Then click on "Start Designing." Then enter the template number 2233 for this product. If you use the "Find a Template" link, it will not be able to find this 2233 product.
* The template is a circular 7.5 inch design.
* If you want a transparent look, you need to match the background color as best you can to the color of the t-shirt. Or, you cut out the background by hand or by a cutting machine, which I actually do not recommend for these dark transfer paper. I made a logo of just words. I did not want a circular background on my shirt. Even if you choose black as your background to match your black shirt, when it comes out, it'll never match, so you will see the outline of your circular background. So I cut most of the background around the words as best I could. But I could not get the transfer to stick to the shirt. I'm using a heat press machine too, not an iron. It just wouldn't stick. On the second attempt, I left the background, and this time it worked. I guess the more volume, meaning the more transfer paper the heat press can contact, the better the chance of transfer. Then again, it might work if you use an iron where you can pinpoint and add pressure to the logo. This is just my experience, cut-outs for dark transfers do not work well with heat press machines.
* I've uploaded the instructions so you can prepare ahead of time.
* These paper work very well, and our prints look great. We're not even using a dedicated photo printer. We have those all in one office printers, and the art is still coming out fine. 5-Stars!

We are rookies at heat pressing. Here are some noteworthy facts we've learned:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
* The transfer process for light vs dark is completely different. For light, the art you create is mirrored when you print it, and when you lay it on your shirt, you lay it face up. The dark version requires you to lay it face down.
* It's a little confusing trying to find the design template at Avery. Here's how you do it. You go to their website, and you scroll all the way down to the bottom. Under the TEMPLATES section, don't click on "Find a Template." Instead, you need to click on "Avery Design & Print." Then click on "Start Designing." Then enter the template number 2237 for this product. If you use the "Find a Template" link, it will not be able to find this 2237 product.
* The template is a circular 7.5 inch design.
* There is no such thing as transparent background unless you cut out the background by hand or by a cutting machine. When I was making a design in Photoshop, I made the background transparent thinking the background won't be printed. I was confusing printers with heat press machines. LOL! The background or parts that you leave blank, your iron or your heat press machine will press on the color white, which is the color of the transfer paper. I had a light cream shirt, and I thought only the parts with my art and color would be pressed on because I had made the background transparent. But what I got was my art on top of a 7.5 inch circular white background. I was confusing my heat-press machine for a printer. LOL! So if you want a transparent look. You need to match the BG color in your photoshop with the color of your shirt. Otherwise, the BG will be white. Or, cut out the white background. Like I said, we're rookies. LOL! I've uploaded the instructions so you can prepare ahead of time.
* These paper work very well, and our prints look great. We're not even using a dedicated photo printer. We have those all in one office printers, and the art is still coming out fine. 5-Stars!

I've always loved coloring and the look of watercolors but have stuck to my ole' reliable ink pens but thought I'd give watercolor pencils a try - and LOVE it! I bought a coloring book and a pack of coloring tarot cards this past Friday and decided to try these watercolors on the tarot cards since they're sturdier material and the results are just perfect. I've been using the watercolor pencils to outline the area I want to color in then breaking out the water and paintbrush and pulling the color into the area and they're turning out great!
Admittedly I would have never gotten brave enough to actually try watercolors as a paint but this... this is more up my alley and a lot of fun!

Yes, that is my huge complaint about scissors... they're made for right handed people and do not sit comfortably in my hand, but with these scissors, there's no finger holes to work your fingers into so it's pick them up, snip and go!  They're great!  My only issue is they are very sharp (pointy) so you have to be sure to replace the plastic cover after you're done using them.  They also do not close like a normal pair of scissors, they remain open and ready to cut unless you set the lock device on them or put the cover on.  Personally I love them for when I'm cross stitching as I can pick them up snip a thread and go about working on the next color or another section, but I can see where the fact that they don't automatically stay closed would be an issue for some people.

can I just say how much I love this embroidery hoop???  I suffer from arthritis which was making holding the hoop to cross stitch difficult - to top that off I recently had an accident which resulted in a partial loss of one of my fingers so I was so scared my cross stitching days were at an end.  In a last ditch effort to save the hobby that I love I ordered this self supporting hoop and couldn't be happier.  This hoop is everything I could have hoped for and even with my one finger in gauze and self adhesive wrapping I'm able to continue my favorite hobby.  I do have to admit I almost didn't order because there's an additional shipping charge and I really hate paying for shipping but well worth the extra cost :)  One of the best purchases I've made in a while!

super cute train set!  I've always wanted a train set to put under the Christmas tree so was thrilled when I saw this train set on the Amazon Vine program.  My son and I immediately set up the set on the table and played with it for a good hour (even turning the dial all the way up to see if we could derail it).  My son's big concern with putting it under the Christmas tree is that it's electric and he thought if the tree's branch hit the track it would catch fire so I convinced him to plug it in and I'd touch the track... NOTHING. LOL  Seriously, it's a simple and cute train set - perfect for the holidays :)

I'm so impressed with the Brother PE525 Embroidery Machine. I'm a long time cross stitcher/embroiderer and have always done all my sewing by hand (even when I've made my boys baby blankets (some 18+ years ago) it was all done by hand, I honestly sucked at using a sewing machine! but I've watched displays of embroidery machines at the state fair and wondered if I would be able to do it to? The answer is "yes I can!"

First off, this machine is able to wind the thread onto the bobbins for you (I thought that was just amazing). After you know what design you want to do (whether you buy one or use one of the built in ones) it's a matter of getting the right thread in there with the correct tension and pretty much the machine does the rest. There are a ton of beautiful designs out there I found by googling 'embroidery machine designs' and even found some Christmas ornaments I want to make BUT had to order a larger hoop since 4 x 4 is really too small for most of what I like. So far I've played a little with the built in designs and with a little trial and error they turned out great. I am now waiting on the larger hoops and metallic thread to arrive so that I can attempt a larger scale project... I'll let you know how that turns out

This is a really good set. 6 molds and 3 different types of flakes to put in your epoxy or however you choose. The molds are thick and demold very well.

This came with everything I needed to make 2 complete 3 tier holders, I just added the epoxy to the molds. The pieces fit the epoxy well when unmolded. The pieces are a little smaller than I thought they would be but still looked good when done.

I can't believe how easy this was to use! You pour half in one container, half in the other, mix together and pour over the piece you're molding. So easy.

This is my process. Cut the bottom off a disposable cup. Lay packing tape on the table, stickey side up. Put your up top down onto the tape. Press down. Take got glue and glue all around the cup sealing between the cup and tape so none of the mold comes out before it's set. Ones that's done, take the piece you are going to mold, put it in the middle of the cup, press hard onto the tape so none of the mold gets under it. Once that's done pour your mixed mold ingredients into the cup. After four hours your mold is ready to be used.

I would not recommend for straw topper molds. It is really really stiff when done.. you can cut down the side and rubber band it back together when using though.

I absolutely love this and can't wait to create more pieces with it. If you are thinking about starting mold making, I highly recommend this product.

This package come with a large pink silicone mat and two smaller blue silicone mats. These work well at keeping my surface clean but are hard themselves to clean. Epoxy comes off but it takes a little to get it to. These are a little on the thinner side.

These arrived quickly and were packed really well. It came with 100 wood ornaments and two types of string to hang them with.  These would be really good for any type of crafting project but I was using it for sublimation and it worked absolutely perfect. They are thick but lightweight. If you were to stain them or paint them, I'm sure the color would hold just as well. These worked perfect for what I needed them for and definitely recommend.

Perfect for resin work. I used these in different types of molds and with the 3 in this set, I can almost always find one to help accent my project. They work well with resin and shine when the light hits them. They are a little messy so I don't recommend opening them in the house the first time.

I've never worked with flowers before but with branching out and trying new things, this was on my to do list. Upon receiving them I was really impressed with how much you get and the variety that comes in it. They are packaged incredibly well. The plastic pouches they come are a pretty thick and protect them during shipping and storage. The first one I opened was lavender and I was so impressed with how well preserved it was and the beautiful purple color. I started making different types of bracelets and coasters with different types and couldn't wait to unmold them. One of my biggest suggestions is to make sure you cut them down to fit whatever project you're doing.  The possibilities are endless with this.

[[VIDEOID:c97b5cb7690a072b7d52a5f3dcc31bfc]] From the moment ordered these, I couldn't wait to get them in. It comes with everything you need to complete one entire set,  screws(with anchors), string and the mold. I added epoxy, glitter, and mica powders to it. The molds poured evenly and the epoxy popped out very easily. I have a meditation area that this is absolutely perfect for. Great product.

[[VIDEOID:56fa195fb4f310f2809d251e4ae7eb3a]] This product arrived quickly and I was so excited to try it. It has little to no smell which is a huge plus when working with epoxy. When I started mixing part A and B together, I couldn't believe how fast it went it mixed together and became clear. It looked almost like water. Its also very very very thin so if you're using this on tumblers, you'll have to apply several layers to get the job done. I've used it to make beautiful coasters, tumblers and straw toppers and it always cleans up well. One of the only difference between this other other named Epoxy is this takes a little longer to dry and it's super thin. Overall, I'm impressed with the product.

[[VIDEOID:54694192a2125d834596f94ef9795d6b]] I have a silhouette cameo4 that I use these with and they are 100% compatible. They come 3 to a pack and are 12x12. They also come with protective sheets to help store them when not in use. I've been using them with htv and regular vinyl and they come off so easy when you need them to. I highly recommend these to any vinyl user.
This product is outstanding! I am a leather worker and was easily able to cut through some fairly thick leather with no issues. They came really sharp and ready to go out of the box.

When the package arrived, some of the molds were deformed a little but letting them sit, they return to their correct shape. The first coaster I made turned out absolutely beautiful. I love how thick the material is and how well it demolds. Having 4 in a pack is perfect to make a whole set at once or you can make one for each individual cup. Add flowers, charms, alcohol inks, glitters, etc.. the possibilities are endless with what you can create with these coaster molds.  These would be great gifts to make for birthdays or holidays.

Overall, it's compact and works for smaller cups which for me, is not something I do a lot of.

Edit 10/11: It started to make noise after the first use. We put a little coconut oil where it was coming from and it's quiet again. Still love this cup turner!

I love this cup turner. It's a beautiful pink color and my favorite part, it's so quiet. It was also so easy to put together. The box comes with a Paint Brush Cleaner. It is a bowl with raised sections all around it and in the bottom. It has a section that attaches to the bowl to raise the paint and brushes. There is another section that attaches to the top for a pallet and there are little slots to hold brushes. Also included is a drawstring bag for storage.

I will be recommending this Paint Brush Cleaner to family and friends.

This Drawing Pencil Set is nice. This set would make a great beginner set. It has everything a beginner would want to start drawing.

The graphite pencils go from a 5H to an 8B. The charcoal pencils are hard, medium, and soft. There are 120 colored pencils. Also included is a coloring book, a sketch pad with three colors of paper, white, tan, and black, white charcoal pencils, markers, blending stumps, an eraser, sharpener, sandpaper, and a drawing tutorial. All of this comes in a nice, zippered case.

I will be recommending this Drawing Pencil Set to family and friends.

This White Cardstock is nice. It cuts well and is the perfect size for cutting designs. I also like the size of the cards for creating small boxes.

The pack comes with 100 sheets of white cardstock. They are 110 lb. card stock making them heavy and perfect for invitations or for small decorations for the holidays. These sheets could also be used for small note cards.

I will be recommending this White Cardstock to family and friends.

This Craft Mat is nice. I needed a mat for the many different crafts I do. This one seemed like the perfect fit. I did use it when I was pouring resin into some molds. Clean-up was fast and easy.

The resealable bag comes with a good size mat. It has a cup for water when painting that is removable to empty and clean. It has places for paint and paintbrushes. It also comes with a sponge.

I will be recommending this Craft Mat to family and friends.

The box comes with 1 gallon of resin and 1 gallon of hardener. There are also gloves, stir sticks, cups, an apron, mica powder, foil flakes, and more. Instructions are also included.

I will be recommending this Crystal Clear Epoxy Resin to family and friends.

There is also a base for the mixer to sit on and two little holes for the paddles. Also included in the box are the paddles, stir sticks, pipettes, cups, and gloves. Instructions are included.

The box comes in 42 colors. Each marker has two ends a brush tip and a fine tip The colors are vibrant and flow smoothly from the markers. These markers are water-based and dry quickly.

I will be recommending these Dual Tip Acrylic Markers to family and friends.

This Epoxy Mixer is nice. I liked how well it mixed the epoxy. I did have a little trouble inserting the paddles, but I was able to get them inserted.

The box comes with a mix with a USB cord. The mixer has a button on the back of the to turn the mixer on. There are also 2 paddles. Instructions are included.

I will be recommending this Epoxy Mixer to family and friends.

This Silicone Mold Making Kit is nice. I love making my own molds. This rubber silicone worked great.

The box comes with two 8.82-ounce bottles of silicon rubber. It also includes 4 stir sticks and a pair of gloves. Instructions for preparing the pour are not included, but the mixing ratio is included.

I will be recommending this Silicone Mold Making Kit to family and friends.

This Acrylic Paint Set is nice. I painted one of the paint-by-numbers paintings. I was disappointed that there was not enough paint in the set to finish the painting. I did mix some paint so I could finish the painting. Nothing frustrates me more than not having the right amount of paint in a paint-by-numbers painting.

The box comes with 2 paint-by-numbers kits. Each kit comes with a framed canvas, paint, and a colored picture of the painting. There is also a pad of 10 sheets of acrylic painting paper, 6- 7.75 inches X 9.75 inches canvas panels, an apron, a mat, 2 pallet knives, and a paint pallet. Also, included is a tabletop easel, a pack of 10 paint brushes, a color wheel, and 24 tubes of acrylic paint.

I will be recommending this Acrylic Paint Set to family and friends even though there was not enough paint to complete the paint-by-numbers kit.

This Paint by Number for Adults Framed Canvas is nice. I started with the irises, and I love how the painting looks. There was more than enough paint to finish the painting.

The kit comes with 2 paint-by-number sets. Each set has a framed 11.8 inches by 11.8 inches stretched canvas brushes, paint, a printed pattern, and wall mounts. One complete kit is for irises and one complete set is for roses. There is also a small, finished color picture of each of the flower sets.

I will be recommending this Paint by Number for Adults Framed Canvas to family and friends.

These Silicone Measuring Cups for Epoxy Resin is nice. I like the silicone cups better than the plastic ones that come with some resin kits. I also like the stirring tool.

The box comes with 1 250 mL cup and 4 100mL cups. It also contains mixing cups, stir sticks, gloves, and pipettes. It also includes a stirring tool.

I will be recommending these Silicone Measuring Cups for Epoxy Resin to family and friends.

These Fat Quarters Fabric Bundles are so pretty. I love the different patterns of each square. I may be making miniature pillows, pillowcases, and quilts for my young cousin.

The pack comes with 8 pieces of beautiful pieces. Each piece is 19.6 inches by 15.7 inches, with most of the pieces being floral patterns. Also included are 8 white buttons.

I have recommended these Fat Quarters Fabric Bundles to family and friends.

These Foam Sheets are nice. I had an idea to use them on a card I am still in the designing phase, but I do think it will work. I am making the card for a young cousin, and I hope it will work. The foam is just the right thickness for so many projects. I will be working on many designs with these.

The pack comes with 96 pieces of foam sheets that are 8.5 inches by 11 inches. The pieces come in a variety of colors, red, orange, white, purple, pink, and more. The pack also comes with 4 sets of googly eyes.

I will be recommending these Foam Sheets to family and friends.

These Adhesive Glue Dots are great. I thought they would be a lot smaller, but I am pleased with the size. I am impressed with how sticky it is. The small sample I did makes me think I will be ordering more of these.

The box comes with 10 rolls of 100 dots. The dots are double-sided, and they are very sticky. The dots are thin and clear. They can be easily removed if put in the wrong place, but they don’t stick as well after removing them.

I will be recommending these Adhesive Glue Dots to family and friends.

This Colored Cardstock Paper Cutter Bundle is nice. I love the angle cut feature of the cutter. I also love all the colors of cardstock.

The box comes with a pack of 72 sheets of cardstock in assorted colors. There are several shades of some colors like green, blue, and pink. It also has black, white, and cream just to name a few. The measurements on the cutter are in inches and centimeters. It has an extendable arm for cutting more than 13 inches. It has angle lines for 45-to-90-degree cuts. Instructions for the cutter are on the back of the packaging.

I will be recommending this Colored Cardstock Paper Cutter Bundle to family and friends.

These Double Sided Adhesive Sheets are nice. I did a sample cutting it out on my Maker. I was pleased with the result. So far, it is still sticking to the pieces and the card.

The pack comes with 20 sheets of double-sided adhesive. Each sheet is 8.3 inches by 11.5 inches. Also included are cutting instructions for a cutting machine. It also has a QR code to watch a video for tumblers.

I will be recommending this Double Sided Adhesive Sheets to family and friends if my card stays together.

This Crystal Clear Epoxy Resin Kit is nice. I used the dark pink pigment, and it was very pretty. I make small pieces to add to my cards and this epoxy worked great.

The box comes with a 10-ounce bottle of hardener and a 10-ounce bottle of resin. It also includes 12 resealable baggies of pigment, gloves, a scraper, two plastic stir sticks, and two measuring cups. Instructions are included.

I will be recommending this Crystal Clear Epoxy Resin Kit to family and friends.

This Polymer Clay Press is cute. I like working with polymer clay but with the small rollers, I could never get it flat enough. This press solves that problem, it works great for me.

The box comes with the press and handle. There is also a clamp to hold it firmly to a table. Instructions are also included in the box.

I will be recommending this Polymer Clay Press to family and friends.

These Artist Filbert Paint Brushes look nice. I have been wanting filbert brushes for a while and was excited to get these. I used them on a small test painting but will be using them later this week on a bigger painting. I love them so far.

The pack comes with 13 brushes. They have beautiful wooden handles; the sizes are on them. The sizes are size 0 to 24 even numbers. The brushes are made of nylon hair.

I will be recommending these Artist Filbert Paint Brushes to family and friends as long as they are as good after several uses as they were on my test painting.

This LET'S RESIN Epoxy Resin Starter Kit is great. I used it to make some snowflakes and some keychains. It mixed up great and cured perfectly.

The box comes with two 22 ounces bottles of epoxy resin. One bottle is marked with “A” and the other with “B”. The mixture is a 1:1 ratio. Also included measuring cups, pour cups, wooden sticks, plastic spoons, gloves, pigment powder, and more. Instructions not included.

I will be recommending this LET'S RESIN Epoxy Resin Starter Kit to family and friends.

This Silicone Mold Making Kit was a little difficult for me. I had several things picked to make a mold. I chose one that was a little bigger than the others. I created my “frame”, added the tape, and poured in the mixed solution. The solution started seeping from under the frame. I had watched a video and put down some clay, emptied the frame, set it on the clay, pressed down, and repoured. I lost 60ml in the first pour.

The box comes with two 20-ounce bottles of silicone rubber A and B. It also comes with gloves, stir sticks, and blocks. It also comes with 8 bottles of pigment and measuring cups. Instructions are on the box.

I will be recommending this Silicone Mold Making Kit to family and friends.

This 3D String Art Crafts Kit for Adults is great. I loved making this bird's nest. I could not stop. The only thing I did not like is the knots to start and end each color. I think if it started on a different color every time it would be as noticeable. There are enough supplies to complete two projects, another frame or board would be required.

The kit comes with a frame with holes in it. It also includes nails to put in the holes, string in 5 colors, stick-on numbers, a hammer, snips, a screwdriver, a clothespin, and things for hanging the finished craft. Instructions are included.

I will be recommending this 3D String Art Crafts Kit for Adults to family and friends.

This Arteza Paint by Number for Adults was great. I loved painting this picture. Once I started it was hard to stop. The picture comes out great. The best paint-by-numbers set I have tried so far.

The box comes with a printed canvas, and a printed piece of paper the same as the canvas. There is a beautiful, finished picture of the painting, 12 tubs of paint, and a pack of brushes. Also included are hanging accessories.

I have already recommended this Arteza Paint by Number for Adults to family and friends.

These Polymer Clay Cutters are nice. I love the different sizes and the earrings I made come look great, but I had to use my own earring components. I am planning to make more earrings with patterns.

The pack comes with a pack of 35 steel clay cutters. There are several different shapes including circles, hearts, diamonds, and more. There are also different sized of each. There is also a plastic container with a variety of circle cutters. They have different color plastic pieces for each size. I did not get the earring components.

I will not be recommending these Polymer Clay Cutters to family and friends because I didn’t receive the earring components.

These Canvas Pads for Painting are great. I love the thickness of the sheets. I also loved the way the paint looked when applied to the canvas sheet.

The pack comes with 2 pads of canvas sheets. Each pad has 10 triple-primed sheets. They are suitable for oil or acrylic paint. Each canvas sheet is 9 inches by 12 inches.

I will be recommending these Canvas Pads for Painting to family and friends

This Crystal-Clear Epoxy Resin worked great. I created several snowflakes with the mold. I think I am obsessed with making snowflakes. I used glow-in-the-dark pigment and they looked amazing.

The box comes with a 16-ounce bottle of epoxy resin and a 16-ounce bottle of hardener. It comes with a snowflake mold, large measuring cups, gloves, pipettes, spoons, glitter, and stir sticks. Instructions are also included.

I will be recommending this Crystal-Clear Epoxy Resin to family and friends.

This Polymer Clay Earring Making Kit is nice. I made a pair of flower earrings with colors inspired by my daffodils. I will be making more very soon. Mine come out great.

The box comes with everything but the oven to create beautiful earrings. The box includes 30 pieces of polymer clay in a variety of colors, a cutting mat, and a roller. Also included are 27 cutter molds, 14 sculpting tools, 20 earrings cards, 20 plastic bags, an acrylic circle, B7000 glue with a mini nozzle, 20 stud earrings with backs, 20 gold ear wires, 20 silver ear wires, 50 gold jumprings, 50 silver jumprings, and instructions.

I will be recommending this Polymer Clay Earring Making Kit to family and friends.

These Canvas Boards are nice. I used one to do a small oil painting. I am happy with the size and the way the paint flows. I was disappointed that the eBook address advertised on the front did not work.

The pack comes with 20 canvases. Each canvas is individually wrapped. They are all 8 inches by 10 inches. The canvases have been triple primed.

I will be recommending these Canvas Boards to family and friends.

These Red Suricata Curved Knit Blocking Combs are great. I do a lot of knitting and crochet that needs to be blocked. I have always used just the pins and it takes longer to get everything pinned. These were very helpful to me, and they worked with my blocking boards.

The box is a sturdy plastic which makes it easy to store the pins easily when not in use. Inside the box are 5 different sizes of combs from a 2 pin to a 10 pin. There are 10 of each size or 50 combs. Also included are 100 T-pins. Instructions are included in the box.

I will be recommending these Red Suricata Curved Knit Blocking Combs to family and friends.

These Arteza Hand Lettering Pens are fun. I was just going to do one thing to test the pens. I ended up doing almost a whole sheet of paper just playing with the different tips.

The box comes with 9 pens. Each pen is different ranging from a fine nib to a bullet nib. They also come in different colors black, gray, and white (gel pen). Also included in the box is a pencil and kneadable eraser. A vague lettering guide is also included.

I will be recommending this Arteza Hand Lettering Pens to family and friends.

This Glow in The Dark Powder is great. I think I might be obsessed with it. I was going to use one mold to try it. It come out great and I ended up making several more things. It works and looks great.

The bag comes with 12 containers of powder. Each container is 4-ounces, and they have a lid and another cover under the lid. There is also a spoon and a UV light included.

I will be recommending this Glow in The Dark Powder to family and friends.

This Crystal-Clear Epoxy Resin worked great. I created several snowflakes with the mold. I think I am obsessed with making snowflakes. I used glitter and I used pigment, and both looked great.

The box comes with an 8-ounce bottle of epoxy resin and an 8-ounce bottle of hardener. It comes with a snowflake mold, pipettes, spoons, gloves, glitter, measuring cups, and stir sticks. Instructions are also included.

I will be recommending this Crystal-Clear Epoxy Resin to family and friends.

This Colored Cardstock worked great for me. I made two cards and the different colors in this pack were just what I needed. I only had to find one color but usually, I am looking for more than that.

The box comes with 90 sheets of double-sided, 65-pound cardstock. Each sheet is 8.5 inches by 11 inches in 8 different colors. There is a web address on the box, but it does not have cardstock!

I will be recommending this Colored Cardstock to family and friends.

This Beginners Embroidery Stitch Practice kit is great. I love that this is something that you can do now and then use as a reference later. I have just gotten started on the first one and I am loving it.

The pack comes with three pieces of material with patterns stamped on them. There is also floss for all three projects and three packs of needles with three needles in each pack. Also included are a seam ripper, a needle threader, a pair of scissors, and a hoop. An instruction sheet is also included. It has a section for setting the hoop, separating the floss, and using the needle threader. Also on the sheet is a stitch guide.

I will be recommending this Beginners Embroidery Stitch Practice kit to family and friends.

These Fabric Paints are great. I wish I had them before I made my little cousin some miniature pieces for her dollhouse. I will have to make her some other things with this paint.

The box comes with a set of 60 colors. There are 32 – 1-ounce bottles of beautiful colored paint. Also 8 – 1-ounce bottles of glitter paint, 8- 1-ounce bottles of neon paint, 10- 1-ounce bottles of metallic paint, and 2- 1-ounce bottles of glow-in-the-dark paint. The box also includes 5 stencils and 3 brushes.

I will be recommending these Fabric Paints to family and friends.

This Fabric Paint For Clothes is great. My little cousin got a dollhouse for her birthday and wanted some pillows. I had some white material, and I created my own stencils for her favorite things. I used this paint to paint the material and to paint on the stencils. They are looking great. I am also going to make her a comforter for her dolls. I love how well the paint looks.

The box comes in 40 beautiful colors. Each color comes in a 2.0 fluid ounce bottle. Also included in the box are some sticker stencils and brushes.

I have already recommended this Fabric Paint For Clothes to family and friends.

The Embroidery Kit for Beginners is great. I just recently started to do embroidery again and I am loving it. I started the set with the red flower kit. So far it is looking pretty good.

The bag comes with 4 complete embroidery kits. Each kit has material with a pattern stamped on it, the thread needed to complete the project, and 3 needles. The bag also has 2 embroidery hoops, a small pair of scissors, a needle threader, and a seam ripper. Also included are instructions for the stitches, how to set up the hoop, separating the floss (thread), and how to use the needle threader.

I will be recommending this Embroidery Kit for Beginners to family and friends.

These Sphere Silicone Molds for Resin have been fun for me. I tried the smallest one as a test. I was able to get the resin out of the mold with a little effort. I have plans to make a few different things with these molds and resin.

The pack comes with 3 spheres. The sizes are from a 0.9-inch sphere to a 4.3-inch sphere. They have a flat section to ensure that it doesn’t roll while filling and curing. The top part stretches to remove the ball on the inside after it has cured. Instructions are not provided.

I will be recommending these Sphere Silicone Molds for Resin to family and friends.

These Arteza Resin Keychain Molds and Accessories are great. I had one before and misplaced it so I was happy to get this one with some things that were not in my other kit. I made two keyrings and will be making more later. I haven’t used the other molds yet but plan to soon.

The pack comes with a flat piece mold that has the alphabet and numbers. It also has a square, hexagon, and circle mold. Also included are a bag of accessories, keychains, pipettes, a drill, and finger tabs.

I will be recommending these Arteza Resin Keychain Molds and Accessories to family and friends.

This Diamond Painting Sealer was frustrating for me. I have used similar sealers, and this is the only one that pulled up my drills. I finally switched to a different brand just to finish sealing my painting.

The box comes with a 4-ounce bottle of sealer. The top of the bottle has a hole in the center for the sealer to come out. The top is also a sponge to help with spreading the sealer.

I will not be recommending this Diamond Painting Sealer to family and friends.

This LED Tracing Light Pad is nice. I got it for diamond painting. The darker colors are harder to see and this light pad helps me see bead placement.

The box comes with a light pad and USB cord. The light pad has three adjustable brightness levels. A cloth for cleaning and instructions are also included.

I will be recommending this LED Tracing Light Pad to family and friends.

This Epoxy Resin Kit is the best I’ve tried so far. For my test, I made some puzzle pieces, some circular pieces, some teardrop pieces, and a triangular piece. I plan to do a little wirework with the triangular piece, and I will be making earrings out of the others. The resin mixed easily and cured completely; most I have used didn’t cure as well.

The box comes with a tray of jewelry molds and a pack of stand-alone molds in different shapes. It also with a 3.4 fluid-ounce bottle of epoxy resin and a 1.7 fluid ounce bottle of epoxy hardener. There are three 10ml bottles of colorant in blue ice, burgundy, and banana yellow. Also included are a box of mica powder, glitter, screws, flowers, and necklace cord. Instructions, pipettes, stir sticks, and mixing cups are also included.

I will be recommending this Epoxy Resin Kit to family and friends.

This Diamond Painting Sealer is not for me. The glue looks horrible on my diamond painting. It looks like school glue. I am not impressed by the thickness or the look of this product.

The box comes with a 4-ounce bottle of sealer. There is a sponge with a hole in the center for spreading the sealer. This product is supposed to give a shine effect.

I will not be recommending this Diamond Painting Sealer to family and friends.

This Light Grip Cutting Mat did great. I had a big project to do, and I used one of the mats. I was able to get all my project cut using one mat. I think I could have cut more because it was still sticky unlike some others, I have used.

The pack comes with three mats. All three of the mats are light grip. Also included in the pack is a plastic scraper. The adhesive is sticky, and it lasts through many cuts.

I will be recommending this Light Grip Cutting Mat to family and friends.

This Colored Cardstock is great. I am always needing a piece of colored cardstock. This pack has 16 beautiful colors.

The pack comes with 80 double-sided sheets of cardstock. There are 16 colors of 65-pound sheets in the pack. Each sheet can be ripped out but the rest of them stay in the pack.

I will be recommending this Colored Cardstock to family and friends.

This Epoxy Resin Clear Crystal Coating Kit works well. I used this kit to create crystals for jewelry. I was happy with the way most of them turned out. The ones that did not were my error, not the product.

The box comes with a 150ml bottle of resin and a 150ml bottle of hardener. It also comes with a plastic drop cloth, stir sticks, gloves, glitter, coloring, and pour cups. Instructions are also included.

I will be recommending this Epoxy Resin Clear Crystal Coating Kit to family and friends.

These Crystal Molds for Resin are cute. I used all the smaller ones 15 of them. I was able to get the pieces out easily. I was not however able to get all the resin out of the mold.

The pack comes with 19 molds. They come in several different sizes. These can be used as pendants by drilling a hole in them or putting an eye pin in the center just before it hardens. Wirework is another option for creating jewelry with these crystals.

I will be recommending these Crystal Molds for Resin to family and friends.

These Silicone Resin Molds are nice. I tried the sphere first. I did have a little trouble getting the epoxy resin out of the mold. I love all the different shapes.

The pack comes with 8 molds. There is the sphere that I used, a pyramid, a cube, a rectangle, a ball, an oblong piece, and a coaster set, with the holder and the coasters. Instructions are also included.

I will be recommending these Silicone Resin Molds to family and friends.

This Diamond Painting Conserver is nice. The glue is a little thin but seems to do a good job. I used it on a few diamond paintings, and it covers well. I don’t know if it peels or turns yellow because I just finished applying the glue. Dries pretty quickly.

The box comes with a 4-ounce bottle of glue. The top has a sponge with a hole. The hole allows the glue to come out and the sponge gives it a smooth application.

I will be recommending this Diamond Painting Conserver to family and friends.

This Diamond Painting Sealer is nice. I used it to seal several diamond paintings. I am very happy with this product. This glue is thick and covers well. I don't know if it will peel or turn yellow because I just finished applying the glue.

The pack comes with two boxes. Each box comes with a 4-ounce bottle of glue. The top is a sponge with a hole in the center for the glue to come out of the bottle. The sponge spreads the glue evenly over the project.

I will be recommending this Diamond Painting Sealer to family and friends.

This Epoxy Resin worked well for me. I used it to make a small pair of earrings using some silicone molds and this epoxy resin. I think they are pretty good for an amateur. I didn’t get bubbles, but I don’t know if this was because my item was so small or if the epoxy is just that good. I will be ordering some bigger mold; I have already started looking and will hopefully be making some larger projects soon.

The box has two 16-ounce bottles of epoxy. One bottle is resin the other bottle is hardener. This was easy to mix, and I didn’t have bubbles. There are instructions included on the back of the box.

I will be recommending this Epoxy Resin to family and friends.

These Christmas Fragrance Oil smell great. I don't really feel like these are fragrances I would pick for Christmas but they do smell good. My favorite is Christmas Wishes. I will put some in a diffuser but I am also going to try it in candles.

The box comes with three bottles. Each bottle has a different fragrance, Christmas Wishes, Secret Garden, and Pink Forest. The box does not come with anything saying how much oil to use in things like soap or candles.

I will be recommending this Christmas Fragrance Oil to family and friends even though these are not the scents I associate with Christmas.

These Dual Tips Colored Pens are not as good as I expected. The image doesn't get enough ink so it is not dark enough. I didn't even use the other end of the pen.

The pack comes in 8 colors. Each pen has a different design on one end and a fine pen on the other end. The designs are 2 colors in the wavy lines, dot-dash line, 2 colors in the dashed line, slanted line, and flowers and hearts.

I will not be recommending these Dual Tips Colored Pens to family and friends.

These Mandala Stencils are great. I got them to use as backgrounds for handmade cards. They worked perfectly.

The pack comes with two packs of 16 stencils. They are well-cut. The stencils were easy to use and the center sections were not as flimsy as some others I have bought.

I will be recommending these Mandala Stencils to family and friends.

This UV Resin Kit for Beginners didn't have all the molds listed. I wanted it mainly for the "orb" mold and the large circle mold. I only got the bottom half of the "orb" mold. There wasn't anything to mix the resin and the dye. The UV light wasn't big enough for the larger projects and it didn't stay on long enough.

This box comes with UV Resin, dye, glitter, and molds. I didn't get all of the molds listed above. It also comes with spoons, stir sticks, and pipettes. There are some jewelry findings included and a too-small UV light.

I will not be recommending this UV Resin Kit for Beginners to family and friends.

This CaptainCrafts New DIY Oil Painting Paint by Numbers was not stretched well. There is no way to tighten the frame so one side of the painting will sink in a little messing up the entire painting. This is very disappointing.

The kit comes with a framed canvas, although it is a bad job. There are 23 tubs of paint and a set of brushes. It also comes with a printed version of the painting to see the numbers if needed and some hooks to hang the painting.

I will not be recommending this CaptainCrafts New DIY Oil Painting Paint by Numbers to family and friends due to the poor quality of the stretched canvas on the frame.

This SINGCHO Permanent Vinyl is really nice. I got it to make cards and decals. So far it is sticking great but if that changes I will come back and revise my review. I don't know how easy it will be to remove because I did not want to remove my project.

The pack includes 58 pieces for vinyl. It comes in many different colors. It does not come with transfer tape.

I will be recommending this SINGCHO Permanent Vinyl to family and friends.

These Polycarbonate Sheets are great. I got them for mixing ink to blend. I will also be using one or two for holiday displays.

The sheets are bendable. They have a plastic removable piece to protect them from scratching. I am really happy with them.

I will be recommending these Polycarbonate Sheets to family and friends.

This Cosmic Travel-Size Coloring Book is a lot of fun. I used gel pens and the ink did not seep through to the backside of the paper. I wanted to use something that I thought might seep through. I chose the gel pens and I love the glitter used in some places in the picture above.

The book comes with 20 pages. It has something to color on one side only. The pages are perforated so they can be taken out of the book to frame and hang on the wall. Paint or ink will not seep through to the back of the paper or the other pages.

I will be recommending this Cosmic Travel-Size Coloring Book to family and friends.

This Aloha Super-Size Coloring Book was so much fun. I used metallic watercolor for the picture above. I used a lot of water and it did not seep through.

This book is large and so are the pictures to color. The pictures to color are only on one side of the page. There are 22 pages to color or paint.

I will be recommending this Aloha Super-Size Coloring Book to family and friends.

These 16 Pieces Geometric Stencils are great. I am going to use them for my handmade cards. I did a sample and it turned out great.

The stencils come with 16 different patterns. The wood pattern is not as sturdy as the others but with some work, I was able to use it and it looked great. The stencils are easy to clean but the ink did leave a stain.

I will be recommending these 16 Pieces Geometric Stencils to family and friends.

This Paradiso Super-Size Coloring Book is nice. I used acrylic markers to color my page. The markers are really wet but they did not seep through the paper.

This book is very large. The pictures are large too. The images are single-sided.

I will be recommending this Paradiso Super-Size Coloring Book to family and friends.

This Permanent Adhesive Vinyl is gorgeous. My small sample turned out great. It was easy to weed and it stuck to my book without any problem. If I have any problems with this vinyl when I do bigger projects I will revise my review.

This vinyl comes in 10 gorgeous colors. It also comes with 2 transfer sheets. The vinyl weeds easily and the adhesion was very good.

I will be recommending this Permanent Adhesive Vinyl to family and friends.

This Holographic Permanent Vinyl looks great. I cut a small sample just to see how it cut and to check how sticky it is. I was happy with my sample and will be using it more later this week. I have a request for a decal made from this vinyl and I will be doing that and more. If I have problems with larger projects I will revise my review.

The pack comes with 16 sheets. The sheets are 12 X 12 inches. There are surface recommendations on the front of the pack. This is a peel and stick vinyl.

I will be recommending this Holographic Permanent Vinyl to family and friends.

This IEHO 200g UV Resin Kit is not great and definitely not for beginners. I wanted to make a pair of earrings. I had in mind what I wanted. I added epoxy, glitter, and dye. I poured it into the mold, which was dirty like it had already been used, I turned on the light included in the kit. My earrings did not cure, not only did they not cure the dye ran out. I did make another pair without the ink and put them in the sun to cure.

The kit comes with a 7-ounce bottle of clear UV Resin, 12 small bottles of dye, 12 tubes of glitter, 3 small jars of foil pieces (I think it's foil, I didn't use it). It also comes with a mold, a mat, cups, sticks, UV light, and more.

I will not be recommending this IEHO 200g UV Resin Kit to family and friends.

These BIHRTC 10PCS Sublimation Keychain Blanks are not for beginners like me. I did not realize that I needed a sublimation paper to use these blanks. I have never even tried sublimation. I went to Amazon the page they were for sale. There is a step-by-step section on one of the pictures. Nowhere in the step-by-step directions, or in the description, or anywhere else did it say I needed the sublimation paper. very disappointed

The set comes with 10 different shaped, double-sided sublimation blanks, white loops, and keyrings. I will be getting some sublimation paper to be able to use these. I will edit when I get the paper and add it to the blanks.

I will not be recommending these BIHRTC 10PCS Sublimation Keychain Blanks to family and friends.

These Crafting Ink Blending Brushes are nice. I used them for my handmade cards. I used them to blend some colors of ink. I also used them to add ink using stencils. Both ways worked great.

The set comes in 14 different colors. Half of them were individually wrapped and the other half were not. The bristles are soft and the handle is comfortable to hold while blending.

I will be recommending these Crafting Ink Blending Brushes to family and friends.

This Diamond Painting Accessories and Tools Kit is great. I love the light pad, it helps see some of the darker areas that may be hard to see without it. I also love the enclosed clips which will keep your work in place.

The kit comes with a LED light pad, it is 33.5 X 13.18 inches. It has three brightness levels. It comes with a roller to press the drills after you have placed them on the canvas. There is also a zippered pouch that is filled with a case for holding drills with stickers to put on top of the lids. There are several diamond pens, tweezers, bags, a plastic spoon, glue clay, binder clips, and a fixing tool.

I will be recommending this Diamond Painting Accessories and Tools Kit to family and friends.

This Dyvicl Skin Tone Pencils Portrait Set is perfect for me. I love drawing and portraits are one of my favorites. I did a little sample and I am very happy with the results. I am eager to start a new portrait.

The pencils come in 12 colors. This makes it easy to do light skin tones and shadows all the way up to dark skin tones with shadows. The pencils go on smoothly with no scratchy feel. The colors look great together and on the sample I did they seemed to blend well.

I will be recommending this Dyvicl Skin Tone Pencils Portrait Set to family and friends.

These 10pcs 10mil Blank Mylar Stencil Sheets work okay. I think I need to do a little more thinking on my project. I did a small sample and I forgot that some pieces needed to stay for the stencil. I will try this again after I have worked through my problem.

The sheet was worked great, my project was my problem, not the mylar. The pack comes with 10 pieces12 X 12 inches sheets. The thickness of these sheets seems to be about the same as the sheets of ready-made stencils I have purchased.

I will be recommending these 10pcs 10mil Blank Mylar Stencil Sheets to family and friends.

This Acrylic Paint Set is beautiful. I used it on a sample and it was beautiful. I am getting cards ready to be painted and I will be using this paint as one of my paints for handmade cards. I love the vibrant colors included in this set.

The set comes in 18 colors and two of those 18 colors are metallic. Each color comes in a 4 oz pouch with a screw top. This is a heavy-body paint, it is acid-free and non-toxic.

I will be recommending this Acrylic Paint Set to family and friends.

These Dyvicl Premium Metallic Markers Pens look great. I got them for handmade Christmas cards. They look good on both white and color cardstock. The colors do look different but they will be perfect for my cards.

These pens come in two colors, silver and gold. There are 4 pens in the pack. The ink is metallic and water-based. They are fade and water-resistant and will be perfect for crafty projects outdoors.

I will be recommending these Dyvicl Premium Metallic Markers Pens to family and friends.

These Dyvicl Acrylic Paint Pens are gorgeous. I have been waiting on them so I could add the gorgeous colors to my handmade cards. I love how bold the color is on black cardstock.

These pens come in 12 water-based colors. They are very bold, especially on black cardstock. They are acrylic paint pigment ink. They are sun-proof and water-proof. Perfect for rock painting, wood, or anything that would be used outdoors.

I will be recommending these Dyvicl Acrylic Paint Pens to family and friends.

This Lartique Painting Kit is perfect for beginners or for people trying something different in their paintings. I used one of the smaller ones to work on painting roses. I plan to paint a few small ones until I get the roses the way I want them, then I will paint a larger picture.

The kit comes with four sizes of panels with six of each size. There are 10 paint brushes and 4 sponge brushes. Also included are 2 paint palettes, 2 sponges, and 1 color wheel. It comes with everything but the paint!

I will be recommending this Lartique Painting Kit to family and friends.

These 80 Permanent Adhesive Backed Vinyl Sheets will be perfect for me. I will be using them on handmade cards as well as handmade decals. The wide variety of colors is just what I needed to get the look from my head to the project. The sample decal I made come out perfect!

The set comes with 25 assorted colors, 4 metal colors, and 5 transfer sheets. There are several of one color so there is no need to worry about not having enough vinyl for a project. The set also includes a paper explaining what this vinyl can and cannot be used on. It also explains the size of the graphics you should and should not use.

I will be recommending these 80 Permanent Adhesive Backed Vinyl Sheets to family and friends.

These Arteza Stretched Canvases are great for small projects. I used mine to practice painting roses before attempting a larger painting. I think they will look great hanging on my wall with a few different flowers grouped together. The paint went on smoothly and covered well.
