This is a nice epoxy resin kit that is perfect for crafting or minor repairs. I used it to encase a Play-Doh figure I made with my daughter. The kit includes a resin and hardener as well as a few small measuring cups. It's been my experience that epoxy resin works the best when you mix it at an ambient temperature of around 75 degrees, so that's what I did with this product. It hardened in about 24 hours and I didn't have any trouble with bubbles. This is a wonderful product to help you preserve and enhance your crafts.

This is a very nice starter kit featuring 12 piece of stained glass. Each piece is 4 X 6 inches and features a different vibrant color. As you can see from the above picture, the glass comes very securely packaged. There is padding surrounding the glass on all sides, and the individual pieces are separated from each other by additional pieces of padding.

The kit also includes a set of lightweight gloves. The glass is thick enough to be relatively durable, but thin enough that it's easy to work with. This is a wonderful kit for getting started with stained glass.

This is an excellent sketch notebook for artists. The spiral binding is durable and makes it easy to turn the pages so that you can always work on a flat surface. There is a purple band along the edge of the notebook to hold it closed when you have finished working for the day. The front and back covers are very thick and provide ample protection for your drawings.

The pages have a good texture and each page features a perforated edge. The notebook also comes with a promotional card that contains instructions for sharing your work online for a chance to win additional notebooks. The 10 inch by 10 inch pages are a great size that provide you with plenty of space to let your creativity flourish. This would be a wonderful sketch notebook to consider for a gift.

I do a lot of watercolor paintings with my daughter. She helps me with the coloration and when that dries I add in details with ink pens. These pens have a wide variety of tip sizes and shapes which provide you with tremendous control over how ink is applied to  your artwork. The kit includes a range that starts at .003 which is perfect for fine details, and goes all the way up to a wide brush that is ideal for shading. The pens are easy to hold and are long lasting. This is a great set that will take your artwork to the next level.

I got this kit for my wife who also loves jigsaw puzzles. It has everything you need to create a fantastic image and does not require any artistic ability. All you have to do is place the colored plastic pieces on the sticky background. The kit includes an applicator that makes it very easy to maneuver and place the pieces. This is a very nice project for a lazy weekend, or if you want to relieve stress after work. It requires enough concentration so that you can forget about your day to day stresses. The results are also very pleasing to look at. This product would make a thoughtful gift.

My daughter is interested in crafting and we got her this handheld sewing machine so that she can make dresses for her dolls. It is a very nice device that is both portable and practical. The unit is battery powered and small enough to put in a drawer. It makes short work out of small tears which allows you to give new life to your clothing. This is a highly functional and practical item that will be of great service to any household. This is a great starter machine to try before you decide whether or not to invest in a larger sewing machine.

This is a pack of 16 very nice 8 inch by 10 inch canvases. The canvases are thick enough to be durable, and they have a good surface for blending and manipulating paint. I got these canvases for my two young daughters because they are amateur artists. They like to do rough versions of their paintings on regular sketch paper, and then do a final version on a canvas like this. These canvases make their projects seem more professional, and they take the work very seriously. I like that they have supplies like this to keep them away from getting lost in digital screens all day long. The 8 inch by 10 inch size is big enough to allow them to start an ambitious project, but small enough that they can manage it without getting frustrated. This is a very nice product.

I've always loved working with pastels, but now that I've tried oil pastels I'm never going back. These feel a little more slick to the touch and they don't make nearly as big a mess in terms of dust. It's a lot easier to control your colors and the final result looks absolutely spectacular.

This set comes with a wonderful range of colors and allows you to do a wide variety of terrific artwork on multiple different types of surfaces. I've been having fun using them on regular sketch paper. This is a great product and will help you unleash your creativity in vibrant color.

My girls use their hot glue gun for a lot of different crafts. They are constantly discovering new projects on YouTube and they go through a lot of hot glue sticks. I was very excited to get this 5 pound box of hot glue sticks to ensure they will have plenty of raw materials going forward. They are much longer than standard sticks, and that allows my kids to work for longer periods without interruption. The sticks fit their gun perfectly and are excellent for a variety of projects.

This is a very nice set of 4 high quality sketch books. Each book has a secure ring binding that sits flat on a surface when you begin to sketch. The back cover is very thick cardboard that provides good protection. The front cover is lightweight but still thicker than the individual pages. The pages have a perforated edge so you can easily remove your drawings from the book if you want. This would be a perfect notebook for making personalized cards or thank you notes. The pages are thick enough to avoid tearing when erasing, and provide a nice drawing surface.

I like to relax by making newsprint paintings. I have a trace board that allows me to transfer an image to newsprint, and then I color it in with these markers. The markers are of excellent quality and allow you to add vibrant color to any image. They have a thick end for extensive shading, and a fine point for detail work. The variety of colors allows you to do three dimensional shading in any color.

They come in a very nice box that works as a storage case. I always have a test pad before I apply a marker to my work in progress because sometimes the color on the cap doesn't quite match up to the color that's produced by the marker. This would be a great set of markers for coloring a comic book. You can use them to make some great works of art.

My daughters have become interested in making flip books, so I was excited to get this portable light pad. It has three brightness settings so you can work on it for a long time without wearing out your eyes. The surface is approximately 31 cm by 21 cm which creates a huge tracing surface. You can place a standard 8" X 10" sheet of office paper on this pad and have space on either side.

This product allows for a ton of creative applications. You can use it to create flip books by making your frame drawings in the context of the previous image. The back light allows for tremendous precision in your illustrating. This is also a nice product for tracing images in general. It's a helpful item for preparing a sheet of paper for a watercolor painting, a pen and ink drawing, or virtually any other artistic medium.

This pad works a lot better than putting a paper up against a window or other bright surface. It's easy to draw on the pad, and it holds up very well to regular use. The pad does not have a battery and needs to be connected to a USB cable in order to work, but that means you don't have to worry about the battery going dead to interrupt you while you're in the middle of a project. We use an extra long USB cable that makes this item very convenient.

This item is a great accessory to artistic expression. It's a very useful pad that will allow you take your creativity in new and exciting directions.

This kit is a nice way to relax. It occupies your mind and helps you alleviate stress. I'm old enough where I need to wear a pair of cheater eyeglasses to see what I'm doing, but my kids can work on it just fine.

The kit includes everything you need to create a vibrant image. The "diamonds" are little plastic pieces that come in a variety of colors. As you work, you'll find they catch the light in a beautiful way and create a truly dynamic image.

The canvas is thick and is labeled with both color and symbols to guide your work. It is coated with an adhesive and you put the colored pieces in place with the included stylus. There is also a tray that allows you to flip over the pieces as you're working so they are correctly oriented prior to application. The kit also includes several small plastic bags to store the remainders.

If you are a fan of jigsaw puzzles, you'll love doing this diamond painting.

My daughter saw one of these diamond paintings and wanted one for her birthday. She's delighted with this kit. It comes with a thick canvas with an adhesive on the front where the colored pieces are applied. The canvas is labeled both with colors and symbols so you put the pieces in the right place. Also included is a stylus, a plastic tray, several small zip lock bags, and the colored pieces to apply to the image. This kit has an especially bright and vibrant image that is relaxing to work on. If you are looking for a fun project that allows you to concentrate on something other than your daily stresses and concerns, this is the perfect kit for you.

I got this set for my kids who are off of school for summer vacation. The markers are about the same size as standard white board markers, and they fit into your hand comfortably for precise usage. The markers have a fine tip on one end and a wide tip on the other end so you have a lot of versatility in applying color. The enormous variety of color variations allows you to do very beautiful shading work. My kids have made some wonderful art projects since we received this set. I think this set would also work great for providing color to something like a comic book. It also comes with a convenient cloth case that helps keep all your markers together. Very nice product!

This is a great set of calligraphy pens that will allow you to pick up a fun, new hobby with a lot of applications. With the ongoing quarantine, I'm constantly on the lookout for new activities that can be enjoyed by the whole family. There are lots of videos and images available online that you can use to teach yourself calligraphy. In a very short amount of time, you can make really cool looking drawings. This set is great for artists who want a versatile set of pens, or for anyone who is curious about picking up a new skill. There is a great variety in pen tips provided with this kit, and the pens leave a smooth, black, uninterrupted line. Great product!

I've never done a diamond painting before so I was curious about this product. It consists of a template with a sticky surface that is covered by a protective layer of transparent plastic. You also receive a variety of colored gemstones that you apply to the template with a small tool. The stones are very small, about half the size of a grain of rice.

The template indicates which stone you need to apply, so you pour the stones into the included tray and begin to place them on the surface. My wife enjoys putting together puzzles for stress relief, and this project is similar to that. As you concentrate on finding and placing the stones, you lose yourself in the task and you get a welcome break from your daily cares or concerns. There is a tremendous sense of satisfaction as the picture takes shape.

There is only one tool included in the kit, but this would be a nice project to do with a partner. It's a relaxing project that yields a very beautiful final result.

Chemistry is a fascinating subject and your kids are never too young to start learning the basic concepts. This kit is perfect for helping older students to visualize complex chemical structures, and it's also ideal for providing younger students a chance to learn through play. Everybody knows that H2O represents water, but once you've built a representation of a water molecule you have a better understanding of the structure.

The kit comes with pieces that represent Hydrogen, Carbon, Oxygen, Nitrogen, Sulfur, Phosphorous, Fluorine, Chlorine, Bromine, Iodine, and Boron. There are also a variety of links that represent various chemical bonds. All the pieces fit into a plastic case, and there's even a hexagonal notebook for diagramming complex structures.

This is a terrific educational tool that allows students a hands-on learning experience. It's great for the home or the classroom.

This is a great set of fabric paint that allows you to express yourself in a new and fun medium. This is an ideal set for children who are looking for an activity for a birthday party or a rainy day. The set comes with 30 different color choices that include metallic, glitter, and even a tube of glow in the dark paint. The set also includes a variety of tips that you can use to change how the paint is applied. You can also push the paint around on the fabric with a brush. You need to let the paint dry for 24 hours after application and 72 hours before washing. If your kids have old clothing that has fallen out of their favor, give it new life by allowing your kids to decorate it with fabric paint. This is a really fun set that helps bring out your fashion sense as well as your artistic side.

You receive a lot of paint in this economically priced set as well as a palette tray and a paint brush. If you're new to painting this is a good starter kit that will give you a wide variety of colors. I wish they had included a couple extra tubes of black and white because those are usually the colors you run out of first, but this kit includes many shades that can be used for lightening and darkening. The paint is easy to control and the color does not change as it dries. I find painting with watercolors to be very relaxing and this is a great set for both beginning and expert artists.

I'll often relax with my kids by recreating 'Calvin and Hobbes' comics. We used to use watercolors, but sometimes that can be imprecise for fine detail. These watercolor pencils are fantastic. They give you absolute control and allow you to blend and shade with ease. I took a picture of a little sketch that took about a minute to do just to show how easily these pencils allow you to push around the color. I've just been dunking the end of the pencil in a glass of water, but I'm sure there are a variety of techniques you can use. If you have a pencil or pen and ink drawing that you want to highlight with precisely placed splashes of color, this watercolor pencil set is the perfect thing for the job.

These days, making slime is one of the most popular hobbies of all kids. My girls are 7 and 9 years old and if they could do nothing but sit around and make slime all day they would do so. This is a great kit that gives you glue in 6 different colors so you can make glow in the dark slime. I mixed up a batch with my daughter today and we found that you get the best results if you shine a light on the glue for a while to bring out its glow in the dark properties. If you leave the glue in a dark place prior to mixing, the luminescence will be dim. Note that this pack is glue only, it does not contain the activating liquid that turns the glue into slime. This is a great starter pack for hours of fun with your kids. This would be a perfect activity for a birthday party!

When did slime become the most popular thing in the world for young children? I have two girls aged 6 and 9 and they absolutely love to make slime. We've done it a dozen times but it never seemed to turn out exactly right. However, this activator worked perfectly, and it even glows in the dark as advertised.

Note that you have to mix the activator with regular Elmer's glue which is not included. It took about a third to half the bottle of this glow in the dark activator to make slime with 5 fluid ounces of glue. The girls were really excited about the slime and we took it into the closet so they could see how well it glowed in the dark.

Making slime with your kids is another fun activity that helps pry them away from computer and cell phone screens for a few minutes. It's a lot of fun!

I'm used to getting tiny jars so this bigger jar is a nice change! Mine was sealed but still had a bunch of the powder in the threads so when I opened it- it went all over my clothes and bed! SO save the box/ bag it came in and open it over that or over the trash! Other than that its great and glows beautifully under my blacklight! We've done a couple resin projects and mixed some of this in and the result is a my creations glowing nicely!! Lots of fun, I'll be buying the other colors now!

Such beautiful colors, and they lay down so opaque and blend so easily. Very creamy, and yet hard and so pigmented. I really love these pencils. The case is innovative and I really enjoyed it. Having all the trays out makes finding the color you want a breeze. Only bad thing about them is their price. Because they are an import from Japan, they cost far more here than they do in Japan. But if you love high quality products, I'm sure you'll love these.

Nice quality and soft wool. No hay bits and no bad mold or barn smells. The colors are all nice and match the pictures perfectly. I weighed the wool, and indeed did get eighty grams. These are a decent price, so if you like the colors, I recommend.

Incredible beautiful and expertly made. I am in love with this spindle. It has great weight and holds yarn so well. Plus, it is sanded and polished so smoothly, it never catches a fiber. It makes yarn making so much nicer to have a quality spindle. This is easily the best spindle I've used, and it's so unique and beautiful looking, it's sure to be treasured forever. I highly recommend this.

Really, love this set. All the metal was smooth, and without defect. Lots of cord lengths, and needle sizes. For the price, I expected these to be bad, but they were great. Only issue is the needles coming loose as you knit, but all brands do that, and they make products you can put on the screw threads that stop that.

I can see what they were going for, and it was close. But I wouldn't recommend this. For one, the instructions are badly translated, on top of using crochet terms I've never heard of before in my life. The print is so tiny, can barely make it out. The written instructions are unusable. It has a QR code to a video, but I can't use QR codes, so that's useless for me.

I thought at least I could use the yarn for something else, it is very soft, but it splits on me nearly every single stitch, I've never used any yarn as terrible as this, I don't even get how it does it. It doesn't come with the backers for the eyes, how are they supposed to stay in? Then add in, it's thirty bucks for a tiny bit of yarn and a QR code! No, as someone who is quite experienced in crochet, I really wouldn't recommend this to others.

The color is so nice and regal. I like the slight shimmer it has to it. The wax melts easily and the color was spot on to the pictures. It takes a stamp well, and stayed stuck to the paper after it cooled. You get a pretty decent amount as well, I believe you could make at least one hundred seals from this.

Very beautiful colors in this pallet. They don't list what pigments are in them, or their light fastness. They are opaque as well. I don't think these are premium quality, but are good for beginners. They were fun to play with, and I will enjoy using them 'til they are used up. They are easy to use, and lay down nicely. Great for practice or quick art you do just for fun. I'd recommend these for younger artists, but not anyone serious about becoming a professional artist.

Very beautiful colors in this pallet. They don't list what pigments are in them, or their light fastness. They are opaque as well. I don't think these are premium quality, but are good for beginners. They were fun to play with, and I will enjoy using them 'til they are used up. They are easy to use, and lay down nicely. Great for practice or quick art you do just for fun. I'd recommend these for younger artists, but not anyone serious about becoming a professional artist.

Very cute book. The cloth cover makes it feel fancy. The paper was real nice, and works so well with markers. The elastic band is not too tight or loose. I love it's handy size and look forward to filling it out completely over time.

Beautiful colors in a nice variety. I needed a large amount for some projects I'm making, and this jar is a huge amount compared to what I'm used to getting. The effect is awesome and everyone always loves projects made with mica powder.

Very beautiful glitter. The mix of all different sizes looks so good in both resin and craft projects. I like how many colors it comes in as well. This amount of glitter will last me a long time, so I consider it a decent value.

Very beautiful glitter. The mix of all different sizes looks so good in both resin and craft projects. I like how many colors it comes in as well. This amount of glitter will last me a long time, so I consider it a decent value.

A pretty cool kit that has pretty much everything you'll need. The yarns and soft and brightly colored. I like the informational booklet they provide as well. The instructions are good. The finished projects were fun to make and look good on the wall. There is enough yarn in the kit so long as you don't make any huge mistakes, which is kinda hard to do.

Absolutely amazing, almost magic bag. It had so many slots, zippers and pouches, I really didn't know why. But then I used every last one of them. It's just amazing how much it holds. Granted, it can't hold all my yarn, but nothing can. But it certainly does hold all my working yarn very nicely, plus yarn for my next few small projects. I have had a few bags, but never really liked any of them, because some had nice features, but not other features. This bag has everything I want, I couldn't have designed it better myself.
Very beautiful tape in the day, and awesome glow at night. Decent stick, and a good amount of tape on each roll. I have really enjoyed these and will absolutely recommend them.

A really great starter set. I would honestly recommend this to anyone that is about to start in a miniature craft. If you've been in it a while though, you will likely have most of what is already in it. Very high quality as I expect from Wargames. You'll get use out of everything in this box. The paints are mini, not regular sized btw. Nothing missing or defective in my box.

This hobby is the most expensive hobby I've ever seen in my life. The price is crazy high, but that just seems standard for the hobby sadly, so I won't take away any stars for that. But just keep in mind, this is the introductory sets price, and an introductory items price is usually low to draw you in, and this one is low counter to what you may think.
A really nice velvety fabric. Such a great value compared to what real velvet costs. The color is well saturated and stunning. It is indeed quite soft and has good stretch to it.

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. They come in a beautiful zipper case, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. They are sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes pencils in tons of colors. I have so many Kalour products, but this one is my favorite.

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. They come in a beautiful zipper case, and for the price, are very nice. Though, Kalour has a larger set that comes in a better looking zipper case, with more colors and a great price. I recommend getting that one, though this one is still a good set.

Very smooth lay down, and great blending. They are sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes pencils in tons of colors. I wasn't super impressed with the metallic pencils, but most pencil companies don't do great metallic.

On the case, if you are careful, you can easily remove the rubber square, and rub the holes it leaves behind, and they will disappear like 99% of the way. Looks so much better IMHO.

So many nice pastel colors, and for such a good price. They come in a beautiful tin, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. They are sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes pencils in tons of colors. My tin was so bashed up, it's useless unfortunately...

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. They come in a beautiful tin, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. They are sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes pencils in tons of colors. As well, the tin comes in a foam box inside a cardboard box, and the tin is flawless.

I really love blending pencils and use blenders all the time. It can get quite expensive, but I found these and they are only a sixth of the price of the name brand. Are they as good as name brand? Very nearly, but not quite, but you really have to be trying to notice, and for the savings, I'm 100% happy with these. Will be using them from now on. As for the burnisher, I've never had one of those before, it worked well, but burnishing isn't something I'm personally big on, but it does work and is nice.

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. They come in a beautiful tin, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. They work with water very nicely, leaving nothing behind if you don't press too hard on the paper. They are sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes pencils in tons of colors and the versatility of water color pencils.

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. I was worried these wouldn't be as nice as my other Kalour pencils, as I noticed these look different, and don't have the name Kalour on any of the colored pencils. It does however have the same quality, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. Sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes creamy pencils in tons of colors.

So many nice vibrant colors, and for such a good price. I was worried these wouldn't be as nice as my other Kalour pencils, as I noticed these look different, and don't have the name Kalour on any of the colored pencils or packaging. It does however have the same quality, and for the price, are very nice. Very smooth lay down, and great blending. Sturdy pencils that sharpen well. I haven't had any problem with any Kalour sets and they look so good in a coloring book. I really like these pencils and do recommend the set for anyone that likes creamy pencils in tons of colors.

I can not express how much I love the new changes to cross stitch. It used to be the thread wasn't sorted, and the fabric was blank, and although I loved cross stitching, I love it just so much more now. This was a great kit, it came with everything needed included, right down to the needles. Thread was sorted, beautiful and had plenty of it. Although the projects are large, they aren't any harder than smaller projects, they just take more time to do. The instructions are full color (Another thing the old kits didn't have, color.) and very large and well made. I am very much in love with the kits from this brand.

Extremely fun introduction to needle felting a flat picture. I have for years done 3D figures, but not anything like this, and it was very fun. It came with everything needed, and the wool didn't smell or have mold. I do wish the foam was the same size as the project being done, that would have been best. The needles were great, not a single one broke on me, but most needles if you use them correctly won't break, and they can last you just about forever.

A great product, I had been wanting something like this for a while, and finally someone made it. I wish it came in other colors, but what I got was really nice quality. The silicon was thick and sturdy, but still flexes when I want it to. It cleans so quickly and easily. Makes blending colors so easy, I do recommend.

The softest yarn I have ever touched. Very deep and shiny black color that is really beautiful. There is a lot in one skein and it's shockingly heavy for a skein of yarn. I think this would have instantly been my favorite yarn ever. However, all three of my skeins had really bad defects. The first skein had three huge, badly tied off knots. Whenever this yarn is cut, it just unravels horribly. The second skein had a huge knot, a snag that pulled out a foot of yarn, and a missed stitch in the yarn, that caused unraveling. I had to tie the yarn off to stop it spreading, as it wouldn't stop. The third Skein had a bad knot and another snag.

Now, since the yarn is chain spun, I have found I can just unravel the whole thing, and that gets rid of most of the problems, makes the yarn look better in the otherwise unfixable spots. But then I don't have the beautiful chunky yarn I was wanting. Still, I have usable yarn that is crazy soft and very well dyed. So I'll just have to make a thin blanket instead. For how much this costs, the amount of defects is not ok.

Very beautiful yarn with an oddly dense/firm feel. Looks good worked up and was a nice, easy yarn to work with. A couple of my skeins had knots, one didn't. Overall, a good yarn and I would recommend it. Keep in mind, it has little to no drape really, though using larger needles/hooks could help with that some.

The thinnest yarn I've seen really, the thread that holds the yarn together is a one, or maybe even lace weight yarn. It's super soft, and you get so much yardage with it. You can use it on it's own, wind a few of the skeins together to make it thicker or add it to another yarn as you stitch. However you use it, it'll look great and feel great. One skein had a knot, the second had two, the third had three and the fourth had no knots, which is decent overall. Very fuzzy and will look good as accents or plushies. Would recommend.

Very soft and beautiful with nice colors. I usually really love faux fur yarns. However, these skeins were in bad shape. One had four knots, a second had four knots, and the third had three knots. As well, two of the skeins were so badly wound up, it was a nightmare unwinding them to do anything with them, it took me all day. I think the machine that winds the skeins malfunctioned. For one of the most expensive yarns I've ever seen in my life, that is not ok. No reason for this price tag either. I would never recommend to a friend.

Very soft and a nice color. The balls are made of three strands of fur all spun together. That's all that I can find good about it. One skein had four large knots. The second skein had three knots, was in two separate pieces had several yards in one section that looked chewed up by a machine and couldn't be used and then another yard elsewhere that also was chewed up and ruined. The third skein had four knots, had many, many, many yards that were chewed up and unusable, just all over the skein, and a forth of it only had two of the three strands of fur, plus to top it off, it was very badly wrapped, unlike the other two. Whatever machine they use to make the skein messed up very badly on that one and I guess they didn't catch it.

Very soft, very beautiful yarn and anything made with this would be so nice. That's what I was thinking as I used my first skein up. One skein I got was perfect, but the other had several knots, and five different large patches where there was no fluff, just string. Some sections of good yarn were too short to be worked with, and had to be tossed out. The amount of joins I had to do was very ugly. The resulting project was not worth the time or money. This is expensive for what you can get. If you could get nothing but good skeins of yarn, then the yarn is great, but I only had 50/50 odds.

Super soft and very beautiful yarn. I love the deep dark teal they went with, and it's a color you don't find very often. One large knot per ball that I got. It was very nice to work with, but is very expensive. For a luxury project though, this is perfect.
Super soft and beautiful chunky yarn. Many knots in each ball though, and it is way too expensive for that. Otherwise, it's a perfect shade of merlot, and was very nice to work with.

This glitter is just gorgeous and fun to look at right out of the box! We've used this in a few resin pouring projects and are always happy with the results! Good variety of colors in this pack. Great deal too.

I got this to make little crystal ball decorations as even the cheaper glass crystal balls or photography spheres are all expensive no matter where you get them! So I got this and started pouring my own little crystal balls with the sphere molds! I've put mini flowers, mini skulls, etc into them and they all come out really cool! This was easy to use, even for a begginer.

Best quality cashmere I've ever had. It doesn't shed and hasn't pilled yet either. Wonderfully soft and such nice colors. But of course, as always, I wish there were more colors. This worked up beautifully, no problems at all. It's so soft, even those I know with very delicate skin don't find it scratchy at all. If you can afford it, or want something very special and luxury, then this is the yarn to go with.

One of the absolute best hand dyed yarns I've ever had. It is 100% worth every penny and I can't recommend it enough. Especially considering it's superwash merino. Every single color is absolutely stunning, and there are so many I have on my wish list, you just don't see these kinds of colors, or color combinations in store bought yarn. It is crazy soft, stretchy, smells great. Works up wonderfully. Everything about these, I love them.

Very beautiful, luxurious and wonderful. But also very expensive. I have loved working with this yarn so much, and I would love to work with more of it. It's colors are so nice, I wish I could get them all. But as is, I would only get this for a very special project. I very highly recommend this for it's quality but also it's look and feel.

The yarn comes in such fun and lovely colors. I also like having variety. The balls are smaller than they should be though. I weighed them all, and most are 22 to 24 grams, with only one being 25. That makes the average 23. As well, the yarn is as rough as craft yarns from the nineties were, and I could never wear anything made out of these. I don't think anyone would enjoy that. So these are what I consider, craft yarns. For the high price, despite the nice selection of lovely colors, it's a hard no for me.

Very typical cotton yarn, if you've used other cotton yarns, then you know what this is like. It's got nice colors, all pastel. It's soft for cotton that isn't mercerized. Works up well, and makes really nice pot holders. It's a little more cost than what I'm used to, but not by a lot, and it is nice yarn. It can split if you are not paying enough attention, but that's just because it's cotton, nothing to do about that.

Super duper soft and plush. It was a very nice yarn to work with, it didn't shed at all or break. One of the nicest yarns I've used and definitely the softest, I only wish it came in more colors so I could buy them all. Would be great as lining or edging, but entire items made out of this are just so wonderful. I can find no fault with the large ball of yarn and highly recommend it.

Very beautiful yarn. Because the cotton is mercerized, it's very soft for a cotton as well. The flat yarn gives a unique appearance on the roll, but that mostly goes away once it's worked up. The roll of yarn is as big as it looks in the pictures, but keep in mind, there is a cardboard tube on the inside, and there is a small amount of yarn on the roll. Each roll had only just enough yarn to make a pot holder. Though that depends on your gauge and stitch of course. As well, it took two months for me to get mine when I ordered them.

Very soft, beautiful yarn. It works up so wonderfully, I can't imagine anyone not liking anything made from this. It's not as tightly wound as other angora yarns I'm used to, so there is more shedding, and it's overall fuzzier. Super light yarn, and the colors are identical to what they showed in the pictures. I'm so happy with it.

Beautiful, soft, cotton blend with a fairly unique look. It worked up so nicely, frogs well despite just a little bit of fuzz. The fuzz is where the yarn gets it's color, and acrylic content from. Because the acrylic is in the fuzz, not fully incorporated into the yarn itself, it has no more stretch than a normal cotton. Like all fuzzy yarns, this is best not washed, and if you must, gently hand wash it without agitation. I really liked this yarn and would fully recommend.

I sell candles as a supplemental income and this melting pot makes my life a lot easier. I melt my wax, stir in my fragrance/dyes, and then pour it into my molds with the spout. Very easy to do and cleanup is easy too. Works flawlessly and seems well made.

Very easy to use, common sense got me through setting it up and now I have several beautiful cakes of yarn.  Though they also have tutorials online. At the end of projects I often have deflated skeins and they have been building up, but now they are all neat and organized, so quickly as well. The metal clamp that holds the swift to the desk, has a sharp bit of metal flashing on it that didn't get taken off, that would leave horrible gouges in my desk, but I simply put a piece of cardboard there, and that solves the problem. I always use that trick anyhow, so the flashing doesn't bother me, but would look better without it. For how much you are saving, I can't say anything bad about this, and highly recommend it. Make sure to use the yarn guide on the winder, as without it the yarn will go too high or low and can get caught in the gears.

I have always really loved Prismacolor, and was so happy when I saw this series of sets. I've done the math, and it's a really good value for the money with how much you get in each set. All of the products inside were really high quality and performed great. I really like the grid, it's so great for beginners, and is a thicker plastic than others I've seen. The digital instructions were comprehensive and perfect for any beginner. I like that it really has all you need to get started. This would be a great gift for a beginner or a good value for someone more experienced. (I have found, if you get all three sets in a theme, they will all fit in one box if you throw out the plastic inserts.)

My only issues are quite small, but I'll list them here anyway. If you get all three sets in the one theme, or all six sets in total, you will get a lot of repeat colors, for example, there is a white pencil in each kit. Not a problem if you are only getting one, and they are common colors, that you'll likely go through eventually if you color a lot. But I do suspect they assumed people wouldn't get them all, or they would have avoided having six of so many colors.

The instructions are a single piece of paper folded in half, with stuff you can look up for free. Most of it is digital, which shouldn't be too much of a problem for most nowadays. Although the digital instructs are really very good, they teach stuff you can look up for free. I would say, look more at what art supplies are in the box, and choose off of that, more so than the picture it shows you can make. As when you learn to color yourself, you can make any picture you want anyways.

The artist roll, if you get one set, has a lot of extra space, but if you get all three sets, can't hold even half the supplies. Which is a testament to just how much you're getting in these sets. I think they could have done without the roll, but it is nice, especially since it's subjective and others may love having it.

I still highly recommend these, because the savings on getting this many Prismacolor art supplies is just such a good deal, it's worth it for them alone. They are all such high quality and with such a variety of different supplies, you'll be able to make almost anything you can imagine.

P.S. To help those new to Prismacolor avoid a common pitfall, remember that to sharpen a Prismacolor pencil without breaking the core, you need to have a super sharp blade, and use a metal sharpener. The ones provided in each kit worked perfectly for me. Also, don't drop the pencil, or it could shatter the core.

Beautiful creamy pencils. Very good laydown, as well as good blending. I really like this brand, and have come to appreciate pencils that aren't round and rolling about the place. Lots of different colors, and a decent amount of metallic shine. These are enjoyable pencils to use, and I highly recommend them.

These tools are really great for my sculpting. With so many different shapes and sizes, they really could be used by themselves. Of course, you can never have enough different shapes in enough sizes, but this set would be great for beginners. I love the color of them so much as well, it really adds so much to them to not be plain grey metal. Everything about them was great quality, I really love these.

I always love Himi gouache, and was so excited to try out the new colors. Each cup of gouache is less than a dollar, you just can't beat that. I love how many colors there are, and how bright and pigmented they all are. They are great for beginners and for just playing around with. You can use them thick and opaque, or water them down a bunch and use them like water colors. I mist mine rarely with distilled water and have had a set of Himi that is many years old and it has never dried out or molded. I always recommend Himi paints.

Really love the case, it holds all the clay, plus the tools. When this clay is used up, I'll absolutely keep using the case for new clay. The clay comes in so many nice colors. It's very soft, which is great for kids, but keep in mind, it won't harden as much as other name brands. They will have some bend to them and could break, so don't make tiny, thin pieces. Would recommend for kids, but not adults.
Flimsy mold set and there were holes in them. Unusable so I threw them in the trash.

Honestly we didn't NEED anything like this but also we really did need it. We have no great use for these seals but they always looked so fun that we had to try our hand at them and they've been a blast to make. We randomly just find ourselves playing with these to make wax seals and put on random scratch paper even. Lots of pretty colors and you can put two colors in one to make a 2 color seal- that comes out really cool!

This is easy to use and the results are beautiful! I could see this being used for so many things!

I didn't like any of the boring switch covers at the hardware store so was super excited when I saw this kit come across. We made new plate covers for the whole house and it added some whimsy to the house- absolutely awesome! You can make any theme you want from Halloween to flowers to Disney ones- there are just so many possibilities!

I did a bunch of horror ones and had a lot of fun with them. They fit in great for my horror themed house.
We've never used anything like this and used it to make a few little molds for wax melts that I sell. The molds were easy to make and they work really well for pouring wax into! No complaints here.

This doesn't really cover any amount of your wrists, just up to the edge of the wrist. It still looks nice though, was easy to do, and worked well. People who had bad results must've not followed instructions or something- we had no issues with it.

I do a lot of laser wood burning projects and this just expanded my abilities, I absolutely love it! I can do much larger pieces now!

Setup was easy and it works fantastic with my Atomstack laser setup I have. No complaints from me, only love for this!

Such beautiful colors in this set. They really make my resin pop, and add a magical flair. It's really easy and has made all my projects better. It's also very nice added to paints, though it takes a lot of pigment to medium to get it opaque, so I highly recommend using it over another color.

Such an amazing set of molds. Lots of sizes, for not a lot of money. I love the numbers on the side, it really helps me keep track of things. But including how much resin they need on the side is what really sets these above the rest. The quality of the silicon is very high, they have lasted a long time, and produce crystals with a high shine. Stunning results.

Miya is my favorite brand of jelly gouache. The price is just so good as well, I can't it cheaper than this. It's just so good for student grade gouache. Opaque, bright and such beautiful colors. So many colors as well, I am spoiled for choice with this set. It dries well, rewets well and lasts so long in the case. I do open it every few months to mist it, if I haven't used it. Works well thick but can also be watered down a ton as well. Always fun to use this gouache. It also lasts so long, I'm sure by the time I fill up my art book, I'll not have put a dent into the paints.

Himi is my favorite brand of jelly gouache. It's just so good for student grade gouache. Opaque, bright and such beautiful colors. It dries well, rewets well and lasts so long in the case. I do open it every few months to mist it, if I haven't used it. Works well thick but can also be watered down a ton as well. Always fun to use this gouache, and I love the included brushes. It also lasts so long, I'm sure by the time I fill up my art book, I'll have barely put a dent into the paints.

Love the variety of sizes you get in one set. It gives a bit of piece of mind, knowing I'm going to have the size I want when I need it. Never had brush tips before and I'm really loving them. Have used on multiple papers and with several mediums and they hold up very well. They were a beautiful rich black that didn't bleed. I'm overall very happy with these and wouldn't mind getting more. I do wonder how much of that price is going to that overly nice box though...

Nice and thick paper. Although I have used multiple different media in this sketch book just fine, I use it mostly for gouache. It's worked really well for any of my dry mediums. It does fray when I go over a spot several times with a wet brush. Quite expensive for what you get, but it's not bad paper, best for practice or beginners.

Nice and thick paper. Although I have used multiple different media in this sketch book just fine, I use it mostly for gouache. It's worked really well for everything. It doesn't pill up when I go over one spot more than once, which I really appreciate. No severe warping and lots of pages to fill. Overall I have no complaints concerning this paper.

Always love Himi products, they never disappoint. This one also lived up to my expectations. So many beautiful colors, it's mesmerizing. Great jelly gouache for beginners. This set will last just about forever. I use this brand very often, just for doodling, practice or making paintings in my sketchbooks. It is not high end professional gouache or colorfast, as you can tell from the reasonable price.

Only issue is it can be tough to close the lid and I don't like how the handles feel when I carry the case, they don't feel like they will hold. Probably just me, but I carry mine from underneath, not the handles, just to keep myself from worry.

Beautiful colors, with a large range. They were good quality paints, spreading and settling quite nicely on textured paper and smooth. Nicely transparent as well. I found nothing to complain about with the paints themselves. The price on the other hand.. But these were really nice, and they perform very well, so I still highly recommend. The included palette was also very nice.

It's been working out great. I got it originally for some home repair work, but my room mate has also been using it to build styrofoam bases for his Halloween village. Pretty versatile tool, especially with the different tips.

Love that I can change out the layout of the pans so much, Plus, make little six pan sets to take out, and leave the rest at home. This case is one of the better ones I own. Now onto the real star of the show, the paints. They are so lovely. From how easily they wet, to how they go down on paper and the lovely range they come in. Usually, metallic watercolors are super unpigmented, but these have been some of the best I've used. Very shiny, and although I could use them on their own, I tend to lay them on top of color I've already put down, and it makes anywhere they are put just pop. I'm not a fan of the water brush pens, so used my own brushes. But I tested them and they were as good as you would expect.

Oh my goodness, you get so much paint with this. lol It's all a great price too when you consider how much you are getting. I have always loved everything I get from this brand and this is no exception. The paint is great for beginners and for me, someone who just likes to paint as a fun hobby. I use them thick as is, and sometimes water them way down and use like water colors and they work great both ways. The colors are beautiful, well chosen and you get fifty different colors, which is amazing. I also really like the included mini palette, I like lots of little spots to hold the colors, and a large area to mix in, and this has both.

Only thing is, I don't carry this by the handles, but from the bottom. It's so heavy, and I don't trust those handles to not break and leave my paints flying towards the ground.

Amazing deal for a canvas pad. I love having the canvas in this format; it's much more convenient for me. The canvas was pretty high quality for the price and when I need more, I would honestly consider getting more from this brand. I found no faults with it at all.

Lots of nice white and thick paper. Has stood up very well to all sorts of dry media and erasing. I do use pens for line work, but otherwise have not tested any wet medias like markers etc with it. I'm sure it wouldn't take to paint at all. It's a good value for how much decent and large paper sheets you're getting. There is better sketching paper, but it's many times the price of this, so I'm going to stick with this.

A fantastic brush pen, for both art and writing. I loved everything about this pen, from the looks and feel to how the ink flowed. The ink was very nice as well. But the best part is that you can refill the cartridges with ink once they are emptied. This pen will last me a long time.

When it came, it smelled so much like feces, I had to take it straight out of the house. I had put it in the garage, but it smelled so bad, I could still smell it in the house, and my husband made me put it outside. After an entire month of airing out, enough smell is gone, that I can bring it into the garage again. But I don't want it in my room. I'm hoping it will air out completely eventually...

As for function, it's actually really great. Wool is the best mat to use for felting, and it's nice and thick, just the right firmness. Nice and large as well. It was quite fuzzy, and I had to felt the outside a bit to smooth it out. As well, there was a lot of hair so thick it looked almost like bits of hay. But I was able to pick most of that off the surface. I'd give this a five out of five if it didn't smell so bad, and if it ever stops smelling, I may bump this up to four stars, but not five.

I got this for the super cute keychain hooks. I really love how high quality they are. They are actually totally usable. I expected to only be able to use them as a keychain. That made them all the more worth it, and I already considered it to be worth the money. The other hooks are equally nice in quality Tunisian hooks. Definitely a set to consider.

A felting pad is a necessity for felting either 3D characters or painting with wool. This pad is a great size, and the foam in the middle will last longer than the cheap ones you get in kits. I didn't like that the felt on the pad is acrylic, and is very hard, the needles don't go through it, and that will make any small or flat pieces very hard to do. Still, it's a huge step up, and maybe the outside will soften in time.

The included bag is a nice touch, and the needle tool is a great upgrade from using only one needle at a time.

The wool was great, vibrant, individually packaged and I love that it didn't smell of mold or rot, as some other wool I have received in the past has. It's a decent deal and the colors offer enough variety, I was able to make quite a few things from them. I would suggest the larger pack though, more colors is always better. The box was too nice for what you are getting oddly, but I used it to box a gift for someone, so I see that as an added bonus.

The rubber blanks are by far the best part of this set. It's a good amount with different sizes and shapes. But the best are the ones with two colors on them. They let you know when you have cut deep enough. I love the gradient ink, but it and the others are a bit sparse on the ink sadly. The biggest downfall of this set is that it does not come with a standard rubber stamp cutting blade. I thankfully had one already, and it's so much better than using a cheap craft knife. I would still buy this though for the replacement rubber blanks.

Really great paint brushes for miniatures. Never could find a set of brushes this small for the tiny details. The bristles on these are very tiny, but the handles themselves are thicker and feel good in the hand, even after a long time painting. I haven't had any shedding and the quality seems high enough, if I take proper care of them, they'll hopefully last a long time. The case they come in is also very nice and I really like it.

A good sketch book for doodling, thumbnails or just practicing. It's not the best though, especially for the price. It's going to be better for younger kids/teens or for beginners just wanting to dabble and practice. Pages are a bit thin, that's the biggest complaint about the paper itself. It is a nice white though and one hundred pages is definitely enough to keep you busy for a real long time.

This is for someone young or a beginner just starting to dabble. It's quality is really not great, but it's not bad either. It's not bright white and it's not very strong paper either. I used and it was ok, but I will stick to better watercolor papers that cost less.

Halloween is my favorite holiday and I love that I have found some washi tape that fits the theme so well. Love the designs. They are very nice quality, vibrant colors and the foil is just the cherry on top. Kind of wish they had them in silver foil, but still, great tape, for a good price. Will absolutely get more when I run out.

They may seem expensive at first, but you really are getting a lot of high quality paint. Especially if you use them for acrylic pours. The formula is quite thick and very well pigmented, which lets me water them down a great deal for pouring, and when you do that, that's when you really get a lot of bang for your buck. Even after so many paintings, I still have so much left, I love it.

First thought, smells a little like a barn. But it's wool, so I'm not surprised. Second thought was OMG it's so soft! This is a great blend of fibers that has made for a stunning feel. The color I got was black, and it's a very well dyed, deep black. This can easily be a scarf or any other kind of worn item and not itch in the slightest.

These are just dazzling and beautiful!! They sparkle like crazy! Their adhesive works really well but if you're needing it to be stuck on permanently or using this outdoors then I'd try some adhesive along with it like the E6000 craft adhesive to make sure it doesn't come off in the weather.

These rolls of rhinestones are definitely worth it and it beats gluing them on one by one!

Such incredible beauty. These colors are really stunning, and they are easy to add into resin or paint. Can also be brushed onto molds before adding resin for stronger effects. Just SO many things it can be used in. Pretty expensive, but it's so worth it for me. I always want to use them in just about everything.

I have never had a resin mold of this quality before, I love it. It's sturdy and durable, but the detail is just amazing! Love the design as well, very imaginative, perfect for Ouija. The planchet is slightly larger than normal, but still a very good size for all my boards. This is honestly nice enough to make some just for display.

I've never had so many colors before, and they are all so nice and bright. This is a high quality leaf, super thin, so you'll have to be careful with it. Has worked great for me in several different crafts, on nails and for book making. The amount of things this can be used in is crazy, I absolutely recommend getting some gold leaf.

Great pattern and kit. It came with absolutely every thing I needed to complete it. It was all high quality as well, even the needle. The thread being presorted was a huge thing for me, and I'm glad more companies are doing this now.
Really great wool. Everything about the wool itself was great. The price however is two times higher than I am used to paying; there are much better deals here on Amazon.

Ordinarily I have to say when reviewing metallic watercolor, that they are all transparent and need many layers to be opaque, these however (while not fully opaque) are the most pigmented, high quality metallic watercolors I have ever used. I absolutely love these and will have to stick with them, as I have reviewed so many brands at this point, and they are still the best so far. They also come in a really nice metal tin. I would like to see the company sell the paints without the tin though, for those who already have plenty of them, and just want to buy the paint, it would save some money and make them just that much better.

Got this for the large selection of colors, and I am very happy with how much wax and different colors I got. The seal works great. However, the spoon to melt the wax is way too small and is unusable, it literally falls into the hole it is supposed to rest on and can't hold enough wax. I had to makeshift something else. One envelope was ripped, but I didn't use them anyway, so that's no big deal.

A great beginner stamp set that comes with just about everything you'll ever need. The ink pads however are sadly lacking in quality, other than that though, it's a great set. Blades were sharp with a good variety of shapes. Plenty of rubber to start with, especially if you cut them to smaller sizes. A good price for how much you get.

I love himi gouache, and have bought several sets of it now. It's very good for beginners or just for playing with. So many colors to choose from and all high quality with good pigmentation. I sometimes use them straight out of the cup, and often water them down a lot. Probably not lightfast, but good in every other way. They have really won me over.

I love himi gouache, and have bought several sets of it now. It's very good for beginners or just for playing with. So many colors to choose from and all high quality with good pigmentation. I sometimes use them straight out of the cup, and often water them down a lot. Probably not lightfast, but good in every other way. They have really won me over.

I love shrink plastic and use it all the time, and this was a good value set to refill my stock. The quality is really high, same as others I've used in the past. The pencils were not, I gave those away, but I'm a color pencil snob, so, take that how you will.

For those reviewers that are saying your plastic curled and was ruined when you put it in the oven, the curling only lasts for a second and is normal and happens with all brands, just put it back in the oven and it'll go flat.

Polyfil prices online are crazy high for some reason. This brand seems to be one of the better priced ones. Quality is fine, it came vacuum sealed which I really liked, it keeps it from taking up much room until I'm ready to open it. If you're buying online, then this is a good brand to get.

I used mine to do small batches since I don't need a lot at once and this stuffs gonna last forever! The colors are vibrant and BEAUTIFUL!! Absolutely love these colors, I've never gotten such beautiful colors out of any of the tie dye kits I've tried!

Not terrible but also not good fur. You get such a small amount for the money and quality. I've seen much better for the same price, and equal quality for much less money. It's great for crafts, but there wasn't enough to do any of the projects I really wanted to do. Ultimately, although the fur is ok, I'd say buy from another seller on Amazon.

Such a unique range of colors in this set, and each is so beautiful. The iridescent shimmer is so intense, you only need a thin layer to get great results. Though the color itself is, like all shimmer paints I've ever tried, quite translucent. I'd recommend an undercoating of an opaque color to avoid having to do lots of layers if you are wanting a solid look. It's a good amount of paint for the price, and I'm glad I got them.

Very useful as armature for oven bake clay sculptures. I'll be sure to always have some wire from now on. Thick wire has not only made my sculptures stronger, but it's just so much easier to make them when they have a solid frame to build on. Was strong enough to hold up my work, but soft enough to bend without needing tools.
This velcro is easy to use. You just cut the size strip you need and sews it on. Decent price for what it is. Velcro holds extremely well.

Loved the wool, it was soft, had no hay or smells and had beautiful bright colors. I weighed them and they were all there. The needles were very good quality and I really liked them so much better than the usual needles you get in other kits. The foam was standard, not much to say about it. Good kit overall.

The wool was great, and it had everything I needed to get started right away. I weighed the wool and everything was at three grams, and a few just slightly over. No weird smells or hay in any of the wool. No missing items and the quality of everything was pretty good. Overall, this was a good starter kit.

Had this on my wish list for a long time, and I finally got some to use as bulk in the center of needle felted figures. It's a really great value, you can't really find better, unless you buy an insane amount. Color doesn't really matter, as it's going in the center of my work, but I can say, it's a very beautiful bright red, that is even in color throughout. Very soft, and has been entirely clean and free of any moldy smells I've gotten from other brands.

Hooked this up to my laptop and it was easy enough to setup but it misses several lines in a row and makes for a crappy job. Ruined several various materials that I tried it on. Doesn't work on anything like metal, it'll only work on wood.

Save your money and get something like an budget Atomstack engraver because this one's terrible!

Very unique color on these, and I've not seen this on any others. The quality of the tools themselves are very high, I've used them so much and they are still in perfect condition. These are my go to tools now. I use mine for polymer clay, not wax and they work great for that. I have no clue what the little silicon things are for and gave those to a friend.

Very shiny, opaque beautiful colors. I am loving their slender size and small tip. Works very well on paper, though I haven't used them on any other kind of surface yet. I am very happy with these so far.

Pretty good, but not the very best. They did blob ink here and there, but otherwise were fine. I do love the colors and the metallic sheen is so pretty. I've been using mine on standard paper, so don't know how they will work on other surfaces

So this just pops right open, there's nothing to it. I love it because on colder days I sometimes need to paint something with my little airbrush but don't wanna do it outside. Now I'll just take it to the garage and use this to keep the paint from spraying out everywhere. Very convenient and stores so flat I just shove it between my washer and dryer, love this thing!
Used this for a few projects and it did amazing on all of them! Easy to use and looks amazing! Definitely will be getting more.

Honestly you can get name brand engravers in this price range so I'm not sure why anyone would spent $400 on this one. That being said, it does work. It does a decent job for a lower end engraver. Doesn't get the smaller details but you need a more expensive finer point laser for that. So for what it is, this is a decent projector.
All of her features are done well. For the price, it's very impressive! Great quality mannequin bust. Does not include hair or wig FYI.

Such beautiful colors, and so many as well! I can't imagine needing more, as this is nearly their entire line. Absolutely enough paint here to paint every army you have and your friend's armies and still have so much to spare. I love my set so much, have used it thoroughly, and have had no problems. Loved the little metal balls they included with.

What I love most about these is the blending and smooth laydown, it's just so good for how little you pay. Plus, it comes with a great little carrier, which I will be using for more pencils once these are used up, and I rather expect I will use these up, as they are good enough to use for most of my work. They aren't professional mind you, and I doubt they are lightfast, but they are good pencils, and will definitely recommend them for anyone who colors in coloring books or as a hobby.

Love the versatility of this set. I've been getting into paracording, and normally you have to buy so many little thigs, but this set had everything I was wanting, all in one. This thing will make just about anything. What I love most is the long pegs can be adjusted to make monkey fists of many sizes, instead of needing a different jig for each size. Everything in this kit is metal, and nice quality.

The pencils sucked, not that I cared, as I got these just for the plastic sheets, so the rest of the stuff was just a nice bonus. I gave the pencils to my friends kids, the rest of the stuff included was really nice quality, so I kept for myself. As for the sheets themselves, they were great quality, did just as they were supposed to. A good set to get.

Such incredible value for the money. Very much loved these. Great at blending and was able to layer quite a bit with these, though keep in mind that paper has a lot to do with just how many layers you'll be able to do. With two hundred colors, you are sure to find just the exact right shade for what you need. I found that many colors, are so close, they might as well be the same, but I just figure, that will keep me from having to buy another set to replace that color when I run out. lol The set comes in a nice box and the pencils themselves look like a lot of the higher quality pencils, and so overall give quite a nice impression. I have had no wax bloom and the colors are pretty vibrant for the price.

Had everything needed included. The thread was sorted, which I always love to see. Instructions were easy and so was the kit itself. The end product is just so darn stinking cute, I love them all so much. The little hidden compartments are great.

The fox kit is a stunning picture, very beautiful and was fun to do. The kit had all the floss needed, and it was pre sorted, which I loved. The needles were good and fabric was as well. The instructions were very easy to follow. Loved this kit and would love to get more from this seller.

This has been a nice set of mica colors. They are measured by weight, not how much they fill the bottle, so many colors look half empty. Sad, as apparently my fav colors tend to be heavy I guess. But I did weigh them all, and you get just what you pay for. I have used these in many different crafts/resins etc and the colors have performed perfectly in everything. Very nice quality.

This kit had everything I needed, plenty of floss left over, so no need to worry about it running out, and best of all, the thread was presorted. That alone makes me love it, but the quality of everything was so nice, the image so beautiful and instructions were really good as well. I have several kits from this company for a reason, and I'm sure I'll end up getting more later as well.

I love this brands gouache so much, so I decided to try out their water colors, and oh my, they were so good. Besides just the product itself looking so nice and fancy, the paint itself was very pigmented, vibrant, mixed well and moved beautifully when I used them. I also loved the swatch card, which I always make one, but the included one was even set up in the unique way the pans were.
One of the best molds I have. It's really standing up well. I love the little runes it makes, they are so nice looking and well decorated. They also make great gifts for friends.

Love the adjustable needle, it was quickly my fav. I have been using the set to make tiny decorative rugs with, and it's so much fun. All you need to get is the yarn, although I'd also recommend a strong glue for the back to hold everything together once finished. I haven't used any of the nine large sewing needles, but everything else has been great.

As soon as I saw washi tape in rainbow, I had to have it, and I must say, I quite love the sets range of colors. As well, the quality has been pretty good. This much washi tape will have me set for a while, with cheerful colors the whole way.

Comes with much more thread than you'll need, so your kids can make friendship bracelets or if you get more cloth, even more cross stitches. Very cute designs that were enjoyed. I would advice caution with the needles if your kids are very young, but they aren't as sharp as sewing needles. I like that this kit had everything needed all in one box. The keyrings are very sturdy as well.

I like that this kit has everything to get started. It even includes things most kits don't, like the white pen, which is very useful for adding highlights. Lots of beautiful colors, and all were high quality. The colors wet easily, and spread nicely on the paper. This kit would be a great gift for any kid into art or wanting to start with water colors.

So much wool to make a lot of cute little critters, and for such a good price. I love that it comes with so many needles, and all the things you need to complete each project. Plus, it has a pretty cute box it comes in. It had plenty of wool for me to fully complete each project, and I had enough wool left over to make some of my own creations. Would be great for beginners.
These colors work great to dye resin! Colors all look good. Good value.

Pretty good pencils for beginners. Mine came all in one piece and using a knife, I have had no problems sharpening them. They are harder than I'm used to, and they aren't near as pigmented as I'd like, but they are good enough, especially for practicing. I have found them to be better on rougher paper.

Perfect for so many crafts, and even just sculpting. I've had a lot of fun with this stuff but I find it necessary for cosplay and making accessories for my sculptures and figures. Easy to adjust and readjust 'til you get it right, and also easy to paint and drill. It's natural color is great for fake monster teeth.
I love these things, they make threading a needle the easiest thing. And this is enough to last many lifetimes and for a really great price. Can't recommend these enough.

Perfect kit. Came with easy instructions, accurate pattern that was easy to read and all the material you need for the entire project. The floss was even presorted which I love. The finished piece is just so nice looking, and will be lovely on the wall. Highly recommended kit.
Pretty good tie dye kit. Easy to use for beginners and color seems to last after being washed even. Decent value.
Easy enough to use. Sets up quickly. Takes a good impression if you know what you're doing and are careful. I'm pretty happy with this overall. Made a couple molds that will last me quite a while!

It's the brand of beginner/student grade paint that I love the most. It really is great, and I use it for all of my gouache paintings now. It's good to have a cheaper gouache when doing lots of studies and practice, or just painting for fun. Fully recommended for just about anything other than professionals or paintings you plan on selling, as I doubt this has any kind of real light permanency.

I was surprised at how well these worked, for the price. I would say these are going to be good for beginners and those wanting to test out the medium on a budget. It comes with a pad of paper, its small, but plenty to do swatches and test them out with. I've been using them for a while now and have had no problems with them at all. These are beautiful budget pencils. The case is a nice sturdy cardboard, with plastic inserts that hold the pencils and sharpeners.
All arrived in perfect shape, they were packaged well. All are flawlessly clear and look amazing! Highly recommend!

48 colors, and all are really pretty. Love how varied they are and with mixing, you can get just about any color you need. The paints are quick to activate and were very creamy and highly saturated with pigment. Vivid colors that laid down nicely on my paper. I especially loved the fluorescent, I've never seen water color like that before and am very happy to own some.

Very concentrated shimmer, which lets you have a lot or water it down for just a little. The color itself is on the sheer side, so you'll probably want to put it over color you've already put down, but it does not skimp on the metallic sheen at all. Very stunning and beautiful. It's not a lot of each color, but it's not a bad amount either, especially with how many pans there are in total.

Expensive for what you ultimately get. A box that is held together with rubber bands. It doesn't feel flimsy when it is empty, but paint weighs a lot and it starts to get wobbly when it's as full as it can be. No instructions, which for me was fun, but I love lego and puzzles, but I'd bet most would hate that. The dividers were too big, I took a few hours to sand them down, and they were still too big and bowed, but after spending so long sanding them, I called it good enough. If you don't put too much paint in it, it'll hold just fine, especially sitting still on a desk. But there is better on Amazon for less money.

I had been needing a set like this one for a long time and I'm glad I finally got one. It's been very handy for a lot of things. But mostly models. The cutters and blades are all very sharp and cut well, and the files have been a God send. Definitely something to get if you are working with miniatures.

Great to use for many different crafts. A main stay for myself. This is a good amount and at a good price. The resin was clear and had little odor when curing and none at all when cured. It has stayed strong and even months later, it hasn't yellowed at all.

These brushes are very soft, but still work great and do just what they should. On the very hardest clays, these may not be firm enough. I'm able to get far better details and smoothing done with these than I could ever do with my fingers alone, so I've been quite happy with these so far. To be fair, mine haven't come apart like others apparently have, but they could be very easily glued back together, so I have no worries for if that ever happens.

Absolutely love these for resin! I've had a ton of fun making things that  glow and putting them all over my living room since I have black lights run along the ceiling. My whole living room glows a lot thanks to this stuff! Very easy to mix in and glows really well! I was truly surprised at this! Great quality product at a good price!

My favorite washi tape by far! The designs are so good, I don't think I'll ever find washi tape I like as much as this. The stickiness was just the right amount too, which can be hard to find. The box that they came in was also great to have, it kept them all in like new condition, and now I use it for other washi tapes since I have used the originals up. I truly hope they make more of this. Very highly recommended.

Such cute movie characters, I was happy to add them to my ever growing collection of felted projects. Came with everything shown and was in good shape. I've had other kits wool smell very moldy, but this wool is in perfect shape, and I had plenty of every color to finish all my projects and have some left over to use on whatever my imagination can come up with.

This is a really neat kit with a nice variety of colors. The wax is easy to melt and stamp and the results are really nice/ professional looking. Very easy to use for a beginner even and produces great results.

One of the easiest cross stitch kits I've ever done, and it looks so sleek and classy on the wall. The kit had everything I needed. Floss was also pre sorted and lots of floss, so I didn't come anywhere near running out. Everything was high quality. Very highly recommended.

This kit indeed has everything needed to complete it. Had plenty of extra floss, so no need to worry about it running out and the needle was good quality as well. A very fun image to do, and it looks great finished.

We've taken these all over town and put them on everything- it's hilarious! Absolutely love this huge bag of googly eyes!

They stick well and look great!

Pretty good quality mannequin- I love it!! I'm thinking of redoing her lips to give it a more glossy red deep shine but that's just personal preference. The lashes look great, makeup looks great, pierced ears, wig cap on so you can just throw on a wig and pin it in place if need be. Also has a spot in the bottom where you could put it on a peg stand or you can just sit her on a flat surface as is!

Overall very impressed with this! I'm gonna transform mine into Marilyn Monroe for my 50s kitchen! This mannequin could be great for many settings!

I have lots of UV resin and this one has performed as well as any of the others. Very highly recommended for your UV resin needs. Good price, and it did indeed cure clear, not yellow and also, it cured quickly. Very versatile, especially for crafters.
