I love this brand of gouache so much. It has a good range of colors that are all beautiful, a sturdy case and the brushes are very nice as well. Over all, I would recommend this to beginners or just those that want to paint as a hobby.

Was able to make a lot of little things with this stuff and I have a lot left. It really is a good amount to get. I love how quickly this cures. I have been using this for several different crafts, and so it's very versatile as well, and should be in every crafting kit. The little silicon cup was very handy to have, and will get a lot of use.

Phenomenal paint, it goes on most anything and the shine is near impossible to beat. A lovely range of colors as well. None of these have ever chipped or cracked on me before. Some are thin enough, they need more than one coat, but they are so worth it for that finale look.

This has a large array of stunning colors. The quality of the pigment is high and there is a lot of the pigment in each stick. I use these in many different crafts to color things and am extremely pleased with the quality. The box is well done also, and none of the sticks were broken when they arrived. I like the paper they are wrapped in, to keep from having to touch the chalk directly.

Such a large amount will keep me from having to rebuy for a long time. It cures very quickly and was clear as well. So far, it has remained clear. UV resin is a necessity in my crafts and can make countless different effects. I absolutely love this stuff and recommend trying it out in your own crafts.

A wet palette is something I have wanted to try for a long time, and now that I have, I consider it a necessity. Both storing and mixing paints was so much easier in this than without, and whereas I am having to keep replacing dried up paint on a regular palette, on this, I was able to go eat and not worry about my paint, it was still fresh, even after having come back to it after sleeping for the night.

This is pretty good gouache, but mostly for beginners. It could have more colors, and some of the paints are dry enough in their tubes that it is very hard to get them out. Still, it's not bad at all, and not something I feel you will regret unless you are looking for much higher quality; but then that would be much more money.

The listing is in English, as well as the box, front and back. It says, in English, that it includes instructions, which it does, but they are in French. I tried to figure out how to use it without instructions, but failed. I can figure out most of it, but have no clue what the "Barre a foule" is or how to use it. I'm sure this would have been an awesome kit, but I won't ever know.

I suggest either add in English instructions, take the English off of the box and listing or leave the English on the box, but clearly say that the English stops at the cover of the box, and all instructions are in French only. Would have saved me a good bit of money and frustration.

Good quality mold, works perfectly!
Really easy to do these molds and make a domino box- we had a blast making a box and made a couple for friends to put their dominos in as well. There are no instructions as many have mentioned but resin molds pretty much never have instructions as all you do is mix your resin and then let it cure per the instructions on the resin bottle. Putting together a little box that slides together isn't hard though, I have no idea why anyone even needs instructions. Anyways- great mold, we love it!

Very thick paper in this book and it comes in a nice hardcover. The pictures are all really nice! Paper is good enough quality you can really use anything from markers to paint on it! This company has a whole line of these books and I love them all! This books already a lot of fun and I'm a couple pages in on coloring it. Definitely recommend!

Super big coloring book (height and width, the thickness is about normal of a thicker coloring book.) Cardstock type thick sturdy paper, works great with paint, markers, crayons, colored pencils, etc...  I colored one page with markers on one book and then I used this book and did it with watercolors and it worked great! Amazing quality book, fanciest coloring book around!

This is a extremely large in size coloring book (talking height and width) and it's a hardcover. Paper is like cardstock thickness- really nice! Pictures are really unique and a lot of fun!

I've colored one page already and really enjoyed it! Can't wait to color the rest! Very good quality book, I've never seen such a fancy coloring book! Love it!
I have put a couple resin figures on this and it lights them up perfectly! I love this base! Wood looks great. Very good quality!
Good quality resin mold! We've done a couple of these with this one and they came out great! Good sturdy mold!

The picture is beautiful and was fun to do. All the drills stuck to the surface very well, and there was enough of all of them to get the entire picture done, with some to spare. Comes with everything needed.

I love the decorative accessories it comes with, they are kind of the best part of this kit. The clay dried so quickly, that it can't be used to make anything detailed. Mine in just a few minutes would no longer stick to itself and I couldn't blend the seams. But if you just make basic shapes to put the accessories into, it works great. It made such cute critters and such, very easily and it's a blast for kids. Just wouldn't recommend for high detail stuff.
This resin kit works great! Mixed it and did a few different little molds and it cured perfectly and looks great! Great quality.
Right out of the package with light use- the tip just snapped off and the thing is broken. Threw it in the trash and got a different one.
Just your average scissors, nothing amazing here. Not too sharp but not too dull. They work for everything I've thrown at them so far. No complaints.

These are actually slightly larger than I imagined which is nice! The mold is well made and has no flaws in it at all. We mixed up some resin and colored it, poured it in the mold, and when it dried we had several neat little daggers! Gonna use them for Halloween props and might even hand some out for Halloween along with the candy! Love this silicone mold!
We've used these for slime and a few other things and they work great! The labels are a really nice touch too! Decent quality product.

Very soft clay, so it would be easy for kids or those with arthritis. Many bright and fun colors, plus it comes with many accessories. This clay baked just fine, without scorching or cracking. I love the case, it holds everything perfectly and will continue to get use, even when I finish up the clay, which will take a while, because of how much there is.
This clay is very soft and has lots of fun colors. It stuck to itself well and baked fine. The finished piece is sturdy and not cracked or burnt. My case did come cracked however.

Comes with a decent range of colors, and I like the bag and brushes well enough. However, it's such a very small amount of paint, and when you need many, many coats of paint to cover up the white of the canvas underneath, it doesn't last long at all. If this was half the price of what it is now, I'd say it would be good to get for kids.

I like having two handles to choose from, and for when I need more than one type of blade at a time. The handles could hold the blades a little tighter. This set is just fine for most simple craft projects which is what I mostly use it for. Wood will dull the blades quite quickly, so I don't recommend it if it's just going to be used for whittling. The case came with a small crack, but nothing that stops the case from working.
Great epoxy! Used it for many projects and it does a fantastic job! Definitely recommend!

Wonderful and beautiful. So good to have these, both to look at and to protect wildlife. There are several butterflies that are photoshopped to be colors that don't exist in real life, while pretty, I wasn't expecting that, and was hoping for more realism and fewer repeats of designs but in different colors. However that is a very small complaint I am sure no one else would even note. So I do still highly recommend these.

Gorgeous birds! I love having these on my windows, to look at and knowing that it will help out wild life as well. I had a dove fly into my window before I got these and it made me so sad, but I'm glad it'll never have to happen again. They stick very well, but can be repositioned without leaving any sticky mess.

The paper is fairly smooth and thin , but still strong and does well with multiple kinds of mediums I have tested on it so far. I have used the larger sized paper to make my own books, and it worked very well for that.
Perfectly smooth and it was indeed bleed proof, so I would highly recommend this. This was the best paper I have used so far for markers and it's a great price as well.

So many colors to choose from and they are all so beautiful. You have quite a lot of each as well, definitely will last me a long while; even though there are countless ways I could use this. Just not in anything that goes down the drain, as these are not biodegradable.
A little on the thin side, but still really good quality. The end results are stunning and it's so awesome to have on the wall. I love having this set.

Beautiful colors, but I have no clue what they are. The box doesn't properly say, and the pans aren't labeled. I went to the listing to see if I could find the right color names, but two images show different names, and the colors aren't even in the right places on those cards. So I have no clue what colors I have, which is very frustrating. Also, the tin I got is not like what is pictured. Good quality pigments though and beautiful colors if you don't care what they are called.

The art supplies in this are so awful, I wouldn't give to any kids, unless they are still in the stage where they just make blobs on the page. The water colors I at first thought were ok, but when they dry, they just wipe off the paper and are gone. The crayons are too soft and streaky. The pencils are the least worst of the art supplies, but you can get so much better for so much less here on amazon. The apron doesn't even fit kids, maybe a very young child could wear it. The paper is not at all useable for wet mediums.

The easel box is very nice, and I pulled all the plastic out of the brown box and will sand and paint the inside to cover all the glue left behind, and then it'll be a nice box too. The canvas are all decent. So I give it three stars for those items. If those are worth the price, then go ahead and get it, but not for the art supplies.

Beautiful colors with a good range, both light to dark and just color selection in general. Case is very handy and is sturdy. I love the bumps on the caps, as it makes it easier for me to open them. Good blending as well, which is important.

This resin was very nice to work with, with a decent working time and low smell. It set up nicely and mixed well with both dyes and mica. It was ready after a day, and set up hard. It came with a lot of useful extras, especially the silicon stuff, as that's reusable.

Stunning colors and shine that come in a nice bottle, instead of a thin baggie. Used in resin and it performed perfectly. Made everything so wonderful and I love it so much. Several bottles were only half full, and I wish they were all full to the top.

Very white paper, that was a little thin, but still stood up to everything I reasonably threw at it. Nicely textured, not too rough that it messes with your image, but not too smooth, so, good tooth. The pages were so large, I decided to use it to make a hand made book, and they worked perfectly for that. I'm enjoying the paper very much and it's a decent price for pages that size..
So cool looking, I don't want to use it all up! Not so sticky that it doesn't come up, but enough that it sticks when you want it to. Vibrant colors and and the foil is beautifully done.

To start with the good, the gouache itself was good quality and the colors beautiful. I normally love GenCrafts products and I love gouache and so I really thought I would love, and wanted to love these.

But it took a long time for me to get to the paint, as the bottles are sealed by metal. It was so strong I simply could not open them, it took forever and I was about to just ruin the whole tube trying to open it, so I ended up having to use a screwdriver to force the seal open, so now all the paints have metal dust in them...

I am sure if I use some more of the paint up, that will go away, as the metal is just at the top. No clue how else to open these though, the metal seals are so strong, that the pointy tip on the cap just blunted when I tried using them. Nothing else but working a screwdriver against them for a while was enough to get through them.

Many of the tubes were not full and a few just straight up half empty Also, they left raw sienna out, which is a shame to be shorted a tube.

Came with everything needed and I love so much that the floss came pre sorted. This kit was just awesome and I loved the end result. It had plenty of floss for each color and the needle was a good quality.
Great quality clay, great variety of colors! These bake and work perfectly. Case arrived in perfect shape too. Definitely recommend these!

Perfect for beginners or those more advanced like myself. It comes with everything needed, including the printed fabric, presorted thread, instructions and needle. A lot of companies don't bother to sort the thread, and I love that they took the extra effort to do that. The washable print on the fabric in my new favorite thing, it came off so easily. More than enough thread in this kit, you definitely won't run out.

Beginner brushes that go perfectly with Himi's gouaches. I love the feel of the handle and how well the bristles hold their shape, even after a lot of use. Lovely pastel shades with accent lettering look very classy. Himi's gouache does stain the bristles on first use though, so don't expect them to stay white.

I love this brand so much. It has great colors and at a great price. For the price of one very small tube of some other brands, you get an entire palette, brushes, twenty four paint colors at a whopping thirty ml each and a container to hold them all in! I recommend this brand to most people.

I do wish however, that you could buy singles of colors, as it is now, when you run out of one color, you have to buy all the colors again to get more of the one.

I'd have given this a perfect five star, but my mold seems to have several air bubbles or some kind of defects in the mold, meaning the resin mold I made with it had lots of large defects on it. I used a very sharp knife to try to cut those off the resin piece I made, but it didn't work too well. Extremely cute and if yours doesn't have defects, I'd say 100% worth it and you would love it.

These look amazing and I can't wait to make even more of them. Everyone I show them to loves them. The small skull removes extremely easily, while the larger one does take a little bit of fiddling, but nothing too bad at all.
These are the cutest little dragons. The molds are very sturdy and thick, without any imperfections. This is a good price for three molds and I highly recommend.

I set this up in the garage and grabbed some plain white shirts, white fabric shoes, and a couple of other little things like bandanas. We spent the day doing it and everything came out just beautiful! The colors seem to be holding pretty well too! The colors come out bright and bold- absolutely gorgeous! Very good quality kit and I'm very pleased with it!
Got tired of having to buy more blades, I think this'll do me for a very long time indeed. Good quality, cut well and are oiled up so they don't rust. I'm quite happy with these.

I now love Pigma Microns. I used to hate them, as even though they are supposed to be waterproof, they have never been in the slightest. But recently someone told me, you have to wait at least an hour to a day before they are waterproof. I wonder if they think that is supposed to be common sense, because that isn't on the packaging anywhere. I really didn't know that, and from going online about it, I am far from the only one. If I can make a recommendation to Pigma for the future, please put that on your packaging, because now that I know that, I really do love your products, and had stayed away for many years, because I thought your products were just really bad about bleeding like crazy.

But I do now, fully recommend Pigma and it's products. Also, I very much love, the new sizes of pens. These don't bleed at all (So long as you let them dry long enough.).

Great molds, well detailed and they lay flat. They worked perfectly with my resin and the glitter they included. Although I love the gears, I don't plan to use them in my dominos. But they are great to have for other projects I'll do later.

A great kit that makes a lot of slime. You can even use some of the extra activator on your own glue to increase the value of this kit. I love how much the slime shimmers and shines. Very fun to play with. It does not come with an airtight container, so you'll have to supply your own.
Good value. These molds worked great! We used them a few times now and love them. Great quality.

Very pretty once done. It was quite easy to do and came with everything needed. Except the water of course. Not the quality of paint I was expecting from a company like Faber. But I guess considering the price, I should have expected that. It took a long time to finish this, mostly because of how many layers it took to get decent color. The black lines near the end also started to come off, but you can't tell unless you are looking for it. I guess it didn't like being wet for that long.

I have to separate the wool from everything else in this review, as they are two wholly separate experiences. First, the non wool items. The tweezers were cheap but everything else was perfect, and it's a crazy good price for it all, even taking into account the wool, I'd still say to get this kit. The non wool items are five stars out of five.

But to get to the wool, omg, it was/is SO foul! I opened the little bags to test it out, and it all smells of a damp basement, but cranked up to ten. I ended up feeling sick to my stomach and having a headache for hours after pulling it all out of it's bags. I left them to air out for a day, and they smelled better, but when I put them back in the bags the next day, they ended up smelling again. It might be usable if you let it air out long enough.. But maybe you should plan on using the wool for wet felting, so you can wash that stank out of it at the same time.

They also cut the wool, which you shouldn't do, as it leaves very short hairs that don't felt well, it should be gently pulled apart. The wool was also light (as in weight) on all colors, adding up to being four grams short in all. The wool part of this kit gets zero out of five stars.

So to balance out both of those reviews, it's a three star overall.

Not artist grade, but very good for beginners. Lots of colors and this set also has decent metallic colors. Love that it comes with lots of brushes, and different types as well. It has a nice metal tin and good watercolor paper to make this a complete kit. One of the gold paints was broken, but I just set them back in place and put a couple drops of water in the cracks, and it set up perfectly, so it wasn't too much of a problem.
This is not a concentrate but sadly a cheap watered-down product. very disappointed with this. The variety of colors is nice but the quality of the product is awful. Unusable for us, we threw it out.

Absolutely not artist-grade, more children's. Still decent quality for what it is and I love how it glows under a black light! Chalky and badly pigmented compared to artist grade, but this is the case with most cheaper watercolors I've tried so it's no big surprise.
Usable and good for practice though. For the price point, it's ok.
Everything in this set is pretty good quality and works as it should. Good quality set.

I work with all sorts of art mediums and these are just perfect little mats for all of my crafts! I put one or two across on my desk and use them for a work surface so I can pull it up after and cleanup is easy! Definitely recommend and love these! Well made sturdy mats.
Used these for resin and they worked perfectly! Absolutely beautiful colors!! A few were a little short and a couple were only half full. But still a good deal for how much you get.

Great little set, was a blast to do! Plus it has everything you need. Great quality minus the one little issue of the needles included. So I didn't take any stars away due to the price point. It's so affordable that the one issue we had wasn't a big deal to us. But it should be noted that the felting needles may come covered in rust. We soaked them in vinegar and the rust came right off, then put a tiny amount of mineral oil on them to keep them from rusting again. It was pretty easy. So if you decide you wanna mess with that, it really wasn't hard to clean them in my opinion tho.
Really nice kit that was a blast to do and the final result is just beautiful!! Definitely recommend this diamond kit- made with quality materials and is a lot of fun to do.

This is a really nice set that consists of quality materials. We really enjoyed doing this one and the finished product is beautiful! We attached a little metal hanger to it and hung it on the wall instead of using it for a bookmark. It's too pretty to tuck it away somewhere! Definitely loved this and the price isn't bad at all!

Definitely get this, it's a must have for anyone who does diamond paintings! It's been an absolute lifesaver to have these to sort the little dots. Everything is great quality and I'm very happy with this kit!

This is one of my favorites and everything was just perfect! No problems/ issues whatsoever. Was just a blast to put together and the final product is a gorgeous picture of sunflowers, I LOVE this kit!

Great quality for the price! Definitely recommend this set for beginners and even more experienced artists! Even though these aren't high priced watercolors- you can still make really nice paintings with them!! Definitely give them a try, you'll love them!

All of them arrived In perfect shape and work flawlessly, zero complaints here!

We had fun with this kit, I'd never done anything like this before but it was a blast! The felt and everything included was as described and of good quality. I have zero complaints about this felt kit and definitely recommend it!
This stuff works great and does what it should! Good value for the decent-sized bottles you get!

This has been one of my favorites because of the image it has! Decent quality set that was a lot of fun to put together! Doesn't have resealable bags but I prefer to dump them all into the little sorter trays that I have anyway. The little diamonds on this stick to the paper like they should. Everything was there and of decent quality. The picture was really pretty once completed!

Even beginners can do this kit pretty easily! It's a lot of fun and you end up with a neat result! A great quality product that's very enjoyable, I definitely recommend this kit! For kids and adults alike!
Everything as described on this set. Nice quality set and lots of fun to do! I have zero complaints with this one and definitely recommend it.

I don't know how this has so many good reviews, these were awful! The pens are so sharp they rip paper if you try to write on anything other than really thick paper. Then the pens don't actually hold any amount of ink like they are supposed to. Then the ink is watered down. Overall this set was just awful and I do not recommend it at all.

The only one good thing about this set is the box holds it all nicely and it looks nice. Overall I would say to skip this cheap pen set though.

It works pretty well and the thermostat seems to work ok. It's not a premium quality set but for the price it's really not bad at all. Definitely a good starting point for people to practice with and decide if they want to keep going with wood burning or not. If you have the skill you can still make some pretty amazing things with this basic burner. Overall I recommend it for the price point.

We were honestly surprised at the quality of this paper, you never know what you'll get with origami paper after all! But this was a pleasant surprise as it's great quality and the price point it's listed for isn't bad at all! Made a few origami things with it and am very pleased with it overall! Definitely recommend.

Definitely a blast putting this one together and the picture came out to be really beautiful! Definitely framing and putting this one up on the wall.

Was pretty easy to do, time consuming as always but I suppose that's the point really. Didn't have any problem with this set at all, highly recommend.

We are just getting started in the diamond paintings and this kit is a must and a great value! All of these things, honestly I don't know what all of them do yet! But the LED lighted board is a perfect work surface and the little sorting trays are my next favorite thing on this!

A fun accessory set that you won't regret getting in my opinion! I definitely recommend this.

Note: the LED board comes with a usb cord and can plug into your computer or an external battery to power it, it does not come with it's own battery. Just letting you know you can't charge it and go unless you bring your own battery. Those are cheap though and I think everyone should have one!

A much better value than our local hobby store. Nothing to go wrong here- it's vellum paper and you get 100 sheets for the price of a small pack of 25 at most stores so i'm happy with it. Made a arcade marquee out of it for my bartop arcade and it's awesome!
It will not stay fastened, falls off constantly.  The quality, durability and price I would rate very highly if it would stay on my wrist!
I was very impressed by the brilliant colors and the amount I received.  My grandchildren and me and a great time making stuff.  I received more than enough to play with.  Five stars!!!
I did not expect it to be so hard to work with. It feels like I’m molding laffy taffy or bubblegum and it’s hard to smooth out the bumps. I tried to make jewelry with it but it’s impossible sadly :/
Made wedding cake toppers
My 11 year old loves this kit. So many color options, plus a helpful instruction booklet. Everything needed - clay, shaping tools, and accessories make this a great purchase.
I bought this for a Christmas present for my step daughter and she loves it.
Wonderful starter kit; so many great colors!
Found this product to lack adhesive quality on some of the sheets. Not enough of the basic colors
For beginners this is a good kit to start making bead jewelry. Instructions are somewhat lacking but available doing a google search. Not recommended if you are more advanced in beading.

This item was okay but it doesn’t quite serve the purpose of bigger projects due to the lack of common colors quantity. Great for exploring and honing skills but not for serious crafters making a medium to large project
This product wasn’t as good as I thought it would be. The adhesive on the vinyl wasn’t very strong and the vinyl of the glitter paper lacked quality. There are better products available.
This item was easy to use but it lacked enough adhesive to consistently use to transfer the vinyl to glass and when it does it leaves adhesive marks on the glass unlike other quality products

I found the product to be usable and there were many colors as advertised. I did find that during the cutting process that the color of the vinyl did rub off on the cutting tool. I am still debating on purchasing this item again as I did not have the rubbing off problem with other vinyl products.

Item arrived as posted but incomplete as there was no transfer paper roll included as advertised. The project would have been easier and faster to complete if the advertised transfer paper was included as it was supposed to be.

First time trying uv resin.  I love it!  It's so much faster than the other kind.  It's weird, but I think it smells really good too.  Love that the bottles come with applicators.  I will be buying this again.
They added measuring cups, stir sticks, and gloves.  It wasn't fully cured in 8 hours but it was a much colder environment than recommended.  Cures nice and clear.

This arrived quickly.  I haven't tried the clay yet, but I'm thoroughly impressed by the ball tools.  They seem to be solid steel.  The booklet suggests you can cure smaller pieces with a hair dryer or by boiling in water.  Its got directions to make a few little trinkets too.  I'm intrigued for sure.  I thought the rolling pin was acrylic, but it's hollow plastic.  As soft as the clay is, I think it will be fine.  There are interesting jewelry findings too.  Overall, this seems like a great purchase!

My 9 yr old has gained so much interest in creating his own "action figures" and has been using other types of clay (cheap and expensive) with varied results.  This was a great buy!  Nice array of colors, tools, and craft trinkets with a low cost.  He opened the Amazon package not knowing what was inside and was elated when checked it out.  I will buy again.
Daughter loved this and worked very well, would buy again. Only thing is the clay blocks are small, as others have mentioned.
The collors are  not  the same  like on the web  picture.
Great value! My daughter loves all the color choices and has already made a ton of fun stuff!
I used these to hot glue on a coat and hat I wore for a Halloween costume as Harry from Home Alone. They did the job!
Nice little kit, great value for the money. We used these with shrinky dinks to make earring and key rings, etc
These are good if you’re making tiny things. I make earrings so these are PERFECT

I got these initially for the purpose of making tiny resin flowers as the molds are silicone. It worked wonderfully for making a variety of small resin flowers and they held up well to 3 pours now. They would work beautifully for clay as intended but they make gorgeously detailed resin flowers as well.

Perfect for making resin flowers for dice or other tiny applications.

This is a great starter set for a kid or teen. We are using these in our art room and they work perfectly. None of them have been dried out and the colors are nicely pigmented. They feel good in your hand. What I really like about them is the selection of colors. It has a nice range of colors which allows our clients to get really creative.

This is a great baking clay beginner set. It has a lot of different colors and some basic tools to get you started without the major brand price tag.

This is great for my clients to make little figures of their emotions. They can use all kinds of colors to represent their feelings and then we bake them and use the figures to act out how those emotions look and brainstorm other ways we can “use up” the feelings.

The clay feels nice if a little different than the brand name ones in craft stores. It compares otherwise and I love the color selection.

I did have a hard time initially when baking these but I had to just sort of do trial and error with our little office oven until I found what generally works (for us it was a little lower temp than recommended, for a little longer). That might not be on the clay and more on the tiny oven.

These are easy to use and a great way to offer kids a lot of different colors at a great value!

This is a great starter set for slime!!

I got this for our occupational therapists to use with clients and the kids love all of the add in options and it is a quick set up activity. This is perfect for fine motor skills, sensory input, and strengthening if you thicken the slime (think theraputty). We also like to have the kids use tiny tweezers to remove things from the slime. This is both challenging and fun for a lot of our clients.

Highly recommended!

This is such a fun set! The Elmer slime “Gue” products are great out of the package but getting to mix it yourself is much more satisfying in my opinion! This set comes with everything you need to make thick, stretchy slime.

It was incredibly easy to make and getting a set like this is a great idea if you aren’t planning to fuss with the ingredients for homemade slime. Buying everything separately is only cost effective if you make A LOT of slime. This makes sense for smaller batches at home and is a great value for trying out slime making.

These are beautiful and so shimmery. You don’t feel the glitter chunks in the slime, which I appreciate. Excellent kit!

This is great nylon/Lycra hammock material! I got a yard of it to test out in my therapy clinic and it feels much nicer and smoother than the others we have. I ordered another 3 and 4 yards in this color and another silvery color to hang up in our sensory gym as Lycra hammocks. They are incredibly stretchy so we layer two to give it more tension for supporting kids of all sizes.

We use the first yard I got to help with stretching and it is perfect. So pleased with these!

I am so impressed with this set for the price! You get a ton of clay tools and accessories plus there is a great variety of colors for kids to choose from.

This is an addition to my therapy art room and my clients love it so far.

The clay feels smooth but somewhat “foamy” is which is actually pretty nice feeling. It doesn’t stick to hands which is a must for my clients with ASD and sensory processing needs.

The clay dried as it should and I’ve had no issues with it cracking.

This is an awesome set! I’ve gotten a few craft sets as a play therapist and this is my absolute favorite!

It has everything you need to do a wide range of crafts. I particularly love the google eyes and the variety is so nice. I’d never seen ones with “eyelids” and these are so cute and give my clients more choices and options for their puppets and creations!

The scissors are so nice and comfortable for smaller hands. The range of materials is great and the quality of the pieces is on par with the kits sold at Michaels for much less. Great value and variety.

I absolutely love this set!

Beads like these are a must have when working with kids and this is so easy to travel with or put in different offices depending on my clients needs that day. Everything fits great in the case and it comes with so many different types of beads. The case even fit most of my other beads (though I have a lot). Everything in this is a great quality and feels like it will hold up to even my more enthusiastic clients.

This is a great gift or a just supply to have on hand for school based or outpatient therapy work.

I am overall very pleased with this chalk market set. There are so many colors so we have a lot of options to use these.

For the most part, they are decently pigmented. Some are thinner than others. These wash off well for the most part. The only ones I’ve noticed any difficulty in clean up with are the metallic ones, they can leave behind a shimmery residue that is a little harder to remove 100%.

The set up is a little tedious as you really have to shake these a ton and pump the tip several times to get it going. Once you’re started though it’s a breeze.

One thing to note is that the tip shapes are not consistent. Some are rounded and some are the chisel type (shark point). This isn’t an issue for me but depending on what you’re using it for, you may have a preference.

I am happy with these for the price and the variety of colors.

I love this silicone mat, it is easy to clean and is a great size. We used this over the weekend to carve a pumpkin and it made cleanup a snap, even with a toddler. The color is really pretty and true to the pictures on the listing. This would be great for most craft projects and protects your surfaces.

It is a great thickness that I would feel comfortable using sharps with it and not damaging my table. The bottom is somewhat sticky to keep it from sliding around while working with it. The top is matte and feels smooth to the touch.

Great value for the money! These are great for many different uses!

We use them for fine motor activities in occupational therapy and they are fantastic! Our kids love them because they are so cute and tiny. I also used them in a few crafts and they are awesome.

They are tricky to get back together if they come apart but love these!

This is a great value and very giftable if you are looking for a present for an artsy kiddo, teen, or adult. I love Mandala art because it is so peaceful and calming. There is something so satisfying about seeing a completed piece and this set has an impressive set of dotters, stencils, and art supplies. I did take a star off because the little easel it comes with is kind of a joke for actual use. Maybe it could be a display stand for a finished piece but it does not seem high enough quality to be functional.

Overall, I am very pleased with this and the ability to make peaceful art to relieve stress and anxiety.

This set is a great gift for a beginning painter. It comes with a set of 12 small paint tubes, a white plastic palette, 4 flat canvas boards, a set of brushes, and a cute mini easel.

The paints are nicely pigmented and you get a decent variety of colors for the price. They are thick and smooth to work with.

The palette is surprisingly thick and very reusable. I anticipated it being a flimsy thin one but this is nice quality.

The easel is just held together with tiny nails/brads so I don’t know how well it will hold up in actual use. It is cute for displaying paintings on when their done.

The canvases are pretty standard, like the little ones you can find at Michaels. The brushes are decent and it’s a nice spread of brush styles to try out.

Overall it is a nice value and would make a great gift or kids activity.

This gun only comes with scissors there is no other piece to turn it into a looping gun though the description says it’s a duel tufting gun that you can switch from cut to loop. I received a replaceable scissor rather than the tool needed to turn it into a loop gun. Therefore three stars for the value of the money I now have to purchase a separate gun that is just for loop.
Not worth the price. As you can see by the pictures absolutely useless. Cheaply made. Not worth the price.

I waste more of this than I should have to. It does weed easily but it does not stick well to the transfer tape that comes with it. You get 1 12x12 piece of strong adhesive transfer tape that is NOT enough for the whole roll. You can only use it 2 times before it will not stick AT ALL. It is very pretty  under epoxy but a nightmare to work with. I charge a premium price up grade due to this products cost and difficulty to work with.

I have zero complaints about the turner. It is slower than my other ones but I prefer that. Must easier to work on a design due to its slower rotation.
Now for the reason I gave it 3 stars. You are given a 3 prong holder that does NOT work. It is not sturdy at all. Someone has to hold it or you have to try to tape it down then fill all 3 prongs but is still very unstable. Why did you not give it 4 posts rather than 3 and tweak the length of it to be sure it will not topple when adding only 1 cup. Its like you are not a tumbler maker or did not test your product before releasing it to be sold. Same with the hard white foam. It is NOT usable in 30oz cups at all it is slick and slips out of the cups due to its hardness and gloss. Switch to the large black soft foam that fits 20oz-35 oz easily. I would simply buy the turner for 15 bucks rather than be sold extra products I can not use. Up grading and raising the prices 5 bucks would so be worth it to me.
Its better than canon.

The main reason I bought this was for the storage case for my resin diamonds. The plastic clear storage lids do not stay closed. When dumping out the color of diamonds I want Ive had a lid on another color pop open and causing the 2 colors to mix. So I do not recommend this kit because of that.
I have used this so much. It speeds up the felting and when making cat ears that must be thin this works so fast. Every needle felter or wet felter needs this tool.

I struggled with this piece wanting to quit. It is beautiful but not enjoyable. The tiles would not stay stuck. I was constantly pushing them down losing pieces etc. I hate double sided tape for diamond painting as it sometimes will not remain stuck to the canvas. Had I known it was not poured glue I would not have purchased this.
Its thick corse and perfect for felting. Not uniform tho and flat.
I love anything mini and these scissors are the best. Super sharp, heavy. Not cheaply made. You will love them.

This is not  the same quality of siser glitter I have purchased in the past. This was thinner and did NOT stick to the carrier sheet. I ruined an entire sheet trying to use this. I will never buy from this seller again. I had a piece of easy weed left I compared it and it was very different. Either I got an old batch YES it does go out of date. It was horrible to work with would not weed. IT was just horrible.

this is my FAVORITE go to vinyl transfer paper. I used it for my clear glass round ornaments. I use it also for wet applications. I had horrible results with transferring large pieces to tile. I needed the clear with the grids for that.
Very small packets of clay. I expected larger for the price, but they’re not terrible

Great! Much better than elmers. It’s clear and really thick while elmers has a very visible yellow tint. Glue was delivered 2 days after purchasing! That is amazing and so so fast. I definitely recommend

I wasn’t trying to spend a ton of money making earrings for myself and my daughters so I went with this one. The clay didn’t bake up well at all. It did bake some after roughly an hour (on and off again in the oven) but no where near as it should. Still bendable which isn’t how it’s suppose to bake up. Worked well enough for us I guess but definitely do not purchase this if you’re planning on making to sell anything. I ended up just ordering other clay that I’ve worked with in the past. It does come with a decent color selection. Be careful as I had about 3 packs that was opened (looked kinda like a mouse had chewed into it) but not enough or bad enough to complain really.

This well-constructed professional portfolio is perfect for delivering or storing works of art (or any moderately-sized rigid item such as presentation charts or display signs.) Moisture-resistant, it's built to last and keep your carefully crafted pieces safe. It arrives folded and can be stored that way between uses (also handy if you are dropping pieces off and don't need to bring them back. Folded, it would fit in a suitcase.)

Most artists can always use another one of these for storage, so it would make a nice gift.

For art or craft applications, this is a good basic set. The tubes are more generous than a lot of other sets and the set includes all the colors you really need. Others are easy to mix. The paint is student grade, but fine for most applications. It goes on smoothly, is nicely pigmented, and mixes well with other brands.

Beginners and teens interested in art would enjoy this as a way to experiment with the medium without a huge investment. They can discover which colors they like best and play with mixing and layering.

Like all acrylics, these paints clean up with soap and water (though I recommend wearing old clothes or covering up. Acrylic dries fast.)

A nice gift for crafters, students, or adults interested in having fun with paints.

If you are expecting Rembrandts, these will disappoint. HOWEVER--these brilliantly saturated, generously-sized sticks are not hard either. They are in between and are a lot of fun. Like all pastels, they vary in hardness. Some colors will layer and others won't. You just have to experiment.

They aren't waxy, but they aren't as dusty as the premium brands. The colors are rich and gorgeous. Paper with the right tooth is even included so you can experiment. It even works with water (don't overdo that.)

Beginners and artists who want to experiment with the medium will have a lot of fun with this set. Don't expect them to be like premium "soft pastels." Accept them for their advantages and you will be happy. Hey, they are a bargain :-).

This gesso is everything you want to seal a canvas or other surface before painting in acrylic or oil. (Yes, you can paint in oil over acrylics, but not the other way around.) It is just the right thickness for brushing out and has excellent covering power. This relatively small bottle is handy for touching up the edges and backs of gallery wrap canvases or for preparing small wood panels. This size is also good for tying out the brand before purchasing a larger container of this excellent paint. I like it.

These paints are thick enough to hold brushstrokes if you want them to. They cover well, and the hues are saturated. (Sometimes sets like this have a lot of white in most of their colors. There are a lot of beautiful tints included here, but the reds and the oranges, for example, are true hues. (I especially LOVE the turquoise. It's gorgeous.)
The tubes are bigger than those in many sets. They are great for classes or Plein air. They are also perfect for people experimenting with acrylics for the first time. (Just be sure to take a sharp pencil with you to break the foil seal on each tube the first time. I suggest they include a point inside the top of each lid to pierce it, but that's a small thing, and a pencil does the trick.)
This set would make a great gift for a teen, a hobbyist, a crafter, or an artist who has always wanted to try acrylics. Include some canvases, a set of brushes, and a card listing the addresses of online lessons. If you've never used acrylics, you will be delighted to learn that they don't smell. they clean up with water, and, depending on how you apply them, they can look very much like oils.
These paints are amazing for the money. I can say this because I have used the very best.

Perfect for creating clean edges in illustrations or protecting the sides of a gallery wrap canvas, this set of easy-lift acid-free tapes is a great addition to any artist's studio. I especially appreciate the variety of widths included.

The acrylic paint in these tubes is student-grade medium viscosity, slightly on the fluid rather than thick side. If you like to paint on site, these tubes are the perfect size for your kit. they are large enough for several sessions, but not too heavy to lug around.

The black and white set would be a great addition to a set of smaller tubes of acrylic paints intended as a gift for a beginner, student, or teen. I've painted with major brands of acrylics for years, and I like these. Keep in mind, they're less pigmented than professional grade paints (as all student-grade tubes are,) but that can be an advantage if you are aware of it.

Use these adhesive dots anywhere you would use double-stick tape, except on painted surfaces. It sticks firmly but comes off if you need to move something (except from painted walls--it doesn't tear or damage paper, but will pull off paint!) Use it to mount photos in scrapbooks, make cards, create posters, and more. This generously-sized package contains enough mounting dots in different sizes to last a long time. A resealable, flat plastic bag easily fits in a desk drawer or craft kit to keep them neat and handy.

My husband found an old framed miniature landscape of mine and put it on this little easel and it looks amazing. This is a great way to show off any miniature picture or framed keepsake. It is made just like a full-sized wooden artist's easel.

Miniature works are popular in many galleries and online now. If you've picked up a special little painting on vacation or are framing an original art greeting card, you might consider one of these. I love ours.

These are wonderful brushes for acrylics. They have just the right amount of resilience and they hold a good quantity of paint. The bristles do not come out and the handles are nicely finished. The filbert shape allows for wonderful control and versatility, as does the large variety of sizes included in this generous set. Price is always a factor when using acrylics, especially on canvas. Chemicals in the paint and the texture of the support are murder on even the best brushes. These perform beautifully and you don't have to worry about wearing them out.
This set would make a nice gift for an acrylic artist, a student, or a teen interested in painting.

The line produced by these black acrylic pens is similar to that produced by a bold-point felt-tipped writing pen, which makes them versatile. they can create outlines, create accents, do lettering, trace stencils, or fill small areas.

Good for students, crafters, and artists.

Crafters, especially rock painters, will love the unusual range of pastel colors. (Colors mixed with a lot of white look gorgeous on dark rocks and other dark surfaces. Bright, pure hues too often do not show up.) There are dark and bright colors in this generous set too. The acrylic ink flows freely and is well pigmented. The light colors show up over darks. The "brush" tips make expressive, lyrical thick and thin lines. Works on a wide variety of surfaces.

Gorgeous professional natural-hair brushes in must-have shapes for watercolor and ink artists. These are beautiful brushes, designed to hold the maximum amount of paint while affording amazing control. They come in an elegant box for gift-giving and storage.

A wonderful gift for an advanced amateur, student, or professional artist--if you can bear to part with them.

This is an amazing set of pigment ink markers. The finest points are really fine and there is also a fairly bold chisel point and a "brush" tip for a variable line. While the ink is waterproof when dry, drying isn't instant and may depend, to some degree on the support. When the ink is still wet, it will smear.

This set includes most of the round brushes an acrylic painter will need. There is even a #24! There are also some very small ones for fine detail. The bristles are springy and responsive. They are attached firmly in metal ferrules, and don't seem to shed. The long wood handles are nicely finished too. Acrylics are brutal on brushes, so I appreciate the bargain price of this set.

Good for acrylic artists at all levels of experience.

These cradled panels are smooth and nicely finished. They are ready for gesso if you want to use them for artwork. The cradles are not deep, but the wood is nice--not heavily grained, so you could stain it , leave it raw, or paint it. The backs of the cradle supports are wide enough to accept hanging hardware, so art created on these panels would not require a frame.

Crafters--you could also use these to create serving trays. They are lightweight, yet sturdy.

Great for artists and crafters!

This generous set includes a wide range of beautiful colors. The acrylic ink flows freely and dries quickly (keep the caps on all acrylic pens). The tips have finer points than many acrylic pens. You can use them to write on your projects, The pastel colors work well even on dark rocks. Some of the other colors are more transparent. If you want to use a bright or dark color and it isn't showing up on your dark rock, do that part of the design in white first, and then add your color over the top.

This set would make a terrific gift for your favorite rock painter, but most crafters would find uses for it.

These bold stencils produce simple capital letters one inch tall. You can use them for tracing or with sponge tools and paint or ink. The slick, transparent (milky) semi-flexible plastic wipes or rinses off easily (if you care.) You can see through the stencils so it's easy to place the letters where you want them. Each letter stencil is separate, not grouped together on one sheet. This makes them easier to arrange and use.

Good for all craft and art applications. Sturdy enough for kids. Elegant enough for mixed media artists.

So far, I've tried these pens on smooth stones and on canvas (over acrylic paint.). The ink flows freely and is opaque over dark colors. The reservoir is generous and the pens, though not refillable, last a long time. The points are firm and make a medium-width (not fine) line. They are also good for filling in designs. Start them (as with all such pens) by pressing down on a different surface (not your project.) They haven't flooded out on me as some others have, but it's always better to err on the side of caution.

A very good set of paint pens for multimedia artists, stone painting hobbyists, and crafters.

Great for model makers, stencil-cutters, collage artists, and anybody who uses craft knives, this set has an innovative flat plastic handle that won't roll on your workbench but is easy to hold. The 6 replaceable tip units are all the same, but the size is versatile and should work for most needs. They are VERY sharp and will make clean cuts in hobby woods, paper, cardboard, and thin plastic. (Be careful) The storage case is transparent and compact enough to slip into a kitchen "everything-drawer" or a toolbox. Handy for yourself or a gift for a crafty friend.

Great for my Plein Air painting bag, this assortment of short-handled brushes has all the detail sizes I will need for painting on site this summer. The bristles are resilient and firm enough for my beloved acrylic paints, and the set is inexpensive enough that I don't have to be distraught when they wear down on rough canvas. I haven't tried them with watercolors or gouache, but they would probably be fine if you like firm rather than soft brushes. This set would make a great gift for any artist, especially an acrylic painter. Even artists who have a lot of brushes can always use more. Great set.

I love watercolor pencils even though I don't always use water with them. They are softer than standard colored pencils and lay down brighter, richer colors. Every brand is a little different. These are super. The color is sumptuous and they take sharpening, even to a point (which could vary by color. I haven't tried them all yet.) They come in a handsome three-'tray metal box that slips easily into a Plein Air bag, backpack, or suitcase.

Whether you are an illustrator, designer, artist, hiker, naturalist, scientist, mapmaker, journal-keeper, or just a kid who loves color, you'll find this set a useful (and inexpensive) addition to your kit.

I love to use this type of nonabsorbent surface for watercolor.  It's easy to get rid of mistakes and bring back brilliant whites. I do abstract backgrounds to warm up. Then I photograph them, wipe them off and they are fresh for the next time, so, while the these sheets are a little more expensive than paper, you can use them again. You can also use the backs. These "papers" don't buckle under the wettest washes or yellow. Turning a wet watercolor on its side creates marvelous runs. In fact, all wet-on-wet techniques work differently when none of the paint soaks in. You can also use these synthetic sheets for acrylic and mixed media, though I love them so much for watercolor, I haven't tried anything else. Artfinity Synthesis sheets are matte and 74lb is more substantial than it would be with paper. This pad compares very favorably with the other brand I have used.

These pens are great for fine artists, graphic artists, and illustrators. They have super-fine metal (I think) tips with free-flowing ink in a selection of beautiful colors. They are perfect for writing or adding fine detail to drawings or designs. Both ends of each pen show the color at a glance. The tight-fitting caps have clips if you want to keep a few in your shirt or work apron pocket. A professional set from a company with a growing thoughtful line of products for artists.

This beautiful kit fits easily among books on a student or artist's shelf. The zippered case encloses a nice sketchbook, a range of drawing pencils from H to 8B. charcoal sticks, graphite sticks in 4 hardnesses, a white vinyl eraser, 3 charcoal pencils, a soft woodless graphite pencil, a sandpaper stick, hand sharpener, and craft knife to manage points, three pulp smudgers in different sizes, a triangular ruler/straight-edge, and a holder that helps you use your favorite pencils down to the last inch.

It's a very nice set and would be an appreciated gift for students of any age. It's nice for artists too, especially those who have limited studio space or like to sketch on the go.

This is a very creative design and is terrific for makeup brushes, which have short handles. Pencils, including watercolor pencils do fit, as well as shorter versions of water reservoir brushes. Unfortunately, the case is too short for even the shortest-handled watercolor or acrylic brushes.

The design is really cool. The idea is, you carry the brushes in the pouch, then, when you want to use them, you unzip the pouch, push down the top part (which slides down over the lower section, and voila, the brushes, pens, or pencils are held in place, but easy to access.

If you want a case for makeup brushes, it's great. I was disappointed that it wasn't tall enough for my art brushes, but it's fine for my watercolor pencils.

I use fine permanent markers to refine pencil sketches before adding watercolors in my Plein Air landscape notes. These have worked well for me. The line quality is perfect for sketching. The points aren't super-fine, but they are thin enough for most detail and hold up well. The ink doesn't soak through the sketchbook paper I have used. (You can see it on the other side of copy paper, however. It doesn't soak all the way through, but it is visible enough to be a problem if that matters.) It doesn't bleed when you add watercolor over the top. I haven't tried it with alcohol markers.

Artists, illustrators, and students will find them useful.

Fill the wells of the plastic palette with your favorite tube watercolors, snap on the lid, snap it back onto the top of the collapsable water bucket, grab a water bottle, a pad of paper, a couple of brushes and go. This clever little silicone unit comes with a gorgeous strap that hooks into two of the brush holders on the side of the bucket. Wonderful for plein air painting, for class, or for a kitchen table studio. It's easy to carry, easy to use, and a cinch to store. Great for artists on the go, students, and teens.

The latch on the palette unit takes a little getting used to. That's the only thing I had trouble with.

These beautiful stencils can be cleaned and used again and again. Almost any kind of paint will work with them. Experiment to see whether sponges, spray, or brushes work best for your project. (As in most things, practice makes perfect.)

The designs are classic. Use them to create artworks or to decorate furniture, fabrics, totes, floors, or walls. The possibilities are limited only by your imagination.

They are also great for tracing. Older children might enjoy tracing them on paper, then coloring their designs with markers.

This set includes a wide range of beautiful colors, two extra tubes of white in a larger size, and even a set of brushes. The student-grade paints are comparable to other student-grade tube acrylics. They are mostly medium viscosity and cover well. Some colors were a little thinner and more transparent than others. This is not unusual.

The brush and tube sizes are intended for small works, but there's enough paint here for a surprising amount of exploring. The set would make a great gift for crafters, teens, adult beginners, or anyone who just wants to play with acrylic without making a big investment. The paint, like all acrylics, thins and cleans up with water and works on many surfaces including canvas, paper, metal, stone, ceramic, and glass.

Lots of great instructional videos are available for free online. Have fun!

Many acrylic pen sets feature pastels because people use them on dark-colored rocks and the added white makes them show up better. If you use paint pens for other purposes, as I do, you will find this set useful. The colors are rich and bright. The ink flows easily into the fine tips (once started) and covers well. This set filled in my palette.

Buy this for yourself if you are a rock painter, card maker, illustrator, or crafter. Also great as a gift for teens.

I've tried out these paints on paper and canvas. They compare well with other tube acrylics I have used. They are highly-pigmented, smooth, and easy to use. They are not heavy-bodied, so if you want impasto effects, these wouldn't be the best choice. They aren't thin either, though. If you want transparent layers, it is easy to mix them with gloss acrylic medium on your palette. (This is also true of other standard acrylic brands in tubes.)

The size of these tubes makes them perfect for taking out into the field or into a class. They are big enough for a few good-sized paintings, but not cumbersome. Once their inner shipping seal is broken, flip-top plastic lids make the tubes easy to open and close.

Fine for students, teens, and children. These paints are also good for Plein air acrylic artists at any level. The price is great too.

This budget-priced set gives a beginner everything necessary to explore the possibilities of watercolor. Generous pans of beautiful color are nested in a nicely-crafted metal case with a palette lid. There's only room for a detail brush inside the case with the paint, but two great water brushes are included in the set.

An included watercolor pad gives you a chance to experiment with the paints. It says it is "cold pressed," but the paper has a linen-like texture on one side and is smooth on the other.

The metal case is a bit large for travel, but it's good for Plein air adventures. The colors are great for landscapes. There are beautiful greens, blues, golds, and darks.

Children, teens, students, crafters, and adult beginners will enjoy exploring the possibilities of the medium with this set.

Any artist, student, or crafter would appreciate this beautiful set of watercolor pencils. The leads are richly pigmented and softer than wax-based pencils. It's easy to use a water brush (or a regular brush) to soften the lines, or even convert a drawn fill to a wash.

It's freezing cold here in the winter, but nice when the sun shines through my car windows. These are great for doing Plein air studies from the passenger or even driver's seat. I may also use them in multimedia sketchbook experimentation. They will combine well with the other water-based media I use for those.

Illustrators will find the soft, rich leads useful for broad areas of color either wet or dry. Crafters will love using them to create cards. With these at home, students will create impressive projects with ease. The simple metal case has unobtrusive branding on the outside and three lift-out layers of pencils inside. It's well made and should last for years. (The pencils are a standard size and could be replaced with any other brand. If giving as a gift, consider buying a pencil holder so your recipient can use favorite colors down to the very last inch.)

These natural-hair brushes are nicely crafted and come in a gorgeous presentation box. The variety of sizes is great for someone just starting out in watercolor. You might want to add a wide flat brush for washes, a fan brush, and a liner, but that would make a perfect starter kit. For the price, these are amazing. They have good points, and the bristles don't fall out.

These pads are not paper textured like canvas. They are actual fine-textured lightweight canvas sheets glue bound in a pad with a sturdy cardboard back, so if you paint outdoors, you don't need a drawing board. Each generously-sized sheet has a slight tooth. It's not a smooth as hot-press paper, but not as textured as cold-pressed. If you like to lift and layer your paint, it's amazing. It can take more punishment than paper. The sheets are lightweight and do buckle a little but would flatten when framed or mounted.

Many watercolorists are moving toward showing work without glass by mounting their paintings on cradled wood panels. If you are one of them, it would be worth giving this novel support a try.

These pads are specifically created for watercolorists, but would also work well for colored pencil, pastel pencil, gouache, ink, and mixed media techniques. I've tried a few things with the first sheet and am looking forward to playing with it more, exploring all of its possibilities. It's similar to watercolor canvas panels, but in a pad.

This set of 20 acrylic markers is great for rocks, ceramics, and crafts, but it's also good for adding detail, text, drawing elements, and signatures to fine art on wood panels, paper, and canvas. You do have to be careful about making sure the ink has saturated, but not over-saturated, the marker tip before using it on a piece of work (do this on a piece of scrap.) It is tricker on rough surfaces and absorbent surfaces like canvas and paper than it is on smooth river rocks,, sealed wood, metal, glass, or ceramic, but the markers do work on these surfaces. It just takes a little experimentation and practice. I'm not terrific with fine brushes, so it's worth it to me. The color range of the set, as you can see from the product photos, is beautiful. A few metallics are included for those who want to make special cards and decorations. This set would make a wonderful gift for crafters and the price is a bargain. I haven't used them long enough to run out, but if you keep the caps on, they should last for quite a while.

These cradled hardboard panels (compare to Masonite) are less expensive than similar ones made of more expensive materials. The supports around the back are smooth to the touch (no splinters or burrs), but wild-grain pine rather than costlier wood. However, the panels are very nicely finished and gessoed. You might even like the grain on the sides. You can leave the frame/braces natural, stain them, or paint them. Add wires and they will be ready to hang. These panels are a good deal. They will save you a lot of time sanding and sealing. Great for multimedia artists, especially those who enjoy working small. They're fine for layers of paint, collage, scratching, sanding, and practically any abuse you want to dish out. Have fun!

Whether you use this ingenious little tool to trace a border or to tear a deckle-like edge, you'll wonder how you ever made cards or tags without it. It's well-made and easy to use. Would make a great gift for a crafter, if you can bring yourself to give it away.

This beautiful handmade paper adds elegance to any project. One side is softer and more absorbent than the other. Both are rich. Use these deckle-edged sheets to mount small artworks for special gifts, create are directly on them, or make calligraphic mottos or quotations. However you use them, you will be delighted with the quality, and the value.

These beautiful see-through drawing boards arrive meticulously packed so they aren't damaged in transit. They are large and a good weight with attached quality clips to hold paper in place. They would make an amazing gift, if you can part with them. Great for professional artists. cartoonists, or illustrators, as well as serious students. A special addition to any studio.

These scissors are smaller than the fabric-shear-size I usually buy. I've found them easier to stash in bathroom drawers, art bags, and the car. They are nice and sharp and easy to use. A very handy set of scissors.

Great for yourself or to give someone special, these amazing palette knives are great quality and made out of actual metal. They are sturdy, springy, and flexible. They're perfect for mixing paint as well as application to wood, canvas, or other surfaces. I use acrylic paint, but they would be just as good for oils. It's so hard to find real metal palette knives now. Most are plastic. These will last for years. For professional artists or students of all ages. Terrific investment.

This is a giftable set of pencils for teens, crafters, and artists. I love soft, dark graphite pencils and most sets have mostly hard leads. This set is stocked with lots of Bs--up to 12B in fact and three extra-rich darks. There are two professional erasers and a sharpener, but pros will like the sanding stick and knife with breakaway tips for precision pointing. Blending stumps in three sizes are included for perfect grays.
There are a dozen watercolor pencils in basic colors plus a few pastels that cover most landscape and portrait needs. (A small watercolor brush is included, but you might want to buy a water brush if you don't already have one for field work.) There are a dozen oil-based colored pencils (my new love) in jewel tones, a dozen metallic pencils for creating stunning cards, and a dozen very nice standard wax-based well pigmented colored pencils. There's even a pencil extender that fits all the pencils so you can use every last beautiful inch.
The case is amazing. Sturdy elastic straps hold all the components in place. Zip it up, and everything stays where it belongs, ready for your next use. It's about the size of a book when you keep it on a shelf.
a handy strap on the "spine" makes it easy to pull out and carry.
This set was thoughtfully designed for artists or anyone who loves colored pencils. It's great for adult coloring, student assignments, card making, illustration, and more. I love it.

I love this beautiful storage case! I filled it with my collection of watercolor markers. Now it's easy to find the colors and tip styles I need.

Its rectangular design allows it to sit on a shelf, while the interior dividers hold my markers in place with stitched elastic and a zipper fastens the outside. There's also a sturdy carrying handle on one end.

Any artist who uses markers or colored pencils will love this case. It would also make a great gift for an art student, illustrator, or adult coloring book enthusiast.

At less than $5 a generous bottle, these standard viscosity (not liquid or heavy-bodied) acrylics are a bargain. There's a beautiful array of mixable colors including primaries, secondaries, black, and white,

The squeezable bottles have reclosable flip dispenser tops making the paint easy to dispense. The set arrives in a nice-looking sturdy cardboard box appropriate for both storage and gifting.

The paint itself is fun to work with and compares favorably with familiar brands.

The set would be great for older children, teens, artists, and crafters. Like all acrylics these paints work well on many surfaces including paper, metal, stone, and wood.

The oil base of these beautiful marking tools makes their color go on rich and brilliant, even on darker supports. They layer wonderfully too.

The spectacular array of pencils stores neatly in a nicely crafted metal box with a handsome cover design. Artists, illustrators, teens, crafters, sketch-bookers, and more will love this set. I love mine!

I ordered these stencils to enhance my paintings and drawings. They are great for either tracing or using with media. Some of the designs are harder to keep in place than others, especially the wavy ones because the plastic is soft and flexible, but, like any tool, practice makes perfect. In general, I think they are better used flat than vertically. Most of the designs could be tiled, but it would take more skill than I have.

For my paintings and drawings, they are the best set I have found. I use parts of them and combine them. Because they are generic geometric shapes, there's no coyright problem.

I ordered this easel for an exhibit, and it is great for showing paintings, drawings, or presentation posters, but I love it for painting too. The strap on the zipper case makes it easy to sling over your shoulder so you can walk to the best outdoor painting locale. It sets up in a flash and is remarkably stable as long as you aren't in a gale. It holds a BIG painting, but I usually just do 16 x 20 acrylics and it's great. The locking sectioned legs work when you are at a table, seated in a camp chair, or standing.

It's amazing for Plein air work, but also for small home studios. Take it down and tuck it on a closet shelf when you aren't working. Folded up, it's really small. It isn't the lightest easel I have, but I'm an--well--not spring chicken, and I don't have any trouble carrying it. Its weight makes it a little steadier.

Verdict? I love it! It's an amazing buy for the price and would make a great gift for a student or artist.

I love the pouches these nice acrylics come in! There's an amazing amount of medium-viscosity paint in each one. The colors mix beautifully and these eighteen hues make anything you need (in fact, I usually take only four or five when I go out painting.)

The colors are rich and beautiful. The paint is smooth, but not fluid. It compares with most standard (not-heavy-bodied) tube brands. It is a little more transparent than some brands, but that can be an advantage if you work in layers.

I like this set very much. The price makes it great for students and beginners, but artists just trying painting outdoors will like it too.

My husband I both love this unique tool. I was able to read the itty bitty model number on the back of my new iPad with ease. I couldn't even see the thing with my regular reading glasses. He's a model railroader so he works with tiny parts and he's older than dirt.

The only downside of this great tool is its weight. It's a bit heavy if you have weak wrists. It's not that heavy though. We both love it and I don't know how they'd make it lighter because the magnifyling glass is really powerful and the lens is thick.

I love the lively line quality created by these beautifully-crafted pens. Illustrators and fine artists will prize them. They are water-based, but not watercolor pens. Once dry (fast) they don't run. Use other media to create washes. They are perfect for lettering, calligraphy, and drawing. They create a delicate, but intuitive line. The colors are beautiful and rich, but my next set will just be black.

They come in a handy plastic case and would make a great gift for artists and illustrators, crafters, bullet journal-keepers, scrapbookers, students, and more. A high-quality set.

I love these quality medium-body acrylic paints! The set includes all my favorite colors, including yellow ochre, burnt sienna, phthalo green, and cobalt blue, as well as brilliant basics. The paints are richly pigmented and creamy. The set is an amazing bargain. The bottles are just the right size--bigger than the largest tubes but not as unwieldy as gallon jugs. (You can refill them from jugs.) They compare well, in my opinion, with more familiar brands. Great for students and beginners, of course, but I'm an artist, and I like them very much. (I also like Milo thicker-bodied paints in jars.)

I do fine art (which lately includes original stencils and collage) and my husband is a model railroader who creates buildings and other HO scale detail. When this set arrived, we were both delighted. There were two handles, two knives with break-away blades, and lots of extra blades for the handles in handy little vial-like containers. There's also a beautiful metric metal ruler/straight edge to use as a cutting guide and even a thick cutting mat.

My husband and I are delighted with this collection. If you buy it as a gift, you will probably want another box.. The one it comes in is soft-ish cardboard which was smoshed in shiment. (Nothing inside was damaged at all and it's fine for us, but cosmetically it didn't rise to the quality of the contents.

I'm doing more collage work these days and this tool is like nothing else in my collection of art supplies. The plastic shaft is beautifully designed to let you see what you are doing as you make your cuts. The fine, sharp edge swivels to follow your hand's direction. It even comes with spare blades.

When you assemble it initially, look for the square hole in the little black piece with the ball shape on one end. That's where the knife unit fits in. You have to push. Thank goodness for the reusable rubbery cap over the very sharp blade. The ball end snaps into the handle and you're ready to go.

This is a beautiful, practical set of brayers for printmakers and multimedia artists. The brayers are well made and the sizes are perfect for most applications. I'm using them for painting abstract acrylics on wood, paper, and canvas. Brayers were suggested in a class I'm taking and I'm glad they were. Acrylic is washable with water so they clean up easily. I haven't used them with oil-based colors, but they are standard rubber and I'm pretty sure they work the same as others of their design. Nice price for this set. They come in a nice, plain cardboard box and would be easy to wrap as a gift.

Most smallish watercolor sets don't have this many colors. The trays of watercolor pans swing out for use and tuck back in to the main unit. You can have one out at a time, or all of them. There's also a swing-out mini palette for mixing.

One of the swing-outs has landscape tones and rich darks. Another has brights and portrait colors. The third has pastels and metallics. The pans are generously sized and richly pigmented. When you use up the color, you can refill them with your own tube favorites. There's a handy silicone strap for carrying and a swivel thumb ring on the bottom for easy use. The whole thing comes in an elegant velvet-like drawstring case with a waterbrush. I love mine.

Get it for yourself, for a student, a beginner, or someone who wants to try watercolor, but doesn't have much space at home for a lot of equipment. A nice gift for mini-studio artists, Plein air painters, travel, crafters, card creators, teens, and more. Lots of fun in a clever little package,

This handy collection of small stencil sheets stores easily in my craft drawer. The designs are classic and easily combined for a variety of projects. I use them for multi-media compositions, but they are super for card makers, journal-keepers, scrapbookers, and crafters. The stencil material is extremely sturdy. The sheets can be used with any medium and reused.

Each of these beautiful markers has a flexible thick-to-thin fiber point for calligraphy and a firm, precision point for detailed drawing and writing.

The markers nest in five clear plastic trays inside a clear plastic case. It's easy to find the colors you need and put them back for easy retrieval next time.

Children, teens, crafters, scrapbookers, card creators, poster makers, designers, students, bullet journalists, adult coloring fans, and artists will love this set.

Great for holiday giving or for that box you keep in the closet for visiting kiddos. I'm using mine for sketchbooks.
Many artists are using wood panels for oil, acrylic, and mixed media works. These panels are a good deal. Sand them lightly, add a couple of coats of gesso, and you're ready to go.

This lightweight but sturdy portable easel is great for Plein air painting, chart displays for presentations, art shows, or even studio use, if you are in a small place and need to stash your painting gear in a closet between sessions. It's quick and easy to set up and the legs, once adjusted, stay in place, even on uneven surfaces. The only thing I didn't get was the crank-like thing for leveling.
