I am totally loving these bezels and I have made so many cute resin necklaces from these. They are super sturdy and are not easily bent at all. I love how they spin around and just make a more unique piece of jewelry because of that. These are great and I am totally getting more of these.
Loving this glitter so much. It mixes into resin very well and looks great so far in all my pieces. Couldn't be happier with it.

This resin works great for jewelry or other projects. Only takes a few minutes to cure and looks beautiful. I have used it to make many different things and also used it as a topper and I love it. It doesn't take a lot to get that beautiful clear top and cures in no time. No complaints at all with this resin.
Canvases are great and are just what I was wanting. I don't have to worry about preparing them ahead of time. When I am ready to paint these are ready to go. Couldn't be happier.

Love this diamond painting. I am taking my time with it but it's beautiful and all the drills are fitting great. It's easy to put together and is great. The glue seems to be good and I don't have any drills popping off or anything so far. I can't wait to get it finished and frame it. So beautiful and sparkly.

Love this diamond painting. I am taking my time with it but it's beautiful and all the drills are fitting great. It's easy to put together and is great. The glue seems to be good and I don't have any drills popping off or anything so far. I can't wait to get it finished and frame it. So beautiful and sparkly.

The glue seems to be good and I don't have any drills popping off or anything so far. I can't wait to get it finished and frame it. So beautiful and sparkly.

I've been using this resin for jewelry and it works great. I love the crystal clear look of this resin and it works great. I know it says it's for floral arrangements but it's good for jewelry and other resin crafts too. It's worked great and I couldn't be happier with it.

These ring sizers are awesome and are totally comfy to wear on all of my rings. I have been losing so much weight and I still love all my jewelry so much so being able to wear them even with so much weight loss. These are nice and snug and make the rings fit perfectly well.

This resin set is great and I have been loving it for helping me portioning out the glitters and other additions to the resin. They are super easy to clean and when mixing the resin as soon as it's dry it peels right off. These are great tools and they have come in handy so many times.

These bracelets are great and perfect for my jewelry making. These are beautifully made and can totally be worn on their own or what I do is make different charms and attach them anywhere on these bracelets. They wear great and stay in place. Very happy with them and great for gifts or for jewelry making.

I wanted these beads because I love the alphabet ones and they look awesome in resin pendants then I started adding in the small glass seed beads and they are just great. These are totally well done and work great for resin pendants and earrings but also for actual beading for necklaces and other kinds of jewelry. Love that they are all packaged in plastic boxes so that they are so much easier to work with.

This set is awesome and the cutting mat is just what I have been wanting. I do so many different crafts and this mat and cutter have been what I have been missing. The mat is super sturdy and even if it bends, after a while it bends back in shape. The cutter has been amazing too and just glides through paper, leather, and other materials without problems. Just such a great set and I totally love it.
These are great eye pen hooks. They work great in my resin pendants and hold really well. They are a perfect size and don't turn or rust over time so far.

Got this set for my little artist and he loves it. Not like he doesn't have so many drawing things but wanted to get him some new stuff and this looked to be a great set that he could take with him where ever he wanted to. Comes with everything you need to sketch and then complete any type of drawing you wanted. Love that everything comes in a great case that can be taken anywhere. My son said everything works great and he loves the colored pencils and said that they are some great quality for this all in one set. He also loved that it came with some charcoal and shading pencils because he had been needing some new ones. Overall, it's a great kit for my artist and I am sure it'd be great for anyone else too.

My son is a great little artist and we are always going through sketchbooks so he was in need of new ones and he can be picky, to say the least. Well, he loves these and he said they are working awesome. He can use pencils or his pastels on this paper and it works great and makes the colors look great on the paper. He loves the size of the sketchbooks too, as he can really go all out when it comes to his drawing and sketches. These are great for beginners but also for any artist.

I love diamond painting but the diamond tool can be a pain when you are working on a project for a while. This pen is great though and makes working on a project a lot less painful. The pen is much easier to hold and just really great quality.
Loving these alcohol inks so much and they work so well with all my resin projects so far. Couldn't be easier to use and totally will last a long time. Great inks for all my projects.

This set is working awesome for my necklaces I have been making. Love that it comes with tape to help keep the resin in place and I love the variety of bezels in this too. The flowers are beautiful too and will work great in my other projects.

First thing first, I hate these plastic snaps. They are horrible and I just really don't like them. That said, I love this kit lol I know, but it's the pliers that make me love this. It's all-metal along with the different sized tools and that alone makes this awesome. I can use this for so much more than just snaps too and have already used it to fix a purse of mine where the metal loop came undone. This made it super easy to fix and push and fasten the new loop thing in place. Despite not liking the plastic snaps, which I just don't like plastic snaps, the pliers are amazing, and what makes this kit worth every penny.

These canvases are great and I am loving just creating and using these for all of my creations. I've used oil and acrylic paints on these canvases and everything has been amazing on these. Love using them and will get more as I need them.

These colored pencils have quickly become such a favorite of mine. I love to color and these have been amazing and then use a water pen to turn these into watercolors, simply amazing. The colors are so incredibly vibrant and just look beautiful on their own, but then you add a little water and it changes the look completely and it's just beautiful. Love using these so much and the colors are just beautiful. Great set for colored pencils and an amazingly beautiful set for water colors.

This art kit is amazing and my son is loving every single thing in this kit. He is such a talented kid and he loves to draw and illustrate all kinds of different things. No matter where he goes he is always working on something. So this kit comes in its own case that stands up and everything is just so good. It has everything from charcoals, pastels that he loves to take out and make a complete mess with them, and then somehow out comes a beautiful work of art. Anyway, this set is great and I am so happy that my son loves it and loves the quality of the kit too. According to him, the quality is top-notch, so I'll take that lol.

My kids and I are loving this bead art kit. I make pixel art a lot anyway and my kids help me make cute things all the time to sell and to decorate with. So when I saw this kit I knew I had to get it and do it with my kids, because let's be honest we all need something to do in this climate we've got going on. Anyway, this kit was a lot of fun and we loved a lot of the fun pictures that were included with this kit and making the different things into keychains and even started making ornaments It comes with almost everything you need, must have your own iron, and is super fun and easy to make your own designs or use the ones you get in the kit. So much fun and the kids get to use their imaginations making their own designs.

This polymer clay kit is awesome. I have just started making all kinds of jewelry and stuff using polymer clay so I knew right away I had to try this kit out and I am not disappointed. All of the clay is so super soft and so malleable and works great for making all my charms for necklaces, bracelets, and earrings. I love the variety of colors that comes in this kit and then not to mention the other colors you can make by mixing them. The tools are great too. They are plastic but they work well with this clay and with the other clay that I use. I also love the storage case too because I can store my other clay in here and it keeps it airtight. Just such a great kit and I am super happy with it. I would totally get it again because of the colors you get. It's great.

This resin kit is awesome and literally comes with everything to start making beautiful resin earrings. Everything cleans up perfectly after making stuff and love that it comes with so many pretty add-ins too. Great set for the beginner for sure.

I make and sell my own earrings and these leather sheets are totally perfect. They cut so easily and just make the most beautiful jewelry pieces. So happy with all the colors and patterns in this set. So happy with these sheets.

I am loving this moldable glue. It's totally easy to use and sticks to everything I've used it on so far. I've been fixing all kinds of things from my crown molding to the handle on a glass. I've also started using it to make different designs and such as glasses. It's literally great for anything you need it for. I didn't know anything like this existed and now I can't believe I've gone so long without having it in my life. Totally crazy great stuff.

I've also started using it to make different designs and such as glasses. It's literally great for anything you need it for. I didn't know anything like this existed and now I can't believe I've gone so long without having it in my life. Totally crazy great stuff.

I didn't know anything like this existed and now I can't believe I've gone so long without having it in my life. Totally crazy great stuff.

These canvases are great. I've been painting for a while now and these are perfect for all my painting needs. They are already primed so they are totally ready to paint on. Love that I get different sizes too so I am ready to paint anything I want.

Honestly, I got this thinking it was a diamond painting but honestly, I am glad that it turned out to be a real paint by numbers painting. It's been so much fun painting again and painting by numbers makes it super easy for someone who's not so great at painting, to begin with. The picture itself is beautiful and I love it so much, I can't wait to get it finished and hung. Everything is very well put together, the paints are easy to work with, and the numbers are printed on the canvas are easy to read and cover very nicely with the paint. It's a great kit that I am loving. I am totally going to do more of these in the future.

This has been my go-to case for keeping all of my diamonds in for diamond painting. I love the convenient containers that this box comes with. They are the same as tic tac containers so it makes it easy to pour the diamonds out and put the diamonds back in the containers. It's a great storage box.

I've been doing diamond painting for a while now and sometimes you find yourself needing extra tools to make getting the diamonds to line up and making sure they are stuck down into place. This kit has everything you need to help place the diamonds, extra wax for the diamond pens, trays, line tools, and even a roller that you can use to help make sure the diamonds adhere to the canvas. It also comes with a divider box with small boxes inside to store all of your diamonds separately but together. Everything comes nicely packed in a zippered pouch where you can keep everything together. It's a great kit to have whether you are a beginner with diamond painting or have been doing it for a while as I have.

I have been doing diamond painting for a while now and I didn't know how much a light board would make doing the painting so much easier. It lights up the canvases so well and makes it super easy to see where you need to put the diamonds and keep everything lined up and looking beautiful. The light brightness can be adjusted to what you need it to be and the included clips are great at holding the canvas to the board without bending the paper or canvas. Loving this light board and it works great.

I love the diamond painting because they are just so fun and relaxing. Then when you are finished you are left with this beautiful work of glittery art that you can frame and hang on the wall. I actually very much love them and this one is just like all the others I have done before. The image is beautiful and the instructions are easy to follow. Totally recommend these for anyone who wants something fun to do.

This resin kit comes with everything you want to start making your own pendants, earrings, or just about any other jewelry you want. The molds are made of very good silicone and so far are holding up very nicely. I've not had any problems what so ever with the pieces not coming out of the mold or anything like that. It's also great that it comes with necklaces and earring findings so that you can start making jewelry right away. Just a great kit and I love it.

I have been getting back into painting lately and have been looking for a good beginner kit and I have found it in this kit. It comes with everything you need to start painting. The paints are great quality and so are the brushes. I have loved using everything in this kit and it's just wonderful for my beginning self.

These stoppers are great, super cute, and work great once you get it on the masks. However, getting them on the masks is a total pain. First, they give you bobby pins to push the string through these stoppers but the bobby pins are too big to go through the stoppers. So you have to bend the bobby pins or find something else to push the strings through the stoppers. Once you get them in though, the stoppers are great and I really like them. However, if you are going to include something to help put the stoppers on, at least make sure it works.
These charms are beautiful and just what I have been looking for when making my jewelry. I can use these for everything from earrings to bracelets. Perfect for all my crafts.

The cutter is just what I've been needing. I love making jewelry and leather earrings are a favorite but cutting the leather can be difficult to cut straight. This cutter works wonderful and cuts through the material like butter. Also, love that this cutter comes with replacement blades. Holds great in the hand and is comfortable to use.

This paint is so great. It's not watered down but goes on smooth and opaque. It isn't the cheap paint you get from Dollar Tree. This paint is great for all my crafts and layers beautifully. I also love the amount you get in each bottle. Perfect set for beginners but also older painters like myself. Loving this set of paints. Just beautiful.
These charms are beautiful and just what I have been looking for when making my jewelry. I can use these for everything from earrings to bracelets. Perfect for all my crafts.

Loving these acrylic markers a lot. They are easy to use and go on beautifully. They are perfect for doing touch-ups on other projects but also using them for all kinds of projects on their own. They work great for any project and I am having fun using them. I might even dare say that they are better than Pasca Pens.

Okay, gonna be honest here, when I first got this and opened the package, the amount of each color you get in the package is small and I mean, super small. Each block of color is roughly half the size of a block of Premio. The colors mix really well and don't stain your hands. The clay is easy to mold and stick together, even with other clay brands. It's easy to work with and holds details really well. When it's time to bake, it bakes just like any other clay. Doesn't shrink very much at all, or any as far as I could tell. It works really well for making jewelry and other things and I am really liking it. Just wish it came in bigger blocks.

Loving these super cute charms. They look great and are the perfect size for any of my jewelry crafts. I can use these for all of my jewelry and they will look amazing. Very happy with these and the quality. Great charms.

Loving these super cute charms. They look great and are the perfect size for any of my jewelry crafts. I can use these for all of my jewelry and they will look amazing. Very happy with these and the quality. Great charms.

Loving these super cute charms. They look great and are the perfect size for any of my jewelry crafts. I can use these for all of my jewelry and they will look amazing. Very happy with these and the quality. Great charms.

Loving these super cute charms. They look great and are the perfect size for any of my jewelry crafts. I can use these for all of my jewelry and they will look amazing. Very happy with these and the quality. Great charms.

This tie-dye kit is so great and seriously comes with everything you need to make some very nice tie-dye cloths. I am in love with the colors this kit comes with but totally wish the bottles were bigger because they are small, which doesn't allow you to do a lot of shirts or other fabrics. Loving the spray nozzles that come with this too. It's become my favorite thing in this kit tbh. Using the sprayers helps me to do a majorly awesome ombre on anything.

As I said before though, the only thing that really kills me about this kit is the small bottles. They are so smaller than I ever thought. All the colors are powders and already in the bottles, which makes mixing all the colors super easy and you don't have to worry about measuring anything. But I totally wish these bottles were bigger so that you could do so much more because depending on how big the item is, you aren't going to be doing a lot of pieces, well, unless they are kid clothes, but adult clothes, not a chance. Therefore, if you got more products in these, it'd be a top-notch kit for sure.

Loving this paint so much. It's working great for all my crafts and goes on smoothly. Loving the colors too and the beautiful metallic of these paints honestly makes me love them so much more. I love using these and they are great.

The paintbrush set is exactly what I was expecting. The brushes come in their own place and are beautifully made. They are easy to clean and love the wooden handles. Very nice to hold and use and apply paint beautifully. Love the palette knife too, and makes mixing paints quick and easy. It's also great to use to apply paint also. As far as the sponge, it's a sponge that you can use for whatever you want. Loving this kit and the storage case these beautiful brushes come in. The case keeps all the brushes together and the brush beautiful.

This brush cleaner is what I've been needing instead of just a glass or jar. This makes cleaning my brushes so much easier and this has a handle holder so that I can lift the brushes out of the water so that it doesn't ruin or get the brushes waterlogged. It's big enough to wash some of the biggest brushes but also the smaller ones too. It being stainless steel makes it easy to clean and just nice looking. The screen inside makes cleaning the brushes super easy and fast. A few rubs on the screen and the brushes are clean and ready to dry. Love this brush cleaner and so glad I got it. I didn't realize how much I needed one until I got it.

These bottles of foil flakes are awesome. They are super easy to use and apply to everything I want to foil. I've been doing loads of fall crafts and this has made foiling pumpkins and other things so easy. A little glue and this stuff is stuck anywhere you want. When finished just put the lid on and store it away. Fun to work with and makes everything look expensive and pretty.

Look, I don't care what this says the pictures make you think that you are getting sheets of gold leaf but instead you are getting jars with folded papers of gold leaf inside them, which then turns those beautiful sheets into flakes of gold leaf. If you are wanting actual gold sheets, these are not want you want and honestly, if you want flakes you don't want this either. I have huge bottles of flakes that cost less and you get way more in the bottles and less trash too. This product is a huge nope for me. I don't know who thought this was a good idea but it just isn't.

While these paint pens are exactly what they are, paint pens, they are so small and so much smaller than other paint pens I've used. These are more of literal paint pens that you'd use to sign cards, posters, or something like that. I use them to put finish the edges of my clay pieces and while it works great, they being so small I will have to get new ones in just a few weeks. These are wonderful for details in the artwork and things like that but not really the best for crafts like earrings and things like that. Otherwise, the colors are perfect, the markers are juicy and the paint flows perfectly from the pens. They are great but I wish there was more paint in these pens.
These rhinestones are beautiful and work so well with my hotfix pen. Love the different colors and totally easy to work with. Just what I wanted.
Love these charms for my jewelry. They are beautifully made and are easy to work with. Just what I was wanting and can't wait to make more earrings and bracelets from these.

I know a lot of people hate glitter but not me, I love the stuff and I use them in everything I craft if I can. These glitters are great but I wish they came in resealable packs. The glitters are beautiful and the colors are amazing. I love the different array of colors you get in this pack. However, these are hard to store in their original packaging because once they are open, you have to store them in something else. Otherwise, great glitter for all your crafts.

These flowers are beautiful and will work great in my resin crafts. They are perfectly dry and look just as beautiful in the pieces as they do in the actual package. These are also great for other crafts as well such as scrapbooks and card making. These flowers are beautiful and very well dried. Very happy with them.

Loving these paint pens so much. They are so beautiful and work great for coloring clay, rocks, and even wood. They are super juicy with the ink flowing wonderfully from these. They all have a beautiful metallic color to them and look great on so many different mediums.

This chainset is perfect for all my jewelry crafts. I love making my own pendants and stuff and this kit is perfect for turning them into beautiful necklaces. I can make them however long or short I need them to be. I can also make bracelets and anklets out of this chain too and it works great. Comes with everything you need to get started making your own necklaces.

This is also a stainless steel chain and findings so no worries about it turning your neck colors or rusting. It's also safe for people with metal sentitivites.

These canvases are awesome and I am loving them. I have been doing so much painting here lately but that means going through a lot of canvases and I am loving these so much more than the others I have been using. These are way easier to store, with them being panels and not the full-on canvases. These also are easy to paint on, just like any other canvas. They are ready to go as soon as you get them and I love using them. Very happy with these.

I do a lot of crafts that I use resin for and this resin works great. It makes beautiful jewelry and dries perfectly clear with no yellowing. It also works perfectly for putting clear coats on clay pieces as well. Easy to mix and use. Also, love that there isn't very much of an odor to this resin either, so no worries with using it inside.

These are great foil flakes. Not only have I used them in my resin pieces but I have used them to foil some wooden pieces too and this worked great. It's so beautiful and looks like the real thing. I am very impressed with these flakes and even more impressed that they don't discolor when in resin or covering them with glue or something else. The best part is they come in a large plastic bottle so it's easy to get the flakes out and easy to put the unused flakes back in.

I have been doing so much painting over the past few months. It's been a hobby of mine that I used to do a lot but just picked back up when covid hit. It's been so great but I needed some new paints as I am beginning to run out. I wanted to give these a try because of the range of color and I am so glad I did. The colors in this package are amazingly beautiful and they are so pigmented. The paints are so rich and go on paper and canvases so smooth. They also blend like a dream too. It's just such a great kit and I love these pants so much. I also love the practice pad that comes in this too. It was great to test the colors on and really see what each of them looked like. Just such a great set of pants for the price.

This is one of the best wood burning kits I have come across. It is perfect for beginners and totally comes with everything you need in order to start learning wood burning. It comes with so many different tools and tips that you can start making all kinds of stuff.

The instructions are easy to read and understand. It's easy to use and so much fun to use too. I am loving learning all of the different designs I can do with this and all the projects that I can do. The tool itself is also so super nice, easy to hold, and easy to read what the temp is and control.

This is such a great set for beginners and just such a great kit in general. Loving it so much and can't wait to start making some bigger projects.

These molds are great. There are many different designs to make earrings and pendants from. Easy to fill with whatever resin you are using. Once the pieces are dry they pop right out of the molds with no problems. Love the designs.

Flowers have been great for all my crafts. Love using them in my resin crafts. They go in beautiful and stay beautiful. These would also work great in scrapbooks as well. They are so beautiful and very happy with them.

Great resin molds. I've already been using them to make pendants and earrings and the molds are working great. The resin doesn't stick to them and the pieces pop right out without problems. Glitters are pretty and mix into the resin with no problems. Love the silicone mixing cups because once the resin dries it can be peeled right out, leaving the cups clean. Great kit for beginners.

These pigments are great. They mix into resin without problems or clumping. They don't come in ziplock bags, which would have made these so much better. However, they are still great and love that these come with a cute measuring spoon to help spoon out the pigments. Great vibrant colors.

Great kit that comes with everything you need to set snaps in all kinds of clothing. The grips are great and feel great in the hand. They are easy to use and love the variety of snaps and fasteners. Great kit to have.

Loving this rhinestone applicator. I have been putting rhinestones on everything from earrings to bags. It has worked awesome on my earrings that I have been making and while it does not recommend putting them on faux leather, I have and they have stuck on very nicely and make my earrings look so super pretty. Works awesome and love using it so much.

I am loving the leather sheet that comes in this pack. The variety of colors and textures are great. The tools are nice, nothing special but do what they should. The templates are what I have the biggest problem with. These templates they speak of is nothing but thin, flimsy, wood pieces that you use to trace on the leather pieces and then use scissors to cut them out. Now, if you are like me, cutting stuff out isn't as easy as normal people. I have horrible pain and inflammation in my hands and joints and it makes using scissors for a long time pretty impossible. So, having dies is the easiest way to do these types of crafts. Not these, easy to break, templates that will not last you a long time, at all. At least the jump rings and earrings come in their own packaging and it keeps everything separate.

While I do really like this kit and most of the things that come in it. I am very disappointed with the templates because they are not good quality at all and could have been done so much better.

These rhinestones are perfect for my jewelry making. I can put these on my earrings and just a little heat and they are stuck down and aren't coming off. I also love the size and color variation in this kit too. I have all the sizes to do anything I want. Such a great set for all your crafting.

This earring making kit is pretty awesome and way better than I expected it to be. The tools are right up there with the good tools and I love the hammer that comes with this set. It is very well made and feels great using it for long periods of time. I am also very happy with the die that comes with this kit also. It's easy to line up on the leather and use the hammer to cut out the pieces. Which speaking of the leather pieces, they are so pretty and each one is glittery, which makes me love them so much more.

So far this kit is so much more than I thought it would be and so much better too. It's great for beginners and experts alike. Just a great kit to start your earring making journey with.

I've been wanting to start making my own earrings and jewelry for a while. I make other types of jewelry and I knew this would be right up my alley. I am loving the leather sheets and the variety of colors and texters that come in this pack. The tools are also pretty great as well. While I have better tools because I make jewelry, these tools are anything to laugh about. The pliers are great, feel great in the hand, and are sturdy enough to bend wire and to help put the earrings together. The hammer is also great in this kit. I am impressed with the quality of it and I feel it is going to last a long time. I also like the dies too. I wish there was a variety of dies with this kit, but something that can just get you set up and started, this kit is pretty great with the dies. They are sturdy and work very well. Overall this is a great kit and totally recommend it for beginners.

I've been wanting to start making my own earrings and jewelry for a while. I make other types of jewelry and I knew this would be right up my alley. I am loving the leather sheets and the variety of colors and texters that come in this pack. The tools are also pretty great as well. While I have better tools because I make jewelry, these tools are anything to laugh about. The pliers are great, feel great in the hand, and are sturdy enough to bend wire and to help put the earrings together. The hammer is also great in this kit. I am impressed with the quality of it and I feel it is going to last a long time. I also like the dies too. I wish there was a variety of dies with this kit, but something that can just get you set up and started, this kit is pretty great with the dies. They are sturdy and work very well. Overall this is a great kit and totally recommend it for beginners.

These are pretty nice. I like the recloseble package they come in. Don't seem to be much different then more expensive brands. So far they seem to be going strong w none dried, and all working. The main difference to me is that these are more of a rough feeling on the paper, as opposed to sakura where the tip is real smooth and not scratching the paper, these are a little bit scratchy, and that is annoying to me, but the price makes it okay!  The sizes and brand writing does wear off the barrel very quickly so ull have a hard time knowing what nib ur grabbing if that happens to u also. The ink is very black and bold with out too much bleed thro. I don't think these are as waterproof as sukura micron because I was able to smear my drawing w a drop of water easily . Instead they are just a bigger tip marker, more like a felt tip in some of the larger sizes. All in all they are a good value and reliable drawing pens for any artist. Especially if ur like me and go thro these pens so quickly , cheaper pens w a couple flaws that u can ignore is a winning deal for me! Happy drawing!

Ok so the reviews on these pens are misleading, maybe they never drawn w pens before. Because while there's many things I like about these there are things I don't love , and they are important in doodling or pen art. So first let me say the sizes are a great variety I do love that! I like that the pens are very dense black ink that flow nice. The tip doesn't feel scratchy or catching . But they do not dry as quickly as Sakura so dragging your hand over it right away can slightly smear the ink. Also it spreads way to quickly. Like a dot on the page doubles in size after it spreads . The ink has no smell so that is great and the package serves as a nice pen holder that recloses and props up open! So they work good and write even but they are not a comparison to Sakura  micro pens. But the ink is more dense and black with these then Sakura so I guess it's guna depend on your preferences. All in all not terrible nor great but I'm still using them so well see how long they last since I draw every day . :) another thing to note is the caps seem to fit really well, snapping on tight, but when u put the lid on the end on the pen to draw, it falls off if I tip it or bump it. But not bad for price

I have enjoyed my order of the Artecho Pouring Effects Medium. This can be used with nearly any acrylic paint. It can also be used alone as a varnish, or a basecoat. This is ideal to use when doing layered painting. I use this with any material I can use acrylic paint. This is very handy for me for heavy body paints. I do not need to own multiple tubes of the same colors when I mix my paint with a medium. I can also use the medium to mix in powdered pigments like mica powder, titanium dioxide, and fine glitters. I can use this to blend with any other material and coat a silicone mold before pouring a resin into it, as long as the acrylic has time to dry. This can be mixed with paints up to a ratio of 1 part paint to 9 parts medium. I have not needed to add such little paint to any medium before, as usually a 1 part paint to 3 parts medium works well for me. I can use this straight in an airbrush and use it as a clear sealant on polyurethane resin castings before coating over with acrylic paints, chalk pastels and watercolor pencils/paint. I can also coat over the medium with acrylic varnish and not alter the pigment colors. I highly recommend anyone that uses acrylics to always have some acrylic mediums on hand as it's great to alter the viscosity when needed. I hope my experience with this acrylic medium is helpful as you kindly click the button below.

I feel very neutral about my order of the 180Pcs Resin Starter Kit. It was well packaged and arrived in good condition. The best feature of this kit are all of the inclusions. There are many more inclusions in the kit than I was able to use with the provided epoxy resin. My conflict with this kit is that it lacks some important safety information. I was also not able to locate a Safety Data Sheet for this resin formula. Epoxy formulas by nature have little VOCs, but that is not a reason to ignore standard safety precautions. This is important information for new users to be able to reference to use the product safely. I always use plastic resins outside my home's ventilation system. I primarily use my garage as it's not at all connected to the ventilation system of my home. I can also exhaust the vapors and odor from the resin outside. I make sure to wear a respirator with cartridges, gloves, goggles and cover any exposed skin. I take standard safety precautions for preparing uncured plastic. This is a resin formula that performs best in shallow layers and is ideal to use with the included mold or those of similar size.  This resin has a good pot life and should be thoroughly mixed. To take the resin casting experience to the next level, this can be de-gassed in a vacuum chamber, poured into a mold and cured in a pressure chamber for the best results possible. Overall I find this kit to be a good value, especially compared to the much smaller, more expensive kits at my local big-box hobby store. I hope my experience with this resin kit is helpful as you kindly click the button below.

I am very pleased with my order of the YIEHO 300g UV Resin Hard type resin. These arrived to me in great condition as they are well packaged. I really like this order because I prefer to have the amount of 300g of the resin in multiple bottles as it's much easier to handle than a single large bottle. This resin is thick out of the bottle and cures to be very hard. It is crystal clear once cured. It's important to note that this resin will not cure without bubbles only if none are introduced or trapped when applying it to a surface of a mold. It also must have full UV light penetration to cure. When used in a silicone mold, the mold must be transparent to allow UV light penetration. It is not chemically compatible with alcohol based inks. UV resin is a different formula than epoxy types of resin. UV resin is ideal for very small castings and doming pieces of jewelry.

I test every new UV resin formula in a 1cm clear silicone bead mold. I use my 36watt UV light for 3 minutes. I apply the resin to the mold using a pipette to avoid bubbles. My finished test beads were perfectly clear, bubble free and perfect. I am using this resin to dome self-made custom eyes I am making for dolls I am customizing. This is an ideal resin for me as it cures crystal clear. I do make sure to use this in my garage where I have good ventilation using a fan to push air from my working area to outside. I wear gloves, goggles, a respirator and cover my skin completely. I do not detect a lingering odor from this resin once it's cured. I find this brand to be a very good value and quality. I am very happy with my order of the UV Resin 500g by MPEDOUR. This arrived in good condition, was sealed and well-packaged. This is a great amount of UV resin, but it's very different compared to epoxy. This usually does not stick well to all types of epoxy resin. It's used for very different projects. I find that the description title is very confusing for this reason. I do know from experience that this can be used as a glue for small pieces of polyurethane resin, as long as UV light can fully penetrate for a full cure.

This is an ideal resin for small casting projects and especially great to dome small items like jewelry pendants. I have been using this resin to make a lens on self-made doll eyes. It's important to make the lens of a doll's eye that is crystal clear. This works great for me. It is very important to use this and all uncured plastics with safety in mind. I work with this resin in my garage, but never inside my home as all the rooms are connected with a shared ventilation system. I also wear protective gear including a respirator, gloves, goggles and coveralls. I use a fan to blow the fumes away from my working corner of my garage to exhaust the air outside.

To ensure that there are no bubbles in my castings, I use a pipette. This cures well under my 36 watt UV nail lamp. I first tested this in a clear silicone bead mold, with a cure time of 3 minutes. It's essential for this resin to have full light penetration to cure. If it goes into a mold, it should be very shallow and clear. Lightfast pigments, small amounts of glitter and tiny inclusions can be used as long as the light can fully penetrate the piece. Possible reasons for an item to remain uncured can be insufficient light penetration and chemical incompatibility. It's always a good idea to test for these with scraps before committing it to a project. I hope that my experience is helpful as you kindly click the button below.

I am very happy with my order of the Hakkin 26Pcs Rubber Stamp Making Kit. This arrived in great condition and was well packaged. I highly recommend that new users start with the soft rubber stamps before moving into linoleum cutting. This material is well suited for block print making. I am very pleased with my order of the Ukatito Uv Resin Crystal Clear Uv Curing. This arrived to my home well-packaged in great condition. I make sure to test every UV resin product the same way for a fair evaluation. I only use resin products in my garage for the purpose of safety. I do not recommend to use this inside a home as rooms often share a ventilation system. I can open the door of my garage and exhaust any off-gassing that occurs while using and curing the resin. I also wear a respirator, gloves, goggles and cover expose skin with adequate clothing. My test mold is clear silicone, which is very important, and I use a 36 watt UV lamp. The beads produced in my test mold are 1cm and I use an exposure time of 3 minutes. The results were amazing. I do not cast bubbles in my beads as I use a pipette to suction away any trapped air. The fully-cured pieces were not tacky, indicating there is not an oxygen inhibition layer. If anyone finds this product to be tacky, it is due to a chemical incompatibility with other products. I do not detect any lingering odor in the fully cured pieces. Clean-up is easy using isopropyl alcohol and baby-wipes.

This resin is thick, hard and indeed cures very clear. As this has passed my test, I am using this to dome some doll eyes I am casting for by collection of Asian ball jointed dolls. This is an ideal medium to use for small items like jewelry. It is essential to observe the limitations of UV curing resin. Most importantly, it must be able to have full UV light penetration to fully cure. An excessive amount of pigment, glitter, inclusions, ect can lead to a failed cure if the light cannot fully penetrate the resin. It also cannot fully cure as light cannot penetrate larger castings. There are many specialty clear silicone molds available from many suppliers that are ideal for use with UV resin. UV resin is great for doming polymer clay pieces and sealing Shrinky Dinks. I have found that UV resin is not chemically compatible with alcohol inks, but can work well with lightfast resin pigments. I highly recommend to test for chemical compatibility when using any new product. This particular resin performed very well, and I consider it high-quality. This resin is a good value for the quality, especially compared to the products currently available in big-box craft stores and trendy resin online shops. I am pleased with my order of the LANBEIDE UV Resin-100g Crystal Clear Hard Type Resin. This is indeed crystal clear, and is very thick when uncured. I really like to use UV resin for small projects like doming small items. When I review a new UV resin brand, I put it though the same exact test for a fair evaluation and review. I use a transparent silicone mold that produces 1cm beads. I expose the mold to UV light using a 36 watt lamp for 3 minutes. I always do this in my garage, as the open door with a fan exhausting the air directly outdoors provides a well-ventilated area. I also make sure to wear gloves, goggles and especially a respirator. I never use this inside my home or without my personal protection gear. The Safety Data Sheet is available from the vendor if you would like to review it. My results were very good, as my beads were crystal clear and bubble free. This resin does not produce bubbles from curing, and they are only present if introduced. It's possible to trap air when pouring, so I do make sure to check and suction away any bubbles with a disposable pipette. Most recently I am using the UV resin to dome the lens of doll eyes I am making myself. It's really important that the resin is crystal clear, as this resin formula is.

Small amounts of glitter, mica powder and UV resin safe pigments can be added. I found that alcohol inks do not work well with UV resin. Lightfast pigments should also be avoided as the UV light used to cure the resin will discolor pigments that are not lightfast. Whatever I use, I do make sure that the light is able to fully penetrate my resin for a full cure. UV resin cannot cure without complete exposure to the light. This is why I make sure to only use clear, transparent silicone molds that are made for UV resin or clear ones I have made myself. I have best results with a low humidity and a temperature around 70°F. There is no oxygen inhibition layer present on this resin after cure, so it is not be tacky. Lack of good light penetration, short exposure time and chemically incompatible products will inhibit curing. There is no lingering odor from this resin after it is fully cured.

I find this is a great UV resin to use as long as the user understands the limitations of UV curing resin. The clean-up is easy, I use baby-wipes and isopropyl alcohol. I am happy with my order of the COLFULINE Button Maker. This is a simple machine anyone can use to make their own button badges.

My only struggle to use this is that there were no assembly instructions, and I did struggle a little to assemble this properly. The sliding die piece does need to have the protruding nubs pointing up so that the horizontal nub on the top of the stationary die will catch it. If this isn't in place properly, the bottom of the sliding dies will not fully depress the springs. I did find that assembly was much easier when I turned the whole unit upside down, this allowed the top nub to slip over the height of the nubs on the sliding piece. Once I put it back down on my work surface I placed the removable pin into the sliding die and secured it in place with a piece of tape so it does not become lost. It seems other reviews did not find this an issue, so it was likely just me that found it to be a problem. I wanted to share in case anyone else experienced similar confusion.

Once the machine is assembled from the box, button badges are a breeze to assemble. The only thing you will need not included in the kit is your paper circle prints. Vinyl will also work if you like. As long as it will fold over the button face, you are good to go! The included circle cutter is very useful to cut the circles to the right size. I used one of the clear Mylar sheets as a template. I am incredibly pleased with my YIEHO 300g UV Resin Kit order. This is important to note that UV resin is best for small projects and for doming small items like jewelry. It is not the same as other resin formulas. This is a comprehensive set. The re-usable silicone cup and tools are good to use to make sure the resin is spread evenly or to stir bubbles out of small spots in a mold. The wooden sticks are great to use to measure and add glitters or pigment powders. I like this resin as it produces perfectly crystal clear castings. This resin is a thick, hard type. While it's uncured, it's thick like molasses and cures solid and hard. When I have a new brand of UV resin, I like to test it the same way every time in a 1cm clear silicone bead mold. I had fantastic results. I do not experience problems with bubbles as I use disposable 3ml pipettes. If a bubble appears in my mold before curing, I simply suction the bubble back into the pipette, never a flame, lighter or heat gun. I left my bead mold full of the resin under a 36 watt lamp for 3 minutes. The results were fabulous.

I really appreciate the included information about safety precautions. The truthful and easy-to-understand safety information makes this my favorite UV resin brand. This is an adult use only product and should never be used around children, pets or anyone else unable of self-protection. For my own health and safety, I use this in my garage I am able to thoroughly ventilate, never within my home's ventilation system. I wear a respirator, goggles, gloves and eye protection along with clothing to cover my skin. I put my used disposable supplies in the outdoor trash when I am done and make sure to allow the garage door to remain open for several minutes to allow all odors and lingering gasses from the curing process to dissipate.

I did test the included light, and it does work well. Exposure times will vary depending on the light source, temperature and ambient humidity level. The included lamp needed 5 minutes of exposure vs the 3 minutes I use with my 36watt nail lamp. This is a good lamp that is included and ideal to use for shallow casting such as buttons or doming jewelry items. I use this on a silicone mat and clean up my workspace with baby-wipes with isopropyl alcohol.

Glitters, mica powder and nail art inclusions are ideal to add to the resin for endless possibilities. I have found that alcohol inks are not chemically compatible and inhibit curing. Resin pigments can be used, but they must be light-fast. As long as light penetration can be achieved, this is great for using in small molds. I like to use special clear silicone molds meant for UV resin as well as doming a lens for self-made doll eyes. I definitely recommend this to responsible adult resin crafting and have used this with friends to make eyes for our dolls. I will re-order when I have depleted my current supply. I am very happy with my order of the Teexpert UV Resin. This is a great value for the twin pack. One thing to keep in mind is that UV resin is better for some uses compared to other types of resin, but not for large casting projects. It works similarly, but it is not the same. I like that this is in two bottles instead of one big bottle, as smaller bottles are easier to handle. I had crystal clear, bubble-free results. When I test a new UV resin product, I like to do a trial the same exact way for every brand to provide a good comparison. I used this Teexpert UV Resin to make 1cm beads in a clear silicone mold. One important thing to keep in mind when using UV resin is that it will not cure without full light penetration. If using a mold, it must be a clear silicone mold. I use a pipette to eliminate bubbles when pouring. If a bubble appears, I simple suction it back into the pipette and let it rise and dispense resin without a bubble before curing. This UV resin will work with resin pigments that are light-fast. It does not work well with alcohol inks as the alcohol chemically inhibits curing. Mica powers, glitter and small embellishments such as nail art inclusions and tiny foils are also compatible. Just make sure that light penetration is achieved for a full cure.

The most important thing to keep in mind is to use the resin safely. I would never recommend UV resin or any other uncured plastic to be handled by children, around pets or anyone else unable to self-protect. This is a product for responsible adults only. This is essential, and you are able to request a Material Safety Data Sheet from the vendor to learn about any resin product and gain an understanding of the risks involved while using the uncured resin product. For my own health and safety, I use the resin in my garage while wearing gloves, a respirator, eye protection and cover my exposed skin. Once I am done, I expose my disposable pipettes and cups to UV light to cure the resin and dispose of it in my household garbage outdoors. I then open my garage door for a through dissipation of any lingering odors. Odors should not be noticed on fully cured resin, a lingering odor means the piece is not fully cured or your work area is not clean. I do prefer resins with odors as this is a good indication I have achieved good ventilation for my workspace. The odors are from the solvents in the formula, some are more odorous than others but a stronger or weaker odor does not mean one product is 'safer' than another. Good ventilation and skin protection is very important.

I cure my pieces using a 36 watt UV nail lamp. My 1cm bead mold will be cured in 3 minutes with no evidence of yellowing from over exposure. This resin is not subject to oxygen inhibition, it will not be tacky if fully cured. It does not need to be washed on fully cured pieces. This can also be cured using natural sunlight for free! I find that it cures best in ambient temperatures around 70°F, a few degrees either way has never inhibited cure for me.

This is a great resin to use for doming small jewelry pieces, using for glue with other types of resin and for small castings. I like to use specialty UV resin molds for fun projects and also use it to dome self-made doll eyes. I do wish the labeling on the bottles was a bit more clear for beginners to have a better understanding of safe use and health risks. I found that the SEVENWELL 35 oz Epoxy Resin isn't one I would recommend for beginners because of the lack of included safety information. I look for this in all resin kits as some ingredients in uncured plastics like epoxy resin can pose health concerns and safety measures are best to take into account before beginning resin crafting. It is true that the non-toxic effects of resin is only present once the 2-part mixture is properly measured, mixed and fully cured. For these reasons I only use epoxy resin in a fully ventilated area, outside my home's ventilation system. I use my garage with the garage door opened with a fan running until the epoxy is fully cured. I wear a respirator, gloves, goggles and cover my skin with protective clothing. I do this to lower the risk of developing allergies and reactions to the uncured resin.

The resin kit otherwise gives the results I expect. This is a coating type of resin with a very short pot life. It's best for very thin layers. In projects that bubbles would create a problem, I cure with a pressure pot. Overall, this is an okay resin for coating, but I don't recommend it for casting, especially in delicate or thin molds. I am overall happy with my order of the YAKAMOZ UV Resin. The resin itself cures crystal clear, and I was able to use it to make 1cm beads with no bubbles. I used this resin in my garage, as it's separate from my home's ventilation system. I used a fan to push air away from my workspace out the large garage door. I wore gloves, goggles, covering clothes and a respirator suited for resin protection. I used a desk lamp to light my working area, avoiding sunlight from entering my space. My mold is a clear silicone mold. It's very important to use a clear mold, as UV light penetration is essential. My mold makes 1cm beads. They turned out fantastically clear. I made sure they were bubble-free by using a pipette. The resin is thick, and ideal for doming techniques, open back bezel pendants, small clear molds or layering. My lamp is 36watts, which cured my 1cm beads in the 3 minute setting. My workspace temperature was 72°F and 52% humidity. I did not find any evidence of an oxygen inhibition layer. My cured beads were safe to touch bare-handed after they cooled in the clear silicone mold. I did not detect any lingering odors from the cured beads. These are the casting results I expect.

There are a few precautions and tips that will be very helpful to new users. I've read the poor reviews and find that many are due to misunderstanding how to use the product. I do strongly suggest that new users understand the best use practices when they work with UV resin. It absolutely must have full light penetration, so the depth of a layer should be very shallow. Inclusions like glitter, powders and flakes should be minimal so the light can cure the resin. It also works well with resin pigment, but I do not recommend using alcohol ink. UV resin is not chemically compatible with everything, so do test anything you plan to include or use as a mold before beginning the project. It's important to use the resin safely, please review the Safety Data Sheet and understand the risks of handing these chemicals, and make a plan to do so safely, which is not difficult. I recommend this for only adults that are able to self-protect. I hope that you find my experience using YAKAMOZ UV Resin helpful and kindly will click the button below.

The YOUNGFOCUS UV resin is the worst UV curing resin I have ever used. I have both purchased my own UV resins and trialed many Amazon Vine UV resin products, totaling well over a dozen. If the poor results were not enough to discourage me, I find the safety information lacking. It should not be difficult to locate a Safety Data Sheet for this product. As I am familiar with UV curing resins, I always make sure to use this outside my home's ventilation system. I wear a respirator, goggles, gloves and cover exposed skin.

I am not overly thrilled or disappointed with my order of Miraclekoo UV Resin. I tested many different resin brands with a 1cm clear silicone bead mold. This formula has a very slight yellow tint which is not quite crystal clear. It's otherwise very transparent and thick. I used a 36 watt UV curing lamp which cured my 1cm beads in 3 minutes. When I use resin I make sure to do so outside my home's ventilation system with good airflow. I wear a respirator, goggles, cover exposed skin and gloves. I did note an odor from the bottle, but this is easy to contain as I store UV resin bottles in individual Mylar bags.

It's very important to remember that the UV resin will only cure if it has full UV light penetration. An exothermic reaction happens during curing, so your mold or pendant may become hot to the touch. When layering, best results happen if previous layers are fully cured and allowed to completely cool. I am very mindful when adding pigments and inclusions so it does not block so much light it may inhibit curing. The cured beads did not have a noticeable odor once they were cured. As this has a slight yellow tint, I find this is more useful to me when I add pigments. The value is fair for the quality and quantity. I LOVE my recent order of YIEHO 200g UV Resin Kit. It's easily a 5-star product. It's important to read the included pamphlet first. I am very thrilled that Yieho lists the ingredients that may cause health and safety concerns and how this may affect a user's or bystander's health. An e-mail address is also listed for any follow-up questions or concerns. I advise new users to request a Safety Data Sheet to become more familiar with safety precautions when using any new resin product. I only use resin products outside the ventilation system of my home. I wear safety goggles, a respirator and cover all my exposed skin. I keep an extra supply of gloves close at hand. I like this set because it has so many useful supplies. It's also important to understand the limitations of UV curing resin as it's different than other formulas. UV light must fully penetrate a piece for an ample amount of time to fully cure.

The low-powered nail lamp included does a good job for very, very small amounts of resin. It comes with a USB cord. I power this with a small USB battery pack. Sunlight will also cure UV resin, but it may take longer depending on your piece. The silicone mat will protect your working surface and cured resin will simply peel free from it. I find clean-up is easy using good quality baby-wipes and isopropyl alcohol. I really love the inclusion of the pigments. A little does go a long way, so it's important to become familiar with the limit of pigments the resin can hold while still resulting in a good cure. The same of course goes for the foil flakes and glitters. A sure way to have a failed cure is to use too much! Too little light exposure for too little time will also fail to cure. The silicone mold is nice for making some fun pendants. To use the included mold, I suggest curing the pieces in several layers and possibly a doming layer after removing the piece from the mold. It's very, very important to use clear silicone molds so the light can penetrate your piece. I separately purchased some of my own clear molds and made beads with the kit. If your piece has a lingering odor, that is a sure sign it has not cured properly. I did not detect a lingering odor in my cured pieces.

My finished beads are 1cm. I used my own 5ml pipettes because I could draw up the medium-thick resin into the pipette and allow the bubbles to rise inside the pipette before I put the resin into my mold. This gives me bubble-free results every time. If you do not introduce bubbles into your casting, you will not have bubbles. UV resin is also very sensitive to humidity and temperature. I cured my pieces with no issue in 45% humidity and the temperature of my area was 72°F. It's very important to have a good understanding of the limitations of UV resin compared to other formulas like epoxy, they do not cure the same way and they are best used for different projects. The resin is crystal clear and medium thick so it's easier to handle than some others. My cured pieces did not shrink, so this makes me believe the formula does not include too much solvents and is a good quality formula.

I am happy to recommend this UV resin kit to certain users. I do not recommend this to children, as resin is an adult-only medium for safety reasons. This is a good kit for anyone that has a basic understanding of the best-use practices for UV curing resin and understands the limits as it must have through UV light penetration to trigger the photo-sensitive polymer catalyst to cure. I hope that my experience is useful to you and you kindly will click the button below!

I had great results using the YAMATCH UV Resin. The key to using UV curing resin with best results is something I have learned on my own. I use a 36W 24Pcs beads 395nm UV Curing Light with Sliding Tray to cure the resin. I made 1cm beads in a clear silicone mold. The resin cured crystal clear, and looks great. UV resin is ideal for very small projects, and will not cure well if the depth exceeds 1cm, in my experience, without inclusions. You absolutely must use a clear silicone mold for the light to penetrate and cure properly. One must also be very careful with inclusions so they do not block the light penetration. I find that using a pipette and letting the air rise to the top makes for an effective way to pour the resin without introducing bubbles. If you find this is an issue for you, try a heat gun to pre-heat your mold or surface of what you intend to dome. UV curing resin is temperature and moisture sensitive, and will not cure well if the humidity is too high or temperature is too low. I used this while my humidity was at 49% and the temperature was 72 degrees Fahrenheit. I do wish more tips were included with the resin, or at least listed on the sales listing. I do not know if there is an odor, I use this with a respirator in my garage with the door open and a fan turned on. I store the bottle sealed in a Mylar bag for my own safety. I did not detect an odor from my cured beads. It can be messy, so I use this with nitrile gloves and cover exposed skin.

Although I had great results, the problem I have with this resin is there is no included safety information. This should be available before a user opens the product. Somewhere, there is a Safety Data Sheet for this resin, but there is no contact information on the packaging to find this. I want the Safety Data Sheet, and I do not think I should have to do a lot of work to find it. I feel strongly this should be easy to find before ordering, so a user can decide if this is a good product for them, and if they have the ability to setup a safe working environment. This is the reason I must regretfully rate this as a 1-star product even though my results are worthy of a 5-Star rating. If the safety information was included, I would recommend this as it's a good value otherwise. I cannot in good conscious recommend this as essential product information is absent. I hope in the near future, this will have better packaging and labeling for the sake of safety and best use practices. I hope my experience is helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

I find that Nicpro 64 Ounce Crystal Clear Epoxy Resin Kit to be a very large amount of product in this kit. I personally don't advise this formula to be used in layers over 1/8" thick. I do appreciate that some tools are included with disposable gloves. I like to use nitrile with a lot of my own equipment, including a respirator and vent-less goggles while covering all my exposed skin and work surface. I wouldn't know to do this if I had not become aware of safety bulletins published by the Center for Disease Control and the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health. This is a bit frustrating, as the FDA (Food and Drug Administration) does not approve crafting supplies or offer recommendations to use finished products with edible products. The information in this listing is very misleading. I cannot locate a Material Safety Data Sheet for this product, and it is not labeled with accurate safety guidance or warnings. Epoxy resin products are not non-toxic unless they are mixed and cured properly, as an end result. Epoxies and resins are chemicals that can form a hard, strong surface when they cure. They are often used in two-part glues or surface coatings. As epoxies cure, they generally turn into much less toxic polymers. These chemicals include styrene, methyl methacrylate, epoxy resins, vinyl chloride, and others.

I do find this product is crystal clear, even in the separte 2-part bottles which is something I look for right out of the package. This is good to use for coating, or multiple layers for a thicker project. I like to use these coating formulas to finish the tops of game boards I cast myself. It is also a good choice to coat paintings. I just cannot recommend this with a good conscious, for the lack of safety information and the misleading marketing. I hope in the near future the company can become more responsible and make a Safety Data Sheet available and be more accurate with safety guidance. I do not recommend this for new users especially, as this can be very confusing. I hope my experience is helpful to you, and you kindly will click the button below.

I'm pleased with the results of the Magicfly Crystal Clear UV Resin. Not all UV resin will come out clear, some have a yellow tent. This is a nicer UV resin that does come out crystal clear. This is great for me, as this is important for my doll eye making and small embellishments. One important thing to note is to take the safety precautions in the instructions seriously. UV resin, and nearly all uncured plastic resins are caustic to skin, so make sure to protect your skin on your hands, wrists and arms. Also, wear some splash resistant goggles. If you have sensitivity to uncured plastic, this may not be the crafting product to you. It does have an odor, but it's not terribly strong. I use a respirator with any uncured plastic resin in my garage, to stay safe.

This is a good kit, has clear instructions and the quality of the resin is good. It's a thicker formula, so you may need to let your piece rest before exposing it to your light for curing. This will aid the bubbles to release and not become encapsulated. UV resin is ideal for small projects, and should be used in thin layers. If you want to complete a thicker piece, work in layers. It can work with powdered pigments, inclusions and CLEAR silicone molds. Just make sure that whatever you use, the resin will have good exposure to the UV light for a good cure.

The Improved UV Resin Kit by Magicdo has left me with a very positive impression. I strongly encourage everyone to review the included pamphlet. Safety First!!! There is good safety information included, unlike most resin products I have purchased in shops and online. This should be used with caution, as uncured plastic resins are caustic by nature and give off gasses/vapors that can lead to accumulative allergies. Please take the proper safety precautions outlined in the pamphlet. A well-ventilated area is not a room of your home with a window open and fan running. This would ideally be an area outside your home's ventilation system with air flow. A garage, shed or patio are all great options to craft safely. I also wear long sleeves, a respirator with organic vapor cartridges, non-vented goggles and gloves, each and every time I use any type of uncured resin product. I have never suffered any ill effects. Please make good decisions when crafting, you cannot buy new skin, eyes or lungs.

This is a really nice product, and I'm very critical with resin products. I'm left impressed and recommend this. I used this to make some clear beads with a mold I already own. They turned our perfectly CLEAR, not tinged yellow like many budget UV resins. This is a thick resin, eliminating bubbles can be achieved by leaving your resin in the mold for enough time to release the bubbles for curing, or you can use a heat gun to temporarily thin the resin and allow excess air to be released. I tried my very low powered UV flashlight and tiny UV 9w nail lamp. They both worked perfectly well to cure the resin in about 6-7 minutes at a 6mm thickness.

The included mold is a great example of the types of molds to use, it is clear and allows the UV light to penetrate through to the resin to activate the photo-reactors in the resin. A pair of gloves, tiny silicone mixing cup, silicone stirring rod, angled tweezers and clear gem mold are all included in the kit. I recommend this kit to anyone interested in UV resin. I would love to give this product a 5-star review, but it lacks the proper labeling to be ASTM D-4236 compliant and I could not find a Safety Data Sheet, nor a link to request one. As a serious consumer advocate, I cannot ignore this oversight. Thank you very much for reading about my experience. I hope it and my insights are helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

One of my favorite versatile art supplies is mica powder. NOSTOSON Mica Powder features a good variety of colors and each is numbered to help keep the set organized. I've ordered several mica powder sets as it's a great inclusion for several different projects. I like to add it to my acrylic paints and mediums, along with many of the other uses mentioned in the listing.

A couple points to note about this set is that some of the color names don't coordinate with other sets, so if you want to order a particular color again, going by the color name may yield a very different result. The powders are in re-sealable bags, which isn't a horrible way to ship and store, but one of the bags came open during shipping and the inside of the box was covered. This cleaned up easily, but it's something to keep in mind when you open your box to prevent spreading the powder where you would rather avoid it. It's always a good practice to don a dust mask when working with any type of powder. Each baggie has 0.1 ounce of powder. This isn't a huge quantity, but should be sufficient for most crafting projects. For large pours in resin, it may be better to use a larger quantity of a single color. I find that on larger objects, less powder can be used if it's brushed on over metal-leaf sizing and sealed.

I find this set to be a fair value, and wouldn't hesitate to recommend it to others. Thank you for reading my review. I hope it is helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

This is not a Quick Release fitting, that is a separate product. I'm happy with my order, but feel the listing is lacking in some information for consumers. If you are unfamiliar with airbrush fittings, there are 3 standard sizes. M5X0.5BSP (M5-0.5BSP) is most common for airbrush fittings that use the spray can propellant often seen on old school needle-less siphon feed styles. 1/8 inch BSP is the most popular fitting size for most artist/hobbyist airbrushes and 1/4 inch BSP is commonly found on your air compressor in your garage/shop. This set allows you to use different types of airbrushes, hoses and other fittings/adapters with different sources of air pressure. You may also need Teflon pipe tape and possibly rubber O-rings for your setup for a secure seal. Please note, this set does NOT have a M5-0.5BSP female to 1/4 inch BSP male or female adapter. Check your setup, and if this fitting is needed, please consider another set. The description does feature good, clear photos of each of the adapters that are appropriately labeled.

I found the threads even and the metal material on par with budget-level fittings. This is sufficient for occasional use for various household tasks, weekend hobby projects and as a back-up for those that use these tools/equipment/supplies professionally. The value is good. I expect these fittings to last me for many years with gentle use and good cleaning habits.

If you have a collection of different airbrushes, like I do, and wish to use them with different hoses, fittings, air compression sources, etc.... then this is a valuable set to have on hand. I am a Vine Member, but did not receive this set for the purpose of review. I purchased this for myself. I review all of my orders from Amazon with the same discretion. The Jacquard Lumiere Exciter Pack with Moshify Brush Set is a very happy order. I enjoyed the vibrancy of the colors and found that they dried quickly and layered very well. These are acrylic paints, so they are water based and also can be blended with different acrylic mediums and can be painted on various grounds. These paints are very rich in pigment, so a little goes a long way. I primarily paint very small items such as doll house miniatures and resin dolls. This paint is ideal for my uses. I especially appreciate that they are bottled with white caps, so I was able to dry a small amount of the paint on each of the caps in lieu of making swatches on paper.

I did not find the packaging obstructive. My package had a few bits of plastic tape on the ends that I easily cut open with my box cutter. These paints contain mica powder, so before use it is important to shake them and gently squeeze the desired amount of paint onto your palette. A gentle squeeze with this type of bottle helps to avoid bubbles.

The brushes are fairly nice. The handles are plastic, the ferrules are metal and the bristles are cut evenly. One is a round brush and the other is flat. These are fair to good quality. With good care and maintenance, these should last me for several years. The gold mica in some of the paints was a little more difficult to remove from my brushes versus the other pigments. I did need to wash my brush more and clean my water jar very thoroughly after use.

I was incredibly pleased with this order. I also recently ordered the Jacquard Products JAC9900 Lumiere/Neopaque Pack, 9-Color and the Jacquard Products Metallic Airbrush Exciter Pack .5oz 9/pkg, 8 colors based on my experience with this exciter pack. The additional packs have expanded my color palette. In my experience, Jacquard makes excellent art supply products and offers great customer support. I have found each of their products available on Amazon from a variety of vendors. I will continue to be a customer of Jacquard products well into the future. I highly recommend this set for the quality of the paints, customer support offered through the customer web-site and forum and the value of the product.

Anesty ATR1 Cordless Airbrush Set has some pros and cons. The biggest issue with this airbrush is that the cups leak slightly where the plastic meets the threaded metal connection port. This can be easily remedied with a small amount of adhesive. It's a single action, meaning that air flows freely while the motor is on, and the trigger allows paint to be applied. This may be a great feature for those completely new to airbrushing. It's just as easy to clean and maintain as any other airbrush I have ever used. The charging cord is included and the box works well for storage between uses. The two air flow functions are easy to adjust using the button on the bottom of the power unit. Overall, this is not a great value, but it's not a terrible one either. The cups do have a straight feed, which makes it much easier to clean. I don't highly recommend this particular airbrush due to the leaking cups, but for anyone willing to add some adhesive/sealant to that joint, it should be a simple remedy. Epoxy Resin Kit for Jewelry DIY lacks proper safety labeling and supporting information. The nature of the chemicals to make plastic causes cumulative allergies with exposure, which varies between users and those sharing their environment. None of the high-risk chemical components are labeled, nor is a Safety Data Sheet made available. Low-odor products are not exempt from proper labeling. Gloves, non-vented eye protection, a well-fitted organic vapor respirator and full covering clothing should be used. Mixing and curing should not occur inside a home's ventilation system, this should be used in a well ventilated area like a garage, shed or patio that is well ventilated. For these reasons, I cannot award this kit a 5-star rating. Which is unfortunate since the entire kit is a nice presentation.

I do like the molds, as they are good quality and have a nice variety for beginning the resin casting hobby. I especially like the seahorse and cut gem shapes. The inclusions and pigments provide a good variety to be creative and a nice opportunity to experiment with different styles. The findings are nice and I'm pleased to see some effort was made to include gloves, even though that is a small amount of the necessary safety gear. The pipettes, mixing and small pouring cups are useful and re-usable if cleaned well. Wood stirring sticks are nice to have as they are disposable. Since the accessories are thoughtfully included, I think a 2 star rating is deserved.

Overall, this would be a fantastic kit if the proper labeling and supporting information was included. I would not recommend this kit to anyone lacking safety knowledge, as the lack of safety labeling is misleading and could cause harm to users or others sharing their environment. I hope in the future, this can be an issue that is resolved. Thank you for reading my review. It is never easy to write critical reviews. I hope you find this information helpful to you, and kindly will click the button below.

Flasoo 13.8 oz Epoxy Resin lacks proper safety information. The epoxy should never be used inside your home's ventilation system as the curing components produce vapors that are harmful.
