I really love using mica powder for many of my artsy projects. This set is wonderful because it's packaged in jars at a very economical price. The jars are foil sealed under the lid. Each lid has a pour spout or sprinkle spout that each close. The colors are very vibrant, and are a very comprehensive variety. I like to mix mica powder with acrylic medium to paint 'makeup' on my customized dolls, emboss multi-media studies and I'd love to try curing it in resin. The only reason I must give this set only 4 stars is because it is not labeled as being ASTM D-4236 compliant. The non-toxic claim is not one I can easily verify. For my uses, this is acceptable, but I would never recommend this set to be used by children. I hope you have found my review useful, and will kindly click the Helpful button below.

LaserPecker Pro Laser Machine was exciting to try. I'm not at all familiar with laser engravers, so this was a first for me. I do most of my art by hand with the aid of the most versatile tools available. I hope that my review as a complete novice will be helpful to you.

The LaserPecker I ordered is the L1 Pro Super bundle. It ships in a cardboard shipper that is lined with foam. The machine is enclosed in a case to hold each component of the set securely. It's important to read the enclosed literature first. This can be a very dangerous product under some circumstances. Children, pets and people that are unable (or unwilling) to follow safe directions to protect themselves should never be in contact with this machine, the laser can cause permanent damage to a living being. It is also an extremely delicate tool. You must follow all the safety protocols, and become familiar with them before operation. I'm laser certified and operate high-powered lasers as part of my medical job, so I take the safety aspect of products and materials very seriously. This product is not difficult to use, but it is not a toy, it's a power tool. I cannot stress enough to be very vigilant with this personal use laser engraver. Always, always wear the safety goggles. You can order more safety goggles, but you cannot order new eyes.

In the Super bundle you will find a pair of goggles, tripod, a specialty magnetic shield, 2 markers, testing paper cardstock, literature, a stand, ruler, guide mat, USB-C power cords with wall adapter and the L1 Pro laser unit. The stand has two hinges that unfold easily. I found the cords intuitive and easy to attach to the stand and the second is between the stand and laser unit. I strongly suggest having canned air and a microfiber cloth on hand to remove dust from the laser's lens. Do not ever touch the lens with your fingers, it's nearly impossible to completely clean away skin oil and similar contaminants from this surface. Instead, use your canned air and microfiber cloth to remove any dust that may land on the lens before use, it can affect the quality of your work. Work in a well-ventilated area as lasers produce plume (fine particulate smoke). Depending on the material you are engraving, this may cause some toxic vapors to escape into the environment.

Once you inspect the kit, the assembly is very easy. It's important to follow the directions to a 't'. The laser's lens should only be placed between 19cm and 20cm from the surface of your item. I found that consistent colors engrave much more evenly and smoothly than a surface with varied darkness. It's a great idea to have some uninterrupted time to become familiar with the operation and limitations before beginning.

I did find the app difficult to use. There is not a way to explore the app without the laser being powered on and connected. The laser is dependent on the mobile app. You must first register with a valid e-mail address to obtain a verification code and set an app pass-code on your mobile device. I had a difficult time getting the laser to pair via Bluetooth to my phone. It took several tries of re-starting my phone and the laser unit itself to pair. It also requires some phone settings to be adjusted, and surprising permissions to be granted. I was very annoyed that the operation of the laser is dependent on my phone's GPS being turned on. I do not see the logic behind this and find it invasive. Upon initial operation, the app forces you to do a tutorial with the provided test sheets. I found that washi tape was useful to hold the cardstock test sheet in place despite the air flow from the fan inside the stand.

I found the test sheet engravings look very crisp, even for very small fonts. I next decided to use my own material, which is a round untreated wood slice from a tree branch measuring approximately 12cm by 10cm and 1cm thick. I made an adjustment for the change in distance from the working surface to the laser's lens using the adjustment buttons located on the stand. I choose a photo from my phone's album and was easily able to crop it into a circular shape. I used the centering preview to place my wood centrally. I picked a photo of my cat, with a light colored floral bedspread in the background. This photo took approximately 1 hour to complete engraving. The quality was good, but as a learning point, the rings in the wood did create an uneven replication in engraved saturation. In future endeavors, I will be more mindful to use evenly colored materials. I am currently considering unfinished wood veneer for my next experiment.

The factor of value will vary from user to user. This laser has some limitations for the materials which are compatible and surface space it can engrave. It is also very dependent on the app, which is new to the market and only works with mobile devices, not a PC. It cannot operate offline and the permissions I find invasive. I've not found a need to contact anyone for product support, but the enclosed literature includes QR codes to connect users on a very popular social media platform to exchange information and contact information for company representative support. The best point of the LaserPecker Pro Laser is the size. Other laser engravers take up an entire desktop, this one takes very little space to operate. It can be powered by a power bank.

I do wish the goggles had their own protective case, or there was a better space for them in the laser's case. I will be ordering a second pair, and cases for the goggles separately. I wish they were stocked in the company's Amazon store. The goggles are extremely important for user safety. The lack of a goggle case and the invasiveness of the app are the reasons I cannot give the product a 5-star rating.

Thank you very much for reading my review. I hope you found it informative and Helpful. Please be kind and click the Helpful button below.

The DiLoro Full Grain Leather Cowhide Fabric is good quality. It's dyed evenly and has a consistant thickness. I measured it, and had results that varied from 1.1mm to 1.3mm, which is fantastic for a material made by nature and processed in Switzerland. It folds well, is smooth and very supple. I rolled it from the folded packet, and the creases were gone in about 3 hours. I'd certainly recommend this to anyone into leather crafts, as a patch for furniture/upholstery or for creative crafting techniques. Thanks for reading my review. Please click Helpful below if my review was useful to you.

I do have to say, this is hands down the best budget watercolor set I have tested for review (out of 20+ sets). Phoenix Artist Grade Watercolor Paint Set Half Pan 24 Colors are ASTM -D4236 compliant and certified by the Arts and Creative Materials Institute. They have an official company page that has contact information listed for consumer support. Each half pan is individually wrapped, with the official ACMI seal of approval and pigment information. The set comes in a metal case, which flips open to be used as a palette. Each half pan is nestled in a removable tray. The half pans are also removable, and you are free to refill them with your own paint. A color guide is included (yay!)

The vibrancy is good, there is a few slightly opaque colors, they bloom well and blended easily. It was not difficult to lift the colors with my brush, and provided a good amount of coverage on my paper. I will be using this set as my primary set. I'm pleasantly surprised.

This is a great value, I would purchase this for myself and others. The price point is budget-friendly. I would recommend this set to anyone and everyone for a good-quality, budget-friendly set. This set will make an excellent gift for yourself, or someone else. Many thanks for reading my review. Please, kindly click the Helpful button below if it was of use to you.

The 3PCS Paint Pen sold by Mifurner, branded Twohands, performs well for me. I did find that the white will take about 3 coats to cover pigmented paper. It also performs well on Yupo paper and works well with alcohol inks. I believe this would be suitable for use on resin as well, but I am unable to verify this. The pens state on the barrel they are ASTM D-4236 and EN71 compliant. I am unable to verify this and do not recommend them for use by children.

They take some time and patience to prime. The tips are firm and have a reasonably fine tip. I was able to get stripes about 1mm wide. I find the set to be an overall good value and recommend them for use by adults only, hobbyists and crafters. Thanks for reading my review! Please be kind and click the Helpful button below.

I found Doodle Hog's Ultimate 36 Premium Watercolor Half Pan Set to be a good value. Half pans are small, as this is watercolor, it reconstitutes quite well. Inside the box, you find a double hinged case, a color guide with guidance (yay!), a do-it-yourself sheet of paper to make swatches and one refillable brush pen. To open the pen, twist it in the opposite direction than you are accustomed to. The only downside is the case is a little awkward to use the palette effectively, as the pins on the hinge are not removable.

The colors are reasonably vibrant for a budget set. Some are unexpectedly weak, most are transparent, a few are more opaque. I only reward the 5 stars for the unexpectedly weak colors. It has all the colors I would expect such as lemon yellow, brunt umber and Egyptian (Prussian) blue. Each cell is removable, if you care to do so, or refill later with a watercolor tube.

The package mentions it is ASTM D-4236 compliant, non-toxic and made in China. I cannot verify the non-toxic claim, but contact information is available on Doodle Hog's official website. Doodle Hog is distributed in the USA from Wisconsin.

This is a decent budget set, features all the expected colors, is presented well and has all the support information I seek. I would recommend this set for children, low-level students and hobbyists. I cannot recommend this set for upper-level students or professional artists, only because it is not intended for those applications and would likely be unsuitable for those uses as the lightfastness and pigment information is lacking. This set would make a very nice gift for yourself, or someone else. Please, click the Helpful button below if you found my review informative.

My personal favorite in the YUBBAEX 10 Rolls Spring Flowers Washi Tape Set is the purple morning glories, they are divine! The set generously provides 10 rolls of printed washi tape. I had some notes I really cannot afford to loose, so I taped them to my computer monitor. It didn't leave any residue on the plastic of my monitor's frame, nor on the thin paper of my notes. I also like to keep plenty of washi tape at hand for masking while using paint or markers, as it's meant to be the gentlest masking tape, even on thin paper! The prints are rich and all the colors used coordinate well. I highly recommend YUBBAEX 10 Rolls Spring Flowers Washi Tape Set to anyone who has a need, and a want for really pretty tape!

Acrylic Paint Markers, Set of 24 Assorted Color Pens by Abeier are big, thick acrylic paint pens. The color guide is on the side of the box. There is quite a bit of paint inside. It took some extra effort than I'm accustomed to for mixing the paint inside. There is a ball bearing to aid in this, so it does work well. The paint does take awhile to prime from the tank, so this takes patience on the user's part. The colors are bright and vibrant and take to paper very well. As they are made with acrylic paint, they are compatible with nearly any surface. Coverage is good, and drying time is minimal. They need to be stored on their side. I could not detect an odor.

I could not find any notes on safety on the pens themselves, nor the package. There is no indication these pens are ASTM D-4236 compliant. For this reason, I cannot reward a 5-star rating, nor recommend them for children. I hope you have found my review useful, and will be kind and click the Helpful button below.

GOTIDEAL Watercolor Paint Set is a very nice set I enjoyed using. I found one issue with the set. No where on the packaging is it indicated this is ASTM D-4236 compliant, lacks any safety information or labeling. For this reason, I cannot give this product a 5-star rating. I would not recommend this set for children.

My GOTIDEAL Watercolor Paint Set has 36 different colors in half pans. A color guide is also included (yay!). Each pan is removable from the plastic tray. A 10 well palette is located in the lid of the tray and is removable. Two water pens are also included. They are refillable, twist open 'backwards' and have nylon bristles. Nylon will stain from the pigments, this is normal. I highly recommend using distilled water with water colors. It's inexpensive and available from most grocery stores.

I found all the colors to be very vibrant. Most are transparent, some are opaque. This is not a good or bad point, but it does determine what techniques can be used, and how well the colors will blend together. They bloom decently well. I'm especially impressed with light blue and plum, these are beautiful colors that are not normally found in comparable sets.

I would recommend this set to adults only, lower-level students and hobbyists. This is budget friendly, packaged incredibly well and the pigments are vibrant and strong. I cannot recommend this set for children due to questionable safety. I find this to be an incredible value for the packaging, variety and vibrancy of the water colors. GOTIDEAL Watercolor Paint Set makes a nice gift for yourself or someone else. I hope my review was Useful to you, and you will be kind and click the button below.

Artists Watercolor Paint Set (36) is a no-name, no-brand generic set. Several vendors can contract these sets to sell with their own branding and packaging. This one is priced fairly. The box states this is compliant to ASTM D-4236, but this is not verifiable, and lacks the official seal of approval of being an approved product. For this reason alone, I cannot afford a 5-star rating.

The Artists Watercolor Paint Set is in a box, a metal tin that folds out on 2 pin hinges to expose the tray of 36 half pans. One pen brush is included. To open it, twist the opposite direction than what you are accustomed. It's useful in different ways, has nylon bristles which will normally stain from pigments. I strongly recommend using distilled water with water colors, it's not expensive and can be found at the grocery.

The set does not come with a color chart, that is only available on the listing and does not match the arrangement of the half pans in the case. Some of the colors proved to be unexpectedly light, others are quite vibrant. They are a mix of opaque and translucent, proving you may need to make your own swatch sheet to reference for use. Some colors bloom well, others do not. Overall, the transparent colors blend decently well. The variety of colors is good, which is what I appreciate. I found all the colors to blend well after wetting, and re-wet well on papers.

Overall, I find this to be a decent value. There are certainly limitations to this set, and will require a bit of trial and error on the part of the painter. However, this is priced well, you get a nice variety and it's a good introduction to water color painting, or nice to have a decent set on a budget. I would use this again and enjoy it. I would recommend this to adults only, hobbyists and lower-level students. I would not recommend this for upper-level students or artists to complete commission work. I hope you found my review Useful, and will kindly click the button below.

RISEBRITE Watercolor Paint Set only posed one issue to me. This is a product that is listed for kids, but there is no safety information beyond this set is not suitable for children under 3 years of age, due to small parts. I cannot find any safety information at all, no safety data sheet, and no supported information to indicate if this product set is ACMI approved or not. Each tube states it is ASTM D-4236 compliant, but this is not verifiable and lacks the official seal. For this reason alone, I cannot afford this set a 5-star rating. I would not recommend this product for children for this reason alone.

I thoroughly enjoyed using RISEBRITE Watercolor Paint Set. It's proved to be a much better quality than I had expected. This set includes many goodies for you beyond the paint tubes. You will find 12 very different brushes that are not usually found in a beginner set, narrow masking tape, a pencil with sharpener, eraser and an empty closing palette tray with 20 small wells and 5 large wells. Each of the 24 tubes are 12ml in size. All mine appear to be full of paint. There is also 20 sheets of 9" x 12" 140lb water color paper to get you started.

I put this paint to the test. I dried several colors in the tray, let is set for weeks and then attempted to paint with it. The dried paint lifted very well from the palette! The colors are all incredibly vibrant. The most happily surprising feature was to find they are all truly transparent. Transparent vs opaque does not mean a water color is bad, it's just a matter of personal preference and determines methods and limitations of use. It's easier to layer and blend transparent water colors. This set blends very well, very easily. I strongly suggest using distilled water when using water colors. It's not expensive, and can be found at the grocery.

I consider this set a very good value. The paints are a very good quality for a budget-friendly beginner set, the inclusions are all thoughtfully selected and useful. The packaging is good and suitable for storage. I would recommend this for adults only. I cannot recommend this set for very young children, serious students or artists. If you found my review Useful in any way, please click the button below.

PAGOS 72 Pieces Watercolor Pencil Set is overall a good value. There are certainly some pros and cons. However, for the price and variety of colors, this is a decent deal to be had. The set comes in a large, flat securely closing metal tin with 2 flimsy plastic trays inside. Each pencil is labeled with a number, and arranged in a reasonable order. However, there is no color chart and the colors are labeled by number only. There is a single brush inside. This brush is really terrible, more suitable for glue application than for painting. I strongly suggest using nearly any other brush, q-tip or sponge over this brush, even if this is meant for younger children. The package is lacking safety information, for this reason alone, I cannot rate this as a 5-star product, and would never suggest this as a gift to younger children due to questionable safety.

The variety of colors is the best point of this set. The pigments come out on paper weak, both dry and wet. I cannot say any of them can reasonably be considered vibrant. This is something that can be nearly overcome with layering, but it will take a bit of work. On the upside, the colors are very transparent, and the binding agents do not interfere very much when drawing, wetting, or re-wetting. I did not find any of the colors to lift with re-wetting, they will blend, but as the pigments are weak, this is a limitation that can be only overcome with layering. The blooming capacity is weak, they can bloom a little, but it's not what I'm accustomed to from higher quality water color pencils. I sometimes like to lift the pigments from the 'lead' of a water color with a small brush, I found this method also produced weak colors overall, but some like 'plum' #62, did produce a stronger pigment. Black #71 did not lift with a brush well at all.

My weak 4-star rating is mostly due to the fact that higher quality water color pencils are incredibly expensive. It's difficult to find open-stock water color pencils nowadays as well. I find this to be an overall good value for budget quality water color pencils. This is a good collection of colors, and the tin is nicely made. These would be suitable for many applications. This is a set that is best suited for hobbyists, lower-level students and children. This is by no means a bad set, but it's not good either. It's good enough for most of us, for many projects. For more serious students, hobbyists with a larger budget and serious artists, I would recommend going with a tried and true product from a well-known company, even if it means a much smaller, limited palette. Thanks for reading, please link the Helpful button below if this review was of use to you.

Longan Craft Watercolor Brush Pens was a pleasant surprise to see how fun they are to use. I actually enjoyed these more than I had expected. The set comes in a box with 2 removable trays of pens inside and a small packet of 8 sheets of water color paper. The colored pens fit snugly into their trays, the empty pens are loose in one section. There is no color chart, which is disappointing. None of the water color pens are labeled for color. There is a colored plastic indicator on the end of each pen, which gives you a general idea of the color inside. The pens are not arranged in any particular order. I could not find any safety or material safety data for the pens. The only safety information says it's not for children under 3, due to small parts. The lack of support and safety information is the reason this product does not have a 5-star rating from me.

The performance of the pens is much better than I had expected based on the packaging. I found no leaks, each pen was ready to go with no work from me. I found all the colors vibrant with the exception of the neon yellow, it dries incredibly pale. Every color is truly transparent. The dried colors will bloom well when re-wetted on paper with no lifting. They blend as well as I would expect, although this does take a little bit of work on my part. I found the pigments to have no visible traces of heavy binding agents. The empty pens can be filled with water (I strongly recommend using distilled water) or any other thin medium and used however you desire. Beware, they are threaded 'backwards' so twist them the opposite way than you would expect to open them. Some release more water than others, the quality is a bit hit and miss.

I cannot comment on the amount of coverage to expect from these pens, that would warrant an update if it's proven to be less than I find acceptable. However, for the ease-of-use, versatility, novelty and vibrancy of the colors, I consider this a very good value. The box shape would be very easy to wrap, and it's very economical compared to big box crafting store items. Longan Craft Watercolor Brush Pens would make an excellent gift for yourself, or someone else. Please click Helpful below if you found my review useful and informative to you.

I have 6 airbrushes, each one has an important purpose. I like to take good care of my things so they last. The 4 oz Airbrush Lubricant from Impresa is ideal for this. I had not lubricated a couple of my airbrushes in a long time. I used to use sewing machine lubricant, which worked fine. It's good to have a product made for my specific use. I use this around the moving parts of the trigger when I am tearing them down, not in the area that has paint flow since my paints and other mediums may not be chemically compatible. I've asked for a safety data sheet to check for myself, but have not had a response from Impresa, which is why I can't rate this as a 5-star product. Until I know for sure, I would only use this sparingly around the trigger mechanism for the moving metal parts that touch, but exclude the paint flow pathway of the brush. I did lubricant treatment yesterday as part of my final clean during use. I'll get the rest out very soon and give them a treatment as well.

I'm very pleased with my order for YUBBAEX Natural Color Washi Tape Set 30 Rolls Decorative Tapes. Each roll is only 1cm wide but has a length of 3 meters. This is a set of 30 rolls, all are a light color and remind me strongly of water colors and happy times. These would be great for a variety of uses. I didn't find that this did any damage to my paper when I taped it down while painting today. Some of the colors do look really, really close to each other. This isn't a bad point for me, because I primarily use this to mask off sections of paper or help me to paint straight lines on my dollhouse miniatures. This would also be great for the organization journals, scrap-booking, card-making or for decorating bedrooms for teens. I especially like this for teens that hand posters on their walls, since it doesn't damage the wall or their posters.

This DEEDYGO 8-Inch Genuine Bone Folder is very nice and has a good weight and balance to it. In comparison to what I found sold in a local big box craft store, this is far superior (heavier and thicker), at a fraction of the price. Because it is bone, and not plastic, you can use it for more than paper. You can also use this during sewing, to quickly press fabric or to smooth hot glue, which is what I just did. For the quality, versatility and size, I highly recommend this product.

For the price, this Sketch Drawing Pencil Set is just okay. The extender doesn't fit any of the round pencils in my set. There are 18 graphite pencils, 10 different types, 3 charcoal pencils a small retractable box knife with a break-away style blade, 3 double sided paper blenders, an art eraser and a kneaded eraser. The case has confused me. I don't like it. There are 2 snap sets, but only one seems necessary, the other is just in the way and makes the outer strap longer. The pencils are pretty good, I do like those. You will need your own sharpener as one isn't included and since the knife is break-away style, I don't find it to be a good idea to use it for sharpening the pencil beyond the graphite tip alone.
I wouldn't strongly recommend this set, although the pencils are good, it's easier to find another set of just the pencils and not pay the extra cost of an awkward case and erasers that are lower quality than what I'm used to.

Ybxjges 9600 pcs Glass Seed Beads is a good order for me. I've wanted to try some beaded jewelry for some time, but just didn't care for many of the beads I've come across. I really like this set because they are the right size, about 3mm to 4mm. They are not all uniform, as is the nature of glass beads. This is a nice set for the color variety. They will coordinate well with both polymer clay beads and UV resin beads. Findings and string is also included, but you will need to have the tools on hand for the findings and scissors to clip the string. The case has 24 cells, each are generously full, 23 different colors and one cell is multi-colored variety. Some of the beads in the multi-color cell match the other 23 colors and some are completely different. I'm overall very pleased with this set and would recommend it for the color variety, amount in the package and I see this as a great value.

These sheets are really pretty. I'm making a doll house and wanted it to look like it had copper flashing on the roof. I looked at paints, paper and then came across this leafing, it's perfect! I will not be sealing the sheets because I think the patina will look nice with the style. I'm also interested to have some left over so I can use it for some multi-media art on plastic paper and alcohol ink, I think it's going to look fabulous.

SIM GOLD LEAF Copper Leaf Sheets are packaged well, as I expected in a cardstock envelope with onion paper between the leaves. All 25 sheets are there. I consider this a fair value, as copper leaf has been difficult to find. I'm very happy with this order and thrilled I've found a solution to my copper flashing material to use for my dollhouse.

My teen son likes to make braided bracelets to share with friends. Usually he uses parachute cord but we decided to try Lystaii Rattail Silk Cord. This is very nice because it's soft. I'm also using some for trim on tiny doll clothes and some jewelry. This bundle is great because the cord is made well, the colors are vibrant and the set is easy to coordinate due to the variation in colors. The quality is good, I didn't find any snags in the threads or fading of the dyes. I would recommend this set for the quality, variety of colors and value.

I have a Babylock Sophia 2, so these are the right sized bobbins for me. The DALARAN 36pcs Bobbins set comes with a case and a measuring tape. My local arts and crafting store charges more for an empty case that this one, and I get 3 dozen bobbins too! The snap is secure, and it stores easily. For anyone that likes to have a big palette of threads, and/or load several bobbins at a time, this is a great addition to have.

When I have a liquid art medium to review, I'm especially interested in the product support information before trying to use the product. Ignoring or dismissing safety information for epoxy products can have some dangerous results. At first glance, the label looks legitimate. A brand name is curiously missing. I don't consider this a major red flag as some products are new to the market, or from a new brand. On the listing, this is branded as Anezus, the vendor is Riberl Direct. Not many resin related products are listed for either. I asked in the Q&A section for information to find a Safety Data Sheet (SDS or MSDS). I have not had a reply from either a brand representative or a vendor.

I received the package. On the label of each bottle a website is listed for unicone-art. I looked at this website thoroughly. This is a brand that sells epoxy and related products, they have a link on their website for their Amazon store, but this product is not listed, nor does the artwork on the label match their product logo. There is some 'safety' information listed on this website. The Safety Data Sheet is bogus. This is a 2-part product, legitimate companies will have 2 safety data sheets for a 2-part epoxy product, one for each part of the formula. Essential information on the 'safety data sheet' is also incomplete and misleading.

There is more misleading information on the label. According to it, this product is made for a company called Creativations LLC. Creativations is a trade show for artists, a non-profit organization. There is also an art group called Creativations that sells art prints to fund cancer research. There is no evidence I can find that this is a legitimate company. Nor are either or these non-profit organizations affiliated with this epoxy product.

I have to say, there are red flags all over this product. I take my role as a consumer advocate very seriously. It appears to me that this is a product that is being sold with bogus labeling, questionable product support and questionable branding. Anyone can print labels and place it on a bottle that they fill with any sort of product from any source. For all I can conclude, this may very well be the case. Since my efforts to find a simple piece of information to use the product with proper guidance has been unfounded, I would not recommend the Anezus Epoxy Resin Kit to anyone else.

My strong suggestion to anyone that does choose to ignore this information and use the product anyhow, is to ignore any claims made on the listing and label. Wear full personal safety protection, including a properly fitted respirator approved for use with volatile organic compounds, preferably with a full face mask or partial face mask with non-vented laboratory goggles, chemical resistant gloves, apron and do so in an environment outside their home's air circulation system. Preferably in a garage, porch, patio, etc.

I do not find Anezus Epoxy Resin Kit to be a sound purchase overall. The only good points to the kit are the molds, they are nice and I have no reason to doubt the silicone is made with enough quality to withstand a few resin castings and produce a nice project. However, money can be spent on a different product with sound consumer support and have the same, if not better results.

AmazonBasics Art Paint Brushes Set includes two dozen brushes. The big theme of this set is variety. The set comes in a white box with three cards of brushes held in place with elastic. The bristles are all nylon and the handles are finished natural wood. I like the handles because I do not ever need to worry about the handle flaking paint while I'm completing a project. The ferrules are well formed and holds the bristles securely.

The first card in the set holds 13 smaller brushes. The brushes include angled, flat, filbert, rigger and round in sizes from 2 to 11. The second card holds 8 medium sized brushes. These include 3 flat, 2 filbert, one angled and 2 round in sizes 14 to 20. The third card holds 2 large flat brushes 1" and 2" with a size 8 fan brush. My fan brush has been misshaped during packaging or shipping. All of the other brushes are evenly cut and hold their bristles well.

One thing to note about white nylon bristles is that darker pigments will stain them, this is normal. The brushes clean easily with brush cleaner and dry nicely when left to lay flat. You can store them in their box or your usual method of storage.

This is an ideal set to keep on hand for general household use, a beginner's brush set, supplemental brushes for artists or for use by children ages over 8. The box would wrap easily and will make a nice gift.

Fundaful Beads Organizer Storage Case is a great storage solution. I really appreciate the 30 tiny jars with labels. The case itself is lined with foam, each little jar is nestled safely inside. The handle is easy to hold. The zipper ensures everything inside stays in it's place. It's a great storage solution for very small supplies like beads, drills, small snaps and buttons. This is a great way to keep organized and be able to take your project materials with you on-the-go.

In the crafting store, you would have to normally buy the tools separately, but this set has everything you need to replace snaps right out of the package. I did not find these difficult to use, and they hold steady for me. I replaced buttons on a favorite top, so I can get more wear out of it, as some had fallen off with time. This is also a great set to have on hand for your own creations. I can go with shiny silver, shiny gold, aged bronze or aged pewter colors. The case was also included, so it's easy to store and find again as needed and keep everything together and organized well. The one thing I will recommend is using a rubber mallet and a piece of felt on top of the anvil. I have a suspicion that others are neglecting this detail and it's not in the instructions but is how I learned to install metal fasteners and has not failed me yet. Don't use your regular nail hammer, it will mar the buttons and make using them nearly impossible.

I found the 30X Illuminated Jewelers Loupe Magnifier ready for use right out of the box. It also comes with it's own storage pouch to keep it in good condition. The battery is replaceable when it is needed. The top lens adjusts up or down, depending on your needs. We examined some found rocks so the kids could have some Earth science lab time at home since they cannot have access to the learning labs at school. This is also handy for me since I have been doing several hobbies on the miniature scale for years. I never had a tool like this before. This would also be nice for seniors to read small print or details. Of course, it's good for working on jewelry as it's being marketed. I am very pleased with the loupe magnifier and would recommend it to anyone with a need.

I had need for some different colors of zippers that are not too common in local shops for my DIY sewing projects. I'm very happy with the set from JILIGUALA. There are 39 and all are different. I was happy to find my bundle was heavy on the blues and greens. I also now have 4 different hues of white and many others too. I would highly recommend this bundle of zippers for the value, color variety and quality of the zippers. I could not have asked for more!

Although it's likely that the Leather Earring Making Kit could be also used for leather, the included material is not leather. It's a type of faux leather that is cloth backed vinyl, maybe. It reminds me of a thick table cloth with hatching texture. Regardless, several patterns are included with different colors and themes from floral, geometric and camo. The camouflage pixelated pattern is very interesting.

I'm unsure how long the die will hold up, but time will tell. A thicker tacky fabric glue will need to be purchased separately to assemble the fabric into earrings. The pliers included are not rounded tip, rather the general use hardware type. Although it's thoughtful to include them, I'm not doing so as I fear they will mar the findings. The findings themselves are average quality, mostly uniform. I found one that seemed to have some burrs but otherwise they are all perfectly usable. The hammer has a good weight to it and the acrylic blocks are a good thickness to hammer.

Overall, I'm not terribly impressed with this kit. I was disappointed in the 'leather' fabric, as it just doesn't easily lend itself for jewelry. Other than that, the other components of the kit are useful in other versatile ways as well, so it's not a completely poor value either. It's a nice kit for an introduction into DIY jewelry making. I would not strongly recommend this kit, but I would not strongly discourage anyone from ordering it either. The value ultimately depends on the priority of the hobbyist.

This is a great starter kit for re-usable customized epoxy tumblers. The high points of the kit are all the thoughtful included extras. You will not need to make many extra purchases beyond your tumbler of choice, epoxy product and safety equipment for using epoxy. The first thing I noticed when setting up the turner is that you can get it to spin clockwise or counter clockwise by turning it off twice and turning it back on. I really like this as it adds some possibilities for adding some embellishments while it's spinning. The base is made of wood, which is great as it's a solid material. The motor is whisper quiet. I wasn't even sure it was on at first!

Other items that are thoughtfully included are a heat gun, 2 foam cup inserts, 2 stands, silicone paddle brushes, a 9" x 8" silicone drip mat, wooden sticks, 10 plastic cups sized 100ml and 60ml, masking tape, disposable gloves, a dozen color glitter set, and instructions with assembly tools. Everything comes in a colorful box which is great to present as a gift and study enough to use for storage between sessions.

I find the LFSUM Cup Spinner Machine Kit to be a great value and I'm very pleased with this order. I recommend this as a great order for beginners or those that wish to expand their cup spinning capacity.

This is a great set of 636pcs Plastic Safety Eyes and Noses from NAIXUEC. Several combinations are possible as there are 5 different sizes of round black button eyes, 6 different colors of eyes with black pupils and 4 different noses for your DIY plush creations. This is a real game-changer for me as I can use these for all sorts of critters I made during the colder months. The security snaps for the back are all a neutral brown. Most of the buttons use  one size but there is a larger size for the bigger round black button eyes. A few bubble defects are in the colored eyes, but I have to look very close to see them. This is a fair value, and a good order for DIY plushies and soft-sculpted dolls.

These are a great size to handle at the workbench. I'm doing some painting with acrylic and found that the KRONICTRON Stretched Canvases are doing very well for me. Some other canvases I've used have had too much tooth despite being pre-coated. I did not feel the need to coat mine with any more gesso. The surface is a nice, clean stark white. They are ready for paint right out of the box. The frames have square corners. These have been well-constructed and are packaged well for secure shipping. I've very pleased with the order for the KRONICTRON Stretched Canvases. They are a great value and quality, for those reasons I would highly recommend this set.

YAKAMOZ 5PCS Box Resin Molds are a perfect thickness for me. I love that the mat is included, which makes for an easy cleanup. One side has some tack, so it will stay in place on your surface while you work. The mat is 15.5" x 11", while the box molds are about 3" in width each. The mat makes enough surface area for the working space needed to pour into the molds. The thickness of the silicone is just right for releasing easily, but not so thin as I fear to tear them. Also included is a 100ml measuring cup made from silicone. These are ideal as they are as easy to clean and maintain as the molds. My kit has a heart, circle and flower trinket box mold.

This is a great value as the lid molds are included and everything is re-usable. They should last with care.

I like to use natural products for my home made candles. I cannot go wrong with these for keeping to my natural inclinations. My candles go into smaller jars, that measure 5cm at the mouth of the jar, so these burn well. I've tried them with soy wax with great success. This set is working well for me. You will get 100! That's a lot of candles and makes for a great value. The centering tool is metal, so it can be used over and over again. The stickers work best if you clean each surface you are fusing with some rubbing alcohol first. I use a straw to center them in the bottom of the jar.  I would recommend these. I'm not 100% certain that they are hemp, as I'm unfamiliar with it but they work just as well as cotton wicks I have used before.

LYTek Mixed Media Paper Hardcover Sketchbook is ideal for making sketches on-the-go as the hardcover keeps the pages nice and flat. The sheets are the perfect thickness for my pencils and charcoal. I've done some erasing, more than I wish to admit, and the paper kept intact. It's a perfect size for me, not too big and not too small. This is a twin pack, so there is one to keep at home and one for travel. This would make a great gift for the artist in your life. I have no worries about whatever media I use as the pages are artist-grade and acid-free.

This PGFUN 49 PCS Dot Painting Kit is a really great set for painting and drawing your own mandalas. The little easel is as adorable as it gets. I really appreciate the variety of sizes for the included dotting tools. The stencils are all a little different. All you need is pens, paint or whatever your drawing medium of choice would be. Six 5 3/4" painted wooden circles are included to get started. I love the dotting tools and the roller sticks as they can be used for a huge variety of projects and materials. There is even a pencil sharpener for both of the pencils included in the compass box. I am incredibly pleased with this set and will use it for not only mandala painting, but also with different types of clay, drawing and painting projects.

I hope you found this review Helpful and will click the button below. Thank you so much for reading my review.

I love this because it makes perfectly tiny little charms. Alsthoug this is listed for resin, it works for polymer clay pieces too! I didn't fear the silicon ripping upon removal. It's a nice silicone used to make the molds. I was really looking for something different, but small, and this fits the bill perfectly. Little pressed flowers will fit perfectly into the charms. The included cups are silicone, so what you mix inside can be cleaned away for re-use, I like that this kit helps me be able do some crafting with less waste.

I do plan to buy a second kit for earrings in the near future. The reason for a second kit is so I can use some of the charms as pairs and mix from the same batch of product so they can match. Earring hooks are included, but to use them, you need a pair of molds, and these are sent as pictured, where one mold of each shape is included. The variety of molds is so great, I do not consider this a set-back in the least.

My kit came with hardware for two 3-tier stands, but was missing the metallic flakes, which isn't a deal-breaker for me. These are easy to use, but you will need a flat surface and the appropriate safety gear and environment to use resin. I really wanted this kit to make my own custom stands for my dolls after using them for the cake stands. Regular doll stands are sort of dull, but I can do whatever I please with these and just use the hardware for a doll stand. These are thinner molds, and cannot keep shape on their own, so you need to make sure they are on a very level surface where they can sit to cure, depending on the resin product you are using, they all vary depending on the type.

I'm quite pleased with the kit, the quality and appearance of the hardware and I'm happy the silicone is the transparent type so I can see better when adding colors myself.

I really like the 100pcs 6" Candle Wicks kit because the wicks are stiff and easy to place. I'm a beginner, so products that are easier to use are best for me. These are great for me because the jars I'm using are narrow, so I've been getting a nice pool. I don't get smoke or popping from these wicks. I do clean my jars before pouring with rubbing alcohol, so the stickers are working well for me. I use a straw to get them centered in the bottom of the jar. While pouring, the centering device is so helpful!. It's very generous of them to include the extra stickers for the metal tabs and the centering device. I find this to be an excellent value and I recommend them for beginners like me, just be sure your container is a good width to match these wicks and you should be set.

I primarily use acrylics, and occasionally watercolors. The DOMMARE 15Pcs Acrylic Paint Brush Set is great for a comprehensive basic set of brushes. A sponge is included, which is nice for gentle cleaning between colors and the palette knife is small, so it can help you mix your paint on a flat palette. The round brushes are acceptable for some watercolor techniques, and the size variety works well for me. I did not find any of the brushes to loose bristles easily and the glue on them for shipping held strong, and was easy to rinse away before use. The case is a thoughtful inclusion, it's well made for a budget set. The only thing I will pick on is the painted handles. Sometimes, with use and regular cleaning, the paint can flake off. This is mostly based on personal experience, but I would find it to be a big improvement to omit the paint from the handles or to use a sturdy plastic material instead. The ferrule is well-formed on each brush, so that is a huge plus. I would advise other users to always let your brushes dry on their side to avoid moisture accumulating between the ferrule, bristles and handle so they may live a long life.

Thank you so much for reading my review. If you found it helpful, please simply click the helpful button below.

The Anezus Epoxy UV Resin Dye is listed specifically for epoxy types of resin. White is more of a milky color and black is like charcoal. These are translucent dyes. 20 flat sticks are included in the box. The dyes each need to be shaken vigorously before use. I've had some trouble with settling more in the Fluorescence Green and Golden Yellow than the other dyes. If you use these, I would advise to shake the bottles before setting anything else up to save your pot time when you are mixing.

There are two major areas of concern with this product. Please remember using all epoxy products involves mixing dangerous chemicals. There is no safety information included in or on the box. The instructions on the box are vague at best. There is not a marking of any sort to lead me to believe this product has been verified or conforms to ASTM D-4236. In other words, there is no reason to believe this product is safe to use or any instructions to use it safely. Therefore, a Safety Data Sheet is not available for this product. If you, like me have chosen to use it otherwise, I would strongly suggest using a complete set of personal safety gear away from an enclosed living area (use outdoors is best). My suggestions for use include a fully sealed face mask with P100 pre-filters and an organic vapor cartridge, or a half-face mask with the same filtered cartridges, non-vented laboratory goggles, hair and skin coverings and chemical resistant gloves. Following use, I would also advise to wash all clothing worn, avoiding contamination, followed by a through shower with soap, shampoo and water. This may sound excessive, but it's best to be as safe as possible than to risk damage to your lungs, eyes or skin. Leave the product to cure outside living areas, with adequate ventilation.

Since there are no safety warnings, or reason to believe this product conforms to ASTM D-4236, I would not recommend it. A 2 star rating is very generous for these reasons, as it would be a fair to good value if it provided standard safety information. I would hope that in the near future, the producing company can remedy this oversight.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful and will click the button below.

I use a Sophia 2 from Babylock. These machines are very similar to Brother machines, so I was not sure these feet would fit, they do. I really wanted to try the wide foot with the grid. Some fabrics I use are very sheer, so it's great to have a wider foot on the machine to keep everything nice and flat. I'm certainly not naturally talented, so guide feet are great for me. It really helps me keep my seams straight and even. I'm very happy with this set and highly recommend it if you have a need for some versatile guides on your machine.

Thanks for reading my review. Please click the Helpful button below if it was for you.

I ordered this because the handle can fit on the side of my outer pot that holds the boiled water. This helps keep it upright the entire time I'm using it. It's thin enough that the heat can get to the wax quickly, so it takes less time and energy to melt the was quickly. Two little spoons were included with a wick holder, which is great. The spoons aren't very long, but they have flat sides, so that is great to mix along the sides. As long as you are not filling the pot too high with wax, it works well. Otherwise, I do wish the were longer than the pot is tall. The spout pours well. This is exactly what I was expecting and I find the value to be good for all that is included, the performance and quality of the pot.

Plastic snap buttons are wonderful for kids clothes, doll clothes, and homemade household items. I love to use these for doll clothes. This set has such a variety that I can match nearly any of my fabrics. I've never seen such a variety of plastic snap buttons before! I've been looking for years. I've used a few different systems for inset snap buttons and eyelets. The piers type is my favorite, it's just much less of a struggle compared to the hammer and anvil style and faster than using the sewing machine. The plastic setter type isn't worth the trouble.

This is a great kit and easy to use once you are used to it. Practice on a scrap or folded paper towel to learn. Included are the pliers for holding the dies, 12 dies for setting snaps, buttons and 4.5mm eyelets, an awl and a screwdriver to switch the dies. Not all the dies are labeled, P3 and P8 are missing labeling. Other than that, some basic instructions are included with pictures. You can set the included 4.5mm eyelets, the metal snap buttons (10mm, I think), plastic snaps in standard sizes of 10mm, 12mm and 15mm.

The color of buttons included in the MILIJIA 260 Sets Snaps with Snap Pliers is really great, greys, brown, blues, pinks, yellows.... of course black and white. You can match nearly any fabric with these. Star, heart and flower shapes are also in the set, which is nice for some fun variety. You can use the pliers over and over again with additional purchases of other plastic snap buttons, metal buttons or eyelets. This replaces 3 separate tools I have in the sewing room.

The MILIJIA 260 Sets Snaps with Snap Pliers kit is a great order. For the same price of the 6 die pliers set I bought at the local crafting/hobby store, I have a better pliers set than can set more sizes of buttons, 4.5 mm eyelets and also metal snap buttons. This one comes with loads of buttons. My old tools are now destined to a yard sale! If I had known about this kit, I would have bought it instead. I consider this kit to be an incredible value for everything that is included. It's a sturdy tool that will last most of us a lifetime with care and proper storage.

The 100 Piece Natural Candle Wicks are great for small candles. They are easy to use because they come with a wick holder and some glue buttons. They adhesive buttons work best on very clean glass, I wipe the bottom of my glass with alcohol first, and didn't have a problem. I'm using very small glasses, so this has given me a good burn. They are generously long, so I'm saving the leftover pieces.

The only thing I will pick on is that I wish the specifications were in or on the package. I've written everything on the bag myself so I don't get confused later.

The HNYYZL 24 Jar Gold Leaf Sheets really impressed me because the set comes in it's own storage jars. This is great so they do not easily become crushed before you are ready to use them. 120 pieces are in the 24 jars. I've not opened them all, but there are plenty of the gold ones that I am most interested in currently. I'm embellishing some miniatures for my dollhouse with them, it looks so fancy! I was nervous that they came folded, but the sheets are super-thin and once you carefully unfold them, they flatten out and adhere just like the types that come in booklets.

This is an incredible value for the packaging, variety and versatility of use. I have so many, I will be able to do dozens of projects. I will not hesitate to order these again!

This is to keep your leather piece steady for a chisel piece, which is sold separately. There are also 2 finger protectors. They are thick enough to hold together well, but not so much they are cumbersome to use. This helps to punch holes in leather or other thick material for eyelets or lacing. I'm using them to place eyelets.

To use these you will need a self-healing mat, the pronged punches, your material and eyelets with some lacing if that's the goal. The DIY Diamond Lacing Stitching Chisel aid Leather Craft Tools are like anvils, but for leather punches.

I hope you have found my review helpful, and will press the button below.

The Floral Washi Tape by lovielf is a very pretty set of tape. These are all soft colors from a taupe background to a soft blue-green. I can't decide which one is my favorite. It might be the lotus, because it looks like a watercolor painting. Each tape is wrapped well. I hang then on a multi-pair pants hanger in my hobby room closet so I can see them easily and choose the best one for my project. I usually use washi tape for paper, I tape the paper down for tracing or while I'm painting with water-based paints. This works well, but I've also 'cheated' and used this to frame some small paintings on paper for display on my cork board. I like to use the flat push pins, so this dresses those up a bit too by putting a bit of tape on the head and trimming around it. This is a thin paper, and the colors printed on it are muted, so they look best on lighter backgrounds if you are using them for decoration.

The adhesive is strong enough to stick, but testing it on reciept paper, it didn't tear it. I use this as a test before using it on my more expensive water color paper. Sometimes I need to mask my art papers, and washi is great for that.

Each roll has a lot of tape, at 7 meters long, so that's 42 meters total! It's really pretty, a great width for my use and doesn't tear even the finest, thinest papers, so I count this set as a great value.

Thanks for your time to read my review. If you found it helpful, please click the button below.

The A3 LED Light Box by SAMTIAN is great for tracing, a backlight, a photo diffuser light, diamond painting kits and general lightbox work. I suppose I've forgotten how large A3 sized papers are. Familiar printer papers are A4 size, A3 is twice as big at 11.7 × 16.5 inches, so you can tape together two A4 papers for tracing or use paper from the large pads. This would be ideal for those big diamond painting projects, as they are larger than standard sized paper. I'm making large transfers for cloth embroidery, tracing doll clothes patterns onto fabric without pins on delicate fabrics and sometimes it's helpful for charcoal and pencil work. I don't do much with animation, but this is a great product for that too as most animation paper is large. It fits well in an easel if you wish to use it that way, sometimes I do this to keep my hand from rubbing away my charcoal and chalk pastel marks.

Some things worth mentioning is this pad is very delicate and thin, about 1/8", so you will want to keep the box for storage and/or travel. It's a bit delicate due to it's wide and thin size. You will need a USB power source. A cable is included, it can use any mini USB plug. If you are going to be away from an outlet, a power bank works well. I highly recommend using a power bank so you can keep everything on the table top and avoid a cord disrupting your work flow. It's powered by LED lights, so it takes very little power to work for a very long time.

You can adjust the brightness by pressing on the power button down. As far as I can tell, there are no different color temperatures, the light it gives is a little on the blue/grey scale, certainly not at all yellow. This works well for me, but it depends your project and personal preference.

I find this to be a great value. I had looked at the options in local art/crafting shops and found those prices much to intimidating for the amount of time I need to use a light box. This is so large, it's only a step away from a light table, so a good deal is found with the A3 LED Light Box by SAMTIAN.

Thanks for taking the time to read my review. If you found it helpful, please click the button below.

The Mont Marte Signature Detail Brush Collection has a good variety for details I especially like the angled spotter. This is great for me because I like to paint miniatures and dollhouse furniture. I usually use acrylic, so the taklon bristles are ideal. It's packaged in a cute little tube inside a bag. This is great for shipping. I'll use the tube for storage by may leave out the bag to avoid any bristles of the brushes being deformed. I appreciate the handle design, as it's easier for me to hold. Sometimes my hands will be sore from awkwardly holding a brush for long periods of time, but these are more ergonomic. This is a great brush set that I would highly recommend.

These are great scissors, at 6" in total length, they are on the smaller side but great for paper crafting. I liked how sharp the tip is, so that was nice. The things I didn't like was they are a little difficult for me to hold. The loop that is bigger didn't quite fit my 2 fingers very well. The blades are a bit thin, so not too great for fabric. They aren't going to be used for fabric by me, so I made them paper scissors, and for that purpose they are much better. I do wish the handle was a little bigger.

I really liked the crystal looking molds, and the big cube, so I wanted to give them a try. They worked well for me, but I did think the silicone was a bit too thin and wobbled a little more than I wish it did. I'll probably never use the ashtray one because no one really smokes anymore. Other than that, I had a nice time using these. They clean up easily, and can stack inside some of the others, so they are easy to store. I found them to be a good value compared to what I saw at my local hobby/crafting shop.

I like these because they are some tough fabric scissors. I went through thick denim like it was butter. It's made in 4 pieces, as the blades are separate pieces from each side of the handle. These are the nicest scissors/shears I've ever owned and the others I had been using were over twice the price. I'd highly recommend these because they are so well-made, cut well and appear built to last. Since it's a tool that has the blades separate from the handle, they would be easier to sharpen than most fabric scissors. I'd order them again, but I strongly feel I will have these for the rest of my life.  I only wish they came with a blade guard, but the box should keep them from poking anything else in the drawer. I'm very pleased.

Canvas Panels for Acrylic Painting Value Pack of 16, 5x7 Inch are great little panels that easily lend themselves for versatility. I put a thin layer of gesso on them and do some quick studies with acrylic paint. The have a bit of tooth to them, if you find it to be too much, another layer of gesso or two will set them up better. That's all up to personal preference. They are all cotton canvas, primed and ready for your own paint if you wish. Each panel is wrapped, so you don't need to worry about environmental exposure for the remaining panels after you open the outer wrapper. They are about 1/8" thick. They are suitable for framing. I've glued a loop of ribbon on the back with a coordinating color, and they are adorable that way too. These are just the right size for little fledgling artists, hobbyists or for seasoned artists. As they are panels, I wouldn't recommend any heavy layers that may be too wet and cause warping, but I've not had that to be an issue for me. This is a great value, 16 in total, so I don't feel like it's a waste of materials if I don't like my outcome.

I'm adding some jewelry pieces I'm making myself for a few special outfits. These NCB Natural Matte Black Onyx Gemstone Loose Beads, 4mm are perfect! They come in double resealable bags with some bonus stringing material. They are certainly very nice and matte. They are uniform in size and shape. This works well for me because I just wanted a few other beads to add for an accent color for some others that are meant to stand out a bit more. I only wish I could find beads just like these in 3mm and 2mm... that would be so great! They're really nice and I'd highly recommend them.

WJPILIS 4 Pack Silicone Soap Mold is a great set of molds. Just like in the promo photos, I have 2 pink, 1 green and one blue mold. I keep the pink for food use and the green and blue for making my own spa products. They work well for bath fizzies and shower steamers. I don't make soap (yet), but like to put my homemade aromatherapy treats into a soap dish in my shower, so this is a really great shape. It's also easy to wrap my items since they are rectangles. The kitchen ones are excellent to divide batter for cakes and place on a cookie sheet into the oven, then onto racks to cool. They are nice little rectangles that can be stacked for tiramisu.

I wouldn't recommend Arteza Mixed Media Paper Foldable Canvas Pads for paints at all. It's better for markers, paint pens or pencil sketches. There isn't enough tooth for most paints and it's too thin to hold much water from wet mediums. This is something that younger children will enjoy, but they will need some help to fold it and keep it folded. We re-enforced the folds with some washi tape, which also looked pretty. This may also be cute for kids' collage work, decoupage or whatever might take a very think layer of PVC glue.

This isn't a horrible paper, but it's not really best used as it's listed. Kids will like it, and it's a novel concept, just not the most appropriate paper type for paints. Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful and will click the button below.

I like the variety of the brush set. You get a little bit of everything. Watercolors usually are best with round brushes with a fat body on the bristles so they can soak up some of the paint and go for several strokes on your paper. The paint isn't what I would really call watercolor.... it's water activated paint, but there is a lot of binders in the pans. There's more binders than anything and it's very firm. It takes a lot of work to pick up much pigment onto the brush. This may be something some may like, but for me, it's just frustrating. Even with patience, wetting the pans and giving the water some time to soak into the pans, the paint is still very, very light. It would be very difficult to reproduce the colors and layering that's featured in the promo photos of the listing.

I do like the brushes, as I prefer a plastic handle over painted or varnished wood that will eventually flake, so that is a great feature to the set for me. I cannot say the set isn't enjoyable, that's up to personal preference. It's not paint I enjoyed using. I wouldn't strongly recommend this set with exception to some very small children. Even for children, there are better sets that would give more pigments and still be water-soluble and non-staining.

I've never made candles myself before. My local shop for candles closed, so I thought now was a good time to try making my own. This is a great beginner kit with plenty of supplies to get started. I get to try a pillar mold, tin containers and a glass container. There are wood and cotton wicks and the stickers to put on the bottom of the container too! I wasn't expecting to really like the fragrances, but they are all really nice and even smelled good to me together. One or two may have leaked a little in the bag, but it wasn't much. The wax is also double bagged, so the kit comes ready to use. I like the box that contains it all because it closes shut with tabs on the side and has a handle. This is easy to keep all the supplies together, so nothing is lost. The thermometer is nice because it's digital with a replaceable button battery. I was a little confused by the thermometer's instructions, because it has a couple features I'm not sure how to use, but it does work great.

The thermometer is something I can use over again, as well as the pillar mold, case, tins, jar and warming pitcher. The only thing I needed to get ready at home was my kitchen scale, plastic cups (for the wax to weigh it) and a saucepan of boiling water to place the metal pitcher into. I also used some rubbing alcohol for cleaning the inside of the jar and to cleanup after I was done, so the scent will not mix with the next one. The chocolate scent reminded me of being at my aunt's house as a kid, it's really amazing. The next time I will try to mix it with the peppermint and see what mint chocolate is like. I don't think I would have ever thought to make a chocolate candle, but I'm happy it was included so I know that I like that now. I'm waiting overnight to let my candle set before I burn my candle. Just making it made my house (and my hands) smell good!

One important note is that the pillar molds will need to be placed into a container to burn because the wax is soy, and it's not typical to use soy for pillar-style candles. Soy is usually best for container type of candles. This isn't in the instructions, so take that advice for what it is worth to you.

The instructions were easy to follow, and I'm glad to have them printed. I've seen video tutorials, but having it in print is helpful to have right beside me when I'm warming the wax and doing my first pour. It's really exciting to do this myself. It has everything I need to see how I like making candles, so if I prefer the tins vs glass, I will know. The instructions also have a code to scan to learn how to make your own pictures to put on the candle. I want to try that next time, I've never heard of that before!

This is a great kit to get yourself to make gifts, or to give the whole kit as a gift. I'm still learning, so this was a nice gift for myself to have some fun between the stresses of daily life. I think handmade gifts are always the best kind, I can't wait to get enough practice to gift candles I made myself.

Thanks for reading my review. If it was helpful to you, please click the button below. Have a blessed day!

I'm so glad to have ordered the New brothread 60 Spools + 12 Spools Wooden Thread Rack because my little drawer of threads was getting to be an out-of-control mess! I have skinny spools, fat spools, silks, polyester and cottons. Colors of all sorts..... you know how it just tends to happen over time. It was fine for awhile, but I started to have trouble reaching for a spool and knowing I had it.... and diverting my attention to sorting around to find it. That isn't an issue anymore. I have some space for a few more spools too, but I'm going to order myself another soon so I can have a place to put more spools for some projects I'll be sewing during the winter. I especially like the baby rack because I can take it down from the wall, load up all the colors I will be using, and have it right beside my machine. When it's time to clean up, I just put it back on the wall and everything looks neat and tidy again. It also holds the spools I have for embroidery, even though I don't machine embroider very often, it's good to have a rack to hold multiple spools at once without loosing lots of desktop space. There is a 1 1/2" between the spindles, good for the fat embroidery spools I have. I've hesitated to buy a spool rack before because none could lay flat against a wall, and I need one that can go on the wall.

I've very pleased with this. Mine came in perfect condition, but due to the nature of package handling, extra spool spindles are included in case yours may become damaged during shipment.

I'm really disappointed in the Kuyal Wooden Easel. When I opened it's Styrofoam packaging and lifted it away, a couple nuts dropped out. The end of the middle leg fell off. I placed in on my table top and tried to open it, found some bolts and washers rolling around loose.... it took me a little while and I *think* I put them back where they are suppose to go.... and then I found a little metal nut spacer stuck in the Styrofoam... so..... I'm really not at all sure what to do. I did open it, but I didn't extend the legs because I'm still unsure I placed the nuts and bolts right. I just looks off. It's nice there is a wooden palette, but that's going to need some finishing. You will need a bottle of linseed oil for it and do several treatments for it to be ready to use... and the wooden palettes are for oil painting. I work in acrylic, but thought to mention that for those that will need to look up the procedure to finish the palette. The drawer is nice to have, especially for travel, so you can place your brush case and tubes or pans inside. There is plenty of room for whatever you may wish to have while traveling. The drawer is a nice size.

It would have been nice if there was some sort of diagram booklet included to show how the legs move to support the easel for use. I'm still not sure I placed the nuts, bolts and washers where they belong. Something doesn't look right and I've looked it over a few different times to try to figure it out. I've not set it up properly for fear that one of the legs will break. The legs are skinny, but the whole easel isn't very heavy. If I knew exactly how this was assembled, it would be the easel I would take with me for plein air painting adventures. This easel is different than the others I own, and I've never used one quite like this before, so although the hardware was loose, there's also some user issues to blame.

This would be a good value, except I'm not 100% sure how to re-assemble it. I have counted off a lot because there were no instructions or at least a diagram for assembly and use. I also cannot comment on the durability or sturdiness since I cannot take it out and about in it's current state. The legs seem very skinny compared to my other beech wood easels.

Thank you for reading my review. If you found it helpful in any way, please click the Helpful button below.

I was not sure what to expect from a 'portable' beech wood easel. I'm pleasantly surprised with the MEEDEN Tripod Field Painting Easel . Mine came with a nylon bag that has pockets. I didn't find the setup difficult at all. All I did was put a bolt through all the leg portions and attach the tray. All the hardware pieces on mine are solid and the wood is cut and sanded well. I strongly suggest becoming familiar with the easel before you are setting up to paint so you can be comfortable before you are ready to work. This goes for any easel or new piece of equipment. Otherwise, you are making a decision that is likely going to lead to frustration.

I can place the canvas holding slides in different positions and tilt it in many different angles. Maybe someone that has never used an easel before may be confused, because you can adjust it many different ways. I appreciate the versatile positions it can afford. I can use this while being seated or standing. It will hold larger canvases that I can ever imagine myself using. I like the tray, it's not too big to get in the way, but it's not so small that it's useless to me. I can set a few water cups on it and more brushes than I would ever use at one time. I like using it for stretched canvas and canvas boards that are on the smaller sizes of 5" x 7" up to 16" by 20" so far.

The choice of portable easels is one that will sit solid every time, or a very light weight one that will likely tip over with a slight gust of wind. I'd prefer a solid easel, myself. I appreciate that I can easily store this in a small space and set it up relatively quickly when inspiration hits. This is portable, but it's not light-weight by my standards. It's heavy to me and care must be taken not to loose the bolt or single wing nut that hold the leg assembly together. It's portable in a way that it can be disassembled with minimal steps and placed back into it's bag. It's not something I could carry out into the wilds easily, but to load it into my car for travel, it will take up very little space, not more than my tripod bag for photography does. So, for short periods of carrying, I can take it from my car a short distance and use it for some plein air painting adventures, probably from my parked car to the beach or into the small grass fields at my local park. I will not be lugging it up and down mountain paths. Maybe a younger, stronger, more fit person may do this, but not me. So, the portability feature is limited to your physical strength and endurance.

Overall, I find this to be a really great value, very affordable for the quality. Most shops that carry easels will price those similar over twice fold the price for this one, even though this one has nicer features like the versatile positions and height adjustment. Not all easels have a tray, so this has a lot to offer.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful and will click the Helpful button below.

The AUBBC 4 Pack Resin Casting Molds with 2 Measuring Cup set is listed for resin, but I'm holding off on any resin products until I find the exact formula, respirator and other safety equipment that is best for me.

The ashtray is fun because when the water accumulates in the steamer, it has a place to settle and dissolve the steamer to release the scents I've added to my ingredients. I used that one just to see what would happen and it had a fun result. The pyramid will be used for my next batch. I may even use some more masculine scents with that one since the pyramid may be more appealing to the guys.

I can use the globe shaped one as 2 pieces to make steamers or make bath bombs if they are put together. I'm having a lot of fun with this set. When I get into resin, I'm going to order a separate set so the toxic resin doesn't cross-contaminate with the food-grade ingredients I'm using for my scented bathing products. The measuring cups are a nice add-on an I appreciate them for mixing my wet ingredients.

Overall, this set really surprised me. Each shape has a flat end so your project can be level while you get the desired shape. I find it to be a good value for all that's included and the silicone is thicker than what I've seen for some molds.

I had hoped to find some thinner paper for tracing and transfers, but just slightly thicker than regular 40gsm (grams per squared meter) tracing paper but thinner than my printer paper. This is perfect. I cannot comment on the animation qualities or ink/dye transfer between pages but it's great for tracing over a light box with pencils. I like the holes, because they fit easily into a folder with brads so I can keep my transfer papers organized without bulking them up into a binder. It also folds well.

It's a bit shorter than regular printer paper by about 1cm, but wider, including the margin for the Acme holes, by about 2cm. Overall it measures 206mm by 299mm as I can measure, so either I am measuring wrong or the description is wrong. The measurements maybe important for the user, so that is why I've decided on the 3-star rating. It's great for my uses though. It's double bagged in two layers of plastic that are both resealable with an adhesive strip.
