The main reason I didn't like the 17 Holes Oval Watercolor Paint Palette is because it's awkward for me to hold. I like to hold palettes with a thumb hole the same way, where the tray rests on my forearm. This is how a left-handed person could hold this palette, but I am right-handed. I realize that some folks are going to hold this with the palette trays away from them and the thumb hole closer to their body, but I can't do this well either, it's much to awkward. I still could not hold my palette and brushes in the same hand as I usually would.

For me, this will only be useful for working on a table top. That's fine, but this will not be anything I can take out for plein air painting, where it would be so helpful to have such a light weight, large tray for water colors. I had hoped to use these trays this way, but that isn't going to happen.

This can also work just as well for thinned acrylics or gouache, the wells are a good size and the over all dimensions are not too big or too small for table top painting. I would not recommend the 17 Holes Oval Watercolor Paint Palette for anything other than water based paints.

This would be a great product for a left-handed person or anyone that wants to work table top, but for me, it's a regrettable order.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you have found it helpful and will click the Helpful button below.

I takes several minutes for the 99Pcs Wood Burning Kit to become hot. This is a tool that takes a lot of patience. The reason being is that although it does burn wood, it's very slow going and you have to hold it an awkward way to avoid burning yourself. This is an overall fair value as you do get several nice accessories that are versatile for use in different styles of crafts. I love the hot knife for cutting foam nice and smooth.

However, it just takes a lot of time to make any marks on wood. Perhaps different types of wood will burn faster than others, but the little piece I had hoped to decorate was taking a very long time to even make a faint mark. It's something that you will need to try when you have plenty of time to spare and are relaxed enough to have the patience. I tried it, and became disappointed because I didn't know it works so slowly and my hand began to hurt from holding it.

I appreciate that the Light Board for Diamond Painting is very thin and light weight. It comes with a plastic stand, cord and two clips. All you need to do is plug it into a wall adapter or a power bank to use. I like using a power bank so I can move it around with me and no fear of anyone tripping on my cord. The clips that came with mine are a different color than those in the picture, but that doesn't affect the use. I just wanted to note that. The plastic stand does fold down flat, but it's a bend in a solid piece of plastic, so it should be handled carefully. The lights are brighter on the powered side, so the light isn't even but it will work for most projects. You can even put it on a clip board.

I like to trace patterns to transfer for hand embroidery. I used to use a window, but this isn't so easy for me as I work at night and keep a night shift schedule. Whenever I wanted to transfer a pattern, I had to wait for a sunny day when I had the energy and time to do it. I like that this light board makes it easier for me to do this anytime I want. I have a diamond painting kit, so I will probably use this for that project as well. There are several times this will come in handy for me. It's very flat, so it stores easily with my paper stash.

This isn't a bad value, but I do think the dotted material embedded in the acrylic takes away from some of the potential light that could be used to light the board a little better. This might be more for the diamond painting though. I just like to have crafting tools that can meet multiple needs. This is also a good surface for doing different paper crafts. I want to try it for some origami folding, so I can just see better than I usually can under lamp light.

I would say that this is a fair value because it does come with some accessories. It is just a little annoying that the light is much brighter on one end, but doesn't inhibit my use of the light board.

I wanted to attach a part of a dress that has a collar separate from the bodice of a dress with tiny chain for one of my bjds (dolls) and I needed some tiny eyelets and a way to set them. I'm using jump rings between each eyelet and length of jewelry chain. I'm happy that the vendor, RD garden and Amazon made this available for review. This one is a set for eyelets of only 1mm! These will even work for very small dolls like Barbie. The eyelets measure 1mm across the inner hole of the face, 2.5mm including the outer rim of the face and are 3mm deep. You can set them in any material up to 2mm in thickness, as you need some space on the eyelet to roll under to form an inner ring for security. You get 100PCS Color Mix Tiny 1.0 mm eyelets and the setting tools which include the anvil, punch and setter. The anvil is 18mm long and 21mm across. Each of the four colors of metal eyelets come in their own little resealable bag. The whole kit is inside it's own padded envelope. I'm using my watch hammer to set them, but getting some practice before putting them into my doll's garment. These would also work well for paper or a variety of other materials.

I'm looking forward to the next meeting of my doll collector's group. We've discussed before where to find both miniature eyelets and the tools to set them and came up short. Now, I know exactly where to find them, Amazon!

Here is a link to a page of other products, including other very small sized eyelets and tools. I will be experimenting with the different sizes very soon!

https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A3RBAUBJ276VB1&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER

Wow, this is a very comprehensive kit of materials to use. I wasn't expecting so many stones, but there are a lot once I saw them in person. I'm especially excited to see the studs. The plastic box is a nice touch and there's a little extra space inside in case you want to add some more of your own supplies. Everything is included to start in the kit. Instructions are also included. I learned to leave the article to set for 24-hours, which I did not know before, but now I do. All you need is some fabric, a surface, an outlet and a design. I like to draw my own designs to embellish some of the little doll clothes I make, but I'm seriously considering dressing up some commercially made Barbie clothes as they could use a little something extra. Most of the rhinestones included are smaller scale, which is great for my purposes, but for others this may be a disadvantage. All the standard sized tips are included. The ZEAVOLA Wool Ironing Mat is 12mm thick, or about 1/2 inch. I made a horrible mistake at one point to iron on top of a towel with steam on a finished wood table, I ruined it. This is a great way to prevent that. I've been using ironing mats since. I appreciate that a silicone iron pad is also included, that is a great idea to include these in a set. I like that it's thick. I sew for dolls of all sizes, so I can place pins in very small pieces and use my applique iron for those, and keep each piece of the tiny garment organized. Knits don't stretch out on top of this mat, the fibers grip the cut pieces into place. It's also gentle on my thin fabrics, even the smooth ones. This is incredibly useful to me, and I wish I had one of these a long time ago. It's much better than the ironing mat I bought last year! My old one was layered and shifted fabrics.

I'm putting a binder clip on one end to store this on a wall. This mat will need to be stored flat. It's not very heavy and very light weight, so you can take it with you if you may need to do that.

It's nice there are different sizes to choose. I will be showing this off to my doll club friends, and I'm sure some of them will be pleased with the performance of the mat. I hope to convert some of them, and make their sewing projects much easier.

This would make a nice gift for any level of fabric artist in your life, or yourself. These are all very useful notions to have on hand.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

There is some prep work involved. This will need to be allowed to flatten, or be helped along with some ironing. The canvas will need to be mounted onto a frame before painting. There is a wall-mounted hook and screw included with the 3 brushes and labeled paint pots. The description is a bit off as it's labeled to be oil paints, but this is actually an Acrylic Paint set. I'm a little bit concerned that the potted paint may be a bit lacking for some of the heavier use colors, so they may need to be thinned a little bit to cover all the designated areas. I strongly suggesting using distilled water to do this. Otherwise, you will need to supply your own supplemental supply of paint. It's a beautiful design, just not quite ready to go out of the package.

I'm being a bit critical of this product because it does mention it to be an oil paint set, it is not. It is acrylic. Also, it does not mention that the canvas will need to be framed, nor offer any instructions or suggestions to do so. You are on your own to frame it. It definitely needs mounted on a frame first, as the canvas included is a bit heavier than I expected.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

I really like that this is so light weight. I put it in a padded portfolio holder and took it on a short trip to visit my mom. I just plugged it into a power bank so I could do this on any table surface. It has it's own USB cord and some handy magnets, so no marring of the photos! I made some trace over copies of some old family photos I can transfer onto a canvas to paint. It's helping me to make some really nice gifts for the older members of my family for Christmas.

Before, I made some templates onto cloth to do my own hand-stitched embroidery. I used to tape paper onto my patio door to do this. The problem was, I live and work at night. On my nights off I usually have the time and energy for this around 2am or so, when it's dark. So, I ended up doing this either when I first wake or right before my bedtime. In addition to having to do this in an awkward position, I would have to dodge other family members and dogs wanting to open and shut the door. It worked, but using A4 Light Board Dimmable Brightness Portable Ultra-Thin Tracing Light Box is something I can do literally anywhere.

For anyone that does so many different projects, those that draw and paint or even read or do photography, this is going to come to be very handy. I have considered some of the similar light boxes the big crafting stores have offered in the past, but this is a much better value and it's portable!

I cannot speak in regards to durability, but if anything happens, I will update my rating.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

This makes for a really nice crafting project. No tools are required, so it's great for little ones too. It's exactly what I expected and they are packaged in a sturdy resealable bag. These are getting involved with a wood burning project at my house. I also want to paint a few using stencils. They remind me of the cool projects my uncle used to make when I was a kid. They also smell really good, and bring back many happy memories. These would be great to make some nice memories of your own. I highly recommend them. There is twine included, which is just a bonus to me. I may use some of it and also what I have on hand in my arts/crafting stash. Imagination is the only limitation using the 5ARTH Natural Wood Slices.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

I love the Chalkola Painting Canvases Variety Pack. It's nice to get a whole set of the most commonly used frames, so these will be very easy for me to frame at home. They are thick and sturdy. I appreciate they came as a set and in their own box, as I will be using that for storage between sessions.

It's a good value and I'll happily order some again when these are all used.

Thanks so much for reading my review.

I'm so happy to have had a chance to try a portable airbrush. It's a very basic model, but other brushes can be mounted onto the mini compressor for those that would like to do so. It's charged very quickly! It's so much more quiet. I paint some dolls on occasion, and this does great with Liquitex varnish for sealing my coloring work. It works well for this as the tip is .4mm. This can be used in the same way as most any airbrush. It is packaged well in it's own sturdy chipboard box. Foam is cut to cushion each piece.

Just as a helpful hint, It's always a good idea to become familiar with a complete break-down of the brush parts. This allows for the more through parts cleaning, and will make any airbrush last much longer and assure your work is consistent.

My only negative criticism is with the airbrush part (compressor is amazing for it's size). The trigger on the airbrush does not make easy adjustments to the flow rate of paint, even though the air passes just fine. This may not be due to the brush, it could be a user error. It's really the only thing that was a little annoying. I'm sure I will get plenty of use from this brush.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

Hiseanllo 56 Patterns Mandala Template Stencils are packaged in sheets of four that you can break-away yourself or keep them as they are. I'm using them for a variety of projects. Some have nice motifs I will be using with an embossing tool for card-making. I'm also going to use some currently for airbrushing and wood burning. I'm tracing them onto fabric to apply some hotfix rhinestones as well. The uses are as endless as your imagination. There were some loose parts from the die-cut process that were easy to remove with my fingers. I didn't find this to be troublesome, but I thought I should mention it. The 56 patterns are varied. Some have nice corners that will look great embossed onto metallic paper. The plastic sheets are thick enough to withstand tracing, but not so much that I would worry about my embossing tool perforating thin papers. I like that the Mandala patterns are so varied but still stick to the theme very well. I like the repeated patterns of trees and triangles as they will look super cute using an airbrush over brown craft paper to make as home-made gift wrap.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful. I'm off to the craft room to continue playing with these!

Pagos Art Easel Stand Beech Wood is packaged well. The instructions are presented as a numbered diagram. I didn't find that terribly difficult to understand. Just follow the steps as they are numbered and don't tighten anything too tight until all pieces are in place because there are fronts and backs to some of the pieces. The wood is nicely finished and smooth. I think it's maple, but listed as 'beech', I'm not sure but it looks very nice. The problems are all in the hardware. The hinge is pre-installed, which would be fine except it's a terrible hinge. Most easels have a little chain to keep the hinge from being hyper-extended, this one has some other mechanism, which is okay but the way the wood is cut, it doesn't mount very smoothly, a chain would still help. The implanted nut to catch onto the center board in the upper horizontal piece that has the upper hinge piece has dislodged. I'm really not sure how to remedy that but I am strongly considering just gluing a peg in it's place.

If the hardware were better, this would be a good easel, but I will be replacing the hinge myself and doing some alterations to make it just a little more stable. It is very pretty, and at a good price point. So, maybe some will not find replacing a couple hardware pieces too difficult or will not find those to be bothersome to them.

Thanks for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

Hot-fix rhinestones usually come in a case that opens many compartment with a single lid. So, if you happen to knock it over, well, you have a big mess to sort. The Portable Bead Slots Diamond Embroidery Box eliminates that issue. It would also be great for diamond painting as it's a very similar problem. Some of the diamond painting kits come with the diamonds packaged in little baggies, which is hard for me to organize very well. I have one that had the diamonds in baggies that tore open, and no where to put the pieces between sessions.

There are several uses for these, especially for those that use seed beads. Powders and glitters would also be secure in the inner compartments. The containers inside the case remind me of Tic-Tac plastic boxes. There are also little labels to use on each inner container. The case has a handle, which is a nice touch. It's small enough to fit in most any craft bags or even larger purses. It's easy to pull off the lids of the inner containers.

It would also be a good way to store and sort flower and vegetable seeds between seasons inside your fridge. There really are many uses!

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

PAPERKIDDO Starry Space Designs Origami Paper (200 Sheets) is a good value for student-grade papers. They have a nice thickness for origami. Personally, I prefer kirigami, but that really doesn't make a difference for folding purposes. The only flaw I found is that not all of the paper is cut square. The designs are much prettier in person than in the photos. The ink is thoroughly set into the paper. You can smell the ink through the package. I like the scent of ink, but for those that are sensitive, this may be seen as another flaw.

These will be very useful for a variety of projects including card-making, scrap-booking, miniature dioramas, decoupage, paper beads and mixed-media collages. These are a great value, and for your stay-at-home students, they will have a lot of fun using the papers for a variety of projects.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

These are not the worst origami papers I have encountered, but they are not of the ultimate quality. PAPERKIDDO Japanese Washi Origami Paper (100 Sheets) are very beautiful. I've tried several brands and types of paper for origami, so I might be very particular in my tastes.

I do wish that the prints were a little smaller. The gold embossing is very elegant and beautiful, but once folded for origami or kirigami the print is lost in folds. The ink is great quality, and really set into the paper which you can tell from outside the packaging. The scent of ink isn't a bad thing for me, I really like it, but some people may be very sensitive to that. I love the packaging. Most of the paper I have purchased over the years comes in a bag, but I really like this box, it's great for both shipping and storage. Some of the papers are slightly off-center and a few were not exactly square.

PAPERKIDDO Japanese Washi Origami Paper (100 Sheets) would a best value for anyone that would like several sheets for a good price, but experienced hobbyists may be disappointed. I will use the rest of the supply for a variety of crafts besides origami and kirigami, so it's not a loss. These will be great for paper beads, decoupage, mixed media projects and making scenes miniatures. I think these would also look great in scrap-booking and card-making projects too.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

It's great that these tools all come together as a nice kit. You can make four different sized tapes, depending on needs. This is much better than the strore-bought bias tape, because I can only find 2 sizes locally. It's much better to make your own, because it saves lots of money and will match your projects perfectly. All you need is an ironing-safe surface, your prepared cut fabric and a little spray starch. I'm glad I will never need to buy pre-made bias tape ever again.

This is a great value for what is included in the kit. I doubt it will fail me anytime in the future. It would make a nice gift for yourself or anyone you know that could use it.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

I bought this last Christmas for my teenage son. I wasn't sure if he would like it, but he does. He's made many bracelets to give to others as gifts. It's really, really hard to find gifts for teen boys, but they seem to like the bracelets. It makes him feel good to give things away that he made himself. Finding extra paracord is very easy, as are extra clasps. It comes with several, but if you or your kids enjoy it, it will be important to get more. It came with a small booklet with simple instructions, but my son quickly found some more patterns to try online. He's had fun with it, and other kids like the bracelets, so I feel it was a good buy.

Easy to use once it’s warmed by kneading it in hands. I was very surprised on the good quality for the price! I’ve only used it for small projects, but no complaints! Definitely recommend for the price!
Clay came half way dried, crumbly and hard to model.
The picture is very deceiving the Clays are very small when the box came in the mail it's only about 6 in by 6 in and that's the whole kit

Not enough of any one color.

The individual packets of clay are so small that they would only be useful for 'accents' on a project. Most of the items that my grand-daughter wanted to make would require much more clay of a given color. So now I am back to shopping...
Love this so very much … only complaint is the thread ….. it’s cheap…. I up graded to the DMC floss…..
Great buy
Pencils were cracked and broken .. tin was bent...... not worth the money to ship it back for a replacement or refund....
Easy to use... I guess ... it’s different then holding the yarn with your fingers....
These were a disappointment, they are not smooth they snag the yarn the plastic tubing bends and creates static so the yarn wind slide very well .....
Great colors, will use all of them in my jewelry making. I would buy these again and in different colors.

This clay pack is just what I expected, wide variety of colors, although I don't know if I would use them all.  I think it will serve my needs very well in learning how to use polymer clay in different projects.
These beads are beautiful, well made and are better than expected. I would buy them again.

This kit would totally get a 5 star rating if it the bottles were slightly bigger. I ran out of colors super fast it seemed and the kit claims to dye up to 36 items. Which is why it gets a 4 star rating.
Granted I was able to dye the following shirts: One 2x, 2 XL's, 2 Med, 2 YL, 3 YM, 2 YS, and a 24mon
But again... I ran out of dye colors fast and had to mix them up towards the end just to finish the shirts.
These for sure do leak quite a bit.  So make sure to have a piece of paper to help absorb the leak through.  Otherwise these are great!
Great color selection. If coloring a large piece then they will run out quick.
Nice set! Lots to play with. Clay is a little wet and hard but once you work it a little, it’s great. No complaints, wonderful for the price.
Size of the sponge applicator end, as it was far larger than I had anticipated. This is a great design. The handle is comfortable to hold and flexible enough to maneuver.
Perfect and handy sewing kit with all the required sewing supplies. Great for travel. great size and colors
Good it works as expected !!
Good product and very useful !! good colors
Great to keep small things separated.
Fun paint for my kid. he loves to do painting and I like the variety of colors you get in this box. It is perfect for him and washes out well.

We ordered this for my kid to play around. It comes in nice box with great precision tools to carve or design as you like.  This clay is very high quality product, it can be used multiple times unlike other kids play clays. Even teen kids for  who are interested in crafting, for sure they would love it.
These are amazing markers!! I love the vibrant colors! Since the markers are double-ended, you have the option for a fine line or a thicker, full stroke.

This stuff was an amazing time! It’s fluffy and super smooth.  The clay feels like a stretchy, soft substance that isn’t sticky. You can mold it into anything and then leave it out to dry. It dries into a very lightweight, airy foam type consistency. My son was even able to make bouncy balls with this because when it dries, it’s bouncy. Pretty cool!
My daughter loves these, and uses all of them!
My daughters love these, lots of fun to paint! Very cute pictures. You get 2 of the same pucture, and a total of 12. So there are 6 different designs. Good buy and worth it!
Perfect gift for my daughter, she loves it! This is hours and hours of fun creativity.
My daughter loves it she really likes this she's doing a lot of little stuff I highly recommend this product

Product is exactly as described! Soft smooth clay, easy to use. A variety of accessories and tools. Although, I do wish it came with a rolling pin. But all in all, it's a nice product and came super fast and packaged nicely.
Kids love it
Lots of colors which I love but not a lot of clay per color. Still great though.

This comes with a lot of fixings for slime but doesn't come with anything to make the actual slime. I felt like that wasn't communicated well, plus it all comes in small bags in a larger bag and was messy but sortable.

I truly love how this set has so many diverse colors. No one color will be the same, and you won't get bored of using one or a few colors. I definitely find it amazing how soft and pliable the clay is, and I can easily mix different colors together to form secondary colors. The customer service is amazing and very responsive as well, which I truly appreciate because I had a question. I also love how this kit comes with so many tools and materials you might need to create your own jewelry or clay model! The tools here work really well even though they're plastic and look a little weak, they're really sturdy and works great.
Extremely oily, it’s easy to shape, but leaves an oil residue behind.

The markings reappear after a short period of time, especially if it was a spot you used a significant amount of ink. It’s hard not to use a lot of ink since the markings didn’t show up very well to begin with and the ink was dry in 3 of the 4 pens I received. The ink would also come out very blotchy.
My dogs tore a hole in the couch… again. We used this to cover the hole before having people over for the holidays, it works great.
Product is much darker than in the photo but we used them anyways. So far so good, they adhered well to my microfiber couch.
I have a 1 year old son and he loves these. At first he didn’t quite understand but he quickly picked it up. The colors are very bold and the crayons are perfect for little fingers
Product appears used. Did not include any of the tools or accessories. Disappointing waste of money.
Just as described
Husband still working on put it together. Happy with it
A nice addition to our town.
Missing at least half the colors shown in the box and in this product listing.
Wrong diamonds/beads were sent with this kit.
Customer service is good. Decent for kids who is trying it for the first time
Good product, my 10 year old loves making jewelry, and playing with it. And few item were missing so they send us replacement, good customer service.
I love these conte crayons with my charcoals
It has more pages than most for the overall value

put on some leather cord and looks great!!!
using to scrapbook photos of my best memories
very good
great value for the money!
they are beautiful wooden beads!

These feel so good and are beautiful

My daughter was thrilled with this Christmas gift. She’s a nine year old artist and loves to make gifts. This has great tools and accessories with a great amount of colors to be as creative as she wants to be!
Might be a user issue but I had really hoped to use for writing on a painting I did. It was a mess.

The product did not work for my daughter’s project (slime) and we used the whole packet. We did “charge” it in full sunlight with no results. That being said, the vendor was very responsive and refunded my purchase without any issue.

I liked that it was fairly soft didn't have to soften it up like most clays on the market, I like all the colors and the case and tools. What I didn't like right away was even though good assortment and beautiful colors the packages were way to small like if you wanted to make something bigger than 1 or 2 inches you would have to order more of the color clay you need, also there's a book that comes with it,it shows you how to make like a lil fairy house when you go to build the base of the house there is not enough in any one color to make that house found same thing with teddy bear that is on there. Also once you open the clay if you are not gonna use right away you need something to seal it in I would personally as the company use small medicine ziploc bags, and once they get put back into the case there is no room for them. Overall it's great for kids they wont care about any of these things unless they wanna use more than once. Good for starters I guess my kids did have lot of fun thats all that mattered to me.

My  8 year old daughter enjoyed making lots of colorful creations with this clay kit. It appeared easy to shape. She used the tools included with it to help sculpt her items. The finished product turned out well after baking. She needed to fix about 2 items with a small piece that came off after cooking.
This wire is lovely and easy to work with. It makes pretty jewelry. I recommend it.

I love the colors of these skeins of yarn. Very pretty. It is nice and soft. I think this yarn is more for experience crafters. It is so small that it is difficult to work with for beginners, like me.

This crochet kit is awesome. I am a brand-spanking noob at crochet. I am impressed by all the great tools in this kit. It has everything I need to get started, and I've tried my hand at...well...making a few chains. :) I'm working on it. I'm using the yarn in this kit as my practice yarn. It's not as nice as the yarns I can get at the store, but it's great for learning. The case is useful, but not very good quality. You might want to search for a better case to carry and organize your stuff. Great for beginners. I recommend it.
This wax melter is great. It is easy to use and I love the pouring spout. It makes it really easy to fill candle jars. I am very happy with this and recommend it.

This jeweler's loupe is great. It swings out of its case easily. The magnification is capable of making the tiniest hallmarks readable. An essential tool for anyone who deals with antiques and collectibles, not just jewelry. I love it and highly recommend it.
Beautiful scissors. They are sharp, comfortable to use and hold, and I love them. I recommend them.
Cheap pins. Aren't sturdy, but you have a lot of them to make up for that.

This is a neat magnet on a stick. It's great for lots of little tasks. I like using it for picking up pins and needles that I inevitably spill on the floor when I am sewing. I also use it on thrifted jewelry to see if it is real gold or silver (if it's magnetic...it's not real).  I love it and recommend it.

This has a nice variety of needles for most of your sewing tasks. I love the case it comes in, it is one of my favorite types of needle cases. It keeps the needles sorted by size and you can easily choose what size needle you need by turning the clear lid till the hole at the top reaches the chamber of needle you desire. Then, just tip it over and the needle comes out. Love it. I recommend it.
This works great. I've lost a lot of weight and don't know what size my fingers are anymore. Makes it hard to buy anything new. I used this to get the correct sizes and it works great. I recommend.
Everything you need to make some really pretty jewelry. Love it.
These are pretty little tins. Great for making candles, but you could use them for other things too, like organizing craft components, or keeping spices in the kitchen. I love them.
Nice little jump rings. They are easy to snap into place with a pair of pliers. I like them.

This is a very nice sewing basket. It has a nice large storage well and a nice tray for smaller tools. I love the little puffy poof on the underside of the lid for sticking pins and needles into. The only thing I wish it had in addition is either a sleeve on the inside of the lid or a space on the tray for my scissors to fit into. They are the most used tool, and it annoys me that I have to lift the try to get to them. It's a small thing, but it bugs me.
This is a great kit. It has everything you need to create beautiful art. I like the colors included. I like that it includes the canvases. I highly recommend.

Where have these things been all my life! I love these things1 They work great. My old eyes have a real problem these days seeing well enough to thread needles. This makes it really easy. I like it better than the usual little loopy thing that I usually use. I highly recommend these.
This pot is great for melting wax. The wax pours easily from the spout. It does everything it is supposed to do. I highly recommend this.

This is a nice selection of embroidery needles, suitable for all kinds of needlework. I love the pretty case that houses them. This would make a great gift for anyone who does needlework. I highly recommend.

Such a pretty rainbow of colors! I love this handy set of sequins. I like having them on hand to use any any project that comes to mind. I am currently using them on making button bracelets. They are easy to use. I highly recommend this kit.
I love these pretty bees. They are so sweet. I'm going to put these on a bracelet that I am making with buttons. They are going to be a nice, sparkly focal point for it. I highly recommend these.
Very nice paper pad. It works great with watercolors. It holds it's shap when wet. I highly recommend.
I love these beeswax pellets. They are easy to use, melt easily and have a lovely honey fragrance. It makes really nice candles. I do recommend this.

These mica powders are really pretty. I like the wide variety of colors in the kit. I was a little disappointed in the final look of the pieces I was making though. I was expecting more shimmer. While the blue that I used made a beautiful, solid, blue color, it didn't really look very shimmery. I also used the pearl white. It also was underwhelming. They were white, but lacking in any glimmer.  Useful, and pretty. I do recommend. Just know that the results may look different than what you are expecting.

I used this to try and make some jewelry. I expected it to dry hard, like the vases that I have already that have this type of resin in them. I was surprised at how rubbery this stuff is. Some of it didn't cure at all. I waited 3 days. Some of the pieces cured to the hardness of the silicone that I molded it in. Some of the pieces never cured and remained tacky. I did use some additives. The ones that didn't cure at all I had put a little bit of glitter in them. None of those cured and are still tacky. I couldn't even get them out of the molds. I had to throw it all away. The blue and white pieces had mica powder in them. They cured better, but a few of them didn't cure all the way. In one of the pictures you can see a blue one with a messed up area. That spot is still wet, while the rest of the piece is dry, but rubbery. These were brand new molds. I've never used them before, so they shouldn't have any weird residue on them to cause this issue.

I used shot glasses to measure a 1 to 1 mix as closely as I could. I made a mix of 1/2 oz for each of the two components. I have no idea why glitter would affect this to make it not harden. That just doesn't make sense.

At any rate, this attempt was a complete fail. I can't use any of the results. It all went in the trash. I do not recommend this stuff.

Wow, these scissors are so pretty! Yes, they are scissors. They are sharp. They cut thread beatifully. They do everything a great pair of thread scissors should do, but dang they are so PRETTY! I mean, I will go for the pretty ones every time. They just make you feel fancy when you use them. Silly, I know, but I love them. I highly recommend them to anyone doing needlework of all kinds.

I like these brushes. They apply the paint smoothly and, most importantly, they don't shed. I did a beginners painting project with my husband using these brushes, some other cheap brushes I had on hand, and acrylic paints. He's a beginner. I'm a little more experienced painter. Right away, we noticed a difference in the brushes. The other cheap brushes we were using began to shed bristles all over the painting. It was horrible. They were everywhere. Not so with these brushes. They applied the paint smoothly, made good, crisp lines, nice texture smudges and didn't shed at all. I'm so glad I picked up this set. The others I threw in the trash. I highly recommend these to anyone.

Neat little pack of canvases.  Great for oil or acrylic painting, fabric art, mixed media and all kinds of other crafting uses. Canvas can be so expensive. This pack makes using canvas more accessible to the average person. I highly recommend for anyone.
What's to say. It's chalk. It makes lines on fabric and works great. It's easy to use and I highly recommend it.

NOT for beginners, and NOT for children!

This product is just plain scary. It comes with loads of warnings about fumes, dust and and other dangerous elements of this resin. None of those warnings were in the description on the product page. I would not have purchased this if I had known.

I am unable to use this. It requires high-powered UV lamps, which I don't have. It also requires an open, well-ventilated space to work in...which I also don't have. I could use it, maybe...outside...but that wil have to wait until summer, because it requires a room temperature in the 70's, and it is winter right now.

This product is just too much trouble to be worth it.

I am very happy with this little pack of fat quarters. It has a nice selection of Christmasy patterns to make something for yourself or to give for Christmas.  They are suitable for all sorts of crafts; quilts, mason jar-lid covers, small Christmas ornaments, etc. I highly recommend.

The quality of faux fur is determined by the thickness of the pile. More fibers per inch will produce a thick, luxurious pile that stands up on it's own. The fibers-per-inch on this fabric is very sparse. The pile mostly just lays down flat, because there aren't enough other fibers for them to hold each other up. I also had a problem with lots of the fur falling out all over the place. Not a fan. I do not recommend.

This copper leaf is very pretty. I've never used this kind of product before, but I've seen gold leaf used, and thought it would be similar. It was. metal leaf is really tricky to work with. The package didn't come with instructions, so I don't know if I was using the right kind of glue or not. I put the copper leaf on an angel ornament I was making. It came out really cute. The copper leaf didn't stick very well in some places, and I can't smooth it out without it just coming off. I think I need to put a clear top coat over it.

Recommendations? This is a neat way to add a interesting touch to craft or art projects. Prepare ahead of time by watching a video on how to use this stuff, and for what kind of glue to use.

It is so nice to have such a nice selection of colors on hand for when the mood strikes me to paint something. This kit has just about any color I would need. It saves me a trip to the store. The paint works well, goes on smooth and covers well. Great for crafts. I do recommend.

I really like this kit. It is full of lots of pretty colors of clay. The carrying case it comes in is a bonus. It keeps your clay organized and you can take it anywhere. The clay is easy to use, easy to bake, and loads of fun. I highly recommend to anyone.

The idea of putting a light on a seam ripper is absolutely brilliant. Why haven't I seen any thing like this before? It really helps my aging eyes to see better, especially when working with small, hard to see threads. My only complaint is that the handle is too short and slim. It makes it hard to grasp, especially with my arthritic hands. I will still use it though, because the light is just to valuable to me. I do recommend.

This hot glue gun works really well. It feeds the glue sticks in nicely, it is easy to load, and the trigger is easy to pull. My only caution is that the glue gets REALLY HOT! I mean, it was steaming when I used it. Be really careful not to get any of the glue on you or you could be looking at a really serious burn. I also recommend putting something down to set it on while you are working to catch drips. I just used a piece of cardboard. I may eventually make a holder for it out of wood. The foot-rest that it comes with works, but it tips over too easily. I like something more stable to prevent accidents. I do recommend this though. It does a good job, and I like that it comes with plenty of glue sticks.
I like how this paint tray looks like a pretty, little flower. Very cute. The petal-cups are roomy to hold a good amount of paint. It is easy to clean. I love it! I highly recommend.

I got this for using at my craft table. I wanted it to protect the table from water and paint when I do my crafting and painting. It works great. It has a nice big surface area for optimal coverage, and it cleans up really easily. If acrylic paint dries on it, it just peels off. This will be great for kids crafting, baking or anything that you need to protect a work surface for. I highly recommend.
These little, gemmy buttons are really pretty. They are suitable for use as buttons or decorations on just about anything you want. I plan to use them to make button bracelets. I highly recommend.

These patches work great. I used one to repair a very bad rip in a favorite flannel robe of mine. It was really easy to use. Just heat up your iron, lay the patch on the fabric (remove the plastic cover first), press, and you are done. The patch is secure and shows no signs of peeling off. I like the nice mix of colors available in this packet. I highly recommend it.

This is an off-brand Velcro. It works just the same and seems to be made of similar quality. I can't tell the difference between the two brands, and I have some of each. I used it on a pillowcase, and it worked great. It was easy to sew on. I sewed it on by hand. I've used my machine before on Velcro-like products and it just never goes well. I recommend this as an alternative to the more expensive Velcro.

I love these scissors. What beauties! They are sturdy and well-made. They are sharp and cut easily, and smoothly  through most any kind of fabric. I've always wanted a really quality pair of scissors and these are it. I am confident that these will last me for many years, and many sewing projects over their lifetime. I highly recommend them.

This yarn bag is really handy. It is roomy, useful, and pretty.  I like the clear plastic cover that lets you see the colors of the skein below, and the built-in holes to draw your yarn though. Nice! There are lots of pockets for holding your needles, scissors and other tools. I highly recommend.

This organizer is very useful. I like all the little containers. They are the perfect size for helping me organize my seed beads and findings. They are fairly see-through so it is easy to see what is inside. If you need to organize small things, this is perfect for you.

This is a very nice embroidery kit especially suited for a beginner. It has everything you need to make a lovely piece of embroidery that can be made into pillow, wall art or other decorative item; thread, needles, stretcher, fabric and instructions on how to make the stitches. Fantastic!

I love these seam rippers! What I love the most is how comfortable they are to hold. The rounded, slightly pudgy handles just fits so well in the hand. Most seam rippers I have used before have rectangular, plastic handles. The handle's sharp corners always dug into my hand and hurt. These seam rippers don't do that. They are so comfortable. The seam ripper is sharp and well suited to the task of ripping seams. I highly recommend these to anyone who wants a better way to rip seams without pain.

This is a great set of essential sewing tools. It is so handy to have extra needles on hand in the off-chance that one should break while I am sewing. It will save me a trip to the sewing store in the middle of a project. I like that there are a few bobbins in the kit as well. You can never have enough bobbins. I highly recommend this kit.

This kit has everything you need to make some nice smelling candles to keep or give as gifts. The only problem is that if comes with NO instructions. Well, the digital thermometer comes with instructions as to how to turn it on and off, but that's it. How do you melt the wax? Who knows, they don't tell you. That is concerning, because I know that paraffin wax can't be melted over direct heat or risk a fire. This kit uses soy wax, but I don't know anything about soy wax, so I don't know if that same danger applies. I'll have to look it up online to find out how to do it safely. How much essential oil do you use per amount of wax? I don't know, there are no instructions that tell you what to do. The orange and tea tree oil smell really nice. The lavender is just okay...it reminds me a little of soap. The peppermint oil smells terrible, harsh, chemically. I have a peppermint plant in my garden and this oil smells nothing like it...at all. I rated this kit low. It is not a "beginner" kit if there are no instructions telling you how to do anything. With some research on Google and YouTube, I'm sure I will be able to use this kit, but it is very disappointing that it can't be used upon opening the box.

I like this travel sewing kit. It seems to have decent quality items in it, a wide variety of thread colors, and the most useful tools for just about any sewing emergency you may have. The case is sturdy and keeps everything contained and neat. I recommend.

I haven't done a paint-by-number since I was a kid. This painting kit is all grown up. A huge step up from the kind I was doing all those years ago. The principle is still the same though. Match the paint (all numbered) to the numbered places that match it on the canvas...and yes...it is canvas. Pretty easy. The challenge is making sure you find all the correct spots to paint for each color, and trying not to use up the pot of paint before you have finished painting that color. The paint pots are really tiny, so I'm a little worried about running out. I plan to do this with my daughter-in-law...once social distancing is over.

One of the main reasons you would buy a paint kit is to have good, quality materials to paint with. You should expect paint that is easy to work, and that includes useful colors to create your project. Right off the bat, there was a problem with the paint. I was trying to paint a shamrock, and chose Emerald (green) as my paint color. To my surprise, the paint that squeezed out of the tube was decidedly NOT emerald green. It was more of a light, celery green. That is a huge error in my book, as emerald is a universally understood color, based on the rich green gem it is named from. This was VERY disappointing to me. That fact alone is the reason for the poor rating that I give this kit. If they can't even get the colors right, there is no reason to buy the kit. The brushes seemed nice, but you can buy nice brushes without getting a paint kit with wonky colored paints. Skip this kit and look elsewhere.

I have always wanted to try felting, and this kit was the perfect opportunity to finally do it. It seems to come with just about everything you need to make a small to medium-sized project, including wool, tools, templates, findings and embellishments.. I decided to make a shamrock brooch for St. Patrick's Day. I had watched some YouTube videos recently on how to make things with wool felting, so I felt that I knew what I needed do. It's a good thing too, because there was very little in the way of instructions included in the kit. There was one page only, that just showed how to load the needle into the tool for felting. More instructions and information for the other tools would have been nice. The tool in the last picture has several needles in it. I could not figure out how to use it. The lack of information alone kept me from rating this kit higher.

Anyway, I successfully crafted a felt shamrock brooch, as you can see in the pictures. It was pretty easy and very enjoyable. I think I did pretty good for a first time. I would have been lost without having watched those videos beforehand. If you are new to the craft, either load up on YouTubes on how to do felting, or buy a basic instruction book along with the kit.

One thing that is essential in a good set of watercolors, is clear colors that are able to blend well and flow effortlessly with the water. This kit does not provide that. I compared the pain in this kit with my tried and true Holbein travel set that I have been using for years. I painted a shamrock. The lobe in the upper left was painted with my Holbein paints. The rest is done with the paints in this kit.

I found this paint to be very problematic in a few areas. First of all, the paint doesn't dissolve well or flow easily in water. When you touch a paint-loaded brush to wet paper, the paint should readily flow out and spread. This paint does not want to spread. You have to pull it around with the brush leaving brush trails wherever you go. I tried to capture this in the pictures above, but I don't think it shows up very well. In the second picture, I tried to show the other problem with the paint. It was leaving solid clumps of paint all over the place...a result of the paint not dissolving well. Perhaps it would work better if you filled pallet cups with a bit of paint and let it dry, using it more like cake paints. I admit that I am not used to using watercolors from a tube, so maybe this is user error? But, it still shouldn't be making brush trails on your piece of art.

The next problem is that the colors don't blend well. I found the results to be rather muddy. I used the same colors with both sets of paints, Emerald Green and Crimson. In the last picture, you can see that the Holbein paints blended nicely and created a nice shaded effect on the leaf. The rest did not blend at all really. In fact, the colors mixed and became really muddy.

I consider myself an adept level watercolorist. I would not recommend these paints for beginners or even adept level painters. Perhaps expert painters would know how to deal with these paints, but honestly, if you are that level of a painter, you would want better paints than this anyway.

I love this lamp! I love how versatile it is. It has 3 different brightness settings at the touch of a button, it is lightweight for ease of moving it around, and can either be used as a desk lamp or clamped to something. My favorite part is the magnifiers. It has a larger viewing area for the lowest magnification...suitable for most task applications, and a small area of more intense magnification when needed. I will be using this lamp for my sewing, embroidery and jewelry making projects. I highly recommend this lamp.

This is a fantastic bundle of fun! So many beautiful colors ready to make something lovely with. I like having embroidery thread on hand in lots of colors, so that if the mood strickes me, I can start creating something right away. There are enough color choices here to be able to start most any project, except for maybe something that requires specific colors not included in this bundle. The thread seems to be quality. My only negative comment is that the thread seems to be very matte looking...not lustrous as I am used to embroidery thread looking. Maybe that's a think now-a-days. Anyway, if you like having thread on hand to pick up and use as whimsy strikes you, this is a great buy.

I really like this embroidery kit. The bamboo frames seem sturdy. They fit tight and secure. They are adjustable to loosen or tighten for accommodating different thicknesses of fabric. I like the array of designs in the kits. They are very pretty and can be done by any skill level, novice or expert alike.

When you read the title description of this listing you will think that you are getting Tourmaline beads for jewelry making. You would be wrong. These are NOT tourmaline beads. They are some sort of compounded, crystalline material that is shaped into round beads and dyed the color of tourmaline. I really take issue with wording in a listing that jumbles up the grammar and confuses the issue. Yes, it says "Color beads" but not putting that before the word "tourmaline", and without a hyphen misplaces the modifier and muddies the meaning.  A correct, descriptive title for these beads would be "Tourmaline-colored compound beads"...or something similar. I thought I was buying tourmaline beads...by the description. I was disappointed in what turned up. Titles and descriptions matter. They are supposed to describe what it is you are about to purchase. To jumble things up is either careless or misleading. Either way, steer clear of these beads.

This tool kit has a variety of cutting tips that should make any wood carver very happy. The tips are VERY sharp, and dig into the wood, making nice clean cuts. I like the handy carrying case that they come in, and it is nice that a sharpening stone is included. The only issue I have with them, is that the handles feel rather small to fit an adult hand (see pictures). If you have small hands, they will probably feel find to you. I would recommend this to anyone needing to do fine detail work in wood carving, but also the tools could be used for other crafts, like lino-block carving or scupting with clay.

These are really lovely markers! The tips feel almost like you are painting with a brush, instead of using a marker. The soft, tapered tip allows you to make strokes of varying widths...perfect for things like calligraphy and graphic design. The ink flows with a nice fluidity, the colors are nicely saturated, and overlapping strokes blend well into each other. I recommend these casual and professional artistic endeavors.

These pins are sharp and do what pins need to do. I used them on a couple of sewing projects and they preformed well. I find that the glass heads are a little too small for my liking. It just makes handling them more fiddly. Also, I have found quite a few pins without their heads, making them unusable.
Gorgeous charm. Fits Pandora bracelet perfectly! My daughter-in-law was overjoyed with this Christmas gift.
Very cute.

I bought this fur to make gnome beards and I couldn't be happier. This fur is long and thick but doesn't shed alot like some of the other furs I've worked with. Also the amount of fur for the price is great. Highly recommended!

I bought this tulle to make a big bird costume. It was so easy to work with and the finished product was perfect! The value was amazing compared to how much each roll goes for at my local craft store. The roll is huge and one was more than enough to complete the project!

As an avid crafter I know will find endless possibilities for this elastic. The price is great and I've already made a second and third purchase of different colors. This elastic is so soft yet durable and the colors are absolutely beautiful and vibrant!

This ribbon a a great value! It is so much cheaper than the local craft and big box stores. The quality is also better than expected. It is slightly thicker then the grosgrain I'm used to, but it's still very easy to work with. Highly recommend!
These binder 3 hole punches are very convenient. My only complaint is that one of them was broken when it arrived. I was able to glue it for now. They are made of plastic and seem to be fragile.
These are really great epoxy stickers. They are clear, stick well and fit perfectly in bottle caps. This price is the best I've seen so far. Have already purchased multiple times.
These charms are really cute. They are easily attached to jumprings to make jewelry. They would make great party favors. The amount of charms for the price is a great value!
This wax cord is perfect for jewelry making and other craft activities. The color variety is nice and the price is great.
Nice variety. I'm using these as hairbow centers. They are cute and work great for the job.
These necklaces are great quality. The quality is consistent with each individual piece. The price is also great. They arrived quickly.
These lobster clasps are perfect. They get the job done. Nice quality and easy to work with. I'm comfortable selling products made with these clasps. I would order again.

This is my first embroidery machine.  I'd never even touched one before. I have to say that this is very easy to learn.  I was able to embroider my very first project the same day I got it! Perfect for beginners!

I love this transfer tape! It is so essy to use. I've used it with many different projects and I've never had an issue. I love the added scraper tool better than any other tool I have. Needless to say I'm on my second roll and will definately be buying again.
Awesome little scissors! I bought them to use with my embroidery machine.  They are perfect.  They're comfortable,  easy to maneuver and sharp. Thinking about getting another pair for other projects.
Such a great variety! So many colors to choose from. This makes any project possible! Came with a free bonus!
Who wants boring scissors? These are really cool looking and also very sharp.
Exactly what I was looking for. And they came in a great little storage box!
This transfer tape works great. Sticky but not too sticky. My only complaint is that it is difficult to see where to place your decal because the tape is not transparent. Otherwise great product.

These mats are perfect! After using my old mats for so long I forgot what it was like to use a brand new mat! I use these mats a lot for my small business and they are holding up really well. Will definitely purchase again!
Good product. Consistent quality. I have used these for bows, ornaments and jewelery. Will purchase again and again
I use these for filling orders in my Etsy store. Great product and adds a nuce touch to my packaging!
I bought this vinyl for a big project and i can honestly say it worked great! Some glitter HTV is difficult to work with (in my opinion) but this vinyl was easy to work with and very sparkly!
I love the variety of colors. These elastics have a variety of uses and you can go to Youtube for many inspirational ideas.  I used these elastics to make headbands with interchangeble bow clips
This is a really great bundle! Such a good deal for the price. I've used this vinyl for many projects and it never disappoints.
Great clay kit for my young artist. We've had this kit for months and have yet to run out of clay. The booklet inside gives really cute ideas and the tools are really useful.
Great bottle caps. I use them for many projects and have no complaints at all. Will definately order again!
Received the clay today amazing delivery in one day. However there appears to be a pubic hair in one of the clay, will need to return this.
Great value. Releases resin easily. Clean up is almost not necessary. So glad I got it!
Fantastic value. Everything I need to get started without wasted time and money.
So good

Love

Love this set but I was missing one of the greens with mine but felt it was to much hassle to send back just over one missing color. But like I  said I do really like this set the only reason it is four stars not five is because of the missing pea green color.

Who doesn't love Sugru?  It can form into anything and it sticks so you can use it to repair anything that is broken.  This multi-color pack is great to keep on hand for emergencies.  I've got one set in my camping gear and one set in my toolbox at home!

My two oldest kids are a ten and eight year old girl. This kit was just fantastic for them. It was so special for them to put it together. It took them quite a while to do it. For the amount of money it cost, they got a fantastic amount of fun time out of it.

There are many things I liked about this book. I loved how extensive it was. There is so much to it. It has hundreds of different supplies for them to use to put together something nice. I love that they can use the creativity. Like many kids, my kids love playing on the tablet and video game systems so it was nice to have something that would keep them busy and not involve mindless games. There are so many ways they can do this. It is great for the summer when they have a lot of time.

I also thought it was very nice for their relationship to have something special like this to do. Sometimes they bicker, so I like to have something that helps keep them together. Finally, I liked that the final product is something they can keep forever. It really is something special for them to have when they are grown up. I really liked this and would recommend it.

My girls have little pieces of jewelry that they need a place for. This yarn tree is great for that. It gives them a place to hang their necklaces and bracelets so they don't get lost. It came out very pretty.

The craft part was fun for them to do. It came with a lot of different types of yarn. The colors looked very nice. It was easy and simple to do. It kept them busy for a little while, which was nice. It is great that they came out with something they could actually keep and use. I was happy with the quality. It seems like something that will last a while.

I think this would also be nice to give as a gift. I would recommend it.

This is a unique idea for a fun toy. My kids have unusual names so it is difficult to get them things that have their names on it. This is neat in that you can write whatever you want on the crayons. It was safe and easy to do. The kids had a lot of fun doing it. They could write their names or whatever they wanted. This would also be great for a teacher, troop leader, church or similar place where they have a bunch of kids together. You can give the kids crayons with their names on it. It makes a good gift for someone and is also fun for someone to make crayons as gifts. It's not super small, however, and does take up a little bit of room. My kids enjoyed it. It is also a unique gift.

This seemed much more laborious than I envisioned. It seems like it will take a while to make a frame and it will definitely not be easy to make it perfect. For a punch there sure were a lot of instructions and then  you do a little of the frame piece by piece. You only get the one shape with this kit...if you want others you have to buy it. I can see someone who really likes scrapbooking and has the time to do this, but it just seemed difficult, complicated, time consuming and hard to get perfect. It did work as it was supposed to, though.

This set of Artisto Premium Colored Pencils are great! It has a really high-end feel with its metal case with a color chart printed on the inside, three trays of premium-looking pencils and a pretty hefty feel when holding it in the hand.

These pencils have good coverage and with the soft-core lead, blending between colors is possible. While I'm not an artist, I do appreciate how different these pencils are from a crayola set that I buy for my kids.

The price of this set is also reasonable. Perhaps professional artists will find issues with these pencils when compared to more-expensive brands, someone like me would never be able to tell.

My daughter got into jewelry making and as with any hobby, there are a lot of tools and accessories to collect. This strand of INZONELAN 8MM Round Glass Stones were just what she needed for her custom bracelets and earrings.

These glass stones have subtle blue accents and are of good quality.

The YUBBAEX Splendid Gold Washi Tape Set stands out with unique patterns in red and gold.  There is an Asian flair to the designs.  It can really inspire some creativity with beautiful results for bullet-journal pages.

The YUBBAEX Bright Mix Gold Washi Tape Set comes with 27 rolls of colorful washi tape of various widths with well-designed patterns, many of which have shiny foil finishes.  They are great to put that extra detail to any bullet-journal page or scrapbook.  The adhesive is good and will not rip up the page (if you're careful) if they need to be removed and repositioned.
These keyrings are the flat-shaped type and they are exactly what my daughter needed for the little leather pendants she likes to make.
Perfect for cables, and you get several packs
These were sooo reasonably priced!!! Painted and glittered some. Really turned out cute. Amazing purchase

You will need to sand these before finishing. They worked great. I sanded and painted scenes on them, then stained outsides and had glass made for inside

LOTS OF LENGTH for wrapping presents and making HUGE bows! I wrapped the box a few times with it, then made a huge bow and embellished the bow with bumble bees that I had purchased through Amazon! It was GREAT!!!!
These bees have back cut, almost crystal looking heads! Really awesome! I put them all over a package and in the bows for a BA  BEE shower. Also, on the place cards. REALLY FUN!!!!!
Easy to use
Good for crafts with kids! Not the best if you’re making earrings. It bubbles after baking and creates imperfections.

I decided to dabble in the art of using polymer clay and this was the 1st item I bought.  Great kit for beginners! Had just about everything I needed to start! Love that it came with accessories to play around with....like making jewelry or a photo holder! Clay was a good softness as well.

A nice step up from Play-doh for our young kids! They've been enjoying making presents for themselves, each other, and us and baking them into permanent artifacts.

Just note that the packets are pretty small: projects work if they're at the jewelry or trinket scale. If your kids are into making useful pottery or recreating Rodin's work at full scale, look elsewhere.

Minuses: I now have a lot of vaguely turd-like baked polymer keychains and earrings.
Pluses: Endless supply of stocking stuffers for in-laws.

Win win, really.

This set has some good and bad things.
There is a good, bright color selection. But blocks are very small so can only make small items. Clay is very oily and can be a little difficult to mold if you have touched it too much. I found putting it in the freezer can help this problem temporarily.
Tools are okay.
It bakes nicely. I put a tent of tinfoil over it in the oven to prevent burning.
Good for beginners, and if you just want to get the feel for polymer clay before investing too much money in it.
Booklet is poorly translated into English, but you get the idea.

The clay itself was so dry it wouldnt mold into anything without crumbling into tiny pieces. It was hard. The accessories it comes with are awesome but you can't use the clay it came with to make them. My daughter was so disappointed. This was a Christmas gift and I had to go buy additional clay from the craft store so she could use the tools and accessories.
Nice product. Fun for my 9 year old daughter. Would have been nice if it came with more than one page to color.
Perfect for DIY wedding projects.
when i got it it was soooo oily I don't know if its because for the clay or... but be on the look for that. Everything else is great other than its smaller than i expected
Love the box ! it came with everything! the only con is that the beads are a bit out of place but other than that i love it :D
My daughters and I are really enjoying making earrings and little figures, nice quality clay and tons of different colors!
This clay does not dry completely. Baked it for the time it said. Will never purchase again

I bought this so I could make diapers for my son and his wife and their new baby!  They love them and I enjoyed a pattern that was easy to follow.

I was pleasantly surprised with this HTV. It’s an absolute breeze to weed, it cuts beautifully and adheres easily.

I used this at 300 for 20 seconds with my heat press 2x on the front and 1x on the back and it took to any fabric with ease.

It washes well with no peeling or lifting.

I’m typically a towel on a table kinda girl but I decided to try the heat press mat and I’m glad I did. This is a LIFE SAVER when doing smaller projects. It’s easier to maintain pressure. It doesn’t slide around. Plus you can take pretty good product pictures with it haha. I’ve used this both on mask and T-shirt vinyling. Worked beautifully.

Definitely happy with my purchase.

This kit was perfect for beginners. Instructions were simple and the process was easy as pie. The candles turned out well but the wax did not hold scent well. I absolutely hated the dye as it was not the colors listed and terrible messy. I would recommend a liquid dye. Overall decent kit and I would recommend.

While the sizes of clay were a bit skimpy in my opinion for a beginner this is the perfect box.

The instructions are terribly translated. The booklet has too few designs in my opinion.

For anyone wondering bake at 275 degrees for 15 minutes to set and harden the clay. Little to no fumage. Be sure to run oven exhaust and fan just Incase.

Dries to a matte finish. Easy to mold but does tend to get very flimsy the more you work with it.

All in all decent box for starting out.

I'm a little disappointed in the cure time. It seems to take forever to set.

The cap is easy to open but I have noticed it does tend to drip quite a bit.

I'm not blown away by this but it will get the job done until I find a better solution.

I made a quick and basic anklet to show how they go together nicely (it's in the video). I plan on making so much more. The quality of these beads are very nice. This is a great starter for polymer clay.  We are new to this and our items have baked nicely.  The colors are vivid.
I absolutely loved it. Was easy to follow, I didn't even do all those fancy stiches & it still turned out really good. I always wanted to try embroidery & this kit was awesome.

I have made a few things with this clay and I know you're supposed to make things thick and it takes days to dry but everything I make chips or breaks so easily. I even used a spray thing that's supposed to help with durability. Hard to gift if it breaks so easily.
Works exactly as it should. It's nice that you get both the sizer and the keychain with the sizes. Makes for a more accurate measurement.

Super fragile. It took a bit to get it together and after we did we couldn't really mess with it or else something would fall off. We just put it on a shelf and haven't touched it since. Definitely thought it was going to be bigger too. All out figurines are bigger. haha
