There are so many pieces that I don't think I'll run out anytime soon. Love the multiple colors from rose gold to brass. The pliers are perfect as I was trying to spread the hoops by hand prior to this set.

I am by no means a painter. I just really enjoy watercolors. I love the lightness they lend to the painting. It's more of a relaxing hobby rather than one I intend to share with others. You barely need a dime sized amount to use for a whole picture. And you can let them dry out and then just add water to start again. They also mix well.

This clay only takes a bit to warm up and get soft. I do have to add that with some colors, like red and black, the color does dye your hand a bit. At least when the clay gets soft and you're kneading it a lot. Just don't go from something like red to white without washing your hands first.

This could just be because of how I used them but they didn't seem to stand out. You see all the time on TIKTOK these days women making marble effect jewelry by mixing in these sheets with polymer clay. I've tried it a few times and there definitely isn't a marble effect. It's just got some shiny spots. I'm not sure if I'm using them wrong but this is where I'm at now.

I just recently started making earrings out of polymer clay. Not to sell but for myself. It's fun. These guys are super easy to use. They hook on and you just push the clamps together and viola you have an earring. Looking forward to making more.
Will be returning this product it looks used package was open and missing pieces.. please give directions on how to go this and get a full return.. thank you
My kids love this clay. Beautiful colors, easy to work with and great price.

If you’re a broke beginner-ish polymer clay sculptor- these are great! I personally did not care about the colors that come with since I paint+seal all of my pieces, but keep in mind that they do the thing that almost every children’s-marketed cheap art supply does, which is adding a bunch of muddy colors that kids aren’t likely to use and only putting a few primary colors in. If you are wanting mostly/all brighter colors, I would suggest looking at individually packaged polymer clay! It also comes with some okay jewelry and keychain mounting stuff, and a few plastic tools. The clay itself is pretty soft and moldable, you do need to knead it a lot to get it soft enough to work with (I’m chaotic and put mine into the blender to soften it but I don’t recommend that) and if you’re planning on making a larger piece, be comfortable with having a semi-soft piece. I baked a 4x4 inch ashtray at 5-15 degrees over the recommended oven temp for about an hour and a half (plus about 5mins of a low broil) and it’s still a bit soft. Just make your clay a little thinner than what your gut reaction is and it’ll bake fine! Overall, great amount and quality of clay for the price. I probably won’t buy again but I am very happy to use the rest of what I have!

This kit is great for beginners or even for more advanced artists! The clay is easy to condition and is nice to work with. When I first bought this, none of the tools and extras came in the box. I was kinda bummed but I emailed the company and they sent me a whole new kit including all the extra goodies. So now I have double the clay which is great for my clay charm business! I recommend!
I love this clay. It's very soft and moldable. Also great for beginners.

I haven't tried every white ink on the market, but this is a great white ink that was recommended by a great miniature painter who probably has tried them all.  The problem with white paint for miniatures, as you know, is that it often clumps and/or looks chalky.  Using white ink helps solve the clumps problem.  It can be mixed with white paint, used on its own, or used to lighten certain colors.  The other secret to smooth white paint finish is adding some gloss varnish.  White is normally very chalky-looking, and naturally resists gloss/shine, so if you add a little (but not too much) you can make your white paints look smooth without being super glossy.  Try it and see.

After many hours of research on different airbrush brands and models, I chose to buy this H&S Infinity CR+ over the Iwata HP-CS. Both are great airbrushes, but after using the Infinity CR+ for awhile I’m 100% sure it was the right decision. There are a few key features that make the Infinity CR+ a better airbrush than its competitors:

The most important (and my favorite) feature that the Infinity CR+ has, which competitors DON’T have, is the paint-flow limiter on the back of the brush. You can quickly toggle it on/off like clicking a pen, and you can adjust the max flow simply by turning the dial. This is SO helpful because it allows you get the EXACT same rate of paint flow each time you pull the trigger back, and not too much paint! As you may know, pulling a dual-action airbrush trigger back a little too far, even for a fraction of a second, can result in overspray and paint runs, ruining your paint job and hard work. But this feature stops that potential problem by setting a (max) limit on the rate that paint (not air) can be released. This makes airbrushing MUCH easier for beginners and intermediates by helping us avoid overspray. This feature is SOOO good it’s easily worth the extra money and its own paragraph.

I also like that this kit comes with 2 needle sizes. I mostly use the 0.4 needle, as the 0.15 needle produces a much narrower paint cone and is really only good for small detail work. It will take you much longer to paint anything of size with the 0.15 or 0.2 needles due to the narrow spray pattern, so those smaller sizes are really only useful for small detail work. If you have to choose one needle, get the 0.4 size! It’s also nice that the brush comes with two sizes of paint pots, though I always use the larger one. It also comes in a plastic case (foam inside) in case you want to travel with your airbrush or just store it away. Most other airbrushes in this price range just come in a cardboard box, not a case.

As you may know, this airbrush is made in Germany (not China like most other/cheap airbrushes) which is a big plus in terms of quality. It sprays super smoothly and has a very high-quality look and feel to it, like an expensive German car. The triple anodized finish is both beautiful and durable, so your airbrush will last a long time. The design/shape of the tip cap both protects the needle point and allows immediate access to clean the tip quickly and easily between sprays. Very smart. Also, you can adjust the firmness/loose-ness of the trigger pull to your liking by turning a dial just behind the trigger. All of these little things and features and details add up to a very superior airbrush. However, you will find many of these things missing on competing airbrushes, and no other airbrush even close to this price range has all of these things. Although the Infinity CR+ is a little bit more expensive, I believe it’s easily the best choice for people wanting to upgrade from a cheap airbrush or even get their first airbrush. IF it is your first airbrush, read up on the basics and assembly/cleaning and care, so you don’t damage airbrush parts. Replacement parts for every quality airbrush (HS, Iwata, or otherwise) are easy to order online but expensive, so learn to be careful with your airbrush from day 1.

IMPORTANT: Did you know that 99% of airbrush problems, such as paint clogs, are caused by user error and NOT the airbrush? Even for cheap airbrushes this is usually true. The honest truth is that if your airbrush is clogging, you simply have NOT mastered paint additives and air pressure. A lot of inexperienced people post negative reviews/comments like “this airbrushes clogs” when it’s almost never the airbrush’s fault. Flow improver, paint thinner or water, and (sometimes) retarder must be mixed in *sufficient proportions* with your paint to prevent clogs, along with enough air pressure. Since temperature, humidity, and even paint thickness can vary, and they all affect paint spraying/drying, there is no exact formula or perfect ratio for paint additives. It simply requires experimentation. To be clear, the H&S Infinity does NOT have any clogging issues, and neither do most quality airbrushes – clogs are simply user errors reported by unskilled users.
During the first use, my airbrush sprayed in an annoying intermittent pulse pattern. In case you happen to run into the same problem, this is almost never a problem with the actual airbrush. Usually, an intermittent pulsating spray is either caused by the front of the airbrush not being tightened enough, or a leaking/faulty air hose or connector. In my case, it was a defective air hose, so first make sure you have no air leaks. Replacing the faulty hose and checking/tightening all of the connectors and the airbrush tip should solve this issue if it happens to you.

Out of the hundreds of things I’ve bought on Amazon this year, this airbrush is probably my favorite and if you’re thinking about getting this – just do it. Treat yourself to something nice.

I gave this set 4 stars instead of 5 for the somewhat misleading display photo, which shows a shiny, smooth chrome-like finish.  The REAL product has a very dull metal finish with scratches and dents - a classic made-in-China look.  Aside from that, all the parts are functional and work without leaking air (at least my set) or any other problems.  All of the parts pictured are included in this set including rubber O-rings on the ends.  One thing to note - one of the quick disconnects has an air flow wheel control (pictured) but that does not really control the air pressure (PSI) so you will need a separate air pressure regulator control (just search Amazon) to do that.

Obviously for the price this is not a masterpiece of carpentry, but it IS an affordable option to store your miniature paints.  If you are on any kind of budget, better to spend your money on paints/brushes/airbrush/other tools rather than expensive paint storage, right?  The wood (?) on this rack is nice and smooth, it's very easy to assemble and held in place by plastic clips (not rubber bands).  Amazon had the good sense to include a few extra plastic clips, too, just in case 2 or 3 break.  I was able to assemble this in just a few minutes without even looking at the directions, and I'm sure you can, too.  I sort of wish it had the little pull-out drawers like my other one, but I can't complain for this price.  Also, it holds 60 total - more than the advertised 52.  There are 52 holes for the Vallejo / Army Painter standard bottle size, and 8 larger holes for the bigger pot-size paints like GW paints.  Not much else to say, but the wood (even though it's lightweight) looks much better than my other (random brand) rack, which has a balsa wood look.  Will probably buy another one when my paint collection grows enough.

My daughter loves these and can probably use for awhile due to the fine tips.  You have to prime the brand new pens by pushing the tips in for several seconds first so the ink can soak in.  After that they work like normal markers.  The colors are glittery and shiny, but some colors are almost the same.  Due to the fine tips, these will work great for drawing or light/small coloring, but will be tedious for coloring large areas.

Another useful high quality color by Vallejo.  Very smooth (no clumps) out of the bottle, but as with all white acrylic paints they get clumpy/funky after sitting for a long time due to the nature of white pigment.  The color is just like the picture - a light off-white with a warm tone, like a light ivory.  Not as bright as the Vallejo Dead White. The paint consistency is normal Vallejo.  I use this paint more than I thought I would when I got it.

Like all Vallejo paints, this is a good quality paint that has the pigment density and consistency that is typical of Vallejo paints.  While it is black, it is not exceptionally dark like black 3.0.  Just a standard black acrylic paint.

Although there are DIY ways to create dirt/mud for miniatures, this is probably the easiest way.  It's a VERY dark brown (darker than most muds) but can be painted over or mixed to lighten or change hue.  You can apply this straight from the pot without any thinning or other tricks.  The texture is something like coarse sand mixed with thick brown paint, and the texture holds as you move it around.  It works great for small minis but may not be economical for big projects.

I bought this to use as a highlight color for painting flesh, and it's pretty good for that purpose.  Make sure to thin it enough, and don't leave the cap open.  4 stars for GW's stupid paint pot design, which encourages spills and your paint to dry out.  GW is too stubborn and/or stupid to switch to dropper bottles, which are superior in every way.  Other than that annoyance, I would give the paint 5 stars, and the shipping was fast, too.

Although some people just use water, plain water doesn't always clean paint perfectly.  It doesn't take much to clog an airbrush, so if you want your airbrush to last, you need a cleaner.  Just mix this with water and spray it through your airbrush before storing it for any length of time.  Simple, effective, and easy to use.

Unlike other great GW washes (like Nuln oil) this one is much more situational.  I only use this on small (28/32mm) figures with flesh, and only with certain fleshtones.  For larger figures, washes often don't look realistic.  Still, this wash works well in those specific situations, but this pot will probably last you years and years, unless you spill it (thanks to the stupid pot design) or leave the lid open.

Retarder medium is a clear medium that makes your paint dry more slowly.  This is the brush-on one, not the airbrush one (although it probably works fine in airbrushes).  If you've never used it, you only need to add a little bit to your paint, not a lot.  It's useful in certain situations, like wet blending 2 colors together in a smooth transition over a large area (since acrylic paint normally dries in seconds) that you might need a minute or two for.  As you know, you can trust Vallejo to make top quality painting products.

I was debating between 4 and 5 stars for this due to the small bottles of watered-down paint.  Vallejo Air paints are just regular Vallejo paints that have been thinned wayyy down with various thinning mediums, but in the same size bottles for the same price.  That's not a very good value.  However, the colors work well together and work perfectly in the airbrush.  The colors (shown in display photo) range from a somewhat pale caucasian flesh color all the way to dark brown/purple colors for shadows.  There is no super-light ivory color for highlighting pale flesh, though.  Thanks to the excellent step-by-step guide included in this set specifically for painting faces with an airbrush, I decided to go with 5 stars.  The guide is really good and written by a damn good professional painter (Angel Giraldez) and takes you through every step.  The guide assumes you have basic airbrush skills, and focuses instead on the actual painting.  What I would like to see from future versions of this set is bigger bottles, and possibly a 2nd bottle of the base flesh color, which will likely run out long before the others.

Everything is as shown in the photo, and works fine.  The clippers arrive sharp but will dull over time (unless you sharpen them), and be careful NOT to get water on them - they are NOT stainless steel and will rust.  Still, at this price you can't beat this set.

If you're new to glazing medium, this provides 2 benefits when you add a bit to your paint.  First, it helps your thinned paint stay together into a more consistent mixture.  Also, the gel-like consistency makes it slightly less watery.  Second, it can help your paint dry more smoothly.  You'll notice this effect particularly when painting with white, because white paint uses bigger pigments that love to clump up together and produce ugly visible bumps.  Glazing medium reduces that effect to provide a somewhat smoother finish.  One side effect, though, is that this medium leaves a glossy finish, so you may need to coat the finished area with a matte varnish if  you don't want the glossy effect.  It is possible to use too much, so use a small/moderate amount.  Anyway, this product is very useful and does exactly what it's supposed to.  This brand is also a recommended medium in several miniature painting videos.  5 stars.

The search for the perfect wet palette continues...this is NOT it. The positive things about this palette are the 2 sponges, which are great - easily the best part and worth keeping. Also, the palette seals well to prevent moisture loss. Also, it's clear so you can see your palette even while it's closed.

As far as problems, there are several. First, the palette is TINY! It's literally the size of my hand (stretched out). That is my fault for not reading the dimensions carefully, but it's still too small for anything other than 28mm/32mm figures with very few colors. Second, the paper is EXTREMELY AWFUL! It's thin and flimsy almost like tissue paper, and will tear when you mix your paint with a brush. I cannot emphasize enough how EASILY this paper tears when wet, and how horrible it is! Also, it lets wayyyy too much water permeate through, creating watery pools of paint after several hours. Obviously this palette was NOT tested before being put into production, as is typical with cheap Chinese junk made in China. My homemade wet palette using paper towels and baking paper inside flat tupperware actually worked much better. I would give this 1 star if it weren't for the great sponges which I will probably use in my homemade wet palette after throwing the rest of this one in the garbage.

This paint is a nice color of green, but a little duller than the display photo.  Useful as a base coat and painting certain plants.  Not a fan of the stupid GW paint pots, which end up drying the paint if you leave them open, and are harder to transfer to your wet palette than dropper bottles.  Wish they would stop being stubborn about their dumb pots and put them in dropper bottles.  Other than that, the paint is OK.

Even though the paint looks very dark in the pot (don't send it back - it's correct) this produces a fairly realistic average caucasian skin tone.  It is a contrast paint, so it works a bit differently than a standard acrylic paint.  To use this properly, first make sure your model has a white base coat.  Any other color will affect the hue/lightness of the finished paint.  Apply a THIN coat to the flesh areas, let dry (now you have a light flesh color) then apply thinly again to the areas you want to be darker.  This is enough to look great for tabletop, but you can further highlight/shade with a cream color and a dark red/brown more if you're going for perfection.  Unless you have at least a couple accurate flesh colors, I would definitely buy this as it's hard to find flesh paint that looks accurate.

This brush arrived in perfect condition with a sharp tip.  The wood handle looks really nice and feels comfortable.  The brush fibers are some sort of synthetic blend which feels coarser than sable.  It paints well, and is VERY firm and snappy.  I love the feel of it.  However, after just 4 sessions the tip is already starting to hook, as all synthetic brushes do.  At $18, this is a major problem!  You can buy a REALLY nice sable brush for this price (that never hooks) so this brush is WAYYYY overpriced!!  As far as synthetics go the brush is nice, but I would not pay more than $3 or $4 for this brush and will never buy Escoda synthetics again unless the price goes WAY down.  Disappointing.

This 000 size brush is not the smallest brush I have (the W&N Cotman 000 is even finer) but it has a razor sharp tip and works great for painting anything tiny.  Unlike synthetic brushes, the tips of fine sable brushes (like the series 7) do not bend or hook over time as long as you take care of them.  Unlike all of my dozens of synthetic brushes, this still has a straight, sharp tip after many uses.  If you don't have a Winsor & Newton series 7, then you have something to look forward to in life!  Just make sure to take care of them and clean/dry them after use.

This review is for the Galeria size 0 brush.  While my Winsor & Newton series 7 (MUCH better brushes) all have super sharp, pointy tips, this brush's tip isn't quite so sharp.  The tip balloons out a bit more than others when paint is added.  This brush is good for base coating small minis and doing the dirty work (washes, metallics) but the mediocre sharpness of the tip makes it hard to paint lines and tiny details.  You'll need to use a different brush for that.  Also, after washing the brush it is more difficult to reshape into a sharp point than other brushes.  4 stars.

This is a contrast acrylic paint - not a regular acrylic paint - so it's a bit thinner than normal acrylic paint, and it gets darker as you apply more coats.  This really should be called Apothecary Light Gray, as the paint is actually light gray, not white.  That is by design though, since you only want your highlights to be pure white when painting white objects - otherwise there is no contrast and the object will look flat.  To use this as intended, you want to coat the area with a white base coat first.  Then, apply a single coat.  The corners and crevices will be slightly darker since contrast paints have an effect somewhat like a wash.  You can apply more to darken targeted areas, and then a gray paint or wash for shadows, to achieve a nice finish with proper shading.  If you are painting a batch of things (tabletop army for example) and don't want to spend a lot of time, this paint is perfect for that too as it provides more contrast than a normal paint.  But, you really do need a white base coat first.  Despite the labeling, I have to give this 5 stars as it really does work well the way its designed to be used.

To be fair, the bottle says "effects" paint, and that's exactly what this is.  This is NOT a standard acrylic paint.  Instead, this paint is very thin and is designed to emulate oxidized metal (like the Statue of Liberty which appears this color even though it's made of copper) and it's perfect for this purpose.  If you want to paint oxidized / aged metal effects, you have just found the perfect paint.  However, if you think it's a pretty sea green color that can be used for a lot of things, you will be disappointed.  The actual color is more dull (less saturated) than the photo and again, the paint is very thin.  Keep that in mind before ordering this paint.

I bought these for very small scale sculpting - specifically for miniatures with gaps and defective areas that need to be touched up with putty.  These are a HUGE improvement over homemade methods such as q-tips, toothpicks, etc.  The silicone tips offer a nice compromise between firmness and flexibility, and the metal sculpting tips on the bottom are sometimes useful.  What I like most about this set is that both ends have a tool, which means less tools you need to buy, and of course the low price.

These look more like wood chips than miniature rocks.  They are mostly a few millimeters in diameter.  If you don't have access to real pebbles/rocks (maybe if you live in an urban area) these will do.  But, if you can get real stones they will be cheaper and more convincing.

This item works and looks mostly like the display photo, but is very flimsy and the only thing holding it together are small rubber bands.  Yes - you read that correctly.  After slotting the wooden pieces together, there are little rubber rings that attach to the outsides to hold your paint rack together.  If you drop this (or even squeeze too hard) it will fall apart.  Given that this product is just a handful of flat, wooden pieces and a packet of rubber bands, I think the price should be reduced.  You can buy a clear plastic nail polish rack with a larger capacity for half the price of this.  However, the addition of little drawers (which don't work very smoothly) is a nice touch, and the little holes in the back for storing paint brushes is nice, too.  The brush holes are very tiny, though, and brushes with medium or thick handles will not fit.  Considering the lack of premium miniature paint storage options, this is still not a bad choice, though.  Just be aware of the aforementioned drawbacks.

For anyone who hasn't used this, it is basically a clear coat that has a very glossy finish when dry.  Very useful for making certain things glossy (a small gem or crystal, for example) since most acrylic paint has a matte finish.  After using it several times, I'm very happy with it.  Not the cheapest, certainly, but Vallejo is a top brand.

Before ordering this, I watched a comparative review of several brands of acrylic primers.  Vallejo earned one of the top overall rankings and most importantly had the strongest chemical bond (this is the point of a primer) between paint and primer.  In other words, paint sticks better to Vallejo primers than most other brands, and is less likely to rub/scrape off.  After using this to prime several models, I can confirm it works much better than my spray-on Krylon primer.  This primer is a bit thick, so it's probably a good idea to thin it *a little bit* with water in order to avoid softening small details.  Although I don't have an airbrush, people say this primer works really well in an airbrush.  You can apply a smooth coat with a brush, too, if you are careful.  I wish they could keep the white version in stock, as I'd like to have that one, too.

Agrax Earthshade is one of the most versatile washes in the world of miniature painting, and I use it on almost every model I paint, even if just a little bit sometimes.  It can be used on almost any shade of brown (and several other colors) to provide added contrast to small details.  I wish it came in a dropper bottle instead of Citadel's stupid pots, but it's still a great product and a must-have for miniature painting.

This paint works very well for painting bones, which is what it's made for.  Apply a thin coat (or even diluted with water/medium) over a white base coat for lighter colored bones, or a thicker coat (or 2 coats) for darker bones.  You can coat just the cracks/details instead of a full second coat to provide added contrast (like a wash) or even use it to paint shadows in some areas.  The more you apply, the darker it gets because it's a contrast paint, not a regular paint.  Takes a bit of getting used to, but it works really well.
I ordered this for my wife, who says she loves it, so it gets 5 stars.

If you have purchased a variety of brushes from different companies, you know the brush numbers mean almost nothing.  There is no standardization.  This brush is listed as a 3/0, for whatever that's worth.  The tip is very sharp, and the brush has a very thin belly.  It is a very small, fine brush - even smaller than 5/0 and 10/0 brushes of other brands.  There aren't many bristles to this brush, so it can be used only for painting tiny little details (such as eyes on 32mm miniatures) and it does that very wel!  In fact, this has become my go-to brush for those super tiny details, and works the best among all of the brushes I own for that purpose.  Note that since this is a synthetic brush, you will need to clean it and shape the tip after use, especially for this brush since the tip is everything.  I would recommend this brush and will probably buy it again when it wears out.  In case you are wondering - yes, there is a noticeable difference in performance between this brush and a "made-in-China special", and is worth the money in my opinion.

This varnish provides a satiny sheen finish (as stated) - not nearly as shiny as the gloss finish nor dull like the matte varnish.  Perfect for finishing objects like a queen's silk robe/dress, or some types of metal objects or even for a polished wood effect.  I use the satin varnish the least of the 3, but it does get used sometimes and I think Vallejo got the "shiny-ness" just right.  Get this if you don't want to be bothered mixing your own satin from gloss and matte and/or demand a uniform mix/finish.

This brush cleaner works great with a little warm water to clean your paint brushes.  If you want your brushes to last more than a week, you need to clean them after every painting session.  Also, a great tip someone gave me: This can (and should) be used to reshape and preserve your brush tips!!  After cleaning the brush, rinse, dab excess water off, then apply some brush cleaner to the tip again, without water, and shape the brush tip straight to a point with your fingers.  The waxy consistency will dry your brush tip the way you shape it, so you have a sharp tips again when ready to paint.  Remember to rinse this soap out first with warm water before painting.

Update: After a lot of usage, these brushes are holding up very well! The tips are fairly good still and seem to hook less/slower than most of my other brushes. They reshape well with brush cleaner/preserver, too. Even though I own some expensive brushes (like Winsor & Newton), I still use these a lot for things like painting metallics and contrast paints, which can quickly damage expensive kolinsky sable brushes. So, this set of mini brushes is a great addition to any collection and I've just ordered another set.

Original Review: First I have to say that the value of this set is incredible for the low price. If you paint miniatures 50mm or smaller, these brushes are perfect. All of mine arrived in new condition with straight, sharp tips. That being said, the size difference of the finest 3 brushes is almost negligible. In fact, my 10/0 and 20/0 appear to be EXACTLY the same (length, width, hair amount, tip sharpness) down to the mm. This is actually great for me since it's my favorite brush of the set and that means I have 2! It's great for painting little details like belt buckles and eyes on tiny 28mm miniatures. The handles of these brushes are very thin, as shown in the display image. As with all brushes, you need to clean them with brush soap at the end of the day/session (and shape the tips to a point) if you want them to last more than 1 week. I will probably buy a couple more of these sets soon before they disappear from Amazon or go up in price.

This paint is quite possibly the worst paint I have in my collection due to the many lumps/chunks throughout, as you can easily see in the photo. It's a shame because the color is a nice, bright white. Even after shaking the pot vigorously for a few minutes, repeatedly, the chunks remain - day after day. No amount of shaking has helped. The chunks range in size from tiny specks to clumps larger than a miniature's eye, rendering this paint unusable in my opinion. After Googling this, apparently many other people are having the exact same problem. GW makes some nice paints, but this one is garbage and if GW wants to preserve it's reputation, they need to RECALL this one and either fix the clumping issue or stop producing it. Yes, I realize that the pigments used to make white paint naturally tend to clump, but other brands of white paint (which I own) don't have NEARLY as bad of clumps, and in many cases none.

If you are about to buy white paint, STOP and do a few minutes of research so you get a good one - NOT this one!

Just got these to replace other, lower quality metallics I was using.  All 8 of the paints have a nice, uniform metallic luster and are thick straight out of the bottle.  Also, these do not fall apart when a bit of water is added, like other (cheaper) paints do.  Another key point is that the hues are accurate for each color when dried.  Don't be alarmed if the hue looks slightly off when wet (for example the Bronze looks a bit greenish when wet) because when they dry, the paints looks great.  I had problems with the hues of other brand metallics (for example the gold looking orange instead of gold), so this is one of the main reasons I bought the Vallejo metallics.  In other words, they look like the color you would expect from the bottle!  If you find your metallic paints unsatisfactory for any of these reasons, I would give these Vallejo metallics a try.

As you can see in the photo (day 1 - just removed from the package and never used) one of the brushes arrived uncapped and it is BENT and FRAYED.  One would expect all of the brushes to arrive with sharp pointed tips like the display image, ESPECIALLY since this is supposed to be a good brand.  I will leave this review at 1 star unless the brush is replaced, and will choose a different brand next time.
My kids draw all the time, and go through a ton of stuff like this as they've become stronger artists, and i found these to be great quality.

[[VIDEOID:56f4ad1de453022859b4a536e5d9e657]] It’s inexpensive, colorful, soft (like taffy!), needs no adhesive (just stick pieces together!), non-toxic, air-drying…the perfect clay for kids/family/hobbyists!

I wasn’t sure if the quality of this clay would stand up to making some mini figurines but I’m impressed! This is a very soft clay so you don’t have to do much to warm it up (conditioning of the clay) but because it’s soft, I would form a basic shape (for the armature), then bake it, then when cool, add more details and bake again. It sands and carves nicely. No noticeable smell when working with it...slight smell when baking but all polymer clays are like that. Nice array of colors...Love it!

These little daubers are really great. They are well made. Good for just getting a little paint or in into a small place in a stencil. Lots of control. The paint washes out very well. I've been using these on gelli prints and I really like the effects you can achieve.

A pad of nondescript, double sided, vintage like paper. Very plain. Good for backgrounds. Some nice ribbon that sort of matches included. I'll be able to use this in my greeting cards, but overall, I wouldn't get too excited about this.

This is the fastest, easiest, best way to make seals. If you have to do a bunch, this is the only way to go.  They are a little bit thinner and more translucent than regular wax pellets. More flexible. I would like to see a little more wax and less glue in the sticks, but it does work better than waiting for wax to melt over a candle for sure.

I had high hopes for this. I wasn't able to get it to work at all. It's kind of hard to apply. Gets underneath your stencil. Followed the instructions but got very minimal burning. I have an Arteza heat gun. Guess it doesn't get hot enough.

I am new to the laser engraving world. So my question is, why don't you get one of these with your engraver? Obviously it's necessary. Secondly why don't they make them with a grid on them so you can position your project? In any case this thing is a beast. Heavy and well made. Bigger than my machine, so I just sat the machine on top.

I used one of these to play around with my new engraver. Worked fine for that. I have used these to paint signs on with no warpage. Great to have in the studio for whatever purpose you want. Cut on the bandsaw very well too.

This was easy to assemble compared to another review I never could get together. No safety glasses included which is just plain stupid. When this docks, it bangs against the frame because something is out of alignment and the instructions are not complete enough to allow me to fix it. I have looked at the website multiple times, and haven't really found the answers I needed. This seems to be a quality piece of machinery, but as always instructions are incomplete, or poorly written. I managed to load the software from the included flash drive. I thought I would just cut out an easy shape (heart) for proof of concept. It wasn't a smooth operation and the edge was very wavy. Then I tried the phone app, which worked remarkably better, but I wasn't able to figure out some of the functions I would have liked to use like cutting a frame around something. I learned more about how to use this from watching a you tube video from someone else who reviewed it. I really don't think it's very safe to send something like this to a beginner with no sound instructions. The videos on the phone app wouldn't load for information. All in all it isn't something I am going to use very much, because I'm not a tech genius and I need better user interface.
Everything also happens in metric and there is no way to change it. Creates a lot of smoke. No work platform is included, so be prepared to find some scrap wood to protect your table.

I like the way these work. Depending on how you store your thread they are really a lifesaver. I have mine in a tackle box, and with the spool savers on the spools they won't fit in the small bins anymore. I think if you were storing these on a spindle storage arrangement, it would be fine. A little hard to put on, but they fit all the different sizes of spools.

Lots of fun for kids or crafters in this bag of goodies. IF you have dies, you can cut these flowers fairly easily. Having them all made up in various combos is nice. Mix and match and use them in any project that wants a little sprucing up.
Finally, some metallic pens that actually work. No fussing around. Just take the lid off and draw very smoothly. Looks great on dark paper or white. Quite the pleasure to use.

Bummer. This has great detail. Maybe too much. Difficult to get the resin in the feet and tail. Also doesn't set level no matter what, so the resin is either oozing towards the head or running out the side. I tried to make it work, but couldn't get it level enough to get a good end result. Haven't tried to unmold yet, but I think that's the least of the problems with this.

What a waste of money and time. This thing has no English instructions. Coupled with the fact that it doesn't melt anything, it's a big failure. It gets hot all over the housing, but the area where the wax is supposed to melt takes forever. This was supposed to be better and faster than a candle, but that's BS.
I really like these. Great for mixed media work. I like to dress up my paint pours and these stencils will be a fun addition to that kind of work.  Very elegant!
I use these sticks for everything. Stirring paint, stirring resin, propping up rocks for sealing. If you do crafting or artwork these are a must have in any studio. Good quality wooden sticks.

Let me first say. that I have used a lot of these wax pellets in the last few years. I make a lot of seals for packaging in my Etsy store. I found this product to be very hard to use. They are more glue than wax and just won't even melt over a tea light candle. When I finally did get it to melt somewhat, the spread of wax when using the stamp was marginal and resulted in a seal I couldn't use.  I like the packaging because it's easier to get the pellets out of the larger jar opening. That's about all the good I can muster for this.

I was hoping to use these for some larger gemstone pi donuts that I want to turn into pendants. They are smaller than I had hoped, but they work well. They actually fit fairly flush to the stone.  Good color selection of metals.
Stunning, rich color. Consistent size.  Very well drilled. These are a pleasure to use.

This mixer works just fine, but you will get bubbles as that is just the nature of resin. Well-made machine that fits in your hand and is way better than a popsicle stick for getting everything incorporated.  The hanging stand is nice. Just be sure to put a silicone mat under it to catch the drips.
I have tried this on several projects and I can't work fast enough to get a good result. There is potential, but it sets up fast and I always have issues trying to get a perfect finish.

I opened these today and discovered that on the surface they seem pretty nice. There are though a lot of small designs that are so detailed that coloring them is hard without a magnifier. I may try some splatter watercolor technique on those as doing them with a watercolor marker is really hard.  Hoping to use these on greeting cards. We'll see if that works with the way I will have to color.
Highly detailed. Good variety. I intend to use these on greeting cards as a removable embellishment. Smooth finish should work for the method I want to use to finish them. Really pleased.
This arrived and over half of it was broken up into dust.  From what I can see the colors were all pretty close in value and not as interesting as I had hoped.

I love to do wet felting of small things and this is very soft wool that will felt well. Nice color combo in the package. Very Autumnal. I'll be doing small, wet felted rocks with this. They are lovely for pocket worry stones.
Nice stamp for winter mail. Good detail. Easy release.
Very good quality. Very detailed, yet they release easily from the wax. Wish they would send more than one handle, but that's just me. Very good design selection.
This is a lovely product, but too thick to cut on my Cameo in it's current configuration. Gorgeous pattern. Just not machine worthy.

These are very small. 3 D printed apparently. Would not have gotten them had I realized. They should come with a handle that fits to make them easier to use.  I was going to use them for texture on gelli printing, but they cannot be exposed to hot water for cleaning. Not my idea of a good product.
Pairs of charms. Good quality. Nice for earrings and other jewelry purposes. Can't wait to get these in use.
Whenever I have a smaller repair job or project I love these pre cut pieces. Easy to grab and go. Consistent size and varied colors.

Nice brushes. Good sizes. The round ones are great at getting into the nooks and crannies. Super set if you just want to get started in chalk painting. Something for every situation. Nice angle brush that works well for trim painting.

This product is quality from the packaging to the containers to the scents themselves. A number one. I love the bottles. Frosted with a textured top so that even someone with compromised grip can get them open. The box they come in is perfect for gifting or storage. I can't wait to make some CBD lotion using these scents!
I like this wired ribbon for use on my handmade greeting cards. It has good texture and nice graphics and themes.
Nice charms for my purposes. They seem a little poodle centric, but otherwise nice and well made.
Just as good as a Fiskars. Sturdy and well made. Tried it on paper and thin cardboard without any trouble. Lots of punch power with this one. Clean cut.

Lots of color selection. Nice sized for stuffing in the top of a gift bag. The way they are folded, makes nice lines for cutting into smaller sheets if that's what you need. Good tissue for whatever needs arise.
These containers are good for more than just candles. I decorated one with decoupage and filled it with my special hand cream. Sealed the lid with a shrink wrap band. Nice gift!
Very bright light. Very hard to get it in focus on the inside of rings and such.  Must be user error.
Nice sized hole and easy to string. Useful for earrings or bracelets or any other jewelry project. Love the stars and hearts shapes.
Very nice color selection. Good melting point and a good impression.
Easy to use and gives a very clear, up close view.

These pellets have a really strong natural smell unlike any beeswax I've had before. I did however try a little in a lotion I was making, and that smell was overridden by my fragrance oils, so I guess it will work.

This is way bigger than I really need, but it is well made and would serve a more serious artist than I am these days.  A great way to preserve your work if you need to move it during classes or between shows, etc.

Where has this gadget been all my life? Fast way to melt wax for tarts or candles. So easy to dispense.  Easy to control the temperature for various kinds of wax. The only thing I would change is the way the interior is shaped on the bottom of the wax reservoir. The center drain needs to be lower or the bottom slanted so that all the residual wax comes out when you open the spout.  Otherwise this is a great thing to use.

I like these jars for the purpose I intend to use them for. They could seal a little tighter if you wanted to use them for spices or something like that. The glass is thick, and they are all cloudy when you unpack them. Run them in the dishwasher and they get clear easily. Plain wooden tops, just scream for decoration of a crafty nature. Lots of possibilities for these. Just the right size.
I love thee precut sheets for wrapping things from my Etsy store. They are great, with no waste and you can match the colors to whatever you are shipping.

This is a pretty good value. A lot of sayings on each sheet. They peel off easily and adhere to paper well. I wish the text was just a few points larger for my uses, but for the most part these are a good deal.
For years I didn't know how to store all the Spellbinders dies. These envelopes solved the problem. They are nicely made, easy to open and close and easy to see through. Perfection!

I got some Halloween ornaments last year to paint and they were way too thin and fragile. This product is very much thicker, but still lightweight. Nice graphics and shapes. Gonna look great on my Halloween tree!

Good selection of papers for various uses. Some are paper, some are vellum like and very translucent. Some already have an adhesive on the back that you can peel off. Better printing and graphic quality than a lot of the ones I have gotten.

In the last 6 months I have reviewed countless lots of washi tape. Very few are worth talking about. This tape has nice graphics, good colors and good quality. It seems a little thicker than the average tape I've been getting and it sticks well. Much better product than average.

I am an artist using colored pencils for 30 years. I have tried all kinds of "blender" pencils. None of them work and this product isn't really any different. I used high quality paper and did some test swatches.  It  didn't so much blend the colors as it just scratched them off the paper.  In my book, nothing works for blending quality colored pencils like Gamsol and a blending stump. Get some and you'll never try this kind of product ever again.

Very different from the older style glitter paper. This is smooth to the touch and folds without cracking.  Still sparkly in the right light. I used this today for several greeting cards. It is really hard to write on, so don't even think about trying that. Use a good adhesive or some good glue because the coating makes it hard to stick things too. Good finished product.

I got these ribbons intending to use them on greeting card projects for texture. I love the colors and the graphics. A little bit of shimmer in the thread on some of them gives them even more  interest.
When they say skinny, they mean 1/8 to 1/4 inch width. Small rolls. The patterns are so small and detailed it is hard to make them out. This looks way better in the picture.

Once again, a lack of decent use instructions causes me to give a poor review. Fortunately, I have book on papermaking that will fill in the many gaps in the video. It does appear though that you will also need 2 sturdy boards or a press to complete this construction.  The items in that are in the kit itself are better quality than most, but written, concise instructions would have resulted in a 5 star review. Not a beginner kit as it is.

Most of these beads are fake. I have no idea what they are made of. There are some that I think might be resin. The tiger eye and aventurine beads are real. The rest are very questionable. They will make a pretty bracelet, but will have zero healing properties as they are not natural stones.

I make greeting cards and am always looking for different kinds of embellishments to use. These are fun. Great selection. Colors could be a little more vibrant for my use. All in all, I will get a lot of use from these and be able to make some awesome cards.

From a distance these sparkle. Up close they look as cheap as they are. The black variety is dull and scuffed. The bail at the top is very thick which makes getting a smaller jump ring on them kind of difficult. All in all, you get what you pay for.

There is a nice variety of colors in these. They are however, inconsistent in the "eyeball" part of the bead. Some are small, some are large, different on each side. It is sort of hard to get 2 to match enough for earrings. The charms are very well made. The included head pins are very short, so if you want to use an accent bead above the charm, use a different pin.
I really needed an influx of head and eye pins. These are way shorter than I am used to. One or two smaller beads and a spacer are all that will go on them.
Love this mosaic glass for small projects. Consistent shapes, pretty colors. The edges aren't sharp. Beats having to get out glass and a cutter when you want to do a small candle holder or something.

WOW. These have a great variation of sizes. Some are almost too small to be useful for anything. I use these on my greeting cards. They are easy to apply with heat and no glue is needed. The end product is sturdy and attractive with no visible glue showing.

I sometimes like to do a little mosaic project without having to get out all the glass cutting tools. These little boxes of precut glass are perfect. Color is pretty true to the picture. Only issue was the container they came in was flimsy and not sealed well, so there were pieces of glass all over the shipping envelope.

Nice sized sheet for a laser cutter if I ever get one that works. In the meantime, these cut well on a band saw and are good for painting, or wood burning.  Also handy for doing vinyl work on for smaller signs.
The labeling or description is a little confusing with this. This is fragrance oil, not for consumption.  Smells great. Gonna try some incense with it first to see how well that works.
First off these are life sized pieces. Very well cut.  Easy to paint on or decoupage on. Many uses. Too big for the project I had in mind, but they are a good product.
I like these pencils. They color smoothly and blend well. Basic color palette, but a good starting place for a beginner. Comes with sharpeners.
These are a good size for using to create a painted puzzle collage or other piecemeal artwork.  Consistent gesso finish and stretched OK.
Nice pad of palette paper. Makes painting easier to clean up. Can be cut easily into a smaller size to fit your specific needs.

Let's be clear from the get go. This is not printer paper or copy paper. Very thin weight. Lovely for origami, but not the printer. Nice selection of colors for larger folding projects. Good for paper flower art.
It's nice to finally get some good washi tape that is clearly printed and vibrant. Nice selection of colors and patterns and I just the project in mind for this.

I'm truly sorry that I persist in trying these products. Once again, I have a box of washi tape that is not useful to me. The graphics on these are very poor quality. Fussy printing, blah colors. If only they could print the tape as nicely as the box. The larger stamp type things that are included are nice enough, but the subject matter is lacking.

I found these paints to be nothing spectacular. I certainly won't be using them. They are nothing special at all. Thin and matte. Not any better than watered down acrylic paint. Not durable. Not pretty. Not sparkly. Nothing to recommend them.

The white pens are pretty useless. They skip and are not very opaque. Watery.  Not great at all. The silver and gold are really good and save the day. Not of the pens are really full of ink, so don't expect that.

Excellent for making any kind of paper project. I was working on greeting cards and I tried these out. They peel off and stick to the project very well. The little spools they are on are easier to work with than a sheet of labels. Easier to dispense one at a time off of a roll.

I like the dual ended deal on these pens. The black is vibrant and really black. They write well. What I don't like is that the sizes aren't marked very well on them. On a Micron marker the width is on the end cap so that if you sit them up in a holder or whatever, you can readily see the size. These, however, have a very small, printed label on the body of the pen which is useless.

I think these kits would be fun to use with kids. The pieces of glass have been fused, so there are no sharp edges to get cut on. The colors are pleasing. Having laid one out, I think I would probably see if I could change the pattern a little so that the grout lines aren't so wide. The wide grout detracts. Extra pieces are included.

I found this to be thicker than other hemp cording I've used. If you are planning to weave this with beads, they need to have a pretty large hole for this to thread through. It is very stiff at the outset but softens with time and use.
These are perfect for decoupaging to glass. No need to cut from a big sheet. Nice color selection.

Very pastel colors of wax in this set. Boring and uninteresting except for the dark pink. The container was also crushed in shipping, so that all the pellets were mixed up loose in the bag. I thought I was going to like the adhesive dots better than the 3 M ones I've been using, but they aren't great either. They are thicker than 3 M. They peel off the paper easier, but don't stick to the seal very well. When you go to pull the backing paper off you have to be really careful that the adhesive stays stuck.

This seemed like a good idea until I tried to use it.  Of course, it comes with no guidance or instruction.  I started off with 4 wax pellets This filled the cavity nicely. However, when you stamp the wax goes up the sides and you are left with a wax "tart" instead of a seal.  Next, I tried 3 pellets. Pretty much the same result.  2 didn't really work wither except that the "seal" was thinner and almost transparent.  No matter what I tried, I couldn't get any better result. At least certainly not something that looks like a traditional wax seal.  The other issue is I have no idea how you would do more than one at a time since the wax sets so quickly. Having all those cavities to use is a waste.
Fabulous Diamond Ribbon. Gorgeous, sparkly colors. Very sticky. In fact, so sticky that it's hard to get the wrapper off. Works terrifically for my project, which is greeting cards.  Love the bling!

I am impressed with this product. It works as well as the name brand Tombow that I have used for many years. Very sticky. A little harder to dispense and I don't like that the protective cap is not attached to the roller. As soon as you take it off it gets lost in your creative mess.

If you have been used to using wax pellets for sealing that you melt in a spoon, then these are a little harder to use. It takes a good bit of wax, which burns slow, to use with the standard sized brass seals that are used today. However, the wax in these is pretty, has a bit of sparkle, and is somewhat translucent.
Crystal clear for tracing. Good quality. Easy to see through. Use a hard pencil though or it will smudge all over the place.  Nice to have smaller sheets to work with.  Good product.

I have to say I didn't find much "high quality" in this product. Some of the paint is thin and watery. Some thick. Some separated. Extremely difficult to open. The bottles are thin plastic and practically are collapsed upon themselves just from shipping. This paint also seems to have a gloss finish to it when it dries. I've noticed that in a lot of the cheaper paints I get. It kind of remains tacky and depending on your usage it is impossible to paint over. Not very good coverage. I tried this for painting some rocks I was giving away. Ended up starting over.
I did some sketching with these today and they are pretty close to the Micron pens I favor. The sizes seem true to form. Nice lines. No smears.
So many of these "gold" pens are not really gold. These write very well, but even after they dry, they aren't really gold per se. The gold has this olive green aspect to it.
Great bit of extra bling on greeting cards and paper projects.
A nice variety of geometric designs great for gelli printing projects. Easy to clean. Fun to use. Quality stencil material. Some of these are really detailed.

This paper sort of reminds me of a hybrid between cardstock and construction paper. It is smooth, but not as smooth as other cardstock. Primary and secondary colors and black. No white in the selection. It will be fine for most projects in my studio.

Finally had a chance today to try this out. What I know:

The first sheet in the pad is stuck to the cover of the pad and is unusable.
Tearing out a sheet must be done carefully and there will be some lose canvas threads to clean up at the edge.
I tried this with both gouache and acrylic and it worked for either, however the canvas has a lot of tooth and your paint has to be just the right consistency for good coverage.
I was able to cut this on my old style guillotine paper cutter and still get a straight, clean edge.

I really needed a drying rack for gelli printing and marbling. When I opened this to put it together, I realized that it is rather large and fairly well built and using for a drying rack would be a waste. So, I am using it for paper pad storage. 12 x 12 pads of paper fit nicely on each shelf making it easier to get to your paper stash. However, it does take up a ton of room in the A frame configuration.  I also had an issue with a screw that stripped out and I couldn't remove it to change the spacing on the base. This is unusual for a hex type screw, but I was using an electric screwdriver to assemble this. So be mindful of not doing that I guess. Even my screw removal tool wouldn't get it out.  That issue made me deduct a couple of stars because this could be a really nice item if it fit together just a bit better in the joints. That was why I was using a mechanical driver for tightness. Definitely use the stabilizer bars in A Frame mode. Otherwise it is somewhat unstable.

In the past I have reviewed many types of "no name" gouache paint, never giving more than a few stars for any of it.  This product was surprising. There is a nice range of colors already to go, no mixing and faffing around needed. Colors are surprisingly opaque and vibrant as well. Creamy consistency. Not sticky. No bad smells. I decided not to peel off the top of each one completely in hopes that it will keep the drying out to a minimum. The main thing to remember about this paint is that if it does, get dry, the addition of a little water will revive the consistency. I am very pleased and look forward to painting some smaller projects and not having to use my very expensive Holbein paint.

I've been playing around with this set today and have to admit that it is disappointing. The lettering guide isn't very large and instructive. Most of the pens seem OK, but I used the brush pen the most and it is already frayed out and goofed up at the point.  Not the greatest.

This is the third time I have gotten something from this manufacturer, and it will be the last. This is one small book of poorly printed pictures. Done on vellum, they are fuzzy and very dark even when applied on a lighter surface.
I got these for use on Etsy packaging and greeting cards. They are easy to use. They can be stamped or drawn on to decorate. A really good size for many craft projects.

I spent some time trying these out yesterday on paper projects. I was not thrilled.

Cons:
Difficult to get the ink to remix after it has been sitting. There are no mixing balls in the containers and the mica settles out and congeals.

Hard to get them open. There are little caps with pins that I used to puncture the tops. Since I didn't know what their purpose was that was what I was using. The inferior instructions on the box say to cut off the tops of the bottles. That would be the way to go.

Because you can't get the ink to mix up very well, it clogs the opening and doesn't dispense.

Not very vibrant.

No blending solution.

I suppose if this is all you can get to use for resin maybe it would be OK. Don't bother if you want to use it on Yupo or something else.

I pulled several of these from the set at random to look at. I am not impressed. Some sets are strange (Song To You), some are just OK. I doubt I will use these in any of my projects. They are just little snippets printed on paper that are repetitive and not that enticing.

I had a new coffee mug that had a label on the bottom. It had already been through the dishwasher multiple times and I couldn't get the sticky stuff off. Used this product this morning and Bingo!. All gone and clean. Easy to spray and then wipe off. Strong solvent odor, but it works.

It's nice to get a large selection of beads. It would be even better if the container they came in was sealed and everything stayed where it started out. Every bead has shifted to one side and popped the lid open so they were all over the shipping container. The square beads have a very large hole. The smaller, round, flat beads have a smaller hole. I got these sorted today and there are plenty of black and white. The other colors, not so ,much. I don't think there are complete alphabets in anything except the black and white. Way too many of the misc. star, moon, heart beads. Kind of a bust.

Lots of things to like about this glue gun.
PROS:
It feeds well by gravity. The sticks loaded in the cartridge drop without too much messing around.
It has a good feel in the hand if you are doing something that requires a long stint of gluing.
It actually sits level and doesn't fall over.
The glue that is included is very flexible once hardened which can be good for some applications

CONS:
The little drip try is a nice feature, but it's a little close to the hot tip of the glue gun and poses a burn risk when you are trying to fold it out of the way.
The directions for loading the cartridge didn't work for me. I filled it first and then installed it into the gun.

All in all a nice upgrade from a really cheap glue gun.

I have a long-standing rule for reviews. No package instructions equals an instant one star. This may be a good product but I have no idea how to use it unless I go online and search out the information. I live in a very rural area, where internet access is not always a given. Written instructions are important to me because I don't always have the option of getting online and finding a video on how to use or assemble something.
I sued this today for gluing some magnets to a wooden cutout. It is clear, but not smelly like E 6000. However it does take awhile to dry and be ready to use.
I have used these for 15 years and won't use anything else. I've tried cheaper but these are the gold standard for linework in my opinion.
I make wet felted projects and this material is the best for bonding to felt.

This arrived today and it appears to be well made. The one thing that isn't well shown in the picture, is that there is a white border along the top edge. I thought it was just the laciness of the product, but it is actually not a solid color lace.

The first thing I noticed about this is the quality. The second is that it is very quiet when it runs. Deducting a star because no matter what I did to the cord it twists the gun in such a way that it won't stand up using the attached stand. This is a problem since it is hot and dangerous to lay down.

Interesting textures and sheens on this paper. Kind of thin. Will work quite well for making craft projects and paper folding projects because it's thinner.  Great way to enliven your paper art collection.
These are nice, thick, smooth slabs. Not all are consistently round, but I wouldn't expect them to be.  Good for rustic type craft projects.
Love the sparkle and light in these tapes. They all coordinate pretty well color wise. Nice selection of patterns and colors. Love the gold!
These are about 50/50 lampwork and rhinestone. There are also some metal sculpted beads thrown in. Not the greatest quality for sure.  I'll figure out a use for them.
Lovely shine and even holes. Good quality.  Very sparkly for your next project!

I do a lot of greeting cards and note cards. These little letters are perfect for accents. Just paint them or stain them to go with whatever your theme is. Lots and I love the 2 different fonts. Very useful!
These are really lovely brads, but it would be nice if there were at least twice as many for the money.

I really like the realistic floral renderings on this tape. Very attractive and well done.  I love the morning glories and the ivy. Can't wait to use these in some greeting cards. Really pretty for art journaling as well.

The colors in this set are exceptional. Very much more a modern color palette than most I've gotten recently.  Beautiful range! Not enamored of the actual stamp. I really wish these companies would change up the stamp offers. One can only use so many roses and trees of life.

One of the best products I have gotten for review in a long time. This is everything a kit should be. It is well packaged for gifting. The instructions are exceedingly well written and informative in all aspects of wax sealing. I love the brass handle much better than the wooden handles because the whole seal stays cool and releases easier. I couldn't try the wax rods that go in a glue gun because my low temp gun only takes small diameter sticks. Complete with a silicone sheet for making seals to use in other ways. I make mine and use them when I wrap my products in my Etsy store. There are adhesive dots that are included so that you can attach the seals after you make them.  Just about as perfect as it gets.

First off I don't have a hot foil whatever and never heard of it. As for embossing, I have been unable to make that work completely without cutting the paper. It's a shame because this is a beautiful, highly detailed piece.
I dropped my old Big Kick ad it landed right on the handle. Broke the most important part after 20 years of service. This however seems to be made just as well and I am thrilled
These are lovely stencils in snowflake patterns. Very varied and nice. Well made.  Fun for any kind of winter crafts.
I don't have a cricut machine but I am using these on a bandsaw. They are good for cutting out small, lightweight things for greeting cards.

You can never have too many fan brushes depending on how you paint. This is a good selection of sizes . I think the handles are just a bit too long for my taste, but all in all they are pretty good for the price.

These come bound together in divisions of the alphabet. 4 packs all told. The printing is OK and made to look like they have been done on an old typewriter. I was skeptical that the spelling would be correct, but on a cursory flip through I didn't find any problems.  Plenty of words represented. I'm hoping to use these on greeting cards.
These are very cheaply made. Some of the scissors don't close all the way to cut. The punches are so small that the detail is lost.  Total waste of money.

I think these would be better suited to scrapbook pages since they are very large. I was hoping to use them on greeting cards, but they are about half the size of an A2 card. They cut well. Clean edges. The little paper bits release from the die fairly well.  Nice dies.
Good detail on the moons. Nice stars and planets. I'll be using these on greeting cards I make. I appreciate that they are all separated into packs instead of all mixed up together.
This mold has lots of potential. I only wish there was a picture included of the finished layout. Easy to unmold. Fun for a window hanging.

I have used stazon ink forever for stamping on things where you really need it to be permanent. High odor, but good results and the ink pads can be refreshed with bottled ink. They will last a long time if you keep them covered.

A little disappointed in this. I wish they would have spent more effort on the actual product than the fancy strap that is included and has no use as far as I can tell. Firstly, this is pink and not red. The collapsible container is nice and holds a fair amount of water. The paint pallet itself is not very large and has very small cavities in it. I am using this for gouache paint and it is hard to snap open and closed. I think it will work OK for black and white and a few colors that I use without mixing. The gasket that fits in seals well to hold the paint. It would be so much more useful at double the size and skip the useless strap that is included. Although there are brushes pictured be aware they are not included either.

Good colors, nice translucent paper. Small circles that do stick well to paper. They look nice on greeting cards stuck as an accent on complimentary colored paper. Nice color selection, but there isn't much on roll and they are overpackaged in labels and plastic.

I needed to replace a very old, heavy metal magnifier light. This one is lightweight, has good magnification and pretty good light. Fastens easily and stays in the position you put it in. It loses one star for me because the cord is too short to reach anything. I have outlets every 4 feet in my studio and I can't reach without an extension cord.
I tried these today and they seem to be nice enough pens. Good selection of widths. Dark ink. Not a beginner, but the ad says thy are for beginners, so it would be nice to include some instruction.

This is a sturdy holder that is actually easy to assemble. The directions are clear. I would add one mention to them and that is to notice that the holes are beveled on one side or the other. Use the beveled side against your screw so that it sits flush in the hole.  Good for washi tape, artists tape and floral tape. I have it all on mine to help organize my space.
These slices are good for painting. Smooth enough for the most part that you don't need to sand additionally. They however did not work for me for pyrography. Too soft and ridged.
These seem to be working as well as Microns. Good fine lines and are great for my needs.
I really needed some of this for my Cameo. I just hate that they don't last long and are expensive.
These are so much easier and sturdier than the wire variety. These have a little hook that pulls the thread through and they are much easier to use.

Great, well conceived canvases. I am going to use these for encaustic wax work. They are wonderfully constructed and they include hangers for the backs and little bumpers for the corners. Best art supply I've gotten in a while!

Fantastic variety of scents and they last quite a while. Not overpowering. Easy to clean out if you use the wax liners that are available now for wax melts. Nice gifting box or break up the box and use them as stocking stuffers.

This is a really nice set of wax melts in lots of good scents. They are so much better than the crappy ones from Better Homes and Gardens that they have at Walmart. Very nice box that they come in and they would be an awesome gift for anyone who doesn't like to burn candles but wants things to smell good!

I really needed some smaller type organizers to get a grip on all the enamel charms and whatnots in bags all over my studio. I like these because they hold a lot. They have 2 levels that can be configured anyway you want. Then the top tray has larger spaces to hold larger amounts of flatter things. Filled up one and will work on the other tomorrow. They are sturdy and stay closed very well.

These embossing folders worked very well in my Sizzix Big Kick machine. Very heavy embossing on thick paper. I think you would have to be very careful if using thin stock as these would impress to the point of tearing. Good selection of themes and a great way to do borders.
I know that metallic paint used to exist that had coverage over other colors. This isn't it. Almost transparent on white. Very translucent on black. Multiple coats did not solve the issue.

I was very happy to get some paint made in the USA. I paint rocks and canvases. Been looking for many years for an acrylic craft paint that covers in one coat. I guess it doesn't exist. This paint is thick and creamy and I had high hopes. When painted over gesso it is very translucent and the coverage was average at best. Nice colors. Will probably work well for paint pouring.
I got these based on the reviews that they were really black.  I don't find them so. They are darker then other 9B's but not really completely black.

First let me say that the detail on this is really good and I really wanted to use them. I picked a stamp at random from the pack. These are wood block stamps not rubber stamps so you have to use them a different way. I got out some block printing ink and a brayer and applied the ink. Then tried printing. Not good. Reapplied the ink and tried a different method with the paper on top and brayered over it. Some better, but not complete.  I want to try these on a gelli block during monoprinting. I really think that will work.

I can't use these as they are not really very flat and my project doesn't have the depth to make these work. They are larger flowers and  only a few are truly pressed flat. Maybe hydrangeas?? Not the best.
Anyone who knows how to use a mask for rubber stamping is going to be able to create some marvelous items with these stamps. Great variety and good quality. Stamp clearly and are easy to clean up.

Always looking for black  "paper" to try my Finetec Mica watercolor on. This is very smooth, but had a bit more tooth to it that Yupo.  The paint worked fine but the coverage wasn't great. I then tried some alcohol inks on it and that was a complete bust. Unless you are mixing white or some other opaque into your ink it doesn't show up at all on black paper. I think this would best be suited to using metallic paint markers on. Would I buy this? Probably not.

These have a very saturated color to them and are quite pretty. Children would love doing crafts with them. They are almost too nice to use for paint stirrers. I think I may have to research a crafty use instead.
Fantastic set of tiny brushes for detail work. Every size and shape possible for whatever tiny space you need to paint. The case is terrific for keeping it all together.  Excellent quality.
I love these sheets. I have an Etsy store and these are the perfect size for wrapping my product. Lots of colors. Easy to grab and you don't need to cut them from a bigger sheet.

These pieces are small and very portable. I think they will be handy for greeting card design. I like that some of the images are the same as ones I have printed out in the past but a little smaller and everything is all together in one place. I have so much paper and images that it's hard to keep track. This is a good selection in a manageable space.

Let me first say that these are about the 6th iteration of painting rocks I have reviewed.  They are by far the best for the money. You get what the picture shows. All smooth, all flat, mostly consistently shaped. I will be able to use all of these rocks for painting. Unlike most of the others that I threw on the landscape pile.
I love this. It is soft and easy to work with. The beads that are included are really pretty and are great accents. Enough for a larger project!

This is a really nice set of face paint. 2 metallic shades in gold and silver. A larger section of white and black and then a very good selection of vibrant accessory colors. Super for Halloween face painting!
As soon as you cut this the gold thread unravels and comes off. It looks great until you use it.

When I think of trays I think of something that will hold a plate or be useful in that way. These are small. Maybe hold a coffee cup but why would you? They are well made and would be better as shadow boxes with a glass cover. I will use mine for a painting shadow box to hang on the wall.
Where has this been all of my life? No more straws for me. This little gadget has some real puff to it. much easier to use with inks and paint pours.
I use these for drawing on dark painted rocks. They sketch well and the outline is easily removed.  Also good for sketching on black watercolor paper before painting.
These are well made and the variety of colors is nice. They are very soft and silky and have a good flow and movement to the. Hope to use them on some bookmarks for gifting.
What a waste of my time. These are tiny.. The largest is about 1.5 inches. No holes for hanging. Cute enough, but not as advertised. Will try to use them on greeting cards.
Plan to use these to make Valentines ornaments for my seasonal tree in the house.  Smooth and easy to paint. All consistent size. not too thin.

I used to make a lot of greeting cards and these dots are invaluable. Name brand is expensive. These are very sticky. Hold paper together quite well. Easy to use and there are lots of them. Great value. I am very pleased.
Using this for holding paper flowers to stems and it seems to be fine.

To call this a coloring book is not fair to the art. This is very much a quality book of illustrations to be colored. Quality paper. The cover lays flat so you can work in it. Everything about this screams high end. Very large and the finished work could be framed or saved in some way. I got several varieties of these books and did notice a couple of repeats from book to book, but that in no way reduces the experience. This one is easier to travel with but the paper and cover have in no way been compromised by the size.

To call this a coloring book is not fair to the art. This is very much a quality book of illustrations to be colored. Quality paper. The cover lays flat so you can work in it. Everything about this screams high end. Very large and the finished work could be framed or saved in some way. I got several varieties of these books and did notice a couple of repeats from book to book, but that in no way reduces the experience.

To call this a coloring book is not fair to the art. This is very much a quality book of illustrations to be colored. Quality paper. The cover lays flat so you can work in it. Everything about this screams high end. Very large and the finished work could be framed or saved in some way. I got several varieties of these books and did notice a couple of repeats from book to book, but that in no way reduces the experience.
Rich and vibrant colors for Fall. All mixed together in the tubs but easy to sort if you want to do a one color seal. Standard pellets that make a nice complete seal if you use 3.
Good value for painting and lots of fun for a kids outing or even adults. These can be painted as fancy as you want and are going to be terrific decor on my Halloween tree.

This is disappointing and I only took it because I thought the light was bigger and better than the one I have. The curing light for this is folded up in a small box and it flimsy. Also only has a USB plug which is an automatic deduction for me.

These stencils are quite large sheets that hold many different designs on each one. That makes them harder to use because everything is connected together. I will probably have to cut them apart for my purpose. Heavy stencil material and not too flexible.

Just once I would like to actually get rocks that look like the pictures. These are fairly smooth. Not that flat and kind of irregularly shaped. I can buy a 30 pound bag for the same money from Home Depot and get about the same quality.

Great detail.
