What I am noticing lately on some of these stamps is the deeper, depressions with sharper edges, It makes for a good wax impression, but I find that getting them to release from the wax is a lot harder. It seems to grab in the nooks and crannies more and I have even torn seals trying to get them out.
All in all these are good for painting. A few oddly shaped stones in my batch, but they are smooth and fairly flat.
I use a lot of this for masking off paintings as it is a lot easier to remove than regular painter's tape. It also works well for wrapping stems on flower arranging.
Have used this for years. I prefer it over Mod Podge for all applications.
Nice selection of sizes and shapes on these brushes. They are very useful for kids to use and also adults doing craft projects.
These are the typical thickness of wooden cut outs. Nice for improving with great painting techniques. Fun project for the kiddos!
Well graduated colors in this pack of crepe. Quite classy. Will work wonderfully for wrapping items I sell and ship. Lovely!
There is something about the texture on these that is very smooth and easy to paint on. I love them.

I love round canvases for paint pouring. These are well made. Stretched pretty good. The stapling on the back is a bit crude but then every canvas I've gotten that isn't way expensive has that problem.  Nice to have the varying sizes to play with.
These are very flat and perfect for painting on. Smooth for the most part. Too expensive to be a regular thing anyone would buy.

These pencils are very hard. They don't blend, but if you just want to scratch around on some paper to doodle, they have some interesting effects. Seem to be very long lasting and might be especially good for kids.

Just finished a painting on one of these. It is fairly smooth and gives a good finish. I think they could be stretched a little tighter.  Moderately well finished on the back and the corners unlike some I have reviewed.  Good practice canvases.

Cheap paint. Smells funny. Not very metallic. If you paint it over a white background you can barely tell it from any other paint. On black it's a little better but not the best. Once it's dry it's very subtle. Messy to open so don't let your kids do that or they will wear all the paint.

This paint is OK at best. It smells bad. It's pretty thin which makes it hard to dispense from the pouches. No way to squirt out just a little. Standard color palette They mix OK but aren't terribly opaque. Takes lots of layers to get good coverage on a canvas.
These are very much a mid range stretched canvas kind of the quality of the Dick Blick bargain brand. Good for practice work.

The paint in these is watery no matter how much you shake them. Not very opaque color. As for the nibs. Well that's an epic fail. It isn't clear in the description, but you have to change the nibs if you want chisel or finer point. First off, that's a messy business. Secondly the nibs are too long and when you press down on them to dispense the paint they either moosh flat, or completely bend over. Very disappointing.
Lovely little, well detailed butterflies. I don't think I would use them on anything that got washed though. I intend to use them in art projects and greeting cards. Nice selection of colors.
Perfect for making your own stencils from files you have on your cutting machine.

I only deducted a star because I really don't need all the text and alphabet stencils that this has. Otherwise they are good. Some may need to be weeded a little in the details but they are easy to use and good to go.

I was skeptical about getting no name dies. These work quite well though. They are very finely detailed, so be sure you have some sort of small tool available to help remove the tiny bits after you cut.
I am loving these stencils. Good variety. Easy to use. This one had a binder clip and all the stencils had the hole lined up. Easy to gather them together right out of the package,

These are so much nicer than I was expecting. They are on the large side.  Detailed with a good selection of recognizable flowers. Also some various kinds of leaves and fauna. Lots of possibilities here for putting together your own combinations.

Kind of small diameter. I got these to paint on and I will have to keep the design very simple. They are lightweight though and well made. Consistent size and  detail work. Should be just right for ornaments on a tree.
A very good set of looms. Good construction.  I like the yarn selection which might be almost enough to make a small hat for practice. Instructions and needles included. A really good beginner kit.

I really didn't expect much when this paint arrived. I've reviewed a lot of no name pouring paint and it all lacs something. This impressed me. First off they don't have a nasty bottle seal that you have to get off. Just flip the top and you can pour the paint.  The other thing that impressed me was the range of colors and the vibrant nature of said colors. Great shades of reds and warm colors. Good stretch of greens. I did a canvas yesterday afternoon and it is dry this morning and everything is still vibrant and rich.  The only thing I noticed is that they seem a little bit thick in my opinion. Easily remedied by adding a bit of extra Floetrol or water. Good flow. The colors do intermix so be careful not to overwork it and end up with mud.
Very heavy paper. It is hard to tell the textured side from the smooth as they both appear to be almost the same to me. Sturdy and thick this holds up to wet media very well.
Jute cording. Very pretty. Because of the nature of jute this is inconsistent in thickness. Good for all kinds of crafty uses or gift wrapping packages.
I like to felt but it's hard on my hands holding the little needles. These handles are easy to use and make everything better.

Light as air. Spongy when dried. No mess on your hands when you use it. No smell. Hard to open the pouches and then get the clay into the little ziploc bags for storage. Not a lot of each color because it compresses so much.  Really more fun as a tactile item then for making anything out of.

What a great set of art tools and papers. Everything a young artist might want to get going on painting and drawing.  A couple of issues. There is a pad that had ready printed designs to paint or color. The paper is not up to watercolor and some of the designs are poorly printed, off center or have the edges cut off.  The other pad of paper included is for mixed media dry work so it won't work with the water color paints either. Nice set of oil pastels and a huge array of crayons. Well  constructed box to hold it all together.  This would be a fantastic gift for a teen or young person starting out.

I get these kinds of books and keep them around to help me with my artist's block when it happens. These have pretty cheap paper and the designs are not that well rendered.  I would consider these more for young people.  They are OK, but not great.
Super selection of colors. Love the little star shaped brads too. Very nice.

For a creative person these have loads of possibilities.  Treat them like cabachons and put them in jewelry. Paint them with dainty little flowers and make magnets or brooches. Just let yourself run wild and add them to all your projects. Smooth and nicely cut. Lots of smalls that would make great earrings.
I tried shaking these but they still write kind of weak and watery. Not nearly as good as the old standby from Sakura.

Was excited for this. Now  very disappointed. First off you need to provide your own hook if you are hanging this on a door. Fortunately I had something that would work. It is really hard to use as far as getting the rolls in and out. The more rolls you get in the harder it is to put the next one in. I finally gave up. Well made.

Beeswax sticks are good for lots of things. I use a lot of it when I am doing Pysanky eggs. Also good for beadwork for waxing your thread. This is a nice box full if wax and will last a long time for whatever use.
Good quality charms for any craft use. Good detail. Nice quality.
Lots of sparkle on these. They are very light so for earrings they would be great. Consistent holes and good quality.

This stuff is amazing. It came today as I was making some greeting cards. I always like some bling on my cards. A little strip of this changed so so to terrific. It is easy to cut, very flexible and boyo does it stick! Big win for me!

I've had lots of Hippie Craft items to review so I was excited to try some vinyl. As soon as I pulled a sheet out I was first aware of the thin nature of it. It cuts just fine and I was hopeful. It however was unable to be weeded. I tried a small thin font and that was a disaster. Then  larger, blockier font and that didn't work either. I finally gave up and balled up the mess for the trash can. Good color palette but no browns, creams or taupe. A little gray. Very disappointing.
I'll be using these in handmade greeting cards. Maybe for embellishments in resin or on artwork. Very nice and well made.

This is a nice product. I automatically deduct one star for USB cables that don't have AC adapters. I hate to open something and then not be able to plug it in until I scavenge around for the right parts. Big no no.  This has a lightly textured surface area which could be a problem for drawing, but worked OK for weeding vinyl.  Good size for my needs.

If you DIY or craft you have to have these brushes. They are essential for so many things.  Use them and toss them to make clean up easy. The brush on these seems a bit smaller than others I've had, but I can't compare with the old ones because I don't have any left.

The shape on these beads isn't really a briolette. They are more of a fat  oval. Nice colors. I've used Czech glass beads for 25 years so I'm a bead snob. We'll see how these work. The container they came in had several broken dividers so everything got mixed up. Might as well save the overpackaging and just put them in a bag for the consumer to sort anyway.
Don't expect the finish to not wear off after a bit of use, but these are all a consistent size and the colors are good. Fun shapes as well with good holes.
These are OK and work OK. It's nice to have the option of 2 adjustments, but if you don't need the extra room that tab is just kind of hanging out and in the way.
So useful for macrame or crocheting projects. These are very well made. Consistent size and smooth. A joy to work with.
This fine line white pen is one of the best I have used. Good opaque paint/ink. Easy to use. Smooth to write with. So far so good!
Soft and easy to work with.  This particular color is a little drab, but will be OK with a brighter embellishments. Nice spool of cording!
Perfect cord for macrame. Easy to work with. Good saturated color. Good value too.
This works fine but produces a really small pot holder. Actually only good for a trivet type thing.
Made a set of these yesterday using this mold.  Deep rune symbol detail is easy to fill in with a paint marker. The mold is thin and easy to unmold.  Finished product is very nice.

I am assuming that because this is paint for outdoors that accounts for the odor. However I couldn't stand the smell and never got very far at testing this out. My plan was to paint a rock. Cure the paint and then sit it out in the weather to see how well it held up. Unfortunately I didn't get very far. I started out to base coat the rock with white.  Normally it takes one or 2 coats for good coverage.  This is thick paint, but not very opaque and I never did achieve coverage.  Didn't really work for me in what I was trying to do.
I used some of these today in a leather project. They added just the interest I was needing and were easy to put in.

This is the first faux leather I've gotten than didn't stink to high heaven. It is very supple, yet thick and really looks like honest to god leather.  Lots of fun ideas in my head since I've seen this and I can't wait to  make some glasses cases and small embellished pouches with this.

These are exceeding my expectations for a paint pen. Nice tip that is fine enough for doing some detail work. I like the clear tube that shows you the paint level in each pen. They are smooth and write well and the paint isn't watery or runny. No odor. My only dislike is the difficulty of getting the outer wrapper off. Pain in the butt.

Fun little hearts with a nice smooth surface. Lots of crafting potential here.  You are only limited by your imagination. Good quality and well shaped. I painted on one and that was fun. You could do mandalas on these or drill a hole and string them with lights or tie them on jars as a label. Many possibilities!

I really like these rocks for painting on. They are smooth and have no pits or dis on them. Nicely shaped.  That said, I wouldn't even entertain buying these. The price is over 1.00 per rock.  No way.

This is nothing special. A good kit for a beginner that just wants to test the waters. Standard 2 part resin seems to set well. The molds are the usual jewelry/ earring molds that seem to be pervasive at the moment. The little critter mold is nice and gives you a mermaids tail and a few small pieces like that. Really nothing remarkable.

I think the marketing on this is kind of confusing. It is basically acrylic metallic paint. Very small amounts. A good consistency for dot work or brush stroke work. Frankly not that excited about the colors. They look a lot better in the picture. The purple is kind of blah mauve. The turquoise is nice but the next darker color of that green really isn't a teal it's just odd. The gold is good. If you are looking for metallic paint there are lots of better values with more diverse color palettes for the money.
I got these to use for hanging Pysanky eggs on a tree. I can string through them and then glue the fitting to the egg. Like the variety of metal colors.

This was folded over and jammed in an envelope resulting in the lid part of the mold being totally out of shape.  I've been trying to warm it up in hopes that it will return to the original shape, but so far no luck.
I really like the detail in these. Just not quite sure what I can do with them. Not deep enough for a decent soap bar.  I like things to have a functional purpose as well as looking good.

I started this in the middle of a regular painting I am doing. This is so much easier because the background is open and things move a long a lot easier. Love the texture that the bigger gems give to the whole thing.

This mold is a failure on so many levels. Who makes a resin mold pink? You can't tell what your colors are going to look like at all. Ridiculous. The mold is also too thin for the structure. I poured this. Cured it for several days and it still deformed when it was put together. Say my phone in it overnight and the whole stand was collapsed in the morning.

Another great diamond painting. This one has gems other than the standard drills which make for a more textured and interesting finished product. The background in this is just painted, no drills need to be applied. This product is a complete kit from stem to stern including a frame for your finished product. Labels included for your drills. They have thought of everything. I love the labels for marking especially. I wish all the companies would do that. Saves a lot of time when you are getting your materials set up.
If you are just starting out in art r painting this set is all you need to get up and running. Nice quality brushes in a fantastic case. Easy to travel with. Good for Plein Air painting.  Wonderful!

I can't wait to get into this painting. It's a really nice size. Not so large it can't ever get finished. Good adhesive. What is totally cool about this is that not only do you have the regular round drills, but some faceted larger diamond like gems and some teardrop shaped pieces for the top of the sun part. This is neat because the whole painting won't be flat it will have depth and dimension not normally seen.  Super idea! The canvas is vibrant as well and will be a lot easier on the eyes to do.

I was quite prepared for these to be like all the other colored pencils I have reviewed. I am pleasantly surprised. The are very good quality. They blend well pencil on pencil and with Gamsole. A huge variety of colors that really are different and not just  so similar you can't tell them apart.  Good storage box. I don't care of the blunt tips and had to sharpen them before I could use them, but I understand why they do that for shipping purposes. They sharpen easily and hold the point with no breakage.

Just what I needed to make some wax tarts so I can stop buying the expensive ones. I think the size of the tart in the rounded containers is a bit small, but the squares are the right size.  Sturdy plastic.
Great detail. Good quality. Lots of variety. The perfect combo!

Very impressed with these dried flowers. Lots of sizes and shapes. Perfectly flat and easy to use in resin work. The colors are obviously dyed and could be just a little bit toned down in some in my opinion.

I just came in from doing some pouring to try this out. I did a small vase to see how it would go and a small canvas. This paint is very thin. Almost on the borderline of too thin. On the vase in the areas where the white was it ran off to a point that you could almost see the glass through the paint. No cell activity out of the bottle. To do these small projects I exhausted 3 bottles of paint. Nice range of colors in the box but no metallics. All in all it's just OK paint.

This dried with a very glossy finish on glass and canvas.
These pins are longer and nicer than most. I like them because the rounded ball makes them easier to pick up if you have stiff fingers.

I found this resin to be very thick and difficult to use. I was trying to fill a few bezel pendant pieces and wanted to embed a charm in them. This was so thick that by the time I got the resin into the corners of the bezel I had almost filled it up which wasn't going to work. I wanted a thin layer on the bottom to cure first and then a  middle layer with the embellishments and a top layer I could dome. It also didn't seem to want to set up under my light. It took more passes than resin I have used previously.

Once again a failing grade for not including some sort of use guidelines. Nothing in or on the package gives you any idea how to use these as far as making them permanent. Should you heat treat? Who knows. I tried a sample piece on a swatch of cotton canvas tote material. Drew on it and let it dry for 1/2 hour. Should it have been longer? Who knows. Took it too the sink under cold water and it didn't come off completely, but faded quite a bit.

These are not fine point markers either, but bullet point.

On the face of it I was really liking this kit. Pandora style beads and the supposed ability to be able to change things around easily, made me excited to play with it. Through trial and error I figured out that the screw end that closes the bracelet had to come off first in order to secure the beads. Mine was stuck. Finally got it off and then couldn't get it to screw on the end of the chain. More trial and error and it's on, but it is cross threaded and now I can't get it off without damage to the findings. So all in all this is another imported disappointment.

The beads are moderate quality and there is a nice selection of charms and large hole beads to string. Some of the charms are gold toned with silver bails attached which looks awful in my opinion.

I loved this paper from the start and the more media I tried on the more I loved it. It is very thick and very heavy weight paper. It has a wonderfully smooth side and a side with just a hint of texture. I tried oil pastels, gouache and watercolor on it. The pastels blended better than any other paper I have tried with them. The watercolor and the gouache worked well too with very minimal buckling of the paper. Depending on your preferred painting media, this could be a one stop paper for everything.

I have been pleased with most things I have gotten from this manufacturer. The gouache paint is very nice. Good thickness, smooth and easy to use. I wish the tubes were a bit bigger, but there is a great variety of colors in the box and they are all sealed until you puncture the opening, so this paint should not dry out.

I am glad to find this painting pen. It is much easier to hold without strain on your hand. Very nice to be able to change the ends for holding more drills. Now I can get rid of some of the pen clutter on my table. I find it a lot easier to lay down and pick up then the cheap clear ones that come with the kits. Easier to see it too.
This rattail is really neon bright. The picture makes it look a lot more subdued than it is. Really in your face kind of colors that aren't what I had in mind. Probably be great for kids projects.

While sewing masks earlier I realized that my thread supply was dwindling and that a lot of it was old. This set is a perfect stop gap of colors until I can replenish at the fabric store. Not too big of a spool and  I love the little nettings that they send to wrap around so they don't unravel.

A wonderful kit if you are just wanting to get started doing this. I have used a lot of this brand of premixed paint with excellent results. The color palette in this is kind of limited but a beginner will still be able to get a good feel for technique. This includes 2 canvas boards which really don't work with paint pouring since they warp in the drying process. I would have liked to see some smaller regular canvases in this instead.
I really wanted more of the green trays since they seem to work the best for me. This was over priced for what you get.
I grabbed this so that I could try out a mini wheel I got for review. It was really too firm to use on that. I think it would be fine on a normal sized wheel that you can apply proper pressure to.

Take a black canvas or black paper and this paint and you will have a gorgeous, sparkly creation. Excellent quality paint that is easy to blend and very smooth. Love the variety of colors in this group. Something for every thing without mixing.

I have always stayed away from using glitter paper in projects because of the way it was made. This must be a new process because this paper is smooth and thinner and gorgeous. Much easier to work with that others I have used in the past. Haven't tried it on the cutting machine yet, but I think it will be terrific because it is smooth.

Pick a word. This is one one of the stupidest ideas I have ever seen.  I used to be a potter. Thought this might be fun to use since I can't work on large pieces anymore.  As usual there are no instructions or guidance whatsoever as to how to use this. If you had never done pottery before you would just have to figure it out on your own.  I started out with a small walnut sized piece of clay. Turned on the wheel and flung the clay across the room. Tried again. Realized the clay was a little too stiff. Slowed it down and flung another piece across the room because the wheel itself moves all over the table. Think using 2 fingers to work and one hand to hold the damn thing in place. What a waste of time and resources.

These molds are so finely detailed that I'm not even sure how we get the resin in them. I'm going to try some tomorrow with UV resin and a very small tip applicator.  That is the only way I can see it working. Very lovely, but they are going to be tricky to cast.
Attractive and fun because they swivel. Perfect for resin work that can be viewed from both sides. They are really kind of thin metal though compared to others I have.
Love the various color selections that make it easy to coordinate with your other findings.

This is hard to use. Palette paper I have had in the past was in a much smaller sheet and thicker.  I will have to take these and cut them in quarters to use in a smaller space. Very thin paper too so plan on putting something under it for stability.

I had just bought a set of Pentel oil pastels. Then I saw these and thought I would compare. I love these. They are soft, smooth and blend well with good coverage. In comparison to the Pentel they are notches above. Great color selection and they are a little thicker in diameter and easy to hold.  Now I'm enthused again about doing some art!

I've been having a lot of fun with these wooden slabs and squares I've been getting. Good for wood burning.  Paint pouring caused them to warp, but brush painting is good. Lots of ways to use them with stencils or hand painting.  These are a nice size for a coaster.
Thick, smooth and vibrant paint. Well mixed out of the bottle.  Much better than craft store paint.

I have been lucky to review a lot of brands of watercolor paper.  Unless you are a professional artist selling in a gallery they really all are pretty much the same for watercolor and gouache. Thick and heavy paper.  Everyone that paints for a living has a certain name brand they like. This is absolutely fine for students and mixed media work.

Great tins. The smaller one is packed inside the larger and they all arrived intact with no bending. The lids slide on and off easily. Smooth surface paint.  Great for altered tins or mixed media book tins. Also good for storing small items in. Easy to decorate or paint on.  A big win for me.

I had a small set of these from a long time ago. When I went to use them they were stiff and not very smooth. I just assumed they had dried out over time. So I ordered another set only to find out these are just as dry and very difficult to blend. They are inexpensive and it shows.

I can't wait to use this in some geodes. It seems to be the  right texture for a change. Other products like this have been way too chunky for the mold. This has a finer consistency and more uniform color. Be careful when opening the package as it is full and there is drawstring bag in the box. I don't get the need for that because it just makes a mess getting to the glass chips.

I love the precision tweezers in this lot, but they are very fragile in the tips so treat accordingly. I dropped a pair and totally bent the points askew.  Very fancy if that kind of thing matters to you. Personally I'd rather have durable.

First thing I noticed was the color it is not a pure, snow white, but a bluish white. The backer is more white than the vinyl.  To use this you have to remember to move all your feed spools to the left on a silhouette machine so that it grabs and feeds straight. Otherwise it goes off course pretty easy.  I like the fact that you can run a wide word without waste, but I am always skeptical of off brand vinyl. We'll see how well the test holds up.

It's been awhile since I bought any wicking. These don't look woven. The look like they are extruded until you burn one and then you can see the fibers. However they are made, they burn cleanly for sure. Love the little stickers to hold them in the bottom of the mold.
I like these molds because you can expand to double nines  The tray mold is really nice too so that you can play without having to stand your dominoes up on end. Perfect set.

Love these. I've been painting on some others I have to make ornaments and they always turn out beautifully. Smooth enough to paint on without further sanding.  One thing to consider with these is that they will spin freely due to the eye hook as opposed to ones that have the hole drilled through the wood itself. On the other hand not having the hole drilled through the wood means you don't have to work your design around it.

Nice rolls of smooth white paper. Thick enough for an easel or other uses. Imagine turning the kids lose to color or draw on it then using it for wrapping paper. Or using one of many printing techniques to do the same. Great for drawing on too.

It's taken me a long time to try this out because I was expecting a big mess. I did tie dye in the 70's and this is totally not that kind of deal. So much easier to mix and use and store the leftovers.
Thy call these Indian Agate,  but they look just like all the Fancy Jasper beads I have. Very well polished and drilled and a nice size.
I like these for choker style necklaces. Great for people that have allergies to other materials and metals. The claps is interesting and I have not used that style before.

Stay at home. Be healthy and diamond paint!! A terrific kit with everything you need to make a gorgeous picture. Great way to pass the time and  have something lovely to show for it. I've been wanting to do this one for some time now and am glad to have gotten it.  Good adhesive and plenty of drills.
These are perfect for screwing into small wooden carvings to turn them into ornaments. They are a little hard to hold onto with aging hands, but they work once you get them started.

Fun with diamond painting. I love this design. A bit different art than most of them. Everything is included and I have yet to do one of these that didn't have enough drills with it, so I have no fear this one will be any different.  Backer sheet is good and sticky.

I tried one of these for a painting this afternoon. I was using gouache. Not a bad result, but the canvases are thin and not stretched very well. I particularly didn't like the label that is stapled to the back of the last canvas in each pack.  Makes getting them apart a bit of a trial.
These are going to be such fun to do.  I like the smaller sized painting because of time constraints. These are perfect.

I've been getting a lot of these recently. I love them. They are well made for the money and this lot has some interesting and neat themes in it. Love the cacti!! Use these in resin shakers and in other resin work. They also go together into earrings very quickly with a few accent beads and findings.

These containers remind me of the kind you get with kits like paint by numbers etc. They snap shut tightly and store paint very well.  Very hard to open.  The large lot of flat brushes is a nice bonus.

I am in love with these. They are beautifully done. Well detailed and colorful. I have several small holes in jeans I want to fix with these and I plan to also just put some on for a little bling. Even my elderly Mother thinks she will use these.  In my experience with these it is best to whip stitch around the edges to keep them in place through many washings.

I have worked in polymer clay since it became available. Because of issues with my hands I can't anymore so I wanted to sell all the big blocks and tools that I had. Sometimes though you just need a little for a project and this set is terrific for that. Great color selection. Small amounts. Soft enough that I can use it with bad hands.  I deducted a star because one of the big things I liked was the storage container that keeps it all together and mine arrived totally crushed.
Large container about 1/4 full. Not a great value. Good color. If you need a lot for a project buy several.
These are way smaller than other bezels I have gotten.  Not a good size for pendants unless they are for children. Good for earrings or multiples on a necklace. Nice color selection

These are better than I thought they might be and have a lot of uses. Funny eyes for felting critters. Earrings with the included posts which are nice.  Good deal all around for someone with imagination.
The written directions and the video are totally different. Different parts, different machines. Nothing matches. 4 hours in and this is still not assembled.
These are well made and perfect for gifting small items. Great color. Good drawstring. Excellent product.
Good value of mixing solution and the bottle to dispense it from. Very fine tip allows complete control.  What a deal!

I found these to be well made. The lids screw on well. The thing about them is that they are attached, 5 containers high. Not so much stackable as linked together. The containers are also very small in volume.  I was hoping to use them to store mica powder for easier dispensing. Not going to hold much more than a teaspoon.

Well made and cut acrylic circles that can be painted or resined.  I use these for leftover paint from paint pours and then I resin over the top to make the key chain. This size is a bit smaller than ones I got previously. Good quality key chain findings are included.

Easy to open bottles with no inner seal to pry off. Rich color. Mixes well. Nice paint. I tried it on some rocks I was doing today that hadn't been undercoated. It did take multiple coats to get coverage and it dried darker than I thought. Might no be the best for that application.

This is the only set you need. I've been using these for several days now and I really like them. No shedding of bristles. I love having the bigger, wider brushes. The white bristles are nice too because you can more easily see if you have the brush clean. A win all around.

These are still difficult to install but they are easier than others I got because thee are soft and not rigid. I finally used a 3 mm metal crochet hook. Pass it through the smaller end of the  "buckle" and out the larger. Grab the cord on the mask and kind of roll the "buckle" back over the end and pull. Sometimes it takes a few tries. About a minute mask for me.
Just used one of these for a paint pour. Seems pretty good. Tight. No sagging. The corners are stapled kind of poorly. Not folded in well.
Fantastic giftable item for a beginner artist or a pro.  Good quality canvases. A good value. All of us old and new can always use brushes and palette knives and a little extra paint.

I found these canvases to be thinner and not stretched as tightly as some others. I think they would work for regular painting, but I'm not so sure they will so OK with paint pouring. They seem saggy in the middle. They also seem very lightweight so whatever the frame is made of it isn't as good as most others. The canvas feels like it isn't as thick either maybe because it isn't stretch as tight. Corners are staples well.

I have several sets of these in various sizes. They are a lot of fun for gelli printing, rocks, embellishing paint pours and a million other things. I love the ring that holds them all together and most times you can just flip to what you want without having to even take it apart.
I've tried and tried, but this doesn't work for me. The bit comes lose too no matter how tight it starts out.

This resin was low odor and easy to pour. Seems to be a bit thinner than others I've tried and that's a good thing for me. Cured overnight. Good clarity.  The only downside I found is that the bottles aren't full. At least an inch plus of headspace at the top.

I found this to be quite nice as far as paint goes. Smooth and it has a good thickness. I mixed some to do a small paint pouring canvas and the colors were vibrant and worked well with other brands of paint. I think this would be a great student paint. Standard palette of colors although I found them to be a bit on the bright side of normal.

I'm wondering if this is what they used to seal the box. I had to get a knife and cut the whole thing apart to get the product out. These sticks are unlike any I have used. They are kind of yellowish and very flexible. They seem to melt consistently and there is a good flow from the glue gun. All of my tests were really stuck well. I think this will be great for wood projects. It does have a long working time and takes a bit to set up. Glue remains flexible after set.
I've been fortunate to try several resins lately. This is becoming one of my favorites. Easy to mix. Works for casting and has a good length of working time.  Very low odor. Crystal clear.
I like the colors and the behavior of this paint, but I don't like the price at all.  Especially for pouring.  Too expensive for the size of the bottles.

The slabs are very well sanded. As was mentioned in the description, the bark is lose and some of it falls off.  Depending on what I decide to do with these I will have to fix that.  The grain on these slabs is so pretty I almost hate to paint over it.

I don't use much carbon paper but what I've had were very large sheets that needed to be cut down. These are the perfect size. Good color selection for whatever you are tracing. The styluses are a great way to trace!
These are very nice for using with UV resin.  Watch a video though so you know how to do it to prevent leakage out the back side. Wish there were a few  circles included in the mix.

The best part of this set is the packs of add ins. Having said that it would have been great if they hadn't deposited glitter all over everything in the box. Open with caution just in case. The only mold that is a 360 degree mold is the bunny. Unfortunately mine is full of whatever escaped the glitter packs and I can't get it washed out of the nooks and crannies. The bears are flat on the back and the design does not go the whole way around. I think they are supposed to be wide enough at the bottom to stand up, but I'm not going to waste resin to find out.  Bookmarks?? Seriously?? Who in their right mind uses an 1/8 inch deep bookmark?
These charms are well made and will surely bring a smile to anyone that sees them. Nice selection of themes. I really love the little mummy faces!
I find these very thin and lightweight. The bail that is attached is a little flimsy too. However these are nice for doing small resin pendants.

Very smooth, creamy paint. Easy to mix for paint pouring. Standard selection of colors that can be intermixed to make lots of other colors. Larger sized bottle is a nice value. Used some today for a paint pour and had no problem with it working with other brands of paint.

This is a nice selection of containers for small items. They fit in various arrangements inside the bigger box if that's the way you have to go. I'm using mine for little charms I use. The only problem I had was getting them open. The little latches are pretty tight and you kind of have to push in instead of pulling outward.

I think these pendants are exceptionally lovely for the butterfly lover. I also think they are maybe a little too detailed to use with resin unless you want to embed the whole thing.  They will also work for greeting card embellishments as well. I make larger paperweight style shakers and I can't wait to do a butterfly themed one of those.

Love these kits. They are so much fun and with a quarantine winter coming they will be lifesavers. Standard accessories including the styluses to lay the drills and the tack to pick them up. I have never done one of these that didn't have enough drills included so I feel safe to say that this will be fine.

This is my second cup spinner in several weeks. I am pleased to say that this one works with mugs and vases. The center support is really necessary if you are doing that kind of vessel.  Well made. Very quiet. A bit of a challenge to put together because the nuts and screws are smallish and hard to handle.  Includes a silicone mat and a silicone spatula for spreading your resin or paint. I deducted one star only because the included foam piece for the turner wouldn't fit on the spindle. The center hole was way too small.  Fortunately I can use one I already have.

These eggs are very well made and need no extra sanding. Smooth. They are a true egg shape with no flat end. I like to use these to make wooden Kokeshi dolls, so I will have to sand a bit of a flat spot to get them to stand up.

I'm not sure I really see the "shift" part of these. I used them in some resin pours today and they look pretty much like any other mica powder. The pink seemed to have a gold shift to it, but the dark green didn't really produce any.  They still look pretty and are easy to dispense from the little bottles.

This is quality paint. I used some today to finish a project that was dry brushed. It worked fantastically. It is thick, vibrant and creamy. Easy to go on. Then I thinned some for a paint pour and it worked very well for that.  Super paint!
I love these for my resin shakers. The perfect size with lots of good themes to use.

The title states this is a beginner set of molds. I beg to differ. The larger molds are going to be OK, but the island molds are a challenge for even the most seasoned artist. A few printed instructions on how to use them would be appreciated. I have watched the video, but these things are so small I don't see the average person getting a really good result.

I was sure I read the description properly. The board  part of this mold is only about 3 x 5 inches. Not 8 inches like I thought. Very fine detail and it is going to be way too tedious for me to pour and finish. It's good quality, but really needs to be a little bit bigger.
I have a big stash of these charms to use in all kinds of resin work. They are the bomb in shakers!
There is an amazing amount of very fine detail in these molds.  They are very thick and good quality. I can't wait to do a chocolate pour in them!
I am addicted to doing resin shakers for people to cheer them up. This is a really nice set of alphabet charms that are well made. Different styles and really cool fonts.

Just unmolded a few beads this morning. The smaller round mold is a bit of a struggle to get out. Then of course there is the issue of having to try to sand  the pour end since they are never quite finished off roundly. That was impossible for me to do. They are too slippery and too small to sand.  When strung together you can't see the pour end if you pay attention when stringing.  I really wish there was a way to get a perfect sphere without having to dome or sand after the fact.

Nice basic molds.  A pack of dried flowers if you are doing embedding work and some glitter. They unmold easily and are pretty no frills. This would be a good starter pack if one is just getting into resin work.

Seriously. The box that these bottles fits in is equivalent to a Haliburton case. Overkill. The little jars don't hold much, so I used this to organize some glitter I use. Easier dispensing from jars than bags. It is really nice, but take the bottles out and use the case for Oakleys or something. LOL
Got to use these today. Nice tools with fine edges and points. Grabbed the vinyl quite well when weeding.
These are smooth, matte finished and screaming for you to do something gorgeous with them. Cork backing. I can't wait to do paint pours on these. What great gifts!
These silicone mats are the best no matter who makes them. If you do resin or painting or anything that makes a mess these are easy to clean up and a must have.
These are very well made charms and are going to be gorgeous in a wide range of applications. I usually cut the ring off the top and put them in resin shakers. Very pretty enamel.

Very disappointing that this doesn't have pre drilled hole spaces on the mold. They send lots of nice handles, but I guess you have to drill your own holes in your finished product. This is an extra step and produces a change to ruin your finished work or make an error.
I love this stuff. It is easy to use and holds great. No mess. Place it where you want. Wonderful innovation.
Good value for paint pouring. Mixes well and pours consistently.

I've been wanting to try one of these for a long time now. This was not a good experience. First off the spindle is lose right out of the box. As soon as any weight goes on it the sagging and tilting begin. It is quiet until it has weight on it. Then it chugs and grinds and stutters along. Does not have a smooth spin at all. There are 2 sizes of foam included. The only problem with that is if you put the big one on whatever vessel goes on there is going to drag on the bottom because there isn't enough clearance between the spindle and the base. It needs a support and needs to be a lot higher.  I tried a mug and the handle drug along the base. I ended up putting a kind of thin glass vase on for my first project. Of course none of the foam fit in the opening and with the weight it was off level immediately. Would not let this go unsupervised during use.

I've been putting off pouring this for some reason. I think I know why. In recent days I have poured some molds that are thicker than these and they have been fragile. This is a really nice idea and the hardware that comes with it is  really nice but I don't think I'm going to waste the resin to find out. If anything I might just pour the smaller rounds because I think that has the best chance of success. I really don't think this will work as poured especially with anything served on it.
Kraft type paper is usually thick like a grocery bag. This is smooth finished and very thin.
I usually do smallish castings and I am finding this too thick to work with and pour and it has very little working time once it is mixed.

These molds are so poorly done that they are nightmare to use. Of course there are no instructions. There is a little nib on the inside of the mold that you use to fit the open end of the pen cartridge into. They the writing end terminates in the end of the mold. I guess. All I know is that you have to have resin so thin it's like water to fill this and get it into the ends of the pen. I don't have thin resin. I only buy brand names and I couldn't get it into the ends. No way to check for bubbles that will also occur in the ends. When they are poured they don't begin to sit level. I thought it was my table, but I got the level and checked and my table is fine. The molds are junk.
Unmolded these this morning and they require a lot of sanding to be usable.
Love these. Very well made. I have also found that you can overlap one on the other design for some and get a totally new mash up. Best part is the binder clip to hold them all together.
Great kit for teens or anyone that wants to get started in wax sealing. Good selection of colors and the ginko leaf seal is perfect!
I tried one of these yesterday and I found them a little too big for me. I use smaller canvases and these are kind of big for my tastes.

Beautiful glass work in this pen. The pros end there.

Cons:
The nib is rough causing scratching and drag on the paper which feathers out the ink.

I tried watercolor paper, marker paper, sketch pad paper and bristol. Never got a clean line that either didn't bleed through or feather out.

The ink is thin, watery and doesn't load on the nib at all. It has some mica mixed in which no matter how much you shake won't stay mixed in and has very little effect on the linework.

Wear gloves when opening the ink because the stoppers are so tight that by the time you get them out you are stained everywhere.

I really cannot recommend this. I was so excited to get one of these. What a disappointment.

I have been using different kinds of resin for months now. Art n Glow, Amazing, Diamond, etc.  I have measuring pour cups to get the ration right.  I did a small pour to test this and everything I unmolded was flexible. Not sticky just not rigid.  Kind of a shame because it didn't have a lot of bubbles and seemed to have a good working time.  I tried a casting today and measured by weight so we will see if that matters. It worked the same, so I expect another failure when unmolding.

A nice value in this I suppose. It really isn't something special though. It's just like someone took sheets of gold foil and kind of tore it up and stuffed it in a jar. It is very hard to deal with and believe me you don't want any air movement when you are using it.

I don't get to knit often, so my work gets stored for long periods of time in between. Then there are cats and they can have a heyday too. This bag is the perfect size. It's not too deep, so things don't get buried in the bottom. Nice zipper compartment on the front for needles and tools.  Good colors and is well made.  I also think being able to completely cover the yarn when you are knitting and run your thread out through the top of the bag will deter the kitty cat interference.

As a paint pouring and resin artist with a conscience, it's nice to not have to use wooden sticks to stir and work with.  These are great. Easy to hold on to and stir with. Easy to scrape the sides of cups with. Resin comes off with a wipe of a paper towel or when it's hardened.  Just an awesome and thoughtful set of tools.
i wanted this to mix large amount of paint for pouring. It is very thick and takes a lot of Floetrol.

I'm beginning to think that this is all made in one factory and just packaged differently. The colors are good but nothing that is different from any others I have. What I do like is that the packs are all labeled and have a number designation. This is helpful for keeping track when you are mixing custom pieces.
Tried this out today and got great cells and good movement.  Works great!

I recently received a Hot Shot brand rhinestone tool. I was skeptical that this would work. These little stones stuck like iron and it was a fun thing to do. The tweezers that were included are very good as well.  Fun to do and lots of possibilities.

I used this today and had my first successful pour ever! I deducted a star because the bottles aren't as full as they could be. This set is expensive for off brand paint. I'm also not really in love with the color palette.

Making resin shakers and needed something that would fit in the filler port to fill them.  They  are a little hard to use as far as syringes go depending on what you are trying to suction up. Had a lot of trouble with glycerin and almost couldn't get the bigger needle in my shakers.

I've been waiting to try this for awhile. Making resin shakers and using this to seal the tops and the injection ports. It seems a little iffy to me as far as clarity and hardness. Maybe it's the UV light I got.  Not sure I would use this on something bigger as I'm not entirely sure it would set.

What an amazing product. I have a handmade kaleidoscope that I needed to fix and didn't want to use a messy liquid glue since it was close to the lens. A few little balls of this and VOILA'. Fixed. Easy to use and easier to control. I think this is going to be my new best friend.
Great bee detail in these molds.  Outstanding that there are 2 sets.  A lot of potential. I make beeswax lotion bars and this is the mold I have been searching for.

This kit is fun for everyone. It took me about 10 minutes to make a bracelet. The first thing I did was  look for a better crochet hook because they ones included are totally flimsy and hard to use. I don't know how a child would even hold on to them. The bands in this kit are thinner and have rounded edges as opposed to another kit I just reviewed. Since the bands vary I didn't find them to be interchangeable between kits.  Great craft for an elder as well. Kind of mindless fun.

These are made with that kind of medium weight, woven felt that they make grow bags out of. It is kind of rough to the touch. I am hoping to decorate these with some rhinestones to make them  kind of fun. They are well made.

Perfect for making resin shaker pieces. A really good selection of designs to please anyone. I love the box to store them in too. Others I have gotten were in bags and they are falling out everywhere.

Looking forward to using this. I have to do a big magnetic wall calendar every month and I keep thinking this will make it more fun. I'll do several rounds of alphabet and put magnets on the back. The letters are a nice size to be seen and to be filled without too much fussing.
I do a lot of chalk marbling and this is a nice color selection for that. Only one broken stick in the box which is amazing since they weren't packaged too well.
I have found that mica in jars is a much better solution than bags. These are easy to dispense from and the colors are excellent!
A nice selection of colors.  Good for making cards or for any craft that you want to tie a tag to or to use as a closure. Looks like butchers twine but is a bit thicker and more paperlike.
Superior in every way. These canvases are a paint pouring artist's dream. They are sturdy and very well put together. They have heft and body and are absolutely wonderful.
Superior in every way. These canvases are a paint pouring artist's dream. They are sturdy and very well put together. They have heft and body and are absolutely wonderful.

I got this so I could practice some paint pouring with cheap paint before I used the good stuff.  Well I got my wish. This is cheap paint for sure. I don't think it is going to be worth the effort to mix for experimentation, but I may try a little. Nice tubes. Limited color selection. Very, very basic.

I have been reviewing and assembling things for a decade at least. Probably longer. This was the most frustrating thing I ever tried to put together. Janky diagrams are not the best instructions.
Once you get past that it is constructed of oak and is well built with the exception of the hinge that allows it fold up for storage. That is the weak link in the whole easel. It barely wants to stay together and I wouldn't trust using this for plein air painting at all. Not unless you can brace it with sand bags around the legs.
It also seems hard to get the sill that you sit your painting on to stay put. You really have to ratchet down that knob so that it doesn't slip.
As good as Strathmore or other brand names. Heavy and thick. Good with all wet media.

Not your ordinary air dry clay. This stays soft and squishy even after it is dried. Kind of fun to squeeze. This kit comes with tools and eyes and little bits to decorate with. What I don't like is that the clay is not in a container, just a bag that you have to open with now way to reseal. They do send some resealable bags to put the clay in.
Lots of fun colors. Not a lot of each one so best to get several kits if you have a ton of kids.

I wasn't expecting much, but I was pleasantly surprised. These really are very nice. Consistent holes and good quality.  I didn't count them, but it doesn't seem like there are 300 of them.  More like half that amount.

Finally got to try this out. It is fantastic. Super easy to unmold and it can hold one or two sticks of incense.  I did my first try in a swirl of 3 different mica powders. I would recommend doing a solid color so that the intricate carvings of the mold stand out and don't get lost in the pattern of the resin.

I have used several different brands of resin for various casting projects. Although this is really not marketed for a casting purpose, I had a good result. I cast some coasters and the end result was lovely. Hard and crystal clear. I was kind of worried when I saw the hardener as it has a blue cast to it. But when it is mixed that seems to diminish or disappear.  Unlike some I have used this doesn't have too much of an odor.  The caveat in the instructions is for not pouring this more than 1/4 inch thick at a time. So I have some pieces that will get a second pour today to be full.

Once again another "kit" without any kind of written instructions. The "leather" has a really awful smell when you open the box. It tends to air out after a while. The earring findings are about what you would expect and are functional. The biggest problem for me is that you have to trace around the wooden templates and then hand cut each earring with scissors. That's really not a great way to do  this.  The sheets of  "leather"  are not really totally flat either, so with the size of the templates they aren't going to be completely flay without some kind of rigid backing. All in all not a great system.
WOW. There are a lot of cool designs in this pack. Lots of uses including gelli printing. Nice size for a lot of different projects. Very good.

This works so much better than trying to draw lines and get everything to line up right. I've done hundreds of mandalas on rocks and canvases over the years and this is going to make it so much more fun.
The video on how to use these is sketchy to say the least. They are useless to me without instructions.
I use a lot of these when I make handmade greeting cards. Lots in the package and they stick really well.
These brushes are more than adequate for any art or craft use. The lot of palettes are going to be perfect for me to mix gouache in. Great for studios use.

If you do art or any kind of crafting, these wooden pieces are for you.  Super smooth finish. Work with painting and wood burning. Also good for vinyl sign applications and just about anything you might want to try. Sturdy enough to hang and very light.

If you do art or any kind of crafting, these wooden pieces are for you.  Super smooth finish. Work with painting and wood burning. Also good for vinyl sign applications and just about anything you might want to try. Sturdy enough to hang and very light.
Mask making is so much easier with this. It can be knotted and threaded in pockets with ease. A little goes a long way. Good stretch.

I've been an artist for a long time but never had any gouache. Wasn't sure why I would need it. Still not sure. This is very much similar to an acrylic paint except that when it dries on your palette you can reactivate it with water. This paint comes in little tubs that fit in the holder provided. They are hard to get in and out. There are no instructions as to whether you should peel off all the tops or not. The lit on the holder fits pretty tightly, but I can't see that opening all of these is not going to get them to all dry out. A little information would be helpful. I did 2 small paintings with this one on black watercolor paper and one on white. Truthfully I could have achieved the same thing with acrylic watered down.
Nice colors and variety of sizes in each bottle. Great for adding to resin. Lots of bling!
Nice roll of elastic. Easy to use off the roll and to cut whatever length you need.
These are great for stirring anything from paint to resin. Lots of craft uses.  Nice amount for a good price.
Nothing super special about these but they will add bling to your project.
I decided to try these with my wax seals. They make a really nice seal. The glue is translucent and very appealing. Seems to take a long time to melt.  Very flexible end product.
I wasn't expecting this to be so wimpy for lack of a better word. It is thin which is good for your ears, but I would use less than other elastic for a mask because it has a lot of stretch.

Very giftable, all inclusive sealing kit.  I love the holder that you can rest the spoon in while the wax melts. It save time standing there holding it while it melts. Not a big fan of the actual seals included. This is the third tree of life I have now.  3 wax pellets will get you a nice full seal. Lovely envelopes included to get you started.  Now if only we could use these on real mail without an extra charge.
A really varied set of enameled metal charms. Good quality. Fun. I'll be using these on greeting cards I make for a little extra bling.

This is at least the 7th or 8th set of colored art pencils I have reviewed in the last 6 months. I am mainly looking for a larger color palette than I have with my original Prismacolor set. After reading about these I though they may be the answer. Good price. Nice presentation box that is easy to use.  Good color selection although some of the colors are so nearly identical that you can't tell them apart with the naked eye. The problem is the pencil core itself. They are not  "soft". When you use them they form hard lines that are then difficult to blend out. Pencil blending does work some. I always use Gamsol for blending and you can just about get the lines out, but frankly the colored area is just not as smooth as it would be with the Prismacolor brand. The colors are not as saturated either as I was led to believe. If you are looking for a pastel kind of look than grab these. If you want something more vibrant don't.

Very small welled palette for mixing or dispensing a wee bit of paint. I like it for mixing watercolor paint and also acrylic. Both are very easy to clean off the ceramic surface.  The lid and the base are heavy and well made.
Darling little charms for jewelry or craft projects. Well made with good enamel work and vibrant colors.

The first thing I check on these kits is the adhesive on the backing.  Sadly this one isn't very sticky. Beautiful colors and great picture. Included are the standard tools and trays.  The drills appear to be well formed and separate in the baggies. This will be a really pretty picture when it is done.

I really like this concept. However this dry media paper wasn't my favorite of the ones I got. I had a hard time getting  a good contrast with the media available to me. I tried a little watercolor  metallic paint to see what would happen and it wasn't good. This is truly for dry work only.
The only problem I was having with the handmade masks I was making was conforming them to my nose. Problem solved and now my glasses aren't fogging up.
I love this paper for mica watercolor paint. It is smooth and easy to work on and sturdy as heck.
I have used these for many years. They are the best as far as I am concerned.
I got this cord not to use for macramé but to use in masks for ties. It is just the right thickness, soft enough to not bother your skin and easy to tie. Perfect!
Got this to use over diamond paintings. It's the third thing I've tried. I don't like it any better than the other for a sealant. Takes all the shine away.

First off this mat is thick and more rigid than a regular Cameo mat.  You may have to adjust your machine in a myriad of ways to get it to feed. Secondly reverse the top and the bottom. The margin at the top is wider than a regular mat so you either have to start you cut lower on the page or flip it over because the bottom margin is more in line with a Cameo mat.  Vinyl was easy to get off the mat because of the backer. Thicker art paper was OK too. Not great with thinner paper.
My old bottle got so clogged I couldn't use it. Good stuff though if you can keep the tip clean.

Well this is the third different kind of felting pad I've tried and non of them are truly exceptional. This one is a nice size. Flat and easy to clean. It is way to hard though for single needle felting.  If you really want to stick something together fast to get a shape you don't want this. It does however give a nice finish. So I'm using my burlap and rice pad for the grunt work and this for finishing.  Really wish there was one pad that did it all besides foam.

I was hesitant about these after reading the other reviews, but I needed something suitable for a cat I was making in needle felting. I must have got a good batch, because they are pretty good. A bit of variation, but I suspect these are handmade and having worked in hot glass I know how hard it is to achieve perfection.
I had a really old grommet kit that was my Dad's and he used the heck out of it. I needed some more grommets anyway and this was good because it had new setters as well. Works like a charm!

Nothing special about these, but they are fine for stamping greeting cards. A little bit low profile so you have to be careful not to ink the edges and transfer that. Otherwise it is an easily readable script and nice to have.
Gorgeous colored wax. 3 pellets melted is enough to get the perfect impression on the larger interchangeable brass stamps.  It is very flexible wax, not at all brittle.

After conducting a few tests with this vinyl I can say that I wouldn't be using it for anything that needed to be really permanent. It cut well on my Cameo with no problems. It passed my thin font weeding test fairly well. As to the adhesion of it, it's not great in my opinion.  It had to be seriously burnished down to the carrier sheet to even be able to weed. Transfer went well, but it really didn't want to stick to the project.  I finally got it down, but wouldn't use it in an application where you want the vinyl to stay. It would certainly have to be sealed. Good color selection though.

I like these for a big pop of bling in artwork. Some of the colors didn't really produce a very noticeable outline and others did.  Nice nib that flows and allows free movement of the pen for swirly and intricate designs.

I've been reviewing a lot of art supplies in the last several months.  Quite a few have been disappointing or not as advertised.  This is some of the best watercolor I have ever used, and I have used most of them.  One thing about cheap watercolor that is nasty is the chalky feel when it dries. This doesn't do that.  At all.  Not a great tin that these come in which is odd, because the 50 tin set up is pretty nice. This one is sort of flimsy. Very saturated colors so even though the pans are small, you can get by with less paint. A really good color palette for sure although I recommend doing the swatch paper that is included because the names of the colors are really not the names I would use. Looking forward to painting something with these and I will be thinning out some of the other pans I have used in the past.

I've been reviewing a lot of art supplies in the last several months.  Quite a few have been disappointing or not as advertised.  This is some of the best watercolor I have ever used, and I have used most of them.  One thing about cheap watercolor that is nasty is the chalky feel when it dries. This doesn't do that.  At all.  Very saturated colors so even though the pans are small, you can get by with less paint. A really good color palette for sure although I recommend doing the swatch paper that is included because the names of the colors are really not the names I would use. Looking forward to painting something with these and I will be thinning out some of the other pans I have used in the past.

I really liked this paper today when I did a trial run. It has a smoother side that was really easy to paint on. It didn't seem to curl a lot with the water the way some do.  I didn't feel that it had a very long working time even wet on wet. It really seemed that once your paint was down it was there and it wasn't moving a lot. It could have been the paint though since I was also reviewing Artistik brand watercolor. I also did a trial with water based markers and was not able to achieve any blending. Normally with Tombow you can blend pen with pen, but on this paper no blending even with water.

Finally after reviewing about 7 or 8 of these sets I found one that has the right sized nibs for my use.  Not too fine, not too wide. Just right for writing or doing detail. Standard color palette, but a nice enough selection of colors.
Good color palette. Nothing unusual though. They seem juicy enough and lay down on paper well. The fine point is very fine. Smallest details only.  Good for a student or beginning artist.

For an off brand of transfer film, this seems to work quite well. Comes with it's own little squeegee to burnish the vinyl. In my tests it picked up fonts and shapes quite well and then released when it needed to. All in all a win!

Firstly there aren't nearly the range of colors that are in the picture. Not by a long stretch. The white isn't white, more of a gray. Most importantly, I couldn't weed this to save my life. Whenever I do a vinyl test I do something with a smaller font and fine detail. If I can do it with Oracal, than I want to do it with the pretender.  I couldn't keep this stuck to the carrier sheet at all for weeding. No matter how much I burnished. I suppose if you do large pieces without detail it might work for you, but I don't work that way. I didn't try to stick it to anything because I couldn't get anything to weed.
If you felt, you need this. Works well, smoothly and seems to be sturdy. No broken needles so far.
This is a good tool for your felting arsenal. Easy to use and great for flattening ears and such.
Nice to have a large quantity of this. Easy to cut and easy to use dry or with water.

Some of the molds in this set are the same as other sets I have and some are different. Some nice earring sets in this. I suppose it depends on what you plan to do. I like the little mixing cups that are silicone and reusable. You can just let the resin harden in them and then pop it out for a clean cup.
I was really liking the concept of these but they leak. The connection between the halves where you push to get water to the brush sprays out all over the place.
If you do diamond painting you have to get one of these. Amazing. No glare because your light is underneath and it makes the whole process easier to see.
These trays are better than little dishes to put your diamonds in. The little raised lines help to get them flipped over and when they are lined up you can really lay diamonds quickly.
I do needle felting and sometimes I use a bit of floss for accent work. This is just the perfect thing to keep nearby. Great color selection neatly presented and easy to get to.  I really like it!

I am really impressed with these. The paper is pretty good for dry media. Markers of course bleed through, but pencils and pastels are fine. The book itself is bound very sturdily.  Heavy and put together well.
I got these planning to use them as armatures in needle felting, but they have no sturdiness at all. Worse than a limp noodle.
Never had a bit of trouble with one of these silicone molds. These are a little thin, so be sure to fill them up to the very top or you won't get a sturdy product.
Lovely colors and good adhesive on this one. Huge. It is going to take a while to complete. So far so good with the diamonds.  Very fun and relaxing thing to do. Try it!

When I first opened the box I was really excited. Nice color chart. Pencils themselves are randomly placed in the trays, so the first thing I did was color grade them into groups. In that process I realized that there was an admiral blue I think that didn't have any core showing. So I shoved it in the sharpener. And sharpened, and sharpened, until the light came on that it was missing the core all together. So one color out. I had a drawing on my table that I wanted to color so I started out. It was a little girl and I immediately realized there aren't any skin tones light enough. So I improvised and then tried to blend. I use Gamsol to blend colored pencil and have for 25 years.  You can't use it with these. Didn't do a thing.  Not happy. Ruined drawing. Good for kids maybe.

I finally managed to get this open today and was pleasantly surprised. The number of things this paper could be used for is astounding. Covering art surfaces. Tables for rustic dining. Just unroll a big piece on the floor and give your kids some crayons. Hours of fun. It could be gelli printed for wrapping paper. The list goes one. Great paper.

When I first opened a tube of this I would have sworn it was acrylic paint. It is very thick and needs to be thinned quite a bit. Great color selection for watercolor paint. Even some pinks actually that you don't have to mix.  I deducted a star because it is impossible to puncture the tubes without ending up with paint everywhere. Gobs of it. So beware.

I used this today to do some monoprinting on coasters. It is a good smooth and creamy paint. Vibrant colors and a good selection.  I deducted one star because the bottles are hard to get open and they aren't completely full.

When I review water based pens like these I use the gold standard of Tombow markers for comparison. I found these pens to be somewhat comparable. The brush nib is flexible and easy to use. You can blend color to color and then clean the nib on paper. Although I don't think the nibs will hold up to this as well as the Tombow. The fine point is really fine and not much use unless you are just writing with it. Vibrant colors and good selection.
Good color palette. Pretty much the standard colors of pan watercolor. The lid doubles as a mixing palette, but mine fell off the first time I opened it and now won't stay on at all.

I tried these on several different surfaces because I wanted to use them to make original art able to be removed from a greeting card for display. Oddly enough they really don't want to stick to mat board paper. Then I stuck one on my smooth surfaced work table and it was better, but not the best. They aren't terribly thick either and when they stick to each other it's not great.

My new rule is one star for items that have no instructions. There are some nifty tools included in this kit, but unless you want to look up the description and try to figure out what they are for, this is a waste of my time. I do like the little boxes with the containers for sorting the diamonds.

This is a nice enough mold. Parts of it are very usable. I deducted a bunch of stars because I had no idea what to do with one of the molds as there were no  pre drilled holes for findings. I dislike having to track down videos to find out information that could easily be included on a sheet in the pack.
I have reviewed and purchased many different kinds of gel and glitter pens. By far these are the best in recent memory. Smooth writing and good colors. Much like a ballpoint pen. Very nice!
Not very much in the packets, but a little goes a long way in resin depending on the effect you are after. Super nice color palette.
Perfect size for a work table doing resin or encaustic wax painting.  Easy to clean.
Not very much in the packets, but a little goes a long way in resin depending on the effect you are after. Super nice color palette. Works with polymer clay too!
Not very much in the packets, but a little goes a long way in resin depending on the effect you are after. Super nice color palette.
Not very much in the packets, but a little goes a long way in resin depending on the effect you are after. Super nice color palette.
Not very much in the packets, but a little goes a long way in resin depending on the effect you are after. Super nice color palette.

I sure am glad I didn't have to buy these. I almost ordered some when I got a chance to review. For the price, they do not burn well at all. It takes forever to get a big enough pool of wax to get a good impression using the larger stamps that are available today. Nice colors, but one stick isn't going to last for more than 7 or 8 impressions.
I sent a lot of art mail and we all use washi tape to decorate our envelopes. This set is all very thin width. Nice patterns and would be especially well suited to wedding invitations.

Once again, a kit with no use instructions. How are you supposed to learn to do this with no guidance. Any item I get for review that has no included instructions automatically gets one star. In the description there are multiple pictures of finished products which don't show anyone, anything.
I have several other sets of stamps just like these. They are terrific. Easy to change the ends and easy to store. Work well and you get a good impression.
I got this to use for small HTV jobs. It is the perfect size for use with an iron and is very thick and protective of your surface.

I had been wanting to try some HTV projects and thought this was a chance to do that. Wasted various parts of several days and I was never able to make this vinyl work. I finally got it to cut on my Cameo, but was unable to weed it at all. Then I remembered people in my vinyl forum talking about cheap HTV and how impossible it is to work with.  This must be what they were referring to.

I had been wanting to try some HTV projects and thought this was a chance to do that. Wasted various parts of several days and I was never able to make this vinyl work. I finally got it to cut on my Cameo, but was unable to weed it at all. Then I remembered people in my vinyl forum talking about cheap HTV and how impossible it is to work with.  This must be what they were referring to.

Getting back into doing jewelry shows. I never had these before, but they are going to improve my display. Very sturdy material. Way better than paper. Simply staple them together after you put your product in there and they are good to go.

I have done paper marbling before, so I do have experience. Having said that, this is an impressive little kit. Everything you need including high quality paper. I like that everything comes in a tray that can be used for the solution. There is a lid to cover it so you can save it and reuse it if all works well.
I deducted a star because some of the "paint" doesn't mix up well in the bottle. I don't know if it has been laying around too long or what, but the white in particular will not mix no matter how well you shake it. Also the red. The black just sinks to the bottom of the tray and makes it so that you can't use your solution more than a few times because it keeps pulling up black from the bottom and making everything muddy. The colors are kind of hard to mix together and it really takes more than you thing to get a good end product. No way are you getting 80 pulls from these bottles of paint. Still it's a fun deal.

These are really a solid 3 1/2, but we don't have that option now do we? I was excited to see these in the nice sturdy carry case instead of the soft sided things the others are in. Until the handle pulled out and fell off 5 minutes into moving these from the house to the studio. The color chart that is included is nice. At least you don't have to write your own, just color in the blocks that are numbered. My set had two number 29's and I am missing a 182. So much for quality control.
On the whole they seem to work as well as any of the off brands and they do blend well.

Been an artist a long time. These are the strangest acrylic paints I've ever used. They are almost translucent. Very creamy. White when added to anything else just disappears. Can't use it to highlight or anything. When it was dry it has a gloss finish. I wouldn't recommend.

I like this for adding to my resin jewelry. The shaker tops are great because it makes it harder to spill all over the place. The only problem is that there are some larger sized shapes, like little flowers, etc. and there is no way you can shake them out. The holes are too small, so you end up having to take the lid off anyway.
Haven't had any of these for a long time. The pens are nice. There are 30 pens, but not 30 different colors. Mostly 2 and 3 of each color. They are easy to hold and easy to squeeze.

I have done dot painting and mandala painting for many years now. On rocks on ATC's on all kinds of things. I have a set of McGill tools that have served me well. This set is OK. There seems to be a bit of duplication in the gradation of tools. I do like the handles and the very smaller sizes. The stencils that came are nice, but there are no instructions or pointers on how you might use them. Some of them are poorly cut and not usable. All in all, if you like to do this painting and don't have tools, it would be a good starter set.
Very consistent size. Obviously dyed, but they will make a great gemstone tree. Good color mix. Nice holes.

Its good on a roll, but great in these pre cut sheets. Just the right size for a table workspace for a small painting project. This stuff stands up to whatever you throw at it. Yesterday it was resin. Never soaked through to the table.  Awesome stuff.

I've been reviewing alcohol markers for a while now. Of all the ones I've had recently these are my least favorite for a lot of reasons. I got really excited when I opened them because along with a numbering system they have the actual color written on them. Good idea except that the descriptions aren't anywhere close. There is no good red in the palette. A lot of the colors are so similar they look the same. These are easy to hold but that's about it. The soft sided carrier is useless as soon as you open it.
