The colors aren't really very saturated. They don't seem to blend as well as others. I could go on, but you get it. I wouldn't recommend these.
Nice pens that are actually metallic. They look great on black canvases and are easy to use. Good color selection.

I had no idea that I could change the blades on my cutter. These fit  like they were custom made. They are very sharp, but there is a little case included that holds all the blades in it. I would not call this cut a true pinking cut. It is more of a rounded scallop cut, so I hope that it will still work and not fray out. Cut through several layers of fabric like it wasn't even there. Excellent!

The patterns on this origami paper are very nice. They remind me of a lot of scrapbooking paper I have seen. I think this is a bit thicker than the usual origami paper, at least what I have used in the past. Having said that, it does fold just fine.  Being double sided makes for a really nice finished product.  I am very pleased.
I love this. It takes a lot of wax to get a good impression, but it is fine craftsmanship for sure.

I like the fact that these have a smaller nib. The only problem is that half of them don't work. They need to be set cap down and left for a few hours so the ink is at the tip. But even than wouldn't get all of them to write and no amount of shaking is gonna make it happen.
This is a great pack with the stumps and the sandpaper strips. Easy to use.

These little blanks are the best. I love making little decorative signs with vinyl. These are just perfect for that. Lightweight and they don't need sanding. Might try some wood burning on a few too and see how that goes.
Boy this cuts fabric like butter. I had forgotten what it was like to have one of these that works. Adjusting to the smaller wheel.

I haven't done and any wax sealing for 25 years at least. This product has me all geeked up to do way more.  What a genius idea. The little pieces are great and melt pretty fast. What is cool is being able to mix 2 together and get something really nifty. Toss the candles that come with the wax. They are useless. Soft and crushed in fact. I did a seal on a piece of watercolor paper because I use that for mail art altered envelopes. Once it had cooled, that stamp wasn't going anywhere. Stuck like glue.  Be sure you have a paper towel handy to wipe out your little spoon when you are finished. Lovely product!

I have a Silhouette Cameo cutting machine.  I did a sample cut on a celtic knot file that was handy using this vinyl. It worked very well. It cut cleanly and weeded without any trouble. It seems a bit thinner than Oracal 651,so I don't know how well it would hold up to outside uses, but it was a pretty sticker and I used it on a gift box.
These are exceptional brushes  for rounds. Big bellies to hold the paint, then tapering out to the finest of points.Super for brush work like Tole and other stroke painting.
This is the 6th set of various paint markers I've reviewed recently. By far the best color selection.  Medium nibs. Acrylic. Good for doing Kindness Rocks and other small artwork. Happy to have them!

I tried these out today and they are good for painting on rocks. They have a fairly large nib, so you really can't do anything complicated. Good for dot work if that's what you do. Vibrant colors, but not great coverage on a darker surface.

Like most watercolor paper this has a smoother side and a rougher side. It is heavy paper and held up to my tests. If you really rub it with a watercolor marker in blending, it will pill and peel, but that is not uncommon for the off brands of paper to do that.
I love this tape. This set actually has some that is wider than most and very thing. All have applications if you are doing mail art. Perfect gift for the cat lover.

Nothing particularly special about these  pens. They are the standard paint pens with a large nib. Shake and press and you are good to go. Good for painting on rocks. Especially if all you do is text on them.

I have made many molds from various things over the years. Why anyone would want to do it this way is beyond me. First you have to weigh each component. Then mix.  Then pour it over your original and a separate container. Wait 12 hours! Then hope that you can get the original out. I don't have the need for many complete 360 degree molds. I much prefer the 2 part putty system that sets in a few minutes. I suppose if you do a lot of epoxy figures, this is handy. Kind of a mess for me.

Interestingly enough these are almost exactly the same as the Artify brand I was given to review a while back. The case, the packaging and most of the brushes are the same. They are good brushes though and easy to handle. Now I can finally ditch the old paintbrushes I've had for 20 years.

Picture is original art.

Very disappointing. These have a great selection of colors, but they have a lot of issues as well. My set had a pen that apparently was leaking. It leaked all over everything in the trays. I had to wipe off most of the pens. The pen that was leaking is now of course dry and useless. Some of these pens seem dry to start with. Don't even to use the fine point end. Too dry. Nothing happens. There is no coloring number system, and no color names on the pens themselves. This means you have to take the lids off to make a test scribble. Almost impossible to get the lids off in the first place and especially on the small nib end.
The brush ends are good, but will not last. I did one piece of art and one of the brushes was already getting ragged. The ink that leaked got all over my hands and will not come off. Not suitable for children.
Blending only happens if you are using watercolor paper. There is no blender pen included, so you have to use water and a paint brush or one of the other pens, which was OK, but not great. These did not blend at all on a smooth substrate.
I have used a set similar to these from Tombow for many years. They have never dried out or failed. Not quite as many colors as this set, but way better quality in all aspects.

Until now I've never had a paint pen I liked. They always leak, have fat tips and get clogged up. This set has nice pointy tips. A great color selection. A couple of issues. The caps are really hard to get off. I had to get a pair of pliers. Be gentle when you prime these for the first time or you will have paint all over everything. I don't think they are going to last long because I played around painting one rock and the color I used the most of had cone down quite a bit. You can see through the barrel on some of the colors.  Probably not suitable for children due to the cap issues and the fact that the paint doesn't come off your hands.

I prefer these kind of canvases in stretched and in board form. They just give you a smoother finished product. I had never seen stretched canvases like this before, so I am happy to have them.  Very quality product.

Well I hate to write this kind of review, but there are a lot of issues with these. First of all they are not really a fine point pen. I use Microns all the time and these equate to a .04.  Not fine at all. Fine is .003. That's fine. They are very thin and difficult to hold.  To top it off, they are not waterproof. AT ALL. I used these to draw an ATC today on watercolor paper. As soon as I laid in the watercolor pencil and touched it with a wet brush, the ink started to run all over the place.  I tried it on different paper and even tried heat setting it with a heat gun, and it still ran. Tried it with alcohol markers and it smeared. This is not a suitable artist product by any means.

I have never used a black canvas but I have some new metallic paint markers that look fantastic on this. I participate in a monthly 5 x 7 canvas art swap online. My partner next month is going to get a fabulously cool painting on one of these.

I've never had a full set of Filberts before and these are really nice. Good for decorative one stroke painting. No stray hairs escaping into the painting. Love these and am pleased to say they are well made.

I tried these today on an ATC I was doing. Very vibrant color. These do not seem chalky like other watercolor pencils I have used. They are more like a colored pencil. They blend easily, but the pigment doesn't go as far as a watercolor pencil might. I love the wide range of colors. They seem very firm as well and not prone to tip breakage. I think once I get used to the way these work I am really going to enjoy them.

The only thing I really don't like is the packaging. The pencils are in 5 separate inner trays in the tin sorted by color. The problem is the trays are very flimsy. When you lift them out, they sag and all the pencils fall out.

I've been wanting one of these for years but was not willing to pay exorbitant money to get one from a local potter.  I will say I wouldn't have picked this color. It's way beyond bright orange. It holds a big ball of yarn and  the yarn stays put as you are pulling on it. I do loom knitting for dishcloths and this is great!

I draw a lot. Mostly illustration type work.  This is a great set of calligraphy and fine line nibs.  They didn't bleed through the paper. If you press too hard though the lines tend to feather a little, so use a gentle tough.  Happy to add these to my drawing arsenal.

I'm not overly impressed with these. They do have a fine nib, but they are not good for filling in a large space. Very streaky colors. The colors are vibrant, but the caps look a lot alike, so it is hard to pick a color specifically without doing a test scribble which wastes ink. I do a lot of fine drawing, but they don't blend at all. I think these are best used for writing only.

Where to start..I have most of the alcohol markers available on the market except Copics. These are Copic knock offs that aren't refillable to the best of my knowledge.

Pros:
Lots of color selection although some of the colors are so similar that the gradation is negligible.
They have a fine end and a broad end and they color very smoothly.
The blender pen actually works to erase little mistakes better than any of the others I have.

Cons:
I absolutely hate the soft sided bag they come in. It is useless. As soon as you unzip it, the markers belly out everywhere. If it just had rigid sides that would help.

I don't like the fact that all the markers are just randomly mixed in the bag, so none of the like colors are together. I will have to spend hours doing a color chart so I know what's what.

The numbering system makes zero sense. I won't know for sure until I do the color chart, but there seem to be large gaps between like colors.

Lastly not so much a pro or a con just informational. I recently got some Artify brand markers to try. These are identical. Even the same numbering system and the colors match.

I've never had a chance to try watercolor canvases stretched, The only kind I've ever seen are the boards,  These are well made and very pure white. It's nice to be able to get quality supplies without having to overpay.

These are fun to play around with. The colors are pretty much neon brights. You have to really shake them up to get the glitter mixed in. Before you do that though be sure the tops are screwed on. I had several that were loose when I took off the cap. Once you get the nib ready to go they color pretty well.

I was very happy when I opened this. A couple of these diamond painting I have bought have very poor adhesive. This one is very tacky and the gems stick well. The diamonds in this set, seem just a tad smaller than others I've made. For that reason I wouldn't recommend this kit for a beginner. I can't wait to finish this because it will be a stunner!

I've bought a lot of gemstone beads and these seem to be very nice. I think they may be color enhanced somewhat. The holes are uniform and even and the beads are consistent in size. These will make gorgeous gemstone trees. I love fluorite.

I've bought a lot of gemstone beads and these seem to be very nice. I think they may be color enhanced somewhat. The holes are uniform and even and the beads are consistent in size. These will make gorgeous gemstone trees.

I grabbed a piece of watercolor paper and played around with these as you can see. The flow is totally amazing with these brushes. It is the most pleasurable painting experience I've had with a brush since the last real hair brush I got. You cannot beat these for calligraphy and watercolor painting.

I've been noodling around with this paint since it came. I've tried a bunch of the different tips, which are hard to clean if you use them. It's nice paint, but hard to control the application on fabric. I've tried it on paper as well and I think that I will probably use it in paper art projects as opposed to fabric. I might try actually painting with it on fabric. It has a good  flow to it. I just found it hard to squeeze the tubes and control the paint.
These brushes are perfect for doing watercolor wash backgrounds. Very fluffy and full and they hold a lot of water or paint. Easy on the hands and enjoyable to use.

I've been looking for a small brayer like the one in this pack every where for weeks. I was thrilled to get these. They are way better made than the Speedball brayers I have been using.  The sizes are perfect for block printing and gelli printing. They are a wonderful product. You will not be disappointed.

I was expecting these to be more Chinese crap, but I am pleasantly surprised. These are akin to the Caran d'ache water soluable crayons except maybe a little softer and way less expensive. Very wide tip though so these are not suitable for smaller detail work. They blend quite well except for a few of the darker metallic shades which didn't really smooth out.  Good color selection including some metallic. For the money this is a great set.

I have used all the name brand markers out there. These are quite good. The colors are vibrant. Easy to blend. The fine nib is a little hard at first compared to some of the others, but you can get in the tight spaces with that end very well. The nature of the nib will insure it's longevity as compared to say Chameleon nibs which have to replaced very frequently. The broad nib is great for covering a lot of space quickly. I love the carrying case. It is perfectly made.

Downside with these is they are not refillable. I also don't see a place to get replacements without buying a sets.
I've been using these for several days now and I have to admit I am very impressed. For the price tag, they are better quality brushes than some I have paid way more for. Fabulous for detailing work.
Expensive but better than the off brands for sure.
I'm really bad about cleaning brushes. This saved a bunch I was ready to toss.

I have used a lot of tools in my life. I thought this would be a good thing to use for some freehand art work on wood. No so. A lot of the issues could probably be mitigated with better instructions in readable English.
Pro's:
Easy to hold if you have small hands

Cons:

Difficulty inserting the engraving tip. The collar that holds the tip was turned so that the set screw wasn't accessible. I had to finally get a pair of pliers and forcibly turn it to get the screw lined up in the small access slot. Then I inserted the tip, tightened and it fell out. The screw has to almost be completely removed and then the tip really jammed in to about half it's depth. Then tighten.

Conflicting information on what kinds of material you can engrave. The ad says glass, the instructions do not. I personally wouldn't try it with this tool because I don't have a death wish.

I tried this on tile. Be sure to wear good eye protection because stuff flies off everywhere on hard surfaces.

Wood was the most successful venture, but only if you used the template to control the tool. It did not work for freehand work. If you turn it up to high it just bounces and leaves a perforated appearance.

The plastic template will not last. The engraving bit is too wide and as soon as you try to use it you bore out the template as well.

I personally cannot recommend this especially for a beginner.
Good selection of colors for the cutting machine.

I just finished a little play piece with this paint and I was very impressed. It has good saturation. It is creamy and brushes well. Blends well. Good mica saturation which gives a lot of shine and light bounce. When the piece was dry it was just as flat as when I started. No warping of the paper.  I think this will work well with gelli printing. Excellent product.
I do a lot of rock painting. This is the best paint. Thick enough to cover in one coat, but smooth.
It's nice to have a product that delivers a consistent slime result. This works quite well and is easy to use. A good value since you don't need much to make a batch.

I was anxious to try this out.

What you need to know:
It comes with one satin ribbon cartridge only. This is just ribbon, not adhesive.
Additional cartridges are 9.99 each. You can buy a lot of ribbon from the store for that money.
Instructions are a bit complicated.
It produces a good product if you need something very specific and personalized, but once again who is going to print a package ribbon that cost 6 or 7 dollars worth of tape?

This may be useful for greeting card making, but overall I think it is too costly to use as shown. An extra cartridge of the adhesive ribbon would have been nice to have and try.

This is a fantastic travel art case or beginner artist case. Everything is included but the paper. All neatly arranged and zippered shut. There is even sandpaper for sharpening the blending stumps. Full array of graphite pencils. A self contained water brush for the watercolor pencils. The thing here that makes my heart go pitty pat is the pencil extender. I have thrown out so many pencil stubs that could have still been used if I had had one of these. This set will make your artist happy whether they are young or old. Fantastic!

I finally got a chance t try these out today.  Soft, not particularly. Not anywhere close to as soft as a good Prismacolor pencil. Blendable. not at all. I tried all the traditional ways of blending that you could use with a colored pencil. One color over another, no good. White pencil over it, no good. Finally I tried a blending tool and some Gamsol and that flat out lifted the pencil all together. I would not recommend these except for a child to use.
Nice roll of vinyl. sticks well and cuts well.

Nice sharp scissors for cutting. Not good for detail work with the points. The reason is that with just the tiniest bit of over pressure on the handles the tips bypass each other and don't cut. Kind of a bummer. I got these for doing some fine paper cuts where the points are the part that you need.
These are amazing. Cut through 18 gauge wire like butter. Cut through heavy cardboard like it wasn't there. Very sharp. Easy to handle and use. I love Fiskars!

I love this cabinet. I've been using cabinets designed for hardware in my art studio for many years. They are clunky and the drawers are brittle and break. This product is super for a craft room. The drawers are heavy somewhat flexible plastic. Kind of narrow, but they don't have dividers to fuss with. I wish I had found these years ago. Now to get organizing!

Been an artist for a long time and used many different kinds of paints. I have never had a watercolor paint that smelled this bad. I don't recall any of them having any odor at all. I've been reviewing some recently and this is not one I would buy or am likely to use.  The colors are just OK, not particularly vibrant. They mix well and blend good if you get it wet enough. All in all I cannot recommend these at all. They are also in metal tubes which in my experience seem to dry out faster.

I generally hate glitter, but I've been making slime for some young folks lately. This selection has great colors. Very fine glitter. Works good for resin casting as well. The lids are OK, but they pop off if you aren't careful. The shaker part of the lid is nice, but there is a seal over the tube that has to be removed. In the process, the seal gets jammed in the lid and you can have a big mess if you aren't careful. Love the colors.

Got this just to make slime with for kids that visit me. They love the stuff and I put it in little tins for them to take along. This glue worked great in my recipe. The bottle size it the perfect measurement. One recipe of this makes 2 really nice stress balls as well. Love it!
I needed some of this to repair a bracelet for a friend. I am reminded why I don't use this stuff in my own jewelry. Difficult to work with.

This is a fairly useless kit unless you are a child maybe. The long crystal type molds are almost impossible to unmold. The small gems are very small. Don't even bother trying to get resin in the tiny round gem molds. Total waste of time and epoxy.

I must say I am impressed. At first I thought this was not going to be a very good product. The silicone liner was all warped in the box. After all the wrapping got off it, it started to regain it's shape and now fits the hard shell quite well. The surprise for me is the quality of the cutting tools that are included. Very well made and sharp.  The mold itself is rather narrow, so the end product is small, but unmolds well and is easier to cut than I had hoped.  Great for a beginner soap maker.
Drips just as bad as any other glue gun.  I gave 2 stars because it isn't quite as stringy, but all in all I wouldn't waste my time with this.

Kind of hoped these would be something useful. I've had gel pens before that weren't Sakura brand and they were OK. These are a huge waste of plastic and packaging. Let me run the list for you according to the package itself.

Extra large ink cartridge: These aren't any bigger than Sakura and don't have a bit more ink in them.
Luxurious Comfort grip: Eh...
Smooth Rollerball Nib: Not so much. The ones I got barely write. They drag and are dry and once the color dries it isn't all the vibrant. These are all glitter colors as well. If you press down harder you can get more ink, but in the meantime you are carving a line in the paper.
Color selection isn't half bad, but the differences between shades are neglible in some cases.

All in all, not worth the money Stick with brand names when buying art supplies.

Always looking for a new marker to use in my art studio. These are fine. Nothing spectacular. I think they would be perfect for brush writing or calligraphy. Colors do blend some over top of each other, but not easily on watercolor paper. The ink flows with a very light hand, but they seem to dry fast, so covering a larger area isn't great.  The brush tips are OK, but they pick up stray lint easily and then the brush becomes contaminated and isn't flowing as smoothly as before.  Would I buy these? Probably not. Good for a student artist, but not a more advanced person.
Nice large glue stick for smaller hands to get hold of. The purple coloring allows you to see where the glue gets placed. Sticks about as well as any glue stick I have used.

This was very easy to mix up. Simple instructions. Green or blue slime. I added a little glitter that I wanted to get rid of. Playing with this stuff yesterday was the most fun I've had in a while. Be a kid again. All the little fingers that come to my house will enjoy this!

Anyone that does hobby or craft work needs a stash of these blades. Great for cutting paper details. They also work well on soft wood. Made in America which is a plus for me. This set included 5 free blades in a small tube. The tube will be perfect for storing used blades prior to disposal.  Very happy to have these.

I suppose I could recommend these to a student,  but I probably wouldn't.  Vibrant colors, but they are not easy to work with. It is impossible to blend out the streaks from the pencil marks. I tried these on Reeves paper and ended up with a pilled up mess. Tried them on Strathmore with no better results. Get yourself some Prismacolor water color or Inktense. Don't waste your time with these.

I gave these a whirl on some AT Coins I am making. The paint is thick, but easily blended once you add some blending medium . The colors are vibrant and dry to a nice smooth finish. Glad these are plastic tubes because they won't dry out as fast. Great range of colors in the palette and they mix well. Good starter set for a student artist.

Been waiting to get this die for making Artist Trading Coins because it can easily cut chipboard for the backs better than my cutting machine. It works great, but I am a little disappointed in the quality of the die because the circle is not perfectly round. Mine has a little place where the circle doesn't meet up so there is a jagged edge. Easily fixed by trimming, but I was hoping to save time, not take time.

Not really sure what this is supposed to be used for, but it sure isn't watercolor, or water based media. It is very thin, about the thickness of the cheap card stock you buy at Michaels.  It has a very smooth texture, which is nice. I tried some wet on wet with watercolors on my palette, and the paper just curled right up. Dry on dry was better, but still this doesn't have any body to it. Next I tried Tombow water based markers, because I use them a lot in drawing and I blend with the blender pen, or water on a brush. This paper couldn't stand up to 2 swipes of blending back and forth without pilling.  I guess it will make nice enough scrap paper, or it may work with acrylics if they aren't too juicy.
Worked fine in my US Art Supply Mat Cutter. Good to have a new blade for sure.

Finally had a chance today to use this paper. It  worked wonderfully! I did some wet on wet and wet on dry techniques and was pleased on all fronts with the way the paper handled the moisture. Held the color well and has a good thickness to it. Excellent quality for all wet painting techniques including acrylic.
Love the little tubes even though they don't hold a lot. Easier to use than the big ones that are always drying up.

I found this really clunky to use. Maybe it just takes practice. Not very clear instructions about the use of the 2 included cutters. Would probably work better if the part you hold was round or square or some symmetrical shape as opposed to what it is. Not ever sure which way to grip this handle as it is. Cuts OK, but it seemed difficult to keep it in the track and make the curves on the ends. I wouldn't recommend this and would  not purchase it.

Totally love this. I have used Ranger's version of work surface for a long time. Expensive and not really worth it in my opinion.  This mat is so much better. It lays flat, not all rolled up and sort of sticks to your table so it isn't sliding around all the time. Thicker kind of silicone material and so much nicer to work on. Wipes off easily.
Nothing fancy about this paper, but the price without shipping is great for origami paper. Nice thin weight. Square sheets. Folds well. Good for a starter pack as there are lots of color choices.

I'm truthfully not sure how I feel about this. I tried to use it to make some paper flowers this morning with little success. No instructions or ideas come with it, so I'm not even sure how wet you have to make it. There appears to be a lot on the roll, I'm just not really sure what might be fun to use it for. I think it will work best for paper mache projects. As soon as I get some balloons, I may try some egg type shapes.  Thinking about weaving strips of it together and wetting it all at once then laying it in a mold of sorts to dry. Might be able to make some bases for trays that way.
This is 100% better than another brand that I just returned. Easy to use and line up.
Best vinyl I have used. Cuts like butter and weeds easily.

I am really not pleased with this paper. No grid, which I didn't notice in the description. Almost impossible to unroll since there is no backer on it. It is stuck to itself in a big way. By the time you get a piece off of the roll it is all stuck together and virtually useless.  Not sure why I thought a rollof this would be a good idea other than the price.
Good transfer paper.
Very well made rings. Heavy.
Good value. A coat of extra gesso and you are good to go.
Good canvas value for vinyl signs.
I like this a whole lot better on glass than the Plaid Folk Art stuff. Way better.
So far I'm not that impressed. Good price, but doesn't stick too well.

This product is essentially tissue paper on a roll. In fact I have tissue paper that is thicker than this stuff. As for using it on a changing table, don't bother. I spread a sheet on my counter and dribbled some water on it. Soaked right through. The paper creates no barrier to moisture or contaminates whatsoever. Total waste of good trees. Great for packing material which is how it should be marketed.

As an artist I have tried most of the markers out there. Because these are Winsor and Newton I thought I'd give it a shot. I was not impressed on any level.

1. Expensive
2. You need special paper. These did not work at all with the Neenah copic paper I normally use. Yupo is OK, but I really dislike that stuff.
3. Unable to blend unless you have the special paper.
4. Oddly shaped case that is supposed to interlock these sets together. I guess you are supposed to lay them down and stack them, because they don't have flat ends to stand on.
5. Kind of dry and they tear up the paper

I really don't think the average artist has much use for these. Being light fast for 100 years is about the only thing these offer and frankly that's not a big deal for most of us. I won't be giving up my alcohol markers for these at any rate.

These are adorable little critters. Everything included and enough to finish the project as advertised.  I would have to say that the age rating may be a bit low for this craft. The large elephant is quite thick and it is a lot to hold on to and to wrap with small hands of a child.  They could do some parts of this, but not all by themselves. Would like to have seen the adhesive pieces pre cut. It would have made the process a little smoother. Good quality yarn and a great finished project.

This is an amazing brush for something that is synthetic. I works as well as an expensive hair brush for calligraphy. Kept it's point well in ink. Easy to hold and control and overall a really great brush. Holds a lot of ink or paint so no need to refresh it. I'm not too fond of the way it is constructed from a cleaning standpoint with all the wire wrappings and acetate. All in all a great brush.
These pads were already fairly dry when they arrived and didn't work for long. The stamps are Kind of generic, but they do come in handy for small spaces.
Using this for the Kindness Rocks Project here. Good sealer as long as you don't spray too thickly.
Great for stippling and stroke work. The perfect consistency for rock painting and craft work. Way better than the more expensive paint and mixes well.

Fantastic paint collection for rock painting. Using this for the Kindness Rocks Project in my town and it is really good. Just the right consistency for stroke work and stippling. Reasonably priced and I prefer it to the more expensive brands. Made in the USA too!

Doing the Kindness Rocks Project in my town and we are using this to base coat our rocks. It is a perfect consistency for painting. Not too thick, but has good coverage. Great price and easy delivery.
I love this mat cutter. It is easy to use once you get it set up and allows you to cut straight or beveled mats. Well made and heavy.
Fiskars always makes a good tool with a good guarantee. This one works well on paper as long as it isn't too thick. Looking forward to paper piecing an altered envelope for my monthly swap.

I'm really tired of spending money on these ad looms. This set has peg problems. The tops are small and the yarn comes off too easily. The pegs pop out continually. The pegs are way too short, making it very difficult to do an e wrap because there isn't enough room on the pegs for the yarn. The quality of the needles is beyond appalling. I bought this because there were mostly good reviews. If people think these looms are good, they haven't used any of the others. Most I've tried though have been awful, except the first set of rounds that  I got.

I was thrilled to get this because of the flexibility of the configurations. I only wish I had bought it Amazon so that I could have returned it. This items has so many flaws in engineering I don't know where to start.

Probably the biggest one that is a show stopper is the fact that the pegs don't stay in the loom when you are knitting. The pieces of the loom itself don't fit all that tightly together either, so the whole thing ends up being kind of flimsy. I got so desperate to make this work I actually epoxied the pegs into the loom and they still aren't sturdy and try to to come out.

The hook is thin, separates the yarn strands all the time and bends if you strain it too much. I paid way more for this than the Amazon price and it is completely useless to me.
I bought this to try making smaller socks on, but it wasn't designed for that. This will only make loomed flowers for hats. The pegs are also very loose and try to come out when you use it.
Nice set of sentiment stamps.

Let me first say I've been stamping for a long time. I've used all kinds of blocks and grids and whatever. These were disappointing to say the least. I so wanted to be thrilled with this set. The small block is really small and I have almost no stamps that are that little.  The 2 medium blocks are good. The positioning grid had no instructions on the use of it and I was unable to find any online. The blocks themselves are kind of flimsy and hard to get a hold of. All in all I won't be trading in my standard, thick acrylic stamping blocks for these.

I've used E6000 for 20 years. Always in a big tube which clogs ups, tears or springs a leak and wastes a lot. These little tubes are the perfect answer to smaller jobs. They are easier to apply with a finer tip and less likely to clog and waste product.

I am very impressed with these pencils. I have a complete set of Prismacolor pencils that I have used for many years. I think these might be better. They sharpen to a fine point and that point doesn't break off or wear quickly. The colors are fab and smooth. These would be absolutely perfect for anyone wanting to do coloring books. They are artist quality as are most Tombow products. What knocked my socks off was the included eraser that is designed for use with colored pencils. It actually worked! Been hoping for something like that for a long time. Way to go Tombow! Now I would love to see a complete set of these pencils. I'd buy them for sure!

I've been using Tombow products for many years and they absolutely cannot be beat. This set is no different.  The calligraphy pens are of the highest quality. The eraser is wonderful and doesn't smear your pencil all around. The pencil included is a 4H and it is easy to use and keeps it's point well. This is a really great value for learning lettering. The included instructions are not all that helpful though. I went to the website to download some practice sheets as well and that isn't the best approach either. The way it is done you need to do 17 pages of printing. It would be nice if they just gave some full alphabet examples of different fonts to practice. Still this is a nice set of pens and markers for art use.
Cutting mats are a necessary evil if you have a machine. They last about 6 weeks if you are using them a lot.
Good quality eggs for craft projects. Delivered in a timely manner.

This arrived today and I am pleasantly surprised. It is rather small, which works for me, but may not be great for larger canvases. Seems well enough made except for the hardware as other people have stated.

These are OK, but really hard to use if you have thicker glue. You also need to be sure you get the needle into the applicator. I missed the first time and completely ruined one of these.

UPDATE: After fighting with this all day making cards, I've decided that it isn't even worth one star. Do not waste your money. Impossible to squeeze any kind of glue out of these.
Great paper that was just what I needed for a cutting machine project.

These scissors don't look like much out of the box, but they are  well conceived and put together. Great idea to have the holes the same so that they can be used right or left. I was kind of skeptical about the way these would cut, so I tried a variety of things including paper, cardboard and the bubble envelope they came in. I was pleasantly surprised on all fronts. They cut quite well, but on the thicker materials they don't quite cut all the way out to the tip. The only thing they would cut at the point was paper. I personally can't see using these in the kitchen even though they are food safe. At best you could open packages or wrappers and maybe cut some herbs, but I don't see them doing more than that. I will use these in my art studio where I cut a variety of things all the time.
Good selection of tips that work well for woodburning.
Very thin cording, but a good product.
Be aware that if you have SVG files you cannot use them on this without the additional 50.00 software. You also cannot return the item without paying postage and a 25% restocking fee. Huge rip off.

This does not support SVG files without the purchase of additional software. No customer service. A ripoff of epic proportion. I am returning it today after spending 3 hours getting it installed I never cut a single file.
This is not an antique gold paint. Not anywhere close. Very bright and brassy.
I got this to punch out canning labels this past summer. I had a different brand that wasn't as well made and it gave up the ghost. This is good quality and works quite well.

Waited and waited to get these for my birthday. I have the full set of pencils, but find that I am using a  brush anyway. What I dislike about these blocks is that the color works a little differently. You need to be darn sure you put it where you want it to stay. There is no working time and no moving it once it is down. Wet paper, dry paper, it's stuck there before it ever gets dry. The color numbers stamped on the blocks are almost illegible if you are trying to keep a color chart.

These canvases are a great value. Twice as many as Micheals for a lot less money. The only place I buy these is Amazon. They work well for acrylics and with the application of watercolor ground, they are fab for inktense pencils and watercolors as well.
A fun way to get a variety of colors to try. I hadn't worked in polymer clay for many years and wanted some small blocks to get going again.  This package fit the bill nicely.
Love this stuff. I got it to treat some 5 x7 canvases for a mail art swap and it works great.

Let me preface this by saying that I have done origami for 30 years and all manner of other art for way longer than that. The instructions for this product are utterly useless. I think I can figure it out based on my experience level, but I would not recommend this for a beginner.

Let me preface this by saying that I have done origami for 30 years and all manner of other art for way longer than that. The instructions for this product are utterly useless. I think I can figure it out based on my experience level, but I would not recommend this for a beginner.

Let me preface this review with saying that I love the Spectrum Noir markers. Have had both generations and recommend them to everyone. They are easy to use and a good value. On the other hand, I am not so "gobsmacked" about the new line of pencils. I bought the complete set because I am an illustrator and I like to have full sets of the tools I use. First off I was put off by the fact that there are 5 tins to sort through to find a color. I'm sure they did this, so folks could buy a tin at a time, but it easy to keep flipping open lids and looking around for just the right color you want. The pencils also fit very snuggly in the boxes and it is difficult to get them out sometimes.
As for the "creaminess" of said pencils, I would say there are some inconsistencies in that as well. Some of the Floral colors are really soft and creamy, some of the browns and grays are hard and scratchy and then there are some that are just so waxy you can't lay down two colors on top of each other.
If you are a rubber stamper or crafter that plans to use them, you will probably be fine with these. They seem to be geared more to that kind of audience. The price is right for what you get. I had no trouble sharpening the few I used. No breakage like I have encountered with the Prismacolor set I've had for 10 years. The blending solution I suspect is just Gamsol which most stampers already have if they've been using pencils before. I was able to achieve the same results using the Prismacolor pencils I had over the markers.
I will say their customer service rocks because they are going to allow me to send these back. This is a great company to deal with, I just think they kind of missed the mark with these.

I had no trouble sharpening the few I used. No breakage like I have encountered with the Prismacolor set I've had for 10 years. The blending solution I suspect is just Gamsol which most stampers already have if they've been using pencils before. I was able to achieve the same results using the Prismacolor pencils I had over the markers.
I will say their customer service rocks because they are going to allow me to send these back. This is a great company to deal with, I just think they kind of missed the mark with these.

Let me preface this review with saying that I love the Spectrum Noir markers. Have had both generations and recommend them to everyone. They are easy to use and a good value. On the other hand, I am not so "gobsmacked" about the new line of pencils. I bought the complete set because I am an illustrator and I like to have full sets of the tools I use. First off I was put off by the fact that there are 5 tins to sort through to find a color. I'm sure they did this, so folks could buy a tin at a time, but it easy to keep flipping open lids and looking around for just the right color you want. The pencils also fit very snuggly in the boxes and it is difficult to get them out sometimes.
As for the "creaminess" of said pencils, I would say there are some inconsistencies in that as well. Some of the Floral colors are really soft and creamy, some of the browns and grays are hard and scratchy and then there are some that are just so waxy you can't lay down two colors on top of each other.
If you are a rubber stamper or crafter that plans to use them, you will probably be fine with these. They seem to be geared more to that kind of audience. The price is right for what you get. I had no trouble sharpening the few I used. No breakage like I have encountered with the Prismacolor set I've had for 10 years. The blending solution I suspect is just Gamsol which most stampers already have if they've been using pencils before. I was able to achieve the same results using the Prismacolor pencils I had over the markers.
I will say their customer service rocks because they are going to allow me to send these back. This is a great company to deal with, I just think they kind of missed the mark with these.

I bought a pack of these on the recommendation of all the reviews. I do a lot of art, envies, ATCs on a daily basis and I go through erasers like crazy.  These are OK, but not as wonderful as everyone thinks. I use a mechanical pencil to draw with and these erasers don't really erase the lines well, but they sure do erase the Micron marker I use for outlining. Have used them on Bristol board and copic  stock and have had the same problem in both places.  Wouldn't recommend them.

I have many border punches and I thought this might be cool to try. It's a really well made and useful product. That said, it's costly and the punches that go in it are just about as much as the tool itself. Not sure who they think is going to spend this kind of money to punch paper. A word of wisdom to anyone who does get this. Save the box. The only instructions you are going to get are on the outside of it. In truth I think you can punch the same strips with the less expensive border punches that Fiskars sells. This thing takes up a lot of room as well. I originally thought it might be a space saver, but the unit that punch fits into is made to hold a 12 inch piece of paper plus another inch or so for the outside of it. Bottom line, it works well, but the cost is prohibitive to buy the punches.
I use a lot of this in artwork and these are a great size. I looke all over and Amazon had the best price. I will be buying more I am sure.

I got this a few weeks ago to try to beef up my stash of tangle patterns without always having to go online to find some. This book has some good hints and patterns and would be a good book for someone starting out. Much better than some of the other "basic" zentangle books. Save your money on the series and start with this one.

I got a small box of these to try before getting the bigger selection. They are vibrant and rich. The semi moist texture is really nice, but it seems to take a lot of water to get the paint to release from the cups and if they sit a little they get really tacky and the brush gets hung up.

I have one of these I've used for years and needed a new one finally. The pop out feature is nice for cleaning, but I find that the roller seems to pick up everything you don't want on there and then it is kind of hard to get clean.

I bought these to try for some stamping on altered art. I have to get a really usable impression of anything except  lace. The advertisements show pearls, seashells etc, but the impressions made would be better for casting clay than stamping on something. They do heat up and reconstitute fairly well.
These blenders are great although they don't seem to last long. As an artist I buy a bunch and keep them handy for daily use. The nibs seem to fray a little with extended use.

I have all the sets of these markers now and the more I use them the more I like them.  The leas several sets seem to have lids that are easier to remove than the first markers I got. One set had some leakage of one of the pens and one set had a duplicate and was missing a color so check them when you get them. Still trying to resolve that issue.

This is my third set of Spectrums in a a couple of weeks. As always the caps fit too tight and several of these markers were leaking all over the box. I didn't realize it until after I opened them up, or I would return them.

I really am conflicted about this stuff. I'll give you the lowdown and you can make your own decision. First off the package I got was not a factory sealed pack. It was wrapped in plastic wrap with a computer generated label in a ziploc bag. So whether I got the full measure I don't know.

Secondly the working time on this is really short. In a matter of a couple of minutes it starts to dry and crack. What I discovered is that by keeping a piece squirreled away in my hand while I was working I could have some ready and keep it from drying out.

When it finally dries it is very translucent which is good I suppose depending on what you want to do with it. It is also extremely flexible, which really didn't work so well for my application.

I will probably just take the rest of this and use it in push molds, but I won't buy it again.

After my love/ hate relationship with these markers resolved, I purchased more in the set. The color selection is good. Still have a gripe with the caps that fit way too tight. Today I opened one and the whole insides fell out on my artwork and ruined it. If they could resolve that issue these would be OK to use.
I used this for the first time today. It seems to work OK although it takes a while to dry. You definitely need to spray a distance from the paper unless you are trying to get big splotches.
Got this for doing beeswax collage ATCs. Good product and a great price as I found out yesterday when I looked at some in the craft store.
These are perfect for doing 3D paper flowers. Well worth the money spent as it will save you a ton of time and effort and produce a much better end result.
This item was much needed for doing 3D paper flowers. i'm sorry I struggled using the wrong tools for so long.

I bought an almost complete set of these alcohol markers at the craft store. I needed the red ones to fill out the set. I was hoping for something refillable, but not as costly as the Copic markers. These are OK at best. They bleed all over the paper despite saying that they don't. The caps fit so tightly that when you twist them off the whole pen comes apart and the refillable insert goes flying across your table and artwork. They bleed through everything including Bristol Board.

I will use them sparingly on glossy paper. They seem to work OK on that. They are fat and take up a lot of room to store.

I got a few of these markers with a gift certificate from a friend. I loved them so much I had to get the set. The color selection is very complete and the included stand makes keeping them together and organized a breeze. They are easy to use and blending is a cinch. The only thing I could complain about is that the cap color is really not representative of the marker color in all cases. I solved that by making up my own chart by swiping the markers on a piece of cardstock and then marking the number next to it. You can get a great watercolor effect with these without the mess of paints or watercolor pencils and water.

Pros:

Easy open packaging
Easy assembly
The manual for English is separate so there is no need to sift through multiple language variations
Included instructional DVD makes it easy to follow along with the different functions
Ability to cut intricate patterns that would not be possible with a regular steel die
Last point feature allows you to get the most out of your material
Pattern versatility

Cons:
Serious learning curve involved to get the most out of the machine
Takes a lot of workspace to set up
Starter cartridge is OK
Difficult to get it lined up
Artwork simplistic

The weak link and the linchpin of the whole system is the cutting pad. If your material won't stick, it can't cut. My cutting pad arrived curles on one end and I have been unable to get it to lay flat. Thusly I have had to use the other end of the mat so that it will feed properly. After just cutting about 15 or 20 things if that many, the pad will no longer hold the paper well enough to cut it. This is the weakest part of the system. If you are using paper smaller than 12 x 12 you are cutting in the same place on the pad everytime and it loses it's adhesive property fairly quickly. I tried the online tip to wash the pad with warm soapy water to revive it, but it still isn't working for anything other than simple paper. I cut mostly cardstock, so right now I am out of business until I can get some spray adhesive to help the adhesion.

What I liked:

The ability to resize shapes and fonts to fit the project without having a lot of large dies taking up space.
The PIP feature is fun.
The border feature is nice, but limited to just a few of the shapes on the cartridge.

What I didn't like:

Difficult to keep the paper stuck to the cutting mat. It really needs to be burnished down. This is supposed to be able to cut foam, chipboard, fabric and shrinky dink plastic as well as vellum and cardstock. Had no luck getting the shrinky dink to stick to the mat at all. Fabric was difficult. Foam was a no go. Didn't have any really thin chipboard, so I couldn't try that. What I discovered was I needed to overestimate the thickness of my material by a factor of one and increase the blade depth by a couple of clicks past what is recommended in order to get a clean, through and through cut.

The cutting mat is hard to keep free of debris. The paper won't stick all that well for cutting, but every little speck of negative cut out is stuck for life.

Had to really left justify my paper on the mat otherwise it kept cutting off the edge. I think there is a way to fix that, but all of these adjustments and fine tunings take more time and key strokes.

There is a tool for lifting the finished cut off the mat and I think that should really be included in the starter set because it is an absolute necessity.

I bought some extra cartridges to use, and overall I would have to say the artwork is rather childish for the most part and somewhat simplistic especially where the shapes are concerned. One of the the cardridges is 75 font work, which is something I don't use very much. I prefer to do text on the computer because of the huge variety of fonts involved. So far the 2 fonts that I have on the starter cartridge and the extra are individual letters which are hard to remove and then realign on your project.

All in all I think this is a very sophisticated machine that is probably overkill for the average artist. For the price tag involved you can buy a lot of Spellbinders Nestabilities Dies for shapes and take up less space with less of a learning curve. Cutting the intricate shapes is nice, but they can't be too small or they are impossible to remove from the cutting mat and hard to use in projects. If you do a lot of font work, then this would probably be a good thing for you. Someone that cuts a lot of multiples of one thing would like this. If you are like me and do original designs every time, this is not worth the big price tag.

After using this for a while longer, I have upgraded it to 4 stars. The new cutting pad I got seems to have a better adhesion than the one that came with the machine in the beginning. I have also purchased several new cartridges and have the eCal software on order. All these things make the eclips a much better product.
I needed a lot of this for a project and this pack was a good value. Works well for items that have solid colored backgrounds.
I needed a lot of this for a project and it worked well. Good price for a large lot. The matte finish is perfect if you are doing anything with an open background.

I chanced upon some of these on sale today. I really liked this pattern. Much to my chagrin there were no instructions on how to use it. I have a Sizzix Big Kick die cut machine and I managed to make it work through trial and error. The website was no help. They tell you to go there for help and then don't make it clear at all where you can get said help. These are touted as being able to be used multiple ways and I would have liked a few more instructions to be included so that they could be used to their full potential. It's a pretty pattern, but a hit or miss deal on how to use them.

I had a chance today to get several of these on sale. I had been wanting to try them for a long time. I have a Sizzix Big Kick machine. The instructions inside the folder with the dies were clear and easy to understand. Make the sandwich and run it through to cut the shape. All good. Then it gets considerably trickier and less infallable. The embossing stage was a joke. I followed all the instructions repeatedly and did not get one single cleanly embossed cut. The problem lies in the die cut moving around when you remove the original cutting pads. If the shape shifts the tiniest bit due to static you are in trouble. I tried multiple times and couldn't get a clean emboss. The embossing feature of these dies are what sets them apart. I went to the website as instructed for "tips" and found no help there whatsoever. Have to give these 2 thumbs down. Maybe with their dedicated machine it would work better, but who needs another piece of equipment cluttering up the work table?
This weight of chipboard is super for covers on albums and in altered media. It is a little hard to cut unless you are using a die cut though.

These pads are a must for using the Ranger Distress Inks. I wish I had figured out a little sooner that they can be rinsed out and reused instead of discarded. Once I came to this conclusion I was a happy camper. Gotta have these.

I bought this transparency paper to use in altered media and it is super. Runs through the printer like a charm with good quality. I used it some for an embossed project and it warped quite a bit with the heat from the embossing gun, but the finished product was still acceptable.
This great paper. I would like to see it in an 8 x 10 size for ease of use. It is a little pricey, but works well.

I love Xyron adhesive for the professional look it gives my greeting cards. I dislike the amount of waste involved and the rather expensive cost. I also am extremely frustrated that they can't put a warning strip at the end so you know when you are going to run out. I have ruined more projects than I can count because I was at the end of a roll and something got stuck partway through the machine. I've contacted them about this, but never got a hint of a response.

I finally got a chance to try this out today. I transferred a picture of one of my cats to the printable fabric and then ironed it on a shirt. The sheets are easy to use and the picture quality is not too bad considering the medium. The colors were pretty good. I think the quality of your printing is going to depend on the type of printer you have and the quality of the photo you are using. I can see this having some nice crafty uses.
I found this product rather thin and runny and very hard to use as advertised. Not to mention it curled up my cardstock into an unusable mess.
These sheets of chipboard are perfect for use in a Sizzix machine with the big dies. Good clean cuts and a nice sturdy end product.
I bought this and really like the way it works. You can make a bunch of great cards in a hurry with these dies. Would like to see a little more variety in the patterns available though.

Easy to use
Great buy for artistic people, good for children who understand responsibility.
This is a very good product for beginners with several colors and tools to work with to create many projects.
I bought this item as a gift for my granddaughter. The packaging and display was beautiful. Bright colors!

This scrapbook is so beautiful.  It looks just as it is pictures.  The picture on the front is vibrant and the quality is very good.  I will definitely purchase this scrapbook again the next time I need to make a book for a teacher, friend, etc.
Kids enjoyed making different objects! Will buy again!

Bought these for my daughter who makes key chains and jewelry. We loved the colors and cute little box they arrived in. The Clay is easily workable and the tools and jewelry fittings were a bonus. Great package for the price!

So small it is ridiculous. My 10 year old daughter can barely use it because the letters are so small. I’ve bought these before for the same
Price that we’re three times the size

Very cute and comfortable, good quality
Easy to mold and work with, doesnt seem to dry out easy which is good. Bakes just fine at 270 for about 30 minutes depending on thickness. Fun colors. Would buy again
Nice size and good quality

After dealing with way too much clay getting stuck all over my wooden roller, I finally ordered this little set. No more tears! The roller is great for polymer clay. If you're trying to roll out bulk items this may not do to the job for you as it can't do many at a time on its small surface area. Happy to have it, though, it made my Christmas crafting more pleasant.

This clay is softer and spongier than what I usually use (rhymes with pulpy) but it bakes up well and firms nicely in the oven. It also takes stamps much better than the other one so I was very pleased with how all the gift tags I was crafting turned out when I stamped names and dates on them. Clean, clear impressions that did not get distorted when releasing pressure from the stamp. I will definitely purchase again but I wish I could buy individual colors because this has a lot of duds I’ll never use.

Arrived quickly, worked perfectly, and helped me finish some touch-ups to a lot of little projects around the house. They were so easy to use that I'm going to buy the wide versions and try them out on a canvas.
We use these in a sensory bin for our homeschool and they are so fun. Kids sit for hours sorting them and matching colors. I will be back to buy a few more to add to our set
My kids had a blast learning origami, great set
Item came as expected, we used this glue to ale home made rainbow glitter slime. Works great!
The covers fell off the first time we opened them. So I gave it a 3 star review.
Excellent set with tons of clay, tools, and accessories!
Cute, handy LITTLE caddy.
I’m getting ready to make a Castiel angel tree topper. Yeah, I’m darn angry about how SPN ended (Destiel forever!), and I’m taking my aggression out on this fine polymer clay!!!
I bought this to go inside my walkers. I thought it would be more stable but it was so thin. It didn’t hold its shape. I definitely will stick to Kraft Tex. If is a much nicer product.
Works great for embroidery. So much better thanstoppung to wind your bobbins!
This clay was so dried out. We could hardly use it. It took a lot of work to get it soft enough to use. Won’t buy this brand again.
Fast shipping! My son was so happy with this! Will buy again!
worked perfect as tiebacks
I really do like this product just wish it was more affordable to use. Also the packaging was broken open when I received it.
this is the only paint that I have found that is non toxic and can be used for dishes, I do wish there was more in a bottle the price point is very high and makes it to expensive to use often.
My box came squished and therefore some of the clay was out of the wrapper and dry
Awesome.
The colors they sent me aren't what they show in the picture at all. They filled the box with tons of gray and muted, swamp greens. Many of the colors advertised were missing.

It was so hard and just crumbled to pieces! Some were ok but most was junk- no amount of kneading would fix it.
Pretty disappointed.

I ordered this and it really is beautiful but I wanted to explain a couple of things:

-There is no sewing involved, it is a foam board that you push the seams of the material in to using a butter knife.

-You have to cut out all the pieces, There is a little extra material for errors but not much. I was hoping there would be a few pieces cute out because that's the part I knew I'd have trouble with. This part ended up being a bit difficult.

-The foam mount is about an inch thick, it sticks out from the wall quite a bit.

In all its nice but it was nothing like I expected, would be a cute project to do with kids.

Got this for my daughter
And its missing several items it was supposed to come with very disappointing cuz she can’t make some of the things she wanted to make
LOVE THE COLORS AND EASE OF USE. WE ALWAYS USE THESE PRODUCTS.
