Perfect for my needs. Can't see as well as years past.
My daughter loves it and was thrilled. But the instructions are a bit strange. I can figure them out but it took me a second because of how they're written.

This clay crumbles just like a cookie…I spent hours designing earrings and many customers asked for a refund because they crumbled in transit…I even used bubble wrap for protection…very disappointing!
I am very happy of the quality of my cutters.  They were very easy to use with my polymer clay jewelry creations, will definitely be buying others
I sell jewelry and bought these to make earrings, however, they all were made for the same foot and only a couple pair matched in design!

These are fantastic!  The links are a little bigger than I expected, about half an inch long, but I like the chain a lot.  It looks unique when used for jewelry.  My only issue is that the stainless steel links are VERY hard to cut through (possibly because I have crappy wire cutters?).  I still gave it five stars, though, because I think having strong, completely sealed links in a necklace (or bracelet, or whatever) is a good thing!  No worrying about the chain breaking mid-link.  I was also thrilled with how much chain I got for the price.

Lots of colors and lots of tools. I'm especially excited about the shimmer powder! The instructions are obviously translated, but are clear enough.

Update: finally found time to use my clay; I'm extremely disappointed. It's lovely and pliable, beautiful colors, easy to work with... But never hardens. Yes I baked it. Yes I had the temperature. I baked them as long as needed. They never solidified and my tiny gnomes fell apart and broke.
Great markers!  Fun colors and they're nice and flexible so they don't snap when you're using them.

I love that I can keep all my hooks and notions together with this!  And I love the owl fabric. It fits two full sets of hooks. It also has two little zippered pickets for mini scissors, a little tin of stitch markers, tape measure, and my chibi.  My only complaint is that my Clover Amour hooks don't fit into the loops in rainbow order (the two smallest hooks fit in the outside loops, then the loops gradually get bigger towards the center).  Still, they all fit and are held securely.  I just get a little obsessive over my colors being in order!

I have a 9 year old girl, a 6 year old boy, and a 4 year old girl.  They all play with these, together.  My 1 year old would probably love them too, but I don't trust her not to put them in her mouth and choke.  The other three love them and have asked for more, as the tub isn't very big.  Plenty for one kid to play with, but beads get scarce when there are three kids fighting over them.
Beautiful hooks.  Really smooth finish, nice and lightweight.  I added a polymer clay handle to a most of them and they worked great for it.  They shipped a lot faster than expected, too.
Really handy tape measure.  A little too much fun, though...my kids won't leave it alone.
My favorite paint markers.  I use them for handpainted signs because I can get such clean lines, or paint larger areas if I need to.

I found this and a D hook here and was so excited...they were't available at my local JoAnn's, but the rest of the set was.  The price was GREAT here, half of what JoAnn's was asking for the others.  The grip is great and I can crochet for a lot longer now.  My favorite set of hooks!

I crochet for at least an hour a day and my old Boye hooks were killing me.  I found these at my local JoAnns, but they didn't carry the H or D size hooks.  I found them here for a GREAT price (half of what JoAnn's charges). I can crochet forever now without my hands cramping.  The grip is really nice and works with all kinds of methods of holding a hook.  Love them!

This clay is literally trash. It’s very greasy and too soft/flimsy to work with. When I buy things and see negative reviews I sometimes ignore them if there’s also positive reviews. If you’re the same way, listen to this negative review. Literally the worst clay ever lol
Great product
Each rubber band snapped immediately. The instructions are GARBAGE. The hooks were all tangled and bent. I wouldn’t recommend this.

So, I bought this for my 7 year old. In order for her to be able to mold the clay, I had to work it into a softer, more malleable state. All in all it's a pretty good set, has a lot of little tools, but be prepared to need to work the clay a bit for younger kids.

The reason I am not giving all 5 stars, is the pens really pulled on my daughters face in order to get any kind of coverage.  It was really difficult to get a sharp line.  However, if you are looking for some fun easy facepaints and you aren't looking to do anything with super fine details, these will be perfect.  They washed off pretty easily too, so that was definitely a plus.

As a multimedia sculptor I am always working with media that I do not want to get on other surfaces and often these medias, such as epoxy resins, can be near impossible to clean once cured.

These PVC mats make a perfect work surface as nothing will stick to them - even epoxy resins. I have found the easiest way to clean epoxy resin off is to just let it set and then peel it off.

These mats are both heat and tear resistant and are made to last. There are 4 large mats in this set allowing for a really large safe work area.

This is an awesome set of mats that will save you time, money and frustration.

So often cost of supplies is what hold people back from exploring their desire to delve into working with different art media. As a multimedia artist I can certainly appreciate the costs that can be involved with different art forms but acrylic painting isn’t one. This is a perfect example of an outstanding set that will provide a quality paint at a reasonable price.

This set comes with 24 large 2 fluid ounce bottles of excellent quality, vibrantly pigmented metallic acrylic paints - and 12 brushes!

Not all paint is created equally and the paint in this set is a perfect viscosity, rich in color and easy to both blend and work with. Many sets come with tubes that are 12ml while these are 2 fluid ounces which is significantly more paint per color. The metallic colors achieve their color effects via the use of mica powders mixed within the paint. The micas work to catch light from different angles causing the paint to catch light and reflect it giving the metallic appearance. This allows for incredible effects that can only be achieved in this way.

Acrylic paint is a great media for anyone wanting to explore painting for the first time. It adheres to all sorts of surfaces, blends easily and dries quickly. Clean up is performed with only soap and water so it’s a perfect activity for kids. While this is a great set for beginners, as someone who has painted for over 30 years I have been thoroughly impressed with the quality of these paints.

If you are using these paints for any project that will be exposed to moisture for any extended period of time you will want to apply a gloss top coat. Avoid using semi gloss or matte as they will interfere with the reflective properties of the mica.

This is an outstanding set of metallic paints that is not only a great deal but also high quality.

This is a great purchase and makes a terrific gift for people of all ages.

As a multimedia artist I use all sorts of supplies in unique ways - I’m always trying to think outside of the box. Long ago I recognized the ability of glitter, sequins and rhinestones to catch light in unusual ways within a 3-dimensional sculpture that adds a a unique characteristic.

Without question not all rhinestones are created equally, especially when you intend for them to hold up for long term use.

Depending on the sculpture I’m working on, I’ve sometimes added large quantities of rhinestones while other times I’ve placed each individual rhinestone with tweezers to achieve the exact effect I seek. When using appropriate glues these will hold up indefinitely. I have also incorporated them into resin sculptures to create amazing depth of design.

I have really enjoyed this set as included is not only a great variety of colors but the shapes and sizes vary which really help in terms of how the catch light differently. When all of the pieces are uniform light is reflected in a uniform pattern giving less visual impact.

This is an awesome set whether you’re interested in using it as a costuming accessory or, like me, as way to enhance artwork. I definitely recommend this awesome set.

So often cost of supplies is what hold people back from exploring their desire to delve into working with different art media. As a multimedia artist I can certainly appreciate the costs that can be involved with different art forms but acrylic painting isn’t one. This is a perfect example of an outstanding set that will provide a quality paint at a reasonable price.

This set comes with 14 large 2 fluid ounce bottles of excellent quality, vibrantly pigmented pearlescent acrylic paint. Not all paint is created equally and the paint in this set is a perfect viscosity, rich in color and easy to both blend and work with. Many sets come with tubes that are 12ml while these are 2 fluid ounces which is significantly more paint per color. The pearlescent colors achieve their color effects via the use of mica powders mixed within the paint. The micas work to catch light from different angles causing the paint to appear differently depending on the angle at which it is viewed. This allows for incredible effects that can only be achieved in this way.

Acrylic paint is a great media for anyone wanting to explore painting for the first time. It adheres to all sorts of surfaces, blends easily and dries quickly. Clean up is performed with only soap and water so it’s a perfect activity for kids. While this is a great set for beginners, as someone who has painted for over 30 years I have been thoroughly impressed with the quality of these paints.

If you are using these paints for any project that will be exposed to moisture for any extended period of time you will want to apply a gloss top coat. Avoid using semi gloss or matte as they will interfere with the reflective properties of the mica.

Arteza is a manufacturer of top quality art products. I have worked with their products for many years consistently finding them produced with the highest quality ingredients and components. This is a brand I have found I am able to depend on whether for my own projects or when recommending products to students I am teaching. If you are looking for consistently top quality art products this is a company I highly recommend turning to.

This is an outstanding set of pearlescent paints that is not only a great deal but also high quality. This is a great purchase and makes a terrific gift for people of all ages.

I am a multimedia artist working in about every medium I can find. Stone, clay, wood, metal, leather, glass, wax, latex, paint, photography, electronics - I am always looking to push both my limits and the limits of the medium.

I absolutely love working with alcohol inks and have found this set outstanding. Alcohol inks are great fun to work with the best part of working with them is the freedom to allow the media to dictate where the art takes you.

I have used these on a multitude of surfaces - I really like using alcohol inks on panes of glass as this allows the inks to run freely, mix and play. If I’m unhappy with an area I’m able to use a blade and remove it. Typically I layer a few panes together creating depth.

I have used these metallic colors as an accent after my other alcohol ink colors have dried.

I have also used these inks to create a gilded effect on multiple items from sculptures to frames.

My best advice for working with any alcohol inks is first, don’t be rigid - allow the medium to flow and have fun. Second, stop when you think it looks good. Less is often more. Let it dry and come back to see what you think later.

In addition to the above stated techniques I have had great success using this set to color epoxy resins. Depending on the quantity of ink added depth of color and opacity will vary from very light to very intense. Always start with less and gradually add more ink.

This is an amazing set that is great to accent other colors or they can be used on a variety of non porous surfaces to create amazing metallic finishes.

This is an outstanding alcohol ink set that you’ll love working with again and again.

I’m a multimedia artist. I work in every media I can find - wood, metal, clay, leather, glass, stone, plastics, paint, inks, photography, resins, mold making and reproductions - I love pushing the limits and finding new ways to utilize media to create.

Among my greatest joys is teaching others what I know and watching their creativity blossom. In that vein I regularly look for starter sets that I’m able to demonstrate with so those who are interested can learn the properties of the media and the basics of how to work with it.

This epoxy resin set has worked out perfectly for my demonstration purposes because I work quickly. I have worked with resins for many years and have a good understanding of working with this media.

I had one of the people who was observing my demonstration purchase this set as they watched me work with it and then rerun to me with their horror story of it setting up halfway through.

Yes - this can happen. As with all mediums there is a learning curve and watching You-Tube videos doesn’t necessarily shorten the curve.

Something to understand is epoxy is unforgiving. Once it begins hardening your time is virtually up. This is a medium that you must work ultra quick - know your plan, have everything in place prior to beginning and plan for things to go wrong with tools available for when they do.

While this listing claims there is enough resin for a river table that is highly doubtful - unless it was tiny. This is better suited for smaller craft projects.

I have repeatedly achieved great results with this set though it is a faster set resin than many others I work with. For many projects that is exactly what I want, if you are wanting to learn that isn’t ideal.

As a multimedia sculptor I often create armatures out of styrofoam. I used to work with a host of saws and knives in this process but working with this hot wire cutter has made my life so much easier.

This is a incredibly versatile and easy to use tool. The interchangeable heads allow for styrofoam to be cut in any pattern and however thick you may need.

While cutting with saws and knives create a mess of loose flying debris this is not a concern when working it this tool as it cleanly slices though every time.

I have found this tool works on multiple materials including pool noodles.

This is an awesome tool that I highly recommend.

I have worked with pouring different resins for around 30 years now. Until relatively recently most people associated epoxy as a glue but it’s awesome that more and more people are experimenting with this media’s incredible potential.

Epoxy Resin can be used in so many different ways to achieve completely different effects. Some epoxy resins are made specifically for coating countertops, while others garage floors. Some are best used to fill silicone molds and the like.

This epoxy is best suited for smaller projects and where you want to project your epoxy off the surface. This is excellent for magnets with images or objects beneath, geodes, to encapsulate objects used as jewelry or as decoration. By mixing resin dyes you can tint the resin while mixing in mica powders the effects become even more complex and interesting.

While nearly all epoxy resins require mixing of the 2 parts - this comes in one container and is activated by UV - which can come from either a UV lamp or the sun.

After making several pieces I intentionally left them in the sun and over my 5 month testing period each of them has remained absolutely crystal clear with no yellowing whatsoever.

This is an excellent introductory set that is easy to work with making it perfect for anyone wanting to explore this amazing medium for the first time.

I love working with Epoxy Resin and testing out new techniques. Effects are literally endless. Explore the internet for inspiration then let your creativity go wild. Fair warning this is addictive.

I love using this set as a way to teach the basics of how to work with this incredible material.

Great product.

I am a multimedia sculptor. I work in about every media and tend to always look for ways to incorporate things that are not expected into my pieces.

This wire has worked perfectly for allowing me to create intricate beaded jewelry of teeth and animal bones for a large wall hanging sculpture and hair ties within another.

I have used this wire on both pieces that are completed and placed inside and out - it has held up incredibly well. One thing to note is this wire is coated so don’t count on a natural patina from this.

This is definitely a high quality product that I will use again.

I am a multimedia artist. I have enjoyed working with mica powders for many years.

Mica powders are created by crushing mica stone that is primarily mined in India. Micas natural color is off white or light gray then oxides are used to color the mica to give it the colorant we see in the end product.

We see micas is use everyday - they are used in automotive paints to create metallic colors, used in makeup and soap but can be, and are, used for far more.

I add a bit of mica prior to my top clear coat when I want to add sparkle or if I’m looking to add another layer of dimension.

The colors in this set are vibrant and catch light wonderfully well. The powder is very fine which makes it easy to mix with any medium you wish without it clumping. As with most chameleon micas the color changing properties in this set depend greatly on the surface to which they are applied, the medium in which they are mixed and the light reflecting.

I’m so often asked about techniques when people have the opportunity to meet me at events. Unlike many artists, I do not believe in keeping trade secrets. I love working with and teaching other artists whenever I have the opportunity. Here are a few quick tips about working with micas.

* First, it’s important to understand micas can pose health dangers due to how fine the particulates are - when working with them, WEAR A MASK. If in, which is incredibly easy to do, they will remain in your lungs indefinitely causing permanent damage to alveoli. Again - WEAR A MASK.
* Micas can be utilized in so many ways - from mixing directly into mediums such as polymer clays or plasters, painting on glass, making acrylic paint, creating makeup or bath bombs, within resin or candle wax - honestly there is very few limits on what micas can be used to add color and sparkle to.
* What makes mica unique to other types of pigment - it will not dilute. Due the mica being stone crushed to a fine powder it does not dilute as other pigments do.

Here are a couple of my tried and tested ways I work with micas regularly

* For acrylic painting - add the approximate amount of mica you want to a small cup (you can add more later if you need to). Make sure it is free of clumps and it helps to spread it over the bottom of the cup by doing a little jiggle. Now add 2 parts acrylic gloss medium and 1 part gloss varnish or water based polyethylene. As with everything I don’t measure, but instead just eyeball it. Once ingredients are combined, mix well paying careful attention to blend any clumps there might be - now add more mica if desired.
* For adding a hint of sparkle - who doesn’t like a flare of the dramatic? In a small spray bottle combine alcohol (for this 70% rubbing alcohol is fine) with a teaspoon of mica. Spray the piece - allow to dry - clear coat.
* As a rub on furniture glaze - add the approximate amount of mica you want to a small cup (you can add more later if you need to). Make sure it is free of clumps and it helps to spread it over the bottom of the cup by doing a little jiggle. Now add clear furniture wax, I’ve been using Dixie Belle Paint Company Best Dang Furniture Wax (10oz, Clear) and love it ( https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07DTJH62P/ref=cm_sw_em_r_mt_dp_D3SHKZFVPYT29NSY49MQ ). Apply as you would any furniture wax.

This is an outstanding chameleon mica set whether you’ve worked with micas for years or this is your first foray. The brushes included in this set are ok though nothing extraordinary.

This is a set I highly recommend.

I am a multimedia sculptor and have worked with mica powders for many years.

These are typically most often used to tint epoxies, makeup and soap but can be, and are, used for far more.

I add a bit of mica prior to my top clear coat when I want to add sparkle or if I’m looking to add another layer of dimension.

The colors in this set are vibrant and catch light wonderfully well - then glow at night! The powder is very fine which makes it easy to mix with any medium you wish without it clumping. As with most unique micas the glow in the dark properties depend greatly on the surface to which they are applied, the medium in which they are mixed (make sure to use ultra clear high gloss medium) and the item absorbing adequate light during the day.

I am a multimedia artist. I paint, sculpt, carve and work in about every medium possible. For years I have saved tiny bottles to mix small amounts of paint within - primarily medicine bottles. The problem with this is I could not accurately see the color of the paint within that I was mixing. This set has been outstanding!

I have really put this set to the test - I have mixed, used and stored hundreds of colors within these perfectly sized containers.

Each of these 60 airtight containers measure 1” in diameter and a bit under 2” tall which is ideal for my mixing of small amounts of paint for specific projects. I’m never sure of exactly how much paint I’ll require so I mix a bit more than I think I’ll need typically leaving myself a bit extra. These containers have allowed me to save this paint for later - and keep it organized.

I have loved this set and highly recommend it!

I am a multimedia sculptor who has been actively creating for over 30 years. I love embellishing pieces with a variety of mixed items. I collect items everywhere I go - antique beads, shells, teeth, stones, pieces of glass, glitter and, as in this set, rhinestones.

Most artists and craftspeople will be familiar with E-6000, it is an amazing adhesive that many of us swear by and purchase by the case. The adhesive in this set is very similar but not exactly the same. The main difference between the two is the ratio of ingredients, so in effect, the adhesives aren’t really that different from each other. Both of these adhesives are solvent based which means that once the solvent has evaporated from the polymer, the adhesive has cured. What this also means is the solvent within them also has the potential to melt softer plastics and should never be used on any type of styrofoam. Both of these adhesives are excellent for adhering no porous items and will assure an excellent bond.

This tools in this set are absolutely perfect for applying rhinestones and I have used them for not only the stones in this set but for many others. Due to my placing rhinestones on many surfaces that are not flat I often require a variety of tools and techniques as I work and I’ve found this set well thought out and very helpful.

The set of stones within this set are excellent. All rhinestones are not the same. Some rhinestones will scratch easily and dull quickly, losing their luster with defeats the purpose of affixing them in the first place. That is not the case with this set. I have love the black stones within my set and turn to them often.

This is an outstanding set I highly recommend.

As most artists will attest working with good products makes a huge difference. This set of metallic paints is among the best I have worked with and I have worked with many.

I have worked with many metallic paints and these have a heavy body though depending on how they are applied opacity can be controlled. This is definitely a benefit rather than a flaw in the paint. Often an semi opaque glaze is perfect for creating an gilded effect over other colors rather than achieving a solid opaque coat.

If a solid coat of color id desired i have found it works best to start with a base coat of a similar color then lay this paint on with shorty heavy strokes.

As you can see in my attached pictures these colors are packed with metallic pigment that really catch the light and stand out.

If completely coated with coats of a clear polyethylene I have found this paint holds up very well outdoors.

I have mixed these paints with a variety of other water based paints making the hues darker, lighter and more vibrant.

This is a set I highly recommend.

Having a quality set of paint brushes makes a huge difference in mastering the skills required to paint with confidence. This set is perfect for those looking to get started. While this could not be considered professional grade it is far better than what you would typically find in beginner sets.

It’s easy for beginners to become frustrated as they are trying to learn when they purchase inferior sets. If you have to battle the tools you are working with your chances of success diminish greatly.

This set of 15 nylon brushes, a pallet knife, 2 sponges and the carrying case is absolutely perfect for all different paints and is bound to impress those of all skill levels.

This is a set I have recommended several times and it has always been appreciated and enjoyed.

I’m a multimedia sculptor. I work in every media I can find wood, metal, clay, leather, glass, stone, plastics, paint, inks, photography. Among my greatest joys is teaching others what I know and watching their creativity blossom. In that vein I regularly look for starter sets that I’m able to recommend so those who are interested can have their own tools when not with me to practice.

This is exactly the type of set I look for to recommend. The price is excellent and the quality is superb for a starter set.

Many starter sets are cheaply made - low quality blades that will break, fall off the handles or quickly dull. The blades on both the whittling knife, curved hook knife, spoon knife, chip carving knife and detail knife are crafted from carbon steel, hold an edge beautifully and are attached to the handles securely.

The handles of each tool are ergonomically shaped making them comfortable to hold and easy to work with - this makes a huge difference for people who are beginning.

This set comes with excellent gloves and a polishing compound, a leather strop and a carrying bag all of which are exceptionally helpful for beginners to have and learn to use as they begin carving.

This is an outstanding set for those who are interested in carving or to give as a gift. I have already recommended it a few times and will continue to do so.

I regularly update my reviews to reflect any changes I may discover and to include helpful tips you may need if you purchase the product. I have used this tote to be able to hold my critical art supplies so that I’m able to easily move from location to location and continue working.

This tote holds far more than I expected - and I have packed it full. The way it folds closed allows it to be carried even if not completely closed.

I have loved using this tote to hold my tools and supplies as I go sit in the sun to work or in front of the TV. I’m able to not only hold my tools and supplies but often tuck the piece I’m working on within the top as I’m moving between locations.

This tote is an outstanding way to keep important tools and supplies organized, together and within easy reach so they can be used and still be mobile.

This has been a great tote that I use almost daily. I have been thoroughly impressed with the smart design and overall quality of this product.

This is definitely a product I recommend.

While I do not do a huge amount of sewing I do have the need to stitch on occasion. Prior to getting this I was always struggling to find everything I needed but this comes with all the critical tools needed for any simple stitching task.

I love that the scissors are not only very sharp but have large holes for your fingers allowing for smooth operation. The shears are super sharp allowing for thread to be cut at an angle for easy threading without the thread fraying. Thankfully the needles have generous sized eye holes making threading much easier than ones with tiny eye holes - and it comes with 2 threaders that I’ve used with the smaller needles. The seam ripper works exactly as it should.

This is an all around excellent set. I have used it about a dozen times since receiving it and only wish it came with a case to hold everything as my greatest difficulty is keeping it all together when I need it - this has more to do with other family members “borrowing” and not returning to the proper location.

A case would definitely make this a 5 star set. Without the case it is a solid 4 and one I would recommend.

I am a multimedia artist working in about every medium I can find. Stone, clay, wood, metal, leather, glass, wax, latex, paint, photography, electronics - I am always looking to push both my limits and the limits of the medium.

I didn’t get these crayons to color with although I’m sure they will work wonderfully for that purpose. I got this set with the intention of melting them. While this may seem strange as you initially read it, I have used crayons in this way for many years.

I purchased high quality LED honeycomb candles but I wanted them to appear as though they had dripped a rainbow of colors. First I melted crayons in the base of each cup and placed the LED candles wrapped in foil. Once the wax hardened I was able to remove the candle leaving the properly sized cup for each candle. Then I carefully held the crayons with hemostats and using a long handled lighter I melted them allowing the wax to drip and flow.

I also used these crayons glued to a canvas and melted with a heat gun. Both projects came out exactly as I intended.

The colors are vibrant and the pigments deep and rich. This is a high quality set of crayons regardless of how you intend to use them.

I definitely recommend AmazonBasics brand in general - and this set of crayons is no different. Top quality at an excellent price!

I developed a process of sculpting leather 23 years ago. Over the years I have evolved my processes to continue to push the limits of both the media and my skills to always produce artwork that is ever evolving.

The leather I work with is furniture grade aniline dyed and is therefore far thinner than the typical leather user in most leather projects that these tools are used with. As I started working with this set I wasn’t sure how much I would end up using them but over the last three months of having them I’ve found them incredibly useful.

This set comes with: Instructions, 9 Curved needles, 9 large-eye leather stitching needles, 9 large-eye blunt needles, 5 nail files, leather pony, 2 leather finger cots, scissors, and a thimble. All of the components are decent quality and will hold up adequately to regular use.

What I create is vastly different from what this set is typically used for but I’ve found it very useful. All of my sculptures hang on the wall. While my central sculpted area is hard I always want the surrounding leather soft and pliable as to show the contrast of the areas and to create multiple effects. I have used the pony, needles and heavy thread to sew hidden stitches to hold this soft leather on the edges and it has worked flawlessly.

As with any tool set I had to play with it a bit to figure out how I could make it work for my needs but this wasn’t a big deal. In the future I may create sets of new arms for the pony where it can grip smaller bunches of leather allowing me to place stitches in even more harder to reach areas.

This is an outstanding set that I’ve loved working with. Leather is a phenomenal media that can be pushed to limits most can’t imagine.

This is an outstanding set at a great price.

So often cost of supplies is what hold people back from exploring their desire to delve into working with different art media. As a multimedia artist I can certainly appreciate the costs that can be involved with different art forms but acrylic painting isn’t one. This is a perfect example of an outstanding set that will provide nearly everything needed to get started.

This set comes with 60 large 22ml tubes of excellent quality, vibrantly pigmented acrylic paint. Not all paint is created equally and the paint in this set is a perfect viscosity, rich in color and easy to both blend and work with. Many sets come with tubes that are 12ml while these are 22ml which is significantly more paint per tube.

In addition to the outstanding paint set this comes with a linen-textured pad of acrylic paper. This set will provide you everything you need except the brushes.

Acrylic paint is a great media for anyone wanting to explore painting for the first time. It adheres to all sorts of surfaces, blends easily and dries quickly. Clean up is performed with only soap and water so it’s a perfect activity for kids. While this is a great set for beginners, as someone who has painted for over 30 years I have been thoroughly impressed with the quality of these paints.

This is an outstanding set that is not only a great deal but also high quality. This makes a terrific gift for people of all ages.

I am a multimedia artist working in about every medium I can find. Stone, clay, wood, metal, leather, glass, wax, latex, paint, photography, electronics - I am always looking to push both my limits and the limits of the medium.

I have worked with a variety of airbrushes for many years but this is my first completely portable airbrush. I questioned if it could be powerful enough to propel the paint adequately, which I can confirm that it is. Obviously this will not be able to work continuously like a standard airbrush system but so often I just need to do a bit of touch up work and this has become my go to tool.

Here are a few things to know about this set:

1. The set comes with Airbrush Gun, Mini Air Compressor, USB Charging Cable, User Manual, Cleaning Brush, Eye Dropper, Removable 20ML Plastic Fluid Cup - there is no plug in charger included. Typically I don’t see this as an issue but because there are so many warnings to not use a charger less than 1A or more than 2A - I believe they should include the charger that is best suited to this product. If that adds a bit to the expense so be it - that is better than customers burning out the airbrush due to charging with an inappropriate charger.
2. That brings me to: It is not recommended to use the airbrush kit while charging, nor is it recommended to use a charger less than 1A or more than 2A. This question is asked repeatedly on the listing for good reason, as an artist, I hate when I’m in a rhythm and working and have to stop because a battery dies. That will happen. This is great for small, quick projects but not something I would recommend for large projects as stopping to recharge inevitability leads to drying lines.
3. The MAXIMUM PSI this airbrush will push is 20 PSI (as a comparison my normal airbrush compressor runs at 40 PSI but can push up to 60 PSI). This is adequate for paints specifically made for airbrushing but if you plan on spraying acrylic paint, or the like, you will need really thin it down to get a spray you’ll be pleased with. While the manufacturer recommends 1:2, I’ve found 1:2.5 or ever 1:3 is required depending on the paint viscosity. This isn’t a big deal but if you are new to airbrushing this can lead to frustration and poor results. If you are seeing drips of paint come out - your paint is too thick for the brush to aerosolize it.
4. As with every airbrush it is absolutely critical that you thoroughly clean every part of the airbrush when you are finished. Depending on the medium you are using to spray, thoroughly clean with water, alcohol or mineral spirits. The biggest problem people have with airbrushing comes from not adequately cleaning the airbrush. If any paint dries within the airbrush it will not operate properly and this one, because of the reduced PSI, is even more susceptible to clogging and thus is even more critical to keep clean.
5. The compressor unit uses a universal 1/8 male thread interface that fits most airbrush guns. This will allows you to connect other brands of airbrushes with ease. I have done this on multiple occasions when I’ve been airbrushing at my standard station and need to be able to get to an area that I can not easily reach, I just disconnect from my standard air compressor and use this one.
6. Realistically you can expect around 40-50 min of spray time from a full charge. While the manufacturer recommends letting the gun rest after using for 30 min straight this just isn’t the type of device I’d use like that. It could be done but it will put a lot of strain on it and you’ll be guaranteed to be interrupted in the midst of painting.

This is an outstanding set that I’ve thoroughly enjoyed using. It can’t replace my more powerful 1/5hp compressor driven standard airbrush but I didn’t intend for it to do that.

As with any art medium learning the proper mixing ratios is critical and with this airbrush that will take a bit or trial and error. That being said, once I recognized the optimum viscosity for this brush I have been amazed at what it can do.

The only issue that keeps me from giving this a 5 star ranking is the manufacturers choice to not include the charger. Prior to plugging this in make sure your charging plug is 1.5A. There are many out there but it’s important to check as to not unintentionally burn out the compressor by charging with too many Amps.

I have really enjoyed using this set and definitely recommend it.

This is an outstanding set of alcohol based markers.

This set comes complete with a wide range of colors allowing for a full spectrum of creative options. The pigments are rich and vibrant.

I have found this to be an excellent set of markers for a wide variety of projects - from drawing, sketching and coloring to even touching up the color on completed sculptures. The ink flows well, dries quickly and holds fast.

The fine pointed tip allows for fine detail and getting into tight spots while the broad head is great for laying down color over a large area.

The caps fit tightly protecting the ink from drying out when not in use.

The marker fit well in the hand and don’t tend to get ink on my fingers as I work.

The set comes in an excellent hard box case which helps keeps them all together and in order so I can always locate the color I’m looking for.

This is an outstanding set that I’ve thoroughly enjoyed using.

I am a multimedia artist working in about every medium I can find. Stone, clay, wood, metal, leather, glass, wax, latex, paint, photography, electronics - I am always looking to push both my limits and the limits of the medium.

I absolutely love working with alcohol inks and Ranger Alcohol Inks is my go to brand in this media. Alcohol inks are great fun to work with the best part of working with them is the freedom to allow the media to dictate where the art takes you.

I have used these on a multitude of surfaces - I really like using alcohol inks on panes of glass this allows the inks to run freely, mix and play. If I’m unhappy with an area I’m able to use a blade and remove it. Typically I layer a few panes together creating depth.

I have used these jet black inks with my other metallic inks creating really incredible patterns that stand out due to the contrasts.

I have also used these inks to create an antiqued effect on multiple items from sculptures to frames.

My best advice for working with any alcohol inks is first, don’t be rigid - allow the medium to flow and have fun. Second, stop when you think it looks good. Less is often more. Let it dry and come back to see what you think later.

This is an amazing set that is a great base when accenting with other colors or they can be used on a variety of non porous surfaces to create amazing finishes.

Ranger is really the best there is and a joy to work with.

I was a bit surprised when reading the reviews that someone was unhappy with the size of this cleaner - I absolutely love it! This is not for cleaning brushes you would use to paint walls, this is really geared towards artist brushes that are used with oil based paints.

For those of us who paint in oils, we understand how expensive high quality oil brushes are and we take great care of our brushes. From buying high quality pure turpentine to washing immediately, using brush conditioners and drying property.

I have red sable hair brushes I have been painting with for over 30 years that perform as well today as the day they were purchased. To say not all brushes and art equipment are created equally is an understatement.

This brush cleaner is really PERFECT. It holds the perfect amount of turpentine without having to overfill it. The turpentine I use, and have found works best for my oil brushes is Mountain Pine 100% Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine found here ( https://www.amazon.com/dp/B083G3GKZV/ref=cm_sw_em_r_mt_dp_fS3zFbJ2588JS ). While it costs far more than what you’ll find in home improvement stores, having your brushes last forever is well worth the cost.

Considering the cost of high quality turpentine, I love that this container holds just the right amount to clean my brushes and has an excellent seal which prevents any evaporation.

The cleaning screen is well designed allowing for working the bristles through the turpentine allowing for complete penetration of the turpentine and removal of all oil paint residue. I can do this quickly and without looking as I’m going from one color to the next.

This is an all around amazing product, especially if used as it’s intended. I could not be more pleased with this item.

Over the years I’ve grown to really believe in the Gorilla line of adhesive products. I have been consistently impressed with their products and this glue gun set is no different.

I am a multimedia artist. I work in every media I can find - wood, metal, clay, leather, glass, stone, plastics, paint, inks, photography, resins, mold making and reproductions - I love pushing the limits and finding new ways to utilize media to create.

Using different adhesive products is a key part of my art and over the years I have mastered the use a wide variety of them. They key is understanding which type of glue is best suited to the application based on the materials you are adhering and then how to best use the glue to achieve the best bond.

Hot glue is particularly good for any item that may have a bit of movement as the movement will not break the bond. In addition, because hot glue is thick it can be used to fill in gaps and create a bond even when the two items are not flush with each other. I tend to use hot glue when adhering larger ornamentation on to a sculpture as it allows me to work quickly. While working I use a small cookie sheet to rest my hot glue on so if any hot glue leaks it will land on it and not be an issue.

In comparison with other glue guns this Gorilla Glue gun is light weight and easy to maneuver. In my tests it heated up to melting temp in 190 seconds. The key to not wasting the glue product is to only feed in the amount of glue that you will need - typically I lay everything out that I will be gluing prior to beginning and then plug in my glue gun. Once it is at temperature I feed my first stick in, if at the proper temperature the glue will quickly melt and emerge the metal nozzle (obviously this will be the hottest part).

The gun has both a high and low setting which is a nice feature which few glue guns offer. On the low setting less glue will melt, it will be thicker and harden more quickly - I rarely use this setting. This setting is particularly useful when working with items that would easily melt such as styrofoam. On high (the temperature setting of most glue guns) the glue will emerge HOT, this will allow you to squeeze the trigger to release the amount of glue you need then by using the hot tip of the gun you can spread the glue then adhere. I do this process in a quick, fluid movement and then apply my adornment to the sculpture.

The adhesive qualities of this Gorilla hot glue is where this product really sticks the five star rating. To put it simply GORILLA KNOWS GLUE. I have worked with hot glue products for over 30 years and this glue has shown to be the best in terms of solid, strong adhesion, ease to work with, and dependability. I always use the 4” sticks as it’s best as to not have wasted glue leak out the tip.

This is an outstanding set that I highly recommend.

As a multimedia artist I use all sorts of supplies in unique ways - I’m always trying to think outside of the box. Long ago I recognized the ability of glitter, sequins and rhinestones to catch light in unusual ways within a 3-dimensional sculpture that adds a a unique characteristic.

Without question not all rhinestones are created equally, especially when you intend for them to hold up for long term use.

Depending on the sculpture I’m working on, I’ve sometimes added large quantities of rhinestones while other times I’ve placed each individual rhinestone with tweezers to achieve the exact effect I seek. When using appropriate glues these will hold up indefinitely. I have also incorporated them into resin sculptures to create amazing depth of design.

I have really enjoyed this set as included is not only a great variety of colors but the shapes and sizes vary which really help in terms of how the catch light differently. When all of the pieces are uniform light is reflected in a uniform pattern giving less visual impact.

This is an awesome set whether you’re interested in using it as a costuming accessory or, like me, as way to enhance artwork. I definitely recommend this awesome set.

I’m a multimedia artist. I work in every media I can find - wood, metal, clay, leather, glass, stone, plastics, paint, inks, photography, resins, mold making and reproductions - I love pushing the limits and finding new ways to utilize media to create.

Among my greatest joys is teaching others what I know and watching their creativity blossom. In that vein I regularly look for starter sets that I’m able to demonstrate with so those who are interested can learn the properties of the media and the basics of how to work with it.

This epoxy resin set has worked out perfectly for my demonstration purposes. I have worked with resins for many years and have a good understanding of working with this media.

This is an excellent set with everything you’ll need to do several small projects - such as refrigerator magnets. This epoxy allows for plenty of working time with setting up after approximately 35 minutes.

As with any epoxy resin it is critical to mix the epoxy and resin thoroughly with the wooden stick prior to pouring. The mica powders are vibrant and the resin hardens to a crystal clear.

This is an excellent starter set that will allow you to experience the fun of working with epoxy resin without the commitment of a large amount of supplies. This is a great value that I definitely recommend.

Anyone who has attempted to make resin covered cups knows the key to having them come out with a smooth, flawless finish is a dependable cup turner and this is exactly that.

This LFSUM Cup Turner does precisely what you’ll need it to do. It turns at exactly the proper speed so you won’t end up with areas that are thicker than others, there will be no drips or waves caused by uneven drying.

A proper cup turner is truly a critical tool if you want to produce a quality resin covered cups and this one by LFSUM comes with everything you’ll need, works well and is made to hold up to extended use.

This is an all around excellent product.

As a multimedia artist I end up doing work that is both very large and very small.

I love including fine detail regardless of the overall size of the piece of art. Leaving something that is a bit of a surprise for people who care enough to discover if they look hard enough.

Working on a very small scale requires a steady hand and specific brushes - and this is an excellent set of brushes for exactly that task.

This set of 15 brushes are all ergonomically shaped and perfectly balanced making it possible to do fine detail without having to grip the brush too tightly. Transfer of paint to your media is smooth and effortless.

I have used these brushes with multiple types of paint and glazes - they both perform well while using and clean up without issue.

This is an excellent set whether you are just starting out or if you’ve been paining for years. I love my set of brushes.

As a multimedia artist I have used and sworn by Fiskars products for years. Simply put they produce the best scissors anywhere.

Fiskars is a Finnish company that has been making scissors since 1649 - yes, 1649 that is not a typo. Their products are made to last and people who use their products tend to swear by them. While their scissors cost more than others it is because they are worth every penny.

Fiskars is constantly innovating and improving their product lines - and they have many.

Fiskars stands behind their products and offers a lifetime guarantee. This is something rarely seen in any tool but they can do it because their products are so well made. Over the many years of using their products I have only had one product that eventually had an issue and they replaced it with no issue whatsoever.

These garden shears are incredible. While they are marketed as for the garden they are exceptional for all around the house and workshop. These are a heavy duty, cut through tough materials shears.

Besides being shears these easily come apart to be used as a blade for slicing, they have a built it bottle opener, awl tip, wire and twine cutter. The safety sheath even has a tape cutter and ceramic scissor blade sharpener.

These are an absolutely incredible set of shears that you will use all of the time around the house. Once you own them for a while you will understand exactly how Fiskars has remained in business as long as they have.

As a multimedia artist I’ve used Macrame Cord over the years for many different projects.

It works great for weaving or as an attached accessory onto a sculpture. I’ve separated the strands and run it through holes in clay sculpture as hair, I’ve done the same dyed it and singed it as hairs on an insect. Coating it with a little wax keeps it from igniting but will allow it to darken with heat...

I’ve often simply separated the cord and used it to tie price tags to items in galleries.

Each of the 3 strands is made up of approximately 60 treads each which can be divided out into whatever sized bunches you need.

As a single cord, with all 3 strands woven together, it is incredibly strong.

This is an excellent product that is so versatile for crafting, gardening or to have just around the house.

This set comes with a book and a mini disc of mp4 video tutorials showing how to tie basic macrame knots.

I would definitely recommend this product.

These canvases are great, I can’t believe you get 32 for the price!  My kids love to paint on canvases for gifts and this set gives them enough for lots of projects. We will definitely be ordering these again!

This is a great set for kids or adults alike. I am very happy with the clay itself, however we received a pink box instead of a blue or clear one as shown, so that was a bit of a disappointment. This cotton macrame cord is really nice. I like this 5 in by 5 in watercolor sketch book because it is perfect to jot down ideas for larger pieces, a small area to practice different paint strokes and great to take traveling. The paper is high quality watercolor, cold pressed paper. I really don’t dislike anything about it. It is perfect for my current needs.
C. Stephan

I really like the variety of colors and the translucent appearance. The metallic sheen adds a pop to one’s work. The carrying case is convenient for travel. I like the amount of the 12 colors as well. The pan is easy to use a # 8 watercolor brush with. Overall, no dislikes.
C Stephan

I liked the number of wells for the various colors. The porcelain water color palette is heavy and durable. I would have liked a cardboard template for the center so that one can paint samples of the colors used. Please see the picture as I made one for my use. Unless one uses all the colors, it would be challenging to clean the entire palette. It is easy to clean each slot with a wet cloth.
C. Stephan

This pen does not require shaking to get the ink to flow, it is easy to hold and the white shows up on painted surfaces such as in art journals. I really like the effect.
C Stephan

I liked the size of the tip, and found that when shaking the pen to get the gold/silver onto the tip, that it came down readily. The pens are easy to hold and also easy to travel with. Great for making marks in journals or for just writing.
C Stephan

I have been experimenting with how to use the metallic colors in my art journal. I find that they are most effective for me as final touches rather than using them to start a page. I have experimented with them as paint for stamping, and mark making. In the above picture, I used the metallic paints with various stamps to make some of the foliage. I also use it on tissue paper to make markers for use as collage pieces. For my taste, the metallic paints are not as attractive as background on a page.
Christine

I liked the variety of colors and the fine tip point. It took a long time to shake the acrylic ink onto the point (about a minute and a half) and several of the pens shaker part was stuck. Also I had to shake the pen vigorously throughout this art project to get the color onto the paper. I used water color paper and the paper got stuck on the point.
C Stephan

I really like the variety of hues and colors this set of premium colors. It is organized by color categories which make it easy to choose, and mix and match. I rated this a 4 because I found that when mixing with water the gouach did not mix easily but rather stayed a bit lumpy. But when applied to watercolor paper in both pictures, they turned out to my satisfaction. The first picture is just the gouache on a card, and the second picture is mixed media for an art journal. I layered the gouache using different colors and liked the effect. I plan to use this set on a lot of my pieces.
Christine

I really liked the zipper carrying case with places for all of the brushes, palette knife, sponge and plastic paint tray. I compared the brushes in the set with sable brushes and the outcome was comparable. This is a great carrying case for taking to an art class, or traveling....
Christine

The sable brushes come in a variety of sizes.... they are all round and hold a lot of paint when wet. I have tried them using water color, acrylic inks, and gouache and though it takes a bit of getting use to, they are great for detail work and shading.. They are easy to hold and the metal is double crimped onto the brush handle.
Chris

I did like the colors provided but I found the brush pens difficult to use. I think the fineliner is too fine for using in art journals or other writing types of art projects. I liked the brush side of the pens for outlining but I tried to blend them and they did not blend well .
Christine

There is an interesting array of tools to create dots with the mandala stencils BUT there are no directions for use of the rods, stylus, paint brushes (that is easy) or modeling tools. I had intended to have my 9 year old Grandaughter try them out and review them but she became overwhelmed with what to do.... I think the kit would really benefit from an instruction book to get a child started and then let her/his imagination go wild.
Christine

I am using these pens for art journaling and making cards using various mediums.  I like the grip of the pens, and the variety of tip sizes.  I really like the fact they are waterproof and smudge proof when dry.  I have been working with them in my art journaling course and find them perfect for doing a variety of art projects.

The daisy shape ceramic paint palette is perfect for watercolor or inks of different colors.  The shape of the ceramic paint palette is perfect for economy of space,  see of placing watercolors or inks in the wells.  Rather than thin  plastic trays, the ceramic material is solid, durable and easy to clean.  The kits comes with two paint palette daisy shapes.  I find it helpful to have helpful warm colors in one and cool colors in the other or different hues in each tray.

The arrtx glitter pens are attractively packaged and the colors are easily seen.  I liked the fine point pens better than the brush point.  The brush points, for my use, were too soft and not as manageable for my art journaling projects.  The colors were muted and some of the pens outer tops which indicates the color, did not match the actual color., ex. The pink.  My preference is to have pens that are brighter tones but that is totally a matter of taste.  I liked the glitter quality of the pens on paper.

Edit: If I want a perfectly flat stick I run them through a ceramic flat iron for hair the double pinch kind. I don’t toss out the ones with imperfections I iron them flat. Not bad at all not perfect, but not bad for the price paid. I’ve used other perfectly shaped craft sticks before but they didn’t look like natural wood and had no wood grain. I don’t know how these other sticks where made so perfectly but they definitely don’t look like or act like actual wood although they where supposed to be.

These however look like actual wood and have grain. And as natural wood is they have some imperfections as to be expected from actual wood such as warps or other issues.
So many of these sticks will have these traits.

But there are ways to shape and sand them, my project involves that it shouldn’t look like it was made from popsicle sticks but from normal wood so when it’s done it does not look like it was made from crafting sticks at all I cut, sand, glue and and carve just like any other wooden project.

So for me they are very practical. I don’t want that elementary school DIY look, stereotypical oh made from popsicle sticks. No for me it must cast doubt that the finished product “used to be popsicle sticks” but not longer resembles them, “made from popsicle sticks no way!” Vs “Oh popsicle sticks” not that I’m against that, but that is not my goal.

Lots of color options, some neons and sparkle ones too that look similar to premo colors. I'm fairly new to polymer clay and wanted a kit with a variety of colors at an affordable price to try to make some jewelry. You get about 1 oz of each color. Comes with a decent plastic case, some jewelry findings, and a few tools. The little booklet has a couple of project ideas and the tiny fruit chips are really cute might use those for resin jewelry.

Made a couple of canes so far and it is easy to work, Soft for cutting so needs to be chilled to not warp, but that might be true of most all polymer clay. Overall I'm happy with the item and feel it is comparable to the brands I'm familiar with from the craft store.

Overall for the cost, this is decent.

Pros :
- Low cost
- Large array of colors
- Bakes as instructed

Cons :
- Small amount of each color. If you need a large amount of one color for a piece, you may run into issues.
- Colors are not as pictured. I got a decent array for my project (colorful rainbow christmas ornaments), but out of sheer luck, seeing that others have received.
- Hardly a con as all polymer clays are this way, but they need to be kneaded and softened a bit by an adult for little kids.

About ten years ago I splurged on the exact same set. It was my favorite watercolor, the best I ever owned and was a huge expense. Smooth bright colors. Never regretted it.
Sadly I lost it while moving some months ago. Just treated myself to a new one.
My previous set would have lasted me another ten years though! Most pans were decently full, and you can replace and customize as they run out.
I expect to never buy watercolors again in my life time :)

The one thing I will say though is this :
Only buy if you are also going to invest in quality 100% cotton watercolor paper and sable brushes. The quality of the pigment will be wasted on any lesser quality paper or applied with other brushes.
I love this with Arches cold press and Raphael red sable/martre rouge brushes. The brush the set comes with is good (nice fine point), but you will want an array of sizes and shapes.
Irregularities in the thread (thicker in places with little balls of fiber embedded in thread) made it very difficult to see through creoe de chine silk. Disappointed.
I bought this set because of the glitter gel and when i opened the box it was missing. Unimpressed. Formula looks similar to other cheap facepaints. Organic certification not present on packaging.

Edited, After finishing the picture, it looks like crap.. and I say this as someone who takes their time and lays straight and does a slow steady job. You cant even see the top half of butterfly. This Diamond art has a lot of good vs the bad. Unlike most I've worked this one ranks top 10 for polish finished diamonds, diamonds equal sizes and shapes, size of canvas vs cost, actually finished picture look (with straight diamonds at the end) and ease of reading the symbols on canvas.
I had alot of fun with this one and as my 2nd ever square diamond kit, the picture looks great. Just make sure you use good diamonds vs the bad ones.
All the diamond colors were polished and had a shine, the symbols are straight foward, no mix ups or odd styles. One of the bigger picture I've seen for a great price. At first I didnt like how they came in zip jewelry bags, everyone before this one had factory sealed seperate bags. Out of 25 or more pics I've done this is the first but after I'm done with my art I put extra diamonds in jewelry bags anyways for storage for layer use. (Incase I want to change the color of a canvas) so it worked out for me after I put a few thought into it. I would now rather have them shipped in bags vs the machine sealed packs.

Some of the diamonds are not finished polished. Some of the diamonds are smaller or bigger to point of noticeably as your working, not so much as a finished product. Unlike others there is not a lot of these misfits in this set. But if you use the off diamonds it has the potential of messing up your straight lines and perfect placement.

For those you you who give bad review based on finished look. Make sure your diamonds are straight. Because if they are just slopped on the canvas your pic isnt going to look like anything but colors. Just like your tv would if the dots weren't in a straight line. So make sure your bad reviews reflect the quality of diamonds and not your lack of artistic abilities.

This glue was tried, tested and proven today.
I used on different applications today and used in few different ways, including rope, wood ,pvc pipe, filling gaps and of course Fingers....
This is some of the best glue I've ever used, that's including tube glue. (Construction adhesives). I am using a good industrial glue gun bought on Amazon. It's the yellow one (which is a great product as well)

The glue sticks are bendable with out breakage and they are softer to the touch. I was using 100w gun setting today. The glue came out hot and ready to go. It filled holes with ease it held like a rock, heres an example, I was making my cat a house with scratch tower on top. I glued rope in strips vertically placed. I would glue about 1" of the top let set for a few mins, I was able to pull the rope tight with out it coming loose. I've got strength and have hella grip and I had ZERO issues. I'm really amazed how well this glue held. It dries pretty quick I think anyways, so be sure you are ready you have 30-60sec at most to place before it starts to setup. It only takes a few mins before your able to move on and let glue finish drying.
It also filled 1/8 gap by placing and allowing it to flow right. It stayed in the gap and filled it exactly like I needed it to. When doing this I was looking down at the gap so gravity should've made the glue just pour onto the inside of my box and it didnt, it stayed where I needed it to and did the job.
Using it on wood.... better than anything you can buy at lowes in a tube. It dries quick and holds like a mofo, I'm impressed to the point I'm glad I bought a big box and for sure plan on buying a couple of more boxes for safe keeping. It also held the pvc pipe I used for the scratch post, worked just as good as the pipe glue. Now I dont know how long this glue will last or if it will crack over a short time. Maybe in cold weather it will disintegrate idk yet only time will tell. BUT,
From here on out I will use this glue for EVERYTHING. I swear it is the best I've seen and a 30 yr life in residental construction that says a lot.. I will update as soon as I know how long the glued pieces will last. I'm building cabinets and some furniture and I will figure out the longevity of glue once applied.

Do make a mistake and buy that white crap.. go gold and win 1st place with everything u use it on. So so so satisfied right now.

Update, April 3rd 2021,
I put this gun to the test today. I used 20 or so wood sticks in it and I'm totally amazed. This is by far the best glue gun I've ever used. And my mother is a very crafty person, so I've had alot of glue guns in my hands through the years. I'm wrong about the trigger, it doesn't need to be hard, there is such a light pull and not alot of tension on the trigger.. I dont think it will cause problems in future. Also changing the sticks, you really dont need to push the new one into the old stick to get it to take. Just slide it in and pull trigger it is such a smooth transition you never know the old is gone and new has replaced. The gun on 100w setting makes the glue bubbly hot. I mean it is perfect for my wood working sticks and my applications. The whole gun in light in your hand and didnt cause my hands or fingers to hurt at all. The squeezing of trigger was so smooth and easy. I'm really impressed with this gun. I'm able to use it hours straight with zero issues. It didnt take but 6-7 mins for glue to be hot enough to work with, about 10 or so mins to get max temp on melted glue. The tip didnt seem to get hot either and when the glue got on the tip it seems to melt away. I had no build up of glue on the tip of gun all day. After using for hours the two worse things I can say about this gun is dont get mad and throw it. It could break and then you wasting a great tool and the metal holder thingy by the tip isnt all that great. It wants to lock in place but the slightest touch forward it will more further and touch the tip. This gets in your way all the time and after 100 times of moving it by hand I stopped using it and laid gun on its side from there on out. This needs to be addressed in future designs. If it wasnt for that 1 fault this gun would be 100% perfection right now its 96% perfect. This is one of the best ideas I've had in tool purchase in a long time. I have not used the glue sticks that come with gun. I bought my own wood glue sticks in bulk and the gun and wood sticks work in perfect harmony together. I'm just so impressed..
As always if any issues arise I will update asap.

The Trigger: it seems like it could cause or have issues with breaking or twisting if you over grip. It isnt stable enough for Industrial use in my opinion. Could have issues.

The Unit: its thick and seems to be well made. It is a bigger unit and has a metal stand to use between glues. Yellow in color and looks nice overall.

The Switch: for 60v_100v is a quality switch dont seem like there should be any issues occur.

The Cord: its is a little thicker than normal power cords. Where it connects to unit is longer than most and thicker more stretch for a better non breakage.

The Tips: it has the normal tip on unit but comes with a 3" skinny tip for a more controlled flow of glue. They are made with a thick bronze material and seems to be very sturdy it also has a small tool for removing and tighting the heads of the 2 different nozzles.

The metal holder (keep tip off surface):
After using today (updated) it doesnt stay in place. It will move further out and then the stand wont hold up gun. It also goes further towards the tip and this gets in the way of spreading your hot glue. I find myself leaving it closed and laying gun on its side. This is a flaw that keeps gun from being a perfect tool. But is not by any means or should not be a deal breaker...

Included Mat: this was a suprise being included, (maybe I didnt see where it came with the set idk) it's a good size mat and will be put to use on smaller projects.

The Case: is ok as expected for the overall price. It could break/crack easily with being rough. Closing the case if everything isnt in a good position inside could be a pain to close and snap and it's possible to where closing the case may try n twist on you at the handle and clips. Once clipped it will hold really well the clips are impressive compared to box itself.

Extras: It also comes with white heat gloves and finger tip protectors.
I havent used this unit yet I'm waiting on my wood glue stick to arrive. Will update if need be and report any problems will gluing that may occure.

I like these 5 diamond sets. This company has a better chart with the symbols vs numbers. The diamonds themselves are ok, by this is mean most 70% are same size n slickness with not a lot of trash. Then the other 30% is either dull (28%) or smaller size or have left over slag still attacked to diamond (2%)
I'm 75% complete with my picture so far it looks good, but I have straighten all diamonds yet so final tally I will update with pic.

HINTS FOR GREAT PIC (if you have a good brand) I have done over 20 diamond art pictures, all brands, sizes and types. Lots of experience went into to these tips..

#1. Take your time and place diamonds one at a time, straight in the center of space, and keep grouping straight as possible. ( This will drastically improve the final quality and ability to see what the actual final picture is when your done.) #2. Try and get perfect placement the 1st time it touches, the glue will hold that 1st stick position and this makes it harder to straighten diamond If its missed placed. #3. Using the muti tool will create a really bad final product and you wont be happy.
#4 after you have completed the picture take a straight edge and move along every row right to left and top to bottom and straighten out the entire rows at one time. The straighter the diamonds the better final result.
(It's no different than using an img machine. If the dots are scattered then your pic wont look right. If the machine lays perfect straight dots tour final pic will be perfect.)
#5. Final you get what you pay for. If the art is 2$ there is a reason. The diamonds will be trash, the numbers hard to see, the puck will end fuzzy no matter how straight the diamonds are placed.

I hope this helps someone.

I really like that these squares are packaged in a group so I can just open up one at a time. The colors were super vibrant. We used these for a kids craft and it turned out great.

These are the perfect size for scooping up those little pieces of glitter or mica powder you need for your resin projects. Everything in this kit is so useful and a great price! Makes my resin projects less frustrating to work on!

Great dominos molds. I love that this set includes little metal gears and glitter to be creative with. You can reuse this silicon mold over and over in order to create these for friends or to sell online. The mold is high quality and creates smooth casts.

This is an easy to use resin that produces good results. It does have some issues with bubbles once cured. This would be a better resin to use with a pressure pot or a vacuum degassing chamber. Bubbles form much easier with quicker curing resin. Here is a tip to reduce bubbles: stir slowly to not introduce a lot of air when mixing part A and part B. This resin is great for smaller projects.

This is a great set for your kids. My four year old daughters haave tons of fun cutting paper with the scrapbooking scissors that cut cute shapes. They also love the whales that punch hearts and music note shapes out of the paper. I really like that this set comes with shimmery paper, it's really fun for this age group! The only thing I wish was that the punches actually punched larger shapes out of the paper, I think it's a bit too small for younger kids (it's confetti sized shapes). If the shapes were larger we could glue them onto other papers and make cards or something. Overall, this set is definitely worth the money and high quality.

This is a HIGH QUALITY notebook, the paper is smooth which allows me to use everything from charcoal to pencil while keeping detail. The paper is thick and I love that it is sepia tone, makes it really nice to be able to sketch the highlights and shadows and not fill in midtones. You won't be disappointed with the quality of this notebook.

These are thick high quality molds with lots of detail! They are very easy to pour and demold. I have a few molds from funshowcase and they always work out great. I use these for resin to decorate boxes for my kids.

All of these pigments are pearlescent colors, no flat. They are all gorgeous and high quality. The powder is extremely small and smooth. I would feel great using this for airbrushing and for resin applications. This is a great price for the quality and amount of powder you get! I included a picture with my hand so you can see the size of the bags. They mix well with resin and I'm very happy!

+ Comes with a pair of gloves
+ HUGE variety of colors
+ Same size as the pigments I order off Etsy, much better price
+ a little bit goes a long way
+ Best for smaller resin projects, would not be enough color to make a resin table, great for jewelry or Orgone pyramids and gems.
