I normally buy my ribbon for Costco and can only afford one ribbon there a year since It's around the same price as this whole pack! This is a much better deal and I would say it's the same quality as the ribbon I normally buy at Costco.

This is so much fun for the kids. Lots of different activities to do. We drew some ice cream cones with numbers on them and had the kids use the pom poms to practice counting. Then we made some butterflies with the pipe cleaners and eyes. Lots of materials in this bag and you're really only limited by your creativity!

This cardstock is ultra smooth, works great in my inkjet printer and my laser printer. Because the paper is so smooth it works great for using with a laminator to foil. I tested this paper with my most finicky foil at the highest temperature and ran it through 4 times. Even with that, none of the foil stuck to the paper, only to the design which is a testament to how smooth this cardstock is. I only wish they sold a black cardstock in this finish!

This polymer clay set is great for kids. There are so many colors included and a cute carrying case which makes clean up easy. The clay does feel different than the Fimo brand I normally buy, it’s a lot easier to model and gets warmer faster which also makes it hard to add pieces together without squishing then into each other and harder to make fine details. To avoid this pick up a can of air for cleaning electronics and spray the clay to cool it down when sculpting, you can also place into the fridge to cool it down. I would say this set is great to try out modeling or if you’re a beginner. I love this set for my kids to introduce them to modeling and it comes with all sorts of tools. It’s a great set.

This is a great activity for toddlers and children to learn coordination. My kids loved these stamps and it was super easy to teach them how to use the inkpads with these little stamps that fit right in their hands. The ink was very bright and colorful. I had to drag them away from the stamp set kicking and screaming about an hour later. This would be super fun for stationary as well with the modern emoji's and icons.

I've never done hotfixing before but have always been interested in it. This set comes with everything you need. The heat gun has an on/off switch instead of just turning on when it's plugged into the wall which I prefer. There are no instructions but reading a few pages on google search results should explain the process easily. I used the 3mm tip for the included gems to make some cute designs. EXTREMELY easy to use. I like that the heat gun comes with other size tips so you can continue to use it in the future with hotfix gems you buy separately!

The colors are vibrant and beautiful in this pen pack. I especially love the blue which has a little bit of a green teal tone to it. These pens are easy to use for beginners who are into brush pen calligraphy. I tried to blend my colors and they weren't great at blending on their own. I enjoy using these pens!

This is a colorful and fun creative material all in a fun travel box.  I think the kids will have unending hours of fun.  We started with fashion accessories for some stuffed animals but you can make so many things with them.  The box is a great size for travelling on road trips or on a plane.

Pros
+ Colors are really bright
+ TONS of colors to choose from
+ if everyone in the group isn't using the same colors, you have way more dye than just '5' shirts worth. We made about 8 shirts and still had 3/4 the dye leftover
+ I like that this kit included table cloths, aprons, gloves, and rubber bands so the only thing I needed to buy was white shirts.

Cons
- Colors don't soak through shirt well, definitely don't try 'dry tie dye' and make sure to wet shirts first.
- Price is expensive, I'm not sure I would pay that much to tie dye shirts when I could buy 5 pre done shirts for around the same price. DIY is supposed to be less expensive right?
- gloves don't actually prevent dye from staining your hands. The dye gets through the gloves they give you. Happened to three of us using the kit.
- I feel like I soaked the shirts I did in dye and had them wet too, but there was a ton of white still on the shirts. I'm not sure if this was my newbie dying technique or the dye.
- Instructions didn't have pictures with each dye technique, only written instructions so it was hard for me to decide what I wanted to do.

Overall I'm really sad about there being so much white on my shirts but I believe its user error from first time tie dying and nothing to do with the kit.

Easy to use mold for resin. I struggled with making sure it was perfectly flat so that it would lay flat when it was finished curing. My resin was a little bit too brittle for the screw at the bottom and broke, I will be trying another resin next time! It's nice because you can make multiple versions of these for different party themes and just take it apart then reassemble it with the new one!

The shimmer on this is gorgeous. I tested it in a variety of situations, the paper I tested is the 107lb cover.

Inkjet Printer
I used the Canon Pixma Pro-100 too print using the manual feed slot. The printer had no issue grabbing onto this thick paper. The ink has a hard time on this paper with color accuracy. No matter what colors I tried to print on this paper it ended up a silvery grey. Make sure your printer can handle paper this thickness and don't use the traditional paper slot, you will need a manual feed slot that doesn't bend the paper much.

Laser Printer
I used the Brother Compact Monochrome Laser Printer, HL-L2350DW to test. The printer did a terrific job on this paper using the manual feed slot. Very sharp black and crisp lines on the text.

Minc Foil Applicator
I struggled with this paper on the Minc, anything past heat setting '2' would have the foil adhere to the shimmer particles in the paper.

Laser Cutting
This paper cuts really well on the laser cutter.

Overall great shimmer paper, reminds me a lot of the cordenon stardream metallic shimmer papers. I don't recommend this for inkjet applications where color accuracy matters.

Super cute packaging. I like the amount that comes in this pack for the price. Very pretty glitter I use in my resin projects to add interest. The colors are accurate to what you see on the site and it comes with some eye swabs to use for makeup. The color is bright an vibrant, a good buy.

This foil adheres well to toner using the Minc laminator. It has a silver appearance with rainbow lines (see photo attached). I would have preferred if the entire foil had this rainbow effect instead of lines of it. Keep in mind that this foil is 6" wide and will not cover a full 8.5x11" sheet of paper. Overall it works well and is a cool holographic effect.

[[VIDEOID:bb3ffcb272aec01bed74259547546c2f]] I mixed just a little bit with some clear resin and was surprised with how much it glowed! I used a UV nail light to charge up the glow then tested it under the table where it was darker as you can see in my video.It was about 1oz of resin to two small pinches of powder. I am really impressed with this glow powder and would order again! My kids love wearing their 'magical' gemstone necklaces!

This thickness is terrific for making armor for cosplay. I made a lot of my armor using these sheets. The sheets aren't very large so you're limited with the size pieces you can make. I would buy these for details and smaller pieces, and buy large sheets for the base shapes of your armor. Price is great for what you get. Cuts easily with a new blade on my utility knife, shapes nicely using the dremel and heatgun and gluing it together is a breeze using contact cement.

Great quality D-Rings, they were too thick for my purposes and I ended up also ordering 1/2 inch d-rings hoping the metal width will be smaller. It's nice how these D-Rings are actually welded together, there's no way they will fall apart
Cuts through two sheets of plastic layered on top of each other easily. My old scissors would give me blisters, these are great.
Great value for beginners.
I used the mat for ONE project and the mat has already lost all of its tack. I will not be purchasing these mats again. At this point I rather use other mats.

The colors are very pretty, it has jewelry accessories, lots of tools to help shape and roll the clay, My 8 yr old granddaughter loves it, she was making all kinds of things. The baking is tricky, depending on the size of your piece, some take a little longer. But other than that this is a great little set. I would have given it 5 stars if the clay was bigger sized
I loved this clay! it was so easy to mold and create with. my daughter was able to make all her favorite roblox characters come to life! the cooking process is also fast and easy!
We have bought this clay so many times. It’s easy to work with and kids (and adults) of all ages love it. The colors are bright and it cooks easily.
Great clay (not messy or sticky), hours of fun.. I'm sure I'll buy again..

Arrived kind of dirty with metal residue. That wasn't a big deal, I would have been fine with cleaning them off, but the quality was really poor. The cutters had a clear gap where the blades didn't meet up and they wouldn't cut bead wire.

This kit comes with just about everything you need to start making your own earrings. The packaging and cards make presentation just like the real thing as if you were selling it in the shop. Though, these are not hypoallergenic (not that I expected them to be for the price). But, nonetheless, a great starter kit and good for practice/recreational work.

This polymer clay is super soft and easy to work. It does get sticky at times when it gets too thin or worked too long, but I just let it sit or make it a bit thicker to fix this issue. The accessories are cute and give you enough supplies for a few cute projects. The case it comes in is super convenient too!

My only downside is that once you start unwrapping the clay, you can't just squish it all back into this box. You're going to have to find some place else to put this.

These are sturdy and easy to use. I'm glad they even come in this little container to keep it all together! I honestly just wish I could find a gold-colored one of this same product :)

Sadly, my paints arrived with about 7-8 of them popped open and spilled on the inside. I doubt it was the sellers fault, since the paint was still fairly wet. I’m guessing in the past day or so they got thrown around in shipping. I lost some paint, but after cleaning everything up, this is still a beautiful set!

This is a great pocket palette! I took this with me on a trip to the other side of the world, and found it easy to use on my airplane ride and on the go. I’ve done many pieces with this set, and the paints are still very full. The one issue I have with this is how since the paints come individually wrapped, they often fall out if I’m not careful. Also, this makes cleaning the lid of the palette tricky. Along with this, the paint brush it comes with often falls out when I’m using it, but I usually just throw it back in and try to pinch the metal casing around it. Overall a very handy and high quality palette!

(The photos are some tester sketches I did with the pens)
I was honestly skeptical when I saw the name “Daskid” printed on the product. I was thinking this was going to be something around the grade of roseart or something else cheap intended for children. I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of these pens! They flow smoothly and lay down vibrant colors onto the page. I’ve been working with watercolors for about two years now, and this was my first experience with brush pens, and my normal painting routine hasn’t been the same! I find myself often going back to these pens.
The pros:
Simple to use, vibrancy, moveable color! Little mess, and easy cleanup. Much more confident than traditional watercolors, and a great quality for the price.
Now for the cons:
They dry really quick, so blending is often tricky. I’ll sometimes lay down a color and go to spread it with the waterbrush pen, only to find it leaves a streak where the color was originally laid down.
Another con would be the color palette itself. It comes with some very similar colors then leaves you with a dull array of others. For example, the red-orange range takes up around 1/3 of the entire range, but there is a single muted brown. This is only a small con though, as they are blend-able, and you’re able to get a much wider array if you know how to work with the colors and are able to work fast enough before they dry.

Overall, a very good product for the price point, would definitely recommend!
This worked well with the cricut for our butterfly heart projects. Very pleased and got one for my sister in law who is the crafting shop queen.
It’s a beautiful picture and I look forward to working on it.
Very small but looks really good. Very happy with item and price.

It came on time and was a good product.  I was hoping it would help a visually impaired friend but it didnt make pottery trimming any easier.

I got this kit to play around and have some fun with my kids. We are having a blast with it!

The clay itself is easy to work with, stays moist (sorry, but it's true), and bakes up perfectly.

It seems, to inexperienced me, to be the same quality as the Sculpey I have. So soft and easy to work with, but not too soft that it's easy to accidentally squish anything.

The tools are super helpful, and while they are plastic they are very sturdy and durable. There is a nice variety included, was able to make all kinds of projects.

The included ring base, beads, and pin base aren't the best quality, and I found myself wishing they had just put more key rings and shape needles in instead.

The phone chains are strings and the jump rings have a tendency to open. Again, wish they'd put more shape needles or key rings in.

The packaging is cute and not overdone, yay for the environment. It is also sturdy enough to hold everything and stand up to repeated use.

I was really surprised at the high quality of the clay and tools offered and would 100% absolutely buy this again.
Great for beginners
Great for beginners

I like these cups and they hold 20 oz of liquid with enough space for the lid to fit properly. I’m not sure why others are mad about the size. I have not tried printing a design because I don’t have the right ink. I do think the cups are sturdy and I like that the straws are metal. I think the price is a little high (about $8 per cup)  but I’m happy to use them.

9 days after receiving the order, I opened it to do crafts with my kids. Every single clay piece was too hard to use. The plastic surrounding each piece was like saran wrap. There's no way it would last without placing in some sort of ziplocs. So if you DO buy this, open immediately and place in better plastic. The colors were neat but I can't speak to it's utility.

I was really looking forward to trying this out. The product I received was so old that it was dried out and would NOT work. Totally defective and totally disappointed. This is the first and only time I'll ever order makeup through amazon

This replacement pack was hard as a rock just like the first order that this one replaced.
But... I don't know how to return this one and get my money back.
Perfect,  beautiful and just what I needed.
Supper soft ! Lots of colors ! Love all the accessories! Will but again!
Bought for my daughter who loves crafting our animals out of clay. These worked just as well as the name brand.
Good quality,  but much smaller than I expected. Great if you're planning on making little things

These are relatively cheap, and it shows. They are not strong like other safety pins I've used in the past, but for the inexpensive price, I suppose that isn't all that surprising. These are good for basic tasks where they will not be stressed a lot, but for bigger jobs, I wouldn't recommend them.

This is a nice woodburning set, with a lot of different parts included. It may take a while for a new user to learn what pieces do what and how they all work, but they are well made, easy to attach and for the price, it is a deal. I'm looking forward to seeing what new creations come out of this set.

Everything arrived quickly and nicely packaged with no damage. I wish the instructions were a bit clearer, but otherwise I think this is a good choice for a basic set to practise with.

This is an inexpensive clay roller compared to many on the market, but for basic handmade projects it works really well. There are no sharp edges and it is pretty sturdy for being plastic. I'm happy with it.

This glue is great for crafting and jewelry and I have no complaints about the way it adheres or the way it dries. it is some pretty tough glue. There is however a downside...This stuff smells truly awful. It is so bad that even when working with it outdoors where there is plenty of ventilation it is nearly choking to use. If they make this without the strong stink I would love to know about it.

I love tis stuff. There is nothing better for fixing breaks in polymer clay figurines or for bonding wet clay to wet clay. I use this every time I do a clay project, particularly for attaching limbs and hair as it dries beautifully and is hard to see when it is dry. It also isn't as messy as some glues intended for use with clay. Part of my tool kit forever.

This does what it claims and is a good, basic modelling clay that is easy to work with and easy to clean up after. However, it does have a bit of a smell...not terribly unpleasant, almost yeasty, in fact. Just...noticeable. It works well though for any basic modelling project and there is plenty of it that can be reused as it never gets hard, even when you leave it out for a long period of time.

These have some great colour choices and good coverage. They are all about even when it comes to consistency and they aren't lumpy or difficult to lay down with a basic brush. The only issue I had was when I went to open them. As with all of these style paints, you have to use the point in the lid to puncture them. With these, that resulted in a huge waste of paint as it bubbled out the top and a mess as it went down the side of the tube. I swear, it was like they were pressurized. Other than that, this is a nice set and even the brushes that they included were higher quality than I expected them to be. Not a bad deal for the price, just make sure you have something under them when you open them or you may make a mess that it will be hard to clean up

These are pretty basic clay tools. The metal ones are far better quality than the plastic ones. I don't really see the point in the plastic ones. They have such bad seems where they were put together that they mark the clay terribly, constantly making you use another tool to smooth it out again. Not bad for beginners but certainly not anything a professional artist would want to deal with.

I really love this clay for making small dolls with. the texture is perfect. it isn't too soft and so pliable that it picks up all the lint at your workstation and grabs every fingerprint, but it also isn't so hard that it crumbles and makes you have to add anything to it to soften it. This works great in the over and seems to have less issues with splitting and breaking apart than other doll clays I have used. The colour is even and makes a good tone for babies and adults alike. I'm thrilled with it and have ordered more since my original purchase. Recommended to intermediate and above clay workers.

I was actually pleasantly surprised by the quality of this clay. It is a little hard to start, but once it warms in your hands for a while it becomes decently malleable and easy to work with. There is a good variety of colours and the clay looks pretty good after baking. I had no issues with it splitting or being overly fragile. This would be a good starter kit for children or anyone wanting to try their hand at polymer clay without too much up front expense. The tools that came with it were pretty basic, but I expected that. A good starter set.

These are pretty good, some of the tools are bent a bit and not perfectly even, especially the needle tools. What didn't make me happy was that the Arteza logo tends to rub off the tools onto the clay you are working with, and since I work mostly in white polymer clay, that wasn't so good. Still, there are a lot of various tools and most of them are pretty durable and well made. There is a nice selection for hand crafting as well as pottery wheel tools.

I didn't love the paintbrushes in this set, most of them were not glued onto the stick properly and fell off as soon as the package was opened. The silicone brushes were okay though and the right size for what I needed them for, not too hard or too soft. Overall it isn't a bad deal for the money, as long as you have a bit of glue to fix the paint brush heads.

I think these are well made and nice looking, They are a good size for small, polymer clay animals. My only problem is that they didn't exactly come in sets. When I got to the end of the bag there were two different coloured eyes and neither of them had another eye to match them. I may have one very strange looking dragon when I use those. Still, overall, for the price, I can't be too disappointed with them.
This was the perfect solution for the wedding band that had become too large. Plenty in the package, arrived quickly and was nicely presented. A good buy for the low price.

If you've been looking for markers to use with adult colour books that won't bleed through on dual sided, decent quality paper, these are the ones to get. I am thrilled with these! There is a wonderful array of colours (including greens and browns) and a fatter tip as well as a very sturdily constructed fine tip. These markers feel good in your hand. They are heavy and cleanly manufactured. The caps close tightly but are not difficult to remove and replace. They hold a lot of ink and each tip is the same, there are not some that pool and some that seem dry.

Even the darker colours do not bleed through on heavier paper and I am so excited that these can be used on dual sided pages without destroying the other side of the page. With sixty colours to choose from with no repeat colours, this should be just the thing for those who want to colour. The thin side also works very well to draw hair, feathers and other things that require fine lines. The colours blend beautifully together. I have zero complaints about these and will most definitely reorder them when these start to run out. Excellent!!!

I've never really thought about Crayola brand being much other than art supplies for kids, but these are surprisingly good for adult colour books and other aged up art projects. The tin they come in is nice and holds way more than just this set of markers. The colours are vibrant and beautiful and lay down on the paper nicely. They don't bleed through on heavier paper and the tips are really well made. I wish they had larger sets available and do find it a little odd that the different tips are not the same colour on the individual pens, but otherwise I am pleasantly surprised by how well these work.

I've bought a lot of more expensive markers that were not as good as these. I would compare the larger end of these to the Zig clean colour markers. I'm impressed. The thin tip is pretty good as well. There is also a nice array of colours to choose from, and they match the tip indicator pretty well. I'll buy these again.

First of all, I'd like to mention that I read all of the Q&A on these markers and the notes from the manufacturer before buying these, so I was prepared for them to leak a bit and to have a stronger odor than some other markers. That isn't an issue. I was also aware that these are not Copics and would like to say that comparing them to Copic markers or saying that they are a cheap alternative is simply ridiculous. Copics are Copics and these are not. They do not perform the same way, do not have a brush tip and are not at all like them. I did not expect them to be.

My experience with these markers has been both good and bad, thus the three star rating. First, the good. There is a nice selection of colours in this set of markers and they arrived in a bag with plenty of room for the markers that zips closed and has a carrying handle on the top to lift them easily. The barrels of the markers are made out of high quality plastic and have a nice feel to them when you hold them in your hand. The shape is not overly awkward and they do not roll off of your art desk because of the shape. The pen colour indicated on the cap is not exact, but most of them are pretty close and the colour and an associated number is on the top of the pen, although some of them are more difficult to read than others, as some of the printing is not very clear or has rubbed away.

The markers are capable of blending with other brands--like Copic, but have a difficult time blending with one another, due to how wet they are. There is a good variety of different shades of each colour, including some that can be used as skin tones. I was particularly impressed with the different shades of yellow, as some marker sets do not have very good yellows and they end up looking more brownish when they are dry. These yellows remain true. I cannot attest to lightfastness yet, as I have only just begun using these.

Here's the not so good:

Yes, I saw that the manufacturer did say you had to be careful removing the caps so that the markers would not bleed. I can understand that under normal circumstances pressure builds up and a bit of ink may escape when the marker is opened, however, the issue I ran into is that some of the caps are so tight, that it is impossible to open them gently. When you force them open LARGE AMOUNTS of ink splatter down from the pen, as you can see in one of the photos I have included. This was the case with many of the bullet tip ends, especially in the darker, more pigmented colours. There were also multiple pens where the tip was pushed out, and I had to use tweezers to push the bullet tip back in.

These markers bleed through paper terribly, regardless of how thick the paper is. We are talking head wound level bleeding here, it does not seem to matter what GSM you use. Bleeding is common with alcohol markers, so this did not lower my opinion of these markers much, but I still thought it was worthy of a mention, in case you wanted to use them for dual sided adult colouring books. I wouldn't recommend it. As for using these for manga art--you would have to be really careful. If you get at all close to the outline of what you are colouring, they will bleed right over the line. Try taking one of these and holding the tip down on the paper for a few seconds, you can watch the ink bloom out around the tip and flood the page instantly. Not great for fine detail.

I do see that there is a full money back guarantee on these markers, and that's great--but I doubt it will make much difference getting a replacement set. I think the issues with these markers are going to be across the board. It's just the way they are made. If you are looking for a cheap set of markers with a lot of different colour choices, this is probably perfect for you. If you are looking for high quality artist markers, then look elsewhere. It depends on what you intend to use them for.

Overall, my opinion is that for the price, these aren't terrible, even with the issues they have, good variety of colours and a comfortable grip make them worth the money to buy. But be cautious opening them over a finished art piece if that's what you intend to use them for, or you may find it flooded with ink in places you didn't intend for ink to go.

I bought the spiral bound version of this book not too long ago and loved the paper, but that book was relatively heavy to be carrying around in my art supply travel bag, so when i found this one I was thrilled. The paper is the same quality, slightly bigger in size (by a half inch) but still great. The paper is a nice cream colour, heavy enough to handle the abuse I put it through with sketching pens and doesn't ruin the tips by gripping them. The paper tears out easily enough without damaging it. This is a great sketchbook for a good price.

If you are using alcohol based markers with this paper, you might find that it still bleeds through the other side of the paper. Not the best art marker paper that I have ever seen and definitely does not get along well with my copic markers, but as a cheap alternative to more expensive paper, it will do if you are just looking for something to practise on. This would be great for kids that are just learning to draw and add color but I wouldn't recommend it for professional art pieces. The paper is just too thin to be conventional.

if you are looking for a basic set of real brush pens for adult color books, for kids or just to mess around with and add a back drop of colour to a picture, these aren't too bad for the price. However, if you are looking for a brush pen that can handle serious artwork, you might not want these. They aren't manufactured the greatest. Some of the brushes were already losing bristles when I first opened them before the first use. Some of the brushes are also inserted wrong into the base and they make funky lines when you use them and there seems to be no way to correct that.

These do have a nice array of colors and all the colors are pretty close to the indication on the end of the pen. The packaging was basic, a plastic tray for each set of twenty-four pens and a plastic (thin plastic) case that they came in that I then threw away because I was afraid I would cut myself to shreds on it. The pens feel good in your hand and the barrel is nice and smooth. They don't blend very well and the ink dries really fast, so if you intend to use them as you would water colour paints, I recommend using an alternate water brush along with them to spread the colour.

Not bad for the price, but definitely not Zig quality.

this book is good for practising manga because it gives you a lot of space to work with and is broken down into easy to follow steps. The front of the book offers a more general view of manga while as you progress it gives you the opportunity to begin learning by following the steps and the online instructional videos. The manga poses included are pretty basic and for beginners, but it would also be a way for an experienced artist to brush up again if they haven't worked in manga for a while. I like these books and think they are really useful. The paper is good quality and there is extra paper in the back for you to practise on.

As with the animals version of this book, the art work is impressive and it has been very useful for my son to learn further art techniques for his art class. I appreciate the way the book is set up, with some basic information in the beginning and then moving on to the art in steps and explaining how to get the effects that are shown in the finished picture. There are also extra pages at the back of the book to practice on and the paper is really good quality and works well with most pencils and graphic pens. Useful and also connected to online video instruction for the extra benefit.

I bought this book to help my middle and older children learn how to draw for art class. The animals in here are great (Of course--Chris Hart) and the steps are explained well both in the book and in the instructional videos you can find online. If you are someone who is intimidated by the idea of drawing things that resemble real life, this would be a great place to begin. The steps make it easy and the tips included make you feel confident.

There is also extra paper in the back of the book for you to practise your drawings. Overall, this was a great buy and one that will help my son learn more than he would have from just trying it on his own.

I bought these for my mum, who loves adult colour books and gel pens. So far, there has only been one issue with these and that is, if you put the cap on the end of the pen whilst you are colouring, the cap can get stuck and refuse to come off. We actually had to break one of the pens to get the top off. Otherwise, these are exactly what they claim to be. There is a great selection of colours that really are different from one another. The refill ink is great because it is like getting twice the pens for one price and the pens don't clog up often and when they do, then it is easy to clear the tip by just depressing them on the paper for a few seconds. The glitter pens are particularly pretty.

Overall, these are a great deal for the price. I will buy them again.

I have used the Staedler lining sets for most of my career as an artist and have always been happy with them, until I began using copic markers. The two just don't get along well and tend to become a smeary mess, so i bought these, since they are copic proof. I don't dislike them, and they are right, once they are dry, they don't smudge and smear like the other pens do, but they also don't weigh much of anything and have a bit of a cheaply made feel to them. The barrels are very light so at first it was hard for me to control where the lines went. After a while I adjusted to them, but in the beginning it wasn't so great.

The ink is permanent and waterproof and does well being erased over the top of. The ink is also very black and saturates the page well and there is a good selection of sizes to choose from including a small and medium brush point, useful if you do manga art as I do, or have large black spaces to fill in. The pens also seem to have plenty of ink in them.

For the price, these aren't bad and at least they work with the copic brand markers well enough.

These are truly the very best markers that I have ever used. I tend to do a lot of manga art and these work amazingly well for that purpose, as well as for landscapes and basic colouring. I am impressed with how easily they blend together and how fine of an area you can work with using the super brush tips. I don't have that much experience using the other tip, as I tend to use the brush tip for most projects.

None of the markers are dried out in the least and the ink goes down smooth and is quite vivid on the page. This set includes a colourless blender, also dual ended, and has a nice variety of colours, particularly the greens, blues and greys. There are also a good selection of flesh tones in this set.

I love that you can replace the nibs on these pens and refill them so many times by buying the corresponding in refill, (between six and eight dollars usually.) These are so versatile and easy to use. The caps have little bumps to stop them from rolling off of your desk when you are using them and the barrel feels nice and solid in your hand when you are working with them. The packaging is great and they all arrived undamaged and nicely wrapped in shrink wrap.

I can certainly see why they are a bit costly, but in my opinion, you get what you pay for and these were worth the expense. They also arrived quicker than what the shipping estimate said, so I was happy with that as well. If you are a professional artist, these will be a worth while addition to your art supply collection.

I really like the designs and colours offered in this paper stack. These are some of the classics and they make great borders and cutouts. The colours play well together and work for a huge variety of different projects. I would definitely buy this exact stack again. Recommended to others looking for good quality paper for crafting. Nice.

These might be cute and colourful, but they really aren't all that easy for children to use. They tend to get hung up on the paper and collect little pieces of paper in the blades so eventually they get hung up and tear the edge of the project to bits. Even as an adult, I found these things frustrating. When you can get them to cut right they do a nice job, but they really could use a bit of work before they are good for kids, especially younger children. I will search for another brand.

These pens can be a bit rough on paper, especially if you are trying to blend them. They come in a nice array of greyscale colours, and they work quite well on various projects, but they will wear a hole right through normal paper, so I would suggest using them with a good quality multi-media paper. The caps are easy to remove and replace and the markers seem to last for a good long wile, so they are worth the expense. I find them useful in various projects, but am still a bit dismayed by what they do to sketch and regular drawing paper.

These are very cute, but sometimes can be a little difficult to separate from the paper backing they come on. Once you manage to separate them, they are very sticky and will not fall off of the craft that you stick them to easily, so they are a good choice for a variety of projects. They are also very cute. I have been using them on a variety of surfaces, including wood, plastic and paper and so far they have done very well.

These bee stickers are absolutely adorable. They are well made and very sticky so you don't have to worry about them falling off once they are in place. They work wonderfully as embellishments for greeting cards and other craft applications and are just so cute! I would definitely recommend these to other people who like to craft. They are a great deal for the money as well.

These are nice embellishments. There is a good variety in the package of multiple shapes and colours and they all seem to be very well made, with no sharp edges. I use these on greeting cards and frames and they add that special little something. I would buy these again and recommend them to other crafters. The price is certainly reasonable enough for the amount you get.

These are some of my very favourite pens for drawing and outlining. The tips are durable and stand up to a lot of repeated use. The lines are wonderfully black and dry almost instantly and are truly permanent as some other brands claim but then fail to be. The sizes in this pack are perfect for anyone looking for reliable pens to sketch, tangle or draw with and the packaging allows you a place to permanently store your pens in between usage. I will continue to buy these as long as they are available.

As always, Sakura delivers the best. I am so glad you can buy individual pen sizes in packs like this, as there are some that I use way more often in my artwork than others. These are perfect, as I have come to expect from this company. Worth all five stars.

I've been mostly happy with these for use with watercolour pencils, but there are a few quirks to be aware of when using them for the first time. First of all, the caps are very hard to pull off the first time (or at least mine were.) I separated the brush from the barrel before tugging at the cap so I wouldn't mess up the barrel and cause it to leak. Secondly, although during usage I haven't had any issues with them leaking at all, when you first start them, quite a lot of water can escape all at once, so you should do that on a cloth or paper towel separate from the art project you are working on.

Otherwise, I like these a lot. I have used them both with standard watercolours and with watercolour pencils and have had good results. The three sizes are perfect for most basic watercolour applications. The nylon tips are durable and don't seem to lose fibers. The pens are comfortable to hold and are easy to fill. Overall, these are great for the price they are offered at.

This is a good eraser to use if you are trying to perfect small spaces within your drawings. As with all erasers, it doesn't completely erase coloured pencils, and it depends on how dark the line is that you are trying to erase. It does a very good job of cleaning up smudges on the paper caused by graphite and rubbing your hand across the picture during the drawing process. It leaves minimal eraser dust behind and is one of the cleaner erasers that I have used. It lasts a decent while so you shouldn't have to reorder all that often, even if you use it regularly. For the price, I think this is a decent eraser for any artist's toolkit.

If you want truly small, complex pages to colour, this is definitely the book to choose. For the most part, this is an amazing colour book. The designs are awesome, the artist is super talented and the layouts vary enough that they are not all exactly the same thing on different pages. The issue I have is the double sided printing. I so badly want to use markers and pens with this book and using coloured pencils all the time gets really old, really quick, especially in a giant book with this much detail, but the paper cannot support most markers and they will bleed through to the other side, essentially ruining the next picture. It is a shame for such a beautiful colouring book to have dual sided pages.

I do, however, love to colour these cities as they have plenty of detail and make you think about colour choice and how to keep things looking authentic (unless you just want to make them as bright as possible.) I also like the size of the book, although it is a bit awkward to find a bookshelf space big enough to store it on. Overall, this is a wonderful book, with excellent artwork, though I still wish the pages were single sided.
I love these. They are very well made and absolutely adorable. Bought them originally for use in card making, but then they looked great on bookmarks. Will order again.

This is a great cardstock sampler. The paper is wonderfully made, nice and thick and smooth so it works very well with many different mediums. The colours are rich and bright and the arrangement of colours is beautiful. I would definitely buy this again, and it is a steal for the price. Perfect for scrapbooking, card making, and many other crafts.

These are decent chalk markers for the price they are offered at. It is a little hard to get the flow started, but once you do, they don't make a mess everywhere and they are pretty easy to use, with a solid, smooth line. I like the way they look on dark surfaces and they are easy enough to clean and put away after use. I'd buy them again.

These aren't terrible, they have the same basic design as the stabilo point 88 markers, but they definitely don't give the same results. They work well enough for basic adult colouring or craft products, but the colours of the ink are not even close to the cap colour on most of the markers. Some of the colours, especially the greens and yellows seem to have a brownish tint even.

They last a decent long while, but they will dry out after a few minutes of use and have to be recapped so you can use them again. The tips are sturdy and don't come apart easily and the caps are easy to remove. Overall, they are a decent set of markers for random usage, but definitely not for an artist who must rely on exact colour.

As far as the fine tips go, these are excellent and the tips are durable. The caps are easy to remove and the colours are pretty true to the pens. The issue I have with them is how light they are. The entire pen casing is made out of plastic and they have a cheap feel. I like pens with some weight behind them that I feel are a bit more resistant to damage, as I travel a lot with my art supplies.

They aren't a bad set and they seem to have plenty of ink, but they definitely aren't exactly what I was expecting for the price.

To be honest, I'm not thrilled with this product. The wire bound book is filled with 60 lb. paper--not the best, especially when using a black art marker. Aside from the paper being really thin, the front cover is a very thin sheet of paper with harder cardboard behind it, and has a rather cheap feel. For anyone serious about zentangling, this will not be the most practical setup. It's just okay.

At first, I really wasn't sure what to think about these. They are enormous and a bit awkward when you first try to use them. There is a bit of a learning curve in the beginning before you figure out how long you need to hold the marker tip against the alcohol toner to get the desired effect you want, but after a while it becomes second nature.

After a few uses, I developed a system of using them and now I have fallen in love with them. The multiple tones you can get out of one pen is pretty amazing and the colours themselves are beautiful and lay down very well on all types of different paper. The dual ends work well in a variety of applications. The scent isn't too bad, but is definitely noticeable, so if you are very sensitive to smells these might not be ideal.

They blend very well together and are easy to use once you have them figured out. There is an instruction booklet included to help make the learning a little easier. I would definitely recommend these to other artists, the price seems high, but it really is very reasonable considering all the different tones and values you get in this one 22 set box.

These are relatively inexpensive, but there might be a reason why. They take a giant with the brute force of an ox to push through a single sheet of paper. The designs were as promised, are cute if you can get them through the paper, but using them on any heavy weight paper is not an option unless you want to put the thing on the floor and step on it. They seriously are, extremely hard to use.

This paper is really good, nice and white with few flaws and is a heck of a bargain for the price. The paper arrived carefully packed, undamaged and is nice and thick so it works well with professional art markers. I have used this as drawing and sketching paper as well and to this point, have no complaints. I will reorder this again when I get close to running out. Recommended.

Well...if you want a tidal wave of ink after you attempt to try and prime the pen, then this might be one way to go. I followed the instructions carefully, and the first thing that happened, was that the nib got stuck up inside the pen. When I finally managed to retrieve it (with tweezers) the pen flooded and made a mess out of the test paper I was using with it. (Thank goodness it wasn't the drawing I was attempting to use it on.) I cleaned up the tip and tried again. Same result. I can't speak for this entire line of products, but this particular pen was a waste of money and effort. I hope they aren't all like that.

These are wonderful accents for any craft project. The adhesive side is nice and sticky and they lay down flat so they work well for greeting cards, bookmarks, etc. The only issue, is that it takes a very light hand to use them as they are super delicate. I love the effect of these when they are used on dark paper, and for the price, you can't go wrong.

I love this paper for the specific use of adding flair to handmade book marks. Tearing the edges makes it look really nice and gives a good contrast and it works very well with the gelly roll white gel pens. The card stock is very heavy and holds up to abuse and for the price, it is a very good deal .

I must take the opportunity to grumble about these, It said there were a hundred in a pack, and yet there are only fifty. The card stock is extremely thin, so if you are going to use these, you may want to add another layer to make them a bit more sturdy. Otherwise, they are very basic and the paper is not the best quality for use with markers. I recommend gel pens or coloured pencils. Certainly avoid anything that can bleed, as the paper is very fibrous and will catch the ink and pull it where you don't want it to go.

Not the best, not the worst. I think it depends on what you intend to use them for.

For the most part, i love all of the gelly roll pens, but these ones...not as much. The ink does stand up on the paper, when the pens actually work, but it s not a dramatic effect, more like a very subtle ridge that is created, one that you will only notice if you are looking for it. The main issue I have with them, is that they cog up frequently and can be a nightmare to restart ink flow with if you have let them sit for a number of days without use.

I think this is a good idea that could still use a bit of refining.

These aren't terrible coloured pencils, but the metallic quality of them isn't very apparent when they are on paper. The colours are pretty, but they tend to break the lead when you try to sharpen them (regardless of whether you use a mechanical sharpener or a hand sharpener. They work okay for adult colour books.

These are not only beautiful and easy to use, but they add a wonderful accent to any project that requires a border. They are the perfect size for greeting cards and also look wonderful in scrapbooking arrangements. The designs are simple and elegant and the colours are wonderful. There is a nice mix of designs and they are great for use with clear glue. I will buy these again.

These pens are some of the best if you are a professional artist, so I was thrilled when I saw that they came in this size package and variety. As usual, they have not let me down. The colours are true to the cap end, the markers perform beautifully and this is a lovely arrangement with a good mix of tips. I will definitely buy these again, as just for the blacks alone they are worth the money.

This is a great set that I have been very happy with.

I am thrilled with these. As a professional artist, it is important to me that the colour of the pen ink matches the indicator on the tip, and these are very, very close to the colour.

The ink is smooth and really stands out against the paper, making the artwork look much more professional and brilliant when held in contrast to other markers.

The tips are well made and durable and the pens seem to have a lot of ink. They work well on a variety of different paper and don't bleed much, so it makes it easy to direct the ink. They blend well and have a minimal scent, so they don't make you dizzy if you are leaning close to them. I am so glad that I bought these, as they have become one of my favourite supplies. I would definitely recommend these to other artists. There is a wide array of colours here, and many shades that you can't find in a standard marker set. I am particularly impressed with the yellows in this set.

My honest opinion of these, is that they are somewhat disappointing. I was hoping that these would be the colours they showed on the cap, but they really aren't. My second issue, is that they chew up paper something awful, even high quality, mixed media paper. When you try to blend them, they manage to nearly tear a hole in the paper. I'm not sure if I just received a poor quality set or if they are all this way, but so far Tombow in the various types I have tried have not readily impressed me.

I think they would work okay for certain applications, coloring perhaps, but for professional artwork use, I just don't think they are for me.

I bought this for my daughter who is very picky when it comes to clay. She is very satisfied and has made a ton of jewelry in just a few days. This is a great value and they are high quality. Great deal all around!
My 7 year old loves this clay! Super easy to bake.
I ordered these for a chili cookoff and they worked perfectly! They're a good size, high gloss print on high quality adhesive.
Great texture but broke apart after baking
Markers are dried out!
Perfect for little hands! And beautiful colors!
This stuff is great! Keeps the kids occupied with no mess. Lots of colors in the box too. I'll buy them again.
I received a used kit!! A lot of the clay was mostly gone!
I would put this more in the firm category than medium. It does hold detail very well. Maybe I just got an old batch but my hands are in agony trying to condition it.
These tools break with just one or two uses. The ends come out. I have had to superglue them in.
I was very surprised at the quality of this paint. For the money, it is quite nice. It dries very fast, so keep that in mind.

These pencils perform better than Artist's Loft from Michaels but not as well as the original Prismacolor before they changed their formula. The color selection is o.k. It could use more variety. They came neatly packaged in the cylinder. One huge deal is that the colors are not labeled on the pencil or anywhere. You just have to swatch every time. The color indicator on the end of the pencil is not exactly what you get.
These markers are aggravating. Once you prime them, you think they are going to be nice. Then in ten seconds, you have to prime them again. And again. And again. Not worth the headache.

These are nice rings but I don't think I got the full 200. I have used about 8 copper colored ones and I am out of that color now. I haven't counted the rest but it looks far short of 200. Maybe 100 if there are a lot more of the other colors.

I got these to use with polymer clay and the impressions are nice and clear. It is hard to use them on the sheet and when you take them off, they won't stick back on and stay. Best just to take them off and put them in a little snap case.

I actually like this a bit better than the fabric kind I got at the hobby store. It doesn't really stretch and it is easy to get wrinkles but it sticks to wood and to itself pretty well. It's not soft and fuzzy like the fabric kind. It is more papery but still good.
I just recently ordered another roll thinking I was running out. I'm still using the first roll and it just doesn't end. Very nice silky cord. Strong and comfortable even with heavy beads.
I'm not sure why they are calling these natural crystal. I found several that were obviously mold produced. They were shaped like a snowman. But they are pretty so I am giving 3 stars.
This is a nice resin for the price. Doesn't cure as fast as some I have used, but doesn't yellow. If it is still a bit tacky just cure longer.
This is really nice cord for the price. Very nice colors and silky. You get plenty for the money. I needed a thinner cord for particular beads and this fit the bill.
I was expecting silky thread tassels because that is what it says. These are tiny cotton tassels that are more fuzzy than silky.

This thing is headed for the trash can. It will cut one or two holes, then you have to empty it. Oh, wait, now it is cutting off center, left, right, high, low. I could chew holes in the paper faster and better.

It says mixed multicolor. It is all one style of multicolor. Nothing mixed about it. They are pretty though. Not as large as they make them sound. You can get a 2mm cord through them but I wouldn't call them big hole beads.

This extruder is just as good as any name brand at what it does. I think the extruding action is a bit labor intensive but that's with any of them. It does however, squeal like a little piggy being tortured when cranking it. I put some dry soap bar shavings on the threads and that helped some.
I'm only giving 2 stars because the shipping was fine. I have not had one bead come out clean. The top is always rough and misshapen. Sad.

These teeny tiny chips are likely glass and not stone of any kind. I found pieces that were obviously molded because they were shaped like a little turned table leg. They do break easily. But if you don't care about authenticity and are willing to look over the brittleness, they are really very pretty.

I just love these little rondelles. They add a little bling to jewelry projects. The hole size is large enough for cord to pass through and the sparkle is eye catching. They seem to be well made and I didn't find any broken bits.

I bought this and another brand at the same time to do comparison. This clay is a bit duller, harder out of the package and very flexible after baked. It is strong, though. It will bend all the way over without breaking. It conditions in a couple of minutes and isn't overly sticky.

I ordered this and another brand at the same time to do a comparison. I was surprised at how rich the colors are as well as the strength after baking. I had been having problems with a major brand breaking and crumbling. This clay is very flexible and strong. I don't know if I would recommend it for anything where hardness is desired. It is soft out of the pack and a bit sticky, maybe refrigerate before use.

If you are on the fence about buying uv resin and you do any kind of resin projects, invest in this and a good uv light. You can't beat it. Cures nicely and is clear. Also, if you are wondering if 200 g is going to be enough, let me assure you, it is plenty. I bought 2 bottles thinking it wasn't much and now I am coating the neighborhood with resin to use it up with the 6 mo shelf life. Please, for the love of all that is holy, have a fan going and maybe even wear a respirator.

These will hold a light pair of earrings very well. If you want to hang heavy pendants on them, be prepared to reinforce with tape on the back. Quite thin card stock. And pretty small. I may have misread the dimensions, but they were smaller than expected.

These are pretty good as far as size, strength and value for the money, unfortunately I have had to throw away some because the hole for the jump ring isn't punched out. That is most likely a machining fault. They hold and do what they are meant to do. They do not have the backward facing tab to help hold the cord, which is a bit disappointing.

I don't know if they sent me the wrong size or not, but these are at most 3 mm with a 1mm hole. I bought them for the hole size meaning to use with cord and twine. These will barely fit a tiny wire. They are about the size of a grain of rice across. Useless. I wish I could give 0 stars for accuracy.

So, the best thing about these is no waste. I was using old yogurt cups and fruit cups. Not ideal for measuring but handy. With these, you can measure and not waste because you can just let the resin cure then peel it right off.
These are super strong little magnets. Your bracelet or necklace won't just come apart and fall off. Very tiny, so you might need a magnifying glass to attach them, but they are good quality.

I was expecting a functional starter set. What I got was a light smaller than my cell phone with only 6 watts of output when the instructions specify to use 36 watts. Why would they send a light that won't even live up to their own instructions. You also get a couple of extremely soft noodle like tools that flop and bend, a childish looking mold and actually a good sized bottle of resin. I can't comment on whether the resin is good quality or not since the light is so awful. I'm only giving more than one star due to resin amount and price. Edit seller reached out and offered a refund. If seller would refund to Amazon account that would be helpful. I got an email asking me to contact. I would prefer to use Amazon as a channel for customer support.

These bead spacers are good quality and thankfully, the holes are large enough for a cord. I am not sure why, but the package was cut open when I got it out of the mailbox. I was trying to open the top and the bag of beads fell out on the floor. The whole bottom corner was cut off. Not the fault of the seller and I'm just glad they didn't fall out in shipping. USPS, thanks a lot.

I ordered the wrong size, due to being terrible with metric conversion. Despite that, they are good quality, heavy and sturdy little things. I will use them for earrings and small pendants. Can't complain.

The price is fantastic. It is actually not bad polymer clay for the price but expect some bars to be dryer and more crumbly than others. This is probably due to color variations and amounts of dye, oils and plastic. Over all not horrible for a beginner or even to make your better clay go further.
If you have ever used polymer clay, you know this brand is good. If not, I recommend it.
This is a LOT of cord. Probably more than I will use for a while. Nice and soft, makes comfy necklaces.
These bails are good quality, not cheap and thin. They are a bit hard to close all the way. I recommend strong pliers and a good grip. If you have arthritis like me, maybe get a helper.
She was so excited about the tools and clay. Great value and beginner’s project!
Great for people who are just starting out. Small amounts of different colors which give a wide variety of choices.
Perfect size and amount for price. Will purchase again.
Good product for the price,  very fragile though.
This clay is amazing! My granddaughter loves to mold tons of different things. The only thing negative I would say is that it stinks when you bake it.
No me gustaron no se ven como en la foto
Besides that bug that came with it, everything else about this is 5 stars. But again the bug is🤢, otherwise the product is🤩
Loooooove this stuff.

My kids love this clay, they have used almost all of this already and we need to buy more soon! Kids are 5 and 7. It will stain certain surfaces as my 5 year old showed me on my wall so just beware of that lol.
Purchased for my niece, she loved all the color options!

Bought this for my 10 year old daughter.
Nice starting set.

Great holiday gifts for young girls.
Gifted to girls ages 6-10.

Do NOT waste your money. I purchased clay at the same price on here and this brand is horrible compared to the other brand. It is dry and not malleable at all. It cracks when you use it.
We already opened it so I cannot return.
Just received! Wonderful, nice and soft. Cant wait for projects
Worked great until trigger broke. Last glue gun we had came from a dollar store and it worked for years. This one is a waste of money.
Fantastic! Has everything you need!

Don't buy these for little kids. They are fairly tricky to assemble. I'm a 36-year old and there were stages in this assembly that were rather difficult. I felt like I might break the metal several times. Luckily I never did and it seems to be pretty sturdy and flexible and has all the correct cutouts and bend creases in all the right places. I would say, if your kid is 13-14, they might be skilled enough to handle this. The whole assembly process took me around 2 hours, uninterrupted. But I'm sure if you've done these before, you might shave off some of that time.
Works great cutting clay. Exactly what I needed.
This is a good beginner polymer clay kit. So many colors to choose from! The clay was easy to work with and created some fun pieces.  The clay baked well and didn't break.
Thinner than imagined but vibrant, fun patterns. They also arrived quickly.
This kit comes with a variety of colors and all the accessories needed to start clay hobbies.
Gifted to a friend who uses the kit to make gifts for friends and family.

I followed the baking instruction, but it cracks every single time!! I even shorten the time(5mintues) and lower the temperature(260F). Don’t buy if you want to have quality work done! Buy it if you just want to practice or simply want to waste money.

I’m using these to store and sell loose tea. I love them! They are creamy white with a semi flat seam in the back. The front is seamless and a lane looks great on it. The top snaps snugly in. Upon opening these, they had a strange smell. I opened all of them and aired them out for 15 minutes and the smell went away. I’m happy with these. I plan on ordering more.

These all measured out as described. They’re all flat and shiny. They’re surfaces easy to paint or use paint pens on. They are shipped very carefully wrapped in bubble wrap. Even though they’re heavy, there was no damage to any of the rocks. I can’t believe IThese all measured out as described. They’re all flat and shiny. They’re surfaces, easy to paint or use paint pens on. They are shipped very carefully wrapped in bubble wrap. Even the other heavy, there was no damage to any of the rocks. I can’t believe I’m paying money for River rocks but here we are. They’re good though.

My fourth grader is using this daily. She says it’s roomy and spacious enough to hold all of her pencils, scissors, etc. The zipper opens and closes smoothly, and without catching. The soft sided material makes it easy to wipe clean.

This eight pack of sublimation cups come with everything you need. They have a straw, a tight, fitting lid, the wrap itself, and a rubber bottom tab. You need to peel off the top of the tab and then stick it to the bottom of the cup yourself. All my cups came out very nice.
My mugs all came out perfect! These have a little bamboo lid/coaster that sets them apart from any kind I’ve seen. Makes them sell very easy!!

My little boy did this for a wood project, and he is very happy with how it turned out. I was completely hands off, other than laying out newspaper to protect the table from paint. He understood the instructions perfectly, and with one coat of paint, got to create this cute little set of bookends. This does come with the hardware, and foam brushes, and glue, and paint. It’s a really fun item for younger kid to create!
Beautiful to look at and nifty to use. But barely holds 1 skein and I ordered the larger size. Still, the quality is so excellent and the concept so unique that I would totally order this again.

This loom comes with three separate projects. With enough yarn for each project. The instructions are perfect and quite thorough. The only thing I had to do for my nine year old is string the initial loom, and then she was able to do the weaving with all the yarn. This is probably most appropriate for ages nine through 13. The yarn is soft and the end products are totally adorable. This really helps your child build their tactile skills. It’s a quality product and I would absolutely order it again.

My 13 yo is using these for painting his D&D characters. They have a precise, fine tip. They don’t shed and seem to wash up to a like-new condition. Obviously he is not making fine art, but they are serving a real purpose and he is very pleased with how they’ve worked out

I like to have these around for the kiddos to use for sketchbooks. They dry out fast so make sure you hear the audible “click” when closed. We try and store these horizontal to ensure the ink is even. Good value, I’d order again!

I got this for my 13 yo son. He has enjoyed using it. It’s a great starter set. It has 24 colors in a tin that snap closed to trace or store. The brushes are decent quality and nice for a beginner. I would recommend this set to anyone.
Even tho I had to cut these to remove the candle, the finished product looked sharp and professional. The lines are straight and material thick. No strange smell. I would order these again.

I’ve enjoyed using these! There’s loads of ink and each pen has 2 different sizes of tips. The top “clicks” audibly to close so you know it secure. You can store all the pens in the box it’s shipped in because it’s sturdy and hold them all snug. So many colors! All very saturated and bright.

These are absolutely magical! 🪄 All the colors in the rainbow smushed together in different combinations in a pencil set. These are chunky and easy to grip. You can isolate a color. The color is saturated on the paper. And these are so unique! ❤️
This is a unique and mindful gift and even makes a meaningful item for personal use. You can relax and wind down while creating zen water drawings. Comes with an easel and a brush and a magic board.

I liked using these so much, I reorder twice. I used the template off their website and selected the transfer template so when it’s printed, it’s backwards. I fired up the trusty iron and in minutes had a personalized tote bag!! So cute. These are simple to use and inexpensive. I highly recommend.

This is actually an excellent pack of brushes. 15 come in the set and they are all different. The bristles don’t fall out when used and the handles are comfortable. Even if they get wet, they don’t slip too bad.

This 4 pack of white sub cups were fun to create and really fun to use. The decal has stayed true even through use and hand washing. The lid snaps in nice and snug with no leaks. I feel like this is the average price for sub cups. And if you’re creating them as a gift for your group, it’s actually very reasonable for a unique item.
I got this as a gift for someone and they said it worked very well and was pigmented just right!

Excellent set of glitter pens! We used these for painting rocks and you can see the color of the pens AND the sparkles. The ink flows easily down through the nib to provide even strokes. It takes a minute to dry completely. The top audibly clicks so you know the pen top in closed.

This has everything! A palette, decent brushes and a plethora of stretched canvas. This would actually make a really gift for a budding artist. It’s all shipped in a shrink wrap package and I would totally order this again

We used these for a kids’ craft. They are all polished and smooth. Some the size of a .50¢ piece, some a bit bigger. They shipped in 1 box, but were divided in 2 bags. We used paint markers and they didn’t smear.

These transfers!! Are you kidding me? How many times as a kid did I DREAM of creating anything I wanted, then putting it on an iron-on and easily transferring it to my t shirt?? My dream has come true! I went to Avery.com and used their myriad of cool templates to create my own design. My inkjet printer spewed out the transfer sheet as I heated up my iron, and 10 minutes later, I was in awe of my new, custom merchandise!!! So awesome! These work flawlessly. Follow the easy steps. 18 in a pack. I just ordered 2 more packs!!

My sons draw EVERYDAY. They have appreciated this little figure so much. The proportions are correct and it’s easy to manipulate. The craftsmanship is awesome. All the joints are reinforced and it’s solid wood. Very happy with this!
Good designs and easy to use. I can tear off with my hands and its easy to remove from where i put it onto. Doesn't leave marks or tears and it very gentle. Also nice variety of sizes.
Very cute and great for journaling. Designs are all different and all very nice. Good quantity and size that partners great with my notebook.

24 bars come in this package. Each one has a  crisp and clean marking. They all smell fresh. You can tell some essential oil was added. They have a faint oil smell. They melt easily, with low heat and mould easily too. Awesome for projects and beauty!
these were good molds and held up fine, but were very sticky and ended up very dirty and I had to clean them off every time I used them.
The resin mixes nicely and Is clear. It hardened as expected and maybe took just a bit longer than usual. Over all a positive experience.

These were soooo adorable and super easy to get on and off the page. They stuck fairly well to everything and were small enough to fit comfortable on anything. They were not very sheer and were in fact very matte. But I honestly liked that about them. Overall I would recommend.
This is as good as any epoxy I’ve used. The top screws on securely to ensure it doesn’t leak. My projects come out very hard and clear. Each bottle is 35 oz. Not too harsh a smell.
10 perfectly shaped, round, dark rocks. Some are about 2.5” and go up to about 3.5”. Easy to paint on. None were broken.

There’s a whole lot going on inside this box. First of, there’s a massive quantity of shells. Some arrived broken. Lots of variety tho. The scallop shells are huge and really fun. There are larger abalone shells which are beautiful. There’s about 1,000 of the teensy spiral shells, those are almost worthless for any project and tons of medium size shells. This ships with a watercolor paint set. Why? Watercolor is is paper. Totally useless. The watercolors come with a paint pen, cool. And a painters palette. Why? That’s for acrylics or oils. I’ll totally use the larger shells but everything is waste.

Quality set of acrylic markers. These all come with the tip dry. You’ll need to shake and press tip down to get paint to flow. Saturated pigments, even application. I like the variety of colors too. These all store upright in the box. Each one has a colored cap to see what color to choose.

Such a frustrating machine to use. I think mine may be broken because I could not for the life of me get the thread to stop bunching up under the fabric. I could get the needle to move up and down about 3 times, then it locked up. I’d look under the fabric and all the thread was bunched up. I tried to reposition the bobbin, tighten the spool, re thread the needle, use the foot peddle, use the manual button. Sigh. It hijacked my entire afternoon and I still can’t get it to work. The directions suck big time too. Very vague with tiny writing and fuzzy pictures. This is a perfect example of cheap, made in China crap. It also looked like my machine was a return/repackage because there was a patch of fabric in the needle/foot and the machine was loaded with a bobbin and threaded already. So I question how “new” it was.

After looking through these 3 books, I feel like all the activities would be most fun for kindergarten and younger. The math is very simple and is usually that of a beginning kinder student, the mazes are fun but not challenging to an 8 year old. Very well laid out, easy to turn pages and the paper is thick enough that ink, if using, doesn't bleed through.

We all oooo'd and awwww'd when this paper arrived. It's huge! The paper easily tears out of the tablet and lays flat. It holds water colors beautifully. None of the paint leaks through to the other side. It doesn't curl after being painted on either. It is not stark white, more of a creamy white and you can see the texture.

This flip book paper is great because it’s thick enough that the ink doesn’t bleed thru to the other side and it can withstand constant bending and flipping without tearing. The pin that secures everything into place has a screw-like top to ensure everything stays tight and in place.

My children are so happy to have an abundance of pom poms and googly eyes. They are always doing crafts and they love the variety of sizes and colors in this bag. The bag has a ziplock closure so there’s no risk of too big a mess.

Fits perfectly into my small hot glue gun. Melts very fast! I love having such a huge quantity of glue. I seem to go thru it really fast. Stores compactly in box it’s shipped in. Very happy with this order.
These work great. 10 different size brushes per pack. The bristles haven’t fallen out ever. They wash up to a like-new condition. The handle feels balanced and is long.

These are half sheets of watercolor paper. They hold the paint perfectly without curling. I wish it came in a bigger pack because my kids used the entire pad in 2 days. Tears out of pad without ripping, creamy white in color. Perfect for cards and crafts.
Basic, primary colors and thick, sturdy paper. Orange, yellow, green, red and blue. No surprises here, just what you expect. Nice for card stock and projects. Fits in printers and doesn't smudge.

Used this for a Halloween face painting event for children. The color variety is awesome. The plethora or shapes and sizes of brushes are perfect too. The paint slides off very easily and quickly though. If you need something with staying power, this is not it. That said, it's perfect for a brief experience, which is what I needed it for AND it washes off 100% completely and easily with soap and water.  Please note, kit comes with paint and brushes only. Not the spider and stencils in my picture included with review.

This is such a fun beginners set! This comes with 2 watercolor pens. They are shaped like a traditional pen, just slightly wider and the bottom is a brushtip, not a pentip. You pop off the top of pen and fill with water and you are set to paint. The set comes with a pad of watercolor paper and a small and vibrant set of watercolors. You really are able to get the feel of how this art form works in a small form. We used all the paper up, so I ordered more but can reuse the pens and of course the paint palette. Stores away compactly too.

These are high quality A7 sized cards and envelopes. They aren't glossy or smooth. They are watercolor paper. But still work beautifully with stamping and pen ink. The envelopes seal with moisture (a good lick!) And all the ones I've used have all stayed sealed. These make such original cards. I'm very happy with them and plan to order more.

These are gorgeous little wooden eggs. Their color is similar to a real brown egg. They are completely smooth and equal in size to a large egg that fits in a standard egg carton.  The kids like to play this game with me called, "hide the fake egg somewhere with the real eggs". They all laugh as I have trouble figuring out which one doesn't belong. Great fun. I though I'd paint these for crafts but enjoy having them out on the counter as decor just as they are.

I've used these about 25 times now and have enjoyed them! No weird smell and they glide easily. The amount of ink seems thick and saturated. The lid closes securely. The fine tip gives you the ability to use pen for precision script or drawing. I used on paper, glass (washed off on glass, but I didn't bake it so, who knows) and wood. I'm really happy with these.

These ones are pretty rad! I love all the colors and they flow easily. The cap audible clicks on so you know they're on tight, they're chunky and easy to hold. The color is consistent thru all strokes and also very concentrated. I took a star off because the color on the cap isn't as true to the color of the ink. I took a picture so you can compare. Most pronounced with greens and orange.
Darling tin! The lids screws on tightly and stays closed. The paint is even and the size is perfect for candles.

Great tags! I used a ballpoint pen and ink didn't smear and dried fast. There is a slight shine/metallic look to these so they look festive and pretty. Comes with twine and there's a hole in the tag for it.
I got this for some glass projects I'm doing. It's small, compact, safe and easy to use. Gives soft and even edges.

These are the best wicks I've ever used. They are a natural brown in color, thicker than I'm used to and not floppy at all. They stand up on their own. They are shipped with a roll of double sided sticky dots that sticks wick to bottom of candle. I don't want to use any other kind now and feel a bit foolish I ever ordered floppy wicks that you need to cut to size.

These silicone molds are really good. Each tray has on average 4 different sets of earring moulds. The trays are extremely flexible so everything pops out without sticking. Even the beaker is silicone so there's no need to worry about broken glass. This set also came with earring hooks!

Beautiful dried flowers! You get 4 sleeves  in this order. 3 are petite, equally sized flowers, and 1 is a set of greens. They are very well preserved, though handle them carefully because they are still delicate. The backside is as pretty as the front so you can use them in 2 sided pendants or charms.

OUTSTANDING MICA POWDER!! I'm thrilled with the quality and quantity in this order. Every color even has a few lighter and a few darker shades so your crafts can get really nuanced if you choose. The powder is quite fine and most has very subtle shimmer. Each plastic bag has a resealable top. 60 colors in all.

This is a really good candle wax kit. You get 10 lb of organic soy wax packed in a thick plastic bag, and shipped in a plastic tote with a latch. There is a large spool of wick material so you can cut it to size. Also comes with wick weights. The wax melts clean and almost pure white.
