I got this for my son who has recently become interested in origami, so he is a beginner. It's a small pad, about 5" x 5". The paper is a little goofy. Fox patterns, lions, boats, ice cream cones. But the paper has marks on it for easier proficiency. There are 152 pieces in all. With 72 different patterns. Comes with a little book of instructions too. Really great as an entry level kit!

I'm only about 1/2 way done with this torture project but its been a lot of fun to do during our summer quarentine/stay at home blah blah blah. The instructions are super clear, kit comes with 1 pad of wax, tray and pick up stick. I love love love the way it looks. Very soft, muted pinks and sandy browns. Nice depth of deep and rich colors through the cow. I cant wait to hang it after completion. Easy enough for my 7 year old to help with, too!
a good kit! it came with all the right materials and cute fabric. the earrings were easy to make.
Lots of beads, well organized, and I love the colors. No beads came botched and they are all pretty strong. Not harsh on skin and smooth.

COME WITH EVERYTHING!! What a fun project for the kids to do! The directions are lacking but ant quick google search will provide you with solid instructions. I was impressed with the amount of stuff you got: twine, gift tags, 4 quality essential oils, a long mixing spoon, a digital thermometer, a generous amount of wax, glass jars, caution stickers, wicks, colors, etc. The candles all came out cuter than I could have hoped for. Can't wait to make more!

My kids don't enjoy using these very much. They are difficult to rip out and frustrating to fold. Also, you'd think the black canvas would make colors pop out,  it just makes color look dull. I would not order these again.

Totally beautiful, massive color pencil set. There are 3 separate trays of 40 pencils in the tin. Each one pre-sharpened, with very little redundancy in colors and shades. The colors are all quite saturated and they have very little tugging on the paper. I ordered this for the kiddos to use during quarentine but honestly, it would make a beautiful gift for the artist in your life.

I got these for my daughters. They use them several times a week to make necklaces and bracelets. The hole is large enough for thread/elastic and the variety is nice; lots of different colors, symbols and letters. We used our own divided storage. It comes with everything in plastic bags.

Thisbus the nicest set of paints I've ever used. I am a novice painter, but have purchased at least 6 different sets over the last few years. These tubes have a vast array of colors all with excellent pigment. The trays the are in slide in and out easily for storage and the lid stays closed so nothing dries out. 48 colors. Very nice!

My son draws everyday and asked for this drawing kit. I don't know what 1/2 the tools are in it, but he does and has been using this happily. The entire kits is secured in a zippered, nylon, black bag. All the tools are held in place with a thick band of elastic. There's a additional grey pencil bag and a small blank sketchbook included. He loves the sandpaper pad to perfect your pencil tip and the wide variety of grafite hardness. I like that it's compact and neat without a massive amount of redundancy.  Seems like this would make a cool gift for a budding artist too.

Awesome pens. They last an incredibly long time and this multi pac is a great value. They are comfortable to hold and glide nicely across paper. We've used them for drawing comics and botanicals. They are advertised as ultra fine point. I just don't think that's correct. They are a little fatter than ultra, giving you slightly less precision.
Perfect gift set for budding artist. 12 watercolor pencils, a fine tipped pen, a sharpener, eraser, paper and brush all included in the box. Made in Germany.

My picture shows old school fiskars compared with these petite ones. The plastic on the handle has been changed. It is no longer shiny. It's a matte finish, which actually seems to help with the grip. Obviously the size is smaller too. But the shape is the same, and the sharpness and precision is second to none! I have to hide them away in my linen closet, because the kids love using them too. And we all know what happens when Mama finds out someone uses her sewing scissors for random craft projects...

These are as good as any canvas we've ever used. Love the larger size-which I always have a hard time finding! They are bright white, have a slight texture, hold the paints well and come in a convenient 6 pack.
Very cute and easy to hang. Super happy with this purchase!

I bought this thinking there would be more colors to choose from. Out of 48 colors there isn’t even 1 red! The clay is really stiff compared to scullpey. I piece got stuck in the roller and we dug it out with a metal tool. Some actual scullpey from 3 years ago also came out with it and was softer the the clay we had opened that day. I wouldn’t buy this again... I am sending it back

My daughter ordered this for her art pens & we are thrilled with it. She for how well organized & accessible the pens are & her parents for the reduced clutter. It is sturdy enough that she moves it from a shelf for storage to her desk for work.
The organizer came as a pile of small planks that had to be assembled. My daughter had it together in a few minutes. As I recall it was all slots & grooves with a few screws to keep the planks from backing out.

The cordless design is very practical & convenient especially since it uses my DeWalt batteries. I like the large size, being unencumbered by cords & that it stands up on the weight of the battery. It's great for large projects with no limitations from the cord length & it doesn't get knocked on its side by the cord like the lightweight glue guns.
This glue gun will probably spend most of its time in the shop because it runs very hot & doesn't have a temperature control or lower temperature settings. We tried to use it on an art project but it wasn't easy to limit the flow & the glue was too hot for the sequins.
The gun heats up in 3 or 4 minutes after switched on. I expected it to go through batteries quicker but half a stick didn't reduce the battery a single bar. The kit came with 20 clear-ish glue sticks that perform well in the gun, I haven't tried any other glue sticks in it yet.

[[VIDEOID:8accb2b71689776ed4b44876683598de]] We had a lot of fun with this kit & good success for being our first project. The mixing instructions are very easy to follow, it's simply equal parts from each container & stir for 3 minutes. The resin stayed fluid for at least 15 minutes while we messed around with it. We also mixed in some acrylic paints & got some vibrant colors.
The resin didn't put off any smell that I could detect. I did get some on my fingers but there was no reaction & it washed off in the sink. It also didn't heat up the mixing cups while we were using it.
We used a silicone candy mold to make little, resin gummy bears. The very bottom of the molds held a tiny bit of air & we didn't get "hands" on some of the bears. Next time we will poke around the molds with toothpicks. We did use a heat gun to get out some bubbles which we thought was working but there is a learning curve.
The clear bears are perfectly clear from back to front but the sides touching the molds are slightly hazy. We may try a mold release the next time to see if we can get them clear on all sides. The instructions recommend a 24 hour setting time which we followed. The bears weren't tacky at all but were slightly flexible. After another day they are totally hard.
The kit came with everything we needed except the molds. It included pouring caps for the resin bottles, some wooden sticks, a couple of disposable pipets & 2 small measuring/mixing cups. The kit also came with silicone gloves & finger covers.

My daughter is deep into arts & especially art supplies. She was excited to get this quality brand & he prefers this type to the watercolor gouache or the tube gouache. It is opaque but can be easily diluted with water to create a gradual wash.
The kit came with a palette knife, three good quality brushes, & a large palette to mix the colors. The container is solid but the gouache dries out gradually so it has to be occasionally sprayed with water to keep it fresh.
She has used it to paint a hand-made Sculpey mushroom, to touch up some dolls & on paper. The only complaint was that the white was not opaque on black paper but the other colors did well.

My daughter picked these clays & quickly made the turtle to test them out. The clays are very hard & need to be kneaded to soften up & work. The lighter colors, yellow & white, break down quicker & are more sticky once they warm up. The darker colors left stains on fingers but the stains washed off.
My daughter used a hair dryer to harden the clay (my wife wouldn't let us use the toaster oven) & it did harden & dry in about 10 minutes. Once hardened, the clay doesn't transfer color to fingers that handle it. We all loved that it came in the plastic case for organization.

I have used Sugru before. It is very easy to use. You open the package, mold the stuff inside like clay & you have a few minutes to make something out of it. In 24 hours it is dry & harder & ready to use. I have used it like a thick glue to hold things together, as an insulator for wires & to make things. With one pack, I was able to stiffen up the end of a charge cord, insulate some lose connectors on another wire & I made nose pads & an ear rest for sunglasses which had lost theirs.
I really like using Sugru but the problem is that it isn't cheap enough to waste & doesn't store well. I didn't know it expired the last time I used it so most of my packets expired before I could use them. Once the packet is opened the contents have to be used or it will harden. I am so frugal that I save up & prioritize projects before I open a packet. The packets that I received in August are best used by March, which is a long time, but there is no way to know when they will expire when ordering.
I'm very happy with the results but I should have gotten my daughter to mold it. She is much better at sculpting than me.

My daughter loved painting on these but they are hard to fold & need tape or maybe glue to help keep the shape once folded. They are very presentable by themselves though without a frame.
The tabs are all there to lock the paper in but it requires a level of coordination that may require practice before we can get them built properly. The paper is nice & heavy & the price is good for this many pages. My daughter is always experimenting with different media & techniques & only a few of the projects she starts ever get finished. This is a good product for her since they are low cost but presentable if she decides to finish them.

I got these for my daughter who does art with all sorts of mediums at school & for fun at home. She used them to outline & shade a painting that I haven't seen but she says they performed very well & were "better than the Micron pens" that I just bought her. She likes using pens for art including watercolors & other paints.
I played with them but do not have any art to display for you. They are very smooth & the ink is dark. The tips have a lot of variety with the smaller sizes being stiff 7 pointed & the larger sizes have longer ink tips with different shapes. The 3mm has a beveled tip which is fun for calligraphy-style writing.
This was a hit as a Christmas present for my granddaughters. They were very creative with what they made with this set.
10year olds enjoyed making unique objects from it.
this is awesome my grandaughters love it, they have made different together, without fighting for over an hour an its something they can come back to. highly recommended ,for kids 5+.
40 mag is perfect to examine coins I collect and jewelry that I make. I have macular degeneration in my eyes, so being able to see close up is a blessing. I make a lot fewer mistakes.
I use these for dying pearls for jewlery making. I don't have to waste hours searching for the exact color I need, I spend a couple of minutes soaking like an Easter egg, and voila! perfect color.
I really like these cute buttons. Perfect for crafts for kids and adults. I can use them for end clasps on braxelets and necklaces. Perfect for a casual look. Very cute for kids clothes.

It's great to have so many different sizes of the same color. These are beautifully made and look just like they are pictured here.  Tere are enough beads to make a nice set of bracelet necklace and ear bobs. (of course it depends on length and style)

...but it takes a long time to dry for a thick coat. Very thin coat dries in about 2 minutes. There is no smell and no fumes.

Looks just like chrome. I'll know better how durable it is after use. If everything checks out as promised, I'll leave this review as is. If not, I'll come back to let you know.

The pens come sealed, so I'm not sure about shelf life.
I can't wait to get started on my project. Easily use one section or the whole chain. The price is about right for the quality, The finish is brilliant, and the stones are very pretty. Very nice.

If you've ever tried to get the bubbles out of resin, you'll appreciate this handy tool. i'm a beginner and this, if used carefully, works great for mixing mica powder. You can mix thoroughly or leave swirls w/o bubbles.

Superior kit for a beginner because it comes with the nonstick work plate that's the right size for small projects such as earrings., an acrylic 3x3 tha you can use to make swirls or cabochons, lots of tools, a roller, open o-rings, earring backs and hooks, and an instruction booklet--although the examples aren't that pretty. Look up polymer clay earrings on YouTube to get a larger array of examples and instructions.

Pearls are beautiful with brilliant colors. The variegated colors blend well with the solid colors. Enough of each color to make several projects. I just made. A pearl chain necklace and bracelet I think are quite nice.

The colors are as vibrant as the sparkles. Nice selection of colors and sizes. Enough of the same kind to make several bracelets or necklace/bracelet combo--with spacers you can make necklace/bracelet/earring combos.

Just like the photo. Very nice.

I love, love this collection of beads. The colors are fabulous. They are color coordinated in the storage containers so if you ever get stuck on color combos, just pick a color you like and use the colors side-by-side it, up-and-down it, or on the diagonal. Perfectly blended colors. Each container holds summer, fall, winter, colors that will work together for fabulous fashion statements.

There are only enough beads in each color for about 1 project. I made this 8.5" bracelet with this many 12/0 beads left over.

...that will continue for a long time. Beautiful charms and beads, easily crafted and easy to switch charms to make dozens of combinations. Enough different charms to satisfy the most discriminant crafting whimsy. Enough charms to make a matching necklace, too.

...that will continue for a long time. Beautiful charms and beads, easily crafted and easy to switch charms to make dozens of combinations. Enough different charms to satisfy the most discriminant crafting whimsy.
Beautiful beads, easily crafted and easy to switch charms to make dozens of combinations. Enough different charms to satisfy the most discriminant crafting whimsy.

Lovely mold that makes a very lovely, smal mantle clock. A word of caution though, they are not kidding about several pourings for curing. As you know, resin gets very hot as it cures, and the material/silicone the mold is made from is a bit thin. So multiple pourings will save your mold for more clocks.

Sweet Password heard my concern and immediately replied in an email. Here's their reply:

Message from seller Sweet Password Inc.:

Dear Resin Lover,
Since some customers have reported the problem of difficult demolding, please allow us to add some operating instructions.

To avoid the material from overheating and melting the mold, it is recommended to pour at least 3 times separately with an interval of more than 20 minutes. It is also recommended that not to make more than 5.3oz of resin in one container each time. Please stir gently, in one direction. We follow the above steps for all thick molds, not just the clock molds.

We sincerely apologize for not guiding correctly. We're planning to make a user's manual of this mold. If there's any other question, please feel free to contact me anytime.
Last, I hope you enjoy making the clocks!

Sweet Password Customer Service

By the time any new customer reads this, the problem will most likely be solved,

Flattens easily, so you need to be careful when bending. Bends easily. Also scratches easily... I had to use a scrap of t-shirt between the wire and my metal pliers. I'm a beginner so this wasn't exactly ideal wire for me. Learned a lot though.
The holes are larger than fresh water pearl holes so you can use ribbons or the thicker hemp strings. There are enough stones of each kind to make one bracelet or use with fillers to make a necklace.

Love these. Very beautiful, nice array of styles. Some in plastic bags, some not, but the storage case is a cute flower, see-through plastic. Some holes are 2mm, some 3mm, some 2.5 mm. The spacer part are just like the photo. Great quality and most are very pretty.

More layers, deeper color and easier to blend even when dry. Does dry fairly quickly--depends on thickness of paint--anywhere from 15 seconds to 20 seconds.

Great assortment of colors, and the white is very opaque. Good value.

Of course this is just a stopgap machine for touch ups, not a regular sewing machine. Good for straight line sewing only. No backup, you have to flip the material to sew over /knot your seam.

The major problem is making straight seams for the "portable" part. Holding with one hand and guiding with the other is tricky to get a straight seam. The plate that hols the fabric is loose enough for free sewing as your hand tries to guide. Like I said, it's tricky.
Everything is beautiful, except I'm not sure i got the correct thing.  was supposed to get 10 and got 6. But the backs are fine quality and comfortable.
Excellent, usable beads. Brilliant color and sparkle. Beveled holes so your thread doesn't break. Love them.

Lots of charms, stones, beads of different sizes, but no instructions. I guess that's what You
tube is for. It would be so great if these companies would do some tutoring videos with their kits so we ca nget a good idea how to use it. Don't know what to do with the charms since no instructions...guess those are for making bracelets.

Very sturdy wire and heavy string. But don't make a mistake because there isn't much of it. Tons of earring hooks, and other findings. All are nicely packed in a see-through plastic storage box. Seems os excellent quality, so I'd say this kit is worth the money for sure.
And really good to fill-in for veteran clay workers. sharp, clean edges. Very durable. Easy clean. And the different sizes are so easy to work with. Highly recommend. Worth the money.

Ech container is rather large. Wasted space if you put small beads in one. Better for larger beads or clay. (not air dry clay because it isn't air tight. Can see contents and colors well. I've got the smaller version, too, which is great for those small packs of clay. Once I figure out what stores the best in it, I'm sure I'll love it.
Stores hundreds to thousands of beads. Can see what is in each container. Easy open and close. secure seal...dropped one and the lid stayed closed--no beads all over the floor. Well worth the money,
just like the photo...I much prefer these to acrylic because the facet edges don't get scratched. Light enough they'll stick with glue or beading.
Perfect clasp strength for a bracelet--no more fumbling or chasing the fastener around the wrist. Pretty, but it won't overpower the beauty og the bracelet. Well worth the money.

I got these for my beadworking, i can paint the thread I use and it disappears among the beads and  basically from view. Very easy to use, quick dry. The point is not sharp, it's about 4mm thick. great for coverage and for pinpoint accuracy.

I was pleasantly surprised that this kits does have everything you need. The charms are so easy to make and the mold expands creativity. Very pleased the flowers are all intact. I thought shipping wold crumble them, but no. They are beautiful. Great for beginners!

Love the colors and how well the blend. The powders need to be hand-woekrd into the polymer clay before rolling into your pasta machine. It will "explode" all over the place if you don't. Otherwise works great!
Clarity is good, colors are nice as well as consistent. I will buy again.  Holes are a nice size and consistent.
Sparkly and excellent colors. Clarity is good. Smooth and large holes. I recommend thes for easy beading.
Love the sturdiness and the wide selection of tools. Highly recommend.
Not so great when trying to thread a beading needle. Works great for ordinary sewing needles with larger eyes.

Very interesting beads. The color covers 1/2 of the beads so when you use them in your jewelry, you'll have a fluid look with them because the color will move. The colors are bright and beautiful with the crackling look.
Absolutely not streatchy, which is very good, but very low tinsel strength, which is not good for beading, and I want the quilting thread to show when I make quilts.
I received the containers (acyually bigger than expected) but not the pegboard and hooks. Ill come back and post more if the pegboard arrives.

Plain colors, perfect for fill-in and background beads as complimentary color or blending colors. The packaging is a perfect storage box, and each color is packed in a ziplock bag.. Perfect, beads aren't rolling everywhere.

I was expecting something to make as in a kit, but all you get are some mixing tools and squirting nozzles. So, I was a little disappointed. However, you can make some very cut things using your silicone molds. The shelf life is a year for unopened bottles and 6 mos for opened bottles--not nearly as long as polymer clay. You can use epoxy over polimer to make stone-like beads such as turquoise, jasper, rose quartz, coral, opal, jade, etc. without the weight of the natural stone Really cool.

that will match just about any color in your bead stash. Laf-shaped with the hole in the top, they will dangle prettily from ears, bracelets, or necklaces, I got them to go with some polimer beads I made . There's just enough for 3 on the bracelet, one for each earring and 7 for the necklace. Perfect. Can't wait to make a design using them as focal beads.
I still think Czech beads are the best. 4mm are rather small, but these beads are so beautiful they seem larger. I love them, and they are a great value for the money.
Photo is of clear beads--I received white with some sort of color iridescent painted on a few sides. Made up, it's pretty, but I would not pick this up off a shelf to buy.
Nice and sturdy unlike many other types, however the tips don't fit into my beading needles, which is the reason I got them. Sigh.

You have every color you could possibly need to "paint" your own Salvadore Dali or Rembrant painting. Being creative with these pens is an extra special dream. No mss, no fuss, but brilliant color just like alcohol pens.

Well worth the money for a beginner or an old pro. Highly recommend. (No coloring pages included.)

Very nice set of different sized scissors, sharp, easy to hold, perfect sizes to tuck into your needlepoint, or mending case. too small to use for cutting to patterns, but the 5" ones are perfect for trimming. Not sharp enough to trin hair, though.

This kit comes with everything you need to finish this project except the frame. Easy to follow instructions except I do recommend you watch some tutorials for diamond painting. You might want to invest in a light board (not expensive), which will make putting the diamonds in the canvas much easier if you have trouble seeing like I do.
I just love these colors. Mr first project is Ssan's at Turtle Soup, dragon scale polymer clay pendant. When it's finished, I might upload a picture of it for you. So far so good.

I had a kit like this way back in college. This is a comparable kit to that very well made kit. The tools are well-made. The handles are sturdy and easy to hold. The box is sturdy as well even tho it's cardboard, it should hold up for years. I love the "spoon" to practice on. Everything is as promised.

If you've ever been around a cotton gin around late August thru October, you know the fresh scent
i'm talking about. Unfold it and spread it out for about 24 hours and it goes away if you don't like it.

Otherwise this batting is so soft and will hold its height unlike the poly-fill you buy at big box stores. Also unlike the poly batting, this batting is even in height from edge to edge. All around quite superior. Very much worth the money.

I love these for their sharpness ad for easy usability. Just one thing, the flowery one hurts the fingers when used for cutting or trimming material. the flowers sort of dig into the skin. I recommend choosing a pair that has smoother handles.

Great product. Im so excited to get this. I've worked with polymer clay before and the colors in this kit can't be beat, bright and so many of them. My sister and I are making animals and dinosaurs for our grand kids.

I love this beautiful canvas. I can hardly wait to get it framed and decorate my wall with this gorgeous still life painting. I've been painting since 1972, and this was so much fun to paint. I was testing it for my daughter who wants to learnhoe to paint still lifes. I recommend this product for sure.

...no mess techniques. Brilliantly nuanced colors and no more allergy-raw hands from needlepoint wool. no more fiddling with hoops, and so much easier to fix a mistake than when needlepointing. However, you can't make pillows with this technique. Mostly wall hangings. The kit includes everything you need and is very reasonably  priced. If you are new to the craft. there are tons of diy videos on youtube. If you like coloring, you'll really like this craft.

...brilliant colors, great coverage without having to keep going over the same spot again and again, no squeaking as you color. you can make your own design and laser print to color,or use ink stamps. (Haven't tried it with my desk jet ink printer. I doubt that will work without smearing.)

They have 2 nibs, of course. I really like them.

This was a great buy. I'm learning to needle tat and my eyesight isn't exactly the best for close up work, however this set has a medium size (using now) and large needles so I can learn with videos. I wasn't into hand crafting when my grandma taught my cousin to tat. Wish I had learned from her. But, it's never too late, and this helps keep my brain young, or so I've heard.

Super for patterns that you want to keep. I make a lot of baby clothes and I got tired of having to cut out a pattern then tape it back together to cut out a larger size. With this stuff, I can cut out and KEEP all the different sizes of each pattern. Now I can make all kinds of clothes for my business as well as for my grandbabies!

I had my doubts about this product because of the hugely diverse reviews. It is sturdy. The canvas holders are easy to position, but the drawer doesn't slide out as easily as expected. The drawer holds a lot of paint and brushes, though. It is compact, but it really does expand to hold a large canvas. The major problem is that the sliders that are supposed to hold the canvas tightly have a tendency to slide apart, so my canvas slides, too. No matter how hard I tighten the screws, the top and bottom still slide apart. I do not have any problem with the angle feature. Those screws stay where I adjust them.

It isn't too heavy even when filled with supplies. I can set the thing on my lap desk and paint very comfortably. I have to have my feet up because of my gout so this is really a godsend for me.

My 10 year old granddaughter and I do a lot of crafts together, one of our favorites is making things with polymer clay. It's very easy to use once you get used to it. Children do need a little guidance with it of course, just like any other craft. My granddaughter was so happy that "Santa" brought her the clay for Christmas.
My granddaughters loved this kit! The oldest is 8 and can do the bracelets with just a little assistance.
The letter are super cute and a great quality. The only issue is when trying to sew them on after heat pressing them to the shirt my sewing machine needle broke. The letters are really thick!
My 4 and 8 year old daughters loved this!! It is so much softer than other kinds of clay, easy to shape. We will buy it again!
It was good and worked for my purposes but on a whole it was smaller than I had anticipated.
I love these pens and I've been using this brand for over a year now. These are exactly what I expected and I still love them. Also it's a steal considering the price.
great for jewlery only one problem not all fit in the organizer
nothing like the description reminded me the chains they have in the quarter machines in baggies and all knotted up when  received was not snake chains just regular links.
Great selection of colors and tools

Great value, my daughter loves to sculpt and all of her creations held together nicely.  The kit lasts a long time as well so didn't have to buy more right away even though she used it Create a lot of different animal sculptures.
Exactly as described product. Glass beads are as pictured with uniform holes size and bead shape

It was easy to use and great for beginners! I love the color selection. We made charms, figurines and some flat pieces to hang in a mobile. I would say be careful of making small fine pieces that stick out (our pikachu charm keep having its ears broken off). I baked them at 250 for 15min BE TO TURN ON YOUR OVEN FAN AND OPEN WINDOWS TO AVOID FUME INHALATION.

I loved that it came with the different striped seed beads! They 4mm (sized 6/0) beads are all opaque and colored. It was as advertised and the container with the beads did a good job of keeping each type in its own separate compartment. There was no mixing up of beads during shipment, which was great. A minority of beads had some variation in size but for friendship bracelets it adds a unique charm which I love.

The set of 4mm (sized 6/0) seed beads came in a clear plastic container with divided sections. The seller was sure to put each type of bead in its own small bag within its section so it didn’t get mixed up in shipment. Beads were colored and opaque and pretty consistent in size. Main issue: these are not all glass beads if any... I made necklaces and bracelets for friends and family and within a week, the color from all the pink and red beads ran off the beads leaving a white plastic. The dye might have stained their clothing in the process.

Works great! I left it in the sun for 30min because it was slightly overcast. It is better to dry out plants you want to use first for the following reasons: 1) light can penetrate better through a dried leaf or flower showing the pattern of its veins. A fresh leaf has water in it making it more opaque and may not lie flat on the fabric 2) if you’re using a glass say from a picture frame, to place on top of your plants to keep them in place, know that fresh plants may develop condensation on the glass. The water can cast a shadow and worst, if water gets on the fabric during this curing period, it will cause stains and marks on the fabric.
I rinsed my cyanotype fabric for 5 minutes, changing the water periodically and it because a dark blue without me having to pour hydrogen peroxide on it to speed up the reaction.
This polymer clay is great for kids! It was easy to manipulate and baked well. Great for beginners.
Nice set of clay but it does not get hard, followed the instructions that were provided.
I'm a beginner...this is a perfect kit for me....so many things to try & do!!!
Terrific selection of colors. Kids make a zoo of creatures and loved doing it!

My daughter has never expressed an interest in needlepointing til Covid happened and now she's all gung ho stitching away. Its a complicated enough design that its really keeping her interest and making her proud to be creating a little work of art for her flowered sofa.
this box holds a multitude of different beads. It makes working with them sane!

I love this kit! I use it to make jewelry.

My only issue is that the colors I received did not match the colors advertised (for example: I have no yellows).
Works like it should. All metal. Should hold up a very long time.

For me, this product worked perfectly and I would buy it again. I could see that that others might find some qualities unwanted, so I only gave it four stars. Like most polymer clays, you have to knead this to get it workable. It seemed to get a little too soft and was sticky at times. Just let it rest when that happens. It's too warm. Small bubbles would also get trapped in the clay when I tried to roll it out. That's undesirable in a clay, but it didn't matter for my project. The colors didn't all have the same sheen after baked. If you aren't going to paint it, that would be a problem. Some were super shiny, some were matte. I painted my project, so it didn't matter. This was just a good way to get a large amount of clay for cheap. I loved that it came with so many tools. I received everything as pictured.
I’ve made some pretty earrings. I love this kit!
I works! I was able to make a few earring with this. However it is time consuming!
The clay was pretty hard. My kid wasn’t able to do much with it because it was too hard to play with.

This is my first experience with polymer clay so I don't have anything else to compare it to. Having said that, I love the variety of colors and once you manipulate it a little by it becomes soft and malleable.
I received an incomplete box.  One color is missing.  The accessories are rusty.  Overall, not happy with it :(

I loved this clay. I made many small structures with it; and they turned out excellent. My tip: you shouldn't bake your clay structures. If you keep them out, they harden over time, and you can also edit them over time as well. For as long as I can remember...in school, when we did clay every year, it was probably one of the most exciting parts for me. The only reason this is 4 stars is because the clay in the box is WAYYY to small, and hard to mold.

There is a great assortment  of clay and tools. This kept my three children entertained for hours.  My youngest got this for her 9th birthday and loves it. I love that it comes in a case that when your done it all first back in, makes clean up easy! My children have made some great models and they bake, so they last longer! I would recommend  this product!
I was very excited  to have won this kit! This kit has a great assortment  of pencils to use. I can not wait to start sketching and my daughter is very excited too! Thank you!
My husband and I bought it as a birthday gift for his 9 y/o niece. She got excited as soon as she got her present and said she couldn’t wait to make everything out of these clays.

I'll just start by saying I normally don't leave bad reviews, but I didn't receive all the parts. I've emailed the seller two days ago, but they still haven't contacted me back despite their claim of being willing to fix any errors. My kit came with no tools or accessories. Literally just the clay and storage box it naturally comes in. I bought it since I'm a beginner since it comes with everything, but I can't work with it the way I want if it doesn't even have the tools. I've seen other reviews say their clay was too soft, but mine was the opposite - super hard and dry. I don't know what happened to my batch, but I'm not impressed with this and won't buy it again. Save yourself some frustration and buy a higher quality kit.

I really only wanted the earring hooks and packaging for them, so the pliers and jumpers were just a bonus. However, the jump rings aren't super sturdy, so if you don't soder them, your finished product can easily fall apart due to the jump rings being so easily bent. I already had jump rings from another kit that are super solid and strong, so this didn't bother me. But if you plan on using this kit alone, be careful in case you get a fragile jump ring batch like I did.
Bought for my daughter for Christmas. She loves it and is a good artist.
I bought these for my husband who has started painting rocks. He loved them!
I bought these for my husband for Christmas as he likes to paint rocks. He loves them!
Bought for hubby. He's enjoying painting rocks.
Very good needles. Would buy again.
I bought this for a home improvement project and it worked a charm. I love the case it came in so it won't get lost or damaged. Recommended.
I buy this every couple of years. Nothing works long anymore because of marketing schemes but they work for a few years OK.
Works well for my husbands and my arts and crafts that we make. Produces a nice, hard, coat of protection under the Mod Podge products that we use on things we make.

I bought this for my husband who is using it for a repair on an antique glass lampshade. It does not want to dry well even days later. Directions were followed to a "T" but it still stays too tacky. I will have to go back to my old brand.
I bought these for my husband who is repairing a glass lamp shade for the detail work necessary. They are excellent for this kind of work and we recommend them.
I bought this for my husband who is repairing a glass lamp. These paints are always great and I love this value set.
I needed a bunch of safety pins so this seemed a good buy. NO complaints on them.
I make Micro Macrame Jewelry and this is a lot easier to use than a clip board or homemade pillow. I highly recommend it for doing Micro Macrame.
They are fine. I use them for making hand sewn journals.
Got these for my hubby who recently started coloring books for grownups. He likes them but not his favorite.

These are great travel-sized sketch pads for budding or even novice artists. I have kids that like to sketch and draw, and this is the perfect size for them to carry around on a trip or into the woods on a hike. The paper seems pretty standard quality, and I love that it comes with two of them in the package for the money. The spiral binding is a great additional touch, I'm very happy with these and would recommend it.

My daughter is a huge fan of Harry Potter and has recently taken up art and writing. This was the PERFECT gift for her, she's loving the aesthetics of it and learning how to write with it. It looks super cool on her desk in her room, and it actually comes with a lot of extra neat stuff for the price. Overall I'm very impressed and would recommend this as a gift for any fan of calligraphy and fantasy!

This is perfect for reupholstering part of my car's arm rest fabric that started melting with age in the hot sun. There is a small strip of old material that is a sort of grey faux leather strip that stretched out and gets sticky when humid now. It looks and feels gross during the summer. Removing the door trim and wrapping this product over that little piece and replacing it not only fixed the aging issue, it actually improved the overall look of the interior!

I wanted to try some resin epoxy for projects with my kids. This is our first kit like this, so I don't have much to compare it to. It is really fun and rewarding to make interesting things out of it! We collected sea glass over the summer and kept it for just such a project, and using this to make it into art was pretty straight forward and an absolute blast. I can't wait to use it again!

These are not necessarily for repeated use in a home or classroom environment- there is not a lot of paint in each set, however it is perfect for single use paint events with many participants. My daughter had a birthday party and this was the perfect warm up activity while waiting for all the guests to arrive. The colors dry bold and strong and it comes with everything you need to get started creating water color art.
Highly recommended.

I wanted to make some custom phone carrying cases out of wallet material, and this set is EXACTLY what I needed. I had previously tried a grommet/eyelet kit, but some of them tend to be more about aesthetic additions than functional ones. These eyelets are the perfect size to be functional for the project I wanted it for. There's plenty of them in the kit, and the tool makes it pretty easy to press them into the hole as desired. Everything I wanted it to be!

This is a great project for everyone in the family! My kids made keychains as gifts to give teachers/friends for the holiday season, and had a blast doing it! The kit comes with everything you'd need and then some. The finished product is very homemade-looking but still high quality, which is exactly the charm we wanted from it. Would recommend!

My daughter needed a new pencil case so we decided to give this one a try. It's really cute and she loves the color and the size of it! There are plenty of pockets and a generous amount of storage inside. Her only complaint is that sometimes she finds it confusing to remember which zipper opens up which pocket. You see, the zippers are right next to each other and it isn't immediately obvious when looking through the window which zipper will open the contents you are looking at. Not a deal breaker, just something that could have been designed differently. Also, there's a metal wire that keeps the shape, however my daughter has noticed that wire starting to bend up into the case, and she can't get it to stay back in its original shape.
Again, just nitpicking as the case really serves its purpose well to hold her things and have plenty of extra storage. It is cute and does what she needs.

I wanted to try this for a craft project that required some eyelets/grommets being bunched into a carrying case. I've never done this before, so I was excited for a kit that hopefully included everything I need. Sadly, despite being called a "Grommet Eyelets Kit", it comes with exactly ZERO grommets or eyelets! It has rivet and rivet caps which are cool for decoratory punches, as well as snap button hardware but nothing that has a hole in the middle to classify as a grommet or eyelet!
The punch tool is pretty useful, and I'll say that it at least helped make the initial hole in the leather and fabric perfectly. But now I have to buy an additional set of grommets or eyelets to do what I had in mind. I feel like I could have skipped this entirely as most sets come with hardware to punch it in. I'm sure it is useful for other things, but feels like it should NOT have claimed to be for eyelets and grommets! Falsely advertised.

This is a really high quality set for artistic kids in your life. I was a little bit concerned that it was overpriced, but once you see it in person it makes sense. First of all, there are a LOT of parts in this set. Enough for multiple kids to use simultaneously. Secondly, the box is actually excellent. The drawers and hinges are better than I expected, very polished and grand looking.
But the most important part? Kid approved. My kids adore this, especially my 11 year old artist daughter. Her eyes lit up and she's over the moon with this, showing me her works in progress in the sketchbook. Would recommend.

I have attempted cell phone repair and spliced wire shrink tubing before, and the instructions always recommend a heat gun. I have been using a hair dryer due to lack of better options. I didn't think a heat gun really made so much of a difference... until I got one. Wow, why didn't I try this sooner?
I just used it with some shrink wire tube connections, and it was so easy and efficient! I love that it has a little metal stand so you don't have to put it down hot onto a table or floor where it might cause damage (unlike a hair dryer), and the localized heat made it easy to target exactly what I wanted and not heating up everything around it at the same time.

After having a neighbor customize some gifts with their woodburning pen, my daughter has always wanted to try one herself.
To be fair, we don't have much to compare this to. I see some of the other reviews here complaining that it doesn't work the way they expect other high end pens to function, but for us, it seems to work. There is a digital temperature readout which I'm not sure my neighbor's has, and it seems to get hot enough to make the cool looking scorched wood we wanted. The only thing I don't like is how big the pen is- I wish it was bigger. The smaller size means you're constantly closer to the hot parts you want to avoid touching. So be careful! But with patience and proper safety precautions, it's a fun little activity for creative people!

I have an 11 year old who wants to learn how to make her own clothes. This is an excellent starter kit, it is pretty simple and straight forward and has a very small footprint- excellent if you don't want to commit to a larger machine that needs to stay out all of the time!
The only catch is the smaller size has limitations for large jobs, but I suppose that is true of all compact sewing machines. So far, my little girl loves it so I couldn't be happier with it!

We decided to try this out at a family get together as a fun activity for everyone. The colors are great and the material plenty pliable yet strong enough to hold its shape until drying. It seems like a solid kit, everyone enjoyed themselves!

I got this for an activity to do one night of Hannuka with our family. Making our own candles is a blast for all ages, and this kit is an excellent starter set to get you going. I see some reviews claim that not all the materials were included, but I suppose there were some badly packed boxes? Or maybe someone expected something that wasn't supposed to be inside- Ours seems to have come with everything we expected to be in it. A good family friendly activity for all!

Sugru is awesome. They call it a "glue" but its really more like a putty epoxy. Yes, it will fuse stuff together, but it is also malleable until it dries, and then it becomes a tough rubber that also happens to be electrically insulating. I used some of this stuff to make an adapter plug for some camera equipment by buying a bare plug and soldering wire to it, but I needed a housing. Sugru to the rescue! It not only covered and insulated the rest of the exposed wire, but made a rubber housing for it as well!
The only problem with this stuff is that there's a shelf life before it hardens and becomes unusable in the packaging. That's why I love that this comes with 8 individually wrapped doses of the stuff! A lot of fun for any DIY savvy tinkerer!

I've used Sugru once before to create an adapter for some battery backs I was DIYing, as it is electrically insulating as well as becomes a hard rubbery object when cured. However, I've never tried colors other than the regular black, so I was super excited to see this option in white and grey! So cool to have choices to match other things for other interesting projects.
I'm a big fan of Sugru and highly recommend it for DIYers!

This is a nice kit, but my wife and kids were excited to try something new and actually found this difficult to pick up. It claims to be for beginners, however without any stitch experience it wasn't very easy to figure out. They actually gave up on it and I had to show it to someone else to figure out how it should really work. Someone who's actually already into and interested in this as a hobby would probably have enjoyed it more. As a gift for someone, if they don't already have some basic knowledge of what it is / how it works, I think they're going to find the instructions frustrating. Otherwise, the materials are good quality and it seems like someone who knows what they are doing will have fun with it.

You get a lot for your money with this marker set. My 7 year old loves them so far and has been using them extensively recently. We are currently in lockdown in NY during the Coronavirus breakout so having plenty of arts and crafts materials on hand has been a real help. The colors are very vibrant, and the dual tip makes them very versatile.

This is a really cool set that has a lot going for it- The markers come in a bin that is really convenient to to store everything in, and the dual end (marker/brush) design is very cool. My 9 year old daughter finds them delightful and fun to use. The only complaint we have is that some colors weren't true to life, for example a neon orange didn't come out quite so neon, which disappointed her a little. Also the brushes are just OK, not as high quality as dedicated brushes, however for the price and what you get, this is a great deal!

My daughter decided to take on scrapbooking, and these are an excellent choice for that. They draw like a reflective metallic color which looks really interesting on almost any colored paper, black or white. I recently tried them out when coloring with one of my kids and I have to admit it was kind of addictive- even regular doodles just look cooler with metallic gel pens!
I haven't used many other gel pens to really compare these to, but my daughter says they are really good and I personally enjoyed playing with them. I'd say they are definitely worth the price.

I know the product says it is for making slime, but don't let that deter you from other non slime-related arts and crafts! This is pretty much standard Elmer's glue that happens to glow in the dark, so you can use it for all sorts of fun projects. My kids immediately started using it as a sort of puffy-paint on paper to make projects for their wall. The different colors really do glow in slightly different colors, although they are different from the colors they are in the light. Of course, it's also great for slime, but I'm happy to say this is fun for a lot more than just that!

My girls were super excited to do this project and I have to award points for it keeping them busy on a rainy summer afternoon. But it turns out it is a little bit more difficult to finish than they expected. It's not actually very hard, but there are a lot little parts that easily get lost if you're not careful, and the repetitive nature of it had them losing interest towards the end. They are 6 and 9 years old for reference, but really should have had an adult actively help them complete it.
My wife and I helped them start, then they insisted they could finish it themselves, so we left them alone. This resin was very easy to use. I have an $80 UV light that my husband uses as a crane technician, and a $10 UV light I used to dry my gel nails. It seemed that no matter what kind of light I used, the resin was left sticky. Left it there for long periods of time if not hours to cure and it still left sticky residue. Perhaps I got a bad batch because nothing seems to fix this problem. The only solution I've read online is to use clear nail polish to coat the resin piece, but I should have to do all of that if this resin states it will dry with UV light. 3 stars because of ease of access and you get what you pay for.

Maybe I was expecting too much or for it to be anywhere near quality such as CraftSmart or Sculpey. I will stick to those brands as the quality of this clay is very difficult to work with, even with layers of clay it was still way too soft for me to even cut a shape out. Free tools along with the clay so can't really complain about those. Otherwise, I'd invest in a whole new set.

i've made ornaments for years and always just used floor wax. floor wax works best but it is really messy and sticky. tried this stuff this year. it is much easier and cleaner so definitely the way to go if you have kids helping you. i do notice the glitter is thinner with this but not enough that it was a problem. don't use the thin nozzle to spray the stuff in because it'll make it foam. take off the whole cap and pour it in slowly for best results.

overall the set was worth it. the clay was soft and a lot of great colors. however, worth mentioning that a few were more dried out and took extra work to soften. and a few had a bit of black mold on them. i just cut those parts out and used it still no problem. but very good to know!

overall the clips are great and work as expected. however, worth mentioning that i got a different design from the tin in the picture. and the clips arrived fitting in the tin, but i've never been able to figure out how to get them back in to all fit with the lid ever since then! it's a tight fit, but just remove a few and all is well!

I really wanted to love this HTV. The patterns are beautiful and vibrant. The first time I put it through my maker it didn’t cut all the way. I decided to try again this time using more pressure. I looked at the transfer sheet and there was some black coloring on it.

I wasn’t sure when I first purchased this what to expect. It was a great deal and then when I used it it was definitely worth the money. It is cheaper than other HTV and gives a quality product I will definitely buy it again.

Work as advertised. No overwhelming smell. Very pleased with purchase.

4+ hr/day EVERYday crocheter

I love this yarn, and it came as my others do. When getting inline I worry it'll be different or subpar quality but this seller is legitimate.
Sturdy and thick but the glue on it isnt as sticky as is like it to be. Fast shipping.

Took me awhile to go through each marker. First of all it comes in the cool bag but the recommended storage be horizon not vertical so you have to lay the bag down. Secondly some of the caps are loose so you have to ensure the cap is on and others are very tight and you struggle to get the cap off. Thirdly the colors from fine to thick tip in the same color can vary greatly or a minor bit. I had to make a color swatch out of the sample picture they sent but didn't think to label tip side till I was almost done. This gives me more colors but also could ruin a project too.
This glue works great for crafting! It dries quickly, but takes 24+ hours to cure. It works great on clay, jewelry, and so much more! It’s easy to use too.
This air dry clay set is huge! You get a big box and there are so many different colors! The clay is great quality too!

This non-slip rug backing is great to help prevent your carpet or rug from sliding all over. It does help with keeping it in place. I like it as you can trim it to fit the size of your carpet/rug too. All you need is some hot glue or carpet glue to attach to your carpet/rug and you are all set!
This epoxy resin set is great for the price. You get a decent amount and a lot of extras! It’s easy to mix and turns out perfect. No odor like it says either. Love it.

My kids enjoy this sculpting clay. They haven’t used it for pottery, but for making figurines, and they love it! It comes in a nice box and has so many accessories with it. It’s such a nice set and the clay is good quality too.

This spray on frost is a great idea for Christmas. You get 4 large cans in the set and I would say they are worth the price. It is made specifically for windows, however we are going to experiment with this stuff to see if it works elsewhere. Will update the review once we spray it as it’s still too early to get out our Christmas decor. :)
This sculpting clay is good quality and my kids love it. It does have a weird scent to it, but it is easy to mold and works great. You get 3 packs of it also. It’s a great value for the price.

These moss rocks are very soft and are good quality. My daughter recently got a skink as a pet and we put these in his habitat. It works perfect. They are lightweight and perfect for him to hide under! They look real even though they are artificial.
I love making my own Halloween creations and this set is perfect for it. It has tools of all sorts and it comes in a nice case. The tools are great quality too. So worth it!
I love making my own Halloween props/crafts and this hot knife is perfect for cutting foam. It is nice quality all around and easy to operate. I love it.

This air dry clay set is great! My kids love it! It comes with so many colors and so many different accessories to use with it. It is easy to mold and it’s a great indoor activity. We just got this set today and my kids have been playing with it non-stop since it was delivered!
My kids love drawing with these outline markers! They write so nicely and look so nice. Great quality overall!
This hot glue gun is great! It charges up quickly and I love the fact that it is cordless. It works just as it should and even comes with 12 glue sticks. It’s so easy to use!
This glow in the dark paint glows exactly like you would expect it to. Perfect for art, Halloween, and so much more! Works great and my kids are very happy with this paint.

These acrylic paint markers are great for drawing! If you use these on paper, you will need a heavy duty paper like card stock or they will bleed through. These are great for all art projects though. Not just on paper! They work just as expected, write nicely, and there are so many colors to choose from.
My kids love art and wanted to try these liquid chrome permanent markers. They write very nicely and they love how they look. All around awesome markers!
These glitter metallic paint markers are soooo pretty and you get so many colors. They are perfect for drawing and my kids love them. They come in a very pretty plastic package as well.
These permanent markers are surprisingly very nice for the price. They work just as well as the name brand at a lower cost.

I remember these from when I was a child, so instantly when I saw them I had to order them. My kids have never tried this before, but now they are hooked! They haven’t stopped making them since we got this set!

The set comes with 20 sheets and a bunch of accessories, including a hole punch to make keychains or ornaments and tweezers. The ornaments are easy to make, however the keychains are a bit difficult to bend (even with using tools), so we just stuck to making ornaments! The best part is that you can freestyle the drawings and make anything you want! This is a great activity to do with your kids, but it does require parental supervision and assistance for the cutting and baking.
My kids love these water based brush markers! They are like painting with watercolors, but are much easier than using a paint brush. So many colors and they are so bright. Awesome quality.

This polymer clay earring making set is so much fun. It comes with 24 different clay colors and has a bunch of accessories and tools so that you can make your own earrings. The clay is very easy to mold and is good quality.
My kids love to paint, so we decided to try this palette paper. It works well and is a great quality paper. They are happy with it!

My kids love this air dry clay! It is soft and easy to mold. It also comes with a clay mat, which I love as it adheres right to your table and helps prevent a mess! It also comes with some handy sculpting tools. It’s a great value for the price.

My kids love this polymer clay, so we thought we would use this to make some jewelry for fun, and they love it! The clay is a nice texture and easy to mold, and it comes with good quality stainless steel cutters to make different shapes, plus a ton of accessories and tools! It is all around a wonderful starter set. The only thing that could make it better is if it came with more clay colors.

This polymer earring making set is a wonderful set, however the polymer clay cubes in it are very tiny, so there isn’t too much clay to go around. It does come with a great amount of accessories and tools though. It would make a great starter set, just be prepared to buy more clay in the near future.

This polymer clay set is a great value. You get 82 colors, tools, and jewelry accessories in this set. It comes in a nice storage container as well. My kids love this stuff! It molds nicely and only takes about 20 minutes to bake. There is a great variety of colors, however the only thing that could make this set better is if it included more black and white as there is only 1 black in the set, and 2 whites in the set. These colors always seem to run out the quickest and it would be nice to have extras of these colors.

Wow! These are amazing, and they truly look like REAL feet! They have amazing painting details and you can actually see the veins. What is neat, is that they are silicone, but include the skeleton inside of the foot. The toes are also posable, so you can move however you want.

These are perfect for Halloween decorations, displaying shoes, or foot jewelry - Really so many uses!

I plan to use these for my Halloween display and can’t wait!
It’s the best puffy paint, and stays on clothes even after washing. The only brand I will buy from now on!

I loved this puffy paint set, as well as my kids. There are so many different colors, and it is so easy to apply. The love faded fast as the shirt and pair of shorts that we painted didn’t survive the washing machine (inside out, and on a gentle cycle). I would not recommend using this puffy paint on clothing, however it would be great on canvases or even rocks.

Wow! You get so much in this art set and my kids love it. It’s great quality stuff too.

You get 24 colors of acrylic paint, 6 paint brushes, 8 blank canvases, 6 themed canvases, a tabletop easel, 2 foam stamps, 1 paint palette, an apron, a sleeve, and the carrying that that comes with it.

The quality of the canvases and paints are so good. The only thing that could have been better is if the tabletop easel was a tad bit bigger, but I am still very happy with this set!
This set of art canvases is great. My kids use them for painting with watercolor and acrylic paints. They are nice quality, and you can use so many different types of paint with these.

This Eyelet Hole Punch Plier by Amazon Basics works just as it should! It is lightweight and has a nice grip. There are so many uses for this. It comes with 100 eyelets and it is incredible for the price.

This Sesame Street art set is so cute! You get so much with it for a great price! The set comes with 2 boxes of Sesame Street 10-Count Jumbo Crayons, 8 Mini Markers, 9 erasers, 1 pair of safety scissors, and 2 sticker books with over 400 stickers in each.

The markers and crayons are easy for little hands to hold onto. The scissors are perfect for a young child and are simple to use. The prints on the erasers are oh so cute and work just as expected. Finally, the stickers! Wow! So many stickers. You get 400 in each booklet and it comes with 2 of them making 800 stickers. The stickers are perfect for school or pre-school as they encourage and compliment hard work. The stickers are easy for my 5 year old to peel off and stick as well. Everything was perfect in this set and highly recommend for pre-school or kinder aged kids!

This polymer clay set is nice, however it is a bit pricey for what you get. The set is small, and while you get 50 colors, the clay blocks are small.

We have tried several different brands of polymer clay, and this is probably my least favorite. The clay was very hard, and was difficult to mold. My 5 year old was struggling with this.

The pros are, is that there is a good variety of colors, and it comes with tools and accessories. The case it comes in is nice too as it has a carrying handle.

I have seen basic sewing kits, and I feel that this is so nice for being called “basic”!

For all that you get with it, it’s really worth the price and a great value! It even comes in a nice zip up carrying case so that you can keep in your car and practically take anywhere with you. So convenient!

I love making epoxy resin art and these silicone molds are perfect! They are easy to use and once the epoxy is cured, they are so easy to pop right out of the mold. The molds are reusable too! It’s a really great set!

My kids love clay projects. This is their first time experimenting with polymer clay, and this is a great beginner’s set. It’s also a great value for all of the colors and tools you get.

It takes a little muscle power to knead the clay. Parents, you may need to help your littles with this part… as did I. It is definitely a fun project to do with your kiddos! There are so many colors to choose from, and you can make so many different things, from jewelry to Christmas ornaments. It’s truly so much fun!

This epoxy set is very heavy! It is a lot so if you do epoxy resin often, then this is a good set for you. The epoxy sat for 72 hours, but still feels a bit soft, so it takes awhile to cure, so you have to have patience! It could be due to the cooler temperatures too as it cures the best in heat.

These silicone molds are great for epoxy resin art. They were easy to fill and did the job well. The ball was the only 1 that had a slight issue- I filled it all the way with epoxy but somehow the one end flattened, so it’s not a true ball shape.

[[VIDEOID:4896901c1c9d6fe24449071fe9ee59ac]] This Hippie Crafter deep pour epoxy worked great on our table. It gives that extra shine for that finishing touch. It’s easy to use the full amount as the epoxy resin and the hardener are already the correct measured amounts. It gets more difficult when using a lesser amount as you have to be precise when measuring as that ratio is 2:1. I am very pleased with the results! Totally radical dude! 😎

We are new to the Epoxy resin art, so this was an experiment, and surprisingly - it was very easy to use! The mixing ratio is 1 to 1, and if you follow the instructions, you get no bubbles at all! You can mix in glitter with it to give it some shine as well. I can’t wait to use this stuff on more projects!

This glitter set is universal and can be used for so many different things! I got it for epoxy resin art, but you can also use for slime making, and so much more. The set includes 10 packs of different colors and a spoon. They look even more beautiful in person!

My kids love this set! It comes in a cute school bus container that includes wheels inside that hook right on. There are 36 different colors to choose from and it has lots of tools and accessories included. It also comes with an instructional booklet which shows you how to make things. The clay dries fast, but it’s cool as you can play with the figures once they are done drying. It’s the perfect indoor activity and great for all ages!

These wood slices smell sooo nice! Like fresh pinewood! They are perfect for all kinds of art- I plan to use these for resin art, but really the opportunities are endless. You can use for coasters or even ornaments.

This marker set is so nice! What I love about these are that each marker has 2 ends: 1 fine tip and 1 thick tip. They also come in a nice carrying case. The only issue is that it’s a bit difficult when putting the markers back under the bands in the case. It’s a bit of a struggle, and can be frustrating. Overall, they are very nice though, with this one tiny flaw.

The 12 piece glitter set is so nice. I got this for resin art, but you can use it for so many different purposes. I love that each container has an option to pour, or sprinkle the glitter. The colors are so pretty!
This watercolor paper is super heavy duty and I love it! You get 2 tablets in the pack (each with 30 pieces of paper). If you love to paint, you need this paper in your life.

This set of flowers is very nice! Perfect for resin art. You get so many colors and tweezers for easy application. We are using these on a project and will update the review once our project is complete. 🙂

This sketchbook by Creative Mark Reflections is very nice quality. The outside covers are heavy duty and embossed. Inside is 80 sheets of premium acid-free, 110 gram (70 lb.) weight, light cream colored paper with a fine even surface texture. The paper is heavier than standard paper, yet extremely smooth. My children love art and this book is perfect for them to draw in. This sketchbook would make the perfect gift for an artist.

My kids love making figures out of modeling clay. We have purchased many through Amazon, however this brand seems to be the best quality and the lowest price! It’s moist and easy to mold, and it dries quickly! You get 36 colors, along with different accessories and tools! This is a fun activity to do indoors!

This diary is really cute! Poppy troll is on the front with fluffy hair. It even comes with a lock and key. It also has a bunch of fun stickers. The only downfall is the marker/pens that come with it are not the greatest. The purple was already dried out and didn’t work.

My daughter loves everything about art and this set is so much fun for her! You get so much with it: stickers, stampers, markers to color with, glitter glue, etc. Any child would love this set! Very nice quality!

This fabric paint set is awesome! You get 30 different colors so the possibilities are endless! You also get a bunch of different sized tips which allow thinner or thicker lines! The bottles are thin and very easy to hold. Amazing quality set!
Solid product. I would purchase it again.
I got my money's worth on this. Thumbs up.
These really work for me in my business. Thumbs up!
It works pretty well. Very durable. I'm happy with my purchase.
Liked it very much and it wasn't that expensive.
A very fine acrylic. Worth the purchase,
You don't have to be a scrabble player to admire the craftsmanship of these letter tiles. But if you love scrabble, it's a bonus!
Works as advertised. I'm very pleased with this purchase, and will buy it again when needed.
Excellent use in my drawing, reasonably priced. I'm very happy with them.
I got my money's worth on this. Absolutely a good buy.
These pens are fantastic! Easy to get started and use!
Good kit
I like this for T-shirt’s I put tshirt over it and it helps center and keep wrinkle free
Arrived as wxpected
Nice set
I like this for my happy planner used to refill my orginal
Cute set
Bought to compare with a higher price for card making with watercolor
I didn’t like that they are brush pens - I thought they had a fine tip to use for writing
Perfect fit replaced lost one
Great value. Bought for gift for tweens
Very pleased with this product.  Thank you.
Very pleased with this product.  Thank you.
Very pleased with this product.  Thank you.
Just as promised.  Excellent.  Thank you.
Just as promised.  Excellent.  Thank you.

The "instruction booklet" is really not - a 4-"page" tiny illustration of how to put in the cartridge, that did not refer to the black plunger things already IN the pens. And a single VERY SMALL quarter-sheet showing a couple of different script styles also does not qualify as an instruction booklet, to my mind. The pens themselves are mediocre quality.
All in all, NOT a good introduction to fountain pens or calligraphy in general. I'd send them back if I could, but in my attempts to make them functional I made them non-returnable. Ah well, at least they WERE cheap.

LOVE these pens.  They've become my go-to for notes on the household calendar, in my planner where I need a fine point to put lots in a small space. Very well made, no smell, and the ink is excellent quality.
So I'm set for the next several years!

It would have helped if the thing didn't have a block at one side, so you could center pages more precisely.  But it does work reasonably well, especially if you want to do several sheets or a thicker paper.  For one or two pages, the Franklin Covey clear plastic punch is a bit easier to use, and half the price.
Easy in the hand, smooth flowing, durable tip and the ink does not bleed or smudge.  I find the 02 size exactly perfect for everything from shopping lists to meeting notes.
Too small to be useful, very flimsy design
The actual cat hair part is not quite as extensive as I'd hoped, but it will still be a very nice little present for a crafter friend who, like all-thumbs me, is a cat lover.

These are NOT extra-fine point, or even fine point, they are medium point, and not great quality either, very mediocre.  Not quite worth sending back but definitely I won't be buying this brand again.  I'd recommend saving your money and buying something better from a physical store where you can actually try the pens and get a sense of the quality.
Not what I expected
Bought this for a friend that loves pottery. He was extremely happy how his pottery looks and how great the product was. He is buying more to satisfy his need for making pottery.

Bought this as a Christmas present for a dear friend and he was absolutely thrilled that he received this. He used it the other day and said it really worked well for his project that he was working on.
These buttons are of the highest quality. I lost a button on my peacoat, I purchased these, what a difference! I love them! Highly recommend!

As an artist, I use a ton of paint. Liquitex has always been a go-to paint for me. I was very pleased with this jar of paint, which was sealed under the cap. Upon removing the seal, it was filled completely to the top. I use white more than any other paint, and I will definitely be purchasing this jar again.

I really love this kit! I just wish the bottle was more like a writing ink bottle... I'm terrified that if it gets knocked over, it's going to look like someone was murdered... this ink is truly blood red! It is powerful stuff...
versatile! Love these patches! I'm covering the top of my comforter with patches! And I loved these little critters!

These scissors blew my mind!
They are very sharp, fit my hand well, and now maybe my kids will leave my pink ones alone... but probably not. They just cut so well. Couldnt beat the price! I've paid the same for just one pair. Thank you!

Lynx is one of my spirit animals. We relate through our senses... it is difficult to find cool Lynx items. This patch... cool! I wanted to honor Lynx, once on my jacket, lynx will go with me everywhere.... although, already kind of does.
Ohhhhh... fierce! Tiger is one of my spirit animals! I loved this patch! It says, "Yeah, I'm beautiful,  but mess with me and you'll be my dinner"! Growl

Stencils come in handy for many things... I just started making bullet journals, and that is why I bought this pack... lots of different letter styles, holidays,  boarders, ect. Totally worth the price!

I was always an acrylics painter, but started using water color paint, then water color pencils, so I wanted to see how the pens worked... these are great! They even come with a practice pad with their web address to watch a tutorial. I have found that I really love water colors, who knew? I would like to thank the company for making it so easy to learn!
This paper is excellent for the price, it's hard to beat. I've used all forms of mixed media, and havent had an issue yet!

I love these beads. I used them to make a mala. There was just the right amount of each stone to have it balance perfectly. I get many compliments. They are all genuine. I feel very safe and grounded when I wear it!
This is pricey, but if you have a specific project, its excellent. There are several varieties of moss that add texture and color...

We go through a lot of tacky glue in my house, buying 3 discounted bottles only makes sense. The last thing i want to have to do in the middle of a project is have to run out for more glue because we ran out.
This is an excellent assortment of eyes. My daughter and i use them a lot. This was a really good deal!
Its a staple for a crafter... i happened to use mine for wrapping around pieces of toothpicks to make worry dolls. Vibrant colors.
There is such a lovely variety of beads in this pack... i was very impressed.

Im a crafter... feathers are essential for crafting with a little girl. They tickle your nose, and color and texture. These were nice size feathers too ive gotten them from other companies, and they are too, too small.
Oh Singer, I love you! Your merchandise is so good. This pack of needles will last me a long time. Stay sharp, beautiful stitches. Variety pack for different types of cloth.

I have been a fiscar scissor purchaser in the past, but i though i would try the Singer scissors because i like their other products. The large ones cut through my fabric witjout flaw, and the smaller set is perfect for snipping threads (and a stray hair or two).
I got these for scrapbooking, but found the tip way too fat for writing notes. I use them for other things... but i was sad they didnt work for me...

I paid a little more for quality. Ive learned that these straight pins will last me a long time. Easy to grab, and if one falls on the carpet... you can find it easily. I grew up with Singer products, i wont use anything but...
If you sew a lot, you cant pass up this quality thread! Worked well on my machine. Held stitches tight, with little breakage. There is a lot on just one spool!

The needles are good! Ill get a lot of use out of them... but the case... not so much. I like having my needles seperated for different projects. The box tends to seperate, wont turn, and needles get stuck under the case. The price was very reasonable... but i would have gladly paid 50 cents or so more for a case that would work nd last as long as the needles will... just saying.

I made reward coins for my daughters bright achievements
Now Im making prize coins. When she does her chores... she gets a coin. Then i have a little store with dollar store items she can "buy". Incentive. Bonding. Learning how to use money... starting a tradition for her to do with her own children some day!

I use these gems on sooo many crafts... the stick on their own, and only need a dab of glue to hold onto items that will be handled rough. The problem is, my daughter keeps "borrowing" them from me! Lol  Keep making more, ill be back!

My teenager "adopted" my sewing basket. So when i started looking for a new one, i saw the name Singer, and i knew it would be quality. I was right! Bonus! It even came with items as a starter kit! Lots of room for small and large items, and the fabric is adorable. Ill have this a long time, or at least till my 7 year old turns 16 and "adopts" this one, lol.

I made so many items out of this fabric! I made my daughter "Rachel Danger" (we are starting her a kids youtube channel) a vest and a sachel. I also made a sachel for my teenager and myself. I used the scraps to make tons of pull-string bags.
Fabric was easy to work with and looks like real suede!
I am using these for badges for kids, and jewelry pins... different sizes, these will last me a while
My daughter and I have made so many great projects! Felt is so easy to work with, and this is quality felt. Thick enough to hold with glue or sewing.

Ok, so this purchased reminded me to read well before purchase. These felt squares were 4 x 4 inches! My daughter and i laughed so hard when they arrived.... but, we found all kinds of uses for them in our crafts.  We made Tibetan prayer flags, camps for the hamsters, and even bonnets and cloaks for her dolls! They were handy!
I love to scrapbook, and these pens work very well for identifying photos on various types of paper!
The tiger is my spirit animals. I put this on the cover of my scrapbook. I recommend this item 4 shirts jackets or anything!
