Good product
Not wild about these! Made from cheap fabrics!

What can I say about bells? They’re colorful, gave a good tone & they're cute to put on ribbons to put on your guests as they enter your home for Christmas. And, who said ‘only cats should be belled!’
This product worked well for my Christmas decorations.
Functional!
Good product!
Cute!
Functional!
Used this one to make some charms and pins. Very easy to use and reasonable price for the amount.

I’m not sure how “real brush” suppose to work. I have some different brand brush pens to write calligraphy and they feel more sturdy than these Arteza ones. These Arteza brushes have a very weak flimsy feel and you cannot put any pressure on it. It’s really hard to use for me, because no pressure means no ink, but putting pressure means bad looking brush stroke. Not for me.

I’d say this is a 3/5 product and it’s not Premium at all. The wood handle doesn’t feel heavy, and the brush hair are not thick and sturdy enough to hold paint like premium brush should do. I paint a lot, used many type of brush and Im sure these works fine to do casual art practice, but I can tell they won’t last for long.

I am doing my second painting from this series. Quality materials and beautiful sparkle when finished. Plenty of drills provided. I bought small plastic containers from the dollar store to store and label drills. Makes it easy to organize and use. I like having one of these out to work on when I need to be quiet and do a mindless beautiful craft.
The material worked very well on my sailboat in part as a waterproof seal for where the mast enters the deck to prevent ocean spray and rain coming through the deck below.
Super easy to use and awesome quality buttons! Can't wait to make buttons for our Comic-Con table. And you can buy other size metal bits to make bigger buttons!
I love my new sewing basket and I received it quickly and in great condition. Thank you.
So soft and perfect for making masks, also replaced waist elastic on a dress with this and it works perfectly.
I bought this for jeans making but find that I use it all the time for pressing.
This is a great pattern. I made one muslin, according to my measurements before cutting into a more expensive fabric. These pants came together so quickly.

Woo Hoo!!!!
I LOVE this Invisible Zipper Foot! It was easy to install and worked beautifully the first time I ever added a zip to a dress.

I don't think the pictures included helped me to figure out how you need to rotate the zipper so that it all works, but (luckily) my pattern did tell me how to rotate the zipper to make this a true "Invisible" zipper.

UPDATE: I've had these picking shears for over a year now and can give a better review. These shears can't really handle layers of cloth. I was using these to grade my seams, but they don't hold up. I'm not grading denim- sometimes it is voile or rayon. And forget cutting a seam!

I am new to sewing- so maybe I don't know what I am talking about.
I was sewing a jersey skirt for a Fit and Flare dress and, I don't know- I just never got it right. I ended up removing the elastic and just using the stretch from the jersey top to create the "flare" part of the dress.

Holy Cow! I LOVE this product! I am new to sewing and have tried other marking pencils or chalks but this one is THEE BEST. At first, I tapped the chalk out but after a minute, I realized that you are just supposed to "write" a straight line and the wheel dispenses the perfect amount of chalk on the fabric. I will ALWAYS have this in my tool basket.

I am a beginning sewer and started to make my own clothes. This wash away tape waw something that I read about on a sewing blog and I thought, "Duh- this makes so much sense." The tape is not absolutely necessary but it does make things much easier.

I bought this case to keep all of my gel nail polish items organized. I was so pleased that the LED lamp fit in the bottom compartment and all of my polishes fit 2 per slot- UNTIL I tried to collapse the trays. The trays will not shut completely with bottles of polish in them!

This must go back.

I like the size of this set, I can drop this in my purse to carry it with me. FYI- there is no black paint, which is kind of a pain. Also a pain- the lid won't stay propped open; it closes on its own all the time.

I ordered this set for my preteens, who are obsessed with paint markers. And these three work just as well as the name brand ones do. I didn't know how useful the big one would be, but it's excellent for fast coverage. The ink/paint inside the markers seems like nice quality too. We like them a lot and I would certainly buy other colors from this brand.

We have a house full of artist, and one of their favorite art activates is to paint and leave those rocks for others to find. So my kids were thrilled when I pulled this "refill" like rock set out of the Amazon box. It is a nice sized set too; we received enough rocks for my girls to paint several rounds. The rocks were plenty big and nice quality. But know that I called it a "refill set" because there are only rocks in this box. It doesn't come with paint brushes, paint, or anything else to decorate the rocks with.

This is a nice refill set that comes with a lot of colors. And you get plenty of strips too. The paper is nice quality and this should last us quite a while.  My teenage girls are learning to quill with me, so we were excited to find this kit! It arrived very quickly too.

This clay was very fluffy and I would describe it a lot like cloud slime. It's quite the colorful set and my girls were more than happy with it! Usually we order the harder, polymer type clay (the kind that needs to be air baked to harden). But this is interesting and fun product too.

The things we didn't like:
-While this kind of clay doesn't stick to our hands, we noticed it does stick TOO GOOD to some things. If it even accidentally touches another color, they stick together like glue. And of course you'll still need to be careful around fabric things like carpeting.
- The colors are hard to get out of the plastic without leaving some of the product behind.

- A couple of the bags were expand to the max, from excess gas. Meaning something was probably growing a couple of the bags.
- You don't get very much of each color either. Each piece being about the size of a golf ball.
- Some of the tools fell apart (the pastel colored long ones with the white heads)

So I am torn. The set seems like a decent deal and you could definitely get quite a few projects out of it for the $10 it was listed at when I wrote this review. But on the other hand, I promise I would order it again.

EDIT: I HAD TO REMOVE THE FOLLOING LINE - "The plastic wrap each color comes in is NOT resealable, so my girls blew through a box of sandwich baggies to store each individual color (remember the colors can't touch)"
THE MANUFACTURE DID INCLUDE RESEALABLE BAGS AND WE MUST HAVE LOST THEM.

This zipper case has been bouncing around in my 13yo backpack for a month now and it is holding up great. The plastic is thick and flexible; and the zipper seems like excellent quality. I was just looking at it this morning and it still looks new. It really is a nice, sturdy bag. I would buy it again, for sure.

I ordered this for my 9yo and I am so happy that I did. It absolutely looks like a ton of a fun and something any creative kid would love. BUT now that this kit is here, I wouldn't consider this a true "beginner" set. I suspect it is not something she is going to want to do independently either.
After reading the instructions and seeing all the colors parts in person, it feels like there are a lot of steps for a beginner. Before we do this exact project, I am going to run to my local craft store and buy a roll of fun yarn to practice some very simple, basic weaving on the loom. Otherwise I think she will get overwhelmed and frustrated that her work isn't turning out like the picture. But overall, I am still happy we found this set. It's adorable.

I ordered this tape for my kids to hang up their art work, without damaging our walls... and it has worked perfectly for that. It's strong, but fun & colorful tape. Plus it comes off both the walls and their art masterpieces without any damage. This was an excellent find and we're really happy with them.

I really like that this painting came on stretched canvas. Others that I have ordered off of amazon came rolled up and creased, and damage like that to a canvas never goes away. Plus this being pre-stretched saves me the hassle of having to frame my creation later, lol.
The set was nice too. The paint was pigmented and thick; and more than enough was provided to complete the project. But know the paint is permeate and will ruin your clothes. The brushes included are fine, but I did end up using my own because I like small ones. Overall this was a nice set and it took me about two weeks to finish. I would have to say it was a good value for how much entertainment I got out if it.

I recently took the advice of a friend and ordered this hem tape.... and it really was so much easier than pulling out my sewing machine. The first time I used it, I wasn't very confident; so I took my time and went slow. But since then, it using this product has saved me a heaps of time. Since I've used it to fix my husband's business pants and to hem up four curtain panels so far. Highly recommend you try it.... it's totally worth $2!

Wool painting has been one of the most fun art projects I've done in a long time; so this was the second time I have ordered a kit like this. It's a really unique way to create art and I am in love. Plus, it's incredibly easy for beginners to learn how to do.
Just know that mine look different because I ordered additional colors of wool from Amazon, so I could personalize mine more. I also twisted and braided some of the yarn, to make some different patterns. And used some of my kid's old Crayola markers to add even more color and shadowing. It's really easy to customize these pieces. Plus it's also really easy it is to fix a mistake in felting (you can just grab the felt and pull that section you don't like back out).
I took away stars because this kit could be better all of the needles broke. I had back ups needles on hand.... but had I not, I would have not been able to finish until I spent more money. Hate that. Plus the one Styrofoam block that is included isn't really strong enough to last through two scenes. Also, I like that they have a visual reference, but the (two) 20 minute instruction videos on YouTube is unnecessarily long.
Overall, I am still very happy that I got to try this kit. It really was a ton of fun and I can't wait to do more felt painting projects in the future.

I ordered these for my oldest child, since he is extremely into drawing at the moment. And I am really happy that I did. The markers really are excellent quality. Even after a month of use, not a single one of them is starting to dry up. I am beyond impressed and think you get a huge variety of different sized pens at a pretty decent price.
But know that the ink is not permanent. They do bleed/smudge if you try to color over them with another ink marker. But I still found them to be an impressive and excellent value.
For reference, I considered myself a professional artist before becoming a stay at home mom. These days I consider art a hobby.

My husband is 3D printing off some game pieces, so I ordered these paints to decorate them. And I am happy to report this set was a decent choice. The paint isn't the thickest, so it didn't do an excellent job of coating. It took several layers for me to get to opaque. If I had been suing these on stretched canvas, I wouldn't have been as happy with results.
The colors are bright and metallic enough though. And I do love the range of colors you get in this set. The paints dried fairy fast, so there isn't a lot of time to blend. I don't think I would buy them again simply because they were not pigmented enough for my liking.
For reference, I considered myself a professional artist years ago before having children, and now art is a hobby for me.

Before I became a stay at home mom, I considered myself a professional artist. I was lucky to have collected half a dozen "Art Bin" storage containers over the years and I loved each one. They worked so well, that I decided to order this box for my artistic teenage daughter. And I couldn't be happier that I did.
The quality of this box is really nice, in my opinion. The plastic drawers aren't quite as sturdy as the Artbin's of yesteryear; but they're still more than sturdy enough. I suspect this bin will hold up excellent. The lid opens and closes easily and the rivets are smooth. Plus this bin is a good size.... it's as big as it looks in the pictures and it holds a lot more than I expected it too. Overall, I have nothing but good things to say about this bin. It was an excellent find.
Hope this review was helpful.

This clay was very fluffy and I would describe it a lot like cloud slime without the hand stickiness. But yet, it's sticks too well to some things. You can't even put the colors near one another without them sticking together. And the colors are hard to get out of the plastic without leaving some of the product behind. And the plastic each color comes in is not resealable, so my girls blew through a box of sandwich baggies to store each individual color.
You don't get very much of each color either. Each piece being about the size of a golf ball.
I ordered this for my girls and both of them said they preferred the harder polymer type clay (the kind that needs to be air baked to harden).
So I am torn. The set seems like a decent deal and you could definitely get quite a few projects out of it for $12. But on the other hand, I can't recommend a product I wouldn't order it again.

The only thing I didn't like about this set is the wool mat does have some loose fuzzies. My felting projects have picked up individual felt from the pad. It's not a deal breaker, but is slightly annoying. Plus I usually break 1 or 2 needles every night, so 8 extra needles really won't last me a long time.
On the positive side, the leather finger protects are made from real leather. And they're wide enough to fit thicker fingers. And the wool pad is plenty big and more than thick enough for my needs. It's a nice set.

I ordered this electronic scissors to help on trash day and I am really happy that I did! I've had far fewer broken nails and paper cuts tearing up cardboard boxes and other plastic recyclables. For the most part, these scissors have met or exceeded my expectations. They've been useful when opening clamshell packages so far too. They are louder than I expected, but I don't mind. These were an excellent find.

This is a really nice, sturdy set of upholstery sewing needles. While I do not need to use them often, I can promise they are incredible useful when the time comes. These types of needles are excellent for stitching up a torn piece of fabric that can't be moved. These set is really nice and were an excellent find. I'm very happy with them.

I am rather impressed with this set. Everything pictured arrived as promised and in nice shape. All of the pieces seem like nice quality too. And the finger guards are made of real leather, so they absolutely protect well from finger pokes! But they also might be too small to fit all fingers. I have average sized hands for a female and I had a really hard time getting one on my thumb. This set was an excellent find and I am happy with it.
Hope this review was helpful.

This was one of the most fun art projects I've done in a long time. I've never felted before, but now I can say I am in love! It's a really unique way to create art. Plus incredibly easy for beginners to learn too.
I personalized this "painting" too. I braided and twisted some of the yarn, to make some different patterns. And used some of my kid's old Crayola markers to add even more color and shadowing. Plus I like how easy it is to fix a mistake in felting (you can just grab the felt and pull that section back out).

I took away stars is because of YouTube only instructions and my needles all broke. The YouTube link is not direct, it is to a channel. None of the titles are written in English, so there's no searching.... so expect to scroll through lots of their videos until you spot your art project. And then it's a 20 minute instruction video, which felt unnecessarily long.
And like I mentioned above, all of the needles broke. Two in the first few minutes of felting, so I was trying to be extra careful with them. But the other three didn't last. Now I have to wait for more to come from Amazon and I'm not always patient person, lol. But I also don't like having to spend more money to complete a project either.
Overall, I am still very happy that I got to try this. It really was a ton of fun and I can't wait to do more felt painting projects in the future.

I considered myself a professional artist years ago, before I took a break for kids. But I have a house full of blossoming artists, so these pencils have been an awesome addition to our collection.
My positives: The colors are bright and vibrant. They center lead is strong and rarely break. And the pencils are made from an nice wood that sharpens easily.
My negatives: I would not consider these professional grade colored pencils. They do not blend or fade as easily, like a Prismacolor or Faber Castell pencil would. And they leave a lot of fallout (color crumbs?). The lead is very waxy, so they tend to leave a sheen.
Overall, I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a professional set of artist grade colored pencils, you'll be pleased with these. They're a great hobby set and you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck, in my opinion. I would purchase this set again for my kids.

My husband is 3D printing off some game pieces, so I ordered these paints to decorate them. And I am happy to report this set was an excellent choice. The paint is thick and did an excellent job of coating. The colors are bright and highly pigmented. The paints didn't dry too fast either, so I was really able to blend them easily. I considered myself a professional artist years before having children, and now art is a hobby for me.

I considered myself a professional artist before I had children, and now I have a household full of blooming child artists. So I can happily say this tracing paper has been really useful. It is thick and sturdy; and we haven't had any sheets tear on us so far. I'd purchase tracing paper or other products from this brand again, for sure. This pad was a fantastic find.

I ordered these for my teenagers and they are beyond thrilled with them. I considered myself a professional artist before kids, so naturally our house is overflowing with art supplies... but even with so many options available to them, these watercolor pens rose to the top of my girl's favorite items. We've had this set for about 6 weeks now, and I still catch the girls drawing with them a couple times a week. So my girls are getting their "money's worth". The ink colors are pretty and bright; and every pen is still juicy even all these weeks later. This set was a fun find and we're all really happy I picked it up!

I had a lot of fun completing this set. The materials were nice quality and I appreciated learning quite a few new tricks. The only thing I didn't love was I felt the instructions were hard to follow. The instructions are simple a series drawn pictures. I ended up on You-tube watching visual tutorials, and that helped immensely. But I still had a good time.
The good news is you get more than enough supplies in this kit. Plenty of string and needles are included. The treading tool is also essential, so I'm happy that was there too. Overall, I would still have to recommend this kit.

This is an excellent set of measuring tapes.... they really are. They seem sturdy and well made. I compared these tapes to several rulers I had around the house and can confirmed that the measurements are correctly printed. Overall, I think these are excellent and a great deal! Highly recommended!!!

We have a house full or aspiring artists, so these glue sticks have been a huge hit. Plus, I'm happy to report that these glue sticks dry clear, as promised. Plus the smells are unique and fun. This new line of glue sticks seems to work every bit as well as original glue sticks in our experience. They're unique and absolutely fantastic. My kids and I would absolutely highly recommend them.

I considered myself a professional artist before I became a full-time stay at home mom. Pastels weren't my go-to medium, but I still was lucky enough to have a nice, hefty collection of Rembrandt and other artist-grade soft pastels. So I can confirm these Paul Ruben pastels are very nice. They are impressively smooth and they blend beautifully. The pigments are very bold. Plus the pastels are a decent size, so the set feels like a good value.
However, I found the colors choices included in this set a bit odd though. This is probably not what I would have started with if I was looking for a beginner set. But if you're looking to grow your collection, I would recommend you consider this set. I can confirm they are impressive and ­ nice quality. And my little PSA: Don't forget to wash your hands after playing with artist's grade supplies.... especially before touching your food! (sorry, that was the mom in me talking)
Hope this review was helpful.

I picked up these organizers because my 9yo is obsessed with diamond painting and I am so happy that I did. These little guys have made a world of difference in helping keep her beads together, and all in one place. They plastic the cases is thick and they feel like nice quality little containers. They're very easy to open one handed too I could see these being useful for a wide variety of projects. We've definitely impressed and would have to recommend them.

I picked this set up for my 9 year old because she is a true artist at heart and also loves making people gifts. Miniature sculptures and jewelry are among her favorite things to make, so this clay has been been one of her favorite presents so far this year. The clay is bright and colorful, and she has more than enough colors to choose from. It's soft and easy to work with, as well. And I haven't notice it staining any clothing or her hands. We both have nothing but good things to say about this product! I would highly recommend this set! It was an excellent find!!
(In the photo, my daughter regrouped similar colors and opened a few times f them before I could take the photo. One light sky blue shade is missing too. Your set will arrive with colors mixed up.)
Hope this review was helpful.

I considered myself a professional artist before I became a full-time stay at home mom... now art is more of a hobby of mine. I picked up these markets to try them out with journaling and list making. And I am happy that I did. I can confirm these marker pens are nice for hobby tasks. Most of the markers are impressively smooth and tips glided beautifully. The pigments are very bold too. I say most because two of the markers (the lime green & the light purple) were partially dried out and gave me some trouble. The other thing that bothered me were the color choices for such a small set. After using them for a while, I'm really bummed there is no true orange, or brown for that matter. But with that said, I still like this set a lot. I would recommend you consider trying this set if you find the color pallet appealing. Overall, I can confirm they are impressive marker pens and are fun to use.

This is a really nice set of skin toned markers... it truly is! We have a lot of hobby/student artists in this house and we love markers, so we all found this set to be a fantastic addition to our art supplies! These markers are juicy and blend easily. Plus the colors are bright, pretty and true to life. We also like the case. It's well made and helps my kids keep these Ohuhu markers all in one spot. Overall, I think there latest set is a really good value. I'd have to recommend it!
Hope this review was helpful.

My daughter has been getting into diamond painting, and if you don't have a proper storage solution, this can become a giant mess in no time.
This case has been a lifesaver! It has 70 large tubes for diamond storage, in a nice padded case. This is obviously the most important part, but the included accessories are nice too! Having extra wax and pens is nice. The collapsible funnel speeds up clean up a TON and makes filling the containers much easier! She has also been getting a lot of use out of the mini trays.. dump out the color you are working on, and when you are done, dump the tray into the funnel, into the tube!
The best part is that this setup has made her self-sufficient so she isn't constantly needing to ask for help. This set is really well made and we're happy to have found it!
~~ I hope you found this REAL review helpful! ~~

I'll begin by saying that I love Arteza. I considered myself an professional artist before I became a stay at home mom, and now I have a house full of mini artists in the making. I have owned a ton of their products, both purchased and gifted to us via the Vine program. And we absolutely love a messy art projects. So I expected this kit to be a huge hit. And it was. We had a blast. My pre-teens and I had a ton of fun making this pour art over spring break. But gosh, not $80 worth of fun.
I don't usually like to comment on the price, but cause a good deal can be subjective. But for what in get in this kit, I just feel you could pick up all the pieces from a craft store (at the retail price) for less than half of what this set cost. This set was listed for $80 at the time of this review. Honestly, I know my review might be a little harsh. But I am just not that impressed seeing as I already had most of the individual products included in the kit, on hand.

We have a ton of artists in this house. So this HUGE brush set was truly a fantastic addition to our arts and crafts table. The brushes have been getting a ton of use lately, and I am happy to report they seem well made. Plus you get more than enough brushes for nearly any project you could think of. Oh and I'm happy to report this set arrived quickly too. We're really impressed and couldn't be happier.

This product is really cool! I've been wanting to try moldable plastic for years... and now that I have, I'm so disappointed that I waited this long. It's really easy to use and the possibilities are endless. The first thing I made was a pen stand, so I could always have my favorite writing utensil upright and visible; not lost to the clutter on my coffee table. I then covered my plastic creation in glitter and added a clear top coat to make it pretty. And I couldn't be happier.
So here is what I learned:
I did not have a food thermometer on hand, so I used the 5 stages of boiling water method to guess what when I was close to 150 and it worked out great.
You'll need something disposable to pick up the melted glob, like a toothpick or a straw. The water is a little to hot to touch at 150 degrees, so please wait a few seconds for the excess water to drip out of the glob before you grab it. While the water was to hot to touch, the plastic was not. I had a couple of minutes to get the shape I wanted, and then a few more minutes after that to smooth down the rough edges. The plastic dried hard and super solid, btw.
Oh the instructions were not kidding. This stuff REALLY sticks to nail polish. It will ruin your manicure. And it makes your polish impossible to get off, even with soaking. Removing it even damaged the top layer of my natural nail. So pretty please do not ignore this warnings on the label like I did. It doesn't end well, lol.
Anyway this product is fun and unique.... and easy use. I'm happy to have gotten to try it. If you have a small project in mind and are eyeing this product up, I would definitely have to recommend you try it. It's fun, too.

This is a really nice set of markers. We have a lot of hobby/student artists in this house and we love markers, so we all found this set to great addition to our art supplies! These markers are juicy and blend easily. Plus the colors are bright and pretty. We also like the case. It's well made and helps my kids keep the markers all in one spot. Overall, I think this latest set is a good value. I'd have to recommend it!
Hope this review was helpful.

UPDATE: I took away stars because the perforated edge is simply too weak. A lot of the pages have detached all by themselves simply from opening (and closing) the covers and flipping to a blank page. It's frustrating, since I would prefer the drawings to stay inside. I highly recommend carrying masking tape for reinforcement
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ORIGINAL REVIEW:

Overall, I think this is a nice drawing pad. The paper quality looks good throughout the book. It would be excellent for a lots of quick sketches because you got a lot of pages.
But I don't think I could recommend this to older serious art students (college aged) and above. I don't think this one would travel well in a backpack. But it would be excellent pad for younger student artists. Especially with it being bound on the shorter side. Overall it's a nice drawing pad and I'm happy I came across it!
For reference, I considered myself an "real" artist way back in the day, primarily focusing on drawing and painting. I ordered this drawing pad for my 11-year-old and he was beyond thrilled.

My 8-year-old was thrilled when I pulled these out of the Amazon bag. She's a little artist and hasn't stopped using these... Nearly every day for the past couple of weeks. They really are a ton of fun and have helped her imagine alll sorts of new creative possiblities. All of the scissors we received in the set were well made and all the sissors close. She hasn't complained about any of them being sticky or too hard to use. This was an excellent and fun find.... We couldn't be happier with this set!

My 8yo artist has really been into sculpting this past year and had used up all of her black. So words can't explain how excited she was when I pulled this out of the amazon bag. It arrived very quick too. I would say the product is as described and acts like any other modeling clay she has ever had. She's almost out of red, so I'll be back to buy from this brand again.

This is a really cool set. I picked it up for my older children (8, 11, and 13) and it is perfect. They're more than enough tools to go around. We recently got a pottery wheel; but they've also come in handy because my 8-year-old is still really into sculpting. The variety of tools is impressive. I would certainly recommend you consider this one.

I am so happy to have found this sewing machine. I would consider myself a hobby artist and beginner seamstress with a slight advantage. I used to watch my mother sew on her vintage Singer growing up. She was good, but this was an old hobby of hers. She could sew wedding gowns, but it only happened on occasion. So I only retained enough to do little things like hem pants or make an easy Halloween costumes. And I am certain this Singer will be perfect for me.
Overall, this machine feels really well made. I am happy to report I am truly impressed with the quality. This machine feels sturdy and think it will work excellent for many years to come. Also, I like how compact, portable and light weight this Singer is. And how it stores away nicely. It is small enough for me to keep under my bed!
I like to set this sewing machine up on my coffee table to use it. But I could see using it at the kitchen table or up on a counter also. The cord is long enough for me to be able to use it anywhere.
I’m finding the machine easy to work with so far too. I was proud to tell my mom that I haven’t busted any needles learning to use it, lol. I don't know if I needed 24 stitch patterns. I'll probably only use stitch patterns A, B and C. But who knows. Having pretty stitch patterns is fun and exciting, so I'm hoping it will encourage me explore.
Overall, I am very happy with it. I found the instructions easy to follow and the machine’s buttons self-explanatory. Again, easy for me to say since I slept next to a vintage Singer for 20 years, lol. But I also haven’t owned one of my own, so I am so happy to have found a modern version. This fits into my life. This was an excellent find.

I considered myself a professional artist years ago (before kids) and I think these are perfect for hobby artists, art students, or older kids. The wood seems solid and I did not see any warping. The canvas is nice quality too and stretched tight. They're really nice and I certainly would come back to repurchase these.

From the moment I pulled this out of the Amazon box, my four kids were thrilled! I actually was too, because this wheel ended up being a lot of fun!!! We've had it for over a month now and it is by far our 8yo daughter's new favorite thing. I would consider her a true artist. But the other three kids still like it an awful lot; and one of the other three pull this wheel out at least twice a week in addition to how much the 8yo uses it on her own. The only thing I didn't "love" about this is kit is that I didn't anticipate how quickly we would need a clay refill! But honestly, I will happily buy clay refills for a toy that is actually getting used vs one that is plastic junk or one that sits and collects dust!
I used to throw pottery back in high school, so it was even fun for me to use as an grown adult decades later. My older children don't necessarily need the shaping tools, but they like using them occasionally. This throwing wheel is pretty powerful and sturdy feeling too, especially for being a children's toy. The plastic is thick and durable. Overall, I am really happy with this kit. It was an excellent find. Truly. My kids like it so much, I've saved the link. I can't wait to buy this for their cousins and friends in the future! This will make a memorable gift, for sure.

These are like aqua beads, which my older kids love but are so tiny, they're difficult for little fingers to use. These being bigger, sit up straight on their own and don't tip over easy. And I love that the fuse part doesn't include me having to break out the iron. All of my kids have tried these beats out and had fun together from start to finish.
But what's different is perler beads sets usually come with fun shaped trays. Like a heart or a dog's shaped pattern. So this set doesn't necessarily promote the type of creativity original perler beads do. I still think there are a lot of fun though and I would buy them again, for sure.

In an effort to reduce screen time, I've insisted my kids start doing art projects. My 13yo daughter wanted to try diamond painting and she is absolutely loving it. This set came with everything she needed. The materials are nice quality and the gems are sparkly bright. I am happy to say she has stuck with it and is almost finished already. And when she is done, this canvas looks like it will be easy to hang. The only thing I didn't love about it is that it came a little wrinkly, but after laying it out flat for a few days under heavy books, it has stayed that way. Definitely consider this one.

Before I had children, I considered myself an "real" artist, lol. But these days, watercolor hobby painting is about all I have time for at the moment. And these brushes are perfect for my current needs. They hold and lay down almost as much pigment as my top end Windsor Newtons and other fancy name brand brushes. They distribute the color evenly as well. If you're in the market for higher quality brushes but aren't ready to $40 on individual brush yet, I would definitely recommend considering this set or this brand. These were an excellent find!

I can't tell you how thrilled my kids were when I pulled this out of the Amazon box. I ordered it for my 8-year-old but my preteens were so interesting, everyone wanted to try it! Myself included!!!
I really like that the set came with everything we needed. I've considered myself an artist since birth, and somehow I've never dabbled in paper craft. I've seen it hundreds of times at the craft stores; but like any new medium, it felt intimidating. I appreciate that everything this beginner family needed was included in the box. The instructions were easy to follow and the materials are high quality. The only downside was that we we all couldn't do it at the same time, lol. It was a ton of fun and I definitely recommend trying this one. Any one of my kids would have been thrilled to receive this as a gift. We will now be paper quilling for years to come!!!
Hope this review was helpful.

I picked this necklace up to add to my husband's Halloween costume. It's pretty nice and shiny, But up close you can tell it is made out of inexpensive materials. I think it's going to work perfectly for what I need it for. I would come back to buy more if I needed them.
Hope this review was helpful.

This kit has gotten a lot more mileage than I expected. We've had it about a month and we're probably around 2/3rds of the way through already. This project set is pretty cool in person.

My 8-year-old daughter is having a blast putting these together. She's a very arts and crafts, kind of girl... But she also has a lot of fun building things. This Scotch kit is kind of a creative mashup of the two. And even though I picked it up for her, it had all my other kids jealous immediately. The others often sit down "to help her".

Initially I like that everything that is needed came included in the set. But leave it to kids to turn anything into a bigger project than it needs to be, haha. This has them running around the house collecting anything and everything to add to their scenes, lol. They're even repurposing and upcycling clean trash. Honestly, the only thing I don't like about this kit is that the kids don't want to throw any of their projects away when they're done! They're paper scenes, so they're obviously fragile and not realistically something we can keep around. So every few days we "say goodbye" to one of their scenes with some destructive effort. They love destroying them like they're Godzilla, using some plastic toy.

Overall, I like that this is a fun way to get the creative ball rolling. This kit provides hand-eye coordination activities, STEM thinking and even requires a little bit of patience at times. Plus it's simply fun. Any one of my children would have been thrilled to receive this as a gift. And I hope Scotch puts out more kids like this. We all definitely recommend this one.
Hope this review was helpful.

This is a huge set, at a great value! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house, so this set is awesome! The pastels feel nice and blend easily. Plus you get so many colors. The case is nice and durable, and keeps the pastels nicely organized. I'd recommend this set. It's a good beginner set for preteens or teens.
Hope this review was helpful.

I got these just to have a physical representation of the virtual currency I already have. Sometimes crypto can get so "theoretical" and virtual, its nice to be able to hold it in your hand, if only for peace of mind.
Nice design, good quality. GO GO CRYPTO

Being that Bitcoin is a digital currency, it is hard to feel the "realness" of it sometimes. It is nice to have a physical representation of what a Bit COIN would look like. I know that there were very limited actual physical Bitcoins early on, but you can't find those now. This looks very close, and is a good substitute.
The coins are well made, if not a bit lighter than I imagined. They say they are 1oz of brass, but it feels a bit lighter. Overall, very fun and I am carrying the silver one around with me in my pocket.

Smells like apricot and peach to me! I like it a lot and it was a great price.
Highly recommended!

At one time I considered myself an artist before I became a stay at home mom, so I was really excited to pick these up for my older children. They're showing a lot of interest in new areas of art and art supplies; but I'm not ready to share my very expensive, artist-grade art supplies with them. I am happy to have gotten to try this set.... it really is an excellent student grade product, in my opinion.
The sticks themselves are sturdy and we didn't find them to break easily. Just the right amount of product comes off the stick (not too much or too little). And we found that these blended well using our fingertips. The colors are very opaque and highly pigmented. I would recommend this set, for sure!!!
PSA: People often ingest pigments and possible toxins on accident, by touching their food. Please encourage kids to wash their hands before eating, once they've moved out of the crayola art supply stage.

Quick Summary:
Excellent quality markers at a good price. Great for hobby and student artists, maybe not for professionals. I wouldn't buy them for myself since they're not refillable or individually replaceable, like Copics. But I would buy them for others.

LONG REVIEW:
I considered myself professional artist years ago, but art today is now a hobby. The one thing I spoil myself with is professional grade art supplies, so I am also lucky enough to own a complete set of Copics. I use them all the time. I had Prismacolor Markers before the Copics.
So I was excited to try this Everblend set. Overall, they're very nice markers in my opinion. I'll be hanging on to these for my older children to use. My pre-teens have outgrown traditional Crayola markers, but I'm not quite ready to share my Copics yet. I would consider my kids beginner student artists, and am happy to say they do take their art seriously. It will be nice to teach them with high quality markers.

Pros:
- The colors are amazingly bright! Every bit as pretty as my Copics.
- They blend beautifully!!!
- They are comfortable to hold and to use. I usually don't like triangle shaped art supplies, but these fit nicely in my hand. I never noticed the shape when I was using them. The extra bonus is these markers don't roll!
- Almost all came "juicy" and full of ink! Some noticeably more full than others. Some noticeably less; a couple (2 or 3) were already starting to make that dry marker squeak.
-Colors: Some very close dup's to Copic's lineup. The dark red, purple and light blue were nearly identical to some copic colors. The light pink and gray's did not have identical matches, but very close still.
- They're affordable!!! Copics are well over twice the price.

Cons:
- They are not refillable. This is my biggest disappointment with these markers. And why I would probably not chose to buy them for myself. They're less environmentally friendly when they're non-refillable too. I hope Arteza has plans to change that.
- Technically they are not individually replaceable either; at least not at the time I wrote this review. You can't go buy one marker if you loose one or if it dries up. Or add easily to this set. The Arteza site might sell 4 pack replacements of just one color shade ($8-16 for four, before s/h). I say might because it's confusing. Only the names and pictures line up, not the product numbers. For example: Lilac is P25 in my set, but listed as A884 on the manufacturers site. This is a new line for Arteza, so this bullet point might become moot.
- No brush tip. I love and use the brush tip side 10x more often than chisel and fine tip markers. Also why I feel the Arteza's aren't for me personally.
- They were bleeding more than my Copics. Keep in mind these were brand new markers and my first time using them, so I was probably heavy handed. Also, my Copics have not been recently re-filled. Of course Copics bleed a lot more when they're freshly filled too.
- The marker box is thick and sturdy. And the rows don't tip over easily. But gosh, I hate that there is no "bottom" to this box set. If you don't remember to pick up both sides from underneath, one half comes sliding out. And when you're done using them, the set is a little tricky to put away.
- The angle of the the cap can make the names and numbers hard to read while using them.

I think had I not owned Copics already, I would have adored this set. It truly would fit my needs as a hobby artist, if I wasn't so darn picky. I really would recommend this set for a lot of artists! This was my first time trying any Arteza product and I am impressed. Nice quality, for sure!!!

This duo pack is an excellent value, in my opinion. This paper is thick and has held up to a variety of supplies. I've mainly used this paper to make homemade cards for friends and family. It holds up well to my artist grade watercolors and copic markers without too much buckling. I'd recommend it!

What a beautiful set... at such a great value! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house and so I've been buying these black and white oil markers for at least three years. The come in handy around the house for quite a few things, beyond just art projects. Anywhere a regular sharpie won't cut it, basically. I didn't know they came in rainbow color set, so we're beyond excited. I'd highly recommend them!!!

Like a lot of people, we've spent most of 2020 at home, social distancing. One of my favorite new hobbies this year is cross stitching. I've only done a couple so far, but I really like this set the best so far! The colors are pretty and the quality of the materials is good. It will take quite a while for me to finish three, so I consider it an excellent value!!!
This set comes with (almost) everything you need to get started. But if you're new to cross-stitching, I would strongly recommend picking up a needle threader. You can get them on here or I saw them for only a dollar in my local box store's sewing section. A threader will save you so much time and stress, plus will ensure this project remains fun!

This set is such a great value! We have a lot of pinterest loving hobby artists in this house, so this starter kit was perfect for us! My daughter and I each had a couple of easy, starter macrame ideas saved and this set gave us every thing we needed to do a couple of projects. The string is nice and thick; and was easy to work with. The decorative beads are cute and my daughter had fun using them in her dream weaver design. And I knotted up a potted plant holder for our bathroom. We both had a lot of fun.... I'd recommend it!!! Can't wait to try more macrame projects in the future.

This is a such a huge set at a great price! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house, so this set was awesome for us!  We mostly use them for rock painting so far, but the possibilities are endless. Sometimes the paint is a little thin and you have to go over the same spot twice. But I've found that to be true of all acrylic paint for the last 30 years, lol. You get so many colors in this set, there is more than enough for my kids to share during project time without fighting. I'd recommend this set!!!

We have a lot of hobby artists in this house, so this set was awesome for us! It is a big set at a great value. We used them for a variety of holiday projects and rock painting so far, and they're working out great. The markers feel well made and the paint is pretty thick. Sometimes you need to go over spots twice, but that is common with any acrylic paint. I'd recommend this set!!!

This is a nice set at a good value at the time I wrote this review! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house and we love markers, so we all found this set to be awesome! The markers blend easily and the colors are pretty. Plus you get enough of each color for virtually any project. The case is nice and keeps the markets all in one spot. I'd recommend it!!!

This is a huge set at a great value! This is set was perfect for us because we have a lot of hobby artists in this house of many different ages! We used them to do a variety of holiday projects this past holiday season to help the moment feel special (as we continued to socially distance). My three kids each painted a picture for their grandma; and the rest I used to paint holiday season images to place around the house. The canvases are well made and very sturdy. I'd recommend them for both student and hobby artists alike!!!

It's the end of 2020 and we've spending a lot of time at home due to Covid. So I am loving DIY paint by numbers art kits right now. This painting is very nice and so pretty! I'm having an awesome time painting this one because the colors are so bright and pretty.
The paints and the brushes that come in this set are nice. The brushes are holding up with very little fraying so far.
The only thing I don't "love" about this set is the canvas came folded. There are creases that I will never get out. But this is my 10th paint by number set around this price point this year and all of the canvas have come folded. II expected it so I didn't deduct a star.
Overall, I think as long as you're realize you are buying a cool art project, you'll enjoy this set a ton. This isn't a heirloom piece of art you'll be hand-me-down to future generations. It's simply for fun, in my opinion. Paint by numbers can be time consuming and take quite a while to finish, so you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck with this set in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again! Highly recommended!!!

I'm love DIY paint by numbers right now. And this one is very nice and so pretty! I'm having an awesome time painting this one because the colors are so pretty. smile the entire time I'm painting it
The paints and the brushes that come in this set are nice. The brushes are holding up with very little fraying so far.
The only thing I don't "love" about this set is the canvas came folded. There are creases that I will never get out. But this is my 9th paint by number set around this price point and all of the canvas have come folded. So it was expected and I didn't deduct a star.
Overall, I think as long as you're realize you are buying a fun art project, you'll enjoy this set. You are not purchasing a heirloom piece of art you can hand-me-down to future generations. And since paint by numbers can be time consuming and take quite a while to finish, you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again!

This is a large set and I think it's a great value! I considered myself an artist before I had kids and now I have a house full of student & hobby artists! My preteens are eyeing up my professional art supplies, but they're worth thousands of dollars. I'm not ready to share. This set has been an excellent happy medium. My older kids feel like they're using "adult art supplies". I've noticed they're taking drawing far more seriously since receiving this too.
So many things come in this set, there is more than enough to share. The pencils were nice and sharpen easily. You really do get everything else you need to start sketching and blending. The case is nice and folds up well. We'd all recommend it!!!

This is a big set at a great value in my opinion! We have a lot of hobby artists in our house and we're 9 months into the 2020 Covid lockdown. We've been doing an ton of art projects to keep busy; so this set has been awesome! I mainly picked them up for rock painting, but I have used them for a couple of different things since then. The colors are bright and the paint is pretty thick. Sometimes I do have to go over some spots more than once, but it didn't bother me since I've always found that to be the case with most brands of acrylic hobby paint. I'd recommend this set!!!

This is a great set! You really do get a lot of tools. It is 2020 and we're 9 months into Covid lockdown. We have a lot of hobby artists in this house and and one of the things we've done to keep busy is rock painting. My kids love to hide their rocks on our neighborhood walks or hikes on the trails for others to find. We've been doing this for a while now, so this dotting tool set was a fun way to change up our designs! And easy and fun for all the kids of all ages (preschool through preteen). Plus you get everything else you need to start dotting. Besides rock painting, I borrowed one from the set to do a little nail art for a holiday manicure. I'd recommend this set!!!

This is a huge set at an excellent value! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house, so this set is awesome! My pre-teens are a little past the age of wanting to using crayola, but I am not ready to share my Copics. The markers are a nice substitutions for students or hobby artists in my opinion. The colors blend pretty well and are bright. The case is also nice too. I'd recommend them!!!
Excellent set of "student-grade" or hobby artist brushes at a nice price! I'd recommend them!!!

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers right now and this one is very nice! The colors are so pretty in this sunset painting. I've had sets from this company before and their paints and the brushes are nice. Their paints are thick and the brushes hold up with very little fraying.
The only thing I didn't love about these kits is the canvas come folded. There are creases that I will never get out. But I've done eight paint by number sets this year around this price point and every single one come folded.
So overall, I like the set a lot. I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a hand-me-down-heirloom artwork but rather a fun project, you'll be very pleased with this set. These DIY paint by numbers can take quite a while to finish... so you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again.

I was a professional artist before I had and family (of eight) and still paint whenever I find the time. I would consider myself more of a hobby artist these days, but still really enjoyed using this paper.
As advertised, I found it to be a true "cold press" paper. My artist grade watercolors (a mix of Holbein, D. Smith and W&N) laid out nicely and as I expected them to perform on this paper. I would buy this paper again for hobbies art and practice! Highly recommended!!!

This is a nice set at a great value! I was a professional artist before I had kids and as a result, we have a lot of hobby artists of various ages in our house. We all love to paint. This set has been awesome for our whole family. We are all enjoying using it.
I would consider this paint "student grade" or good for hobby artists. I expected these Pearlescent paints to be a bit more sheer as than other similar quality watercolors we own (any pearlescent watercolors I have ever tried over the last 30 years of painting have been thinner side). But you still get a good variety of colors and some are still quite bright. Plus, the paper that is included is also a nice smooth, cold press, type of paper. And you'll still have plenty of paint left over after you've used up the paper, I promise! I'd highly recommend this set!!!

Wow! This is a huge set at a great value! We have a lot of hobby artists of various ages in our house, so this set was awesome for us. The pipe cleaners are good quality and came in a variety of nice colors. You get plenty in this set to create a ton of projects. I'd highly recommend this set!!!

These are an excellent value! The colors are bright and the paint is thick. The set comes with a nice variety of colors too. I can't recommend this set enough! My kids have been using this paint several times a week for so many different projects and I'm certain I'll be back to buy more soon!!!

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers right now and this one is very nice! My 6yo wanted me to paint one for her room and this one was too perfect. The colors are so pretty, so I'm having extra fun time painting this one. The paints and the brushes that come in this set are nice. The brushes are holding up well so far with very little fraying.
The only thing I didn't love about this set is the canvas came folded. So there are creases that I will never get out. But this is my eight paint by number this year (2020, social distancing) and sets around this price point have all come folded. Overall, I like the set a lot. I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a hand me down heirloom art and more of a fun, time consuming project, you'll be pleasantly pleased with this set. These DIY paint by numbers can take quite a while to finish... so you get a lot of entertainment "bang for your buck" in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again. So pretty!

This is a nice set of detailing brushes at a great price! I found the sizes of the brushes to be labeled correctly and the and shape of the brushes to be consistent with other brands I already own. They're holding up well so far. I would recommend them, especially if you like this type of grip in a brush!

I liked this set a lot. The selection of fabric that I received felt well made and the fabric was thicker than I expected. My kids and I mostly do hobby crafts and sewing, so this selection was a welcome addition. The colors are bright and held up well to a round in the washing machine. I'd recommend this set!

This is a huge set at a great value! We have a lot of hobby artists in this house, so this set is awesome! The pencils feel high quality and sharpen easily without breakage. Plus you get everything else you need to start sketching and blending. The case is nice and folds up easily. I'd recommend it!!!

It's November of 2020 and Covid has kept most of us home now for 8 months. I'm obsessed with keeping myself busy with all sorts hobby art projects, including adult coloring books. These pencils have been an awesome addition to my collection.
The colors are bright and vibrant. Plus they sharpen easily and rarely break. Overall, I like the set a lot. I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a professional set, you'll be pleased with these. They're a great hobby set and you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again.

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers right now and my son wanted me to paint him one to hang in his room. This one is very nice and I would consider it perfect for beginners! The paints and the brushes that come in this set are good quality. The brushes are holding up with very little fraying so far. And the paint is thick, so I'm not finding myself having to repaint some areas twice.
The only thing I didn't "love" about this set is the canvas came folded. So there are creases that I will never get out. But this is my seventh paint by number set around this price and all have come folded. I wasn't surprised. Overall, I like the set a lot. I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a hand me down heirloom art and realize this is a simple, fun, art project, then you'll be very pleased with this set. These DIY paint by numbers take quite a while to finish, so you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again.

My daughter (and I) are really into painting rocks to leave for other to find on our walks. These paint markers have been an excellent addition to our art supplies. They're bright and easy to use. And the ink dries very quick. Sometimes we have to go over the same spot twice, but I've found that to be true of all paint markers (even the name brands).  I look forward to using these on more projects in the future. Highly recommended!!!

I really like these paint brushes!!! It's fall of 2020 and like most people, I've been spending a lot of time at home due to covid. Painting is my new hobby and I am obsessed.
I am finding these paint brushes to be incredibly useful! I've been using these nearly every day for a two weeks and am finding that they are holding up great. They are keeping their shape and I am seeing very little fraying. Plus this set is so big. It comes with a huge variety of brushes. I would recommend this set! It's a great deal.

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers right now and this one is so nice! I'm having a lot of fun painting this one because it has so many pretty colors. The paints and the brushes that came in this set are nice too. I'm finding the brushes are holding up with not too much fraying so far.
The only thing I didn't love about this set is the canvas came folded. There are creases that I will never get out. But this is my 7th paint by number set around this price point and all of the canvas have come folded. I've come to expected it, to be honest.
Overall, I like the set a lot. As long as you're realize you are purchasing a fun project and not a hand-me-down heirloom piece of art, I think it's a great value. Paint by numbers are time consuming projects. And while they will take you a while to finish, you get a lot of entertainment "bang for your buck" in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again. Highly recommended!

I picked this up to make homemade watercolor cards to some friends and relatives who I know could use a special pick-me-up this coming season. This paper is great! It stretches surprisingly well and is pretty smooth in my opinion. But know I also prefer a smooth cold press over rougher paper. It was easy to sketch my design on top of an erase without damage. At the time of this review, I would consider it a good value at the price listed. Highly recommended!

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers right now and this one is very nice! I'm having a fun time painting this one. The paints and the brushes that come in this set are nice. The brushes are holding up with very little fraying so far.
The only thing I didn't love about this set is the canvas came folded. So there are creases that I will never get out. But this is my sixth paint by number set around this price point and all have come folded.
So overall, I like the set a lot. I think as long as you're realize you are not purchasing a hand me down heirloom art and more of a fun, time consuming project, you'll be pleased with this set. These DIY paint by numbers can take quite a while to finish... so you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. I would purchase from this company again.

I'm obsessed with DIY paint by numbers at the moment and this one is just too pretty! I'm having a nice time painting this one. This set in particular has really nice thick paints, so I'm not finding myself having to repaint over a lot of the spaces. Repainting is really time consuming, so I appreciate that! Two of the three brushes are holding up pretty nicely too.
What I didn't love about this set:
- It came very tightly folded, so I will never get out all the creases (that's very common with these types of sets).
- I've come across a dozen or so spaces that are unnumbered so far. They're very tiny spaces.
- Part of this painting comes prefilled with the gray spaces already filled in and I'm not sure why. Plus there is a tub of paint labelled "zero", but there is no zero printed anywhere on the canvas (so I'm assuming this is the paint I'm supposed to use to cover the preprinted grey or maybe the unnumbered tiny spots).
- The largest of the three brushes fell apart immediately. The bristles just fell out.

So overall, I like the set a lot. I have tons of extra brushes and I'm used to getting creased canvas at this price point. I think as long as you're realize you are purchasing a fun project and not a hand me down heirloom, you'll be pleased with this set. These DIY paintings can take quite a while to finish... so you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck in my opinion. Despite the cons I listed, I would honestly purchase from this company again.

This light board is amazing. Its absolutely huge while not being too heavy or cumbersome. It feels like it is made out of a thick, durable Plexiglas like acrylic material. And I love that the plastic top has no boarders (that helps from crinkling your paper on accident). On the highest setting, the board is ridiculous bright... almost too hard to look at bright. But the brightness is easy enough to adjust. And it recharges really quickly. I can't recommend this light board enough!!!

I picked up this kit to do with my daughter and we had a blast! The felt is nice quality and the instructions were easy enough to follow. I do have a lot of experience with sewing but this was the first time I've tried a felt. It is a super cute kit that is great for beginners. And since several projects are included, I think it's a great value. We both highly recommend it.

I picked this up for my 10yo because she was excited to learn how to start sewing "like her mom". Plus she loves cats! I thought this would be a great way to learn how to handle and learn to manipulate a real needle for the first time and so far, she's doing great!
This art kit has been a ton of fun for her so far. She is so unbelievably proud. But know I do have to help her knot the string the first couple of times and have to be available to help her undo mistakes (like when she gets the thread stuck). My extra tip would be to pick up a needle threader for a dollar at your local box store... this made a huge difference in letting her do most of this project on her own (i.e. separate to thread it herself, thread the needle, etc).
The kit comes with plenty of extra thread, which was appreciated since we all make mistakes when we are learning a new skill. I also think it's a great value, especially seeing as how it is going to take her a ton of hours to finish. I'd highly recommend it!

This is a really cute origami set! My oldest was introduced to the art last spring (before the schools closed due to Covid) and has been asking to do more ever since.
We're going into our six month in lockdown and the school have announced they are remaining closed. Now that I know they won't be going back this fall, I'm desperately trying to find new activities to help keep our sanity. This kit has perfect!
The funny thing is I did lots of origimi as a kid, so I honestly thought this would be easy for me to show them. But I'm having to go back and relearn too. Which has been actually fun for me as well. Just know beginners will probably need to start at the beginning of the book with the more simpler designs.
The paper that comes in this kit is colorful and thick. The instructions are easy enough to follow and the folding techniques easy enough to learn. My kids and I are having a great time doing this together. And they're so proud of their creations. We would all highly recommend this kit!

After seeing someone recommend this in a facebook group, My husband picked it up for minor touch ups some of his vintage collectible toys. Mostly transformers, I believe but he has tried it here and there on other toys. He said it worked great and stuck to the plastic well.  The color was a great match. Again I emphasized he only used it on very minor, small spots.  Nothing major. He would recommend it.

I love everything this Starry night castle mash-up!!!
I'm an artist, or at least I was before becoming a stay at home mom. I temporarily quit because before kids, one of my paintings would take me over a month to complete as it was. Nowadays, I suspect one project would take me over a year to finish! So I've turned to Paint-by-numbers like these, as well as other art kits, to scratch my artistic itch.
I'll start by saying you get a lot of entertainment bang for your buck with this kit. This canvas is huge! I pictured it next to a soda can for size reference. It's going to take a while to complete!
The paints are a decent quality and dry quick enough for when you need to paint a second layer to get full opacity. They came vacuum sealed, which ensured they didn't dried out before I opened them. I've been using the brushes that came with this kit for several days now and I have had no problems with them fraying or coming undone yet (take that with a grain of salt. Artists are taught how to properly uses and care for brushes to prevent damage, since our brushes often cost between $25 or $50 or even a $100 dollars). If you find yourself sticking with this, you'll probably want to go pick up a a couple of extra detail brushes for a few bucks using a 40% coupon at your local art craft store. I also really like when I'm done painting for the night, clean up is a breeze.
I'm not sure what else to say besides it's a ton of fun! I can't wait to put the kids to bed every night so I can get back to painting!!!!

What I love: Beautiful set!!! This thread is silky smooth And so it's going to work great for my embroidery projects! It came complete, with all the colors, as advertised. I also appreciate that there is a duplicate of each color that I can store away in a drawer... I hate it when I have to leave a project unfinished because I ran out of one colors to finish it (my favorite craft store is half an hour away, I only get to go every few weeks).
The downside: While there is wonderful assortment of colors, I would say it's lacking in an array of reds and blues. And the four browns are pretty similar in color. But it's not a deal breaker, since you get so many other colors.

This is a nice kit! With that said, I took away a star because I feel the colors in this would be better suited for embroidery art than as a friendship braclet kit. There is a beautiful variety of browns and greens; and there is a nice selection of reds and yellows. But many of the blues are similar in color and it's lacking in the oranges and purples; colors I think a pre-tween would love the most. And there are no greys. Not to mention the the scissors and the pin needle wheel do not fit inside the plastic box.
On the positive side, the thread is silky smooth and the storage box is high quality. And I love that the thread already comes pre-wrapped around a bobbin and is organized by color. It means I can jump right into my project without having to organize thread for hours. And you still get an amazing array of colors in this kit. I would consider this a great buy!

These were a bit smaller and weaker feeling than I expected. I picked them up because I like to make my cats homemade feather toys. I especially appreciated that these were natural, not dyed. I expect my cats to play rough and break down all feather toys eventually, but these simply won't hold up for my particular need. With that said, you still get a lot of feathers in this pack (a lot for the price these were listed at the time of writing this review, that is). I'm guessing most people will have completely different uses in mind, so take my review with a grain of salt.

Easy for her to pull up the edge of tape from the roll and even easier for her to tear/cut off without needing my help.  That's extra bonus for her since she's very independent.  She's probably used them for a 100 different projects so far. It's not super sticky tape.  This tape feels a lot like that blue painter's tape but still holds well enough.  Allows her to display her art work on her bedroom walls without leaving residue or to undo the tape without damage when she changes her mind.  Great buy for little artists.

Nice enough that I came back for a second one. Down a star because both came wrapped in two thinly padded bubble wrap envelopes!!! It's a metal tin guys, so no surprise both trays arrived dented. The manufacture, and then amazon, should be using real boxes!!! Luckily, the dents on mine were mostly minor and cosmetic. However, the mixing pan lid on one did have a big dent!!! I was able to flatten this major dent back out so that it would close properly and the mixing tray was still useful. Overall, it's a nice product... but know you're running the risk of having to deal with return.

PAN SHIFT: Yes, the pans shift. They need too, lol. You have to be able get two fingers around each pan to pop them back out again. Here are some ideas on how to get around this minor annoyance.
1) As suggested by others, magnets glued to the bottom of pans might work if they are thin enough. 2) order extra empty pans and squeeze one more into the dead space = becomes very tight row of 13. But keep in mind you'll have to pop out an outside pan every time you want to take get to a middle color pan 3) by chance, I discovered that my pans stay in place fine because I had made little cardboard dividers. I did it to visually the pans into groups of three. 4) shove just about anything small into one end to keep them from shifting around! and I mean anything.... a heavy magnet, a Lego brick... or heck, even a marshmallow, lol 5) as pointed out by another reviewer, you can bend the metal tabs in. The pans will still wiggle a bit, but not shift.

I have used this 100% roving yarn before. I'm going to use it this time to knit and then felt a pair of slippers.

I have used this pattern twice to make a wrap dress one in a solid fabric and the second in a geometric pattern. The style is very nice and flattering. Like most Style Arc patterns the instructions can be a bit cryptic. I had a tricky time with the collar. But after watching a few online sewing videos, it turned out fine. Great dress.
This item does what it is supposed to do. I used to hate having my sewing machine foot pedal slip around when I was sewing. Now with this pad under the pedal, it doesn't move around by itself.
Cute dress. Easy to follow pattern.

I needed these for a holiday gift for my family.  These silver and gold cord accents really made the pieces pop!  A great value for a lot of cord.  They had a beautiful metallic sheen and sparkle to them.  Very little stretch, they held tension well and when cut they frayed very little.  Well made, sturdy product.

I had a burn hole in my sleeve for years that was 6" x 4" and jagged on all sides. Two rows of Tenacious Tape side by side over lapping on the inside and outside did the trick! I doubled up on each side in order to really make sure it adhered and was more translucent and months later it's still on there with no peeling! I'm super impressed by this product, I didn't think it would work as well as it does!

Read the descriptions for the colors, get the fabric or reinforced nylon tape!!

Nylon Tape - Black, Red, Orange, Yellow)
Ripstop Nylon Tape - Green, Off White)
Plastic/Vinyl Tape - Transparent

The plastic transparent tape apparently crinkles and and fights the original material. The nylon based tapes are like your outdoor gears original material and move with it more naturally. I got the off-white ripstop tape and you can see the interior ripstop material grid embedded in the tape for strength. So far it's held up beautifully, great product, I highly recommend to repair your gear and give it a second life!

I was used this to make a feather head dress for a party and it ended up looking great.  I used this to cover where all the other feathers were glued together around the base, it acted as a sort of trim to conceal and blend all the vertical feathers.  It worked great!  But I agree that the feathers were a bit more sparse than advertised and smaller in size.  Still, for the price and my needs this filled out the look and is still a nice product.  But for the price I'd set you expectation about 30% lower than the photos and thats what you're likely to get.  I'd still recommend this, feathers are pricey, but be realistic.

I'm a fabricator, I'm always trying to interpret designs from the page into reality.  This quick to use angle finder is a must have. Whether I'm transferring angles from the architect to a 1x6 or translating something in the field to the piece I'm creating I have to reference and compute angles everyday.  This angle finder has multiple reference lines to accommodate your piece and can be flipped or reversed and still give you clean information.  The wing nut locks the arms in place for moving from one material to another.  This is a no fuss tool that any carpenter, welder, fabricator should have.  Mine gets borrowed all the time in the shop.

I had to do repair work on a taxidermy bear where I had to reconstruct a fair amount of the jaw and face which had been broken off in a fall. Simply knead together equal parts of each component and then apply like clay to whatever surface you're repairing. It is tacky enough to adhere with pressure but still smooth and pliable enough to be worked with for an hour or so before it begins to set up.

I've used this to repair chips in ceramic mugs with great success. After applying just smooth it with a wet paper towel to remove any excess and bring the epoxy sculpt flush with the surface of the piece.

Very easy to use and endlessly craftable. A great product that hardens beautifully with plenty of working time to get it right.
There was enough straw in this mini bale to pad out 4 cat boxes.  Worked in a pinch.  But if I needed a miniature bale of straw this one was pretty cute and fun.

I needed to make a cast for a concrete planter and it couldn't have been easier.  Just mix equal parts A and B, stir vigorously and pour.  The resulting silicone mold is rigid enough that it's made it through a dozen uses without tearing but pliable enough that I can get the concrete form out without issue.  Good stuff, works great, very easy to use.
It is too heavy and uncomfortable to use.

Needed handles for a bag I crochet & these are perfect!!! Great size!!! The finish was so pretty. Great quality!
Find me on Etsy.....Sunny in NY Creations.
These bags are a big hit!!!

Well, I'll go first.....
Not really happy with these. Needed bag handles for bags I make & these were pretty in the picture.....but the ones I got are a little dusty & the finish is less than impressive.
Debating if it's worth the grief to return it.....or try to use them......

Bought these to use for embellishing picture frames we painted & to be honest, they are way smaller than expected. But they are so cute! Perfect for my project. And yes, I would buy them again!

We bought the birds & flower set. Very cute!
Bought this for storing Barbie's shoes for my step-daughter.  Perfect fit!  While they were smaller than I realized, they were still perfect for the job.

Bought this for our 6 year old who loves painting her face. She likes being a mime so the white was used up first.
They colors were good. Very bright. And they wash off really well. We used baby wipes & then soap & water. It didn't dry out or irritate our skin.
I would definitely get this again!

I bought this to store my colored pencils. Someone pointed out about the little gap at the bottom, so I just keep my pencils points up & life is good.
