This product CAN work, but it isn't the miracle cure we all want this kind of thing to be (so maybe it deserves more than 3 stars?). I had a tear in a fairly new winter coat and attempted to fix it with Tear Mender. This was a tear in the outer layer, and I wasn't sure how well this product would stick to the fabric. I decided to follow the instructions for mending nylon fabric and first glued a piece of cotton cloth to the inside. This worked, but the glue is much thinner than expected and kind of ran all over. In the end it DID fix the tear, but ended up being a bit more messy than I anticipated and my patch jog was incredibly visible. The other issue was that because this product is pretty runny it soaked completely through and I ended up gluing the pocket closed. Thankfully I was able to pry the pocket open, but that resulted in the undoing of my patch work. I will definitely try this again after I remove some of the glue from the first attempt, and I think it will be much more successful the second time.

I am used to Cricut and Siser HTV brands. In my opinion, both of these brands are much easier to weed. If you are used to these brands, you should also know that this brand has a thicker, more matte transfer film AND it is not sticky. I expected those things to be deal breakers, but they ended up not being.

The fact that this transfer film isn’t sticky DOES make it trickier. However, the fact that the film is thicker seems to prevent it from moving around as much as I expected. Surprisingly, I ended up liking the thicker film with regards to heat pressing as I can apply more heat, but it is difficult to remove. Unlike other brands that do better removing when a little warm, this brand DOES NOT. It really does need to be WELL heated and then fully cooled to look it’s best.

The vinyl of this brand appears to be thinner that what I am used to. I also though this was going to be a downside, but in all honesty, it created more of a professional “screen print” look rather than a “I made this shirt at home” look. It also makes it easier to overheat and shrink. This is not a product for the beginner.

It definitely is more difficult to weed and apply and the risk of error is far greater than some other brands, but for a professional finish (when done correctly) I think it does a great job!

Product worked very well. As a warning, though, I added more that was necessary and ended up with a color significantly darker than teal. It is better than what I started with, but not what I wanted. I recommend dying a sample if possible to start.

Ordinarily, I don't write a review of a paint by number until I have completed it, but this kit is an exception because there are three excellent features that the seller does not mention. First, the canvas is color coded to help you find numbers. Second, the paint is rich in texture and covers without touch up, and third, both a clear photo a and fully detailed printout of the canvas are included.

The result of producing this painting was pleasing. After I framed it, I gave it as a gift. The colors were vibrant and were easily applied with little thinning with water. The four stars reflect my level of satisfaction with the three shades of yellow paint which required multiple applications.

This painting was so relaxing and fun to work on that as an experienced painter I almost felt guilty. The colors are vibrant and it is numbered adequately. This is the first work I have ever mounted on canvas. I used a blank 16x20 frame and it stretched snuggly. I usually glue my blank canvases to understatement and make my own frames to finish them. This painting did not need framed as it is very whimsical. A gift for my granddaughter
This painting was a positive experience. It will be a gift. The paint covered very well, and the various shades of purple were deep and rich resulting in a vibrant work.
So much intricate detail made this a favorite challenge to date. The paints were very fresh and did not require much thinning. The paper copy of the canvas to-scale was extremely helpful.
This was a breeze to paint, and it turned out much prettier than shown in the image online. It isn't framed yet, but it will be a nice gift for a family member.

The colors seem a bit "off" in this kit when compared to the photo advertising it online.  I expected it to be much brighter and the girl's face is really hidden by shading.  To me, the painting lacks light.  Even the duck is grey.  The kit is a bit short on the several of the dark colors, so I had to mix and match some other acrylics to finish.  One other drawback was the fact that there is no paper printout of the painting in the kit.  I took several photos of the canvas with my cell phone before I started painting, and I am very glad that I did because the difference between some of the colors is barely detectable.  Overall, however, it looks like the image in the ad, but the colors are definitely off.

My first thought when I saw this was "what a strange thing".
Turns out, I love it! I do a lot of wool work, like candle mats, runners, and even larger works. This thimble really helps! When it gets tough to pull the needle thru multiple layers I can just pop the thumb piece on and pull the needle through. I don't use it the whole time I work since the piece that fits on my thumb tends to flop around. It is easier for me to use by putting it on as I need. If I'm at a particularly difficult place I will just leave it on.

Beautiful, quality Pearle Cotton.
I use this for wool felt projects and it works beautifully. Not prone to tangling, good price per ball.

These toggles work great with elastic cords on masks.
Easy to get the cord thru, doesn’t pull my hair.
Easy to get ahold of, really nice for working with wool felt appliqué. Small, precise piecing is easier with these pins. But please note that they are shorter. They are more of a specialized tool.

Hooray for this gadget! Every once in a while I find a gadget that works....and this is one of them! I can use it on my sewing machine that threads from the left and on the one that threads from the right. It works from both directions.

Really great scissors! Small enough to fit in my portable kit and very, very sharp. Especially sharp points that are really good for getting accurate cuts on wool felt. Lots of finger room in the handles. Love the serrated edges on the blades that help keep the fabric from slipping around while I cut. And they are great for embroidery work too!

Very nice scissors. These are a good in between pair of scissors. I have the embroidery scissors which are smaller but these are the perfect size for cutting out shapes from wool felt. They grip the fabric as I cut so I have much more accurate shapes when I am finished. The 7 1/2 they are talking about is the overall length including the handle. It is not the blade length like other scissors are measured~but they are really wonderful. They are not to long to be very comfortable while I use them and they are very sharp. The point on the end are sharp too......be careful!

These bars shipped immediately when I placed my order and arrived two days later from New York! (I'm in AZ)
They arrived safely packaged in a bubble wrap envelope. The bars are the perfect size for my use. I will use a couple of them for sewing where they work well to keep the thread from knotting and the others for cosmetic making. The wax is good quality when I pull the thread thru it pulls nicely leaving an even coating and no clumps. Would definitely buy again from this seller! Better quality wax than I have seen in a long time!

Was a little concerned that these bobbins might not work in my machine. No worries! They work great in my Juki TL98. I have only used bobbins with cardboard sides and always removed one of the sides before putting them in the bobbin case. No need to do that with these plastic bobbins. They worked well for both piecing and free motion quilting. Pre-wound bobbins hold a lot more thread so there is less winding and changing the bobbins. I would buy these again if needed but I expect it to be a long time before I need them!
I will purchase again.

I'm a beginner. I picked this one because of the pad. I don't want canvas cluttering my house while I learn. I plan on using both sides of the paper. I tried a lot of colors and brushes and have no complaints with any of them.

This tool is great for the hobbyist. Cuts cleanly through styro foam and leaves no crumbles of foam. Let the heated wire do the work . Don't pull or put pressure on the wire just guide it for best results. Winons also has great customer service. I highly recommend this tool but read all instructions and use is safely because the carving wires do get very hot.

These markers are precise and very easy to use.  Excellent shape and size adds to ease of use.  I now own these in ALL colors made!  No more chalk or pencils for me, these are simply the best markers out there.
I use this batting exclusively on all my quilts.  It is extremely easy to work with and wonderfully natural.  Not too heavy and not too light, just perfect.

This ruler is a great addition to my quilting ruler stash.  It is an excellent size that enables you to work with it very easily, and take along with you.  Markings on the ruler are done very well and extremely helpful.

Love this product as I now never, ever run out of thread when sewing or quilting!!!  I purchased this thread in black, neutral and white and it is excellent.  Perfect for quilting projects that require lots of thread to be available!
This case is great and allows me the ability to store two different size rotary cutters inside.  I am also able to store the instructions for blade changing as well.  Great case and very portable!

This is just a basic "start to sew" set of tools.  I purchased two of these sets for some young girls interested in learning to sew.  These basic sets have most everything a beginner needs.  Wonderful value and quality.

Absolutely great buy!! I ordered these to tie down stoles, scarves, jackets, saris etc. They are very well made, beautiful and the pin is sharp enough to go through fabric safely. The different sizes are great for assorted applications. Going to order another set for a gift.

Got this to for a Kenmore sewing machine to replace the motor drive belt that is no longer available.  It's an adequate generic solution.  A round stretch belt like this one works fine, but it does not grip as well as the original v-style belt, so sometimes it will slip if I push the machine a little too hard.

It's possible that all CA glue is essentially the same. But several things make ZAP products my choice. 1) Variety of formulations. ZAP makes different "thinness" formulations, and I use them all because each has useful applications. This thin formulation is very thin, and is consistently so from bottle to bottle. It is thin enough to wick into cracks and seams by capillary action, which is exactly why you want the thin formula. 2) Packaging - the bottle has both an inside and outside cap, and they seal so well that the glue lasts a long time. Other brands dry up pretty quickly, but not ZAP. Also, It comes with a long, thin applicator tip, and if you clean it up after each use, can be reused over and over. 3) Adhesion - I've never run comparison tests, but it is as strong as any similar product I have worked with.
It’s oversized and easily holds poster prints and larger sizes.  It does not have a firm/sturdy insert so you will need this

We bought this to do some Christmas crafting, and the quality of the paper itself is a pretty good quality, it’s heavy paper and the sparkle is pretty nice and evenly dispersed.
I only had two issues. 1, folding is not something that works well with this paper, the sparkle all kind of falls off when you fold 2, if you cut roughly (like kids do) or accidentally tear (like kids do) the sparkle basically kind of peels off in that area, so it impacts how usable the paper is.

Overall I would recommend to older users and for anything not involving folding.

We tried to do a lot of family activities this year for Christmas, and this is one of the things that we did. I loved making these ornaments, we were able to make 7 with all the tiles that came with this kit, so I was really happy about that. They look really cute on a tree, and they are really not difficult to make at all. This kit would work for either adults or children, and I really liked that it included a glue gun with it because we didn't have one in our house. I would highly recommend this.

I love these diamond paintings they are great for passing the time during quarantine, my mom and I bought matching ones and would do them together over FaceTime while we chatted. Great to keep you occupied.
These are super cute. I love diamond painting, they really do relax me and help pass the time especially during this whole social isolation thing. These are well made and very cute. Highly recommend.

I’ve always been a fan of sharpie, and when I was looking to organize my fridge, I needed something I could write on my various containers that would both be readable as well as washable. This fits this market to a tee. My only issue I had is something after you push down for more ink, it makes too much ink come out for the first couple letters of writing, but then it goes right back to being the right amount. I’m very happy with this pen, highly recommend.
I love planner stickers, and these are no exception. This group makes a fantastic collection of planner stickers, I haven't found one I don't like yet.

I like these stickers, just like almost all the stickers made by me & my BIG ideas, they work great with their planner, for the most part. Some of the stickers are a little too big to work great with the planner, but other than that these are great and I like them a lot.

I bought these for my bullet journal and I really like these. The colors are really bright, so I can use them on any background (sometimes with yellow and tan you can't use them on a white sheet), but these work really well. They are easy to peel off the sheet and don't tear easily and they stick pretty well too. I would buy these again.

I liked these stickers. I bought them to use in my bullet journal because I'm not great at cute calligraphy yet, and these definitely do the trick. Some of the colors on the stickers probably have to be used on a really dark background in order for them to be truly visible (like the tan and yellow colors), but other than that I have no complaints.

I really did love this spray. I used it to decorate some mason jars for Christmas, and I was really happy with how glittery this spray was, as well as how good the coverage was with only one or two passes. The biggest downside to this spray is that sometimes it comes out a little weaker than other times, so you don't always get completely even coverage. Also the glitter does kind of get everywhere, but that's kind of how things are when you work with glitter of any kind. All in all I would totally buy this spray again, and it worked great on glass.

I bought this ink pad for a new address stamp I bought for the holidays, I wanted something that looked like the gold foil trend, as well as something a bit sparkly, and on point for the Christmas color scheme. This ink pad fit the bill completely. I was really impressed with how well the sparkly component came across, it really looks like gold foil. Also in the past I have bought some ink pads that dry out after like two uses, but that did not happen with this pad at all, Each time I used it with my stamp it was as fresh and had the best coverage. I would say that the best thing to do when storing this ink pad is to leave it upside down so the ink stays at the top of the sponge pad, it helps to make sure you have all the ink at the top when you use the stamp.
In the future I am going to be sticking with this brand, this was a great purchase for me.

I bought this scrapbook case to hold some of my more large sized paper (the 12x12 sheets). I liked that it was fairly deep so it accommodates a large amount of paper in it, my biggest issue with the case is that it pops open way to easily. I figured based on the pictures that it would lock closed with those little snaps, but the snaps don't really close that well, sometimes when I am moving the box around my office the snaps with become unlocked. I wish I had found a case that locked better.

I really needed to find a way to organize my loose pictures, I had been keeping them in one of those acid free photo boxes for a long time, but whenever I needed to find something in the box it took forever. Why I like these organizer is that I was able to group my photos into six categories and it makes it so much easier to find what I am looking for. Each box holds a significant amount of pictures, and I really love that they fit into a larger box so they are all centralized. All in all this was a great purchase for me and I would recommend it to anyone who needs to find a way to organize pictures.

I bought this sewing machine right after buying our first house. I knew there were going to be a lot of project in our future that would involve sewing, like making curtains for my house, and personalized custom dog beds. I really love this sewing machine, it was a great cost, but very efficient. It took me a while to figure out how to use it (I have never sewn before in my life), but after reading the instructions a couple of times and watching some YouTube videos online, I was able to make curtains and dogs a plenty. I am looking forward to my next project using this machine, and recommend it to anyone who is looking for a starter sewing machine.

Great pins. When I accidently sew over them with an industrial machine (often) they bend instead of breaking making them much safer. They are sharp enough to make draping easy and the sheer quantity means that if they get dull or sticky from sizing, they can be tossed with minimal guilt. Very good deal.

I can't believe I just paid $6 for one skein of floss. It's really laughable I thought I was getting a pack of 12. So, now I'm out the money and I still have to go to the store to buy for floss. ridiculous.

Very simple. Very basic. Does what it is meant to do. I don't think a thread rack needs to be very fancy, since it's the brightly colored thread that is on display. I bought one and then bought another.

I have been searching for a manageable crafting tool for a while now. I've looked at full size Dremel tools and small drills and nothing seemed to be what I was looking for. I do most of my crafting on my living room coffee table, so it was important that it not be noisy. The other tools were either powerful, but too heavy for me or light weight and not powerful enough. This little beauty is perfect. It only comes with a few drill bits, but you can also get an accessory kit with sanding disks and buffers and such for not much more. The handle is soft and easy on your hands. I think this may be unfairly branded as a "woman's tool" but it's really perfect for anyone who wants to do close work and doesn't want to pull out something that is too large for the job. I highly recommend this item.[[ASIN:B0019N4VHK Designed For Her Creative Rotary Tool Accessory Pack]]

Great needlemanship skills. Looks great on my work coveralls.

Comes shrink wrapped.  Is a decent amount of raffia but the quality of it is not great.  Lots of short pieces.  As I use, if I find that it is better than my original use of the first bunch, I will update my review.

These are great for crafts for adults and for kids.  I wish they were a little bit bigger but they are good quality.  I was a little disappointed about how they were packaged but it didn't seem to affect them.

I bought this to use on the Christmas tree as a garland.  It looks really nice and there is plenty of ribbon for this purpose.  I tied a couple of bows with this too and it looks really nice and is easy to work with.
Great candle tins, pack of 24.  Perfect for DIY candlemaking.

We can never have enough Raffia Ribbon - it is perfect for tying around products that we make on our farm.  Its not super sturdy, it does tear if you pull too hard, but I am just typing simple bows so it works just fine.  Th quality is good and it is nice large spool.
These are a nice 24 pack of tins.  Nice size at 8oz too.  Quality is very good and will make lovely candles.
Nice pack of 24 tins.  Solid, all black with semi sealing lids.  Can be used for candles or even salves.  Nice looking.
Very pretty colors in this set of 4 rolls. Great for flower arrangement accent, hairbows and even scrapbooking.
Nice little tool when you need to punch holes.  This works great and is a good price too.
Nice white glass containers for candles with bamboo lids.  Pack of 12.  Even nicer than expected.

The way tape measures get lost around here, I ordered these two cloth tape measures to just stick in a drawer when a search begins for a tape measure!  One has already to come out and saved the day, so it was a good idea to just have these available!  We do sew sachets for our lavender, and all our fancy measuring tools tend to disappear.  These are good cloth tapes - strong and just like the ones my grandmother used for years.  Good choice!
Nice quality tulle and a nice quantity for the price.  There are many potential uses.  The tulle is bright white and will make nice drawstring bags for our bath salts.
Really disappointed in these.  They really look weird - not what I expected for some reason.  I don't think they are pretty and will not use.
Great muslin fabric for our lavender eye pillow inserts.  100% cotton and unbleached, this is really nice and easy to sew on.  Exactly what I was hoping for.
These are great and so many - 60.  If you make candles or salves, these are great  Love the matte black.  These are really beautiful and will look great with any brand.
Great for labeling products or tying to gifts or gift baskets.  The quality of the paper is great and the ties are a decent length.  Nice to have a good quantity of 100.

We were hopeful that these attractive tapes would be strong enough to hold and decorate our lavender fire-starters (wrapped up tight and trimmed lavender stalks).  The adhesion is just okay for this, and the paper won't be strong enough to hold for very long.  To be fair, the tapes weren't meant for this!  They are very pretty, and are probably plenty strong for their normal use.

I ordered this so I could make a family name sign for our community marker at the end of the road.  For this purpose, the product worked well.  It definitely takes a little testing to get to the right tip, but there are 23 to choose from.  I am not sure I will use this again, but its a nice thing to have if the need arises.

This is a great concept but has some flaws.  The wax does take a while to heat up and once at temp, act fast so your was doesn't burn.  Mine doesn't leak or hasn't leaked yet but there is some waste once the was gets below the spigot.  Works well for my limited candle making.

Nice set for drawing using pencil or charcoal.  Comes with pencils, charcoal, eraser and other media to create different effects.  Includes sketchpad and nice storage case.  This makes a great gift for the artist in your life.
For the price, I expected more.  The loupe didn't come with instructions and I have had a hard time with the focus which is the primary reason for ordering.  This was a little disappointing.

It just makes sense that, knowing that a good coloring book and loads of colors in crayons or pens always tamed our children when "quiet time" was necessary.  There was something really theraputic about giving a child a coloring book and good place to color.  And now, it is working for adults - and for me!  Amazing how wrapped up you get - all cares flying out the window, as we spend just a short time of coloring.  Love this coloring book - good quality paper and interesting subjects.  Great choice!

These would be great for tying to Christmas packages, labeling stored items or for retail packaging.  We will be applying our logo to them and tying them to some of our products.  I think they will look beautiful and will likely get more.

I am a bit disappointed in this set of scrapbook fabrics because the sizes are really small.  This really limits what you can do with them whether scrapbooking or not.  There is a variety of patterns which is nice but I expected more from this set.

I have been looking for a set of these for awhile.  Most are too small.  I wish these were a little bit bigger but they will work.  They are really beautiful and I will be able to use them right away for Christmas dinner.  Will create a unique and beautiful dinner table.  Love!

We will be making lavender-scented candles, and these 8 oz. sizes with lids are perfect for what we plan.  The lid is essential until the candle is used to store the fragrance efficiently.  Packaged in these tins, the candles will be both giftable and saleable. Good choice.

For the DIY person who is into making all sorts of products, this set allows you to add color to your creation.  It’s easy to do - nice instructions help, and the mica powder is easy to mix for all kinds of lotions and potions.  Great gift for the person who is into this endeavor - colors are well-packaged in sealed jars for safety.  Great choice!

These are really well-made little easels that are perfect to hold canvases on a table just at the right level.  Perfect for comfortable painting.  These are quality easels - nice wood, and put together to last.  Very pleased with these easels - as are the grandchildren!

We’ll be working with some goat owners to collaborate on making lavender-scented soap. So these soap molds are just what we needed.  We really like working with the silicone molds - so much easier to remove the soap! We’ll need to order more as we get our quantity needs set up.  Good choice!

This is a beautiful table-top easel set - nice wooden briefcase houses everything we need to get set up.  When we showed the  family, everyone wanted one - and we bet it will get raves when we take it to art class.  Very nice, and exactly what we needed.  Great quality.

Great for all our projects  - so comfortable to use with the loop grip!  We were thinking projects with the grandchildren, but these are sturdy and strong enough to cut lavender, as well.  The bright colors make them easy to find - can hang them up, find them easily on a large table!  These are a great idea!
We grow lavender and we are always looking for ties for our bundles.  Love the color and the amount of yardage.  This will tie a good number of bundles.

This kit has everything you need to make very nice candles - it’s a learning experience - and once you have used this kit, you’re ready to get into some serious candle-making.  Good instructions are easy to follow.  Must stress that this is not for small children - it’s for adults who understand the dangers.  If you want to get into candle-making, this is where to start.  Good kit.

We raise lavender and alpacas, and have a product line that includes both.  These tags are perfect when we need to include instructions or just needed information on care.  Our theme uses brown paper bags and boxes, so these tags are perfect.  Large enough for us to put our company info on one side and necessary info about the product on the other.  Good source,

Great quality ribbon - we’ll be using it to tie our lavender bundles that we take to the Farmer’s Market.  Our colors are lavender, gray and brown paper bag - our labels are white with colorful lavender and this ribbon will just set off each bundle to look like it’s worth what we’re asking!  Great ribbon! We’ll be ordering more - maybe in lavender!

Some of our lavender products are at home in plastic containers - especially lavender bath beads in the bathroom.  Nice gift for children in an unbreakable jar.  We like the water-tight lids - which lets us consider a wider range of products for these jars.  Good choice for many purposes.

This kit will allows us to learn about candle-making - everything we need is included, and we just need to set a time and place to get started.  The kit is impressive in the instructions and all that is provided.  We will eventually be making lavender-scented candles, so this kit will get us started.  May need to order another!
We make lavender products - like sachets - from this silk fabric.  This fabric is particularly good for our purpose - it’s easy to work with, and is just the right weight.  Good choice.

It’s all set up and ready to go for the dark days of winter, when we are searching for things to keep busy.  We’re all ready with several paint by number canvases - the easel is set up (sets up in a flash) and we just need to sit down and paint!  Nice easel, and it folds up so we can take it outside next spring.  Everything we need to become budding artists - we just need to sit down and get started.  Great easel and set-up.  The grandkids will love this, and may help us with our projects!

The Christmas cheer is raging over here with these fun wooden ornaments! My grandson begged me to let him paint them all day and we made it happen. He is so excited to hang them up tomorrow with the little bells they came with.

Great canvases - perfect for practicing techniques - the smaller size is less intimidating!  Really good for encouraging young artists.  This is a good supply for the craft room.  Good quality canvases, ready to go.  Good choice!

Beautiful silk fabric - 36X44, which allows for much creativity - we have several projects, experimenting with different uses.  This the quality we were hoping to receive for our use.  The width is surprisingly wide, and gives us ample.  Very pleased to get this very soft, high quality sample.

Comes in a nice zip up case, which holds a huge supply of every grade of sketching pencil, erasers, and all the needed accessories.  Also a small sketch pad.  This kit is planned for portability - it would be easy to take with you on a trip - or just to the park for some sketching.  Everything in one place.  Good for the beginning sketcher, the one beginning to sketch again, or the seasoned sketcher.

Great kit - wish the canvas had been rolled instead of folded, which requires some stretching.  Lots of colors, lots of numbers .  This is not something you will paint in an evening.  If you paint it well, you should end up with something framable.  Might be a bit tedious getting used to being so very precise in tiny spots - but you can always go sloppy and say it’s a Van Gogh!  Should be fun!

We decided that, current life conditions being what they are, a little paint by number would be a stress reliever.  However, we’re not so sure, looking at this canvas - with all its numbers corresponding to paint colors - jillions of paint colors, paint brushes and instructions.  First we have to unfold the canvas - it didn’t come rolled, which was disappointing  - and stretch it on a board to make it perfectly flat.  Then the the fun will begin.  These are first rate kits - we’re just hoping the “artist” can match the excellence of the kits.

Once assembled, this is a great easel - putting it together is not that much fun, especially if assembling isn’t your strong suit!  Together, It’s just what you need - it’s lightweight to carry around, sets up easily and is sturdy.  Nice wood, too.  Good choice!

Great for the beginner or the person just taking up sewing again.  This small sewing machine has many of the features of a full-size machine.  It offers a variety of stitches, sews backward or forward, operates with bobbins, and uses either a power adapter or batteries.  It runs smoothly delivering a smooth, regular stitch.  It has a foot pedal for those who are used to having one.  Really pleased wit this energetic sewing machine!

When we cut our lavender and wrap it in lavender tissue, the perfect finishing touch is the jute tie and the kraft paper tags, with our logo labels attached.  These arrived just in time for July 4th, when we visit the neighbors with our first bouquets.  Very well received - the jute and the paper tags are sturdy and hold up well.  Very pleased with both.  Good quality with that “country farm” touch.

We got as far as “painting kit” and the picture of a dog, and thought, wow, what fun.  It turns out to be a “Painting with beads” - tiny little beads applied with a “dotting pen”.  For those who are acquainted with this form of art, this kit comes with a color coded canvas, a dotting pen and numerous colors of “beads” to complete the painting.  We’re hoping that several older grandchildren will take on this project - it might be too much for anyone under 10 or so.

My daughter is resurrecting her artist talent after years behind a computer, so loves this set to get her started again.  Forty-eight colors is an unbelievable range - who knew there could be so many?  This would be a great gift for grandchildren - up through teenage - coupled with a couple of canvasses.  Good quality paints - and just all the colors alone make you want to paint!  Good gift choice!

These are pens with pigment color for writing, outlining, etc. -  waterproof - these are serious pens - not for children. Any artist will appreciate these but these are not a toy for children!  Your artist friend will appreciate them as a gift!

Everything you need to safely make candles.  Lots of good instructions and ideas.  So nicely packaged - this is serious candle-making.  Check the picture for a list of all that is included.  Parental supervision is definitely required - but this is a great activity to have some meaningful interaction with children or grandchildren.  Great gift for the crafter!

Wow, this really works well.  Good instructions to get us going - amazing the number of uses.  We were able to fix a number of toys, affix some artificial flowers that fell - I even attached a bouquet of live lavender to a wrought iron shelf where it could dry and provide some color in the meantime!  So glad  we have this with a good quantity of glue sticks included.  Great gift for the crafter!

We were interested in this because of our work with lavender, and we wondered if this tulle ribbon would be something we could use in packaging our products. We really liked the tulle for its  quality and versatility - and the color is perfect  for our use.  So glad we’ve discovered this source to help us create the look of quality in our products.

The unicorn arrived broken at the neck, but it is successfully glued back together, and this grandma has assured her granddaughter that the glued area will be a perfect spot for a necklace she can paint!  So, we’re happy with the unicorn which she can’t wait to paint!  Maybe shipping during a less crowded season will ensure an undamaged arrival.

This is a bit messy, but the grandkids enjoy making pictures.  Lots of good ideas and different colors of sand.  We ended up setting this up outside on the picnic table, as exuberance was making the interior surroundings a bit gritty!  This is a good experience and a totally new one for most kids - and they love something new.

A wonderful supply of construction paper - our crafty group is going to have a ball with this.  Every color is represented, and the paper is fairly sturdy for many projects.  Great addition to our supply shelf!

This is a beautiful train set!  Can't believe it - Lionel  has been making trains since 1900 - and this latest version, is about as perfect as it can be.  There's enough track to make three different layouts, so it's easy to make the set fit in any shape space.  The cars are a locomotive with a headlight, train whistle and bell, a coal car, a crate carrier with removable crates and a caboose.  It's battery powered, so no messy cords as I remember from the old days (how many times were we all tripping over the cords?).  The packaging says "ready-to-play", but you'll need 3 AAA batteries and 6 C batteries.  Lucky that I read the product info, so I was prepared to get the train up and running.  It's easy to get the track laid out, and the train runs very smoothly.  Took me a few to get used to the remote, but, let's face it, it's probably been 60 years since this Grandma operated a train, and, never before, with a remote control!  Now, we're going to put this away with the Christmas stuff, as the grandchildren will love seeing this run around the tree.  I'm very happy with this train set.  It's well-constructed, and looks like it can withstand heavy play when that time comes.  Nice to welcome Lionel back into our lives!  A tried and true company, known for it's quality and good customer service.
N/A
Adorable and works well. Very happy with this. Just as cute in person as it is in the picture.

I see why it takes 28 days to cure as it is sticky after a full day of drying. I wish there was a way to speed that process. This is the beginning of the 28 days so no review on being able to wash it yet.

This is my first time using this product. My piece on which it is being used is not finished yet, but so far I love the texture and lustre of the medium it gives to my project. Will try to update when completed
These were a great idea for use to convert wooden gingerbread "cookies" into ornaments as I did not want to spoil the designs by drilling holes in the wood.
I needed backs that would take the weight of the glass cabochons and these worked perfectly! My client was happy with her earrings and gifts.
I haven't opened my package yet, but so far, they look like just what I wanted, an array of sizes of this shape brush.
Perfect for my artwork!
Love their mattes, wish I had read the item listing better as I really wanted the backing boards and bags too...
Makes a beautiful presentation of my artwork!
work well
work well
These are pretty, but not made as well as I would like, some separate fairly easily, not what I want my customers to experience.
Love it!
Works great!
Love ranger inks!

I do not write a lot of reviews. This sketchbook deserves a review. It is the perfect size and works amazing with alcohol. Markers are usually use. Alcohol markers with ink and colored pencils. I would give it a higher rating than five stars if I could. So,if you are working with alcohol markers, I highly recommend you giving this sketchbook a try.

I bought this to be my New Years Journal. I am an artist and I also write in a journal daily. The selling point to me was the weight of the bamboo paper. I was not disappointed at all. I definitely feel I got an amazing deal on an amazing product. I’ll purchase blank books and sketchbooks throughout the year, I am not always happy with my purchase because I’m very critical of the paper. This one is definitely a winner I highly recommend.
They are almost to me a combination of watercolor and gouache.
I have a collection of sketchbooks like you would not believe. This is by far one of my favorite ones.
Got this for my Ohuhu markers and my Poscas.  I LOVE IT.  Whether you want to store your markers horizontal or vertical, this is the storage container you need. I highly recommend it.

Huge fan of this sketchbook. If you like Moleskine then you are going to LOVE these. Great for markers and colored pencils. The paper is white, thick, and lays flat when opened. The feel of the book itself just feels elegant for a sketchbook.

Awesome markers. I am an artist. I constantly am adding stock to my art supplies. I have owned Primacolor markers and I have to say these markers are much better to work with and streak less. For the price, you cannot go wrong. I use colored pencils with them and I love the possibilities.

I still cannot believe the price on these brushes and the quality they are. I have used the high dollar watercolor brushes and they are like night and day from the less expensive ones. I wasn't expecting but I was hoping by reading reviews that these were going to blow my mind. And they did. They hold water like the high dollar brushes but are truly inexpensive.I am still in awe! I love getting a good deal. This is an amazing deal and I highly recommend these brushes. If you are like me and want to use the best but sometimes just cannot afford it...These are the answer!
I ordered two of these and one had the wire pulled out at the top which I was able to fix. But the other had a bend in the front cover. I had bought both for a gift. Very disappointed.

These are just as described and work well for your floral needs. I also use these to attach any kind of hanging objects into my enclosures for my pets. It works very well and since the wires are very thin they will fit through the small holes in the vivariums on the top so I can hang things. These are exactly what I needed.

This is a great set of fine point glitter pens that cover the entire rainbow spectrum and then some. There is no color to wish for. These are just so much fun to draw with and perfect for the artist inside you. These also make for a fantastic gift.

These metallic glitter markers are too stinking cute. They pop especially on colored paper. In this set you get 20 pens with fine point and 10 brush pens, a calligraphy type marker. These are perfect for the artist in your family. You get some really neat colors in this set that will look fantastic on especially darker backgrounds. This will be a great gift as well.

I LOVE these markers. They are amazing and a must have for any arts lover! You get 24 markers and each one has a thin marker on one end and a thick marker on the other end in the same color which is great. You get 24 different "skin colors" although some of these I am not sure who has those kinds of colors but hey, that is what art is :)

These are easy to use and it even comes with a colorless blender that works really well. If you are not great in the arts department, fear not, these also make a great gift and with the holidays around the corner ..... at less than $20 the price is more than fair and they do come in a case so you can keep your set together at all times.

They stuck on at first but then started to come of. Since the color was a fairly good match and they were on the inner thigh area,  I secured them by stitching them in. And I didn't even wash these yet.  Can't really recommend them.

I bought this mannequin head to use for mask making and mask painting. It is perfect.  The construction is very solid and stable. I will be using this for all of my mask making needs and to display my Star Wars helmets when not in use. I highly recommend this product.

The entire pad was wrinkled as if it had been exposed to humidity. I'm using it to practice calligraphy with brush pens, and some pens bleed through and/or feather a bit. I won't purchase this brand again. I got a pad of Bachmore tracing paper that is around the same price and arrived in perfect condition.
Excellent tracing paper! Arrived in great condition, with no bends or wrinkles. I'm using it for practicing calligraphy with brush pens, and the pens don't bleed through or feather.
I’m making this for one of my granddaughters. It’s easy to read, and it is a very good well put together Craft.
My son. Good, beautiful picture.
It’s every thing I needed at the time. It’s perfect. See through, sturdy and compact.
Good sturdy tools for Diamond Painting.
Product is durable and sturdy. Can see my inside well.
This was easy to read and do. Very fun. My grandsons loved it.
I loved this. It was my first Diamond Painting. Easy, great product.
Great product. Just what I needed for Diamond Painting.
I could not of done my projects with out the light pad. It was and still GREAT.

The zipper bag broke the first time I zipped it, my wax glue sheet was bent so badly, I could only salvage about half of them and my labels were also so bent and half peeled up that I could, again, only salvage about half of them. BUT, I rated it for these reasons, the main reason I wanted to buy this. The roller is amazing in quality. It's good and sturdy. I am also in love with the large, blue tray. It is very nice. The width and depth make life much easier in terms of shaking your drills/diamonds flat and neatly lined up. So, those two things are five star items in a two or even one star pack.

It is very cute and the pen feels good to hold, but the tweezers are pointless to pick up anything remotely small. The light is great... Until you load the tip of the pen with wax glue, which stifles the light significantly. The multi-drill/diamond tips don't seem to be very useful. But, I don't regret my purchase. It arrived swiftly and fully charged. But, I am still on the hunt for the right drill/diamond pen.

*Update review*
After making my own pen, I decided to use the light pen as an easy to handle flashlight for the hard to see symbols on my canvases.
This penlight holds NO charge, the light fades in and out no matter how fully charged it is, and I BARELY use it. So, the elbow tip and the tin it all came in turned out to be the only things I use.

The colour of the drills/diamonds are off, the shades aren't right. The end result is so far from the showed image I can't help but be disappointed. Also, worth mentioning, using these kits often for alleviating symptoms of severe anxiety, I have done a lot of them. All in varying sizes and quality. This is, hands down, the least sticky canvas I have ever seen. The drills/diamonds fall off constantly. So, watch out for that. I will say, it was easy to read and as I always seem to, I enjoyed doing it. I am just  a bit disappointed I bought two for gifts and compared to the image shown to purchase, which is stunning, the end result is... Kind of ugly.

Found the ribbon great for bow making as well as several other craft projects I was making. Very nice quality material. Wire in the ribbon was sturdy and held its shape when making bows. Would definitely recommend and will purchase again.
Works great for durable surface when cutting products being used in various craft projects. Will definitely purchase again for other work area.
The colour of the beach is not as vibrant as the picture, but they came on time, well packaged, and The colour is OK come on so I will be keeping them
Not even close.
This wire is beautiful, soft and easy to work with. The color will come off easily when bending with tools so take caution.
These beads are beautiful, versatile and work well with other beads. I love the craftsmanship and the vibrant colors.  I will probably order the entire rainbow.

The brushes arrived as expected and were as advertised. They are not what I would call "professional" maybe "beginner set" would be more appropriate of a description. They are good quality brushes for the price. I needed something to supplement my more expensive pieces and these are just right for those practice paintings or projects I don't want to subject my best brushes. I'll purchase again if I'm unable to go to the art store in person and buy them.
Worth it for the price!
Buenos lápices para los coloring book de adultos. Muy económicos.

I think this was a cute idea to make a Christmas/Holiday marker set. I hope they do more holiday themed sets in the future. Examples: Valentine's Day, Saint Patrick's Day, Halloween, 4th of July, and etc. It would be awesome and I would buy them all. It would be nice that they would be colors you can't get in the bigger sets. If you do go that route make sure to give me credit Arrtxx haha jk. I love their markers though they are awesome dupe to Copics.

This was a gift for my 7 year old and he loved them. The glitter is beautiful and the pastels were pretty. I did notice that they don't show up very well on dark colors. That is a deal breaker for some. There were a good 7-8 gel pens that were dried up and most of them were the classic  with a few of the glitter ones from each of the glitter lines. Other than that they are a great deal and love that they have names and you can buy refills. I do wish they came in a decent case to store them in. Now I have to buy him a case to store them. He is happy overall so I am happy overall.

I love how you can mix and layer colors so easily. I am more on the beginner level and these are very nice pencils. They have a great feel to them. There were some that were somewhat scratchy but for the price that isn't too big of a deal. I haven't noticed much of an off center core yet. I have only used them 3 times so far. I would definitely buy them again. I di wish they had open stock/individually purchased. I use a lot of blues in my work it would be great to buy the colors I use most. I guess I will buy the Faber castell polychromos when I use one up. I am pleased regardless.

I love Castle Arts pencils! They are wonderful! I do wish these metallic pencils were more of a "WOW" pencil. They aren't as spectacular as I'd hoped. The Case is alright, not the best quality. That could be improved some, but at least I don't have to buy a case for now. I do wish there was a black swatch sheet inside as well as the white one. I am blown away by the paper pads in the case. They are so stinking cute! I love the size perfect for traveling. I wish and hope you guys sell these in your shop very, very, VERY SOON. Please at least think about it. I'm sure people would love to buy them. I am very happy with my purchase at the end of the day. You won't regret it! Very nice!

I got this set for my 7 year pld that is dipping his toes into Alcohol marker art. I am pleased with the color selection but some were dry and not pleasant to use. The caps were also extremely hard to get off for him and myself. The smell was stronger than a copic marker. After awhile he has to take a break as it gives him a headache. It still is a good value especially with how many you get in the set. I wish I could have gotten a replacement set but I didn't know about the markers being dry until Christmas day...so I highly doubt I will receive one. I might be buying him the ohuhu's at some point.... basically the bottom line is they are good for beginners just have to help with the caps. They aren't good for people with arthritis. I also wish the came with a swatch card.

I just love pastel colors so this was a must. The quality is very good! I love most of the colors included. I do wish that in every set they have that they would include both a white and black swatch sheet to see what they look like on both. Especially for crafters that use multiple backgrounds for projects, or colorists that like to do coloring on black or kraft paper. Another Castle Arts set you won't be disappointed by. I hope to one day get the Gold set and the woodless watercolor pencils set. Sure wish I could receive them as a gift from friends and family for my birthday so I can save my money for more art supplies 😆.

I am not happy that the tabs that hold the containers shut were broken and at the bottom of the box when I opened it. Now I have to find another container to store them in when I bought these thinking the containers would be good quality. Boy was I wrong! Super flimsy super cheap. Good thing the product is the main star otherwise I would give 1 star. Quality control needs to be done better. I wish they would send out new containers without me having to send my items back so I don't have to spend more money on something better.....

I love these pencil cases I love how slim they are and how neat they look on my book shelf. I bought 7 of these ones and 6 of another brand of the same product. These cases are perfect for my huge pencil collection. I organized my professional colored pencils in them. You can easily store 77 pencils in these.  I have two cases each for the professional colored pencils and I have a big case I bought for my budget pencils. They look so beautiful lined up all in order. I highly recommend these cases. I hope to order more in the future!

My package was slightly dented when delivered. Wish that Amazon took better care of the packages that came through there. Especially when things may be gifts for others. I love these pencils though,  great alternative to prismacolor and other name brands. If I didn't have to send these back just to get a better tin I would but I don't have time to wait for another set to arrive. Plus these pencils are now used.
I love this palette and how the bottom is flat. I do wish it was able to hold more half pans, especially for people that have a bigger collection.
These were a gift. They were very happy with the product.

The colors are vibrant and beautiful but I like to be able to rework some of my paintings and do lifting techniques and these don't work for that. I do love the vibrancy. It is amazingly bright! I do love mixing it into other colors though to make a new texture.
I think this is a great way to see what colors you want to invest more in from the Daniel Smith line. I now know what colors I am looking forward to buying for Christmas as a gift to myself lol.
Love love love the case and the colors are pretty too. I am a fan of Paul rubens paint line. I will be experimenting some more with these.

The bristles were bent at the very tip and I can't get them to be straight. I wish my camera worked to send a picture. I was very disappointed with this brush quality because I couldn't use it to get a fine tip even when wet. I wish I could have gotten a new one.

I love how great the ink flows with little to no problems. The only dislike I have is the case. It would be better if it was easier to get the gel pens out that are close to the edge. The way it opens makes it hard for those closest to the zipper to be taken out. I would love the case if it didn't have that as a problem.
These paints rewet very easily and are so pigmented! Well worth the price!
I love how much glitter there is. The only thing I don't like is how fast it can get used up. They are very addictive. So sparkly!
I love how many colors I can put in this tiny palette. I wish it didn't stain at times. I expect that for most plastic palettes.

Let me start of by saying I love Paul Runens watercolors. I love how gorgeous the colors are. I love that they give you pigment information and love how they present their products like little gifts that look special. However, I am not pleased with this tin for this set. I love the well shape for mixing. I love the cool texture and color on the outside. I am not pleased with the inside and how the pans are held in. It is difficult to get your brush in there to even grab pigment. I also don't like how it is opened and closed. It is sometimes difficult to open compared to their other tins I have purchased in the past. I do recommend this paint in this set.  The tin I would like to be like their other tins but with the wells being the same shape and the texture being on them. That is why that is not a 5 star rating.

I do love the colors in this set. I am not a fan of how they tend to leak at times and that the product gets used up very quickly. I love the mermaid markers, I think they are very convenient to have with you on the go. They are gorgeous when they are down on paper. I am not sure if there are refills for this set or not. If there are I will definitely be buying them.
I love how nice they hold up. I did notice some bristles were not laying flat with the rest upon opening and wetting them. I do like them though, they feel like high quality brushes while painting.
Perfect. I've been buying this type of tape measure for years and they are very durable. Somehow they keep getting borrowed or lost and I have to buy a new one every few years.

Perfect for holding all of my colored pencils! I just started adult-coloring ...lol...for something to do while watching tv and i must admit i am hooked! Crazy i know! This holds them all on my coffee table and makes it easy to utilize and then close them up nice and tidy for the next night! Great little case!
I use these to transfer embroidery patterns onto tea towels. It is graphite so it does smear a bit. The kids love to steal them and trace pictures/etc. onto plank paper for projects.
I'm pretty sure it said prime, but the shipping took forever. I was going to cancel, but it showed up and it was good quality and beautiful.

When I first started beading, I had Super Lon thread which I was using for micro-macrame.  However, for beaded ornaments, I found this thread was better as it didn't break when pulled on and had little fraying (only at the needle). It's easy to tie off and so far, doesn't break.  I will note that I moved to Firewire for necklaces/bracelets as it's more a non-natural thread (like a thin fishing line): thought it might stand less of a chance to break.  But I do like this thread very much: will purchase individual spools when I run out. BTW: This seller's price is the lowest I found, including those on etsy.

I've been sewing clothes (that never fit well on me) for longer than I want to admit and I never thought I'd use one of these forms.  I'm now making macrame clothing and find it invaluable: plan to start on some clothes sewing that I've had sitting for years.  It's lighter weight than I thought: you can't be rough with it and you cannot pin through it: make the physical adjustments carefully and slowly ... use the clips instead of pins.  I love it!!  Probably would have been more happy making clothes if I'd had one.  For the price, it's a must ... right along with the sewing machine.  Doesn't take up too much space: I put an old t-shirt on it so I don't ruin the original fabric.  The MED fits me fine (a bit large in the chest) but the rest of the measurements are wonderful: I was able to overlap the chest area to bring it down an inch.  Make sure your measurements are within the form measurements: you can always pad it up for specific areas but there's no way to take the form down smaller.  I really like it and wish I'd purchased it years ago.

Nice braided cord for macrame: I prefer the braided cords as I'm a bit OCD and like their finish. This cord has a looser braid and does not have a white solid core: combing it out shouldn't be an issue.  I like the Likeecords, too: however, their braid is much tighter (but I was able to paint on it!).  Good cord: nice colors. Will purchase again.
While the product is on the yellow side of 'natural' and will not match the color of most natural cord, it is very nice cord that is soft and knots well.  I will be buying in other colors!

I have now purchased just about all of the packets of this company's wool.  I love it.  The colors are fabulous and the wool is soft: I'm felting with it.  There were no issues with 'bugs' (yes, wool can have bugs in it) and I've found that with what I've purchased, I have a myriad of colors and am very happy with the selection and quantity!  I would definitely recommend Wistyria products: and they're in my home state so that's a win, too!

I purchased the 3mm white (bright white it is) twine ... 100% cotton ... very nice.  It's wound tightly: packed a bit harder than my 4mm twisted macrame cord (which is softer and squishier).  I purchased this twine to make macrame angels: it works wonderfully!  I really like this cord as it doesn't fray until I comb it out: nice tight knots on the macrame. I would recommend and will purchase again.

I've got several color blends of this type of yarn and I just love it for pom poms.  The yarn is sturdy and thick: not as many windings for pompoms as other yarns.  And once cut, this yarn fluffs up perfectly!  Makes a really sturdy pompom: nice a full.  I'm using machine embroidered faces for animals (off Etsy) and they're coming out simply adorable!

This will work wonderfully for machine embroidered hand sanitizer cases!  The 'fabric' is thin so it should accept the embroidery well (with a good stabilizer) and then, accept the T5 snaps.  I can't wait to get going on these!  I've saved this item as I believe I'll purchase it again!

I did some research on wall thread storage and found this unit to be the best: the width between the posts is the most of all those I researched at 1-7/8" (just shy of 2") which means that my embroidery cones (both large and small) can fit on the pegs side by side without having to rotate them!  (I'm too OCD for that). I'm mounting them on the wall, one below the other.  Now I can get them out of the box they're in and not have to go hunting to find what I need: they can all sit in number order and I can grab what I need instantly!  The small spools will sit on the pegs, too but I have those in the Madiera cabinet so no problem there.  Really like this product: very sturdy ... I'm mounting with E-Z Anchors (love these!) so there's no question about weight.  Like the metal product rather than wood: just seems more solid.  If you have threads, this is a great product!

I use it for machine embroidery as a background for a number of designs but this year, I'm putting 3D butterflies onto it in a shadowbox.  The fabric DOES NOT STRETCH (unlike most velvet) and has a wonderful sheen to it.  Not a particular high nap so with a washable topper, it will work out just fine.  Difficult to find no-stretch velvet ... but this one is a keeper and a re-orderer.  The color tends to the side of tawny ... not a bright silver by any means ... will go well in a black shadowbox and not detract from the butterflies. I am extremely pleased with this fabric.
Best results for making dog, cat paw prints and bird and reptile claw prints.
Ordered the pack with 3 hoop sizes (6)
This is the best stuff I have ever used for slicking edges down on leather.
Small little tool but does exactly what I needed it to do.
Love it. It heats up quickly. I've made a couple dozen shirts for co-workers with no problems.
Great for chalk design
I love this product for my photos, scrapbooks, crafting.  And honestly getting any sticker residue off any thing it touches. Great for removing those stamps you accidentally used on an envelope.

These are great for writing quick notes or signing cards. The colors are great but the tips make riding a little scratchy and skips some. I’m not sure if you could use these for adult coloring books since I don’t use them for that. But for the price it wouldn’t hurt for someone to try.
These are actually better than the namebrand rotary cutters I have been paying for. I will definitely buy again.
This has been a great addition to my sewing box. It makes Pressing seems for hamming so much easier.
This has made cutting fabric for my cricut So much easier for any 12 x 12 project.
This has made all the difference when it comes to removing seams. There is a bit of a learning curve, but once you get the hang of it you’ll never want to do it another way.
The cuffs are cheaply made, they break easy but that's actually OK, because my wrist is the size of a kids, so I can snap all my watches in place. Everything else is nice.
Terrific!
The vellum was what I expected, but I was looking for vellum with smaller gold dots closer together. I should have paid closer attention to the size.
These are really cute and just the right "thickness."
I like the look of these tapes, but they are very thin.
Just what I ordered. Very cute!
Mini squares help line up stamps correctly.
Only disappointment was from the size.. they are extremely small.. buyer beware
Wire is too thin.
Trying
Does not get as hot as I thought it would.  But works very well for the heat that it puts out.
I use these for alcohol painting their beautiful and vibrant I mix them with 91% alcohol and paint with them
They’re about what I expected for the price. Not great not terrible. The nibs are quite thick. The search for great white paint markers goes on.
The gold one was great until it clogged up. After that when I pressed it to get the ink flowing, a puddle came out. Then it clogged again. It's now useless. Waste of money.

Such vibrant and beautiful colors. Very soft, easy to work with. I just started getting into pastels and I am so pleased with these. My only dislike is common to all soft pastels: they are messy. The dust gets everywhere. And I don't want it in my lungs or my mouth, which can happen by blowing off the dust, or accidentally touching the mouth with dusty fingers. If I do continue to grow with these I'll consider wearing a mask (oh boy!) and an air filter, but I'm more likely to use them sparingly as part of mixed media pieces. This is a great product. They are AP certified, which I understand means they do not use the carcinogenic pigments found in other art supplies--oils, pastels, etc. So that part is reassuring and appreciated. Definitely need some workable fixative to go with, so I ordered the same brand of that today.

I love these. I recently got into pastels after spending a lot of time with watercolors. I really like the loose details I can apply with these over watercolors. They are so clean and easy compared to pastel sticks. A great addition to my growing art supply collection. I hope to buy more.

The colors are beautiful, like all pastels. They are far less dusty than my Sennelier sticks. That's the best I can say about them.

When stacked there is no way to see or find the color you want without unscrewing them all. I could make labels for the side of the tubs, but I shouldn't have too. In application, the sponges are awkward to use and a poor design for pastels. On the video I watched, it showed the sponges wiping easily clean by rubbing on a paper towel, but there is no way all that pigment is coming off on a dry paper towel. So to avoid color contamination you need to constantly change the sponges--and constantly buy more sponges.

The tool is shaped like a little trowel--not very controllable. The coverage is okay, not great. Sticks are better.

I read up on the cancer warning. Unfortunately this applies to all pastels and paints in general with certain pigments. Pastels are the worst offender because of inhaling dust and accidentally ingesting from fingers. I don't think I'll keep these. They seem like they are the product of an engineer and heavy marketing, not a serious artist. They are way overpriced, and I hate all the plastic.

This product is mostly a large and irresponsible amount of plastic. The tubs are made to look like there is a much larger amount of product when in reality it is very shallow. Severely overpriced. I ordered two sets. This one I will keep because I’ve opened it. The other one I will return because I want no part of this.

The pans are not refillable, so once the small amount of product is used up, the plastic container is trash. The pans are stackable, but there is no way to see the colors once they are stacked. It’s very gimmicky, with no good application for creating serious art.
Great colors and easy to handle. I love the colors on the cap. Nice smooth feel in use. Would like more colors!
Lots of my elastic waist pants are too tight, sadly. This replaced the thick binding types and is much softer and more comfortable.

These are certainly creamy, as advertised. Think dense lipstick creamy. I'm not sure what application they would have for me--my art is too detailed and precise, but I can see their use for bolder and more abstract designs. I only tried them out on a piece of journal paper, so I'll see what they do on Bristol or watercolor paper. A big issue for me is that they stay tacky for a long time. So I can see smearing and smudging as problems. Of course there is nothing wrong with smearing and smudging if that is the desired look. But it prevents me from giving them to my grandchildren. Not planet friendly at all.

I enjoyed putting this together because it was interesting and actually quite ingenious. But that's where the fun stopped. I wish I had read the reviews, because it absolutely doesn't work for watercolors. The description of "up to" a certain size tube is not the truth, because it doesn't state the minimum. It doesn't even hold smaller brushes. But the reason why I'm going to get rid of it, rather that use it for some other type of storage in my studio, is that the particle boards are unfinished on the ends, and those tiny particles were all over my hands during assembly. Since I work with my hands all day, and would be touching this thing frequently, and I have no clue what is in the particles, I decided the unknown risk to my health isn't worth it. There is also quite an odor from it--possible some off gassing going on.

The paints are awesome, but the tin is unfinished on the sides of the piece that folds out towards you. Both sides are knife sharp. By contrast, the cheapo set I bought has a rounded edged tin. I will have to see if I can put the very expensive paints into the cheapo tin. I lopped off two stars for the tin, because at this price I should not have to adapt the product just so I don't slice my fingers. Don't always get what you pay for.
A tiny little tube that costs almost as much as the full size tube. Rip Off

This is about as low density as foam gets. The description of high density is utterly false. When compressed it immediately reduces to about a quarter inch so provides useless  cushioning. A waste of money.

Great value for money. I wanted a natural theme this year, cutting way back on the plastic junk. I stamped these with ink to make them the color I wanted. There were lots of them, so I was able to get a very full look on my tree. I have had so many compliments! But they'd also look great without the addition of color. Good quality.

I used this as garland on my Christmas tree, along with other types of varying widths. It's so very pretty--a seashell pink with gold border that fits perfectly with my Nutcracker look. Love it. Good value.
I love this tape. It glues securely and is easy to cut and the backing comes off easily. I am enjoying using it in my paper crafting.
I love the design. It inks up pretty well. A few gaps in the solid spaces but I enjoy coloring them in. Very pretty for Christmas cards.

Delivered rapidly but the print is so faint I can barely read it. No measurements, and a lack of any other advice, given on the pattern. You have to go to the website to figure it out, which at this price is asking too much. I expected to buy a complete pattern with complete information needed to make the dress. There are many other options available so I will more than likely return this expensive one.

So many chips and imperfections, obviously occurring during the cutting process. Also, unless the piece is divisible exactly by 10mm square, there will need to be some cutting, just like with all tiles. I guess I could have peeled each tiny tile off and stuck it down individually but for the project I had in mind it just wasn't worth the investment of time. I certainly wouldn't want to cover a piece of furniture in them--would cost a fortune and take forever. There were no instructions whatsoever. I really wish I hadn't bought them.

Pretty papers in a cube. I should have checked the size though, because they are very small, about 2 1/2" x 2 1/2". They will work fine for my purposes, and they're actually kind of cute, but not what I think of as standard size origami paper.
Really great glue for paper crafts that require a fine point.
Great black safety pins for when visibility is either desired or not, depending on use.

Perfect for my needs. I go back and forth between bamboo and metal. The yarn I am working with I felt needed bamboo. I was glad to be able to get these delivered in a timely manner, so that by the time I'd swifted my skeins they arrived. Pleased with the length and the amount of flexibility as well as the quality. Yarn slides nicely from the cable to the needle.
Very nice cable needles. Cabling away on my sweater with these. Just the right size. Good value.
So nice and clean to use. The colors are bright and lively on the pages.
I've been using this for sometime and it is simply wonderful for diamond dots. You can adjust the brightness at a few different levels and it's not too heavy.
Perfect shades and great value for the price.

This kit is so nice! My first set of boxes were a bit defective (some cubes wouldn't close)  so I emailed the company & they sent me a new set right away.. perfect! If I needed more, I would 100% buy this again.
This was my first diamond painting & it couldn't have gone any smoother. I'm now addicted! The adhesive glue was very strong & there were plenty of diamonds left over.

Good tool for cutting your vinyl. I used to cut it with my knife thinking these are that all necessary, but boy was I wrong. It keeps your work flow up and makes cutting large sections of vinyl so much less of a hassle.

I wanted to use these mixed with vase filler for wedding decor. 200 sounds like a lot, but honestly, it doesn't make a dent in the effect I wanted. I ended up not using them. Good quality with no holes. Just not enough to bother with for the price.
I love these!! They're beautiful!

The round mold looked so nice, the design so pretty on the picture…but, the mold is terrible! Very soft silicone and it was packaged flat, so there is no way to make it round again.
I tried to make a candle in it, because I assumed it would get round when filled, but never happened.
Only good thing…it unmolded well!
I bought this flowers to use them in my soaps and candles. They look so nice!

I got this as a gift from my children to make candles and I love the smell!
It reminds me of the splash I always bought from Victoria Secret 20 years ago.

Kind of small if you want to make bigger candles. Capacity is okey for smaller amounts of wax.
It came with 2 tins for container candles. Works well.

I love this micas. I bought them last year and have been using them in my cold process soaps and my candles. I recommend to dissolve in oil before adding to soap.
The colors are vibrant and for the amount you get, it is cheaper than other micas I buy.
In the little samples of 24 you get colors they do not offer in bigger amount. Pitty!
Will buy again, but a little goes a long way, so…I still have the ones from last year.

I wanted to write a good review , because I loved the shade of red, it looks so beautiful and it looks of good quality, but when I found the pieces that were taped together I said: Really!
I read the other reviewes that complained about it, but wanted to give it a try…. well, my mistake!

I love this mold! It is sturdy, easy to use and comes with instructions. To make shure the candle slips out easy I covered the inside with some olive oil and voilá! ...came out! I only use it for oil based wax  or a mixture of waxes candles,  because I imagine that a 100% beeswax candle will maybe to hard to get out...but I will still try it.

Very cute bear. Making soap and people love them for baby showers. Just be careful pulling it out and spay a lot of alcohol before pouring the soap and you will have a beautiful soap and the mold will last.
Very nice mold! Good quality. Soap gets out very easily.

Disappointed! I only have used 4 colors so far for handmade soap; Space blue was nothing like blue, it was dark grey; Purple was not purple, it is more like dark violet brown? Pink is not pink, it is an orange coral; the only color that came out very vibrant and nice, was Shamrock Green. Let's see what happens when I use the other ones...!
Light and comfortable to use..
Very light and comfortable to use.
Good for kids!

This is good. Blocks separate cleanly but stay together well. I’m using to reconfigure a handgun safe to accommodate a second gun. Size of each block is good, too. Smaller than the ones that came originally in my Stealth safe.
